sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कथं विराटनगरे मम पूर्वपितामहाः ।अज्ञातवासमुषिता दुर्योधनभयार्दिताः ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kathaṁ virāṭanagare mama pūrvapitāmahāḥ ,ajñātavāsamuṣitā duryodhanabhayārditāḥ.
english: Janamejaya asked: "How did my ancestors, afflicted by the fear of Duryodhana, live in secret (ajñātavāsa) in the city of Virāṭa?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Janamejaya asked: "How did my ancestors, afflicted by the fear of Duryodhana, live in secret (ajñātavāsa) in the city of Virāṭa?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा तु स वराँल्लब्ध्वा धर्माद्धर्मभृतां वरः ।गत्वाश्रमं ब्राह्मणेभ्य आचख्यौ सर्वमेव तत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā tu sa varāँllabdhvā dharmāddharmabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,gatvāśramaṁ brāhmaṇebhya ācakhyau sarvameva tat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Thus, that foremost upholder of natural law (dharma), having obtained boons (vara) from Dharma (the deity), went to the hermitage (āśrama) and recounted all of that to the brahmins."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: "Thus, that foremost upholder of natural law (dharma), having obtained boons (vara) from Dharma (the deity), went to the hermitage (āśrama) and recounted all of that to the brahmins."

sanskrit: कथयित्वा तु तत्सर्वं ब्राह्मणेभ्यो युधिष्ठिरः ।अरणीसहितं तस्मै ब्राह्मणाय न्यवेदयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: kathayitvā tu tatsarvaṁ brāhmaṇebhyo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,araṇīsahitaṁ tasmai brāhmaṇāya nyavedayat.
english: Having recounted all of that to the brahmins, Yudhiṣṭhira then presented the (recovered) fire-sticks (araṇi) and their accessories to that brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. Having recounted all of that to the brahmins, Yudhiṣṭhira then presented the (recovered) fire-sticks (araṇi) and their accessories to that brahmin.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा धर्मपुत्रो महामनाः ।संनिवर्त्यानुजान्सर्वानिति होवाच भारत ॥४॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā dharmaputro mahāmanāḥ ,saṁnivartyānujānsarvāniti hovāca bhārata.
english: Thereafter, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the magnanimous son of Dharma (the deity), having dismissed all his younger brothers, spoke as follows, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Thereafter, King Yudhiṣṭhira, the magnanimous son of Dharma (the deity), having dismissed all his younger brothers, spoke as follows, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: द्वादशेमानि वर्षाणि राष्ट्राद्विप्रोषिता वयम् ।त्रयोदशोऽयं संप्राप्तः कृच्छ्रः परमदुर्वसः ॥५॥
transliteration: dvādaśemāni varṣāṇi rāṣṭrādviproṣitā vayam ,trayodaśo'yaṁ saṁprāptaḥ kṛcchraḥ paramadurvasaḥ.
english: We have been exiled from our kingdom for these twelve years. Now, this thirteenth year, which is extremely difficult to endure, has arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. We have been exiled from our kingdom for these twelve years. Now, this thirteenth year, which is extremely difficult to endure, has arrived.

sanskrit: स साधु कौन्तेय इतो वासमर्जुन रोचय ।यत्रेमा वसतीः सर्वा वसेमाविदिताः परैः ॥६॥
transliteration: sa sādhu kaunteya ito vāsamarjuna rocaya ,yatremā vasatīḥ sarvā vasemāviditāḥ paraiḥ.
english: O Kaunteya, O Arjuna, please choose a suitable place for us to dwell, where we all may live unknown to our enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. O Kaunteya, O Arjuna, please choose a suitable place for us to dwell, where we all may live unknown to our enemies.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।तस्यैव वरदानेन धर्मस्य मनुजाधिप ।अज्ञाता विचरिष्यामो नराणां भरतर्षभ ॥७॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,tasyaiva varadānena dharmasya manujādhipa ,ajñātā vicariṣyāmo narāṇāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Arjuna said: "O lord of men (manujādhipa), O best among Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), by the boon of Dharma (dharma) alone, we shall indeed wander unknown to all people."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Arjuna said: "O lord of men (manujādhipa), O best among Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), by the boon of Dharma (dharma) alone, we shall indeed wander unknown to all people."

sanskrit: किं तु वासाय राष्ट्राणि कीर्तयिष्यामि कानिचित् ।रमणीयानि गुप्तानि तेषां किंचित्स्म रोचय ॥८॥
transliteration: kiṁ tu vāsāya rāṣṭrāṇi kīrtayiṣyāmi kānicit ,ramaṇīyāni guptāni teṣāṁ kiṁcitsma rocaya.
english: However, I shall name some delightful and secure kingdoms for our residence. From among them, please choose one.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. However, I shall name some delightful and secure kingdoms for our residence. From among them, please choose one.

sanskrit: सन्ति रम्या जनपदा बह्वन्नाः परितः कुरून् ।पाञ्चालाश्चेदिमत्स्याश्च शूरसेनाः पटच्चराः ।दशार्णा नवराष्ट्रं च मल्लाः शाल्वा युगंधराः ॥९॥
transliteration: santi ramyā janapadā bahvannāḥ paritaḥ kurūn ,pāñcālāścedimatsyāśca śūrasenāḥ paṭaccarāḥ ,daśārṇā navarāṣṭraṁ ca mallāḥ śālvā yugaṁdharāḥ.
english: Around the Kurus, there are many beautiful and prosperous kingdoms, rich in food: Pañcālas, Cedis, Matsyas, Śūrasenas, Paṭaccarās, Daśārṇas, Navarāṣṭras, Mallās, Śālvas, and Yugandharas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Around the Kurus, there are many beautiful and prosperous kingdoms, rich in food: Pañcālas, Cedis, Matsyas, Śūrasenas, Paṭaccarās, Daśārṇas, Navarāṣṭras, Mallās, Śālvas, and Yugandharas.

sanskrit: एतेषां कतमो राजन्निवासस्तव रोचते ।वत्स्यामो यत्र राजेन्द्र संवत्सरमिमं वयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: eteṣāṁ katamo rājannivāsastava rocate ,vatsyāmo yatra rājendra saṁvatsaramimaṁ vayam.
english: O King (rājan), which of these residences is agreeable to you? O King of Kings (rājendra), we will reside for this year wherever that may be.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. O King (rājan), which of these residences is agreeable to you? O King of Kings (rājendra), we will reside for this year wherever that may be.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एवमेतन्महाबाहो यथा स भगवान्प्रभुः ।अब्रवीत्सर्वभूतेशस्तत्तथा न तदन्यथा ॥११॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,evametanmahābāho yathā sa bhagavānprabhuḥ ,abravītsarvabhūteśastattathā na tadanyathā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), this is indeed so, just as that glorious (bhagavān) Lord (prabhu), the sovereign over all beings (sarvabhūteśa), has spoken. It is precisely so, and not otherwise.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), this is indeed so, just as that glorious (bhagavān) Lord (prabhu), the sovereign over all beings (sarvabhūteśa), has spoken. It is precisely so, and not otherwise.'

sanskrit: अवश्यं त्वेव वासार्थं रमणीयं शिवं सुखम् ।संमन्त्र्य सहितैः सर्वैर्द्रष्टव्यमकुतोभयम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ tveva vāsārthaṁ ramaṇīyaṁ śivaṁ sukham ,saṁmantrya sahitaiḥ sarvairdraṣṭavyamakutobhayam.
english: Certainly, for our stay, a charming, auspicious, and comfortable place, entirely free from fear (akutobhayam), must be located after we have consulted with everyone.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. Certainly, for our stay, a charming, auspicious, and comfortable place, entirely free from fear (akutobhayam), must be located after we have consulted with everyone.

sanskrit: मत्स्यो विराटो बलवानभिरक्षेत्स पाण्डवान् ।धर्मशीलो वदान्यश्च वृद्धश्च सुमहाधनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: matsyo virāṭo balavānabhirakṣetsa pāṇḍavān ,dharmaśīlo vadānyaśca vṛddhaśca sumahādhanaḥ.
english: Matsya (King Virāṭa) is powerful; he will protect the Pāṇḍavas. He is righteous (dharmaśīla), generous, elderly, and very wealthy.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. Matsya (King Virāṭa) is powerful; he will protect the Pāṇḍavas. He is righteous (dharmaśīla), generous, elderly, and very wealthy.

sanskrit: विराटनगरे तात संवत्सरमिमं वयम् ।कुर्वन्तस्तस्य कर्माणि विहरिष्याम भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: virāṭanagare tāta saṁvatsaramimaṁ vayam ,kurvantastasya karmāṇi vihariṣyāma bhārata.
english: O dear (tāta), we shall live in King Virāṭa's city for this year, performing his tasks (karma), O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. O dear (tāta), we shall live in King Virāṭa's city for this year, performing his tasks (karma), O Bhārata.

sanskrit: यानि यानि च कर्माणि तस्य शक्ष्यामहे वयम् ।कर्तुं यो यत्स तत्कर्म ब्रवीतु कुरुनन्दनाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yāni yāni ca karmāṇi tasya śakṣyāmahe vayam ,kartuṁ yo yatsa tatkarma bravītu kurunandanāḥ.
english: Whatever tasks (karma) of his we are capable of performing, let each one of you, O sons of Kuru (kurunandanāḥ), say what that task is.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. Whatever tasks (karma) of his we are capable of performing, let each one of you, O sons of Kuru (kurunandanāḥ), say what that task is.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।नरदेव कथं कर्म राष्ट्रे तस्य करिष्यसि ।विराटनृपतेः साधो रंस्यसे केन कर्मणा ॥१६॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,naradeva kathaṁ karma rāṣṭre tasya kariṣyasi ,virāṭanṛpateḥ sādho raṁsyase kena karmaṇā.
english: Arjuna said: "O king (naradeva), how will you perform your duty (karma) in his kingdom? O good one (sādho), by what task (karma) will you live for King Virāṭa?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. Arjuna said: "O king (naradeva), how will you perform your duty (karma) in his kingdom? O good one (sādho), by what task (karma) will you live for King Virāṭa?"

sanskrit: मृदुर्वदान्यो ह्रीमांश्च धार्मिकः सत्यविक्रमः ।राजंस्त्वमापदा क्लिष्टः किं करिष्यसि पाण्डव ॥१७॥
transliteration: mṛdurvadānyo hrīmāṁśca dhārmikaḥ satyavikramaḥ ,rājaṁstvamāpadā kliṣṭaḥ kiṁ kariṣyasi pāṇḍava.
english: O King (rājan), O son of Pāṇḍu, you are gentle, generous, modest, righteous (dhārmika), and truly mighty. Afflicted by misfortune (āpad), what will you do?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. O King (rājan), O son of Pāṇḍu, you are gentle, generous, modest, righteous (dhārmika), and truly mighty. Afflicted by misfortune (āpad), what will you do?

sanskrit: न दुःखमुचितं किंचिद्राजन्वेद यथा जनः ।स इमामापदं प्राप्य कथं घोरां तरिष्यसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: na duḥkhamucitaṁ kiṁcidrājanveda yathā janaḥ ,sa imāmāpadaṁ prāpya kathaṁ ghorāṁ tariṣyasi.
english: O King (rājan), as people know, no sorrow (duḥkha) is suitable for you. How will you, having met this terrible misfortune (āpad), overcome it?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. O King (rājan), as people know, no sorrow (duḥkha) is suitable for you. How will you, having met this terrible misfortune (āpad), overcome it?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।शृणुध्वं यत्करिष्यामि कर्म वै कुरुनन्दनाः ।विराटमनुसंप्राप्य राजानं पुरुषर्षभम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,śṛṇudhvaṁ yatkariṣyāmi karma vai kurunandanāḥ ,virāṭamanusaṁprāpya rājānaṁ puruṣarṣabham.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: O descendants of Kuru, listen to what work (karma) I will do, having approached King Virāṭa, the foremost among men (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. Yudhiṣṭhira said: O descendants of Kuru, listen to what work (karma) I will do, having approached King Virāṭa, the foremost among men (puruṣa).

sanskrit: सभास्तारो भविष्यामि तस्य राज्ञो महात्मनः ।कङ्को नाम द्विजो भूत्वा मताक्षः प्रियदेविता ॥२०॥
transliteration: sabhāstāro bhaviṣyāmi tasya rājño mahātmanaḥ ,kaṅko nāma dvijo bhūtvā matākṣaḥ priyadevitā.
english: I will become an assembler of the court for that great-souled king (mahātman), taking the name Kaṅka, a brāhmaṇa (dvija), skilled in dice, and fond of gambling.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 20. I will become an assembler of the court for that great-souled king (mahātman), taking the name Kaṅka, a brāhmaṇa (dvija), skilled in dice, and fond of gambling.

sanskrit: वैडूर्यान्काञ्चनान्दान्तान्फलैर्ज्योतीरसैः सह ।कृष्णाक्षाँल्लोहिताक्षांश्च निर्वर्त्स्यामि मनोरमान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiḍūryānkāñcanāndāntānphalairjyotīrasaiḥ saha ,kṛṣṇākṣāँllohitākṣāṁśca nirvartsyāmi manoramān.
english: I will prepare delightful dishes, refined, and made with ingredients that are lapis lazuli-colored and golden-colored, along with fruits and lustrous juices, and also those with black and red (spots/ingredients).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 21. I will prepare delightful dishes, refined, and made with ingredients that are lapis lazuli-colored and golden-colored, along with fruits and lustrous juices, and also those with black and red (spots/ingredients).

sanskrit: आसं युधिष्ठिरस्याहं पुरा प्राणसमः सखा ।इति वक्ष्यामि राजानं यदि मामनुयोक्ष्यते ॥२२॥
transliteration: āsaṁ yudhiṣṭhirasyāhaṁ purā prāṇasamaḥ sakhā ,iti vakṣyāmi rājānaṁ yadi māmanuyokṣyate.
english: I was formerly a friend to Yudhiṣṭhira, as dear as life itself. I will tell this to the king if he questions me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 22. I was formerly a friend to Yudhiṣṭhira, as dear as life itself. I will tell this to the king if he questions me.

sanskrit: इत्येतद्वो मयाख्यातं विहरिष्याम्यहं यथा ।वृकोदर विराटे त्वं रंस्यसे केन कर्मणा ॥२३॥
transliteration: ityetadvo mayākhyātaṁ vihariṣyāmyahaṁ yathā ,vṛkodara virāṭe tvaṁ raṁsyase kena karmaṇā.
english: Thus, this has been explained by me to you regarding how I will live. O Vṛkodara (Bhīma), by what activity (karma) will you engage yourself in Virāṭa?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 1 Verse 23. Thus, this has been explained by me to you regarding how I will live. O Vṛkodara (Bhīma), by what activity (karma) will you engage yourself in Virāṭa?

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।पौरोगवो ब्रुवाणोऽहं बल्लवो नाम नामतः ।उपस्थास्यामि राजानं विराटमिति मे मतिः ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,paurogavo bruvāṇo'haṁ ballavo nāma nāmataḥ ,upasthāsyāmi rājānaṁ virāṭamiti me matiḥ.
english: Bhīma said: 'I will declare myself as Ballava by name, the chief cook. I will serve King Virāṭa; this is my intention (mati).'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Bhīma said: 'I will declare myself as Ballava by name, the chief cook. I will serve King Virāṭa; this is my intention (mati).'

sanskrit: सूपानस्य करिष्यामि कुशलोऽस्मि महानसे ।कृतपूर्वाणि यैरस्य व्यञ्जनानि सुशिक्षितैः ।तानप्यभिभविष्यामि प्रीतिं संजनयन्नहम् ॥२॥
transliteration: sūpānasya kariṣyāmi kuśalo'smi mahānase ,kṛtapūrvāṇi yairasya vyañjanāni suśikṣitaiḥ ,tānapyabhibhaviṣyāmi prītiṁ saṁjanayannaham.
english: I am skilled in cookery, and I will prepare dishes. I will even surpass those well-trained cooks who have previously prepared his dishes, thus generating satisfaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. I am skilled in cookery, and I will prepare dishes. I will even surpass those well-trained cooks who have previously prepared his dishes, thus generating satisfaction.

sanskrit: आहरिष्यामि दारूणां निचयान्महतोऽपि च ।तत्प्रेक्ष्य विपुलं कर्म राजा प्रीतो भविष्यति ॥३॥
transliteration: āhariṣyāmi dārūṇāṁ nicayānmahato'pi ca ,tatprekṣya vipulaṁ karma rājā prīto bhaviṣyati.
english: I will bring large piles of wood. Witnessing this extensive work (karma), the king will become pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. I will bring large piles of wood. Witnessing this extensive work (karma), the king will become pleased.

sanskrit: द्विपा वा बलिनो राजन्वृषभा वा महाबलाः ।विनिग्राह्या यदि मया निग्रहीष्यामि तानपि ॥४॥
transliteration: dvipā vā balino rājanvṛṣabhā vā mahābalāḥ ,vinigrāhyā yadi mayā nigrahīṣyāmi tānapi.
english: O king, if strong elephants or very powerful bulls are to be subdued by me, I will subdue them also.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. O king, if strong elephants or very powerful bulls are to be subdued by me, I will subdue them also.

sanskrit: ये च केचिन्नियोत्स्यन्ति समाजेषु नियोधकाः ।तानहं निहनिष्यामि प्रीतिं तस्य विवर्धयन् ॥५॥
transliteration: ye ca kecinniyotsyanti samājeṣu niyodhakāḥ ,tānahaṁ nihaniṣyāmi prītiṁ tasya vivardhayan.
english: And whoever among the fighters may contend in the assemblies, I will defeat them, thereby increasing his satisfaction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. And whoever among the fighters may contend in the assemblies, I will defeat them, thereby increasing his satisfaction.

sanskrit: न त्वेतान्युध्यमानान्वै हनिष्यामि कथंचन ।तथैतान्पातयिष्यामि यथा यास्यन्ति न क्षयम् ॥६॥
transliteration: na tvetānyudhyamānānvai haniṣyāmi kathaṁcana ,tathaitānpātayiṣyāmi yathā yāsyanti na kṣayam.
english: Indeed, I will certainly not kill these combatants by any means. Instead, I will bring them down in such a way that they will not meet utter destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. Indeed, I will certainly not kill these combatants by any means. Instead, I will bring them down in such a way that they will not meet utter destruction.

sanskrit: आरालिको गोविकर्ता सूपकर्ता नियोधकः ।आसं युधिष्ठिरस्याहमिति वक्ष्यामि पृच्छतः ॥७॥
transliteration: ārāliko govikartā sūpakartā niyodhakaḥ ,āsaṁ yudhiṣṭhirasyāhamiti vakṣyāmi pṛcchataḥ.
english: I was a chef, a cattle butcher, a preparer of curries, and a superintendent for Yudhishthira. That is what I will say to anyone who asks.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. I was a chef, a cattle butcher, a preparer of curries, and a superintendent for Yudhishthira. That is what I will say to anyone who asks.

sanskrit: आत्मानमात्मना रक्षंश्चरिष्यामि विशां पते ।इत्येतत्प्रतिजानामि विहरिष्याम्यहं यथा ॥८॥
transliteration: ātmānamātmanā rakṣaṁścariṣyāmi viśāṁ pate ,ityetatpratijānāmi vihariṣyāmyahaṁ yathā.
english: O ruler of the people, I will sustain myself (ātman) by my own (ātman) efforts. This I declare: I will live in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. O ruler of the people, I will sustain myself (ātman) by my own (ātman) efforts. This I declare: I will live in this manner.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।यमग्निर्ब्राह्मणो भूत्वा समागच्छन्नृणां वरम् ।दिधक्षुः खाण्डवं दावं दाशार्हसहितं पुरा ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yamagnirbrāhmaṇo bhūtvā samāgacchannṛṇāṁ varam ,didhakṣuḥ khāṇḍavaṁ dāvaṁ dāśārhasahitaṁ purā.
english: Yudhishthira said: Formerly, Agni, having assumed the form of a Brahmin and desiring to burn the Khandava forest (khāṇḍava) with a conflagration, approached the best of men, who was accompanied by Dasharha (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. Yudhishthira said: Formerly, Agni, having assumed the form of a Brahmin and desiring to burn the Khandava forest (khāṇḍava) with a conflagration, approached the best of men, who was accompanied by Dasharha (Krishna).

sanskrit: महाबलं महाबाहुमजितं कुरुनन्दनम् ।सोऽयं किं कर्म कौन्तेयः करिष्यति धनंजयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: mahābalaṁ mahābāhumajitaṁ kurunandanam ,so'yaṁ kiṁ karma kaunteyaḥ kariṣyati dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: What action (karma) will this Arjuna perform, this son of Kunti (Kaunteya) and conqueror of wealth (Dhānañjaya), who is of great strength, mighty-armed, unconquered, and the delight of the Kurus?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. What action (karma) will this Arjuna perform, this son of Kunti (Kaunteya) and conqueror of wealth (Dhānañjaya), who is of great strength, mighty-armed, unconquered, and the delight of the Kurus?

sanskrit: योऽयमासाद्य तं दावं तर्पयामास पावकम् ।विजित्यैकरथेनेन्द्रं हत्वा पन्नगराक्षसान् ।श्रेष्ठः प्रतियुधां नाम सोऽर्जुनः किं करिष्यति ॥११॥
transliteration: yo'yamāsādya taṁ dāvaṁ tarpayāmāsa pāvakam ,vijityaikarathenendraṁ hatvā pannagarākṣasān ,śreṣṭhaḥ pratiyudhāṁ nāma so'rjunaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati.
english: What will that Arjuna do – he who, having encountered that great forest fire, satisfied the fire god (pāvaka); who conquered Indra while riding a single chariot; and who slew the serpents and Rākṣasas? He is indeed known as the foremost among those who confront opponents.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. What will that Arjuna do – he who, having encountered that great forest fire, satisfied the fire god (pāvaka); who conquered Indra while riding a single chariot; and who slew the serpents and Rākṣasas? He is indeed known as the foremost among those who confront opponents.

sanskrit: सूर्यः प्रतपतां श्रेष्ठो द्विपदां ब्राह्मणो वरः ।आशीविषश्च सर्पाणामग्निस्तेजस्विनां वरः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sūryaḥ pratapatāṁ śreṣṭho dvipadāṁ brāhmaṇo varaḥ ,āśīviṣaśca sarpāṇāmagnistejasvināṁ varaḥ.
english: The Sun is the best among all who shine; the Brahmin is the most excellent among bipeds. Similarly, the venomous snake is the foremost among serpents, and Fire is the best among the radiant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. The Sun is the best among all who shine; the Brahmin is the most excellent among bipeds. Similarly, the venomous snake is the foremost among serpents, and Fire is the best among the radiant.

sanskrit: आयुधानां वरो वज्रः ककुद्मी च गवां वरः ।ह्रदानामुदधिः श्रेष्ठः पर्जन्यो वर्षतां वरः ॥१३॥
transliteration: āyudhānāṁ varo vajraḥ kakudmī ca gavāṁ varaḥ ,hradānāmudadhiḥ śreṣṭhaḥ parjanyo varṣatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Among weapons, the thunderbolt (Vajra) is the best; and among cattle, the bull (kakudmin) is the most excellent. The ocean is the best among reservoirs of water, and Parjanya is the most excellent among those who shower rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Among weapons, the thunderbolt (Vajra) is the best; and among cattle, the bull (kakudmin) is the most excellent. The ocean is the best among reservoirs of water, and Parjanya is the most excellent among those who shower rain.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रश्च नागानां हस्तिष्वैरावतो वरः ।पुत्रः प्रियाणामधिको भार्या च सुहृदां वरा ॥१४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraśca nāgānāṁ hastiṣvairāvato varaḥ ,putraḥ priyāṇāmadhiko bhāryā ca suhṛdāṁ varā.
english: Dhritarashtra is the foremost among serpents; Airavata is the best among elephants; a son is superior among beloved ones; and a wife is the best among friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. Dhritarashtra is the foremost among serpents; Airavata is the best among elephants; a son is superior among beloved ones; and a wife is the best among friends.

sanskrit: यथैतानि विशिष्टानि जात्यां जात्यां वृकोदर ।एवं युवा गुडाकेशः श्रेष्ठः सर्वधनुष्मताम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yathaitāni viśiṣṭāni jātyāṁ jātyāṁ vṛkodara ,evaṁ yuvā guḍākeśaḥ śreṣṭhaḥ sarvadhanuṣmatām.
english: Just as these (mentioned in the previous verse) are distinguished in each of their respective categories, O Vrikodara (Bhima), so too is the young Gudakesha (Arjuna) the foremost among all archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. Just as these (mentioned in the previous verse) are distinguished in each of their respective categories, O Vrikodara (Bhima), so too is the young Gudakesha (Arjuna) the foremost among all archers.

sanskrit: सोऽयमिन्द्रादनवरो वासुदेवाच्च भारत ।गाण्डीवधन्वा श्वेताश्वो बीभत्सुः किं करिष्यति ॥१६॥
transliteration: so'yamindrādanavaro vāsudevācca bhārata ,gāṇḍīvadhanvā śvetāśvo bībhatsuḥ kiṁ kariṣyati.
english: This very one (Arjuna) is not inferior to Indra, nor to Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), O Bharata. What will this wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow, mounted on white horses, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), accomplish?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. This very one (Arjuna) is not inferior to Indra, nor to Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa), O Bharata. What will this wielder of the Gaṇḍīva bow, mounted on white horses, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), accomplish?

sanskrit: उषित्वा पञ्च वर्षाणि सहस्राक्षस्य वेश्मनि ।दिव्यान्यस्त्राण्यवाप्तानि देवरूपेण भास्वता ॥१७॥
transliteration: uṣitvā pañca varṣāṇi sahasrākṣasya veśmani ,divyānyastrāṇyavāptāni devarūpeṇa bhāsvatā.
english: Having resided for five years in the abode of the thousand-eyed (Indra), divine weapons were acquired by the glorious one (Arjuna), who appeared in a divine form.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. Having resided for five years in the abode of the thousand-eyed (Indra), divine weapons were acquired by the glorious one (Arjuna), who appeared in a divine form.

sanskrit: यं मन्ये द्वादशं रुद्रमादित्यानां त्रयोदशम् ।यस्य बाहू समौ दीर्घौ ज्याघातकठिनत्वचौ ।दक्षिणे चैव सव्ये च गवामिव वहः कृतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yaṁ manye dvādaśaṁ rudramādityānāṁ trayodaśam ,yasya bāhū samau dīrghau jyāghātakaṭhinatvacau ,dakṣiṇe caiva savye ca gavāmiva vahaḥ kṛtaḥ.
english: I consider him the twelfth of the Rudras and the thirteenth of the Adityas. His arms are equally long, and the skin on both his right and left arms is hardened by the impact of the bowstring, resembling the calluses on oxen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. I consider him the twelfth of the Rudras and the thirteenth of the Adityas. His arms are equally long, and the skin on both his right and left arms is hardened by the impact of the bowstring, resembling the calluses on oxen.

sanskrit: हिमवानिव शैलानां समुद्रः सरितामिव ।त्रिदशानां यथा शक्रो वसूनामिव हव्यवाट् ॥१९॥
transliteration: himavāniva śailānāṁ samudraḥ saritāmiva ,tridaśānāṁ yathā śakro vasūnāmiva havyavāṭ.
english: He is like the Himalayas among mountains, the ocean among rivers, just as Indra (śakra) is among the gods (tridaśa), and Agni (havyavāṭ) among the Vasus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. He is like the Himalayas among mountains, the ocean among rivers, just as Indra (śakra) is among the gods (tridaśa), and Agni (havyavāṭ) among the Vasus.

sanskrit: मृगाणामिव शार्दूलो गरुडः पततामिव ।वरः संनह्यमानानामर्जुनः किं करिष्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: mṛgāṇāmiva śārdūlo garuḍaḥ patatāmiva ,varaḥ saṁnahyamānānāmarjunaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati.
english: He is like a tiger among animals (mṛga), and Garuda among flying creatures. What will Arjuna, who is the best among those arming themselves, do (against him)?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. He is like a tiger among animals (mṛga), and Garuda among flying creatures. What will Arjuna, who is the best among those arming themselves, do (against him)?

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।प्रतिज्ञां षण्ढकोऽस्मीति करिष्यामि महीपते ।ज्याघातौ हि महान्तौ मे संवर्तुं नृप दुष्करौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,pratijñāṁ ṣaṇḍhako'smīti kariṣyāmi mahīpate ,jyāghātau hi mahāntau me saṁvartuṁ nṛpa duṣkarau.
english: Arjuna said: 'O King (mahīpate), I will take the vow, "I am a eunuch," because, O King (nṛpa), my two prominent bowstring marks are difficult to conceal.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 21. Arjuna said: 'O King (mahīpate), I will take the vow, "I am a eunuch," because, O King (nṛpa), my two prominent bowstring marks are difficult to conceal.'

sanskrit: कर्णयोः प्रतिमुच्याहं कुण्डले ज्वलनोपमे ।वेणीकृतशिरा राजन्नाम्ना चैव बृहन्नडा ॥२२॥
transliteration: karṇayoḥ pratimucyāhaṁ kuṇḍale jvalanopame ,veṇīkṛtaśirā rājannāmnā caiva bṛhannaḍā.
english: O king, having adorned my ears with earrings resembling fire, and with my hair braided, I will be known by the name Brihannala.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 22. O king, having adorned my ears with earrings resembling fire, and with my hair braided, I will be known by the name Brihannala.

sanskrit: पठन्नाख्यायिकां नाम स्त्रीभावेन पुनः पुनः ।रमयिष्ये महीपालमन्यांश्चान्तःपुरे जनान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: paṭhannākhyāyikāṁ nāma strībhāvena punaḥ punaḥ ,ramayiṣye mahīpālamanyāṁścāntaḥpure janān.
english: Repeatedly reciting various stories and assuming a feminine demeanor, I will entertain the king and other people within the inner palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. Repeatedly reciting various stories and assuming a feminine demeanor, I will entertain the king and other people within the inner palace.

sanskrit: गीतं नृत्तं विचित्रं च वादित्रं विविधं तथा ।शिक्षयिष्याम्यहं राजन्विराटभवने स्त्रियः ॥२४॥
transliteration: gītaṁ nṛttaṁ vicitraṁ ca vāditraṁ vividhaṁ tathā ,śikṣayiṣyāmyahaṁ rājanvirāṭabhavane striyaḥ.
english: O king, in Virata's palace, I will teach the women various kinds of singing, dancing, and instrumental music.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 24. O king, in Virata's palace, I will teach the women various kinds of singing, dancing, and instrumental music.

sanskrit: प्रजानां समुदाचारं बहु कर्मकृतं वदन् ।छादयिष्यामि कौन्तेय माययात्मानमात्मना ॥२५॥
transliteration: prajānāṁ samudācāraṁ bahu karmakṛtaṁ vadan ,chādayiṣyāmi kaunteya māyayātmānamātmanā.
english: O son of Kunti, by speaking extensively about the behavior and many actions (karma) of people, I will conceal my own self (ātman) through my illusion (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 25. O son of Kunti, by speaking extensively about the behavior and many actions (karma) of people, I will conceal my own self (ātman) through my illusion (māyā).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य गेहेऽस्मि द्रौपद्याः परिचारिका ।उषितास्मीति वक्ष्यामि पृष्टो राज्ञा च भारत ॥२६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya gehe'smi draupadyāḥ paricārikā ,uṣitāsmīti vakṣyāmi pṛṣṭo rājñā ca bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, when questioned by the king, I will say, 'I am Draupadi's maidservant, and I have resided in Yudhishthira's house.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 26. O descendant of Bharata, when questioned by the king, I will say, 'I am Draupadi's maidservant, and I have resided in Yudhishthira's house.'

sanskrit: एतेन विधिना छन्नः कृतकेन यथा नलः ।विहरिष्यामि राजेन्द्र विराटभवने सुखम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: etena vidhinā channaḥ kṛtakena yathā nalaḥ ,vihariṣyāmi rājendra virāṭabhavane sukham.
english: O sovereign king, I will live happily in Virata's palace, disguised by this artificial means, just as Nala did.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 2 Verse 27. O sovereign king, I will live happily in Virata's palace, disguised by this artificial means, just as Nala did.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।किं त्वं नकुल कुर्वाणस्तत्र तात चरिष्यसि ।सुकुमारश्च शूरश्च दर्शनीयः सुखोचितः ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kiṁ tvaṁ nakula kurvāṇastatra tāta cariṣyasi ,sukumāraśca śūraśca darśanīyaḥ sukhocitaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said, 'O dear Nakula, what will you be doing there? You are so delicate, brave, handsome, and accustomed to comfort!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said, 'O dear Nakula, what will you be doing there? You are so delicate, brave, handsome, and accustomed to comfort!'

sanskrit: नकुल उवाच ।अश्वबन्धो भविष्यामि विराटनृपतेरहम् ।ग्रन्थिको नाम नाम्नाहं कर्मैतत्सुप्रियं मम ॥२॥
transliteration: nakula uvāca ,aśvabandho bhaviṣyāmi virāṭanṛpateraham ,granthiko nāma nāmnāhaṁ karmaitatsupriyaṁ mama.
english: Nakula said, 'I will be the horse-keeper for King Virata. My name will be Granthika. This work (karma) is very dear to me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. Nakula said, 'I will be the horse-keeper for King Virata. My name will be Granthika. This work (karma) is very dear to me.'

sanskrit: कुशलोऽस्म्यश्वशिक्षायां तथैवाश्वचिकित्सिते ।प्रियाश्च सततं मेऽश्वाः कुरुराज यथा तव ॥३॥
transliteration: kuśalo'smyaśvaśikṣāyāṁ tathaivāśvacikitsite ,priyāśca satataṁ me'śvāḥ kururāja yathā tava.
english: O King of Kurus, I am skilled in both horse training and veterinary medicine for horses. My horses are always as dear to me as your horses are.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. O King of Kurus, I am skilled in both horse training and veterinary medicine for horses. My horses are always as dear to me as your horses are.

sanskrit: ये मामामन्त्रयिष्यन्ति विराटनगरे जनाः ।तेभ्य एवं प्रवक्ष्यामि विहरिष्याम्यहं यथा ॥४॥
transliteration: ye māmāmantrayiṣyanti virāṭanagare janāḥ ,tebhya evaṁ pravakṣyāmi vihariṣyāmyahaṁ yathā.
english: To the people in the city of Virata who invite me, I will speak thus, explaining how I intend to reside there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. To the people in the city of Virata who invite me, I will speak thus, explaining how I intend to reside there.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।सहदेव कथं तस्य समीपे विहरिष्यसि ।किं वा त्वं तात कुर्वाणः प्रच्छन्नो विचरिष्यसि ॥५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,sahadeva kathaṁ tasya samīpe vihariṣyasi ,kiṁ vā tvaṁ tāta kurvāṇaḥ pracchanno vicariṣyasi.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'Sahadeva, how will you reside near him? Or what will you do, my dear, while moving about in disguise?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. Yudhishthira said: 'Sahadeva, how will you reside near him? Or what will you do, my dear, while moving about in disguise?'

sanskrit: सहदेव उवाच ।गोसंख्याता भविष्यामि विराटस्य महीपतेः ।प्रतिषेद्धा च दोग्धा च संख्याने कुशलो गवाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sahadeva uvāca ,gosaṁkhyātā bhaviṣyāmi virāṭasya mahīpateḥ ,pratiṣeddhā ca dogdhā ca saṁkhyāne kuśalo gavām.
english: Sahadeva said: 'I will become the cow accountant for King Virata. I will be skilled in restraining cows, milking them, and counting them.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. Sahadeva said: 'I will become the cow accountant for King Virata. I will be skilled in restraining cows, milking them, and counting them.'

sanskrit: तन्तिपाल इति ख्यातो नाम्ना विदितमस्तु ते ।निपुणं च चरिष्यामि व्येतु ते मानसो ज्वरः ॥७॥
transliteration: tantipāla iti khyāto nāmnā viditamastu te ,nipuṇaṁ ca cariṣyāmi vyetu te mānaso jvaraḥ.
english: Let it be known to you that I am known by the name Tantipala. I will perform my duties expertly, so let your mental fever (jvara) depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. Let it be known to you that I am known by the name Tantipala. I will perform my duties expertly, so let your mental fever (jvara) depart.

sanskrit: अहं हि भवता गोषु सततं प्रकृतः पुरा ।तत्र मे कौशलं कर्म अवबुद्धं विशां पते ॥८॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hi bhavatā goṣu satataṁ prakṛtaḥ purā ,tatra me kauśalaṁ karma avabuddhaṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: Indeed, O lord of the people, I was formerly appointed by you consistently for duties related to cows. There, my skill (kauśalam) in work (karma) was understood.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. Indeed, O lord of the people, I was formerly appointed by you consistently for duties related to cows. There, my skill (kauśalam) in work (karma) was understood.

sanskrit: लक्षणं चरितं चापि गवां यच्चापि मङ्गलम् ।तत्सर्वं मे सुविदितमन्यच्चापि महीपते ॥९॥
transliteration: lakṣaṇaṁ caritaṁ cāpi gavāṁ yaccāpi maṅgalam ,tatsarvaṁ me suviditamanyaccāpi mahīpate.
english: O lord of the earth, the characteristics and conduct of cows, and whatever is auspicious about them - all of that is very well known to me, as are other things too.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. O lord of the earth, the characteristics and conduct of cows, and whatever is auspicious about them - all of that is very well known to me, as are other things too.

sanskrit: वृषभानपि जानामि राजन्पूजितलक्षणान् ।येषां मूत्रमुपाघ्राय अपि वन्ध्या प्रसूयते ॥१०॥
transliteration: vṛṣabhānapi jānāmi rājanpūjitalakṣaṇān ,yeṣāṁ mūtramupāghrāya api vandhyā prasūyate.
english: O King, I also know bulls that have revered marks, such that even a barren woman gives birth after smelling their urine.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. O King, I also know bulls that have revered marks, such that even a barren woman gives birth after smelling their urine.

sanskrit: सोऽहमेवं चरिष्यामि प्रीतिरत्र हि मे सदा ।न च मां वेत्स्यति परस्तत्ते रोचतु पार्थिव ॥११॥
transliteration: so'hamevaṁ cariṣyāmi prītiratra hi me sadā ,na ca māṁ vetsyati parastatte rocatu pārthiva.
english: I will behave in this way, for indeed my affection is always here. No one else will know me. May that be pleasing to you, O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. I will behave in this way, for indeed my affection is always here. No one else will know me. May that be pleasing to you, O King.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।इयं तु नः प्रिया भार्या प्राणेभ्योऽपि गरीयसी ।मातेव परिपाल्या च पूज्या ज्येष्ठेव च स्वसा ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,iyaṁ tu naḥ priyā bhāryā prāṇebhyo'pi garīyasī ,māteva paripālyā ca pūjyā jyeṣṭheva ca svasā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira spoke: Indeed, this beloved wife of ours is even more important than our very lives. She must be protected like a mother and revered like an elder sister.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. Yudhiṣṭhira spoke: Indeed, this beloved wife of ours is even more important than our very lives. She must be protected like a mother and revered like an elder sister.

sanskrit: केन स्म कर्मणा कृष्णा द्रौपदी विचरिष्यति ।न हि किंचिद्विजानाति कर्म कर्तुं यथा स्त्रियः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kena sma karmaṇā kṛṣṇā draupadī vicariṣyati ,na hi kiṁcidvijānāti karma kartuṁ yathā striyaḥ.
english: By what kind of action (karma) will Kṛṣṇā Draupadī conduct herself? For she truly does not know how to perform any work, as women do.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. By what kind of action (karma) will Kṛṣṇā Draupadī conduct herself? For she truly does not know how to perform any work, as women do.

sanskrit: सुकुमारी च बाला च राजपुत्री यशस्विनी ।पतिव्रता महाभागा कथं नु विचरिष्यति ॥१४॥
transliteration: sukumārī ca bālā ca rājaputrī yaśasvinī ,pativratā mahābhāgā kathaṁ nu vicariṣyati.
english: And how indeed will this delicate and youthful princess, renowned, devoted to her husband (pativratā), and greatly fortunate, conduct herself?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. And how indeed will this delicate and youthful princess, renowned, devoted to her husband (pativratā), and greatly fortunate, conduct herself?

sanskrit: माल्यगन्धानलंकारान्वस्त्राणि विविधानि च ।एतान्येवाभिजानाति यतो जाता हि भामिनी ॥१५॥
transliteration: mālyagandhānalaṁkārānvastrāṇi vividhāni ca ,etānyevābhijānāti yato jātā hi bhāminī.
english: She is familiar only with garlands, perfumes, ornaments, and various clothes, for indeed, a beautiful woman (bhāminī) like her is born into such circumstances.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. She is familiar only with garlands, perfumes, ornaments, and various clothes, for indeed, a beautiful woman (bhāminī) like her is born into such circumstances.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।सैरन्ध्र्योऽरक्षिता लोके भुजिष्याः सन्ति भारत ।नैवमन्याः स्त्रियो यान्ति इति लोकस्य निश्चयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,sairandhryo'rakṣitā loke bhujiṣyāḥ santi bhārata ,naivamanyāḥ striyo yānti iti lokasya niścayaḥ.
english: Draupadi said: 'O Bhārata, in this world, Sairandhrīs are unprotected women available for service. Other women do not behave in this way; this is the firm conviction of society.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Draupadi said: 'O Bhārata, in this world, Sairandhrīs are unprotected women available for service. Other women do not behave in this way; this is the firm conviction of society.'

sanskrit: साहं ब्रुवाणा सैरन्ध्री कुशला केशकर्मणि ।आत्मगुप्ता चरिष्यामि यन्मां त्वमनुपृच्छसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: sāhaṁ bruvāṇā sairandhrī kuśalā keśakarmaṇi ,ātmaguptā cariṣyāmi yanmāṁ tvamanupṛcchasi.
english: I am that very Sairandhrī who proclaims herself skilled in hairdressing. I shall conduct myself in a self-protected manner, in the way you are inquiring about me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. I am that very Sairandhrī who proclaims herself skilled in hairdressing. I shall conduct myself in a self-protected manner, in the way you are inquiring about me.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णां प्रत्युपस्थास्ये राजभार्यां यशस्विनीम् ।सा रक्षिष्यति मां प्राप्तां मा ते भूद्दुःखमीदृशम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇāṁ pratyupasthāsye rājabhāryāṁ yaśasvinīm ,sā rakṣiṣyati māṁ prāptāṁ mā te bhūdduḥkhamīdṛśam.
english: I shall approach and serve Sudeshna, the glorious queen. She will protect me when I have reached her. May such sorrow not befall you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. I shall approach and serve Sudeshna, the glorious queen. She will protect me when I have reached her. May such sorrow not befall you.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कल्याणं भाषसे कृष्णे कुले जाता यथा वदेत् ।न पापमभिजानासि साधु साध्वीव्रते स्थिता ॥१९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kalyāṇaṁ bhāṣase kṛṣṇe kule jātā yathā vadet ,na pāpamabhijānāsi sādhu sādhvīvrate sthitā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), you speak wisely, just as one born into a virtuous family would. You do not know sin, as you are truly established in the sacred vows of a chaste woman."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), you speak wisely, just as one born into a virtuous family would. You do not know sin, as you are truly established in the sacred vows of a chaste woman."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कर्माण्युक्तानि युष्माभिर्यानि तानि करिष्यथ ।मम चापि यथाबुद्धि रुचितानि विनिश्चयात् ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,karmāṇyuktāni yuṣmābhiryāni tāni kariṣyatha ,mama cāpi yathābuddhi rucitāni viniścayāt.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "You (all) will certainly perform the actions you have mentioned. And those actions are also agreeable to me, according to my understanding."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "You (all) will certainly perform the actions you have mentioned. And those actions are also agreeable to me, according to my understanding."

sanskrit: पुरोहितोऽयमस्माकमग्निहोत्राणि रक्षतु ।सूदपौरोगवैः सार्धं द्रुपदस्य निवेशने ॥२॥
transliteration: purohito'yamasmākamagnihotrāṇi rakṣatu ,sūdapaurogavaiḥ sārdhaṁ drupadasya niveśane.
english: Let this priest of ours protect the sacred fire rituals (agnihotra) in Drupada's dwelling, accompanied by the cooks and kitchen overseers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. Let this priest of ours protect the sacred fire rituals (agnihotra) in Drupada's dwelling, accompanied by the cooks and kitchen overseers.

sanskrit: इन्द्रसेनमुखाश्चेमे रथानादाय केवलान् ।यान्तु द्वारवतीं शीघ्रमिति मे वर्तते मतिः ॥३॥
transliteration: indrasenamukhāśceme rathānādāya kevalān ,yāntu dvāravatīṁ śīghramiti me vartate matiḥ.
english: Let these men, led by Indrasena, take only the chariots and go quickly to Dvārakā; this is my intention.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. Let these men, led by Indrasena, take only the chariots and go quickly to Dvārakā; this is my intention.

sanskrit: इमाश्च नार्यो द्रौपद्याः सर्वशः परिचारिकाः ।पाञ्चालानेव गच्छन्तु सूदपौरोगवैः सह ॥४॥
transliteration: imāśca nāryo draupadyāḥ sarvaśaḥ paricārikāḥ ,pāñcālāneva gacchantu sūdapaurogavaiḥ saha.
english: These women, who are Draupadi's attendants in every way, should go to the Pañcāla kingdom along with the cooks and royal servants.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. These women, who are Draupadi's attendants in every way, should go to the Pañcāla kingdom along with the cooks and royal servants.

sanskrit: सर्वैरपि च वक्तव्यं न प्रज्ञायन्त पाण्डवाः ।गता ह्यस्मानपाकीर्य सर्वे द्वैतवनादिति ॥५॥
transliteration: sarvairapi ca vaktavyaṁ na prajñāyanta pāṇḍavāḥ ,gatā hyasmānapākīrya sarve dvaitavanāditi.
english: And it must be declared by everyone, 'The Pāṇḍavas are not to be found; they have all departed from the Dvaitavana, having left us behind.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. And it must be declared by everyone, 'The Pāṇḍavas are not to be found; they have all departed from the Dvaitavana, having left us behind.'

sanskrit: धौम्य उवाच ।विदिते चापि वक्तव्यं सुहृद्भिरनुरागतः ।अतोऽहमपि वक्ष्यामि हेतुमात्रं निबोधत ॥६॥
transliteration: dhaumya uvāca ,vidite cāpi vaktavyaṁ suhṛdbhiranurāgataḥ ,ato'hamapi vakṣyāmi hetumātraṁ nibodhata.
english: Dhaumya said: 'Even if the matter is already known, it should be communicated by friends out of affection. Therefore, I too will speak; please understand only the reason.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. Dhaumya said: 'Even if the matter is already known, it should be communicated by friends out of affection. Therefore, I too will speak; please understand only the reason.'

sanskrit: हन्तेमां राजवसतिं राजपुत्रा ब्रवीमि वः ।यथा राजकुलं प्राप्य चरन्प्रेष्यो न रिष्यति ॥७॥
transliteration: hantemāṁ rājavasatiṁ rājaputrā bravīmi vaḥ ,yathā rājakulaṁ prāpya caranpreṣyo na riṣyati.
english: Alas, O princes, I am telling you about this royal residence: how a servant, having entered the royal household, can conduct himself without suffering harm.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. Alas, O princes, I am telling you about this royal residence: how a servant, having entered the royal household, can conduct himself without suffering harm.

sanskrit: दुर्वसं त्वेव कौरव्या जानता राजवेश्मनि ।अमानितैः सुमानार्हा अज्ञातैः परिवत्सरम् ॥८॥
transliteration: durvasaṁ tveva kauravyā jānatā rājaveśmani ,amānitaiḥ sumānārhā ajñātaiḥ parivatsaram.
english: O Kauravya, it is indeed difficult to reside in a royal palace for one who, despite being knowledgeable and highly worthy of respect, is treated without honor and remains unrecognized for a whole year.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. O Kauravya, it is indeed difficult to reside in a royal palace for one who, despite being knowledgeable and highly worthy of respect, is treated without honor and remains unrecognized for a whole year.

sanskrit: दिष्टद्वारो लभेद्द्वारं न च राजसु विश्वसेत् ।तदेवासनमन्विच्छेद्यत्र नाभिषजेत्परः ॥९॥
transliteration: diṣṭadvāro labheddvāraṁ na ca rājasu viśvaset ,tadevāsanamanvicchedyatra nābhiṣajetparaḥ.
english: One should obtain an opportunity as if destined, and one should not trust kings. One should seek out such a seat where another person cannot trouble him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. One should obtain an opportunity as if destined, and one should not trust kings. One should seek out such a seat where another person cannot trouble him.

sanskrit: नास्य यानं न पर्यङ्कं न पीठं न गजं रथम् ।आरोहेत्संमतोऽस्मीति स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: nāsya yānaṁ na paryaṅkaṁ na pīṭhaṁ na gajaṁ ratham ,ārohetsaṁmato'smīti sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: Thinking "I am esteemed," one should not mount his vehicle, nor his couch, nor his seat, nor his elephant, nor his chariot, if one resides in a royal dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Thinking "I am esteemed," one should not mount his vehicle, nor his couch, nor his seat, nor his elephant, nor his chariot, if one resides in a royal dwelling.

sanskrit: अथ यत्रैनमासीनं शङ्केरन्दुष्टचारिणः ।न तत्रोपविशेज्जातु स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥११॥
transliteration: atha yatrainamāsīnaṁ śaṅkeranduṣṭacāriṇaḥ ,na tatropaviśejjātu sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: Furthermore, he should never sit in a place where ill-intentioned persons might suspect him while he is seated, if he resides in a royal dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Furthermore, he should never sit in a place where ill-intentioned persons might suspect him while he is seated, if he resides in a royal dwelling.

sanskrit: न चानुशिष्येद्राजानमपृच्छन्तं कदाचन ।तूष्णीं त्वेनमुपासीत काले समभिपूजयन् ॥१२॥
transliteration: na cānuśiṣyedrājānamapṛcchantaṁ kadācana ,tūṣṇīṁ tvenamupāsīta kāle samabhipūjayan.
english: One should never instruct a king who has not asked for advice. Instead, one should attend upon him silently, showing him reverence at appropriate times.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. One should never instruct a king who has not asked for advice. Instead, one should attend upon him silently, showing him reverence at appropriate times.

sanskrit: असूयन्ति हि राजानो जनाननृतवादिनः ।तथैव चावमन्यन्ते मन्त्रिणं वादिनं मृषा ॥१३॥
transliteration: asūyanti hi rājāno janānanṛtavādinaḥ ,tathaiva cāvamanyante mantriṇaṁ vādinaṁ mṛṣā.
english: Kings indeed resent those who speak untruths. Similarly, they also disrespect a minister who speaks falsely.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Kings indeed resent those who speak untruths. Similarly, they also disrespect a minister who speaks falsely.

sanskrit: नैषां दारेषु कुर्वीत मैत्रीं प्राज्ञः कथंचन ।अन्तःपुरचरा ये च द्वेष्टि यानहिताश्च ये ॥१४॥
transliteration: naiṣāṁ dāreṣu kurvīta maitrīṁ prājñaḥ kathaṁcana ,antaḥpuracarā ye ca dveṣṭi yānahitāśca ye.
english: A wise person should never, by any means, cultivate friendship with the king's wives, nor with those who frequent the inner palace, nor with those whom the king despises, nor with those who are hostile.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. A wise person should never, by any means, cultivate friendship with the king's wives, nor with those who frequent the inner palace, nor with those whom the king despises, nor with those who are hostile.

sanskrit: विदिते चास्य कुर्वीत कार्याणि सुलघून्यपि ।एवं विचरतो राज्ञो न क्षतिर्जायते क्वचित् ॥१५॥
transliteration: vidite cāsya kurvīta kāryāṇi sulaghūnyapi ,evaṁ vicarato rājño na kṣatirjāyate kvacit.
english: And having obtained his (the king's) knowledge or permission, one should undertake even very small tasks. A king who conducts himself in this manner never suffers any harm.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. And having obtained his (the king's) knowledge or permission, one should undertake even very small tasks. A king who conducts himself in this manner never suffers any harm.

sanskrit: यत्नाच्चोपचरेदेनमग्निवद्देववच्च ह ।अनृतेनोपचीर्णो हि हिंस्यादेनमसंशयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: yatnāccopacaredenamagnivaddevavacca ha ,anṛtenopacīrṇo hi hiṁsyādenamasaṁśayam.
english: One should serve him diligently, just as one would serve fire and a deity. Indeed, if he is treated with dishonesty, he would undoubtedly inflict harm.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. One should serve him diligently, just as one would serve fire and a deity. Indeed, if he is treated with dishonesty, he would undoubtedly inflict harm.

sanskrit: यच्च भर्तानुयुञ्जीत तदेवाभ्यनुवर्तयेत् ।प्रमादमवहेलां च कोपं च परिवर्जयेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yacca bhartānuyuñjīta tadevābhyanuvartayet ,pramādamavahelāṁ ca kopaṁ ca parivarjayet.
english: Whatever the husband (bhartā) instructs or asks, she should comply with that very thing. She should avoid negligence, disrespect, and anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. Whatever the husband (bhartā) instructs or asks, she should comply with that very thing. She should avoid negligence, disrespect, and anger.

sanskrit: समर्थनासु सर्वासु हितं च प्रियमेव च ।संवर्णयेत्तदेवास्य प्रियादपि हितं वदेत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: samarthanāsu sarvāsu hitaṁ ca priyameva ca ,saṁvarṇayettadevāsya priyādapi hitaṁ vadet.
english: In all her explanations and justifications, she should declare what is beneficial and pleasing. Indeed, she should speak to him what is beneficial, even more than what is pleasing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. In all her explanations and justifications, she should declare what is beneficial and pleasing. Indeed, she should speak to him what is beneficial, even more than what is pleasing.

sanskrit: अनुकूलो भवेच्चास्य सर्वार्थेषु कथासु च ।अप्रियं चाहितं यत्स्यात्तदस्मै नानुवर्णयेत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: anukūlo bhaveccāsya sarvārtheṣu kathāsu ca ,apriyaṁ cāhitaṁ yatsyāttadasmai nānuvarṇayet.
english: She should also be agreeable to him in all matters and conversations. Whatever is unpleasant and harmful, she should not narrate that to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. She should also be agreeable to him in all matters and conversations. Whatever is unpleasant and harmful, she should not narrate that to him.

sanskrit: नाहमस्य प्रियोऽस्मीति मत्वा सेवेत पण्डितः ।अप्रमत्तश्च यत्तश्च हितं कुर्यात्प्रियं च यत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: nāhamasya priyo'smīti matvā seveta paṇḍitaḥ ,apramattaśca yattaśca hitaṁ kuryātpriyaṁ ca yat.
english: A wise person should serve, even if he considers, "I am not favored by him." Moreover, he should diligently and carefully perform actions that are both beneficial and pleasing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. A wise person should serve, even if he considers, "I am not favored by him." Moreover, he should diligently and carefully perform actions that are both beneficial and pleasing.

sanskrit: नास्यानिष्टानि सेवेत नाहितैः सह संवसेत् ।स्वस्थानान्न विकम्पेत स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: nāsyāniṣṭāni seveta nāhitaiḥ saha saṁvaset ,svasthānānna vikampeta sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: One should not attend to matters displeasing to the king, nor should one associate with those who are hostile to him. One should not waver from one's own proper position. Such a person should indeed dwell in the royal residence.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. One should not attend to matters displeasing to the king, nor should one associate with those who are hostile to him. One should not waver from one's own proper position. Such a person should indeed dwell in the royal residence.

sanskrit: दक्षिणं वाथ वामं वा पार्श्वमासीत पण्डितः ।रक्षिणां ह्यात्तशस्त्राणां स्थानं पश्चाद्विधीयते ।नित्यं विप्रतिषिद्धं तु पुरस्तादासनं महत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇaṁ vātha vāmaṁ vā pārśvamāsīta paṇḍitaḥ ,rakṣiṇāṁ hyāttaśastrāṇāṁ sthānaṁ paścādvidhīyate ,nityaṁ vipratiṣiddhaṁ tu purastādāsanaṁ mahat.
english: A wise person should sit on either the right or the left side (of the king). Indeed, the position for armed guards is ordained to be behind (the king). However, a prominent seat in front is always prohibited.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. A wise person should sit on either the right or the left side (of the king). Indeed, the position for armed guards is ordained to be behind (the king). However, a prominent seat in front is always prohibited.

sanskrit: न च संदर्शने किंचित्प्रवृद्धमपि संजपेत् ।अपि ह्येतद्दरिद्राणां व्यलीकस्थानमुत्तमम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: na ca saṁdarśane kiṁcitpravṛddhamapi saṁjapet ,api hyetaddaridrāṇāṁ vyalīkasthānamuttamam.
english: One should not whisper anything, even if it's a significant matter, when in the presence (of the king or others). For indeed, this is an excellent opportunity for malicious individuals or the envious to cause mischief.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. One should not whisper anything, even if it's a significant matter, when in the presence (of the king or others). For indeed, this is an excellent opportunity for malicious individuals or the envious to cause mischief.

sanskrit: न मृषाभिहितं राज्ञो मनुष्येषु प्रकाशयेत् ।यं चासूयन्ति राजानः पुरुषं न वदेच्च तम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: na mṛṣābhihitaṁ rājño manuṣyeṣu prakāśayet ,yaṁ cāsūyanti rājānaḥ puruṣaṁ na vadecca tam.
english: One should not reveal among people what has been falsely spoken concerning the king. Furthermore, one should not speak to a person whom kings envy.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. One should not reveal among people what has been falsely spoken concerning the king. Furthermore, one should not speak to a person whom kings envy.

sanskrit: शूरोऽस्मीति न दृप्तः स्याद्बुद्धिमानिति वा पुनः ।प्रियमेवाचरन्राज्ञः प्रियो भवति भोगवान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: śūro'smīti na dṛptaḥ syādbuddhimāniti vā punaḥ ,priyamevācaranrājñaḥ priyo bhavati bhogavān.
english: One should not become arrogant thinking, "I am brave," or, "I am intelligent." Rather, by consistently acting in a pleasing way towards the king, one becomes dear (priya) to him and enjoys prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. One should not become arrogant thinking, "I am brave," or, "I am intelligent." Rather, by consistently acting in a pleasing way towards the king, one becomes dear (priya) to him and enjoys prosperity.

sanskrit: ऐश्वर्यं प्राप्य दुष्प्रापं प्रियं प्राप्य च राजतः ।अप्रमत्तो भवेद्राज्ञः प्रियेषु च हितेषु च ॥२६॥
transliteration: aiśvaryaṁ prāpya duṣprāpaṁ priyaṁ prāpya ca rājataḥ ,apramatto bhavedrājñaḥ priyeṣu ca hiteṣu ca.
english: Having attained hard-to-gain sovereignty (aiśvarya) and having received the king's favor, one should be vigilant concerning the king's favorites and his beneficial interests.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. Having attained hard-to-gain sovereignty (aiśvarya) and having received the king's favor, one should be vigilant concerning the king's favorites and his beneficial interests.

sanskrit: यस्य कोपो महाबाधः प्रसादश्च महाफलः ।कस्तस्य मनसापीच्छेदनर्थं प्राज्ञसंमतः ॥२७॥
transliteration: yasya kopo mahābādhaḥ prasādaśca mahāphalaḥ ,kastasya manasāpīcchedanarthaṁ prājñasaṁmataḥ.
english: Whose anger causes great affliction, and whose favor yields great results - who, being approved by the wise, would even mentally desire misfortune for him?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. Whose anger causes great affliction, and whose favor yields great results - who, being approved by the wise, would even mentally desire misfortune for him?

sanskrit: न चोष्ठौ निर्भुजेज्जातु न च वाक्यं समाक्षिपेत् ।सदा क्षुतं च वातं च ष्ठीवनं चाचरेच्छनैः ॥२८॥
transliteration: na coṣṭhau nirbhujejjātu na ca vākyaṁ samākṣipet ,sadā kṣutaṁ ca vātaṁ ca ṣṭhīvanaṁ cācarecchanaiḥ.
english: One should never bite or distort the lips, nor should one interrupt a speech. One should always sneeze, pass wind, and spit gently.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 28. One should never bite or distort the lips, nor should one interrupt a speech. One should always sneeze, pass wind, and spit gently.

sanskrit: हास्यवस्तुषु चाप्यस्य वर्तमानेषु केषुचित् ।नातिगाढं प्रहृष्येत न चाप्युन्मत्तवद्धसेत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: hāsyavastuṣu cāpyasya vartamāneṣu keṣucit ,nātigāḍhaṁ prahṛṣyeta na cāpyunmattavaddhaset.
english: Even when some humorous situations are present, one should not rejoice too intensely, nor should one laugh like a madman.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 29. Even when some humorous situations are present, one should not rejoice too intensely, nor should one laugh like a madman.

sanskrit: न चातिधैर्येण चरेद्गुरुतां हि व्रजेत्तथा ।स्मितं तु मृदुपूर्वेण दर्शयेत प्रसादजम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: na cātidhairyeṇa caredgurutāṁ hi vrajettathā ,smitaṁ tu mṛdupūrveṇa darśayeta prasādajam.
english: One should not behave with excessive firmness, for that would indeed lead to arrogance. Instead, one should display a smile that is gentle initially and arises from a clear, gracious disposition.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 30. One should not behave with excessive firmness, for that would indeed lead to arrogance. Instead, one should display a smile that is gentle initially and arises from a clear, gracious disposition.

sanskrit: लाभे न हर्षयेद्यस्तु न व्यथेद्योऽवमानितः ।असंमूढश्च यो नित्यं स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥३१॥
transliteration: lābhe na harṣayedyastu na vyathedyo'vamānitaḥ ,asaṁmūḍhaśca yo nityaṁ sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: One who does not rejoice in gain, is not distressed when insulted, and is always undeluded - that person is fit to dwell in the king's court.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 31. One who does not rejoice in gain, is not distressed when insulted, and is always undeluded - that person is fit to dwell in the king's court.

sanskrit: राजानं राजपुत्रं वा संवर्तयति यः सदा ।अमात्यः पण्डितो भूत्वा स चिरं तिष्ठति श्रियम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ rājaputraṁ vā saṁvartayati yaḥ sadā ,amātyaḥ paṇḍito bhūtvā sa ciraṁ tiṣṭhati śriyam.
english: A minister who is wise and always serves the king or the prince will endure in prosperity for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 32. A minister who is wise and always serves the king or the prince will endure in prosperity for a long time.

sanskrit: प्रगृहीतश्च योऽमात्यो निगृहीतश्च कारणैः ।न निर्बध्नाति राजानं लभते प्रग्रहं पुनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: pragṛhītaśca yo'mātyo nigṛhītaśca kāraṇaiḥ ,na nirbadhnāti rājānaṁ labhate pragrahaṁ punaḥ.
english: A minister who is both supported and restrained for good reasons does not bind the king and obtains support again.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 33. A minister who is both supported and restrained for good reasons does not bind the king and obtains support again.

sanskrit: प्रत्यक्षं च परोक्षं च गुणवादी विचक्षणः ।उपजीवी भवेद्राज्ञो विषये चापि यो वसेत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: pratyakṣaṁ ca parokṣaṁ ca guṇavādī vicakṣaṇaḥ ,upajīvī bhavedrājño viṣaye cāpi yo vaset.
english: A discerning and wise person who speaks favorably of the king's qualities, both in his presence and absence, and who resides in his domain, should be a dependent (servant) of the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 34. A discerning and wise person who speaks favorably of the king's qualities, both in his presence and absence, and who resides in his domain, should be a dependent (servant) of the king.

sanskrit: अमात्यो हि बलाद्भोक्तुं राजानं प्रार्थयेत्तु यः ।न स तिष्ठेच्चिरं स्थानं गच्छेच्च प्राणसंशयम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: amātyo hi balādbhoktuṁ rājānaṁ prārthayettu yaḥ ,na sa tiṣṭhecciraṁ sthānaṁ gacchecca prāṇasaṁśayam.
english: Indeed, that minister who forcibly seeks to exploit the king will not long retain his position and will instead face a danger to his life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 35. Indeed, that minister who forcibly seeks to exploit the king will not long retain his position and will instead face a danger to his life.

sanskrit: श्रेयः सदात्मनो दृष्ट्वा परं राज्ञा न संवदेत् ।विशेषयेन्न राजानं योग्याभूमिषु सर्वदा ॥३६॥
transliteration: śreyaḥ sadātmano dṛṣṭvā paraṁ rājñā na saṁvadet ,viśeṣayenna rājānaṁ yogyābhūmiṣu sarvadā.
english: Having considered one's own ultimate welfare (śreyas), one should not discuss it with the king. One should also never try to surpass the king in appropriate circumstances.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 36. Having considered one's own ultimate welfare (śreyas), one should not discuss it with the king. One should also never try to surpass the king in appropriate circumstances.

sanskrit: अम्लानो बलवाञ्शूरश्छायेवानपगः सदा ।सत्यवादी मृदुर्दान्तः स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: amlāno balavāñśūraśchāyevānapagaḥ sadā ,satyavādī mṛdurdāntaḥ sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: One who is unwearied, strong, valorous, and always inseparable like a shadow, truthful, gentle, and self-controlled - such a person should serve in the royal court.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 37. One who is unwearied, strong, valorous, and always inseparable like a shadow, truthful, gentle, and self-controlled - such a person should serve in the royal court.

sanskrit: अन्यस्मिन्प्रेष्यमाणे तु पुरस्ताद्यः समुत्पतेत् ।अहं किं करवाणीति स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: anyasminpreṣyamāṇe tu purastādyaḥ samutpatet ,ahaṁ kiṁ karavāṇīti sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: When another person is being given an order, the one who immediately steps forward from the front, saying, "What shall I do?", such a person should serve in the royal court.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 38. When another person is being given an order, the one who immediately steps forward from the front, saying, "What shall I do?", such a person should serve in the royal court.

sanskrit: उष्णे वा यदि वा शीते रात्रौ वा यदि वा दिवा ।आदिष्टो न विकल्पेत स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥३९॥
transliteration: uṣṇe vā yadi vā śīte rātrau vā yadi vā divā ,ādiṣṭo na vikalpeta sa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: Whether in hot weather or in cold, whether by night or by day, one who, when given an order, does not waver or hesitate - such a person should serve in the royal court.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 39. Whether in hot weather or in cold, whether by night or by day, one who, when given an order, does not waver or hesitate - such a person should serve in the royal court.

sanskrit: यो वै गृहेभ्यः प्रवसन्प्रियाणां नानुसंस्मरेत् ।दुःखेन सुखमन्विच्छेत्स राजवसतिं वसेत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: yo vai gṛhebhyaḥ pravasanpriyāṇāṁ nānusaṁsmaret ,duḥkhena sukhamanvicchetsa rājavasatiṁ vaset.
english: Whoever departs from their home and does not recall their loved ones, and seeks happiness through hardship, such a person should reside in the king's palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 40. Whoever departs from their home and does not recall their loved ones, and seeks happiness through hardship, such a person should reside in the king's palace.

sanskrit: समवेषं न कुर्वीत नात्युच्चैः संनिधौ हसेत् ।मन्त्रं न बहुधा कुर्यादेवं राज्ञः प्रियो भवेत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: samaveṣaṁ na kurvīta nātyuccaiḥ saṁnidhau haset ,mantraṁ na bahudhā kuryādevaṁ rājñaḥ priyo bhavet.
english: One should not wear attire similar to the king, nor should one laugh very loudly in his presence. One should not disclose secret counsel (mantra) in many different ways. By following these guidelines, one will become dear to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 41. One should not wear attire similar to the king, nor should one laugh very loudly in his presence. One should not disclose secret counsel (mantra) in many different ways. By following these guidelines, one will become dear to the king.

sanskrit: न कर्मणि नियुक्तः सन्धनं किंचिदुपस्पृशेत् ।प्राप्नोति हि हरन्द्रव्यं बन्धनं यदि वा वधम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: na karmaṇi niyuktaḥ sandhanaṁ kiṁcidupaspṛśet ,prāpnoti hi harandravyaṁ bandhanaṁ yadi vā vadham.
english: One who is engaged in a duty (karma) should not touch any wealth. Indeed, by stealing property, one incurs imprisonment or even death.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 42. One who is engaged in a duty (karma) should not touch any wealth. Indeed, by stealing property, one incurs imprisonment or even death.

sanskrit: यानं वस्त्रमलंकारं यच्चान्यत्संप्रयच्छति ।तदेव धारयेन्नित्यमेवं प्रियतरो भवेत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: yānaṁ vastramalaṁkāraṁ yaccānyatsaṁprayacchati ,tadeva dhārayennityamevaṁ priyataro bhavet.
english: Whatever conveyance, clothing, ornament, and anything else the king bestows, one should always wear/use only that. By doing so, one will become more dear (priyatara) to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 43. Whatever conveyance, clothing, ornament, and anything else the king bestows, one should always wear/use only that. By doing so, one will become more dear (priyatara) to him.

sanskrit: संवत्सरमिमं तात तथाशीला बुभूषवः ।अथ स्वविषयं प्राप्य यथाकामं चरिष्यथ ॥४४॥
transliteration: saṁvatsaramimaṁ tāta tathāśīlā bubhūṣavaḥ ,atha svaviṣayaṁ prāpya yathākāmaṁ cariṣyatha.
english: Dear sons, desiring to maintain this conduct for one year, you will then, upon reaching your own land, move about as you please.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 44. Dear sons, desiring to maintain this conduct for one year, you will then, upon reaching your own land, move about as you please.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अनुशिष्टाः स्म भद्रं ते नैतद्वक्तास्ति कश्चन ।कुन्तीमृते मातरं नो विदुरं च महामतिम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,anuśiṣṭāḥ sma bhadraṁ te naitadvaktāsti kaścana ,kuntīmṛte mātaraṁ no viduraṁ ca mahāmatim.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "We have been taught well; hail to you. No one else has spoken these words to us, apart from our mother Kuntī and the great-minded Vidura."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 45. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "We have been taught well; hail to you. No one else has spoken these words to us, apart from our mother Kuntī and the great-minded Vidura."

sanskrit: यदेवानन्तरं कार्यं तद्भवान्कर्तुमर्हति ।तारणायास्य दुःखस्य प्रस्थानाय जयाय च ॥४६॥
transliteration: yadevānantaraṁ kāryaṁ tadbhavānkartumarhati ,tāraṇāyāsya duḥkhasya prasthānāya jayāya ca.
english: You, venerable sir, should indeed undertake whatever task is next, for the sake of overcoming this suffering, for our departure, and for our victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 46. You, venerable sir, should indeed undertake whatever task is next, for the sake of overcoming this suffering, for our departure, and for our victory.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्ततो राज्ञा धौम्योऽथ द्विजसत्तमः ।अकरोद्विधिवत्सर्वं प्रस्थाने यद्विधीयते ॥४७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastato rājñā dhaumyo'tha dvijasattamaḥ ,akarodvidhivatsarvaṁ prasthāne yadvidhīyate.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, thus addressed by the king, Dhaumya, the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), properly performed all that is prescribed for a departure.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 47. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, thus addressed by the king, Dhaumya, the foremost among the twice-born (dvija), properly performed all that is prescribed for a departure.

sanskrit: तेषां समिध्य तानग्नीन्मन्त्रवच्च जुहाव सः ।समृद्धिवृद्धिलाभाय पृथिवीविजयाय च ॥४८॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ samidhya tānagnīnmantravacca juhāva saḥ ,samṛddhivṛddhilābhāya pṛthivīvijayāya ca.
english: He kindled those fires and offered oblations with mantras, for the attainment of prosperity, growth, and the conquest of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 48. He kindled those fires and offered oblations with mantras, for the attainment of prosperity, growth, and the conquest of the earth.

sanskrit: अग्निं प्रदक्षिणं कृत्वा ब्राह्मणांश्च तपोधनान् ।याज्ञसेनीं पुरस्कृत्य षडेवाथ प्रवव्रजुः ॥४९॥
transliteration: agniṁ pradakṣiṇaṁ kṛtvā brāhmaṇāṁśca tapodhanān ,yājñasenīṁ puraskṛtya ṣaḍevātha pravavrajuḥ.
english: After circumambulating the fire and the Brahmins, who possessed asceticism (tapas) as their wealth, and placing Yājñasenī (Draupadī) at the forefront, the six of them then set off.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 4 Verse 49. After circumambulating the fire and the Brahmins, who possessed asceticism (tapas) as their wealth, and placing Yājñasenī (Draupadī) at the forefront, the six of them then set off.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ते वीरा बद्धनिस्त्रिंशास्ततायुधकलापिनः ।बद्धगोधाङ्गुलित्राणाः कालिन्दीमभितो ययुः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,te vīrā baddhanistriṁśāstatāyudhakalāpinaḥ ,baddhagodhāṅgulitrāṇāḥ kālindīmabhito yayuḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Those heroes, with their swords strapped on, quivers filled with weapons, and arm-guards (made of iguana skin) bound, proceeded towards the Kālindī (Yamunā River).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Those heroes, with their swords strapped on, quivers filled with weapons, and arm-guards (made of iguana skin) bound, proceeded towards the Kālindī (Yamunā River).

sanskrit: ततस्ते दक्षिणं तीरमन्वगच्छन्पदातयः ।वसन्तो गिरिदुर्गेषु वनदुर्गेषु धन्विनः ॥२॥
transliteration: tataste dakṣiṇaṁ tīramanvagacchanpadātayaḥ ,vasanto giridurgeṣu vanadurgeṣu dhanvinaḥ.
english: Then, proceeding on foot, they followed the southern bank, dwelling as archers in mountain fortresses and forest strongholds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Then, proceeding on foot, they followed the southern bank, dwelling as archers in mountain fortresses and forest strongholds.

sanskrit: विध्यन्तो मृगजातानि महेष्वासा महाबलाः ।उत्तरेण दशार्णांस्ते पाञ्चालान्दक्षिणेन तु ॥३॥
transliteration: vidhyanto mṛgajātāni maheṣvāsā mahābalāḥ ,uttareṇa daśārṇāṁste pāñcālāndakṣiṇena tu.
english: Those great archers, mighty and powerful, who were striking down herds of deer, proceeded north of the Daśārṇa region and south of Pāñcāla.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. Those great archers, mighty and powerful, who were striking down herds of deer, proceeded north of the Daśārṇa region and south of Pāñcāla.

sanskrit: अन्तरेण यकृल्लोमाञ्शूरसेनांश्च पाण्डवाः ।लुब्धा ब्रुवाणा मत्स्यस्य विषयं प्राविशन्वनात् ॥४॥
transliteration: antareṇa yakṛllomāñśūrasenāṁśca pāṇḍavāḥ ,lubdhā bruvāṇā matsyasya viṣayaṁ prāviśanvanāt.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, proclaiming themselves to be hunters, entered the Matsya territory from the forest, passing between the Yakṛllomas and the Śūrasenas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. The Pāṇḍavas, proclaiming themselves to be hunters, entered the Matsya territory from the forest, passing between the Yakṛllomas and the Śūrasenas.

sanskrit: ततो जनपदं प्राप्य कृष्णा राजानमब्रवीत् ।पश्यैकपद्यो दृश्यन्ते क्षेत्राणि विविधानि च ॥५॥
transliteration: tato janapadaṁ prāpya kṛṣṇā rājānamabravīt ,paśyaikapadyo dṛśyante kṣetrāṇi vividhāni ca.
english: Then, having arrived at the populated country, Draupadi spoke to the king: 'Look, solitary footpaths are visible, and various fields too.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. Then, having arrived at the populated country, Draupadi spoke to the king: 'Look, solitary footpaths are visible, and various fields too.'

sanskrit: व्यक्तं दूरे विराटस्य राजधानी भविष्यति ।वसामेह परां रात्रिं बलवान्मे परिश्रमः ॥६॥
transliteration: vyaktaṁ dūre virāṭasya rājadhānī bhaviṣyati ,vasāmeha parāṁ rātriṁ balavānme pariśramaḥ.
english: Evidently, the capital of Virāṭa is still far away. Let us stay here for this remaining night; my exhaustion is immense.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. Evidently, the capital of Virāṭa is still far away. Let us stay here for this remaining night; my exhaustion is immense.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।धनंजय समुद्यम्य पाञ्चालीं वह भारत ।राजधान्यां निवत्स्यामो विमुक्ताश्च वनादितः ॥७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,dhanaṁjaya samudyamya pāñcālīṁ vaha bhārata ,rājadhānyāṁ nivatsyāmo vimuktāśca vanāditaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said, "O Dhananjaya, O Bharata, escort Panchali (Draupadi). We, having been released from the forest, will now reside in the capital city."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Yudhishthira said, "O Dhananjaya, O Bharata, escort Panchali (Draupadi). We, having been released from the forest, will now reside in the capital city."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तामादायार्जुनस्तूर्णं द्रौपदीं गजराडिव ।संप्राप्य नगराभ्याशमवतारयदर्जुनः ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tāmādāyārjunastūrṇaṁ draupadīṁ gajarāḍiva ,saṁprāpya nagarābhyāśamavatārayadarjunaḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said, "Arjuna, swiftly taking Draupadi as a king of elephants (gajarāṭ) might, reached the vicinity of the city and then made her dismount."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. Vaishampayana said, "Arjuna, swiftly taking Draupadi as a king of elephants (gajarāṭ) might, reached the vicinity of the city and then made her dismount."

sanskrit: स राजधानीं संप्राप्य कौन्तेयोऽर्जुनमब्रवीत् ।क्वायुधानि समासज्य प्रवेक्ष्यामः पुरं वयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sa rājadhānīṁ saṁprāpya kaunteyo'rjunamabravīt ,kvāyudhāni samāsajya pravekṣyāmaḥ puraṁ vayam.
english: Having reached the capital city, the son of Kunti (Yudhishthira) asked Arjuna, "Where shall we deposit our weapons and then enter the city?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. Having reached the capital city, the son of Kunti (Yudhishthira) asked Arjuna, "Where shall we deposit our weapons and then enter the city?"

sanskrit: सायुधाश्च वयं तात प्रवेक्ष्यामः पुरं यदि ।समुद्वेगं जनस्यास्य करिष्यामो न संशयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sāyudhāśca vayaṁ tāta pravekṣyāmaḥ puraṁ yadi ,samudvegaṁ janasyāsya kariṣyāmo na saṁśayaḥ.
english: "O dear one (tāta), if we enter the city armed, we will undoubtedly cause great agitation among its people."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. "O dear one (tāta), if we enter the city armed, we will undoubtedly cause great agitation among its people."

sanskrit: ततो द्वादश वर्षाणि प्रवेष्टव्यं वनं पुनः ।एकस्मिन्नपि विज्ञाते प्रतिज्ञातं हि नस्तथा ॥११॥
transliteration: tato dvādaśa varṣāṇi praveṣṭavyaṁ vanaṁ punaḥ ,ekasminnapi vijñāte pratijñātaṁ hi nastathā.
english: Then, we must re-enter the forest for twelve years. For it was indeed promised by us that if even one of us were to be recognized, this would be the consequence.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. Then, we must re-enter the forest for twelve years. For it was indeed promised by us that if even one of us were to be recognized, this would be the consequence.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।इयं कूटे मनुष्येन्द्र गहना महती शमी ।भीमशाखा दुरारोहा श्मशानस्य समीपतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,iyaṁ kūṭe manuṣyendra gahanā mahatī śamī ,bhīmaśākhā durārohā śmaśānasya samīpataḥ.
english: Arjuna said: 'O king of men, this very large and dense śamī tree is in a hidden place. It has massive branches and is difficult to climb. It stands near the cremation ground.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. Arjuna said: 'O king of men, this very large and dense śamī tree is in a hidden place. It has massive branches and is difficult to climb. It stands near the cremation ground.'

sanskrit: न चापि विद्यते कश्चिन्मनुष्य इह पार्थिव ।उत्पथे हि वने जाता मृगव्यालनिषेविते ॥१३॥
transliteration: na cāpi vidyate kaścinmanuṣya iha pārthiva ,utpathe hi vane jātā mṛgavyālaniṣevite.
english: Moreover, O king, no human being is found here. Indeed, it (the tree) has grown in a forest that is off the beaten path and frequented by deer and wild animals.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. Moreover, O king, no human being is found here. Indeed, it (the tree) has grown in a forest that is off the beaten path and frequented by deer and wild animals.

sanskrit: समासज्यायुधान्यस्यां गच्छामो नगरं प्रति ।एवमत्र यथाजोषं विहरिष्याम भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: samāsajyāyudhānyasyāṁ gacchāmo nagaraṁ prati ,evamatra yathājoṣaṁ vihariṣyāma bhārata.
english: Having deposited the weapons in this (tree), let us proceed towards the city. In this way, O descendant of Bharata, we will dwell here as we desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. Having deposited the weapons in this (tree), let us proceed towards the city. In this way, O descendant of Bharata, we will dwell here as we desire.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा स राजानं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।प्रचक्रमे निधानाय शस्त्राणां भरतर्षभ ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā sa rājānaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,pracakrame nidhānāya śastrāṇāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus spoken to King Yudhiṣṭhira, whose inner self (ātman) was devoted to righteousness (dharma), he (Arjuna), the foremost of the Bhāratas, then began the task of depositing the weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus spoken to King Yudhiṣṭhira, whose inner self (ātman) was devoted to righteousness (dharma), he (Arjuna), the foremost of the Bhāratas, then began the task of depositing the weapons.

sanskrit: येन देवान्मनुष्यांश्च सर्पांश्चैकरथोऽजयत् ।स्फीताञ्जनपदांश्चान्यानजयत्कुरुनन्दनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: yena devānmanuṣyāṁśca sarpāṁścaikaratho'jayat ,sphītāñjanapadāṁścānyānajayatkurunandanaḥ.
english: By him (Arjuna), the one with a single chariot, gods, humans, and serpents were conquered. The delight of the Kurus (Arjuna) also conquered flourishing territories (janapadas) and other lands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. By him (Arjuna), the one with a single chariot, gods, humans, and serpents were conquered. The delight of the Kurus (Arjuna) also conquered flourishing territories (janapadas) and other lands.

sanskrit: तदुदारं महाघोषं सपत्नगणसूदनम् ।अपज्यमकरोत्पार्थो गाण्डीवमभयंकरम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tadudāraṁ mahāghoṣaṁ sapatnagaṇasūdanam ,apajyamakarotpārtho gāṇḍīvamabhayaṁkaram.
english: Pārtha (Arjuna) rendered that noble, mighty-sounding, destroyer of enemy hosts, and fear-dispelling Gaṇḍīva bow stringless.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. Pārtha (Arjuna) rendered that noble, mighty-sounding, destroyer of enemy hosts, and fear-dispelling Gaṇḍīva bow stringless.

sanskrit: येन वीरः कुरुक्षेत्रमभ्यरक्षत्परंतपः ।अमुञ्चद्धनुषस्तस्य ज्यामक्षय्यां युधिष्ठिरः ॥१८॥
