transliteration: yena vīraḥ kurukṣetramabhyarakṣatparaṁtapaḥ ,amuñcaddhanuṣastasya jyāmakṣayyāṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira removed the inexhaustible bowstring from that bow, by which the hero and tormentor of enemies (Arjuna) had protected Kurukṣetra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. Yudhiṣṭhira removed the inexhaustible bowstring from that bow, by which the hero and tormentor of enemies (Arjuna) had protected Kurukṣetra.

sanskrit: पाञ्चालान्येन संग्रामे भीमसेनोऽजयत्प्रभुः ।प्रत्यषेधद्बहूनेकः सपत्नांश्चैव दिग्जये ॥१९॥
transliteration: pāñcālānyena saṁgrāme bhīmaseno'jayatprabhuḥ ,pratyaṣedhadbahūnekaḥ sapatnāṁścaiva digjaye.
english: The mighty Bhīmasena, by whom the Pañcālas were conquered in battle, single-handedly repelled many enemies during his conquest of regions.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. The mighty Bhīmasena, by whom the Pañcālas were conquered in battle, single-handedly repelled many enemies during his conquest of regions.

sanskrit: निशम्य यस्य विस्फारं व्यद्रवन्त रणे परे ।पर्वतस्येव दीर्णस्य विस्फोटमशनेरिव ॥२०॥
transliteration: niśamya yasya visphāraṁ vyadravanta raṇe pare ,parvatasyeva dīrṇasya visphoṭamaśaneriva.
english: Upon hearing the twang of his bowstring, the enemies fled from battle, as if a split mountain were bursting or a thunderbolt were exploding.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. Upon hearing the twang of his bowstring, the enemies fled from battle, as if a split mountain were bursting or a thunderbolt were exploding.

sanskrit: सैन्धवं येन राजानं परामृषत चानघ ।ज्यापाशं धनुषस्तस्य भीमसेनोऽवतारयत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: saindhavaṁ yena rājānaṁ parāmṛṣata cānagha ,jyāpāśaṁ dhanuṣastasya bhīmaseno'vatārayat.
english: O faultless one (anagha), by whom King Jayadratha was seized, Bhīmasena removed the bowstring from his (the king's) bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. O faultless one (anagha), by whom King Jayadratha was seized, Bhīmasena removed the bowstring from his (the king's) bow.

sanskrit: अजयत्पश्चिमामाशां धनुषा येन पाण्डवः ।तस्य मौर्वीमपाकर्षच्छूरः संक्रन्दनो युधि ॥२२॥
transliteration: ajayatpaścimāmāśāṁ dhanuṣā yena pāṇḍavaḥ ,tasya maurvīmapākarṣacchūraḥ saṁkrandano yudhi.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena), by whom the western region was conquered with his bow, and the heroic Bhīmasena (known as Saṃkrandana) pulled off the bowstring from his (opponent's) bow in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. The Pāṇḍava (Bhīmasena), by whom the western region was conquered with his bow, and the heroic Bhīmasena (known as Saṃkrandana) pulled off the bowstring from his (opponent's) bow in battle.

sanskrit: दक्षिणां दक्षिणाचारो दिशं येनाजयत्प्रभुः ।अपज्यमकरोद्वीरः सहदेवस्तदायुधम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇāṁ dakṣiṇācāro diśaṁ yenājayatprabhuḥ ,apajyamakarodvīraḥ sahadevastadāyudham.
english: Through which (campaign), the lord (prabhu), Sahadeva, who followed righteous conduct, conquered the southern direction. The hero (vīra) Sahadeva then rendered his weapon (bow) stringless.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. Through which (campaign), the lord (prabhu), Sahadeva, who followed righteous conduct, conquered the southern direction. The hero (vīra) Sahadeva then rendered his weapon (bow) stringless.

sanskrit: खड्गांश्च पीतान्दीर्घांश्च कलापांश्च महाधनान् ।विपाठान्क्षुरधारांश्च धनुर्भिर्निदधुः सह ॥२४॥
transliteration: khaḍgāṁśca pītāndīrghāṁśca kalāpāṁśca mahādhanān ,vipāṭhānkṣuradhārāṁśca dhanurbhirnidadhuḥ saha.
english: Together, with their bows, they deposited golden and long swords, as well as very valuable quivers and razor-edged arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. Together, with their bows, they deposited golden and long swords, as well as very valuable quivers and razor-edged arrows.

sanskrit: तामुपारुह्य नकुलो धनूंषि निदधत्स्वयम् ।यानि तस्यावकाशानि दृढरूपाण्यमन्यत ॥२५॥
transliteration: tāmupāruhya nakulo dhanūṁṣi nidadhatsvayam ,yāni tasyāvakāśāni dṛḍharūpāṇyamanyata.
english: Having ascended that (platform/area), Nakula himself placed the bows in those spaces which he considered to be secure.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. Having ascended that (platform/area), Nakula himself placed the bows in those spaces which he considered to be secure.

sanskrit: यत्र चापश्यत स वै तिरो वर्षाणि वर्षति ।तत्र तानि दृढैः पाशैः सुगाढं पर्यबन्धत ॥२६॥
transliteration: yatra cāpaśyata sa vai tiro varṣāṇi varṣati ,tatra tāni dṛḍhaiḥ pāśaiḥ sugāḍhaṁ paryabandhata.
english: And where he indeed saw a place that remained hidden, even with continuous rain for years, there he bound those (bows) very tightly with strong ropes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. And where he indeed saw a place that remained hidden, even with continuous rain for years, there he bound those (bows) very tightly with strong ropes.

sanskrit: शरीरं च मृतस्यैकं समबध्नन्त पाण्डवाः ।विवर्जयिष्यन्ति नरा दूरादेव शमीमिमाम् ।आबद्धं शवमत्रेति गन्धमाघ्राय पूतिकम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: śarīraṁ ca mṛtasyaikaṁ samabadhnanta pāṇḍavāḥ ,vivarjayiṣyanti narā dūrādeva śamīmimām ,ābaddhaṁ śavamatreti gandhamāghrāya pūtikam.
english: The Pāṇḍavas tied up the body of a dead one. People will avoid this śamī tree from a distance, smelling the foul odor and thinking, "A corpse is tied here."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 27. The Pāṇḍavas tied up the body of a dead one. People will avoid this śamī tree from a distance, smelling the foul odor and thinking, "A corpse is tied here."

sanskrit: अशीतिशतवर्षेयं माता न इति वादिनः ।कुलधर्मोऽयमस्माकं पूर्वैराचरितोऽपि च ।समासजाना वृक्षेऽस्मिन्निति वै व्याहरन्ति ते ॥२८॥
transliteration: aśītiśatavarṣeyaṁ mātā na iti vādinaḥ ,kuladharmo'yamasmākaṁ pūrvairācarito'pi ca ,samāsajānā vṛkṣe'sminniti vai vyāharanti te.
english: Saying, 'This mother is not one hundred and eighty years old.' They will also declare, 'This is our family custom (dharma), which was also practiced by our ancestors.' Thus indeed, they (the Pāṇḍavas) will speak to those gathered at this tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 28. Saying, 'This mother is not one hundred and eighty years old.' They will also declare, 'This is our family custom (dharma), which was also practiced by our ancestors.' Thus indeed, they (the Pāṇḍavas) will speak to those gathered at this tree.

sanskrit: आ गोपालाविपालेभ्य आचक्षाणाः परंतपाः ।आजग्मुर्नगराभ्याशं पार्थाः शत्रुनिबर्हणाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: ā gopālāvipālebhya ācakṣāṇāḥ paraṁtapāḥ ,ājagmurnagarābhyāśaṁ pārthāḥ śatrunibarhaṇāḥ.
english: The Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), who were tormentors of foes and destroyers of enemies, came near the city, informing even the cowherds and shepherds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 29. The Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), who were tormentors of foes and destroyers of enemies, came near the city, informing even the cowherds and shepherds.

sanskrit: जयो जयन्तो विजयो जयत्सेनो जयद्बलः ।इति गुह्यानि नामानि चक्रे तेषां युधिष्ठिरः ॥३०॥
transliteration: jayo jayanto vijayo jayatseno jayadbalaḥ ,iti guhyāni nāmāni cakre teṣāṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira created these secret names for them: Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 30. Yudhiṣṭhira created these secret names for them: Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala.

sanskrit: ततो यथाप्रतिज्ञाभिः प्राविशन्नगरं महत् ।अज्ञातचर्यां वत्स्यन्तो राष्ट्रे वर्षं त्रयोदशम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tato yathāpratijñābhiḥ prāviśannagaraṁ mahat ,ajñātacaryāṁ vatsyanto rāṣṭre varṣaṁ trayodaśam.
english: Then, in accordance with their vows, they entered the great city, intending to live for the thirteenth year in that kingdom during their incognito (ajñātacaryā) period.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 5 Verse 31. Then, in accordance with their vows, they entered the great city, intending to live for the thirteenth year in that kingdom during their incognito (ajñātacaryā) period.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो विराटं प्रथमं युधिष्ठिरो राजा सभायामुपविष्टमाव्रजत् ।वैडूर्यरूपान्प्रतिमुच्य काञ्चनानक्षान्स कक्षे परिगृह्य वाससा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato virāṭaṁ prathamaṁ yudhiṣṭhiro; rājā sabhāyāmupaviṣṭamāvrajat ,vaiḍūryarūpānpratimucya kāñcanā;nakṣānsa kakṣe parigṛhya vāsasā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira first approached Virāṭa, who was seated in the assembly hall. He (Yudhiṣṭhira), having taken up golden dice that resembled lapis lazuli, held them in his armpit with a cloth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira first approached Virāṭa, who was seated in the assembly hall. He (Yudhiṣṭhira), having taken up golden dice that resembled lapis lazuli, held them in his armpit with a cloth.

sanskrit: नराधिपो राष्ट्रपतिं यशस्विनं महायशाः कौरववंशवर्धनः ।महानुभावो नरराजसत्कृतो दुरासदस्तीक्ष्णविषो यथोरगः ॥२॥
transliteration: narādhipo rāṣṭrapatiṁ yaśasvinaṁ; mahāyaśāḥ kauravavaṁśavardhanaḥ ,mahānubhāvo nararājasatkṛto; durāsadastīkṣṇaviṣo yathoragaḥ.
english: The king (Yudhiṣṭhira), greatly renowned and the enhancer of the Kuru dynasty, a highly esteemed person honored by kings, approached the famous lord of the kingdom (Virāṭa). He (Yudhiṣṭhira) was formidable, like a serpent (uraga) with potent venom.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. The king (Yudhiṣṭhira), greatly renowned and the enhancer of the Kuru dynasty, a highly esteemed person honored by kings, approached the famous lord of the kingdom (Virāṭa). He (Yudhiṣṭhira) was formidable, like a serpent (uraga) with potent venom.

sanskrit: बलेन रूपेण नरर्षभो महानथार्चिरूपेण यथामरस्तथा ।महाभ्रजालैरिव संवृतो रविर्यथानलो भस्मवृतश्च वीर्यवान् ॥३॥
transliteration: balena rūpeṇa nararṣabho mahā;nathārcirūpeṇa yathāmarastathā ,mahābhrajālairiva saṁvṛto ravi;ryathānalo bhasmavṛtaśca vīryavān.
english: That great chief among men (Yudhiṣṭhira), by his strength and his form, and moreover by his radiant appearance, was like a god. He was like the sun (ravi) enveloped by masses of great clouds, and, though covered by ash, like a fire (anala) that is still powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. That great chief among men (Yudhiṣṭhira), by his strength and his form, and moreover by his radiant appearance, was like a god. He was like the sun (ravi) enveloped by masses of great clouds, and, though covered by ash, like a fire (anala) that is still powerful.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं प्रसमीक्ष्य पाण्डवं विराटराडिन्दुमिवाभ्रसंवृतम् ।मन्त्रिद्विजान्सूतमुखान्विशस्तथा ये चापि केचित्परिषत्समासते ।पप्रच्छ कोऽयं प्रथमं समेयिवाननेन योऽयं प्रसमीक्षते सभाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ prasamīkṣya pāṇḍavaṁ; virāṭarāḍindumivābhrasaṁvṛtam ,mantridvijānsūtamukhānviśastathā; ye cāpi kecitpariṣatsamāsate ,papraccha ko'yaṁ prathamaṁ sameyivā;nanena yo'yaṁ prasamīkṣate sabhām.
english: Upon seeing that Pāṇḍava approaching, resembling the moon covered by a cloud, King Virāṭa asked his ministers, the twice-born (dvija), the chief charioteers, and whoever else was gathered in the assembly: "Who is this person who has just arrived and surveys the court with such an expression?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. Upon seeing that Pāṇḍava approaching, resembling the moon covered by a cloud, King Virāṭa asked his ministers, the twice-born (dvija), the chief charioteers, and whoever else was gathered in the assembly: "Who is this person who has just arrived and surveys the court with such an expression?"

sanskrit: न तु द्विजोऽयं भविता नरोत्तमः पतिः पृथिव्या इति मे मनोगतम् ।न चास्य दासो न रथो न कुण्डले समीपतो भ्राजति चायमिन्द्रवत् ॥५॥
transliteration: na tu dvijo'yaṁ bhavitā narottamaḥ; patiḥ pṛthivyā iti me manogatam ,na cāsya dāso na ratho na kuṇḍale; samīpato bhrājati cāyamindravat.
english: It is my firm conviction that this man is neither a twice-born (dvija), nor the best of men, nor a lord of the earth. He has no servant, no chariot, and no earrings near him, yet he shines like Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. It is my firm conviction that this man is neither a twice-born (dvija), nor the best of men, nor a lord of the earth. He has no servant, no chariot, and no earrings near him, yet he shines like Indra.

sanskrit: शरीरलिङ्गैरुपसूचितो ह्ययं मूर्धाभिषिक्तोऽयमितीव मानसम् ।समीपमायाति च मे गतव्यथो यथा गजस्तामरसीं मदोत्कटः ॥६॥
transliteration: śarīraliṅgairupasūcito hyayaṁ; mūrdhābhiṣikto'yamitīva mānasam ,samīpamāyāti ca me gatavyatho; yathā gajastāmarasīṁ madotkaṭaḥ.
english: Indeed, the physical characteristics indicate that he is an anointed king (mūrdhābhiṣikta) - such is the thought (mānasam) in my mind. He approaches me, free from any anxiety, just as a powerful, rutting elephant approaches a lotus pond.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. Indeed, the physical characteristics indicate that he is an anointed king (mūrdhābhiṣikta) - such is the thought (mānasam) in my mind. He approaches me, free from any anxiety, just as a powerful, rutting elephant approaches a lotus pond.

sanskrit: वितर्कयन्तं तु नरर्षभस्तदा युधिष्ठिरोऽभ्येत्य विराटमब्रवीत् ।सम्राड्विजानात्विह जीवितार्थिनं विनष्टसर्वस्वमुपागतं द्विजम् ॥७॥
transliteration: vitarkayantaṁ tu nararṣabhastadā; yudhiṣṭhiro'bhyetya virāṭamabravīt ,samrāḍvijānātviha jīvitārthinaṁ; vinaṣṭasarvasvamupāgataṁ dvijam.
english: While he (Virāṭa) was thus pondering, Yudhiṣṭhira, that best of men, approached Virāṭa then and said, "May the Emperor understand that a twice-born (dvija) person has arrived here, seeking a livelihood, having lost all his possessions."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. While he (Virāṭa) was thus pondering, Yudhiṣṭhira, that best of men, approached Virāṭa then and said, "May the Emperor understand that a twice-born (dvija) person has arrived here, seeking a livelihood, having lost all his possessions."

sanskrit: इहाहमिच्छामि तवानघान्तिके वस्तुं यथा कामचरस्तथा विभो ।तमब्रवीत्स्वागतमित्यनन्तरं राजा प्रहृष्टः प्रतिसंगृहाण च ॥८॥
transliteration: ihāhamicchāmi tavānaghāntike; vastuṁ yathā kāmacarastathā vibho ,tamabravītsvāgatamityanantaraṁ; rājā prahṛṣṭaḥ pratisaṁgṛhāṇa ca.
english: O sinless one (anagha), O lord (vibho), I wish to reside here near you as one who is free to move about as he pleases. Immediately after this, the king, delighted, said to him, 'Welcome!' and 'Please accept [my hospitality].'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. O sinless one (anagha), O lord (vibho), I wish to reside here near you as one who is free to move about as he pleases. Immediately after this, the king, delighted, said to him, 'Welcome!' and 'Please accept [my hospitality].'

sanskrit: कामेन ताताभिवदाम्यहं त्वां कस्यासि राज्ञो विषयादिहागतः ।गोत्रं च नामापि च शंस तत्त्वतः किं चापि शिल्पं तव विद्यते कृतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: kāmena tātābhivadāmyahaṁ tvāṁ; kasyāsi rājño viṣayādihāgataḥ ,gotraṁ ca nāmāpi ca śaṁsa tattvataḥ; kiṁ cāpi śilpaṁ tava vidyate kṛtam.
english: My dear (tāta), I speak to you with pleasure. From which king's country have you arrived here? Please tell me your lineage and name truthfully, and what skill or art (śilpa) have you mastered?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. My dear (tāta), I speak to you with pleasure. From which king's country have you arrived here? Please tell me your lineage and name truthfully, and what skill or art (śilpa) have you mastered?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।युधिष्ठिरस्यासमहं पुरा सखा वैयाघ्रपद्यः पुनरस्मि ब्राह्मणः ।अक्षान्प्रवप्तुं कुशलोऽस्मि देविता कङ्केति नाम्नास्मि विराट विश्रुतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhirasyāsamahaṁ purā sakhā; vaiyāghrapadyaḥ punarasmi brāhmaṇaḥ ,akṣānpravaptuṁ kuśalo'smi devitā; kaṅketi nāmnāsmi virāṭa viśrutaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'Formerly, I was a friend of Yudhiṣṭhira, the Vaiyāghrapadya. Furthermore, I am a Brahmin (brāhmaṇa). I am skilled in throwing dice, a dice player. O Virāṭa, I am renowned by the name Kaṅka.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'Formerly, I was a friend of Yudhiṣṭhira, the Vaiyāghrapadya. Furthermore, I am a Brahmin (brāhmaṇa). I am skilled in throwing dice, a dice player. O Virāṭa, I am renowned by the name Kaṅka.'

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।ददामि ते हन्त वरं यमिच्छसि प्रशाधि मत्स्यान्वशगो ह्यहं तव ।प्रिया हि धूर्ता मम देविनः सदा भवांश्च देवोपम राज्यमर्हति ॥११॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,dadāmi te hanta varaṁ yamicchasi; praśādhi matsyānvaśago hyahaṁ tava ,priyā hi dhūrtā mama devinaḥ sadā; bhavāṁśca devopama rājyamarhati.
english: Virāṭa said: 'Indeed, I grant you whatever boon you desire. Rule over the Matsyas, for I am certainly subject to you. Indeed, I always hold dear those clever dice players. And you, O god-like one, are worthy of a kingdom.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. Virāṭa said: 'Indeed, I grant you whatever boon you desire. Rule over the Matsyas, for I am certainly subject to you. Indeed, I always hold dear those clever dice players. And you, O god-like one, are worthy of a kingdom.'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।आप्तो विवादः परमो विशां पते न विद्यते किंचन मत्स्य हीनतः ।न मे जितः कश्चन धारयेद्धनं वरो ममैषोऽस्तु तव प्रसादतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,āpto vivādaḥ paramo viśāṁ pate; na vidyate kiṁcana matsya hīnataḥ ,na me jitaḥ kaścana dhārayeddhanaṁ; varo mamaiṣo'stu tava prasādataḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: O Lord of the people, a great dispute has arisen. There is nothing that exists without the Matsya (king's) authority. Let this be my boon, granted by your grace: no one whom I defeat (in gambling) shall retain their wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. Yudhishthira said: O Lord of the people, a great dispute has arisen. There is nothing that exists without the Matsya (king's) authority. Let this be my boon, granted by your grace: no one whom I defeat (in gambling) shall retain their wealth.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।हन्यामवध्यं यदि तेऽप्रियं चरेत्प्रव्राजयेयं विषयाद्द्विजांस्तथा ।शृण्वन्तु मे जानपदाः समागताः कङ्को यथाहं विषये प्रभुस्तथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,hanyāmavadhyaṁ yadi te'priyaṁ care;tpravrājayeyaṁ viṣayāddvijāṁstathā ,śṛṇvantu me jānapadāḥ samāgatāḥ; kaṅko yathāhaṁ viṣaye prabhustathā.
english: Virata said: If anyone were to do something displeasing to you, I would kill even one who should not be killed, and I would banish the twice-born (dvija) from my kingdom. Let all the assembled citizens listen to me: just as I am the lord in this kingdom, so too is Kanka.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. Virata said: If anyone were to do something displeasing to you, I would kill even one who should not be killed, and I would banish the twice-born (dvija) from my kingdom. Let all the assembled citizens listen to me: just as I am the lord in this kingdom, so too is Kanka.

sanskrit: समानयानो भवितासि मे सखा प्रभूतवस्त्रो बहुपानभोजनः ।पश्येस्त्वमन्तश्च बहिश्च सर्वदा कृतं च ते द्वारमपावृतं मया ॥१४॥
transliteration: samānayāno bhavitāsi me sakhā; prabhūtavastro bahupānabhojanaḥ ,paśyestvamantaśca bahiśca sarvadā; kṛtaṁ ca te dvāramapāvṛtaṁ mayā.
english: You shall be my companion, riding in the same vehicle, richly clothed and with abundant drink and food. You shall always observe both what is inside and what is outside, and by me, your door has been made open.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. You shall be my companion, riding in the same vehicle, richly clothed and with abundant drink and food. You shall always observe both what is inside and what is outside, and by me, your door has been made open.

sanskrit: ये त्वानुवादेयुरवृत्तिकर्शिता ब्रूयाश्च तेषां वचनेन मे सदा ।दास्यामि सर्वं तदहं न संशयो न ते भयं विद्यति संनिधौ मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: ye tvānuvādeyuravṛttikarśitā; brūyāśca teṣāṁ vacanena me sadā ,dāsyāmi sarvaṁ tadahaṁ na saṁśayo; na te bhayaṁ vidyati saṁnidhau mama.
english: If anyone, afflicted by lack of livelihood, speaks against you, you should always speak to them on my behalf. I shall give you everything; there is no doubt. There will be no fear for you in my presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. If anyone, afflicted by lack of livelihood, speaks against you, you should always speak to them on my behalf. I shall give you everything; there is no doubt. There will be no fear for you in my presence.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं स लब्ध्वा तु वरं समागमं विराटराजेन नरर्षभस्तदा ।उवास वीरः परमार्चितः सुखी न चापि कश्चिच्चरितं बुबोध तत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ sa labdhvā tu varaṁ samāgamaṁ; virāṭarājena nararṣabhastadā ,uvāsa vīraḥ paramārcitaḥ sukhī; na cāpi kaściccaritaṁ bubodha tat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus obtained the boon of an alliance with King Virāṭa, that best among men, the hero [Yudhiṣṭhira] lived there, highly honored and happy. And yet, no one at all recognized his true identity.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. Vaiśampāyana said: Having thus obtained the boon of an alliance with King Virāṭa, that best among men, the hero [Yudhiṣṭhira] lived there, highly honored and happy. And yet, no one at all recognized his true identity.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथापरो भीमबलः श्रिया ज्वलन्नुपाययौ सिंहविलासविक्रमः ।खजं च दर्वीं च करेण धारयन्नसिं च कालाङ्गमकोशमव्रणम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,athāparo bhīmabalaḥ śriyā jvala;nnupāyayau siṁhavilāsavikramaḥ ,khajaṁ ca darvīṁ ca kareṇa dhāraya;nnasiṁ ca kālāṅgamakośamavraṇam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then arrived another [Pāṇḍava], Bhīma of mighty strength, radiant with splendor and possessing the majestic gait of a lion. He was holding a churning stick and a ladle in his hand, along with a black-hilted, sheathless, and flawless sword.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then arrived another [Pāṇḍava], Bhīma of mighty strength, radiant with splendor and possessing the majestic gait of a lion. He was holding a churning stick and a ladle in his hand, along with a black-hilted, sheathless, and flawless sword.

sanskrit: स सूदरूपः परमेण वर्चसा रविर्यथा लोकमिमं प्रभासयन् ।सुकृष्णवासा गिरिराजसारवान्स मत्स्यराजं समुपेत्य तस्थिवान् ॥२॥
transliteration: sa sūdarūpaḥ parameṇa varcasā; raviryathā lokamimaṁ prabhāsayan ,sukṛṣṇavāsā girirājasāravā;nsa matsyarājaṁ samupetya tasthivān.
english: He, in the guise of a cook, with supreme splendor, shone upon this world like the sun. Wearing very dark garments and possessing the firm majesty of a king of mountains, he then stood before King Matsya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. He, in the guise of a cook, with supreme splendor, shone upon this world like the sun. Wearing very dark garments and possessing the firm majesty of a king of mountains, he then stood before King Matsya.

sanskrit: तं प्रेक्ष्य राजा वरयन्नुपागतं ततोऽब्रवीज्जानपदान्समागतान् ।सिंहोन्नतांसोऽयमतीव रूपवान्प्रदृश्यते को नु नरर्षभो युवा ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ prekṣya rājā varayannupāgataṁ; tato'bravījjānapadānsamāgatān ,siṁhonnatāṁso'yamatīva rūpavā;npradṛśyate ko nu nararṣabho yuvā.
english: Seeing him approaching, the king then spoke to the assembled citizens, saying, "This young man, whose shoulders are lofty like a lion's, appears exceedingly handsome. Who, indeed, is this best among men?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. Seeing him approaching, the king then spoke to the assembled citizens, saying, "This young man, whose shoulders are lofty like a lion's, appears exceedingly handsome. Who, indeed, is this best among men?"

sanskrit: अदृष्टपूर्वः पुरुषो रविर्यथा वितर्कयन्नास्य लभामि संपदम् ।तथास्य चित्तं ह्यपि संवितर्कयन्नरर्षभस्याद्य न यामि तत्त्वतः ॥४॥
transliteration: adṛṣṭapūrvaḥ puruṣo raviryathā; vitarkayannāsya labhāmi saṁpadam ,tathāsya cittaṁ hyapi saṁvitarkaya;nnararṣabhasyādya na yāmi tattvataḥ.
english: I am unable to comprehend the splendor of this man (puruṣa), who is like the sun and has never been seen before, even though I ponder it. Similarly, even today, though I deeply consider the mind of this bull among men (nararṣabha), I do not truly grasp its essence (tattva).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. I am unable to comprehend the splendor of this man (puruṣa), who is like the sun and has never been seen before, even though I ponder it. Similarly, even today, though I deeply consider the mind of this bull among men (nararṣabha), I do not truly grasp its essence (tattva).

sanskrit: ततो विराटं समुपेत्य पाण्डवः सुदीनरूपो वचनं महामनाः ।उवाच सूदोऽस्मि नरेन्द्र बल्लवो भजस्व मां व्यञ्जनकारमुत्तमम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tato virāṭaṁ samupetya pāṇḍavaḥ; sudīnarūpo vacanaṁ mahāmanāḥ ,uvāca sūdo'smi narendra ballavo; bhajasva māṁ vyañjanakāramuttamam.
english: Then, the magnanimous Pāṇḍava, appearing very humble, approached Virāṭa and spoke these words: 'O king, I am a cook named Vallava. Please employ me as an excellent preparer of dishes.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. Then, the magnanimous Pāṇḍava, appearing very humble, approached Virāṭa and spoke these words: 'O king, I am a cook named Vallava. Please employ me as an excellent preparer of dishes.'

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।न सूदतां मानद श्रद्दधामि ते सहस्रनेत्रप्रतिमो हि दृश्यसे ।श्रिया च रूपेण च विक्रमेण च प्रभासि तातानवरो नरेष्विह ॥६॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,na sūdatāṁ mānada śraddadhāmi te; sahasranetrapratimo hi dṛśyase ,śriyā ca rūpeṇa ca vikrameṇa ca; prabhāsi tātānavaro nareṣviha.
english: Virāṭa said: 'O giver of honor, I do not believe your claim of being a cook. Indeed, you appear like Indra (sahasranetratpratima). By your splendor, your form, and your valor, you shine, dear one, as second to none among men here.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. Virāṭa said: 'O giver of honor, I do not believe your claim of being a cook. Indeed, you appear like Indra (sahasranetratpratima). By your splendor, your form, and your valor, you shine, dear one, as second to none among men here.'

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।नरेन्द्र सूदः परिचारकोऽस्मि ते जानामि सूपान्प्रथमेन केवलान् ।आस्वादिता ये नृपते पुराभवन्युधिष्ठिरेणापि नृपेण सर्वशः ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,narendra sūdaḥ paricārako'smi te; jānāmi sūpānprathamena kevalān ,āsvāditā ye nṛpate purābhava;nyudhiṣṭhireṇāpi nṛpeṇa sarvaśaḥ.
english: Bhīma said: 'O king, I am your cook and servant. I know only how to prepare excellent soups, those, O king, that were formerly tasted thoroughly by King Yudhiṣṭhira himself.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. Bhīma said: 'O king, I am your cook and servant. I know only how to prepare excellent soups, those, O king, that were formerly tasted thoroughly by King Yudhiṣṭhira himself.'

sanskrit: बलेन तुल्यश्च न विद्यते मया नियुद्धशीलश्च सदैव पार्थिव ।गजैश्च सिंहैश्च समेयिवानहं सदा करिष्यामि तवानघ प्रियम् ॥८॥
transliteration: balena tulyaśca na vidyate mayā; niyuddhaśīlaśca sadaiva pārthiva ,gajaiśca siṁhaiśca sameyivānahaṁ; sadā kariṣyāmi tavānagha priyam.
english: O king, no one equal to me in strength exists, and I am always skilled in wrestling. I have contended with elephants and lions. O sinless one, I will always do what is pleasing to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. O king, no one equal to me in strength exists, and I am always skilled in wrestling. I have contended with elephants and lions. O sinless one, I will always do what is pleasing to you.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।ददामि ते हन्त वरं महानसे तथा च कुर्याः कुशलं हि भाषसे ।न चैव मन्ये तव कर्म तत्समं समुद्रनेमिं पृथिवीं त्वमर्हसि ॥९॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,dadāmi te hanta varaṁ mahānase; tathā ca kuryāḥ kuśalaṁ hi bhāṣase ,na caiva manye tava karma tatsamaṁ; samudranemiṁ pṛthivīṁ tvamarhasi.
english: Virāṭa said: 'Indeed, I grant you a boon concerning the kitchen, and you should perform skillfully, for you speak competently. However, I do not consider your caliber of work suitable for such a task (as kitchen duties); you are worthy of ruling the entire earth, which is bounded by the ocean.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. Virāṭa said: 'Indeed, I grant you a boon concerning the kitchen, and you should perform skillfully, for you speak competently. However, I do not consider your caliber of work suitable for such a task (as kitchen duties); you are worthy of ruling the entire earth, which is bounded by the ocean.'

sanskrit: यथा हि कामस्तव तत्तथा कृतं महानसे त्वं भव मे पुरस्कृतः ।नराश्च ये तत्र ममोचिताः पुरा भवस्व तेषामधिपो मया कृतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yathā hi kāmastava tattathā kṛtaṁ; mahānase tvaṁ bhava me puraskṛtaḥ ,narāśca ye tatra mamocitāḥ purā; bhavasva teṣāmadhipo mayā kṛtaḥ.
english: As is your desire, so it shall be done. You shall be appointed by me in the kitchen. And you shall be made the chief by me over those men who were formerly accustomed to me there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. As is your desire, so it shall be done. You shall be appointed by me in the kitchen. And you shall be made the chief by me over those men who were formerly accustomed to me there.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा स भीमो विहितो महानसे विराटराज्ञो दयितोऽभवद्दृढम् ।उवास राजन्न च तं पृथग्जनो बुबोध तत्रानुचरश्च कश्चन ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā sa bhīmo vihito mahānase; virāṭarājño dayito'bhavaddṛḍham ,uvāsa rājanna ca taṁ pṛthagjano; bubodha tatrānucaraśca kaścana.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Thus, Bhīma was appointed in the kitchen and became very dear to King Virāṭa. O king, he lived there, and no common person, nor any attendant there, recognized him.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Thus, Bhīma was appointed in the kitchen and became very dear to King Virāṭa. O king, he lived there, and no common person, nor any attendant there, recognized him.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः केशान्समुत्क्षिप्य वेल्लिताग्राननिन्दितान् ।जुगूह दक्षिणे पार्श्वे मृदूनसितलोचना ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ keśānsamutkṣipya vellitāgrānaninditān ,jugūha dakṣiṇe pārśve mṛdūnasitalocanā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the black-eyed one (asitalocanā), having gathered up her soft, faultless hair with curled ends, hid it on her right side.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the black-eyed one (asitalocanā), having gathered up her soft, faultless hair with curled ends, hid it on her right side.

sanskrit: वासश्च परिधायैकं कृष्णं सुमलिनं महत् ।कृत्वा वेषं च सैरन्ध्र्याः कृष्णा व्यचरदार्तवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vāsaśca paridhāyaikaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ sumalinaṁ mahat ,kṛtvā veṣaṁ ca sairandhryāḥ kṛṣṇā vyacaradārtavat.
english: And having put on a single large, black, very dirty garment, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi), having assumed the disguise of a hair-dresser (sairandhrī), wandered about like one distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. And having put on a single large, black, very dirty garment, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadi), having assumed the disguise of a hair-dresser (sairandhrī), wandered about like one distressed.

sanskrit: तां नराः परिधावन्तीं स्त्रियश्च समुपाद्रवन् ।अपृच्छंश्चैव तां दृष्ट्वा का त्वं किं च चिकीर्षसि ॥३॥
transliteration: tāṁ narāḥ paridhāvantīṁ striyaśca samupādravan ,apṛcchaṁścaiva tāṁ dṛṣṭvā kā tvaṁ kiṁ ca cikīrṣasi.
english: Men running about and women rushed up to her. Having seen her, they asked her, "Who are you, and what do you wish to do?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. Men running about and women rushed up to her. Having seen her, they asked her, "Who are you, and what do you wish to do?"

sanskrit: सा तानुवाच राजेन्द्र सैरन्ध्र्यहमुपागता ।कर्म चेच्छामि वै कर्तुं तस्य यो मां पुपुक्षति ॥४॥
transliteration: sā tānuvāca rājendra sairandhryahamupāgatā ,karma cecchāmi vai kartuṁ tasya yo māṁ pupukṣati.
english: "O King," she said to them, "I have come as a hair-dresser (sairandhrī). Indeed, I wish to do work for him who desires to support me."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. "O King," she said to them, "I have come as a hair-dresser (sairandhrī). Indeed, I wish to do work for him who desires to support me."

sanskrit: तस्या रूपेण वेषेण श्लक्ष्णया च तथा गिरा ।नाश्रद्दधत तां दासीमन्नहेतोरुपस्थिताम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tasyā rūpeṇa veṣeṇa ślakṣṇayā ca tathā girā ,nāśraddadhata tāṁ dāsīmannahetorupasthitām.
english: Because of her appearance, her attire, and her soft speech, they did not doubt that maidservant who had arrived seeking food.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. Because of her appearance, her attire, and her soft speech, they did not doubt that maidservant who had arrived seeking food.

sanskrit: विराटस्य तु कैकेयी भार्या परमसंमता ।अवलोकयन्ती ददृशे प्रासादाद्द्रुपदात्मजाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: virāṭasya tu kaikeyī bhāryā paramasaṁmatā ,avalokayantī dadṛśe prāsādāddrupadātmajām.
english: Indeed, King Virata's highly esteemed wife, the Kaikeyī (Queen Sudeṣṇā), observing from the palace, saw Drupada's daughter (Draupadi).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. Indeed, King Virata's highly esteemed wife, the Kaikeyī (Queen Sudeṣṇā), observing from the palace, saw Drupada's daughter (Draupadi).

sanskrit: सा समीक्ष्य तथारूपामनाथामेकवाससम् ।समाहूयाब्रवीद्भद्रे का त्वं किं च चिकीर्षसि ॥७॥
transliteration: sā samīkṣya tathārūpāmanāthāmekavāsasam ,samāhūyābravīdbhadre kā tvaṁ kiṁ ca cikīrṣasi.
english: Having observed her, who was of such an appearance, helpless, and wearing a single garment, she (the queen) called out and said, 'O gentle lady, who are you and what do you intend to do?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. Having observed her, who was of such an appearance, helpless, and wearing a single garment, she (the queen) called out and said, 'O gentle lady, who are you and what do you intend to do?'

sanskrit: सा तामुवाच राजेन्द्र सैरन्ध्र्यहमुपागता ।कर्म चेच्छाम्यहं कर्तुं तस्य यो मां पुपुक्षति ॥८॥
transliteration: sā tāmuvāca rājendra sairandhryahamupāgatā ,karma cecchāmyahaṁ kartuṁ tasya yo māṁ pupukṣati.
english: She (Draupadi) said to her (the queen), 'O royal lady, I am a sairandhrī, and I have arrived. I wish to perform service (karma) for whoever wishes to nourish me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. She (Draupadi) said to her (the queen), 'O royal lady, I am a sairandhrī, and I have arrived. I wish to perform service (karma) for whoever wishes to nourish me.'

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।नैवंरूपा भवन्त्येवं यथा वदसि भामिनि ।प्रेषयन्ति च वै दासीर्दासांश्चैवंविधान्बहून् ॥९॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,naivaṁrūpā bhavantyevaṁ yathā vadasi bhāmini ,preṣayanti ca vai dāsīrdāsāṁścaivaṁvidhānbahūn.
english: Sudeshna said: "Beautiful lady, those of such noble birth are not like you say they are. Indeed, they send many such maids and servants."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. Sudeshna said: "Beautiful lady, those of such noble birth are not like you say they are. Indeed, they send many such maids and servants."

sanskrit: गूढगुल्फा संहतोरुस्त्रिगम्भीरा षडुन्नता ।रक्ता पञ्चसु रक्तेषु हंसगद्गदभाषिणी ॥१०॥
transliteration: gūḍhagulphā saṁhatorustrigambhīrā ṣaḍunnatā ,raktā pañcasu rakteṣu haṁsagadgadabhāṣiṇī.
english: She has concealed ankles, compact thighs, three deep features (such as navel, voice, and intellect), six prominent features (such as breasts, shoulders, and nails). Her five extremities (palms, soles, corners of eyes, tongue, and nails) are reddish, and she speaks with a warbling voice like a swan's.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. She has concealed ankles, compact thighs, three deep features (such as navel, voice, and intellect), six prominent features (such as breasts, shoulders, and nails). Her five extremities (palms, soles, corners of eyes, tongue, and nails) are reddish, and she speaks with a warbling voice like a swan's.

sanskrit: सुकेशी सुस्तनी श्यामा पीनश्रोणिपयोधरा ।तेन तेनैव संपन्ना काश्मीरीव तुरंगमा ॥११॥
transliteration: sukeśī sustanī śyāmā pīnaśroṇipayodharā ,tena tenaiva saṁpannā kāśmīrīva turaṁgamā.
english: She has beautiful hair, lovely breasts, a dark complexion, and full hips and breasts. She is endowed with all these qualities, just like a Kashmiri mare.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. She has beautiful hair, lovely breasts, a dark complexion, and full hips and breasts. She is endowed with all these qualities, just like a Kashmiri mare.

sanskrit: स्वरालपक्ष्मनयना बिम्बोष्ठी तनुमध्यमा ।कम्बुग्रीवा गूढसिरा पूर्णचन्द्रनिभानना ॥१२॥
transliteration: svarālapakṣmanayanā bimboṣṭhī tanumadhyamā ,kambugrīvā gūḍhasirā pūrṇacandranibhānanā.
english: She has beautiful eyes with fine, dense eyelashes, lips red like the bimba fruit, a slender waist, a neck like a conch, hidden veins, and a face resembling the full moon.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. She has beautiful eyes with fine, dense eyelashes, lips red like the bimba fruit, a slender waist, a neck like a conch, hidden veins, and a face resembling the full moon.

sanskrit: का त्वं ब्रूहि यथा भद्रे नासि दासी कथंचन ।यक्षी वा यदि वा देवी गन्धर्वी यदि वाप्सराः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kā tvaṁ brūhi yathā bhadre nāsi dāsī kathaṁcana ,yakṣī vā yadi vā devī gandharvī yadi vāpsarāḥ.
english: O gentle one, please tell me who you are, for you are certainly not a maidservant. Are you a Yakṣī, a goddess, a Gandharvī, or an Apsarā?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. O gentle one, please tell me who you are, for you are certainly not a maidservant. Are you a Yakṣī, a goddess, a Gandharvī, or an Apsarā?

sanskrit: अलम्बुसा मिश्रकेशी पुण्डरीकाथ मालिनी ।इन्द्राणी वारुणी वा त्वं त्वष्टुर्धातुः प्रजापतेः ।देव्यो देवेषु विख्यातास्तासां त्वं कतमा शुभे ॥१४॥
transliteration: alambusā miśrakeśī puṇḍarīkātha mālinī ,indrāṇī vāruṇī vā tvaṁ tvaṣṭurdhātuḥ prajāpateḥ ,devyo deveṣu vikhyātāstāsāṁ tvaṁ katamā śubhe.
english: Are you Alambuṣā, Miśrakeśī, Puṇḍarīkā, or Mālinī? Are you Indrāṇī or Varuṇī? Or are you a daughter of Tvaṣṭā, Dhātā, or Prajāpati? Among these goddesses, who are renowned among the gods, which one are you, O auspicious one?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. Are you Alambuṣā, Miśrakeśī, Puṇḍarīkā, or Mālinī? Are you Indrāṇī or Varuṇī? Or are you a daughter of Tvaṣṭā, Dhātā, or Prajāpati? Among these goddesses, who are renowned among the gods, which one are you, O auspicious one?

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।नास्मि देवी न गन्धर्वी नासुरी न च राक्षसी ।सैरन्ध्री तु भुजिष्यास्मि सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥१५॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,nāsmi devī na gandharvī nāsurī na ca rākṣasī ,sairandhrī tu bhujiṣyāsmi satyametadbravīmi te.
english: Draupadī replied: 'I am not a goddess, nor a Gandharvī, nor an Asurī, nor a Rākṣasī. Indeed, I am a Sairandhrī, a dependent attendant. I tell you this truth.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. Draupadī replied: 'I am not a goddess, nor a Gandharvī, nor an Asurī, nor a Rākṣasī. Indeed, I am a Sairandhrī, a dependent attendant. I tell you this truth.'

sanskrit: केशाञ्जानाम्यहं कर्तुं पिंषे साधु विलेपनम् ।ग्रथयिष्ये विचित्राश्च स्रजः परमशोभनाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: keśāñjānāmyahaṁ kartuṁ piṁṣe sādhu vilepanam ,grathayiṣye vicitrāśca srajaḥ paramaśobhanāḥ.
english: I know how to arrange hair. I skillfully prepare unguents. And I will string diverse, exceedingly beautiful garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. I know how to arrange hair. I skillfully prepare unguents. And I will string diverse, exceedingly beautiful garlands.

sanskrit: आराधयं सत्यभामां कृष्णस्य महिषीं प्रियाम् ।कृष्णां च भार्यां पाण्डूनां कुरूणामेकसुन्दरीम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ārādhayaṁ satyabhāmāṁ kṛṣṇasya mahiṣīṁ priyām ,kṛṣṇāṁ ca bhāryāṁ pāṇḍūnāṁ kurūṇāmekasundarīm.
english: I attended to Satyabhāmā, Krishna's beloved queen, and Draupadī, the wife of the Pāṇḍavas, who was the most beautiful woman among the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. I attended to Satyabhāmā, Krishna's beloved queen, and Draupadī, the wife of the Pāṇḍavas, who was the most beautiful woman among the Kurus.

sanskrit: तत्र तत्र चराम्येवं लभमाना सुशोभनम् ।वासांसि यावच्च लभे तावत्तावद्रमे तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatra tatra carāmyevaṁ labhamānā suśobhanam ,vāsāṁsi yāvacca labhe tāvattāvadrame tathā.
english: I roam about in various places, acquiring beautiful things. As long as I obtain clothes, I enjoy myself accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. I roam about in various places, acquiring beautiful things. As long as I obtain clothes, I enjoy myself accordingly.

sanskrit: मालिनीत्येव मे नाम स्वयं देवी चकार सा ।साहमभ्यागता देवि सुदेष्णे त्वन्निवेशनम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: mālinītyeva me nāma svayaṁ devī cakāra sā ,sāhamabhyāgatā devi sudeṣṇe tvanniveśanam.
english: "Mālinī" - this is indeed the name the queen herself gave me. O Queen Sudeshṇā, that very person, I have come to your abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. "Mālinī" - this is indeed the name the queen herself gave me. O Queen Sudeshṇā, that very person, I have come to your abode.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।मूर्ध्नि त्वां वासयेयं वै संशयो मे न विद्यते ।नो चेदिह तु राजा त्वां गच्छेत्सर्वेण चेतसा ॥२०॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,mūrdhni tvāṁ vāsayeyaṁ vai saṁśayo me na vidyate ,no cediha tu rājā tvāṁ gacchetsarveṇa cetasā.
english: Sudeshṇā said: "Indeed, I would place you upon my head; I have no doubt about that. But if not, then the king here would certainly approach you with his entire mind."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. Sudeshṇā said: "Indeed, I would place you upon my head; I have no doubt about that. But if not, then the king here would certainly approach you with his entire mind."

sanskrit: स्त्रियो राजकुले पश्य याश्चेमा मम वेश्मनि ।प्रसक्तास्त्वां निरीक्षन्ते पुमांसं कं न मोहयेः ॥२१॥
transliteration: striyo rājakule paśya yāścemā mama veśmani ,prasaktāstvāṁ nirīkṣante pumāṁsaṁ kaṁ na mohayeḥ.
english: Observe the women in the royal palace, and also those in my own house; captivated, they gaze at you. What man would you not enchant?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. Observe the women in the royal palace, and also those in my own house; captivated, they gaze at you. What man would you not enchant?

sanskrit: वृक्षांश्चावस्थितान्पश्य य इमे मम वेश्मनि ।तेऽपि त्वां संनमन्तीव पुमांसं कं न मोहयेः ॥२२॥
transliteration: vṛkṣāṁścāvasthitānpaśya ya ime mama veśmani ,te'pi tvāṁ saṁnamantīva pumāṁsaṁ kaṁ na mohayeḥ.
english: Also observe the trees standing in my house; even they seem to bow down to you. What man would you not enchant?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. Also observe the trees standing in my house; even they seem to bow down to you. What man would you not enchant?

sanskrit: राजा विराटः सुश्रोणि दृष्ट्वा वपुरमानुषम् ।विहाय मां वरारोहे त्वां गच्छेत्सर्वचेतसा ॥२३॥
transliteration: rājā virāṭaḥ suśroṇi dṛṣṭvā vapuramānuṣam ,vihāya māṁ varārohe tvāṁ gacchetsarvacetasā.
english: O lovely-hipped one, King Virata, upon seeing your superhuman form, would abandon me, O excellent-hipped woman, and go to you with his whole heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. O lovely-hipped one, King Virata, upon seeing your superhuman form, would abandon me, O excellent-hipped woman, and go to you with his whole heart.

sanskrit: यं हि त्वमनवद्याङ्गि नरमायतलोचने ।प्रसक्तमभिवीक्षेथाः स कामवशगो भवेत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: yaṁ hi tvamanavadyāṅgi naramāyatalocane ,prasaktamabhivīkṣethāḥ sa kāmavaśago bhavet.
english: O flawless-limbed one, O long-eyed one, any man upon whom you would cast your gaze, he would certainly become captivated and subservient to desire (kāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. O flawless-limbed one, O long-eyed one, any man upon whom you would cast your gaze, he would certainly become captivated and subservient to desire (kāma).

sanskrit: यश्च त्वां सततं पश्येत्पुरुषश्चारुहासिनि ।एवं सर्वानवद्याङ्गि स चानङ्गवशो भवेत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: yaśca tvāṁ satataṁ paśyetpuruṣaścāruhāsini ,evaṁ sarvānavadyāṅgi sa cānaṅgavaśo bhavet.
english: O charmingly smiling woman, and O woman of entirely faultless limbs, whoever constantly looks at you will thus fall under the sway of Kāmadeva (the god of love).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. O charmingly smiling woman, and O woman of entirely faultless limbs, whoever constantly looks at you will thus fall under the sway of Kāmadeva (the god of love).

sanskrit: यथा कर्कटकी गर्भमाधत्ते मृत्युमात्मनः ।तथाविधमहं मन्ये वासं तव शुचिस्मिते ॥२६॥
transliteration: yathā karkaṭakī garbhamādhatte mṛtyumātmanaḥ ,tathāvidhamahaṁ manye vāsaṁ tava śucismite.
english: O pure-smiling woman, just as a female crab carries offspring that lead to its own death (ātman), so too do I consider your presence to be of a similar nature (i.e., bringing death).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. O pure-smiling woman, just as a female crab carries offspring that lead to its own death (ātman), so too do I consider your presence to be of a similar nature (i.e., bringing death).

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।नास्मि लभ्या विराटेन न चान्येन कथंचन ।गन्धर्वाः पतयो मह्यं युवानः पञ्च भामिनि ॥२७॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,nāsmi labhyā virāṭena na cānyena kathaṁcana ,gandharvāḥ patayo mahyaṁ yuvānaḥ pañca bhāmini.
english: Draupadi said: 'O passionate woman, I am not to be obtained by Virāṭa, nor by anyone else in any manner. Five young Gandharvas are my husbands.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. Draupadi said: 'O passionate woman, I am not to be obtained by Virāṭa, nor by anyone else in any manner. Five young Gandharvas are my husbands.'

sanskrit: पुत्रा गन्धर्वराजस्य महासत्त्वस्य कस्यचित् ।रक्षन्ति ते च मां नित्यं दुःखाचारा तथा न्वहम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: putrā gandharvarājasya mahāsattvasya kasyacit ,rakṣanti te ca māṁ nityaṁ duḥkhācārā tathā nvaham.
english: They are the sons of some great and mighty Gandharva king. And they constantly protect me, because I am of difficult (or strict) conduct (dharma), and so it is with me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. They are the sons of some great and mighty Gandharva king. And they constantly protect me, because I am of difficult (or strict) conduct (dharma), and so it is with me.

sanskrit: यो मे न दद्यादुच्छिष्टं न च पादौ प्रधावयेत् ।प्रीयेयुस्तेन वासेन गन्धर्वाः पतयो मम ॥२९॥
transliteration: yo me na dadyāducchiṣṭaṁ na ca pādau pradhāvayet ,prīyeyustena vāsena gandharvāḥ patayo mama.
english: If anyone does not offer me food remnants, nor wash my feet, my husbands, the Gandharvas, would be pleased by my residence (here).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. If anyone does not offer me food remnants, nor wash my feet, my husbands, the Gandharvas, would be pleased by my residence (here).

sanskrit: यो हि मां पुरुषो गृध्येद्यथान्याः प्राकृतस्त्रियः ।तामेव स ततो रात्रिं प्रविशेदपरां तनुम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: yo hi māṁ puruṣo gṛdhyedyathānyāḥ prākṛtastriyaḥ ,tāmeva sa tato rātriṁ praviśedaparāṁ tanum.
english: Indeed, if any man (puruṣa) desires me as he would other ordinary women, then he shall enter another body that very night (i.e., he will die).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. Indeed, if any man (puruṣa) desires me as he would other ordinary women, then he shall enter another body that very night (i.e., he will die).

sanskrit: न चाप्यहं चालयितुं शक्या केनचिदङ्गने ।दुःखशीला हि गन्धर्वास्ते च मे बलवत्तराः ॥३१॥
transliteration: na cāpyahaṁ cālayituṁ śakyā kenacidaṅgane ,duḥkhaśīlā hi gandharvāste ca me balavattarāḥ.
english: And I, O lady, cannot be swayed or disturbed by anyone. For my husbands, the Gandharvas, are indeed of a fierce disposition, and they are exceedingly powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. And I, O lady, cannot be swayed or disturbed by anyone. For my husbands, the Gandharvas, are indeed of a fierce disposition, and they are exceedingly powerful.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।एवं त्वां वासयिष्यामि यथा त्वं नन्दिनीच्छसि ।न च पादौ न चोच्छिष्टं स्प्रक्ष्यसि त्वं कथंचन ॥३२॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,evaṁ tvāṁ vāsayiṣyāmi yathā tvaṁ nandinīcchasi ,na ca pādau na cocchiṣṭaṁ sprakṣyasi tvaṁ kathaṁcana.
english: Sudeshna said, 'I shall arrange for your stay here just as you, O Nandini, wish. And you shall certainly not touch anyone's feet or food remnants at all.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. Sudeshna said, 'I shall arrange for your stay here just as you, O Nandini, wish. And you shall certainly not touch anyone's feet or food remnants at all.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं कृष्णा विराटस्य भार्यया परिसान्त्विता ।न चैनां वेद तत्रान्यस्तत्त्वेन जनमेजय ॥३३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ kṛṣṇā virāṭasya bhāryayā parisāntvitā ,na caināṁ veda tatrānyastattvena janamejaya.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O Janamejaya, in this way, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was consoled by Virāṭa's wife, and no one else there truly knew her identity.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. Vaiśampāyana said: O Janamejaya, in this way, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was consoled by Virāṭa's wife, and no one else there truly knew her identity.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सहदेवोऽपि गोपानां कृत्वा वेषमनुत्तमम् ।भाषां चैषां समास्थाय विराटमुपयादथ ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sahadevo'pi gopānāṁ kṛtvā veṣamanuttamam ,bhāṣāṁ caiṣāṁ samāsthāya virāṭamupayādatha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Sahadeva also, having adopted an excellent disguise as one of the cowherds and having mastered their language, then approached King Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Sahadeva also, having adopted an excellent disguise as one of the cowherds and having mastered their language, then approached King Virāṭa.

sanskrit: तमायान्तमभिप्रेक्ष्य भ्राजमानं नरर्षभम् ।समुपस्थाय वै राजा पप्रच्छ कुरुनन्दनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tamāyāntamabhiprekṣya bhrājamānaṁ nararṣabham ,samupasthāya vai rājā papraccha kurunandanam.
english: Having observed that eminent man approaching, shining brightly, King Virāṭa then went near him and questioned the delight of the Kurus (Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. Having observed that eminent man approaching, shining brightly, King Virāṭa then went near him and questioned the delight of the Kurus (Sahadeva).

sanskrit: कस्य वा त्वं कुतो वा त्वं किं वा तात चिकीर्षसि ।न हि मे दृष्टपूर्वस्त्वं तत्त्वं ब्रूहि नरर्षभ ॥३॥
transliteration: kasya vā tvaṁ kuto vā tvaṁ kiṁ vā tāta cikīrṣasi ,na hi me dṛṣṭapūrvastvaṁ tattvaṁ brūhi nararṣabha.
english: Or whose son are you? Or from where do you come? And what, dear one, do you wish to do? For indeed, I have never seen you before. O eminent man, tell me the truth!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. Or whose son are you? Or from where do you come? And what, dear one, do you wish to do? For indeed, I have never seen you before. O eminent man, tell me the truth!

sanskrit: स प्राप्य राजानममित्रतापनस्ततोऽब्रवीन्मेघमहौघनिःस्वनः ।वैश्योऽस्मि नाम्नाहमरिष्टनेमिर्गोसंख्य आसं कुरुपुंगवानाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa prāpya rājānamamitratāpana;stato'bravīnmeghamahaughaniḥsvanaḥ ,vaiśyo'smi nāmnāhamariṣṭanemi;rgosaṁkhya āsaṁ kurupuṁgavānām.
english: Having approached the king, who was a tormentor of foes, he then spoke with a voice like a great mass of clouds. 'I am a Vaiśya by the name of Ariṣṭanemi. I was formerly a cowherd for the foremost among the Kurus.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Having approached the king, who was a tormentor of foes, he then spoke with a voice like a great mass of clouds. 'I am a Vaiśya by the name of Ariṣṭanemi. I was formerly a cowherd for the foremost among the Kurus.'

sanskrit: वस्तुं त्वयीच्छामि विशां वरिष्ठ तान्राजसिंहान्न हि वेद्मि पार्थान् ।न शक्यते जीवितुमन्यकर्मणा न च त्वदन्यो मम रोचते नृपः ॥५॥
transliteration: vastuṁ tvayīcchāmi viśāṁ variṣṭha; tānrājasiṁhānna hi vedmi pārthān ,na śakyate jīvitumanyakarmaṇā; na ca tvadanyo mama rocate nṛpaḥ.
english: O foremost among men, I wish to dwell with you, for I do not know those lion-like kings, the Pārthas. It is not possible for me to live by any other occupation, and no other king apart from you is pleasing to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. O foremost among men, I wish to dwell with you, for I do not know those lion-like kings, the Pārthas. It is not possible for me to live by any other occupation, and no other king apart from you is pleasing to me.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।त्वं ब्राह्मणो यदि वा क्षत्रियोऽसि समुद्रनेमीश्वररूपवानसि ।आचक्ष्व मे तत्त्वममित्रकर्शन न वैश्यकर्म त्वयि विद्यते समम् ॥६॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,tvaṁ brāhmaṇo yadi vā kṣatriyo'si; samudranemīśvararūpavānasi ,ācakṣva me tattvamamitrakarśana; na vaiśyakarma tvayi vidyate samam.
english: Virāṭa said: 'Are you a Brahmin or a Kṣatriya? You possess a majestic form like a lord whose dominion reaches the ocean's boundary. Tell me the truth, O destroyer of enemies, for no work befitting a Vaiśya is apparent in you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. Virāṭa said: 'Are you a Brahmin or a Kṣatriya? You possess a majestic form like a lord whose dominion reaches the ocean's boundary. Tell me the truth, O destroyer of enemies, for no work befitting a Vaiśya is apparent in you.'

sanskrit: कस्यासि राज्ञो विषयादिहागतः किं चापि शिल्पं तव विद्यते कृतम् ।कथं त्वमस्मासु निवत्स्यसे सदा वदस्व किं चापि तवेह वेतनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kasyāsi rājño viṣayādihāgataḥ; kiṁ cāpi śilpaṁ tava vidyate kṛtam ,kathaṁ tvamasmāsu nivatsyase sadā; vadasva kiṁ cāpi taveha vetanam.
english: From the domain of which king have you come here? And what skill or craft have you mastered? How will you perpetually reside among us? Tell me also, what is your salary here?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. From the domain of which king have you come here? And what skill or craft have you mastered? How will you perpetually reside among us? Tell me also, what is your salary here?

sanskrit: सहदेव उवाच ।पञ्चानां पाण्डुपुत्राणां ज्येष्ठो राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।तस्याष्टशतसाहस्रा गवां वर्गाः शतं शताः ॥८॥
transliteration: sahadeva uvāca ,pañcānāṁ pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ jyeṣṭho rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tasyāṣṭaśatasāhasrā gavāṁ vargāḥ śataṁ śatāḥ.
english: Sahadeva said: King Yudhishthira, the eldest among the five sons of Pandu, owned eight hundred thousand cows, organized into hundreds of hundreds of herds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. Sahadeva said: King Yudhishthira, the eldest among the five sons of Pandu, owned eight hundred thousand cows, organized into hundreds of hundreds of herds.

sanskrit: अपरे दशसाहस्रा द्विस्तावन्तस्तथापरे ।तेषां गोसंख्य आसं वै तन्तिपालेति मां विदुः ॥९॥
transliteration: apare daśasāhasrā dvistāvantastathāpare ,teṣāṁ gosaṁkhya āsaṁ vai tantipāleti māṁ viduḥ.
english: Others possessed ten thousand (cows), and yet others had twice that number. Among those cow-keepers, I was indeed known as Tantipāla.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. Others possessed ten thousand (cows), and yet others had twice that number. Among those cow-keepers, I was indeed known as Tantipāla.

sanskrit: भूतं भव्यं भविष्यच्च यच्च संख्यागतं क्वचित् ।न मेऽस्त्यविदितं किंचित्समन्ताद्दशयोजनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhūtaṁ bhavyaṁ bhaviṣyacca yacca saṁkhyāgataṁ kvacit ,na me'styaviditaṁ kiṁcitsamantāddaśayojanam.
english: What has been, what is destined to be, and what will be, as well as anything calculable anywhere - nothing within an area of ten yojanas is unknown to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. What has been, what is destined to be, and what will be, as well as anything calculable anywhere - nothing within an area of ten yojanas is unknown to me.

sanskrit: गुणाः सुविदिता ह्यासन्मम तस्य महात्मनः ।आसीच्च स मया तुष्टः कुरुराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥११॥
transliteration: guṇāḥ suviditā hyāsanmama tasya mahātmanaḥ ,āsīcca sa mayā tuṣṭaḥ kururājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: The excellent qualities of that great-souled (mahātman) person were very well known to me. And that King of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, was pleased with me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. The excellent qualities of that great-souled (mahātman) person were very well known to me. And that King of the Kurus, Yudhishthira, was pleased with me.

sanskrit: क्षिप्रं हि गावो बहुला भवन्ति न तासु रोगो भवतीह कश्चित् ।तैस्तैरुपायैर्विदितं मयैतदेतानि शिल्पानि मयि स्थितानि ॥१२॥
transliteration: kṣipraṁ hi gāvo bahulā bhavanti; na tāsu rogo bhavatīha kaścit ,taistairupāyairviditaṁ mayaita;detāni śilpāni mayi sthitāni.
english: Indeed, cows quickly multiply and no disease ever afflicts them here. I have learned this through various methods, and these skills reside within me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. Indeed, cows quickly multiply and no disease ever afflicts them here. I have learned this through various methods, and these skills reside within me.

sanskrit: वृषभांश्चापि जानामि राजन्पूजितलक्षणान् ।येषां मूत्रमुपाघ्राय अपि वन्ध्या प्रसूयते ॥१३॥
transliteration: vṛṣabhāṁścāpi jānāmi rājanpūjitalakṣaṇān ,yeṣāṁ mūtramupāghrāya api vandhyā prasūyate.
english: O King, I also know bulls possessing auspicious characteristics, by smelling the urine of which even a barren cow becomes fertile and gives birth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. O King, I also know bulls possessing auspicious characteristics, by smelling the urine of which even a barren cow becomes fertile and gives birth.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।शतं सहस्राणि समाहितानि वर्णस्य वर्णस्य विनिश्चिता गुणैः ।पशून्सपालान्भवते ददाम्यहं त्वदाश्रया मे पशवो भवन्त्विह ॥१४॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,śataṁ sahasrāṇi samāhitāni; varṇasya varṇasya viniścitā guṇaiḥ ,paśūnsapālānbhavate dadāmyahaṁ; tvadāśrayā me paśavo bhavantviha.
english: Virata said: 'I will give you hundreds of thousands of cattle, each specifically identified by its qualities, along with their herdsmen. May my cattle here remain under your protection.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. Virata said: 'I will give you hundreds of thousands of cattle, each specifically identified by its qualities, along with their herdsmen. May my cattle here remain under your protection.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा स राज्ञोऽविदितो विशां पते उवास तत्रैव सुखं नरेश्वरः ।न चैनमन्येऽपि विदुः कथंचन प्रादाच्च तस्मै भरणं यथेप्सितम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā sa rājño'vidito viśāṁ pate; uvāsa tatraiva sukhaṁ nareśvaraḥ ,na cainamanye'pi viduḥ kathaṁcana; prādācca tasmai bharaṇaṁ yathepsitam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Thus, that lord of men (Yudhisthira), unknown to the king, lived happily there. Moreover, no one else recognized him at all. And the king provided him with the desired maintenance.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Thus, that lord of men (Yudhisthira), unknown to the king, lived happily there. Moreover, no one else recognized him at all. And the king provided him with the desired maintenance.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथापरोऽदृश्यत रूपसंपदा स्त्रीणामलंकारधरो बृहत्पुमान् ।प्राकारवप्रे प्रतिमुच्य कुण्डले दीर्घे च कम्बू परिहाटके शुभे ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,athāparo'dṛśyata rūpasaṁpadā; strīṇāmalaṁkāradharo bṛhatpumān ,prākāravapre pratimucya kuṇḍale; dīrghe ca kambū parihāṭake śubhe.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then another was seen - a huge man endowed with abundant beauty, wearing women's ornaments. He had put on long, auspicious, golden conch-shell bangles and large earrings resembling ramparts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then another was seen - a huge man endowed with abundant beauty, wearing women's ornaments. He had put on long, auspicious, golden conch-shell bangles and large earrings resembling ramparts.

sanskrit: बहूंश्च दीर्घांश्च विकीर्य मूर्धजान्महाभुजो वारणमत्तविक्रमः ।गतेन भूमिमभिकम्पयंस्तदा विराटमासाद्य सभासमीपतः ॥२॥
transliteration: bahūṁśca dīrghāṁśca vikīrya mūrdhajā;nmahābhujo vāraṇamattavikramaḥ ,gatena bhūmimabhikampayaṁstadā; virāṭamāsādya sabhāsamīpataḥ.
english: With his mighty arms and the prowess of an intoxicated elephant, having let his many long hairs fall loose, he then approached Virāṭa near the assembly hall, shaking the earth with his gait.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. With his mighty arms and the prowess of an intoxicated elephant, having let his many long hairs fall loose, he then approached Virāṭa near the assembly hall, shaking the earth with his gait.

sanskrit: तं प्रेक्ष्य राजोपगतं सभातले सत्रप्रतिच्छन्नमरिप्रमाथिनम् ।विराजमानं परमेण वर्चसा सुतं महेन्द्रस्य गजेन्द्रविक्रमम् ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ prekṣya rājopagataṁ sabhātale; satrapraticchannamaripramāthinam ,virājamānaṁ parameṇa varcasā; sutaṁ mahendrasya gajendravikramam.
english: Seeing him - who had approached the king on the assembly floor, disguised by a strategem (sattrapraticchanna), an annihilator of enemies, shining with supreme splendor, the son of the great Indra, and possessing the valor of a lordly elephant...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. Seeing him - who had approached the king on the assembly floor, disguised by a strategem (sattrapraticchanna), an annihilator of enemies, shining with supreme splendor, the son of the great Indra, and possessing the valor of a lordly elephant...

sanskrit: सर्वानपृच्छच्च समीपचारिणः कुतोऽयमायाति न मे पुरा श्रुतः ।न चैनमूचुर्विदितं तदा नराः सविस्मितं वाक्यमिदं नृपोऽब्रवीत् ॥४॥
transliteration: sarvānapṛcchacca samīpacāriṇaḥ; kuto'yamāyāti na me purā śrutaḥ ,na cainamūcurviditaṁ tadā narāḥ; savismitaṁ vākyamidaṁ nṛpo'bravīt.
english: (The king) asked all those nearby, "From where does this person come? I have not heard of him before." And the men then could not tell him what was known. Highly astonished, the king then uttered this statement.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. (The king) asked all those nearby, "From where does this person come? I have not heard of him before." And the men then could not tell him what was known. Highly astonished, the king then uttered this statement.

sanskrit: सर्वोपपन्नः पुरुषो मनोरमः श्यामो युवा वारणयूथपोपमः ।विमुच्य कम्बू परिहाटके शुभे विमुच्य वेणीमपिनह्य कुण्डले ॥५॥
transliteration: sarvopapannaḥ puruṣo manoramaḥ; śyāmo yuvā vāraṇayūthapopamaḥ ,vimucya kambū parihāṭake śubhe; vimucya veṇīmapinahya kuṇḍale.
english: A man endowed with all excellent qualities, charming, dark-complexioned, youthful, and resembling a leader of an elephant herd. Having removed the two beautiful bracelets from his wrists, and having untied his braid, he put on earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. A man endowed with all excellent qualities, charming, dark-complexioned, youthful, and resembling a leader of an elephant herd. Having removed the two beautiful bracelets from his wrists, and having untied his braid, he put on earrings.

sanskrit: शिखी सुकेशः परिधाय चान्यथा भवस्व धन्वी कवची शरी तथा ।आरुह्य यानं परिधावतां भवान्सुतैः समो मे भव वा मया समः ॥६॥
transliteration: śikhī sukeśaḥ paridhāya cānyathā; bhavasva dhanvī kavacī śarī tathā ,āruhya yānaṁ paridhāvatāṁ bhavā;nsutaiḥ samo me bhava vā mayā samaḥ.
english: You should become one with a topknot and beautiful hair, dressed differently, an archer, armored, and armed with arrows. Mounting a chariot, among those who are swift, you should be equal to my sons or equal to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. You should become one with a topknot and beautiful hair, dressed differently, an archer, armored, and armed with arrows. Mounting a chariot, among those who are swift, you should be equal to my sons or equal to me.

sanskrit: वृद्धो ह्यहं वै परिहारकामः सर्वान्मत्स्यांस्तरसा पालयस्व ।नैवंविधाः क्लीबरूपा भवन्ति कथंचनेति प्रतिभाति मे मनः ॥७॥
transliteration: vṛddho hyahaṁ vai parihārakāmaḥ; sarvānmatsyāṁstarasā pālayasva ,naivaṁvidhāḥ klībarūpā bhavanti; kathaṁcaneti pratibhāti me manaḥ.
english: Indeed, I am old and wish to be excused (from battle). You must valiantly protect all the Matsyas. My mind perceives that those with such a form (as yours) are certainly not eunuchs.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. Indeed, I am old and wish to be excused (from battle). You must valiantly protect all the Matsyas. My mind perceives that those with such a form (as yours) are certainly not eunuchs.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।गायामि नृत्याम्यथ वादयामि भद्रोऽस्मि नृत्ते कुशलोऽस्मि गीते ।त्वमुत्तरायाः परिदत्स्व मां स्वयं भवामि देव्या नरदेव नर्तकः ॥८॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,gāyāmi nṛtyāmyatha vādayāmi; bhadro'smi nṛtte kuśalo'smi gīte ,tvamuttarāyāḥ paridatsva māṁ svayaṁ; bhavāmi devyā naradeva nartakaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: "I sing, I dance, and I play musical instruments. I am excellent in dance and skilled in music. O King, you yourself should entrust me to Uttarā; I will be the queen's dancer."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. Arjuna said: "I sing, I dance, and I play musical instruments. I am excellent in dance and skilled in music. O King, you yourself should entrust me to Uttarā; I will be the queen's dancer."

sanskrit: इदं तु रूपं मम येन किं नु तत्प्रकीर्तयित्वा भृशशोकवर्धनम् ।बृहन्नडां वै नरदेव विद्धि मां सुतं सुतां वा पितृमातृवर्जिताम् ॥९॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu rūpaṁ mama yena kiṁ nu ta;tprakīrtayitvā bhṛśaśokavardhanam ,bṛhannaḍāṁ vai naradeva viddhi māṁ; sutaṁ sutāṁ vā pitṛmātṛvarjitām.
english: This is indeed my current form, but what is the use of describing that which would greatly increase sorrow? O king, know me as Bṛhannadā, a son or a daughter deprived of father and mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. This is indeed my current form, but what is the use of describing that which would greatly increase sorrow? O king, know me as Bṛhannadā, a son or a daughter deprived of father and mother.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।ददामि ते हन्त वरं बृहन्नडे सुतां च मे नर्तय याश्च तादृशीः ।इदं तु ते कर्म समं न मे मतं समुद्रनेमिं पृथिवीं त्वमर्हसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,dadāmi te hanta varaṁ bṛhannaḍe; sutāṁ ca me nartaya yāśca tādṛśīḥ ,idaṁ tu te karma samaṁ na me mataṁ; samudranemiṁ pṛthivīṁ tvamarhasi.
english: Virāṭa said: Indeed, I grant you a boon, Bṛhannadā. Make my daughter dance, and also those others who are like her. However, this deed (karma) of yours is not considered appropriate by me; you deserve the entire earth (pṛthivī) encircled by the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Virāṭa said: Indeed, I grant you a boon, Bṛhannadā. Make my daughter dance, and also those others who are like her. However, this deed (karma) of yours is not considered appropriate by me; you deserve the entire earth (pṛthivī) encircled by the ocean.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।बृहन्नडां तामभिवीक्ष्य मत्स्यराट्कलासु नृत्ते च तथैव वादिते ।अपुंस्त्वमप्यस्य निशम्य च स्थिरं ततः कुमारीपुरमुत्ससर्ज तम् ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,bṛhannaḍāṁ tāmabhivīkṣya matsyarā;ṭkalāsu nṛtte ca tathaiva vādite ,apuṁstvamapyasya niśamya ca sthiraṁ; tataḥ kumārīpuramutsasarja tam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: King Matsya, having observed that Bṛhannadā excelled in arts, dance, and music, and having also perceived her stable lack of masculinity, then sent him to the women's quarters.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. Vaiśampāyana said: King Matsya, having observed that Bṛhannadā excelled in arts, dance, and music, and having also perceived her stable lack of masculinity, then sent him to the women's quarters.

sanskrit: स शिक्षयामास च गीतवादितं सुतां विराटस्य धनंजयः प्रभुः ।सखीश्च तस्याः परिचारिकास्तथा प्रियश्च तासां स बभूव पाण्डवः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa śikṣayāmāsa ca gītavāditaṁ; sutāṁ virāṭasya dhanaṁjayaḥ prabhuḥ ,sakhīśca tasyāḥ paricārikāstathā; priyaśca tāsāṁ sa babhūva pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: And that mighty Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), son of Pāṇḍu, taught the daughter of Virāṭa, as well as her companions and attendants, singing and instrumental music. He became dear to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. And that mighty Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), son of Pāṇḍu, taught the daughter of Virāṭa, as well as her companions and attendants, singing and instrumental music. He became dear to them.

sanskrit: तथा स सत्रेण धनंजयोऽवसत्प्रियाणि कुर्वन्सह ताभिरात्मवान् ।तथागतं तत्र न जज्ञिरे जना बहिश्चरा वाप्यथ वान्तरेचराः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tathā sa satreṇa dhanaṁjayo'vasa;tpriyāṇi kurvansaha tābhirātmavān ,tathāgataṁ tatra na jajñire janā; bahiścarā vāpyatha vāntarecarāḥ.
english: Thus, that self-controlled (ātman) Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) lived there during the ritual session, performing agreeable deeds with those women. Consequently, the people present, whether moving outside or residing inside, did not recognize him, who had arrived in such a manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. Thus, that self-controlled (ātman) Arjuna (Dhanañjaya) lived there during the ritual session, performing agreeable deeds with those women. Consequently, the people present, whether moving outside or residing inside, did not recognize him, who had arrived in such a manner.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथापरोऽदृश्यत पाण्डवः प्रभुर्विराटराज्ञस्तुरगान्समीक्षतः ।तमापतन्तं ददृशे पृथग्जनो विमुक्तमभ्रादिव सूर्यमण्डलम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,athāparo'dṛśyata pāṇḍavaḥ prabhu;rvirāṭarājñasturagānsamīkṣataḥ ,tamāpatantaṁ dadṛśe pṛthagjano; vimuktamabhrādiva sūryamaṇḍalam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Then another lordly Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) became visible to King Virāṭa, who was inspecting the horses. The common people beheld him approaching, like the sun's orb (sūryamaṇḍalam) freed from a cloud."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Then another lordly Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) became visible to King Virāṭa, who was inspecting the horses. The common people beheld him approaching, like the sun's orb (sūryamaṇḍalam) freed from a cloud."

sanskrit: स वै हयानैक्षत तांस्ततस्ततः समीक्षमाणं च ददर्श मत्स्यराट् ।ततोऽब्रवीत्ताननुगानमित्रहा कुतोऽयमायाति नरोऽमरप्रभः ॥२॥
transliteration: sa vai hayānaikṣata tāṁstatastataḥ; samīkṣamāṇaṁ ca dadarśa matsyarāṭ ,tato'bravīttānanugānamitrahā; kuto'yamāyāti naro'maraprabhaḥ.
english: Indeed, he gazed at those horses here and there, and the Matsya King (Virāṭa) saw him observing. Then, the enemy-slayer (amitrahā) spoke to his attendants: 'From where does this man, radiant like an immortal, come?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. Indeed, he gazed at those horses here and there, and the Matsya King (Virāṭa) saw him observing. Then, the enemy-slayer (amitrahā) spoke to his attendants: 'From where does this man, radiant like an immortal, come?'

sanskrit: अयं हयान्वीक्षति मामकान्दृढं ध्रुवं हयज्ञो भविता विचक्षणः ।प्रवेश्यतामेष समीपमाशु मे विभाति वीरो हि यथामरस्तथा ॥३॥
transliteration: ayaṁ hayānvīkṣati māmakāndṛḍhaṁ; dhruvaṁ hayajño bhavitā vicakṣaṇaḥ ,praveśyatāmeṣa samīpamāśu me; vibhāti vīro hi yathāmarastathā.
english: "This man intently observes my horses. Surely, he must be a discerning expert in horses. Let him be brought near to me quickly, for he truly shines like a hero, just like an immortal."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. "This man intently observes my horses. Surely, he must be a discerning expert in horses. Let him be brought near to me quickly, for he truly shines like a hero, just like an immortal."

sanskrit: अभ्येत्य राजानममित्रहाब्रवीज्जयोऽस्तु ते पार्थिव भद्रमस्तु च ।हयेषु युक्तो नृप संमतः सदा तवाश्वसूतो निपुणो भवाम्यहम् ॥४॥
transliteration: abhyetya rājānamamitrahābravī;jjayo'stu te pārthiva bhadramastu ca ,hayeṣu yukto nṛpa saṁmataḥ sadā; tavāśvasūto nipuṇo bhavāmyaham.
english: Approaching the king, the destroyer of enemies said, "May victory be yours, O King, and may you be well. O King, I am always esteemed for my skill with horses, and I can be your expert charioteer."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. Approaching the king, the destroyer of enemies said, "May victory be yours, O King, and may you be well. O King, I am always esteemed for my skill with horses, and I can be your expert charioteer."

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।ददामि यानानि धनं निवेशनं ममाश्वसूतो भवितुं त्वमर्हसि ।कुतोऽसि कस्यासि कथं त्वमागतः प्रब्रूहि शिल्पं तव विद्यते च यत् ॥५॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,dadāmi yānāni dhanaṁ niveśanaṁ; mamāśvasūto bhavituṁ tvamarhasi ,kuto'si kasyāsi kathaṁ tvamāgataḥ; prabrūhi śilpaṁ tava vidyate ca yat.
english: Virata said, "I will provide conveyances, wealth, and a residence; you are indeed worthy to be my charioteer. Where are you from? Whose family do you belong to? How did you come here? Please tell me what skills you possess."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. Virata said, "I will provide conveyances, wealth, and a residence; you are indeed worthy to be my charioteer. Where are you from? Whose family do you belong to? How did you come here? Please tell me what skills you possess."

sanskrit: नकुल उवाच ।पञ्चानां पाण्डुपुत्राणां ज्येष्ठो राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।तेनाहमश्वेषु पुरा प्रकृतः शत्रुकर्शन ॥६॥
transliteration: nakula uvāca ,pañcānāṁ pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ jyeṣṭho rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tenāhamaśveṣu purā prakṛtaḥ śatrukarśana.
english: Nakula said, "Among the five sons of Pandu, the eldest is King Yudhishthira. By him, I was formerly employed in matters of horses, O destroyer of enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. Nakula said, "Among the five sons of Pandu, the eldest is King Yudhishthira. By him, I was formerly employed in matters of horses, O destroyer of enemies."

sanskrit: अश्वानां प्रकृतिं वेद्मि विनयं चापि सर्वशः ।दुष्टानां प्रतिपत्तिं च कृत्स्नं चैव चिकित्सितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: aśvānāṁ prakṛtiṁ vedmi vinayaṁ cāpi sarvaśaḥ ,duṣṭānāṁ pratipattiṁ ca kṛtsnaṁ caiva cikitsitam.
english: "I know the intrinsic nature (prakṛti) of horses and their training thoroughly. I also understand the management of unruly horses and all their medical treatment."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. "I know the intrinsic nature (prakṛti) of horses and their training thoroughly. I also understand the management of unruly horses and all their medical treatment."

sanskrit: न कातरं स्यान्मम जातु वाहनं न मेऽस्ति दुष्टा वडवा कुतो हयाः ।जनस्तु मामाह स चापि पाण्डवो युधिष्ठिरो ग्रन्थिकमेव नामतः ॥८॥
transliteration: na kātaraṁ syānmama jātu vāhanaṁ; na me'sti duṣṭā vaḍavā kuto hayāḥ ,janastu māmāha sa cāpi pāṇḍavo; yudhiṣṭhiro granthikameva nāmataḥ.
english: My conveyance will never be timid, nor do I possess any vicious mare. How then could I have (bad) horses? People, however, call me, that scion of Pāṇḍu, Yudhiṣṭhira, by the name Granthaika.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. My conveyance will never be timid, nor do I possess any vicious mare. How then could I have (bad) horses? People, however, call me, that scion of Pāṇḍu, Yudhiṣṭhira, by the name Granthaika.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।यदस्ति किंचिन्मम वाजिवाहनं तदस्तु सर्वं त्वदधीनमद्य वै ।ये चापि केचिन्मम वाजियोजकास्त्वदाश्रयाः सारथयश्च सन्तु मे ॥९॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,yadasti kiṁcinmama vājivāhanaṁ; tadastu sarvaṁ tvadadhīnamadya vai ,ye cāpi kecinmama vājiyojakā;stvadāśrayāḥ sārathayaśca santu me.
english: Virāṭa said: 'Whatever horse-drawn conveyance I possess, let all of it be under your control right now. And whatever horse-trainers I have, let them also be dependent on you as charioteers.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. Virāṭa said: 'Whatever horse-drawn conveyance I possess, let all of it be under your control right now. And whatever horse-trainers I have, let them also be dependent on you as charioteers.'

sanskrit: इदं तवेष्टं यदि वै सुरोपम ब्रवीहि यत्ते प्रसमीक्षितं वसु ।न तेऽनुरूपं हयकर्म विद्यते प्रभासि राजेव हि संमतो मम ॥१०॥
transliteration: idaṁ taveṣṭaṁ yadi vai suropama; bravīhi yatte prasamīkṣitaṁ vasu ,na te'nurūpaṁ hayakarma vidyate; prabhāsi rājeva hi saṁmato mama.
english: If this is indeed agreeable to you, O god-like one, then tell me what wealth you deem appropriate. Work with horses is not suitable for you, for you shine like a king and are highly esteemed by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. If this is indeed agreeable to you, O god-like one, then tell me what wealth you deem appropriate. Work with horses is not suitable for you, for you shine like a king and are highly esteemed by me.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्येव हि दर्शनेन मे समं तवेदं प्रियदर्श दर्शनम् ।कथं तु भृत्यैः स विनाकृतो वने वसत्यनिन्द्यो रमते च पाण्डवः ॥११॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasyeva hi darśanena me; samaṁ tavedaṁ priyadarśa darśanam ,kathaṁ tu bhṛtyaiḥ sa vinākṛto vane; vasatyanindyo ramate ca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Truly, to me, this pleasing sight of yours, O dear one, is like that of Yudhiṣṭhira. But how does that blameless scion of Pāṇḍu, separated from his servants, live and find joy in the forest?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. Truly, to me, this pleasing sight of yours, O dear one, is like that of Yudhiṣṭhira. But how does that blameless scion of Pāṇḍu, separated from his servants, live and find joy in the forest?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा स गन्धर्ववरोपमो युवा विराटराज्ञा मुदितेन पूजितः ।न चैनमन्येऽपि विदुः कथंचन प्रियाभिरामं विचरन्तमन्तरा ॥१२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā sa gandharvavaropamo yuvā; virāṭarājñā muditena pūjitaḥ ,na cainamanye'pi viduḥ kathaṁcana; priyābhirāmaṁ vicarantamantarā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus, that young man, resembling the best of the gandharvas, was honored by King Virāṭa, who was delighted. Yet, no one else could somehow recognize him as he moved about, delighting everyone in their midst.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus, that young man, resembling the best of the gandharvas, was honored by King Virāṭa, who was delighted. Yet, no one else could somehow recognize him as he moved about, delighting everyone in their midst.

sanskrit: एवं हि मत्स्ये न्यवसन्त पाण्डवा यथाप्रतिज्ञाभिरमोघदर्शनाः ।अज्ञातचर्यां व्यचरन्समाहिताः समुद्रनेमीपतयोऽतिदुःखिताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁ hi matsye nyavasanta pāṇḍavā; yathāpratijñābhiramoghadarśanāḥ ,ajñātacaryāṁ vyacaransamāhitāḥ; samudranemīpatayo'tiduḥkhitāḥ.
english: Thus, the Pāṇḍavas, whose sight was unfailing and whose vows were firm, resided in the Matsya kingdom. Though they were lords of the sea-girt earth, they diligently observed their period of incognito living, enduring great sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. Thus, the Pāṇḍavas, whose sight was unfailing and whose vows were firm, resided in the Matsya kingdom. Though they were lords of the sea-girt earth, they diligently observed their period of incognito living, enduring great sorrow.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।एवं मत्स्यस्य नगरे वसन्तस्तत्र पाण्डवाः ।अत ऊर्ध्वं महावीर्याः किमकुर्वन्त वै द्विज ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,evaṁ matsyasya nagare vasantastatra pāṇḍavāḥ ,ata ūrdhvaṁ mahāvīryāḥ kimakurvanta vai dvija.
english: Janamejaya said: O twice-born one (dvija), while the mighty Pāṇḍavas were thus living in the city of Matsya, what did they do next?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: O twice-born one (dvija), while the mighty Pāṇḍavas were thus living in the city of Matsya, what did they do next?
