sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं ते न्यवसंस्तत्र प्रच्छन्नाः कुरुनन्दनाः ।आराधयन्तो राजानं यदकुर्वन्त तच्छृणु ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ te nyavasaṁstatra pracchannāḥ kurunandanāḥ ,ārādhayanto rājānaṁ yadakurvanta tacchṛṇu.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Listen now to what those Kuru princes, living disguised there and propitiating the king, did thereafter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Listen now to what those Kuru princes, living disguised there and propitiating the king, did thereafter.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरः सभास्तारः सभ्यानामभवत्प्रियः ।तथैव च विराटस्य सपुत्रस्य विशां पते ॥३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraḥ sabhāstāraḥ sabhyānāmabhavatpriyaḥ ,tathaiva ca virāṭasya saputrasya viśāṁ pate.
english: Yudhishthira, as a dice-player, became dear to the assembly members. And similarly, he became dear to Virata, the lord of the people, along with his son.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. Yudhishthira, as a dice-player, became dear to the assembly members. And similarly, he became dear to Virata, the lord of the people, along with his son.

sanskrit: स ह्यक्षहृदयज्ञस्तान्क्रीडयामास पाण्डवः ।अक्षवत्यां यथाकामं सूत्रबद्धानिव द्विजान् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa hyakṣahṛdayajñastānkrīḍayāmāsa pāṇḍavaḥ ,akṣavatyāṁ yathākāmaṁ sūtrabaddhāniva dvijān.
english: Indeed, that Pandava (Yudhishthira), being an expert in dice (akṣa), made them play in the gambling hall as he wished, as if they were birds tied by strings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Indeed, that Pandava (Yudhishthira), being an expert in dice (akṣa), made them play in the gambling hall as he wished, as if they were birds tied by strings.

sanskrit: अज्ञातं च विराटस्य विजित्य वसु धर्मराट् ।भ्रातृभ्यः पुरुषव्याघ्रो यथार्हं स्म प्रयच्छति ॥५॥
transliteration: ajñātaṁ ca virāṭasya vijitya vasu dharmarāṭ ,bhrātṛbhyaḥ puruṣavyāghro yathārhaṁ sma prayacchati.
english: And Yudhishthira, the king upholding natural law (dharma) and a tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), having acquired wealth without Virata's knowledge, used to distribute it appropriately to his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. And Yudhishthira, the king upholding natural law (dharma) and a tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), having acquired wealth without Virata's knowledge, used to distribute it appropriately to his brothers.

sanskrit: भीमसेनोऽपि मांसानि भक्ष्याणि विविधानि च ।अतिसृष्टानि मत्स्येन विक्रीणाति युधिष्ठिरे ॥६॥
transliteration: bhīmaseno'pi māṁsāni bhakṣyāṇi vividhāni ca ,atisṛṣṭāni matsyena vikrīṇāti yudhiṣṭhire.
english: Bhimasena also sold various meats and other edibles, which were generously provided by King Matsya (Virata), for Yudhishthira.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. Bhimasena also sold various meats and other edibles, which were generously provided by King Matsya (Virata), for Yudhishthira.

sanskrit: वासांसि परिजीर्णानि लब्धान्यन्तःपुरेऽर्जुनः ।विक्रीणानश्च सर्वेभ्यः पाण्डवेभ्यः प्रयच्छति ॥७॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsi parijīrṇāni labdhānyantaḥpure'rjunaḥ ,vikrīṇānaśca sarvebhyaḥ pāṇḍavebhyaḥ prayacchati.
english: Arjuna, having obtained worn-out garments from the inner chambers, sells them and gives [the proceeds] to all the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. Arjuna, having obtained worn-out garments from the inner chambers, sells them and gives [the proceeds] to all the Pandavas.

sanskrit: सहदेवोऽपि गोपानां वेषमास्थाय पाण्डवः ।दधि क्षीरं घृतं चैव पाण्डवेभ्यः प्रयच्छति ॥८॥
transliteration: sahadevo'pi gopānāṁ veṣamāsthāya pāṇḍavaḥ ,dadhi kṣīraṁ ghṛtaṁ caiva pāṇḍavebhyaḥ prayacchati.
english: Sahadeva, the Pandava, also assuming the disguise of a cowherd, gives curds, milk, and ghee to the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. Sahadeva, the Pandava, also assuming the disguise of a cowherd, gives curds, milk, and ghee to the Pandavas.

sanskrit: नकुलोऽपि धनं लब्ध्वा कृते कर्मणि वाजिनाम् ।तुष्टे तस्मिन्नरपतौ पाण्डवेभ्यः प्रयच्छति ॥९॥
transliteration: nakulo'pi dhanaṁ labdhvā kṛte karmaṇi vājinām ,tuṣṭe tasminnarapatau pāṇḍavebhyaḥ prayacchati.
english: Nakula also, having obtained wealth when his work with the horses was completed and that king was pleased, gives [it] to the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. Nakula also, having obtained wealth when his work with the horses was completed and that king was pleased, gives [it] to the Pandavas.

sanskrit: कृष्णापि सर्वान्भ्रातॄंस्तान्निरीक्षन्ती तपस्विनी ।यथा पुनरविज्ञाता तथा चरति भामिनी ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇāpi sarvānbhrātṝṁstānnirīkṣantī tapasvinī ,yathā punaravijñātā tathā carati bhāminī.
english: The distressed Draupadi (kṛṣṇā), also observing all those brothers, behaves in such a way that she remains unrecognized.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. The distressed Draupadi (kṛṣṇā), also observing all those brothers, behaves in such a way that she remains unrecognized.

sanskrit: एवं संपादयन्तस्ते तथान्योन्यं महारथाः ।प्रेक्षमाणास्तदा कृष्णामूषुश्छन्ना नराधिप ॥११॥
transliteration: evaṁ saṁpādayantaste tathānyonyaṁ mahārathāḥ ,prekṣamāṇāstadā kṛṣṇāmūṣuśchannā narādhipa.
english: O King, thus, those great warriors, mutually accomplishing their objective and watching Draupadi, then lived concealed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. O King, thus, those great warriors, mutually accomplishing their objective and watching Draupadi, then lived concealed.

sanskrit: अथ मासे चतुर्थे तु ब्रह्मणः सुमहोत्सवः ।आसीत्समृद्धो मत्स्येषु पुरुषाणां सुसंमतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: atha māse caturthe tu brahmaṇaḥ sumahotsavaḥ ,āsītsamṛddho matsyeṣu puruṣāṇāṁ susaṁmataḥ.
english: Then, in the fourth month, a very grand and prosperous festival dedicated to Brahma (brahman) took place among the Matsyas, highly esteemed by all men.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. Then, in the fourth month, a very grand and prosperous festival dedicated to Brahma (brahman) took place among the Matsyas, highly esteemed by all men.

sanskrit: तत्र मल्लाः समापेतुर्दिग्भ्यो राजन्सहस्रशः ।महाकाया महावीर्याः कालखञ्जा इवासुराः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tatra mallāḥ samāpeturdigbhyo rājansahasraśaḥ ,mahākāyā mahāvīryāḥ kālakhañjā ivāsurāḥ.
english: O King, there, from all directions, thousands of wrestlers gathered - huge-bodied and mighty, like the Kālakañja demons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. O King, there, from all directions, thousands of wrestlers gathered - huge-bodied and mighty, like the Kālakañja demons.

sanskrit: वीर्योन्नद्धा बलोदग्रा राज्ञा समभिपूजिताः ।सिंहस्कन्धकटिग्रीवाः स्ववदाता मनस्विनः ।असकृल्लब्धलक्षास्ते रङ्गे पार्थिवसंनिधौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: vīryonnaddhā balodagrā rājñā samabhipūjitāḥ ,siṁhaskandhakaṭigrīvāḥ svavadātā manasvinaḥ ,asakṛllabdhalakṣāste raṅge pārthivasaṁnidhau.
english: Puffed up with prowess and exceedingly mighty, they were highly honored by the king. With shoulders, hips, and necks like lions, they were pure and high-minded. They had repeatedly achieved their objectives in the arena, in the presence of the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. Puffed up with prowess and exceedingly mighty, they were highly honored by the king. With shoulders, hips, and necks like lions, they were pure and high-minded. They had repeatedly achieved their objectives in the arena, in the presence of the king.

sanskrit: तेषामेको महानासीत्सर्वमल्लान्समाह्वयत् ।आवल्गमानं तं रङ्गे नोपतिष्ठति कश्चन ॥१५॥
transliteration: teṣāmeko mahānāsītsarvamallānsamāhvayat ,āvalgamānaṁ taṁ raṅge nopatiṣṭhati kaścana.
english: Among them, there was one mighty wrestler who challenged all others. As he pranced in the arena, no one dared to confront him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. Among them, there was one mighty wrestler who challenged all others. As he pranced in the arena, no one dared to confront him.

sanskrit: यदा सर्वे विमनसस्ते मल्ला हतचेतसः ।अथ सूदेन तं मल्लं योधयामास मत्स्यराट् ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadā sarve vimanasaste mallā hatacetasaḥ ,atha sūdena taṁ mallaṁ yodhayāmāsa matsyarāṭ.
english: When all the wrestlers became disheartened and dejected, then the king of Matsya had his cook fight that wrestler.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 16. When all the wrestlers became disheartened and dejected, then the king of Matsya had his cook fight that wrestler.

sanskrit: चोद्यमानस्ततो भीमो दुःखेनैवाकरोन्मतिम् ।न हि शक्नोति विवृते प्रत्याख्यातुं नराधिपम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: codyamānastato bhīmo duḥkhenaivākaronmatim ,na hi śaknoti vivṛte pratyākhyātuṁ narādhipam.
english: Being thus urged, Bhīma reluctantly made his decision, for he could not openly refuse the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 17. Being thus urged, Bhīma reluctantly made his decision, for he could not openly refuse the king.

sanskrit: ततः स पुरुषव्याघ्रः शार्दूलशिथिलं चरन् ।प्रविवेश महारङ्गं विराटमभिहर्षयन् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa puruṣavyāghraḥ śārdūlaśithilaṁ caran ,praviveśa mahāraṅgaṁ virāṭamabhiharṣayan.
english: Then that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghraḥ), moving with the sluggishness of a lion, entered the great arena, delighting King Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 18. Then that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghraḥ), moving with the sluggishness of a lion, entered the great arena, delighting King Virāṭa.

sanskrit: बबन्ध कक्ष्यां कौन्तेयस्ततस्तं हर्षयञ्जनम् ।ततस्तं वृत्रसंकाशं भीमो मल्लं समाह्वयत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: babandha kakṣyāṁ kaunteyastatastaṁ harṣayañjanam ,tatastaṁ vṛtrasaṁkāśaṁ bhīmo mallaṁ samāhvayat.
english: Then Bhīma (Kaunteya) tied his girdle, gladdening the people. Subsequently, Bhīma challenged that wrestler, who was as formidable as Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 19. Then Bhīma (Kaunteya) tied his girdle, gladdening the people. Subsequently, Bhīma challenged that wrestler, who was as formidable as Vṛtra.

sanskrit: तावुभौ सुमहोत्साहावुभौ तीव्रपराक्रमौ ।मत्ताविव महाकायौ वारणौ षष्टिहायनौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: tāvubhau sumahotsāhāvubhau tīvraparākramau ,mattāviva mahākāyau vāraṇau ṣaṣṭihāyanau.
english: Both of them were endowed with great enthusiasm and intense prowess. They were like two gigantic, sixty-year-old elephants in rut.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 20. Both of them were endowed with great enthusiasm and intense prowess. They were like two gigantic, sixty-year-old elephants in rut.

sanskrit: चकर्ष दोर्भ्यामुत्पाट्य भीमो मल्लममित्रहा ।विनदन्तमभिक्रोशञ्शार्दूल इव वारणम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: cakarṣa dorbhyāmutpāṭya bhīmo mallamamitrahā ,vinadantamabhikrośañśārdūla iva vāraṇam.
english: Bhīma, the destroyer of foes, tore the wrestler from his position and dragged him with his two arms, roaring loudly at him just as a tiger roars at an elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 21. Bhīma, the destroyer of foes, tore the wrestler from his position and dragged him with his two arms, roaring loudly at him just as a tiger roars at an elephant.

sanskrit: तमुद्यम्य महाबाहुर्भ्रामयामास वीर्यवान् ।ततो मल्लाश्च मत्स्याश्च विस्मयं चक्रिरे परम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tamudyamya mahābāhurbhrāmayāmāsa vīryavān ,tato mallāśca matsyāśca vismayaṁ cakrire param.
english: The mighty-armed, valorous Bhīma, having lifted him up, began to whirl him around. Consequently, both the wrestlers and the Matsya people were greatly astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 22. The mighty-armed, valorous Bhīma, having lifted him up, began to whirl him around. Consequently, both the wrestlers and the Matsya people were greatly astonished.

sanskrit: भ्रामयित्वा शतगुणं गतसत्त्वमचेतनम् ।प्रत्यपिंषन्महाबाहुर्मल्लं भुवि वृकोदरः ॥२३॥
transliteration: bhrāmayitvā śataguṇaṁ gatasattvamacetanam ,pratyapiṁṣanmahābāhurmallaṁ bhuvi vṛkodaraḥ.
english: Having whirled the wrestler, who was now lifeless and unconscious, a hundred times, the mighty-armed Vṛkodara (Bhimasena) crushed him onto the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 23. Having whirled the wrestler, who was now lifeless and unconscious, a hundred times, the mighty-armed Vṛkodara (Bhimasena) crushed him onto the ground.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्विनिहते मल्ले जीमूते लोकविश्रुते ।विराटः परमं हर्षमगच्छद्बान्धवैः सह ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasminvinihate malle jīmūte lokaviśrute ,virāṭaḥ paramaṁ harṣamagacchadbāndhavaiḥ saha.
english: When that world-renowned wrestler Jīmūta was slain, King Virāṭa, along with his kinsmen, experienced supreme joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 24. When that world-renowned wrestler Jīmūta was slain, King Virāṭa, along with his kinsmen, experienced supreme joy.

sanskrit: संहर्षात्प्रददौ वित्तं बहु राजा महामनाः ।बल्लवाय महारङ्गे यथा वैश्रवणस्तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: saṁharṣātpradadau vittaṁ bahu rājā mahāmanāḥ ,ballavāya mahāraṅge yathā vaiśravaṇastathā.
english: Out of immense joy, the great-souled king, in the grand arena, bestowed abundant wealth upon Ballava, just as Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera) does.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 25. Out of immense joy, the great-souled king, in the grand arena, bestowed abundant wealth upon Ballava, just as Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera) does.

sanskrit: एवं स सुबहून्मल्लान्पुरुषांश्च महाबलान् ।विनिघ्नन्मत्स्यराजस्य प्रीतिमावहदुत्तमाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa subahūnmallānpuruṣāṁśca mahābalān ,vinighnanmatsyarājasya prītimāvahaduttamām.
english: In this way, by striking down very many powerful wrestlers and men, he brought the highest satisfaction to the King of Matsya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 26. In this way, by striking down very many powerful wrestlers and men, he brought the highest satisfaction to the King of Matsya.

sanskrit: यदास्य तुल्यः पुरुषो न कश्चित्तत्र विद्यते ।ततो व्याघ्रैश्च सिंहैश्च द्विरदैश्चाप्ययोधयत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: yadāsya tulyaḥ puruṣo na kaścittatra vidyate ,tato vyāghraiśca siṁhaiśca dviradaiścāpyayodhayat.
english: When no man equal to him could be found there, he then fought with tigers, lions, and also elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 27. When no man equal to him could be found there, he then fought with tigers, lions, and also elephants.

sanskrit: पुनरन्तःपुरगतः स्त्रीणां मध्ये वृकोदरः ।योध्यते स्म विराटेन सिंहैर्मत्तैर्महाबलैः ॥२८॥
transliteration: punarantaḥpuragataḥ strīṇāṁ madhye vṛkodaraḥ ,yodhyate sma virāṭena siṁhairmattairmahābalaiḥ.
english: Again, having entered the inner palace and being among the women, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) was made to fight by Virāṭa against mighty, maddened lions.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 28. Again, having entered the inner palace and being among the women, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) was made to fight by Virāṭa against mighty, maddened lions.

sanskrit: बीभत्सुरपि गीतेन सुनृत्तेन च पाण्डवः ।विराटं तोषयामास सर्वाश्चान्तःपुरस्त्रियः ॥२९॥
transliteration: bībhatsurapi gītena sunṛttena ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,virāṭaṁ toṣayāmāsa sarvāścāntaḥpurastriyaḥ.
english: Bhībhātsu (Arjuna), the Pāṇḍava, also pleased King Virāṭa and all the women of the inner palace with his singing and excellent dancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 29. Bhībhātsu (Arjuna), the Pāṇḍava, also pleased King Virāṭa and all the women of the inner palace with his singing and excellent dancing.

sanskrit: अश्वैर्विनीतैर्जवनैस्तत्र तत्र समागतैः ।तोषयामास नकुलो राजानं राजसत्तम ॥३०॥
transliteration: aśvairvinītairjavanaistatra tatra samāgataiḥ ,toṣayāmāsa nakulo rājānaṁ rājasattama.
english: With well-trained, swift horses that he gathered from various places, Nakula pleased the king, who was the best among kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 30. With well-trained, swift horses that he gathered from various places, Nakula pleased the king, who was the best among kings.

sanskrit: तस्मै प्रदेयं प्रायच्छत्प्रीतो राजा धनं बहु ।विनीतान्वृषभान्दृष्ट्वा सहदेवस्य चाभिभो ॥३१॥
transliteration: tasmai pradeyaṁ prāyacchatprīto rājā dhanaṁ bahu ,vinītānvṛṣabhāndṛṣṭvā sahadevasya cābhibho.
english: O chief (abhibho), the king, being pleased after seeing Sahadeva's well-trained bulls, gave him a great deal of wealth, which was due to be given (pradeyam).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 31. O chief (abhibho), the king, being pleased after seeing Sahadeva's well-trained bulls, gave him a great deal of wealth, which was due to be given (pradeyam).

sanskrit: एवं ते न्यवसंस्तत्र प्रच्छन्नाः पुरुषर्षभाः ।कर्माणि तस्य कुर्वाणा विराटनृपतेस्तदा ॥३२॥
transliteration: evaṁ te nyavasaṁstatra pracchannāḥ puruṣarṣabhāḥ ,karmāṇi tasya kurvāṇā virāṭanṛpatestadā.
english: Thus, those excellent men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), remaining disguised, resided there at that time, performing the duties of King Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 12 Verse 32. Thus, those excellent men (puruṣarṣabhāḥ), remaining disguised, resided there at that time, performing the duties of King Virāṭa.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।वसमानेषु पार्थेषु मत्स्यस्य नगरे तदा ।महारथेषु छन्नेषु मासा दश समत्ययुः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vasamāneṣu pārtheṣu matsyasya nagare tadā ,mahāratheṣu channeṣu māsā daśa samatyayuḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: As the Pārthas, those great warriors (mahārathas), were dwelling disguised in the city of Matsya at that time, ten months elapsed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: As the Pārthas, those great warriors (mahārathas), were dwelling disguised in the city of Matsya at that time, ten months elapsed.

sanskrit: याज्ञसेनी सुदेष्णां तु शुश्रूषन्ती विशां पते ।अवसत्परिचारार्हा सुदुःखं जनमेजय ॥२॥
transliteration: yājñasenī sudeṣṇāṁ tu śuśrūṣantī viśāṁ pate ,avasatparicārārhā suduḥkhaṁ janamejaya.
english: O lord (viśām pate), O Janamejaya, Yājñasenī, who was deserving of attendance (paricārārhā) herself, nevertheless resided there very miserably, serving Sudeṣṇā.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. O lord (viśām pate), O Janamejaya, Yājñasenī, who was deserving of attendance (paricārārhā) herself, nevertheless resided there very miserably, serving Sudeṣṇā.

sanskrit: तथा चरन्तीं पाञ्चालीं सुदेष्णाया निवेशने ।सेनापतिर्विराटस्य ददर्श जलजाननाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tathā carantīṁ pāñcālīṁ sudeṣṇāyā niveśane ,senāpatirvirāṭasya dadarśa jalajānanām.
english: The commander-in-chief of Virata thus saw Draupadi, who possessed a lotus-like face, moving about in Sudeshna's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. The commander-in-chief of Virata thus saw Draupadi, who possessed a lotus-like face, moving about in Sudeshna's abode.

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा देवगर्भाभां चरन्तीं देवतामिव ।कीचकः कामयामास कामबाणप्रपीडितः ॥४॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā devagarbhābhāṁ carantīṁ devatāmiva ,kīcakaḥ kāmayāmāsa kāmabāṇaprapīḍitaḥ.
english: Having seen her, radiant like a celestial being and moving about like a goddess, Kīcaka, tormented by the arrows of love, desired her.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. Having seen her, radiant like a celestial being and moving about like a goddess, Kīcaka, tormented by the arrows of love, desired her.

sanskrit: स तु कामाग्निसंतप्तः सुदेष्णामभिगम्य वै ।प्रहसन्निव सेनानीरिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥५॥
transliteration: sa tu kāmāgnisaṁtaptaḥ sudeṣṇāmabhigamya vai ,prahasanniva senānīridaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Indeed, the commander (Kīcaka), tormented by the fire of love, approached Sudeshna and, as if laughing, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. Indeed, the commander (Kīcaka), tormented by the fire of love, approached Sudeshna and, as if laughing, spoke these words.

sanskrit: नेयं पुरा जातु मयेह दृष्टा राज्ञो विराटस्य निवेशने शुभा ।रूपेण चोन्मादयतीव मां भृशं गन्धेन जाता मदिरेव भामिनी ॥६॥
transliteration: neyaṁ purā jātu mayeha dṛṣṭā; rājño virāṭasya niveśane śubhā ,rūpeṇa conmādayatīva māṁ bhṛśaṁ; gandhena jātā madireva bhāminī.
english: Never before have I seen such a beautiful woman here in King Virata's palace. With her form, she truly maddens me greatly, like intoxicating wine produced from fragrance, this lovely lady.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. Never before have I seen such a beautiful woman here in King Virata's palace. With her form, she truly maddens me greatly, like intoxicating wine produced from fragrance, this lovely lady.

sanskrit: का देवरूपा हृदयंगमा शुभे आचक्ष्व मे का च कुतश्च शोभना ।चित्तं हि निर्मथ्य करोति मां वशे न चान्यदत्रौषधमद्य मे मतम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kā devarūpā hṛdayaṁgamā śubhe; ācakṣva me kā ca kutaśca śobhanā ,cittaṁ hi nirmathya karoti māṁ vaśe; na cānyadatrauṣadhamadya me matam.
english: O beautiful one (śubhe), tell me, who is this goddess-like woman (devarūpā) who captivates the heart? Who is she, and from where does this lovely woman (śobhanā) come? Indeed, she churns my mind and brings me under control, and today I consider no other remedy for this.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. O beautiful one (śubhe), tell me, who is this goddess-like woman (devarūpā) who captivates the heart? Who is she, and from where does this lovely woman (śobhanā) come? Indeed, she churns my mind and brings me under control, and today I consider no other remedy for this.

sanskrit: अहो तवेयं परिचारिका शुभा प्रत्यग्ररूपा प्रतिभाति मामियम् ।अयुक्तरूपं हि करोति कर्म ते प्रशास्तु मां यच्च ममास्ति किंचन ॥८॥
transliteration: aho taveyaṁ paricārikā śubhā; pratyagrarūpā pratibhāti māmiyam ,ayuktarūpaṁ hi karoti karma te; praśāstu māṁ yacca mamāsti kiṁcana.
english: Oh, this beautiful (śubhā) maidservant of yours, this one appears to me with such a fresh (pratyagrarūpā) form! Indeed, she performs a duty (karma) for you that seems unsuitable (ayuktarūpam) for her. Let her govern me, and (let her rule over) whatever little I possess.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. Oh, this beautiful (śubhā) maidservant of yours, this one appears to me with such a fresh (pratyagrarūpā) form! Indeed, she performs a duty (karma) for you that seems unsuitable (ayuktarūpam) for her. Let her govern me, and (let her rule over) whatever little I possess.

sanskrit: प्रभूतनागाश्वरथं महाधनं समृद्धियुक्तं बहुपानभोजनम् ।मनोहरं काञ्चनचित्रभूषणं गृहं महच्छोभयतामियं मम ॥९॥
transliteration: prabhūtanāgāśvarathaṁ mahādhanaṁ; samṛddhiyuktaṁ bahupānabhojanam ,manoharaṁ kāñcanacitrabhūṣaṇaṁ; gṛhaṁ mahacchobhayatāmiyaṁ mama.
english: Let this woman adorn my grand house (gṛham)  -  a house possessing abundant elephants, horses, and chariots (prabhūtanāgāśvaratham), immense riches (mahādhanam), endowed with prosperity (samṛddhiyuktam), plentiful drinks and food (bahupānabhojanam), captivating to the mind (manoharam), and splendidly adorned with various golden ornaments (kāñcanacitrabhuṣaṇam).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. Let this woman adorn my grand house (gṛham)  -  a house possessing abundant elephants, horses, and chariots (prabhūtanāgāśvaratham), immense riches (mahādhanam), endowed with prosperity (samṛddhiyuktam), plentiful drinks and food (bahupānabhojanam), captivating to the mind (manoharam), and splendidly adorned with various golden ornaments (kāñcanacitrabhuṣaṇam).

sanskrit: ततः सुदेष्णामनुमन्त्र्य कीचकस्ततः समभ्येत्य नराधिपात्मजाम् ।उवाच कृष्णामभिसान्त्वयंस्तदा मृगेन्द्रकन्यामिव जम्बुको वने ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sudeṣṇāmanumantrya kīcaka;stataḥ samabhyetya narādhipātmajām ,uvāca kṛṣṇāmabhisāntvayaṁstadā; mṛgendrakanyāmiva jambuko vane.
english: Then, Keechaka, having obtained permission from Sudeshna, subsequently approached the princess (narādhipātmajām) (Draupadi). At that time, he spoke to Krishna (Kṛṣṇām) (Draupadi), coaxing her, just as a jackal (jambuka) in the forest (vane) would approach a lioness's cub (mṛgendrakanyām).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. Then, Keechaka, having obtained permission from Sudeshna, subsequently approached the princess (narādhipātmajām) (Draupadi). At that time, he spoke to Krishna (Kṛṣṇām) (Draupadi), coaxing her, just as a jackal (jambuka) in the forest (vane) would approach a lioness's cub (mṛgendrakanyām).

sanskrit: इदं च रूपं प्रथमं च ते वयो निरर्थकं केवलमद्य भामिनि ।अधार्यमाणा स्रगिवोत्तमा यथा न शोभसे सुन्दरि शोभना सती ॥११॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca rūpaṁ prathamaṁ ca te vayo; nirarthakaṁ kevalamadya bhāmini ,adhāryamāṇā sragivottamā yathā; na śobhase sundari śobhanā satī.
english: O beautiful woman (bhāminī), this beauty and your youthful age are utterly useless today. Just like a most excellent garland that is not worn, you do not shine, O beautiful one (sundari), even though you are inherently beautiful.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. O beautiful woman (bhāminī), this beauty and your youthful age are utterly useless today. Just like a most excellent garland that is not worn, you do not shine, O beautiful one (sundari), even though you are inherently beautiful.

sanskrit: त्यजामि दारान्मम ये पुरातना भवन्तु दास्यस्तव चारुहासिनि ।अहं च ते सुन्दरि दासवत्स्थितः सदा भविष्ये वशगो वरानने ॥१२॥
transliteration: tyajāmi dārānmama ye purātanā; bhavantu dāsyastava cāruhāsini ,ahaṁ ca te sundari dāsavatsthitaḥ; sadā bhaviṣye vaśago varānane.
english: I will abandon my old wives; let them become your female servants, O lovely-smiling one (cāruhāsinī). And I, O beautiful one (sundari), will always remain like a servant to you, ever submissive, O lovely-faced one (varānanā).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. I will abandon my old wives; let them become your female servants, O lovely-smiling one (cāruhāsinī). And I, O beautiful one (sundari), will always remain like a servant to you, ever submissive, O lovely-faced one (varānanā).

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।अप्रार्थनीयामिह मां सूतपुत्राभिमन्यसे ।विहीनवर्णां सैरन्ध्रीं बीभत्सां केशकारिकाम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,aprārthanīyāmiha māṁ sūtaputrābhimanyase ,vihīnavarṇāṁ sairandhrīṁ bībhatsāṁ keśakārikām.
english: Draupadi said: 'O son of a charioteer (sūtputra), you consider me here to be unworthy of pursuit  -  a woman deprived of her social class (varṇa), a chambermaid (sairandhrī), repulsive, and a hairdresser.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. Draupadi said: 'O son of a charioteer (sūtputra), you consider me here to be unworthy of pursuit  -  a woman deprived of her social class (varṇa), a chambermaid (sairandhrī), repulsive, and a hairdresser.'

sanskrit: परदारास्मि भद्रं ते न युक्तं त्वयि सांप्रतम् ।दयिताः प्राणिनां दारा धर्मं समनुचिन्तय ॥१४॥
transliteration: paradārāsmi bhadraṁ te na yuktaṁ tvayi sāṁpratam ,dayitāḥ prāṇināṁ dārā dharmaṁ samanucintaya.
english: I am another's wife; good fortune be with you! This is not proper for you right now. Wives are dear to all living beings. Reflect deeply on (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. I am another's wife; good fortune be with you! This is not proper for you right now. Wives are dear to all living beings. Reflect deeply on (dharma).

sanskrit: परदारे न ते बुद्धिर्जातु कार्या कथंचन ।विवर्जनं ह्यकार्याणामेतत्सत्पुरुषव्रतम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: paradāre na te buddhirjātu kāryā kathaṁcana ,vivarjanaṁ hyakāryāṇāmetatsatpuruṣavratam.
english: Your intention (buddhi) should never, under any circumstances, be directed towards another man's wife. Indeed, refraining from improper actions is the vow (vrata) of a virtuous person.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. Your intention (buddhi) should never, under any circumstances, be directed towards another man's wife. Indeed, refraining from improper actions is the vow (vrata) of a virtuous person.

sanskrit: मिथ्याभिगृध्नो हि नरः पापात्मा मोहमास्थितः ।अयशः प्राप्नुयाद्घोरं सुमहत्प्राप्नुयाद्भयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: mithyābhigṛdhno hi naraḥ pāpātmā mohamāsthitaḥ ,ayaśaḥ prāpnuyādghoraṁ sumahatprāpnuyādbhayam.
english: Indeed, a man (nara) who is falsely greedy, having a sinful nature (pāpātmā) and entrenched in delusion (moha), will obtain terrible infamy and acquire very great fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. Indeed, a man (nara) who is falsely greedy, having a sinful nature (pāpātmā) and entrenched in delusion (moha), will obtain terrible infamy and acquire very great fear.

sanskrit: मा सूतपुत्र हृष्यस्व माद्य त्यक्ष्यसि जीवितम् ।दुर्लभामभिमन्वानो मां वीरैरभिरक्षिताम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: mā sūtaputra hṛṣyasva mādya tyakṣyasi jīvitam ,durlabhāmabhimanvāno māṁ vīrairabhirakṣitām.
english: O son of Suta, do not exult! By reveling (mādya) and presuming (abhimanvāna) to take me (mām) – me who is difficult to obtain (durlabhām) and protected (abhirakṣitām) by heroes (vīra) – you will abandon your life (jīvitam).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. O son of Suta, do not exult! By reveling (mādya) and presuming (abhimanvāna) to take me (mām) – me who is difficult to obtain (durlabhām) and protected (abhirakṣitām) by heroes (vīra) – you will abandon your life (jīvitam).

sanskrit: न चाप्यहं त्वया शक्या गन्धर्वाः पतयो मम ।ते त्वां निहन्युः कुपिताः साध्वलं मा व्यनीनशः ॥१८॥
transliteration: na cāpyahaṁ tvayā śakyā gandharvāḥ patayo mama ,te tvāṁ nihanyuḥ kupitāḥ sādhvalaṁ mā vyanīnaśaḥ.
english: Nor, indeed, can I be taken by you, for the Gandharvas are my husbands. Angered (kupita), they would surely kill you. Enough (alam) of this! Do not cause me to perish.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. Nor, indeed, can I be taken by you, for the Gandharvas are my husbands. Angered (kupita), they would surely kill you. Enough (alam) of this! Do not cause me to perish.

sanskrit: अशक्यरूपैः पुरुषैरध्वानं गन्तुमिच्छसि ।यथा निश्चेतनो बालः कूलस्थः कूलमुत्तरम् ।तर्तुमिच्छति मन्दात्मा तथा त्वं कर्तुमिच्छसि ॥१९॥
transliteration: aśakyarūpaiḥ puruṣairadhvānaṁ gantumicchasi ,yathā niścetano bālaḥ kūlasthaḥ kūlamuttaram ,tartumicchati mandātmā tathā tvaṁ kartumicchasi.
english: You desire to undertake a journey with men who are inherently incapable. It is just like a senseless child or a dull-witted person (mandātman), standing on one bank, wishing to cross to the opposite bank. Similarly, you wish to accomplish something impossible.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. You desire to undertake a journey with men who are inherently incapable. It is just like a senseless child or a dull-witted person (mandātman), standing on one bank, wishing to cross to the opposite bank. Similarly, you wish to accomplish something impossible.

sanskrit: अन्तर्महीं वा यदि वोर्ध्वमुत्पतेः समुद्रपारं यदि वा प्रधावसि ।तथापि तेषां न विमोक्षमर्हसि प्रमाथिनो देवसुता हि मे वराः ॥२०॥
transliteration: antarmahīṁ vā yadi vordhvamutpateḥ; samudrapāraṁ yadi vā pradhāvasi ,tathāpi teṣāṁ na vimokṣamarhasi; pramāthino devasutā hi me varāḥ.
english: Even if you descend deep into the earth, or fly high upwards, or flee across the ocean, you will not escape (vimokṣa) them. For my formidable divine protectors (devasutāḥ) are indeed superior.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. Even if you descend deep into the earth, or fly high upwards, or flee across the ocean, you will not escape (vimokṣa) them. For my formidable divine protectors (devasutāḥ) are indeed superior.

sanskrit: त्वं कालरात्रीमिव कश्चिदातुरः किं मां दृढं प्रार्थयसेऽद्य कीचक ।किं मातुरङ्के शयितो यथा शिशुश्चन्द्रं जिघृक्षुरिव मन्यसे हि माम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tvaṁ kālarātrīmiva kaścidāturaḥ; kiṁ māṁ dṛḍhaṁ prārthayase'dya kīcaka ,kiṁ māturaṅke śayito yathā śiśu;ścandraṁ jighṛkṣuriva manyase hi mām.
english: Kīcaka, why do you, like some sick person, so persistently entreat me, who am like the night of universal dissolution? Do you indeed consider me to be like a child, lying on its mother's lap, who wishes to grasp the moon?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 13 Verse 21. Kīcaka, why do you, like some sick person, so persistently entreat me, who am like the night of universal dissolution? Do you indeed consider me to be like a child, lying on its mother's lap, who wishes to grasp the moon?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्रत्याख्यातो राजपुत्र्या सुदेष्णां कीचकोऽब्रवीत् ।अमर्यादेन कामेन घोरेणाभिपरिप्लुतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pratyākhyāto rājaputryā sudeṣṇāṁ kīcako'bravīt ,amaryādena kāmena ghoreṇābhipariplutaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having been rejected by the princess, Kīcaka, who was overwhelmed by a terrible, boundless desire (kāma), then spoke to Sudesṇā.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having been rejected by the princess, Kīcaka, who was overwhelmed by a terrible, boundless desire (kāma), then spoke to Sudesṇā.

sanskrit: यथा कैकेयि सैरन्ध्र्या समेयां तद्विधीयताम् ।तां सुदेष्णे परीप्सस्व माहं प्राणान्प्रहासिषम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā kaikeyi sairandhryā sameyāṁ tadvidhīyatām ,tāṁ sudeṣṇe parīpsasva māhaṁ prāṇānprahāsiṣam.
english: O queen (Kaikeyī), please arrange for me to meet Sairandhrī. O Sudeshṇā, you must seek her out, lest I give up my life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. O queen (Kaikeyī), please arrange for me to meet Sairandhrī. O Sudeshṇā, you must seek her out, lest I give up my life.

sanskrit: तस्य तां बहुशः श्रुत्वा वाचं विलपतस्तदा ।विराटमहिषी देवी कृपां चक्रे मनस्विनी ॥३॥
transliteration: tasya tāṁ bahuśaḥ śrutvā vācaṁ vilapatastadā ,virāṭamahiṣī devī kṛpāṁ cakre manasvinī.
english: Having heard his lamenting words many times then, Queen Sudeshṇā, the high-minded woman, felt compassion.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. Having heard his lamenting words many times then, Queen Sudeshṇā, the high-minded woman, felt compassion.

sanskrit: स्वमर्थमभिसंधाय तस्यार्थमनुचिन्त्य च ।उद्वेगं चैव कृष्णायाः सुदेष्णा सूतमब्रवीत् ॥४॥
transliteration: svamarthamabhisaṁdhāya tasyārthamanucintya ca ,udvegaṁ caiva kṛṣṇāyāḥ sudeṣṇā sūtamabravīt.
english: Having considered her own interest, and having reflected upon his (Virāṭa's) desire, and also the agitation of Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), Sudeshṇā spoke to the charioteer (sūta).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. Having considered her own interest, and having reflected upon his (Virāṭa's) desire, and also the agitation of Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), Sudeshṇā spoke to the charioteer (sūta).

sanskrit: पर्विणीं त्वं समुद्दिश्य सुरामन्नं च कारय ।तत्रैनां प्रेषयिष्यामि सुराहारीं तवान्तिकम् ॥५॥
transliteration: parviṇīṁ tvaṁ samuddiśya surāmannaṁ ca kāraya ,tatraināṁ preṣayiṣyāmi surāhārīṁ tavāntikam.
english: You should prepare wine and food, specifically for the festival day. There, I will send her to you as one bringing wine.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. You should prepare wine and food, specifically for the festival day. There, I will send her to you as one bringing wine.

sanskrit: तत्र संप्रेषितामेनां विजने निरवग्रहाम् ।सान्त्वयेथा यथाकामं सान्त्व्यमाना रमेद्यदि ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra saṁpreṣitāmenāṁ vijane niravagrahām ,sāntvayethā yathākāmaṁ sāntvyamānā ramedyadi.
english: There, you should console her, having sent her to a secluded place where she is unrestrained, as she wishes. If she is consoled in this way, she might find pleasure.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. There, you should console her, having sent her to a secluded place where she is unrestrained, as she wishes. If she is consoled in this way, she might find pleasure.

sanskrit: कीचकस्तु गृहं गत्वा भगिन्या वचनात्तदा ।सुरामाहारयामास राजार्हां सुपरिस्रुताम् ॥७॥
transliteration: kīcakastu gṛhaṁ gatvā bhaginyā vacanāttadā ,surāmāhārayāmāsa rājārhāṁ suparisrutām.
english: Kīcaka, however, having gone home, then on his sister's command, procured wine that was fit for a king and well-filtered.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. Kīcaka, however, having gone home, then on his sister's command, procured wine that was fit for a king and well-filtered.

sanskrit: आजौरभ्रं च सुभृशं बहूंश्चोच्चावचान्मृगान् ।कारयामास कुशलैरन्नपानं सुशोभनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: ājaurabhraṁ ca subhṛśaṁ bahūṁścoccāvacānmṛgān ,kārayāmāsa kuśalairannapānaṁ suśobhanam.
english: And he had a great quantity of goat and sheep meat, along with many diverse wild animals, prepared by skilled persons. He also arranged for splendid food and drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. And he had a great quantity of goat and sheep meat, along with many diverse wild animals, prepared by skilled persons. He also arranged for splendid food and drink.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्कृते तदा देवी कीचकेनोपमन्त्रिता ।सुदेष्णा प्रेषयामास सैरन्ध्रीं कीचकालयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tasminkṛte tadā devī kīcakenopamantritā ,sudeṣṇā preṣayāmāsa sairandhrīṁ kīcakālayam.
english: When that was done, Queen Sudesṇā, having been requested by Kīcaka, then dispatched the Sairandhrī to Kīcaka's residence.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. When that was done, Queen Sudesṇā, having been requested by Kīcaka, then dispatched the Sairandhrī to Kīcaka's residence.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।उत्तिष्ठ गच्छ सैरन्ध्रि कीचकस्य निवेशनम् ।पानमानय कल्याणि पिपासा मां प्रबाधते ॥१०॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,uttiṣṭha gaccha sairandhri kīcakasya niveśanam ,pānamānaya kalyāṇi pipāsā māṁ prabādhate.
english: Sudeshṇā said, "Get up, O Sairandhrī, and go to Kīcaka's residence. O beautiful one, bring a drink; thirst torments me."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. Sudeshṇā said, "Get up, O Sairandhrī, and go to Kīcaka's residence. O beautiful one, bring a drink; thirst torments me."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।न गच्छेयमहं तस्य राजपुत्रि निवेशनम् ।त्वमेव राज्ञि जानासि यथा स निरपत्रपः ॥११॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,na gaccheyamahaṁ tasya rājaputri niveśanam ,tvameva rājñi jānāsi yathā sa nirapatrapaḥ.
english: Draupadī said, "O princess, I should not go to his residence. O queen, you yourself know how shameless he is."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. Draupadī said, "O princess, I should not go to his residence. O queen, you yourself know how shameless he is."

sanskrit: न चाहमनवद्याङ्गि तव वेश्मनि भामिनि ।कामवृत्ता भविष्यामि पतीनां व्यभिचारिणी ॥१२॥
transliteration: na cāhamanavadyāṅgi tava veśmani bhāmini ,kāmavṛttā bhaviṣyāmi patīnāṁ vyabhicāriṇī.
english: And I, O glorious one with faultless limbs, will not act as I please in your house and become unfaithful to my husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. And I, O glorious one with faultless limbs, will not act as I please in your house and become unfaithful to my husbands.

sanskrit: त्वं चैव देवि जानासि यथा स समयः कृतः ।प्रविशन्त्या मया पूर्वं तव वेश्मनि भामिनि ॥१३॥
transliteration: tvaṁ caiva devi jānāsi yathā sa samayaḥ kṛtaḥ ,praviśantyā mayā pūrvaṁ tava veśmani bhāmini.
english: And you, O queen, O glorious one, certainly know the condition that was established by me previously, when I first entered your house.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. And you, O queen, O glorious one, certainly know the condition that was established by me previously, when I first entered your house.

sanskrit: कीचकश्च सुकेशान्ते मूढो मदनदर्पितः ।सोऽवमंस्यति मां दृष्ट्वा न यास्ये तत्र शोभने ॥१४॥
transliteration: kīcakaśca sukeśānte mūḍho madanadarpitaḥ ,so'vamaṁsyati māṁ dṛṣṭvā na yāsye tatra śobhane.
english: O beautiful-haired lady, Kicaka is foolish and arrogant from passion. He will insult me upon seeing me. O lovely one, I will not go there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. O beautiful-haired lady, Kicaka is foolish and arrogant from passion. He will insult me upon seeing me. O lovely one, I will not go there.

sanskrit: सन्ति बह्व्यस्तव प्रेष्या राजपुत्रि वशानुगाः ।अन्यां प्रेषय भद्रं ते स हि मामवमंस्यते ॥१५॥
transliteration: santi bahvyastava preṣyā rājaputri vaśānugāḥ ,anyāṁ preṣaya bhadraṁ te sa hi māmavamaṁsyate.
english: O princess, you have many obedient maidservants. Please send another one; may it be well with you. For he will certainly disrespect me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. O princess, you have many obedient maidservants. Please send another one; may it be well with you. For he will certainly disrespect me.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।नैव त्वां जातु हिंस्यात्स इतः संप्रेषितां मया ॥१६॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,naiva tvāṁ jātu hiṁsyātsa itaḥ saṁpreṣitāṁ mayā.
english: Sudeshna replied, 'He would never harm you, whom I have sent from here.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. Sudeshna replied, 'He would never harm you, whom I have sent from here.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्यस्याः प्रददौ कांस्यं सपिधानं हिरण्मयम् ।सा शङ्कमाना रुदती दैवं शरणमीयुषी ।प्रातिष्ठत सुराहारी कीचकस्य निवेशनम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityasyāḥ pradadau kāṁsyaṁ sapidhānaṁ hiraṇmayam ,sā śaṅkamānā rudatī daivaṁ śaraṇamīyuṣī ,prātiṣṭhata surāhārī kīcakasya niveśanam.
english: Vaishampayana said: Thus, Sudeshna gave her a bronze vessel with a golden lid. She, apprehensive and weeping, having sought refuge in fate, set out, carrying wine and provisions, for Kicaka's residence.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. Vaishampayana said: Thus, Sudeshna gave her a bronze vessel with a golden lid. She, apprehensive and weeping, having sought refuge in fate, set out, carrying wine and provisions, for Kicaka's residence.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।यथाहमन्यं पाण्डुभ्यो नाभिजानामि कंचन ।तेन सत्येन मां प्राप्तां कीचको मा वशे कृथाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,yathāhamanyaṁ pāṇḍubhyo nābhijānāmi kaṁcana ,tena satyena māṁ prāptāṁ kīcako mā vaśe kṛthāḥ.
english: Draupadī said: "Just as I acknowledge no man other than the Pāṇḍavas, by that truth, may Kīcaka not bring me, who has been seized (by him), under his control."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 18. Draupadī said: "Just as I acknowledge no man other than the Pāṇḍavas, by that truth, may Kīcaka not bring me, who has been seized (by him), under his control."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उपातिष्ठत सा सूर्यं मुहूर्तमबला ततः ।स तस्यास्तनुमध्यायाः सर्वं सूर्योऽवबुद्धवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,upātiṣṭhata sā sūryaṁ muhūrtamabalā tataḥ ,sa tasyāstanumadhyāyāḥ sarvaṁ sūryo'vabuddhavān.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, that helpless woman (Draupadī) stood facing the sun god for a moment. That Sun god understood everything concerning that slender-waisted woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 19. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, that helpless woman (Draupadī) stood facing the sun god for a moment. That Sun god understood everything concerning that slender-waisted woman.

sanskrit: अन्तर्हितं ततस्तस्या रक्षो रक्षार्थमादिशत् ।तच्चैनां नाजहात्तत्र सर्वावस्थास्वनिन्दिताम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: antarhitaṁ tatastasyā rakṣo rakṣārthamādiśat ,taccaināṁ nājahāttatra sarvāvasthāsvaninditām.
english: Having disappeared, the Sun god then ordered a protector (yakṣa) for her defense. And that (protector) did not abandon her, the blameless one, there in all circumstances.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 20. Having disappeared, the Sun god then ordered a protector (yakṣa) for her defense. And that (protector) did not abandon her, the blameless one, there in all circumstances.

sanskrit: तां मृगीमिव वित्रस्तां दृष्ट्वा कृष्णां समीपगाम् ।उदतिष्ठन्मुदा सूतो नावं लब्ध्वेव पारगः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāṁ mṛgīmiva vitrastāṁ dṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇāṁ samīpagām ,udatiṣṭhanmudā sūto nāvaṁ labdhveva pāragaḥ.
english: Seeing her, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was terrified like a doe and approaching him, the charioteer (Kīcaka) rose with delight, just like a voyager who has found a boat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 14 Verse 21. Seeing her, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was terrified like a doe and approaching him, the charioteer (Kīcaka) rose with delight, just like a voyager who has found a boat.

sanskrit: कीचक उवाच ।स्वागतं ते सुकेशान्ते सुव्युष्टा रजनी मम ।स्वामिनी त्वमनुप्राप्ता प्रकुरुष्व मम प्रियम् ॥१॥
transliteration: kīcaka uvāca ,svāgataṁ te sukeśānte suvyuṣṭā rajanī mama ,svāminī tvamanuprāptā prakuruṣva mama priyam.
english: Kichaka said: "Welcome, O beautiful-haired woman! My night has been truly auspicious. You have come as my mistress; please grant my wish."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Kichaka said: "Welcome, O beautiful-haired woman! My night has been truly auspicious. You have come as my mistress; please grant my wish."

sanskrit: सुवर्णमालाः कम्बूश्च कुण्डले परिहाटके ।आहरन्तु च वस्त्राणि कौशिकान्यजिनानि च ॥२॥
transliteration: suvarṇamālāḥ kambūśca kuṇḍale parihāṭake ,āharantu ca vastrāṇi kauśikānyajināni ca.
english: Let them bring golden necklaces, conch shells, a pair of golden earrings, silken garments, and animal hides.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. Let them bring golden necklaces, conch shells, a pair of golden earrings, silken garments, and animal hides.

sanskrit: अस्ति मे शयनं शुभ्रं त्वदर्थमुपकल्पितम् ।एहि तत्र मया सार्धं पिबस्व मधुमाधवीम् ॥३॥
transliteration: asti me śayanaṁ śubhraṁ tvadarthamupakalpitam ,ehi tatra mayā sārdhaṁ pibasva madhumādhavīm.
english: I have a splendid bed prepared for you. Come there with me and drink the Mādhvī liquor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. I have a splendid bed prepared for you. Come there with me and drink the Mādhvī liquor.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।अप्रैषीद्राजपुत्री मां सुराहारीं तवान्तिकम् ।पानमानय मे क्षिप्रं पिपासा मेति चाब्रवीत् ॥४॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,apraiṣīdrājaputrī māṁ surāhārīṁ tavāntikam ,pānamānaya me kṣipraṁ pipāsā meti cābravīt.
english: Draupadi replied: "The princess sent me to your presence to bring her liquor. She said, 'Bring me a drink quickly, as I am thirsty.'"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. Draupadi replied: "The princess sent me to your presence to bring her liquor. She said, 'Bring me a drink quickly, as I am thirsty.'"

sanskrit: कीचक उवाच ।अन्या भद्रे नयिष्यन्ति राजपुत्र्याः परिस्रुतम् ॥५॥
transliteration: kīcaka uvāca ,anyā bhadre nayiṣyanti rājaputryāḥ parisrutam.
english: Kīcaka said, "O gentle lady, other women will carry the princess's fermented liquor."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. Kīcaka said, "O gentle lady, other women will carry the princess's fermented liquor."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्येनां दक्षिणे पाणौ सूतपुत्रः परामृशत् ।सा गृहीता विधुन्वाना भूमावाक्षिप्य कीचकम् ।सभां शरणमाधावद्यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityenāṁ dakṣiṇe pāṇau sūtaputraḥ parāmṛśat ,sā gṛhītā vidhunvānā bhūmāvākṣipya kīcakam ,sabhāṁ śaraṇamādhāvadyatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus, the son of the charioteer (Kīcaka) seized her with his right hand. Seized, and shaking herself vigorously, she threw Kīcaka to the ground and ran for refuge to the assembly hall where King Yudhiṣṭhira was.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus, the son of the charioteer (Kīcaka) seized her with his right hand. Seized, and shaking herself vigorously, she threw Kīcaka to the ground and ran for refuge to the assembly hall where King Yudhiṣṭhira was.

sanskrit: तां कीचकः प्रधावन्तीं केशपक्षे परामृशत् ।अथैनां पश्यतो राज्ञः पातयित्वा पदावधीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tāṁ kīcakaḥ pradhāvantīṁ keśapakṣe parāmṛśat ,athaināṁ paśyato rājñaḥ pātayitvā padāvadhīt.
english: Kīcaka seized her by her hair as she was running. Then, in the presence of the king, he threw her down and kicked her with his foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. Kīcaka seized her by her hair as she was running. Then, in the presence of the king, he threw her down and kicked her with his foot.

sanskrit: ततो योऽसौ तदार्केण राक्षसः संनियोजितः ।स कीचकमपोवाह वातवेगेन भारत ॥८॥
transliteration: tato yo'sau tadārkeṇa rākṣasaḥ saṁniyojitaḥ ,sa kīcakamapovāha vātavegena bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, then that demon who had been appointed by the Sun god (Arka) at that time, carried Kīcaka away with the speed of wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. O Bhārata, then that demon who had been appointed by the Sun god (Arka) at that time, carried Kīcaka away with the speed of wind.

sanskrit: स पपात ततो भूमौ रक्षोबलसमाहतः ।विघूर्णमानो निश्चेष्टश्छिन्नमूल इव द्रुमः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa papāta tato bhūmau rakṣobalasamāhataḥ ,vighūrṇamāno niśceṣṭaśchinnamūla iva drumaḥ.
english: He then fell to the ground, struck by the strength of the Rākṣasas. Reeling and motionless, he was like a tree whose roots have been severed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. He then fell to the ground, struck by the strength of the Rākṣasas. Reeling and motionless, he was like a tree whose roots have been severed.

sanskrit: तां चासीनौ ददृशतुर्भीमसेनयुधिष्ठिरौ ।अमृष्यमाणौ कृष्णायाः कीचकेन पदा वधम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tāṁ cāsīnau dadṛśaturbhīmasenayudhiṣṭhirau ,amṛṣyamāṇau kṛṣṇāyāḥ kīcakena padā vadham.
english: And Bhīmasena and Yudhiṣṭhira, both seated, saw her, unable to endure Kīcaka's assault on Kṛṣṇā with his foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. And Bhīmasena and Yudhiṣṭhira, both seated, saw her, unable to endure Kīcaka's assault on Kṛṣṇā with his foot.

sanskrit: तस्य भीमो वधप्रेप्सुः कीचकस्य दुरात्मनः ।दन्तैर्दन्तांस्तदा रोषान्निष्पिपेष महामनाः ॥११॥
transliteration: tasya bhīmo vadhaprepsuḥ kīcakasya durātmanaḥ ,dantairdantāṁstadā roṣānniṣpipeṣa mahāmanāḥ.
english: Then, the magnanimous (mahāmanas) Bhīma, eager to kill that wicked Kīcaka, gnashed his teeth in anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. Then, the magnanimous (mahāmanas) Bhīma, eager to kill that wicked Kīcaka, gnashed his teeth in anger.

sanskrit: अथाङ्गुष्ठेनावमृद्नादङ्गुष्ठं तस्य धर्मराट् ।प्रबोधनभयाद्राजन्भीमस्य प्रत्यषेधयत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: athāṅguṣṭhenāvamṛdnādaṅguṣṭhaṁ tasya dharmarāṭ ,prabodhanabhayādrājanbhīmasya pratyaṣedhayat.
english: Then, O King, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), pressed Bhīma's thumb with his own thumb and restrained him, out of fear of rousing Bhīma (prabodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. Then, O King, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), pressed Bhīma's thumb with his own thumb and restrained him, out of fear of rousing Bhīma (prabodhana).

sanskrit: सा सभाद्वारमासाद्य रुदती मत्स्यमब्रवीत् ।अवेक्षमाणा सुश्रोणी पतींस्तान्दीनचेतसः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sā sabhādvāramāsādya rudatī matsyamabravīt ,avekṣamāṇā suśroṇī patīṁstāndīnacetasaḥ.
english: Having reached the entrance of the assembly hall, the fair-hipped Draupadi, weeping, spoke to the Matsya king, while looking at her husbands who were dejected in spirit.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. Having reached the entrance of the assembly hall, the fair-hipped Draupadi, weeping, spoke to the Matsya king, while looking at her husbands who were dejected in spirit.

sanskrit: आकारमभिरक्षन्ती प्रतिज्ञां धर्मसंहिताम् ।दह्यमानेव रौद्रेण चक्षुषा द्रुपदात्मजा ॥१४॥
transliteration: ākāramabhirakṣantī pratijñāṁ dharmasaṁhitām ,dahyamāneva raudreṇa cakṣuṣā drupadātmajā.
english: Protecting her dignity and her vow (pratijñā), which was consistent with natural law (dharma), the daughter of Drupada (Draupadi) seemed to burn with a fierce gaze.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. Protecting her dignity and her vow (pratijñā), which was consistent with natural law (dharma), the daughter of Drupada (Draupadi) seemed to burn with a fierce gaze.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।येषां वैरी न स्वपिति पदा भूमिमुपस्पृशन् ।तेषां मां मानिनीं भार्यां सूतपुत्रः पदावधीत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,yeṣāṁ vairī na svapiti padā bhūmimupaspṛśan ,teṣāṁ māṁ māninīṁ bhāryāṁ sūtaputraḥ padāvadhīt.
english: Draupadi said, 'The enemy of these (my husbands) does not sleep with his foot touching the ground. Yet, the son of a charioteer has struck me, their proud wife (bhāryā), with his foot!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. Draupadi said, 'The enemy of these (my husbands) does not sleep with his foot touching the ground. Yet, the son of a charioteer has struck me, their proud wife (bhāryā), with his foot!'

sanskrit: ये दद्युर्न च याचेयुर्ब्रह्मण्याः सत्यवादिनः ।तेषां मां मानिनीं भार्यां सूतपुत्रः पदावधीत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: ye dadyurna ca yāceyurbrahmaṇyāḥ satyavādinaḥ ,teṣāṁ māṁ māninīṁ bhāryāṁ sūtaputraḥ padāvadhīt.
english: Those who would give but never beg, who are devoted to sacred duties (brahmaṇyāḥ) and are truthful (satyavādinaḥ) - their proud wife (bhāryā), me, has been struck by the son of a charioteer with his foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. Those who would give but never beg, who are devoted to sacred duties (brahmaṇyāḥ) and are truthful (satyavādinaḥ) - their proud wife (bhāryā), me, has been struck by the son of a charioteer with his foot.

sanskrit: येषां दुन्दुभिनिर्घोषो ज्याघोषः श्रूयतेऽनिशम् ।तेषां मां मानिनीं भार्यां सूतपुत्रः पदावधीत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yeṣāṁ dundubhinirghoṣo jyāghoṣaḥ śrūyate'niśam ,teṣāṁ māṁ māninīṁ bhāryāṁ sūtaputraḥ padāvadhīt.
english: The son of the charioteer (sūtaputra) kicked me, their proud wife (māninīm), me whose husbands' loud drum-roars and twangs of bowstrings are constantly heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 17. The son of the charioteer (sūtaputra) kicked me, their proud wife (māninīm), me whose husbands' loud drum-roars and twangs of bowstrings are constantly heard.

sanskrit: ये ते तेजस्विनो दान्ता बलवन्तोऽभिमानिनः ।तेषां मां मानिनीं भार्यां सूतपुत्रः पदावधीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ye te tejasvino dāntā balavanto'bhimāninaḥ ,teṣāṁ māṁ māninīṁ bhāryāṁ sūtaputraḥ padāvadhīt.
english: The son of the charioteer (sūtaputra) kicked me, their proud wife (māninīm), me whose husbands are those glorious, self-controlled, powerful, and proud men.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 18. The son of the charioteer (sūtaputra) kicked me, their proud wife (māninīm), me whose husbands are those glorious, self-controlled, powerful, and proud men.

sanskrit: सर्वलोकमिमं हन्युर्धर्मपाशसितास्तु ये ।तेषां मां मानिनीं भार्यां सूतपुत्रः पदावधीत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarvalokamimaṁ hanyurdharmapāśasitāstu ye ,teṣāṁ māṁ māninīṁ bhāryāṁ sūtaputraḥ padāvadhīt.
english: The son of the charioteer (sūtaputra) kicked me, their proud wife (māninīm), me whose husbands, even though bound by the strictures of righteousness (dharma), could destroy this entire world.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 19. The son of the charioteer (sūtaputra) kicked me, their proud wife (māninīm), me whose husbands, even though bound by the strictures of righteousness (dharma), could destroy this entire world.

sanskrit: शरणं ये प्रपन्नानां भवन्ति शरणार्थिनाम् ।चरन्ति लोके प्रच्छन्नाः क्व नु तेऽद्य महारथाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: śaraṇaṁ ye prapannānāṁ bhavanti śaraṇārthinām ,caranti loke pracchannāḥ kva nu te'dya mahārathāḥ.
english: Where indeed are those great charioteers (mahāratha) today, who become a refuge for those who have surrendered and for those seeking shelter, yet now wander hidden in the world?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 20. Where indeed are those great charioteers (mahāratha) today, who become a refuge for those who have surrendered and for those seeking shelter, yet now wander hidden in the world?

sanskrit: कथं ते सूतपुत्रेण वध्यमानां प्रियां सतीम् ।मर्षयन्ति यथा क्लीबा बलवन्तोऽमितौजसः ॥२१॥
transliteration: kathaṁ te sūtaputreṇa vadhyamānāṁ priyāṁ satīm ,marṣayanti yathā klībā balavanto'mitaujasaḥ.
english: How can they, who are mighty and immensely powerful, tolerate their dear, chaste wife being tormented by the son of a charioteer (Kīcaka), just as if they were impotent?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 21. How can they, who are mighty and immensely powerful, tolerate their dear, chaste wife being tormented by the son of a charioteer (Kīcaka), just as if they were impotent?

sanskrit: क्व नु तेषाममर्षश्च वीर्यं तेजश्च वर्तते ।न परीप्सन्ति ये भार्यां वध्यमानां दुरात्मना ॥२२॥
transliteration: kva nu teṣāmamarṣaśca vīryaṁ tejaśca vartate ,na parīpsanti ye bhāryāṁ vadhyamānāṁ durātmanā.
english: Where, then, are their indignation, valor, and prowess? Those who do not protect their wife who is being tormented by an evil-minded person.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 22. Where, then, are their indignation, valor, and prowess? Those who do not protect their wife who is being tormented by an evil-minded person.

sanskrit: मयात्र शक्यं किं कर्तुं विराटे धर्मदूषणम् ।यः पश्यन्मां मर्षयति वध्यमानामनागसम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: mayātra śakyaṁ kiṁ kartuṁ virāṭe dharmadūṣaṇam ,yaḥ paśyanmāṁ marṣayati vadhyamānāmanāgasam.
english: What can I do here in Virāṭa's kingdom regarding this transgression of natural law (dharma)? For he (King Virāṭa), while seeing me, an innocent woman, being tormented, tolerates it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 23. What can I do here in Virāṭa's kingdom regarding this transgression of natural law (dharma)? For he (King Virāṭa), while seeing me, an innocent woman, being tormented, tolerates it.

sanskrit: न राजन्राजवत्किंचित्समाचरसि कीचके ।दस्यूनामिव धर्मस्ते न हि संसदि शोभते ॥२४॥
transliteration: na rājanrājavatkiṁcitsamācarasi kīcake ,dasyūnāmiva dharmaste na hi saṁsadi śobhate.
english: O King, you do not behave at all like a king in the matter of Kīcaka. Indeed, your conduct (dharma), which is like that of robbers, does not look good in this assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 24. O King, you do not behave at all like a king in the matter of Kīcaka. Indeed, your conduct (dharma), which is like that of robbers, does not look good in this assembly.

sanskrit: न कीचकः स्वधर्मस्थो न च मत्स्यः कथंचन ।सभासदोऽप्यधर्मज्ञा य इमं पर्युपासते ॥२५॥
transliteration: na kīcakaḥ svadharmastho na ca matsyaḥ kathaṁcana ,sabhāsado'pyadharmajñā ya imaṁ paryupāsate.
english: Keechaka is not upholding his natural law (dharma), nor is Matsya (King Virata) in any way. And even the assembly members, who know what is not natural law (dharma), continue to attend upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 25. Keechaka is not upholding his natural law (dharma), nor is Matsya (King Virata) in any way. And even the assembly members, who know what is not natural law (dharma), continue to attend upon him.

sanskrit: नोपालभे त्वां नृपते विराट जनसंसदि ।नाहमेतेन युक्ता वै हन्तुं मत्स्य तवान्तिके ।सभासदस्तु पश्यन्तु कीचकस्य व्यतिक्रमम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: nopālabhe tvāṁ nṛpate virāṭa janasaṁsadi ,nāhametena yuktā vai hantuṁ matsya tavāntike ,sabhāsadastu paśyantu kīcakasya vyatikramam.
english: O King Virata, I do not reproach you in this assembly of people. Indeed, I am not fit to kill Keechaka in your presence. However, let the members of the assembly witness Keechaka's transgression.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 26. O King Virata, I do not reproach you in this assembly of people. Indeed, I am not fit to kill Keechaka in your presence. However, let the members of the assembly witness Keechaka's transgression.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।परोक्षं नाभिजानामि विग्रहं युवयोरहम् ।अर्थतत्त्वमविज्ञाय किं नु स्यात्कुशलं मम ॥२७॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,parokṣaṁ nābhijānāmi vigrahaṁ yuvayoraham ,arthatattvamavijñāya kiṁ nu syātkuśalaṁ mama.
english: Virata said: I do not know about the quarrel (vigraha) between you two that took place out of my sight. Without understanding the true nature of the matter, how can there be any well-being for me?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 27. Virata said: I do not know about the quarrel (vigraha) between you two that took place out of my sight. Without understanding the true nature of the matter, how can there be any well-being for me?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्तु सभ्या विज्ञाय कृष्णां भूयोऽभ्यपूजयन् ।साधु साध्विति चाप्याहुः कीचकं च व्यगर्हयन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatastu sabhyā vijñāya kṛṣṇāṁ bhūyo'bhyapūjayan ,sādhu sādhviti cāpyāhuḥ kīcakaṁ ca vyagarhayan.
english: Vaishampayana said: Thereupon, the assembly members, having understood the situation, further honored Krishna (Draupadi). They exclaimed, "Excellent! Excellent!" and condemned Keechaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 28. Vaishampayana said: Thereupon, the assembly members, having understood the situation, further honored Krishna (Draupadi). They exclaimed, "Excellent! Excellent!" and condemned Keechaka.

sanskrit: सभ्या ऊचुः ।यस्येयं चारुसर्वाङ्गी भार्या स्यादायतेक्षणा ।परो लाभश्च तस्य स्यान्न स शोचेत्कदाचन ॥२९॥
transliteration: sabhyā ūcuḥ ,yasyeyaṁ cārusarvāṅgī bhāryā syādāyatekṣaṇā ,paro lābhaśca tasya syānna sa śocetkadācana.
english: The courtiers said, "The man who has this beautiful woman with lovely limbs and long eyes as his wife would achieve a supreme gain and would never grieve at all."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 29. The courtiers said, "The man who has this beautiful woman with lovely limbs and long eyes as his wife would achieve a supreme gain and would never grieve at all."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं संपूजयंस्तत्र कृष्णां प्रेक्ष्य सभासदः ।युधिष्ठिरस्य कोपात्तु ललाटे स्वेद आसजत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ saṁpūjayaṁstatra kṛṣṇāṁ prekṣya sabhāsadaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirasya kopāttu lalāṭe sveda āsajat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "As the members of the assembly praised Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) upon seeing her there, sweat appeared on Yudhiṣṭhira's forehead, born of anger."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 30. Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "As the members of the assembly praised Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) upon seeing her there, sweat appeared on Yudhiṣṭhira's forehead, born of anger."

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्राजपुत्रीं कौरव्यो महिषीं प्रियाम् ।गच्छ सैरन्ध्रि मात्र स्थाः सुदेष्णाया निवेशनम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: athābravīdrājaputrīṁ kauravyo mahiṣīṁ priyām ,gaccha sairandhri mātra sthāḥ sudeṣṇāyā niveśanam.
english: Then Kīcaka, who was like a Kuru prince, spoke to the princess (Draupadī) and his dear queen (Sudesnā): 'Go, Sairandhrī! Do not remain here. Go to Sudesnā's residence!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 31. Then Kīcaka, who was like a Kuru prince, spoke to the princess (Draupadī) and his dear queen (Sudesnā): 'Go, Sairandhrī! Do not remain here. Go to Sudesnā's residence!'

sanskrit: भर्तारमनुरुध्यन्त्यः क्लिश्यन्ते वीरपत्नयः ।शुश्रूषया क्लिश्यमानाः पतिलोकं जयन्त्युत ॥३२॥
transliteration: bhartāramanurudhyantyaḥ kliśyante vīrapatnayaḥ ,śuśrūṣayā kliśyamānāḥ patilokaṁ jayantyuta.
english: Wives of heroes, devotedly following their husbands, endure hardship. Moreover, by suffering through their service, they conquer the celestial realm of their husbands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 32. Wives of heroes, devotedly following their husbands, endure hardship. Moreover, by suffering through their service, they conquer the celestial realm of their husbands.

sanskrit: मन्ये न कालं क्रोधस्य पश्यन्ति पतयस्तव ।तेन त्वां नाभिधावन्ति गन्धर्वाः सूर्यवर्चसः ॥३३॥
transliteration: manye na kālaṁ krodhasya paśyanti patayastava ,tena tvāṁ nābhidhāvanti gandharvāḥ sūryavarcasaḥ.
english: I believe that my husbands (patayaḥ) – your masters – do not yet perceive the opportune moment for their anger (krodha). Therefore, the Gandharvas, resplendent like the sun, are not rushing to punish you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 33. I believe that my husbands (patayaḥ) – your masters – do not yet perceive the opportune moment for their anger (krodha). Therefore, the Gandharvas, resplendent like the sun, are not rushing to punish you.

sanskrit: अकालज्ञासि सैरन्ध्रि शैलूषीव विधावसि ।विघ्नं करोषि मत्स्यानां दीव्यतां राजसंसदि ।गच्छ सैरन्ध्रि गन्धर्वाः करिष्यन्ति तव प्रियम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: akālajñāsi sairandhri śailūṣīva vidhāvasi ,vighnaṁ karoṣi matsyānāṁ dīvyatāṁ rājasaṁsadi ,gaccha sairandhri gandharvāḥ kariṣyanti tava priyam.
english: You are ignorant of the proper time (akālajñā), O Sairandhrī; you roam about like an actress (śailūṣī). You are causing a disturbance for the Matsyas who are gambling in the royal assembly. Go, O Sairandhrī! The Gandharvas will certainly do you a favor (priyam).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 34. You are ignorant of the proper time (akālajñā), O Sairandhrī; you roam about like an actress (śailūṣī). You are causing a disturbance for the Matsyas who are gambling in the royal assembly. Go, O Sairandhrī! The Gandharvas will certainly do you a favor (priyam).

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।अतीव तेषां घृणिनामर्थेऽहं धर्मचारिणी ।तस्य तस्येह ते वध्या येषां ज्येष्ठोऽक्षदेविता ॥३५॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,atīva teṣāṁ ghṛṇināmarthe'haṁ dharmacāriṇī ,tasya tasyeha te vadhyā yeṣāṁ jyeṣṭho'kṣadevitā.
english: Draupadī said: 'I, a woman upholding her sacred duty (dharma), endure greatly for the sake of those pitiable ones (my husbands). Here in this world, those who harass me are certainly destined for death by those whose eldest (brother) is a master of dice (akṣadevitā).'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 35. Draupadī said: 'I, a woman upholding her sacred duty (dharma), endure greatly for the sake of those pitiable ones (my husbands). Here in this world, those who harass me are certainly destined for death by those whose eldest (brother) is a master of dice (akṣadevitā).'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा प्राद्रवत्कृष्णा सुदेष्णाया निवेशनम् ।केशान्मुक्त्वा तु सुश्रोणी संरम्भाल्लोहितेक्षणा ॥३६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā prādravatkṛṣṇā sudeṣṇāyā niveśanam ,keśānmuktvā tu suśroṇī saṁrambhāllohitekṣaṇā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having thus spoken, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) fled to Sudēṣṇā's residence. The beautiful-hipped (suśroṇī) Draupadī, with her hair loosened and her eyes red with rage (saṃrambha), indeed (fled).'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 36. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having thus spoken, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) fled to Sudēṣṇā's residence. The beautiful-hipped (suśroṇī) Draupadī, with her hair loosened and her eyes red with rage (saṃrambha), indeed (fled).'

sanskrit: शुशुभे वदनं तस्या रुदन्त्या विरतं तदा ।मेघलेखाविनिर्मुक्तं दिवीव शशिमण्डलम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: śuśubhe vadanaṁ tasyā rudantyā virataṁ tadā ,meghalekhāvinirmuktaṁ divīva śaśimaṇḍalam.
english: Her face, having stopped crying, then shone like the moon's orb freed from a streak of clouds in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 37. Her face, having stopped crying, then shone like the moon's orb freed from a streak of clouds in the sky.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।कस्त्वावधीद्वरारोहे कस्माद्रोदिषि शोभने ।कस्याद्य न सुखं भद्रे केन ते विप्रियं कृतम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,kastvāvadhīdvarārohe kasmādrodiṣi śobhane ,kasyādya na sukhaṁ bhadre kena te vipriyaṁ kṛtam.
english: Sudeshṇā asked, "Who killed you, O woman with beautiful thighs? Why are you crying, O beautiful one? Who today lacks happiness, O gracious one? By whom has something unpleasant been done to you?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 38. Sudeshṇā asked, "Who killed you, O woman with beautiful thighs? Why are you crying, O beautiful one? Who today lacks happiness, O gracious one? By whom has something unpleasant been done to you?"

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।कीचको मावधीत्तत्र सुराहारीं गतां तव ।सभायां पश्यतो राज्ञो यथैव विजने तथा ॥३९॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,kīcako māvadhīttatra surāhārīṁ gatāṁ tava ,sabhāyāṁ paśyato rājño yathaiva vijane tathā.
english: Draupadī said, "Kīcaka killed me there when I had gone to bring wine for you. He did this in the assembly hall, while the king watched, just as if it were a deserted place."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 39. Draupadī said, "Kīcaka killed me there when I had gone to bring wine for you. He did this in the assembly hall, while the king watched, just as if it were a deserted place."

sanskrit: सुदेष्णोवाच ।घातयामि सुकेशान्ते कीचकं यदि मन्यसे ।योऽसौ त्वां कामसंमत्तो दुर्लभामभिमन्यते ॥४०॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇovāca ,ghātayāmi sukeśānte kīcakaṁ yadi manyase ,yo'sau tvāṁ kāmasaṁmatto durlabhāmabhimanyate.
english: Sudeshṇā said, "O woman with beautiful hair, I will have Kīcaka killed if you agree, that man who, maddened by lust, considers you, who are unattainable, to be his."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 40. Sudeshṇā said, "O woman with beautiful hair, I will have Kīcaka killed if you agree, that man who, maddened by lust, considers you, who are unattainable, to be his."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।अन्ये वै तं वधिष्यन्ति येषामागः करोति सः ।मन्ये चाद्यैव सुव्यक्तं परलोकं गमिष्यति ॥४१॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,anye vai taṁ vadhiṣyanti yeṣāmāgaḥ karoti saḥ ,manye cādyaiva suvyaktaṁ paralokaṁ gamiṣyati.
english: Draupadi said: "Indeed, others will kill him - those against whom he commits offenses. And I believe that he will very clearly go to the other world today itself."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 15 Verse 41. Draupadi said: "Indeed, others will kill him - those against whom he commits offenses. And I believe that he will very clearly go to the other world today itself."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सा हता सूतपुत्रेण राजपुत्री समज्वलत् ।वधं कृष्णा परीप्सन्ती सेनावाहस्य भामिनी ।जगामावासमेवाथ तदा सा द्रुपदात्मजा ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sā hatā sūtaputreṇa rājaputrī samajvalat ,vadhaṁ kṛṣṇā parīpsantī senāvāhasya bhāminī ,jagāmāvāsamevātha tadā sā drupadātmajā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "She, the princess, having been insulted by the son of a charioteer (Karṇa), blazed up in rage. Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā), the passionate woman, seeking the death of the army commander, then went directly to her dwelling."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "She, the princess, having been insulted by the son of a charioteer (Karṇa), blazed up in rage. Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā), the passionate woman, seeking the death of the army commander, then went directly to her dwelling."

sanskrit: कृत्वा शौचं यथान्यायं कृष्णा वै तनुमध्यमा ।गात्राणि वाससी चैव प्रक्षाल्य सलिलेन सा ॥२॥
transliteration: kṛtvā śaucaṁ yathānyāyaṁ kṛṣṇā vai tanumadhyamā ,gātrāṇi vāsasī caiva prakṣālya salilena sā.
english: Having performed the purification (śauca) ritual properly, Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā), the slender-waisted woman, then indeed washed her limbs and her two garments with water.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. Having performed the purification (śauca) ritual properly, Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā), the slender-waisted woman, then indeed washed her limbs and her two garments with water.

sanskrit: चिन्तयामास रुदती तस्य दुःखस्य निर्णयम् ।किं करोमि क्व गच्छामि कथं कार्यं भवेन्मम ॥३॥
transliteration: cintayāmāsa rudatī tasya duḥkhasya nirṇayam ,kiṁ karomi kva gacchāmi kathaṁ kāryaṁ bhavenmama.
english: Weeping, she pondered a resolution for that suffering: "What should I do? Where should I go? How can my task be accomplished?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. Weeping, she pondered a resolution for that suffering: "What should I do? Where should I go? How can my task be accomplished?"

sanskrit: इत्येवं चिन्तयित्वा सा भीमं वै मनसागमत् ।नान्यः कर्ता ऋते भीमान्ममाद्य मनसः प्रियम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ cintayitvā sā bhīmaṁ vai manasāgamat ,nānyaḥ kartā ṛte bhīmānmamādya manasaḥ priyam.
english: Having thus reflected, she went to Bhīma in her mind. No one else, apart from Bhīma, could accomplish what is dear to my heart today.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. Having thus reflected, she went to Bhīma in her mind. No one else, apart from Bhīma, could accomplish what is dear to my heart today.

sanskrit: तत उत्थाय रात्रौ सा विहाय शयनं स्वकम् ।प्राद्रवन्नाथमिच्छन्ती कृष्णा नाथवती सती ।दुःखेन महता युक्ता मानसेन मनस्विनी ॥५॥
transliteration: tata utthāya rātrau sā vihāya śayanaṁ svakam ,prādravannāthamicchantī kṛṣṇā nāthavatī satī ,duḥkhena mahatā yuktā mānasena manasvinī.
english: Then, having risen at night, she (Draupadī), leaving her own bed, ran, desiring her protector. Kṛṣṇā, a chaste woman (satī) who had a protector, was afflicted by great mental (mānasa) sorrow, though she was a high-minded woman (manasvinī).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. Then, having risen at night, she (Draupadī), leaving her own bed, ran, desiring her protector. Kṛṣṇā, a chaste woman (satī) who had a protector, was afflicted by great mental (mānasa) sorrow, though she was a high-minded woman (manasvinī).

sanskrit: सा वै महानसे प्राप्य भीमसेनं शुचिस्मिता ।सर्वश्वेतेव माहेयी वने जाता त्रिहायनी ।उपातिष्ठत पाञ्चाली वाशितेव महागजम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sā vai mahānase prāpya bhīmasenaṁ śucismitā ,sarvaśveteva māheyī vane jātā trihāyanī ,upātiṣṭhata pāñcālī vāśiteva mahāgajam.
english: She of the pure smile, Pāñcālī (Draupadī), having found Bhīmasena in the kitchen, approached him. She was like a completely white, three-year-old female buffalo (māheyī) born in the forest, and she approached him like a female elephant in heat (vāśitā) approaches a great elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. She of the pure smile, Pāñcālī (Draupadī), having found Bhīmasena in the kitchen, approached him. She was like a completely white, three-year-old female buffalo (māheyī) born in the forest, and she approached him like a female elephant in heat (vāśitā) approaches a great elephant.

sanskrit: सा लतेव महाशालं फुल्लं गोमतितीरजम् ।बाहुभ्यां परिरभ्यैनं प्राबोधयदनिन्दिता ।सिंहं सुप्तं वने दुर्गे मृगराजवधूरिव ॥७॥
transliteration: sā lateva mahāśālaṁ phullaṁ gomatitīrajam ,bāhubhyāṁ parirabhyainaṁ prābodhayadaninditā ,siṁhaṁ suptaṁ vane durge mṛgarājavadhūriva.
english: The blameless one (aninditā), embracing him with both arms, awakened him. She was like a blooming creeper (latā) clinging to a mighty sāla tree (mahāśālam) on the bank of the Gomati river, and she awakened him just as a lioness (mṛgarājavadhūḥ) awakens a sleeping lion in a formidable forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. The blameless one (aninditā), embracing him with both arms, awakened him. She was like a blooming creeper (latā) clinging to a mighty sāla tree (mahāśālam) on the bank of the Gomati river, and she awakened him just as a lioness (mṛgarājavadhūḥ) awakens a sleeping lion in a formidable forest.

sanskrit: वीणेव मधुराभाषा गान्धारं साधु मूर्च्छिता ।अभ्यभाषत पाञ्चाली भीमसेनमनिन्दिता ॥८॥
transliteration: vīṇeva madhurābhāṣā gāndhāraṁ sādhu mūrcchitā ,abhyabhāṣata pāñcālī bhīmasenamaninditā.
english: The blameless Pāñcālī (Draupadī), whose sweet speech was skillfully modulated to the Gāndhāra note like a lute (vīṇā), addressed Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. The blameless Pāñcālī (Draupadī), whose sweet speech was skillfully modulated to the Gāndhāra note like a lute (vīṇā), addressed Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ किं शेषे भीमसेन यथा मृतः ।नामृतस्य हि पापीयान्भार्यामालभ्य जीवति ॥९॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha kiṁ śeṣe bhīmasena yathā mṛtaḥ ,nāmṛtasya hi pāpīyānbhāryāmālabhya jīvati.
english: “Arise! Arise! Why do you lie here like a dead man, Bhīmasena? Indeed, no most wicked man lives after having seized a wife belonging to one who is still alive.”
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. “Arise! Arise! Why do you lie here like a dead man, Bhīmasena? Indeed, no most wicked man lives after having seized a wife belonging to one who is still alive.”

sanskrit: तस्मिञ्जीवति पापिष्ठे सेनावाहे मम द्विषि ।तत्कर्म कृतवत्यद्य कथं निद्रां निषेवसे ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasmiñjīvati pāpiṣṭhe senāvāhe mama dviṣi ,tatkarma kṛtavatyadya kathaṁ nidrāṁ niṣevase.
english: “My enemy, that most wicked leader of the army, still lives after having committed such an act today. How can you indulge in sleep?”
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. “My enemy, that most wicked leader of the army, still lives after having committed such an act today. How can you indulge in sleep?”

sanskrit: स संप्रहाय शयनं राजपुत्र्या प्रबोधितः ।उपातिष्ठत मेघाभः पर्यङ्के सोपसंग्रहे ॥११॥
transliteration: sa saṁprahāya śayanaṁ rājaputryā prabodhitaḥ ,upātiṣṭhata meghābhaḥ paryaṅke sopasaṁgrahe.
english: He, roused by the princess, gave up his sleep. Resembling a cloud, he stood up on the bed with its coverings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. He, roused by the princess, gave up his sleep. Resembling a cloud, he stood up on the bed with its coverings.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्राजपुत्रीं कौरव्यो महिषीं प्रियाम् ।केनास्यर्थेन संप्राप्ता त्वरितेव ममान्तिकम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: athābravīdrājaputrīṁ kauravyo mahiṣīṁ priyām ,kenāsyarthena saṁprāptā tvariteva mamāntikam.
english: The Kuru ruler then spoke to his dear queen, the princess: "For what purpose have you arrived so hurriedly in my presence?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. The Kuru ruler then spoke to his dear queen, the princess: "For what purpose have you arrived so hurriedly in my presence?"

sanskrit: न ते प्रकृतिमान्वर्णः कृशा पाण्डुश्च लक्ष्यसे ।आचक्ष्व परिशेषेण सर्वं विद्यामहं यथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: na te prakṛtimānvarṇaḥ kṛśā pāṇḍuśca lakṣyase ,ācakṣva pariśeṣeṇa sarvaṁ vidyāmahaṁ yathā.
english: Your complexion is not normal; you appear thin and pale. Explain everything in full detail so that I may understand it completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. Your complexion is not normal; you appear thin and pale. Explain everything in full detail so that I may understand it completely.

sanskrit: सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं द्वेष्यं वा यदि वा प्रियम् ।यथावत्सर्वमाचक्ष्व श्रुत्वा ज्ञास्यामि यत्परम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: sukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ dveṣyaṁ vā yadi vā priyam ,yathāvatsarvamācakṣva śrutvā jñāsyāmi yatparam.
english: Whether it is happiness or sorrow, something unpleasant or something dear, tell me everything exactly as it is. After hearing it, I will understand what is most important.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. Whether it is happiness or sorrow, something unpleasant or something dear, tell me everything exactly as it is. After hearing it, I will understand what is most important.

sanskrit: अहमेव हि ते कृष्णे विश्वास्यः सर्वकर्मसु ।अहमापत्सु चापि त्वां मोक्षयामि पुनः पुनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ahameva hi te kṛṣṇe viśvāsyaḥ sarvakarmasu ,ahamāpatsu cāpi tvāṁ mokṣayāmi punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Indeed, O Kṛṣṇā, I alone am trustworthy for you in all matters. And I will certainly rescue you again and again from calamities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. Indeed, O Kṛṣṇā, I alone am trustworthy for you in all matters. And I will certainly rescue you again and again from calamities.

sanskrit: शीघ्रमुक्त्वा यथाकामं यत्ते कार्यं विवक्षितम् ।गच्छ वै शयनायैव पुरा नान्योऽवबुध्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: śīghramuktvā yathākāmaṁ yatte kāryaṁ vivakṣitam ,gaccha vai śayanāyaiva purā nānyo'vabudhyate.
english: Quickly state whatever task you wish to be done, as you desire. Then go to sleep, before anyone else becomes aware.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. Quickly state whatever task you wish to be done, as you desire. Then go to sleep, before anyone else becomes aware.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।अशोच्यं नु कुतस्तस्या यस्या भर्ता युधिष्ठिरः ।जानन्सर्वाणि दुःखानि किं मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ॥१॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,aśocyaṁ nu kutastasyā yasyā bhartā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,jānansarvāṇi duḥkhāni kiṁ māṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi.
