english: Draupadī said, 'How can she not be an object of sorrow, she whose husband is Yudhiṣṭhira? You know all these sufferings; why do you question me?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. Draupadī said, 'How can she not be an object of sorrow, she whose husband is Yudhiṣṭhira? You know all these sufferings; why do you question me?'

sanskrit: यन्मां दासीप्रवादेन प्रातिकामी तदानयत् ।सभायां पार्षदो मध्ये तन्मां दहति भारत ॥२॥
transliteration: yanmāṁ dāsīpravādena prātikāmī tadānayat ,sabhāyāṁ pārṣado madhye tanmāṁ dahati bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bhārata, that incident, when Prātīkāmī brought me there on the pretext of a slave, in the midst of the counselors in the assembly, that still burns me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. O descendant of Bhārata, that incident, when Prātīkāmī brought me there on the pretext of a slave, in the midst of the counselors in the assembly, that still burns me.

sanskrit: पार्थिवस्य सुता नाम का नु जीवेत मादृशी ।अनुभूय भृशं दुःखमन्यत्र द्रौपदीं प्रभो ॥३॥
transliteration: pārthivasya sutā nāma kā nu jīveta mādṛśī ,anubhūya bhṛśaṁ duḥkhamanyatra draupadīṁ prabho.
english: O Lord, what daughter of a king (a noble lady) like me, could possibly live after having experienced such intense suffering, other than Draupadī herself?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. O Lord, what daughter of a king (a noble lady) like me, could possibly live after having experienced such intense suffering, other than Draupadī herself?

sanskrit: वनवासगतायाश्च सैन्धवेन दुरात्मना ।परामर्शं द्वितीयं च सोढुमुत्सहते नु का ॥४॥
transliteration: vanavāsagatāyāśca saindhavena durātmanā ,parāmarśaṁ dvitīyaṁ ca soḍhumutsahate nu kā.
english: Who indeed, having been exiled to the forest, would be able to tolerate a second assault by that wicked Saṃdhava?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. Who indeed, having been exiled to the forest, would be able to tolerate a second assault by that wicked Saṃdhava?

sanskrit: मत्स्यराज्ञः समक्षं च तस्य धूर्तस्य पश्यतः ।कीचकेन पदा स्पृष्टा का नु जीवेत मादृशी ॥५॥
transliteration: matsyarājñaḥ samakṣaṁ ca tasya dhūrtasya paśyataḥ ,kīcakena padā spṛṣṭā kā nu jīveta mādṛśī.
english: Who like me, having been kicked by Kīcaka with his foot, in the presence of the Matsya king and while that scoundrel himself was looking on, would indeed be able to live?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. Who like me, having been kicked by Kīcaka with his foot, in the presence of the Matsya king and while that scoundrel himself was looking on, would indeed be able to live?

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधैः क्लेशैः क्लिश्यमानां च भारत ।न मां जानासि कौन्तेय किं फलं जीवितेन मे ॥६॥
transliteration: evaṁ bahuvidhaiḥ kleśaiḥ kliśyamānāṁ ca bhārata ,na māṁ jānāsi kaunteya kiṁ phalaṁ jīvitena me.
english: O Bhārata, O son of Kuntī, you do not truly know me as I am thus tormented by many sorts of afflictions. What benefit is there in life for me?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. O Bhārata, O son of Kuntī, you do not truly know me as I am thus tormented by many sorts of afflictions. What benefit is there in life for me?

sanskrit: योऽयं राज्ञो विराटस्य कीचको नाम भारत ।सेनानीः पुरुषव्याघ्र स्यालः परमदुर्मतिः ॥७॥
transliteration: yo'yaṁ rājño virāṭasya kīcako nāma bhārata ,senānīḥ puruṣavyāghra syālaḥ paramadurmatiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, O tiger among men, this person named Kīcaka, who is King Virāṭa's army commander and his brother-in-law, is utterly evil-minded.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. O Bhārata, O tiger among men, this person named Kīcaka, who is King Virāṭa's army commander and his brother-in-law, is utterly evil-minded.

sanskrit: स मां सैरन्ध्रिवेषेण वसन्तीं राजवेश्मनि ।नित्यमेवाह दुष्टात्मा भार्या मम भवेति वै ॥८॥
transliteration: sa māṁ sairandhriveṣeṇa vasantīṁ rājaveśmani ,nityamevāha duṣṭātmā bhāryā mama bhaveti vai.
english: That wicked soul, seeing me living in the king's palace in the guise of a female attendant, always said to me, "Be my wife!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. That wicked soul, seeing me living in the king's palace in the guise of a female attendant, always said to me, "Be my wife!"

sanskrit: तेनोपमन्त्र्यमाणाया वधार्हेण सपत्नहन् ।कालेनेव फलं पक्वं हृदयं मे विदीर्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: tenopamantryamāṇāyā vadhārheṇa sapatnahan ,kāleneva phalaṁ pakvaṁ hṛdayaṁ me vidīryate.
english: O slayer of enemies, my heart is breaking apart like a ripened fruit by time, due to being solicited by that one who deserves death.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. O slayer of enemies, my heart is breaking apart like a ripened fruit by time, due to being solicited by that one who deserves death.

sanskrit: भ्रातरं च विगर्हस्व ज्येष्ठं दुर्द्यूतदेविनम् ।यस्यास्मि कर्मणा प्राप्ता दुःखमेतदनन्तकम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhrātaraṁ ca vigarhasva jyeṣṭhaṁ durdyūtadevinam ,yasyāsmi karmaṇā prāptā duḥkhametadanantakam.
english: And you should censure your eldest brother, the one addicted to disastrous gambling, by whose action (karma) I have received this endless suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. And you should censure your eldest brother, the one addicted to disastrous gambling, by whose action (karma) I have received this endless suffering.

sanskrit: को हि राज्यं परित्यज्य सर्वस्वं चात्मना सह ।प्रव्रज्यायैव दीव्येत विना दुर्द्यूतदेविनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: ko hi rājyaṁ parityajya sarvasvaṁ cātmanā saha ,pravrajyāyaiva dīvyeta vinā durdyūtadevinam.
english: Who indeed, after abandoning his kingdom and all his possessions along with himself, would gamble (or stake) for the sake of renunciation (pravrajyā) alone, unless he were a disastrous gambler?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. Who indeed, after abandoning his kingdom and all his possessions along with himself, would gamble (or stake) for the sake of renunciation (pravrajyā) alone, unless he were a disastrous gambler?

sanskrit: यदि निष्कसहस्रेण यच्चान्यत्सारवद्धनम् ।सायंप्रातरदेविष्यदपि संवत्सरान्बहून् ॥१२॥
transliteration: yadi niṣkasahasreṇa yaccānyatsāravaddhanam ,sāyaṁprātaradeviṣyadapi saṁvatsarānbahūn.
english: Even if one were to gamble away a thousand gold coins (niṣka) and other substantial wealth, morning and evening, for many years, (it would still represent an immense fortune).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. Even if one were to gamble away a thousand gold coins (niṣka) and other substantial wealth, morning and evening, for many years, (it would still represent an immense fortune).

sanskrit: रुक्मं हिरण्यं वासांसि यानं युग्यमजाविकम् ।अश्वाश्वतरसंघांश्च न जातु क्षयमावहेत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: rukmaṁ hiraṇyaṁ vāsāṁsi yānaṁ yugyamajāvikam ,aśvāśvatarasaṁghāṁśca na jātu kṣayamāvahet.
english: Gold, riches, garments, conveyances, draught animals, goats and sheep, and herds of horses and mules – such (a vast fortune) would never suffer diminution (kṣaya).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. Gold, riches, garments, conveyances, draught animals, goats and sheep, and herds of horses and mules – such (a vast fortune) would never suffer diminution (kṣaya).

sanskrit: सोऽयं द्यूतप्रवादेन श्रिया प्रत्यवरोपितः ।तूष्णीमास्ते यथा मूढः स्वानि कर्माणि चिन्तयन् ॥१४॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ dyūtapravādena śriyā pratyavaropitaḥ ,tūṣṇīmāste yathā mūḍhaḥ svāni karmāṇi cintayan.
english: He, this very person, having been deprived of his fortune by means of gambling, sits silently like a fool, pondering his own deeds (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. He, this very person, having been deprived of his fortune by means of gambling, sits silently like a fool, pondering his own deeds (karma).

sanskrit: दश नागसहस्राणि पद्मिनां हेममालिनाम् ।यं यान्तमनुयान्तीह सोऽयं द्यूतेन जीवति ॥१५॥
transliteration: daśa nāgasahasrāṇi padmināṁ hemamālinām ,yaṁ yāntamanuyāntīha so'yaṁ dyūtena jīvati.
english: He whom ten thousand elephants, adorned with lotuses and golden garlands, used to follow as he moved about on earth – this very person now subsists by gambling.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. He whom ten thousand elephants, adorned with lotuses and golden garlands, used to follow as he moved about on earth – this very person now subsists by gambling.

sanskrit: तथा शतसहस्राणि नृणाममिततेजसाम् ।उपासते महाराजमिन्द्रप्रस्थे युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tathā śatasahasrāṇi nṛṇāmamitatejasām ,upāsate mahārājamindraprasthe yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Similarly, hundreds of thousands of men of immeasurable valor serve the great king Yudhishthira in Indraprastha.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. Similarly, hundreds of thousands of men of immeasurable valor serve the great king Yudhishthira in Indraprastha.

sanskrit: शतं दासीसहस्राणि यस्य नित्यं महानसे ।पात्रीहस्तं दिवारात्रमतिथीन्भोजयन्त्युत ॥१७॥
transliteration: śataṁ dāsīsahasrāṇi yasya nityaṁ mahānase ,pātrīhastaṁ divārātramatithīnbhojayantyuta.
english: And a hundred thousands of maidservants, who constantly serve in his great kitchen, feed guests day and night, holding dishes in their hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. And a hundred thousands of maidservants, who constantly serve in his great kitchen, feed guests day and night, holding dishes in their hands.

sanskrit: एष निष्कसहस्राणि प्रदाय ददतां वरः ।द्यूतजेन ह्यनर्थेन महता समुपावृतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: eṣa niṣkasahasrāṇi pradāya dadatāṁ varaḥ ,dyūtajena hyanarthena mahatā samupāvṛtaḥ.
english: This one, the best among givers, having given thousands of gold coins, was indeed afflicted by a great misfortune (anartha) born of gambling.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. This one, the best among givers, having given thousands of gold coins, was indeed afflicted by a great misfortune (anartha) born of gambling.

sanskrit: एनं हि स्वरसंपन्ना बहवः सूतमागधाः ।सायंप्रातरुपातिष्ठन्सुमृष्टमणिकुण्डलाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: enaṁ hi svarasaṁpannā bahavaḥ sūtamāgadhāḥ ,sāyaṁprātarupātiṣṭhansumṛṣṭamaṇikuṇḍalāḥ.
english: Indeed, many bards and panegyrists, endowed with pleasant voices and adorned with shining jeweled earrings, attended him evening and morning.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. Indeed, many bards and panegyrists, endowed with pleasant voices and adorned with shining jeweled earrings, attended him evening and morning.

sanskrit: सहस्रमृषयो यस्य नित्यमासन्सभासदः ।तपःश्रुतोपसंपन्नाः सर्वकामैरुपस्थिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: sahasramṛṣayo yasya nityamāsansabhāsadaḥ ,tapaḥśrutopasaṁpannāḥ sarvakāmairupasthitāḥ.
english: A thousand sages, endowed with asceticism (tapas) and scriptural knowledge, were always members of his assembly, and they were provided with all their desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. A thousand sages, endowed with asceticism (tapas) and scriptural knowledge, were always members of his assembly, and they were provided with all their desires.

sanskrit: अन्धान्वृद्धांस्तथानाथान्सर्वान्राष्ट्रेषु दुर्गतान् ।बिभर्त्यविमना नित्यमानृशंस्याद्युधिष्ठिरः ॥२१॥
transliteration: andhānvṛddhāṁstathānāthānsarvānrāṣṭreṣu durgatān ,bibhartyavimanā nityamānṛśaṁsyādyudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Always with an untroubled mind, Yudhishthira constantly supports all the blind, the old, the helpless, and those in distress throughout his kingdoms, out of compassion.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. Always with an untroubled mind, Yudhishthira constantly supports all the blind, the old, the helpless, and those in distress throughout his kingdoms, out of compassion.

sanskrit: स एष निरयं प्राप्तो मत्स्यस्य परिचारकः ।सभायां देविता राज्ञः कङ्को ब्रूते युधिष्ठिरः ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa eṣa nirayaṁ prāpto matsyasya paricārakaḥ ,sabhāyāṁ devitā rājñaḥ kaṅko brūte yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: He, this very Yudhishthira, having reached such a miserable state, is now a servant of King Matsya. In the assembly, he speaks as Kanka, the king's dice-player.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. He, this very Yudhishthira, having reached such a miserable state, is now a servant of King Matsya. In the assembly, he speaks as Kanka, the king's dice-player.

sanskrit: इन्द्रप्रस्थे निवसतः समये यस्य पार्थिवाः ।आसन्बलिभृतः सर्वे सोऽद्यान्यैर्भृतिमिच्छति ॥२३॥
transliteration: indraprasthe nivasataḥ samaye yasya pārthivāḥ ,āsanbalibhṛtaḥ sarve so'dyānyairbhṛtimicchati.
english: In the time when he was dwelling in Indraprastha, all kings were his tributaries. Today, that very person desires maintenance from others.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. In the time when he was dwelling in Indraprastha, all kings were his tributaries. Today, that very person desires maintenance from others.

sanskrit: पार्थिवाः पृथिवीपाला यस्यासन्वशवर्तिनः ।स वशे विवशो राजा परेषामद्य वर्तते ॥२४॥
transliteration: pārthivāḥ pṛthivīpālā yasyāsanvaśavartinaḥ ,sa vaśe vivaśo rājā pareṣāmadya vartate.
english: Earthly rulers, protectors of the earth, were once subservient to him. Now, that very king, helpless, is under the control of others today.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. Earthly rulers, protectors of the earth, were once subservient to him. Now, that very king, helpless, is under the control of others today.

sanskrit: प्रताप्य पृथिवीं सर्वां रश्मिवानिव तेजसा ।सोऽयं राज्ञो विराटस्य सभास्तारो युधिष्ठिरः ॥२५॥
transliteration: pratāpya pṛthivīṁ sarvāṁ raśmivāniva tejasā ,so'yaṁ rājño virāṭasya sabhāstāro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Having oppressed the entire earth with his power, just like the sun (raśmivān) with its radiance (tejasā), this very Yudhiṣṭhira is now a courtier in the assembly of King Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. Having oppressed the entire earth with his power, just like the sun (raśmivān) with its radiance (tejasā), this very Yudhiṣṭhira is now a courtier in the assembly of King Virāṭa.

sanskrit: यमुपासन्त राजानः सभायामृषिभिः सह ।तमुपासीनमद्यान्यं पश्य पाण्डव पाण्डवम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: yamupāsanta rājānaḥ sabhāyāmṛṣibhiḥ saha ,tamupāsīnamadyānyaṁ paśya pāṇḍava pāṇḍavam.
english: O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), behold that Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), whom kings, along with sages, once attended in the assembly, now attending to another person!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. O Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), behold that Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), whom kings, along with sages, once attended in the assembly, now attending to another person!

sanskrit: अतदर्हं महाप्राज्ञं जीवितार्थेऽभिसंश्रितम् ।दृष्ट्वा कस्य न दुःखं स्याद्धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: atadarhaṁ mahāprājñaṁ jīvitārthe'bhisaṁśritam ,dṛṣṭvā kasya na duḥkhaṁ syāddharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Seeing Yudhiṣṭhira, the greatly wise one, who is undeserving of such a state, resorting to this for mere survival, and whose very being (ātman) is righteousness (dharma)  -  whose heart would not be filled with sorrow?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 27. Seeing Yudhiṣṭhira, the greatly wise one, who is undeserving of such a state, resorting to this for mere survival, and whose very being (ātman) is righteousness (dharma)  -  whose heart would not be filled with sorrow?

sanskrit: उपास्ते स्म सभायां यं कृत्स्ना वीर वसुंधरा ।तमुपासीनमद्यान्यं पश्य भारत भारतम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: upāste sma sabhāyāṁ yaṁ kṛtsnā vīra vasuṁdharā ,tamupāsīnamadyānyaṁ paśya bhārata bhāratam.
english: O hero, descendant of Bharata! Behold today that descendant of Bharata whom the entire earth (vasundharā) once served in the assembly, now serving another!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 28. O hero, descendant of Bharata! Behold today that descendant of Bharata whom the entire earth (vasundharā) once served in the assembly, now serving another!

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधैर्दुःखैः पीड्यमानामनाथवत् ।शोकसागरमध्यस्थां किं मां भीम न पश्यसि ॥२९॥
transliteration: evaṁ bahuvidhairduḥkhaiḥ pīḍyamānāmanāthavat ,śokasāgaramadhyasthāṁ kiṁ māṁ bhīma na paśyasi.
english: O Bhima, why do you not see me, thus tormented by manifold sorrows like an orphan, and situated in the midst of an ocean of grief?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 17 Verse 29. O Bhima, why do you not see me, thus tormented by manifold sorrows like an orphan, and situated in the midst of an ocean of grief?

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।इदं तु मे महद्दुःखं यत्प्रवक्ष्यामि भारत ।न मेऽभ्यसूया कर्तव्या दुःखादेतद्ब्रवीम्यहम् ॥१॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,idaṁ tu me mahadduḥkhaṁ yatpravakṣyāmi bhārata ,na me'bhyasūyā kartavyā duḥkhādetadbravīmyaham.
english: Draupadi spoke: 'O descendant of Bharata, this indeed is my great sorrow (duḥkha) which I am about to relate. No ill-will (abhyasūyā) should be held against me; I say this out of grief.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Draupadi spoke: 'O descendant of Bharata, this indeed is my great sorrow (duḥkha) which I am about to relate. No ill-will (abhyasūyā) should be held against me; I say this out of grief.'

sanskrit: शार्दूलैर्महिषैः सिंहैरागारे युध्यसे यदा ।कैकेय्याः प्रेक्षमाणायास्तदा मे कश्मलो भवेत् ॥२॥
transliteration: śārdūlairmahiṣaiḥ siṁhairāgāre yudhyase yadā ,kaikeyyāḥ prekṣamāṇāyāstadā me kaśmalo bhavet.
english: When you used to fight with tigers, buffaloes, and lions in the arena, while Kaikeyi was watching, then I would experience a feeling of great distress (kaśmala).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. When you used to fight with tigers, buffaloes, and lions in the arena, while Kaikeyi was watching, then I would experience a feeling of great distress (kaśmala).

sanskrit: प्रेक्षासमुत्थिता चापि कैकेयी ताः स्त्रियो वदेत् ।प्रेक्ष्य मामनवद्याङ्गी कश्मलोपहतामिव ॥३॥
transliteration: prekṣāsamutthitā cāpi kaikeyī tāḥ striyo vadet ,prekṣya māmanavadyāṅgī kaśmalopahatāmiva.
english: And Kaikeyi, having risen to observe, would also speak to those women, after seeing me, the faultless-limbed one, as if I were overcome by distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. And Kaikeyi, having risen to observe, would also speak to those women, after seeing me, the faultless-limbed one, as if I were overcome by distress.

sanskrit: स्नेहात्संवासजान्मन्ये सूदमेषा शुचिस्मिता ।योध्यमानं महावीर्यैरिमं समनुशोचति ॥४॥
transliteration: snehātsaṁvāsajānmanye sūdameṣā śucismitā ,yodhyamānaṁ mahāvīryairimaṁ samanuśocati.
english: I believe that this pure-smiling one (Draupadi), out of affection arising from our shared residence, grieves for this cook (Bhīma), who is being tormented by mighty heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. I believe that this pure-smiling one (Draupadi), out of affection arising from our shared residence, grieves for this cook (Bhīma), who is being tormented by mighty heroes.

sanskrit: कल्याणरूपा सैरन्ध्री बल्लवश्चातिसुन्दरः ।स्त्रीणां च चित्तं दुर्ज्ञेयं युक्तरूपौ च मे मतौ ॥५॥
transliteration: kalyāṇarūpā sairandhrī ballavaścātisundaraḥ ,strīṇāṁ ca cittaṁ durjñeyaṁ yuktarūpau ca me matau.
english: Sairandhrī is of a beautiful form, and Ballava is exceedingly handsome. Furthermore, the minds of women are hard to know; in my opinion, these two appear well-matched.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. Sairandhrī is of a beautiful form, and Ballava is exceedingly handsome. Furthermore, the minds of women are hard to know; in my opinion, these two appear well-matched.

sanskrit: सैरन्ध्री प्रियसंवासान्नित्यं करुणवेदिनी ।अस्मिन्राजकुले चेमौ तुल्यकालनिवासिनौ ॥६॥
transliteration: sairandhrī priyasaṁvāsānnityaṁ karuṇavedinī ,asminrājakule cemau tulyakālanivāsinau.
english: Sairandhrī, because of their affectionate association, constantly experiences compassion. And these two have been residing in this royal palace for the same period.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. Sairandhrī, because of their affectionate association, constantly experiences compassion. And these two have been residing in this royal palace for the same period.

sanskrit: इति ब्रुवाणा वाक्यानि सा मां नित्यमवेदयत् ।क्रुध्यन्तीं मां च संप्रेक्ष्य समशङ्कत मां त्वयि ॥७॥
transliteration: iti bruvāṇā vākyāni sā māṁ nityamavedayat ,krudhyantīṁ māṁ ca saṁprekṣya samaśaṅkata māṁ tvayi.
english: She, speaking such words, always informed me. And seeing me angry, she began to suspect me regarding you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. She, speaking such words, always informed me. And seeing me angry, she began to suspect me regarding you.

sanskrit: तस्यां तथा ब्रुवत्यां तु दुःखं मां महदाविशत् ।शोके यौधिष्ठिरे मग्ना नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे ॥८॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ tathā bruvatyāṁ tu duḥkhaṁ māṁ mahadāviśat ,śoke yaudhiṣṭhire magnā nāhaṁ jīvitumutsahe.
english: As she spoke thus, great sorrow (duḥkham) seized me. Plunged into grief (śoka) for Yudhiṣṭhira, I have no desire to live.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. As she spoke thus, great sorrow (duḥkham) seized me. Plunged into grief (śoka) for Yudhiṣṭhira, I have no desire to live.

sanskrit: यः सदेवान्मनुष्यांश्च सर्पांश्चैकरथोऽजयत् ।सोऽयं राज्ञो विराटस्य कन्यानां नर्तको युवा ॥९॥
transliteration: yaḥ sadevānmanuṣyāṁśca sarpāṁścaikaratho'jayat ,so'yaṁ rājño virāṭasya kanyānāṁ nartako yuvā.
english: He who, from his single chariot, conquered the gods, humans, and serpents (sarpa)  -  that same young man is now a dancer for the daughters of King Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. He who, from his single chariot, conquered the gods, humans, and serpents (sarpa)  -  that same young man is now a dancer for the daughters of King Virāṭa.

sanskrit: योऽतर्पयदमेयात्मा खाण्डवे जातवेदसम् ।सोऽन्तःपुरगतः पार्थः कूपेऽग्निरिव संवृतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yo'tarpayadameyātmā khāṇḍave jātavedasam ,so'ntaḥpuragataḥ pārthaḥ kūpe'gniriva saṁvṛtaḥ.
english: He whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, who satisfied Agni (jātavedas) in the Khāṇḍava forest – that Pārtha (Arjuna) is now dwelling within the inner apartments, like a fire covered in a well.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. He whose spirit (ātman) is immeasurable, who satisfied Agni (jātavedas) in the Khāṇḍava forest – that Pārtha (Arjuna) is now dwelling within the inner apartments, like a fire covered in a well.

sanskrit: यस्माद्भयममित्राणां सदैव पुरुषर्षभात् ।स लोकपरिभूतेन वेषेणास्ते धनंजयः ॥११॥
transliteration: yasmādbhayamamitrāṇāṁ sadaiva puruṣarṣabhāt ,sa lokaparibhūtena veṣeṇāste dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Arjuna (dhanañjaya), from whom his enemies always felt fear - that best among men (puruṣarṣabha) - now remains in a guise scorned by the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. Arjuna (dhanañjaya), from whom his enemies always felt fear - that best among men (puruṣarṣabha) - now remains in a guise scorned by the people.

sanskrit: यस्य ज्यातलनिर्घोषात्समकम्पन्त शत्रवः ।स्त्रियो गीतस्वनं तस्य मुदिताः पर्युपासते ॥१२॥
transliteration: yasya jyātalanirghoṣātsamakampanta śatravaḥ ,striyo gītasvanaṁ tasya muditāḥ paryupāsate.
english: At the resounding twang of whose bowstring, enemies used to tremble; now, delighted women attend upon his singing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. At the resounding twang of whose bowstring, enemies used to tremble; now, delighted women attend upon his singing.

sanskrit: किरीटं सूर्यसंकाशं यस्य मूर्धनि शोभते ।वेणीविकृतकेशान्तः सोऽयमद्य धनंजयः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kirīṭaṁ sūryasaṁkāśaṁ yasya mūrdhani śobhate ,veṇīvikṛtakeśāntaḥ so'yamadya dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: He, on whose head a sun-like diadem used to shine, that very Arjuna (dhanañjaya) now has the ends of his hair disfigured by a braid.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. He, on whose head a sun-like diadem used to shine, that very Arjuna (dhanañjaya) now has the ends of his hair disfigured by a braid.

sanskrit: यस्मिन्नस्त्राणि दिव्यानि समस्तानि महात्मनि ।आधारः सर्वविद्यानां स धारयति कुण्डले ॥१४॥
transliteration: yasminnastrāṇi divyāni samastāni mahātmani ,ādhāraḥ sarvavidyānāṁ sa dhārayati kuṇḍale.
english: He, the great-souled one (mahātman), in whom all divine weapons and the foundation of all knowledge (vidyā) reside, now wears earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. He, the great-souled one (mahātman), in whom all divine weapons and the foundation of all knowledge (vidyā) reside, now wears earrings.

sanskrit: यं स्म राजसहस्राणि तेजसाप्रतिमानि वै ।समरे नातिवर्तन्ते वेलामिव महार्णवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yaṁ sma rājasahasrāṇi tejasāpratimāni vai ,samare nātivartante velāmiva mahārṇavaḥ.
english: Indeed, thousands of kings, unparalleled in their might, cannot surpass him in battle, just as a great ocean cannot transgress its shore.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. Indeed, thousands of kings, unparalleled in their might, cannot surpass him in battle, just as a great ocean cannot transgress its shore.

sanskrit: सोऽयं राज्ञो विराटस्य कन्यानां नर्तको युवा ।आस्ते वेषप्रतिच्छन्नः कन्यानां परिचारकः ॥१६॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ rājño virāṭasya kanyānāṁ nartako yuvā ,āste veṣapraticchannaḥ kanyānāṁ paricārakaḥ.
english: This very young man, who is a dancer for King Virata's daughters, dwells here disguised, acting as an attendant to the princesses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. This very young man, who is a dancer for King Virata's daughters, dwells here disguised, acting as an attendant to the princesses.

sanskrit: यस्य स्म रथघोषेण समकम्पत मेदिनी ।सपर्वतवना भीम सहस्थावरजङ्गमा ॥१७॥
transliteration: yasya sma rathaghoṣeṇa samakampata medinī ,saparvatavanā bhīma sahasthāvarajaṅgamā.
english: O Bhima, the earth, along with its mountains, forests, and all its stationary and moving creatures, would indeed tremble at the sound of his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. O Bhima, the earth, along with its mountains, forests, and all its stationary and moving creatures, would indeed tremble at the sound of his chariot.

sanskrit: यस्मिञ्जाते महाभागे कुन्त्याः शोको व्यनश्यत ।स शोचयति मामद्य भीमसेन तवानुजः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yasmiñjāte mahābhāge kuntyāḥ śoko vyanaśyata ,sa śocayati māmadya bhīmasena tavānujaḥ.
english: O Bhimasena, when that great-souled (mahābhāge) one was born, Kunti's sorrow disappeared. But now, your younger brother (Arjuna) causes me grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. O Bhimasena, when that great-souled (mahābhāge) one was born, Kunti's sorrow disappeared. But now, your younger brother (Arjuna) causes me grief.

sanskrit: भूषितं तमलंकारैः कुण्डलैः परिहाटकैः ।कम्बुपाणिनमायान्तं दृष्ट्वा सीदति मे मनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhūṣitaṁ tamalaṁkāraiḥ kuṇḍalaiḥ parihāṭakaiḥ ,kambupāṇinamāyāntaṁ dṛṣṭvā sīdati me manaḥ.
english: My heart sinks when I see him approaching, adorned with ornaments and golden earrings, holding a conch in his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. My heart sinks when I see him approaching, adorned with ornaments and golden earrings, holding a conch in his hand.

sanskrit: तं वेणीकृतकेशान्तं भीमधन्वानमर्जुनम् ।कन्यापरिवृतं दृष्ट्वा भीम सीदति मे मनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: taṁ veṇīkṛtakeśāntaṁ bhīmadhanvānamarjunam ,kanyāparivṛtaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīma sīdati me manaḥ.
english: O Bhīma, my heart sinks when I see Arjuna, the one with a formidable bow, with his hair braided and surrounded by maidens.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. O Bhīma, my heart sinks when I see Arjuna, the one with a formidable bow, with his hair braided and surrounded by maidens.

sanskrit: यदा ह्येनं परिवृतं कन्याभिर्देवरूपिणम् ।प्रभिन्नमिव मातङ्गं परिकीर्णं करेणुभिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yadā hyenaṁ parivṛtaṁ kanyābhirdevarūpiṇam ,prabhinnamiva mātaṅgaṁ parikīrṇaṁ kareṇubhiḥ.
english: When indeed I see him, having a divine form, surrounded by maidens, just like an elephant in rut surrounded by female elephants...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. When indeed I see him, having a divine form, surrounded by maidens, just like an elephant in rut surrounded by female elephants...

sanskrit: मत्स्यमर्थपतिं पार्थं विराटं समुपस्थितम् ।पश्यामि तूर्यमध्यस्थं दिशो नश्यन्ति मे तदा ॥२२॥
transliteration: matsyamarthapatiṁ pārthaṁ virāṭaṁ samupasthitam ,paśyāmi tūryamadhyasthaṁ diśo naśyanti me tadā.
english: When I see Arjuna (pārtha), the master of purpose, present before King Matsya Virāṭa, standing amidst musical instruments, then my sense of direction vanishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. When I see Arjuna (pārtha), the master of purpose, present before King Matsya Virāṭa, standing amidst musical instruments, then my sense of direction vanishes.

sanskrit: नूनमार्या न जानाति कृच्छ्रं प्राप्तं धनंजयम् ।अजातशत्रुं कौरव्यं मग्नं दुर्द्यूतदेविनम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: nūnamāryā na jānāti kṛcchraṁ prāptaṁ dhanaṁjayam ,ajātaśatruṁ kauravyaṁ magnaṁ durdyūtadevinam.
english: Surely, the revered lady (Kunti) does not know the great hardship that Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) has endured, nor of Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), the scion of Kuru, who is steeped in misery after playing that disastrous game of dice.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. Surely, the revered lady (Kunti) does not know the great hardship that Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) has endured, nor of Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), the scion of Kuru, who is steeped in misery after playing that disastrous game of dice.

sanskrit: तथा दृष्ट्वा यवीयांसं सहदेवं युधां पतिम् ।गोषु गोवेषमायान्तं पाण्डुभूतास्मि भारत ॥२४॥
transliteration: tathā dṛṣṭvā yavīyāṁsaṁ sahadevaṁ yudhāṁ patim ,goṣu goveṣamāyāntaṁ pāṇḍubhūtāsmi bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata (Krishna), having thus seen Sahadeva, the youngest and lord of battles, coming among the cows in the guise of a cowherd, I have become pale.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. O descendant of Bharata (Krishna), having thus seen Sahadeva, the youngest and lord of battles, coming among the cows in the guise of a cowherd, I have become pale.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्य वृत्तानि चिन्तयन्ती पुनः पुनः ।न विन्दामि महाबाहो सहदेवस्य दुष्कृतम् ।यस्मिन्नेवंविधं दुःखं प्राप्नुयात्सत्यविक्रमः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sahadevasya vṛttāni cintayantī punaḥ punaḥ ,na vindāmi mahābāho sahadevasya duṣkṛtam ,yasminnevaṁvidhaṁ duḥkhaṁ prāpnuyātsatyavikramaḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one (Krishna), repeatedly pondering Sahadeva's actions, I cannot find any misdeed of his, for which the truly valorous one should experience such suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. O mighty-armed one (Krishna), repeatedly pondering Sahadeva's actions, I cannot find any misdeed of his, for which the truly valorous one should experience such suffering.

sanskrit: दूयामि भरतश्रेष्ठ दृष्ट्वा ते भ्रातरं प्रियम् ।गोषु गोवृषसंकाशं मत्स्येनाभिनिवेशितम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: dūyāmi bharataśreṣṭha dṛṣṭvā te bhrātaraṁ priyam ,goṣu govṛṣasaṁkāśaṁ matsyenābhiniveśitam.
english: O best among the Bhāratas (Krishna), I grieve after seeing your dear brother (Sahadeva), who, resembling a magnificent bull among the cows, was appointed (as a cowherd) by King Matsya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. O best among the Bhāratas (Krishna), I grieve after seeing your dear brother (Sahadeva), who, resembling a magnificent bull among the cows, was appointed (as a cowherd) by King Matsya.

sanskrit: संरब्धं रक्तनेपथ्यं गोपालानां पुरोगमम् ।विराटमभिनन्दन्तमथ मे भवति ज्वरः ॥२७॥
transliteration: saṁrabdhaṁ raktanepathyaṁ gopālānāṁ purogamam ,virāṭamabhinandantamatha me bhavati jvaraḥ.
english: When I see him, enraged and clad in red garments, leading the cowherds, and greeting King Virāṭa, then a fever (distress) arises in me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 27. When I see him, enraged and clad in red garments, leading the cowherds, and greeting King Virāṭa, then a fever (distress) arises in me.

sanskrit: सहदेवं हि मे वीरं नित्यमार्या प्रशंसति ।महाभिजनसंपन्नो वृत्तवाञ्शीलवानिति ॥२८॥
transliteration: sahadevaṁ hi me vīraṁ nityamāryā praśaṁsati ,mahābhijanasaṁpanno vṛttavāñśīlavāniti.
english: Indeed, the noble lady (Kuntī) always praises my heroic Sahadeva, saying that he is endowed with great lineage, of good conduct, and virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 28. Indeed, the noble lady (Kuntī) always praises my heroic Sahadeva, saying that he is endowed with great lineage, of good conduct, and virtuous.

sanskrit: ह्रीनिषेधो मधुरवाग्धार्मिकश्च प्रियश्च मे ।स तेऽरण्येषु बोद्धव्यो याज्ञसेनि क्षपास्वपि ॥२९॥
transliteration: hrīniṣedho madhuravāgdhārmikaśca priyaśca me ,sa te'raṇyeṣu boddhavyo yājñaseni kṣapāsvapi.
english: He is restrained by modesty, sweet-spoken, righteous (dhārmika), and dear to me. O Yājñasenī, he should be recognized by you in the forests, even during the nights.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 29. He is restrained by modesty, sweet-spoken, righteous (dhārmika), and dear to me. O Yājñasenī, he should be recognized by you in the forests, even during the nights.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा व्यापृतं गोषु वत्सचर्मक्षपाशयम् ।सहदेवं युधां श्रेष्ठं किं नु जीवामि पाण्डव ॥३०॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā vyāpṛtaṁ goṣu vatsacarmakṣapāśayam ,sahadevaṁ yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ kiṁ nu jīvāmi pāṇḍava.
english: Having seen him busy with the cows, sleeping at night on a calfskin - Sahadeva, the foremost among warriors - how can I possibly continue to live, O Pāṇḍava?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 30. Having seen him busy with the cows, sleeping at night on a calfskin - Sahadeva, the foremost among warriors - how can I possibly continue to live, O Pāṇḍava?

sanskrit: यस्त्रिभिर्नित्यसंपन्नो रूपेणास्त्रेण मेधया ।सोऽश्वबन्धो विराटस्य पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: yastribhirnityasaṁpanno rūpeṇāstreṇa medhayā ,so'śvabandho virāṭasya paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: He who was always endowed with three (qualities) – beauty, skill in weaponry, and intelligence – that very one is now the horse-binder of Virāṭa. Behold the reversal of time!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 31. He who was always endowed with three (qualities) – beauty, skill in weaponry, and intelligence – that very one is now the horse-binder of Virāṭa. Behold the reversal of time!

sanskrit: अभ्यकीर्यन्त वृन्दानि दामग्रन्थिमुदीक्षताम् ।विनयन्तं जवेनाश्वान्महाराजस्य पश्यतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: abhyakīryanta vṛndāni dāmagranthimudīkṣatām ,vinayantaṁ javenāśvānmahārājasya paśyataḥ.
english: Crowds gathered around, watching him tie the rope. Look at him controlling the horses with speed, in the presence of the great king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 32. Crowds gathered around, watching him tie the rope. Look at him controlling the horses with speed, in the presence of the great king.

sanskrit: अपश्यमेनं श्रीमन्तं मत्स्यं भ्राजिष्णुमुत्तमम् ।विराटमुपतिष्ठन्तं दर्शयन्तं च वाजिनः ॥३३॥
transliteration: apaśyamenaṁ śrīmantaṁ matsyaṁ bhrājiṣṇumuttamam ,virāṭamupatiṣṭhantaṁ darśayantaṁ ca vājinaḥ.
english: I saw him  -  glorious, excellent, and brilliant  -  serving Virāṭa, the Matsya king, and showing the horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 33. I saw him  -  glorious, excellent, and brilliant  -  serving Virāṭa, the Matsya king, and showing the horses.

sanskrit: किं नु मां मन्यसे पार्थ सुखितेति परंतप ।एवं दुःखशताविष्टा युधिष्ठिरनिमित्ततः ॥३४॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu māṁ manyase pārtha sukhiteti paraṁtapa ,evaṁ duḥkhaśatāviṣṭā yudhiṣṭhiranimittataḥ.
english: O Pārtha, tormentor of foes, what do you think of me? Do you consider me happy? Indeed, I am beset by hundreds of sorrows because of Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 34. O Pārtha, tormentor of foes, what do you think of me? Do you consider me happy? Indeed, I am beset by hundreds of sorrows because of Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: अतः प्रतिविशिष्टानि दुःखान्यन्यानि भारत ।वर्तन्ते मयि कौन्तेय वक्ष्यामि शृणु तान्यपि ॥३५॥
transliteration: ataḥ prativiśiṣṭāni duḥkhānyanyāni bhārata ,vartante mayi kaunteya vakṣyāmi śṛṇu tānyapi.
english: Therefore, O Bhārata, O son of Kuntī, other especially distinct miseries are present in me. I will tell you about them; please listen to those as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 35. Therefore, O Bhārata, O son of Kuntī, other especially distinct miseries are present in me. I will tell you about them; please listen to those as well.

sanskrit: युष्मासु ध्रियमाणेषु दुःखानि विविधान्युत ।शोषयन्ति शरीरं मे किं नु दुःखमतः परम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: yuṣmāsu dhriyamāṇeṣu duḥkhāni vividhānyuta ,śoṣayanti śarīraṁ me kiṁ nu duḥkhamataḥ param.
english: While you (all) are living, various miseries indeed are drying up my body. What greater suffering (duḥkha) could there possibly be than this?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 18 Verse 36. While you (all) are living, various miseries indeed are drying up my body. What greater suffering (duḥkha) could there possibly be than this?

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।अहं सैरन्ध्रिवेषेण चरन्ती राजवेश्मनि ।शौचदास्मि सुदेष्णाया अक्षधूर्तस्य कारणात् ॥१॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,ahaṁ sairandhriveṣeṇa carantī rājaveśmani ,śaucadāsmi sudeṣṇāyā akṣadhūrtasya kāraṇāt.
english: Draupadi said: "I, living in the king's palace in the guise of a female attendant (sairandhrī), am a cleaner for Sudēṣṇā because of the gambler."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. Draupadi said: "I, living in the king's palace in the guise of a female attendant (sairandhrī), am a cleaner for Sudēṣṇā because of the gambler."

sanskrit: विक्रियां पश्य मे तीव्रां राजपुत्र्याः परंतप ।आसे कालमुपासीना सर्वं दुःखं किलार्तवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: vikriyāṁ paśya me tīvrāṁ rājaputryāḥ paraṁtapa ,āse kālamupāsīnā sarvaṁ duḥkhaṁ kilārtavat.
english: O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), behold my severe degradation (vikriyā), I, a princess. I endure this period, undergoing all suffering (duḥkha) indeed like one in deep distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), behold my severe degradation (vikriyā), I, a princess. I endure this period, undergoing all suffering (duḥkha) indeed like one in deep distress.

sanskrit: अनित्या किल मर्त्यानामर्थसिद्धिर्जयाजयौ ।इति कृत्वा प्रतीक्षामि भर्तॄणामुदयं पुनः ॥३॥
transliteration: anityā kila martyānāmarthasiddhirjayājayau ,iti kṛtvā pratīkṣāmi bhartṝṇāmudayaṁ punaḥ.
english: Indeed, for mortals, the fulfillment of objectives, whether victory or defeat, is impermanent. Knowing this, I again await the prosperity of my masters.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. Indeed, for mortals, the fulfillment of objectives, whether victory or defeat, is impermanent. Knowing this, I again await the prosperity of my masters.

sanskrit: य एव हेतुर्भवति पुरुषस्य जयावहः ।पराजये च हेतुः स इति च प्रतिपालये ॥४॥
transliteration: ya eva heturbhavati puruṣasya jayāvahaḥ ,parājaye ca hetuḥ sa iti ca pratipālaye.
english: That very same factor which leads to a man's victory also becomes the cause of his defeat; understanding this, I await the outcome.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. That very same factor which leads to a man's victory also becomes the cause of his defeat; understanding this, I await the outcome.

sanskrit: दत्त्वा याचन्ति पुरुषा हत्वा वध्यन्ति चापरे ।पातयित्वा च पात्यन्ते परैरिति च मे श्रुतम् ॥५॥
transliteration: dattvā yācanti puruṣā hatvā vadhyanti cāpare ,pātayitvā ca pātyante parairiti ca me śrutam.
english: Men, having given, then beg; and others, having killed, are killed. Furthermore, having caused others to fall, they themselves are made to fall by others. This is what I have heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. Men, having given, then beg; and others, having killed, are killed. Furthermore, having caused others to fall, they themselves are made to fall by others. This is what I have heard.

sanskrit: न दैवस्यातिभारोऽस्ति न दैवस्यातिवर्तनम् ।इति चाप्यागमं भूयो दैवस्य प्रतिपालये ॥६॥
transliteration: na daivasyātibhāro'sti na daivasyātivartanam ,iti cāpyāgamaṁ bhūyo daivasya pratipālaye.
english: There is no undue burden for destiny, nor is there any bypassing of destiny's course. Thus, having indeed repeatedly understood this teaching of destiny, I (continue to) observe.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. There is no undue burden for destiny, nor is there any bypassing of destiny's course. Thus, having indeed repeatedly understood this teaching of destiny, I (continue to) observe.

sanskrit: स्थितं पूर्वं जलं यत्र पुनस्तत्रैव तिष्ठति ।इति पर्यायमिच्छन्ती प्रतीक्षाम्युदयं पुनः ॥७॥
transliteration: sthitaṁ pūrvaṁ jalaṁ yatra punastatraiva tiṣṭhati ,iti paryāyamicchantī pratīkṣāmyudayaṁ punaḥ.
english: Just as water that stood before remains there again, so desiring this cyclical return, I await a new rise (of my fortune) once more.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. Just as water that stood before remains there again, so desiring this cyclical return, I await a new rise (of my fortune) once more.

sanskrit: दैवेन किल यस्यार्थः सुनीतोऽपि विपद्यते ।दैवस्य चागमे यत्नस्तेन कार्यो विजानता ॥८॥
transliteration: daivena kila yasyārthaḥ sunīto'pi vipadyate ,daivasya cāgame yatnastena kāryo vijānatā.
english: Indeed, when one's objective (artha), even if well-managed, fails due to destiny (daiva), then a discerning person should exert effort for the opportune arrival of destiny (daiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. Indeed, when one's objective (artha), even if well-managed, fails due to destiny (daiva), then a discerning person should exert effort for the opportune arrival of destiny (daiva).

sanskrit: यत्तु मे वचनस्यास्य कथितस्य प्रयोजनम् ।पृच्छ मां दुःखितां तत्त्वमपृष्टा वा ब्रवीमि ते ॥९॥
transliteration: yattu me vacanasyāsya kathitasya prayojanam ,pṛccha māṁ duḥkhitāṁ tattvamapṛṣṭā vā bravīmi te.
english: Regarding the purpose of this statement of mine that has been made, you may ask me, who am sorrowful, for the truth. Or, even if unasked, I shall tell it to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. Regarding the purpose of this statement of mine that has been made, you may ask me, who am sorrowful, for the truth. Or, even if unasked, I shall tell it to you.

sanskrit: महिषी पाण्डुपुत्राणां दुहिता द्रुपदस्य च ।इमामवस्थां संप्राप्ता का मदन्या जिजीविषेत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: mahiṣī pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ duhitā drupadasya ca ,imāmavasthāṁ saṁprāptā kā madanyā jijīviṣet.
english: I, who am the chief queen (mahiṣī) of the Pāṇḍavas and the daughter of Drupada, having reached this terrible state, who else other than me would desire to live?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. I, who am the chief queen (mahiṣī) of the Pāṇḍavas and the daughter of Drupada, having reached this terrible state, who else other than me would desire to live?

sanskrit: कुरून्परिभवन्सर्वान्पाञ्चालानपि भारत ।पाण्डवेयांश्च संप्राप्तो मम क्लेशो ह्यरिंदम ॥११॥
transliteration: kurūnparibhavansarvānpāñcālānapi bhārata ,pāṇḍaveyāṁśca saṁprāpto mama kleśo hyariṁdama.
english: O scion of Bharata, O subduer of enemies, my affliction has indeed arrived, humiliating all the Kurus, and even the Panchalas and the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. O scion of Bharata, O subduer of enemies, my affliction has indeed arrived, humiliating all the Kurus, and even the Panchalas and the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: भ्रातृभिः श्वशुरैः पुत्रैर्बहुभिः परवीरहन् ।एवं समुदिता नारी का न्वन्या दुःखिता भवेत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: bhrātṛbhiḥ śvaśuraiḥ putrairbahubhiḥ paravīrahan ,evaṁ samuditā nārī kā nvanyā duḥkhitā bhavet.
english: O slayer of enemy heroes, what other woman, thus accompanied by many brothers, fathers-in-law, and sons, would be distressed?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. O slayer of enemy heroes, what other woman, thus accompanied by many brothers, fathers-in-law, and sons, would be distressed?

sanskrit: नूनं हि बालया धातुर्मया वै विप्रियं कृतम् ।यस्य प्रसादाद्दुर्नीतं प्राप्तास्मि भरतर्षभ ॥१३॥
transliteration: nūnaṁ hi bālayā dhāturmayā vai vipriyaṁ kṛtam ,yasya prasādāddurnītaṁ prāptāsmi bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, surely, when I was but a child, I must have indeed committed some offense against the Creator (Dhātṛ), by whose dispensation I have received this misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. O best of Bharatas, surely, when I was but a child, I must have indeed committed some offense against the Creator (Dhātṛ), by whose dispensation I have received this misfortune.

sanskrit: वर्णावकाशमपि मे पश्य पाण्डव यादृशम् ।यादृशो मे न तत्रासीद्दुःखे परमके तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: varṇāvakāśamapi me paśya pāṇḍava yādṛśam ,yādṛśo me na tatrāsīdduḥkhe paramake tadā.
english: O Pāṇḍava, behold my complexion and appearance, such as it is now. Such a state was not mine even in my supreme distress at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. O Pāṇḍava, behold my complexion and appearance, such as it is now. Such a state was not mine even in my supreme distress at that time.

sanskrit: त्वमेव भीम जानीषे यन्मे पार्थ सुखं पुरा ।साहं दासत्वमापन्ना न शान्तिमवशा लभे ॥१५॥
transliteration: tvameva bhīma jānīṣe yanme pārtha sukhaṁ purā ,sāhaṁ dāsatvamāpannā na śāntimavaśā labhe.
english: O Bhīma, you certainly know the happiness I experienced in the past, O Pārtha. Now, having fallen into servitude, I am helpless and find no peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. O Bhīma, you certainly know the happiness I experienced in the past, O Pārtha. Now, having fallen into servitude, I am helpless and find no peace.

sanskrit: नादैविकमिदं मन्ये यत्र पार्थो धनंजयः ।भीमधन्वा महाबाहुरास्ते शान्त इवानलः ॥१६॥
transliteration: nādaivikamidaṁ manye yatra pārtho dhanaṁjayaḥ ,bhīmadhanvā mahābāhurāste śānta ivānalaḥ.
english: I believe this situation is divinely ordained, given that Pārtha Dhanañjaya, the mighty-armed one with a formidable bow, sits here like a subdued fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 16. I believe this situation is divinely ordained, given that Pārtha Dhanañjaya, the mighty-armed one with a formidable bow, sits here like a subdued fire.

sanskrit: अशक्या वेदितुं पार्थ प्राणिनां वै गतिर्नरैः ।विनिपातमिमं मन्ये युष्माकमविचिन्तितम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: aśakyā vedituṁ pārtha prāṇināṁ vai gatirnaraiḥ ,vinipātamimaṁ manye yuṣmākamavicintitam.
english: O Pārtha, the destiny of living beings is truly impossible for humans to comprehend. I consider this particular downfall of yours to be unforeseen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 17. O Pārtha, the destiny of living beings is truly impossible for humans to comprehend. I consider this particular downfall of yours to be unforeseen.

sanskrit: यस्या मम मुखप्रेक्षा यूयमिन्द्रसमाः सदा ।सा प्रेक्षे मुखमन्यासामवराणां वरा सती ॥१८॥
transliteration: yasyā mama mukhaprekṣā yūyamindrasamāḥ sadā ,sā prekṣe mukhamanyāsāmavarāṇāṁ varā satī.
english: You, who were always like Indra and dependent on my glance, yet I, who am a superior woman, now look to the faces of other, inferior women.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 18. You, who were always like Indra and dependent on my glance, yet I, who am a superior woman, now look to the faces of other, inferior women.

sanskrit: पश्य पाण्डव मेऽवस्थां यथा नार्हामि वै तथा ।युष्मासु ध्रियमाणेषु पश्य कालस्य पर्ययम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: paśya pāṇḍava me'vasthāṁ yathā nārhāmi vai tathā ,yuṣmāsu dhriyamāṇeṣu paśya kālasya paryayam.
english: O Pāṇḍava, behold my current state, which I certainly do not deserve, especially while you are all still alive! See this reversal of time's fortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 19. O Pāṇḍava, behold my current state, which I certainly do not deserve, especially while you are all still alive! See this reversal of time's fortune.

sanskrit: यस्याः सागरपर्यन्ता पृथिवी वशवर्तिनी ।आसीत्साद्य सुदेष्णाया भीताहं वशवर्तिनी ॥२०॥
transliteration: yasyāḥ sāgaraparyantā pṛthivī vaśavartinī ,āsītsādya sudeṣṇāyā bhītāhaṁ vaśavartinī.
english: The entire earth, which extended to the ocean, was once under her control; today, I, frightened, am a subordinate of Sudeṣṇā.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 20. The entire earth, which extended to the ocean, was once under her control; today, I, frightened, am a subordinate of Sudeṣṇā.

sanskrit: यस्याः पुरःसरा आसन्पृष्ठतश्चानुगामिनः ।साहमद्य सुदेष्णायाः पुरः पश्चाच्च गामिनी ।इदं तु दुःखं कौन्तेय ममासह्यं निबोध तत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: yasyāḥ puraḥsarā āsanpṛṣṭhataścānugāminaḥ ,sāhamadya sudeṣṇāyāḥ puraḥ paścācca gāminī ,idaṁ tu duḥkhaṁ kaunteya mamāsahyaṁ nibodha tat.
english: She for whom there were escorts in front and followers behind, I, that very person, now walk both in front and behind Sudeṣṇā. This sorrow, O son of Kuntī, is unbearable for me; understand that.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 21. She for whom there were escorts in front and followers behind, I, that very person, now walk both in front and behind Sudeṣṇā. This sorrow, O son of Kuntī, is unbearable for me; understand that.

sanskrit: या न जातु स्वयं पिंषे गात्रोद्वर्तनमात्मनः ।अन्यत्र कुन्त्या भद्रं ते साद्य पिंषामि चन्दनम् ।पश्य कौन्तेय पाणी मे नैवं यौ भवतः पुरा ॥२२॥
transliteration: yā na jātu svayaṁ piṁṣe gātrodvartanamātmanaḥ ,anyatra kuntyā bhadraṁ te sādya piṁṣāmi candanam ,paśya kaunteya pāṇī me naivaṁ yau bhavataḥ purā.
english: I, who never ground body ointment (gātrodvārtanam) for my own self (ātman), except for Kuntī, that very I today grind sandalwood, blessings be upon you! O son of Kuntī, behold my two hands; they were not like this before.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 22. I, who never ground body ointment (gātrodvārtanam) for my own self (ātman), except for Kuntī, that very I today grind sandalwood, blessings be upon you! O son of Kuntī, behold my two hands; they were not like this before.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्यस्य दर्शयामास किणबद्धौ करावुभौ ॥२३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityasya darśayāmāsa kiṇabaddhau karāvubhau.
english: Vaiśampāyana said, "Thus, he showed him both hands, which were marked by calluses."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 23. Vaiśampāyana said, "Thus, he showed him both hands, which were marked by calluses."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।बिभेमि कुन्त्या या नाहं युष्माकं वा कदाचन ।साद्याग्रतो विराटस्य भीता तिष्ठामि किंकरी ॥२४॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,bibhemi kuntyā yā nāhaṁ yuṣmākaṁ vā kadācana ,sādyāgrato virāṭasya bhītā tiṣṭhāmi kiṁkarī.
english: Draupadī said, "I, who never feared Kuntī or any of you, now stand today, frightened, as a maidservant before Virāṭa."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 24. Draupadī said, "I, who never feared Kuntī or any of you, now stand today, frightened, as a maidservant before Virāṭa."

sanskrit: किं नु वक्ष्यति सम्राण्मां वर्णकः सुकृतो न वा ।नान्यपिष्टं हि मत्स्यस्य चन्दनं किल रोचते ॥२५॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu vakṣyati samrāṇmāṁ varṇakaḥ sukṛto na vā ,nānyapiṣṭaṁ hi matsyasya candanaṁ kila rocate.
english: What will the Emperor say to me? (He will observe) whether the cosmetic work is well-done or not. Indeed, it is said that sandalwood paste prepared by another is not pleasing to the Matsya queen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 25. What will the Emperor say to me? (He will observe) whether the cosmetic work is well-done or not. Indeed, it is said that sandalwood paste prepared by another is not pleasing to the Matsya queen.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सा कीर्तयन्ती दुःखानि भीमसेनस्य भामिनी ।रुरोद शनकैः कृष्णा भीमसेनमुदीक्षती ॥२६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sā kīrtayantī duḥkhāni bhīmasenasya bhāminī ,ruroda śanakaiḥ kṛṣṇā bhīmasenamudīkṣatī.
english: Vaiśampāyana said, "The beautiful woman, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), recounting Bhīmasena's sufferings, wept softly while gazing at Bhīmasena."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 26. Vaiśampāyana said, "The beautiful woman, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), recounting Bhīmasena's sufferings, wept softly while gazing at Bhīmasena."

sanskrit: सा बाष्पकलया वाचा निःश्वसन्ती पुनः पुनः ।हृदयं भीमसेनस्य घट्टयन्तीदमब्रवीत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sā bāṣpakalayā vācā niḥśvasantī punaḥ punaḥ ,hṛdayaṁ bhīmasenasya ghaṭṭayantīdamabravīt.
english: Sighing repeatedly with a voice choked with tears, she said this, stirring Bhimasena's heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 27. Sighing repeatedly with a voice choked with tears, she said this, stirring Bhimasena's heart.

sanskrit: नाल्पं कृतं मया भीम देवानां किल्बिषं पुरा ।अभाग्या यत्तु जीवामि मर्तव्ये सति पाण्डव ॥२८॥
transliteration: nālpaṁ kṛtaṁ mayā bhīma devānāṁ kilbiṣaṁ purā ,abhāgyā yattu jīvāmi martavye sati pāṇḍava.
english: O Bhima, I must have committed a great offense against the gods in the past, because I, being unfortunate, am still alive when I ought to die, O Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 28. O Bhima, I must have committed a great offense against the gods in the past, because I, being unfortunate, am still alive when I ought to die, O Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: ततस्तस्याः करौ शूनौ किणबद्धौ वृकोदरः ।मुखमानीय वेपन्त्या रुरोद परवीरहा ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatastasyāḥ karau śūnau kiṇabaddhau vṛkodaraḥ ,mukhamānīya vepantyā ruroda paravīrahā.
english: Then Vrikodara (Bhima), the slayer of enemy heroes, brought her trembling hands, which were swollen and covered with calluses, to his face and wept.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 29. Then Vrikodara (Bhima), the slayer of enemy heroes, brought her trembling hands, which were swollen and covered with calluses, to his face and wept.

sanskrit: तौ गृहीत्वा च कौन्तेयो बाष्पमुत्सृज्य वीर्यवान् ।ततः परमदुःखार्त इदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tau gṛhītvā ca kaunteyo bāṣpamutsṛjya vīryavān ,tataḥ paramaduḥkhārta idaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: And then, the mighty son of Kunti (Bhima), intensely afflicted by sorrow, having taken those two (hands) and shed tears, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 19 Verse 30. And then, the mighty son of Kunti (Bhima), intensely afflicted by sorrow, having taken those two (hands) and shed tears, spoke these words.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।धिगस्तु मे बाहुबलं गाण्डीवं फल्गुनस्य च ।यत्ते रक्तौ पुरा भूत्वा पाणी कृतकिणावुभौ ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,dhigastu me bāhubalaṁ gāṇḍīvaṁ phalgunasya ca ,yatte raktau purā bhūtvā pāṇī kṛtakiṇāvubhau.
english: Bhīmasena said: Shame upon my arm-strength and upon Arjuna's (Phalguna's) Gāṇḍīva bow! For these two hands of mine, which were formerly soft and red, have now become calloused.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Bhīmasena said: Shame upon my arm-strength and upon Arjuna's (Phalguna's) Gāṇḍīva bow! For these two hands of mine, which were formerly soft and red, have now become calloused.

sanskrit: सभायां स्म विराटस्य करोमि कदनं महत् ।तत्र मां धर्मराजस्तु कटाक्षेण न्यवारयत् ।तदहं तस्य विज्ञाय स्थित एवास्मि भामिनि ॥२॥
transliteration: sabhāyāṁ sma virāṭasya karomi kadanaṁ mahat ,tatra māṁ dharmarājastu kaṭākṣeṇa nyavārayat ,tadahaṁ tasya vijñāya sthita evāsmi bhāmini.
english: In Virāta's (Virāṭa's) assembly, I was about to inflict great destruction. But there, the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja) [Yudhiṣṭhira] restrained me with a mere glance. Understanding his intention, O passionate woman, I remained still.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. In Virāta's (Virāṭa's) assembly, I was about to inflict great destruction. But there, the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja) [Yudhiṣṭhira] restrained me with a mere glance. Understanding his intention, O passionate woman, I remained still.

sanskrit: यच्च राष्ट्रात्प्रच्यवनं कुरूणामवधश्च यः ।सुयोधनस्य कर्णस्य शकुनेः सौबलस्य च ॥३॥
transliteration: yacca rāṣṭrātpracyavanaṁ kurūṇāmavadhaśca yaḥ ,suyodhanasya karṇasya śakuneḥ saubalasya ca.
english: And the expulsion from the kingdom, and the slaying of the Kurus, and of Suyodhana, Karṇa, and Śakuni, the son of Subala (Subala).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. And the expulsion from the kingdom, and the slaying of the Kurus, and of Suyodhana, Karṇa, and Śakuni, the son of Subala (Subala).

sanskrit: दुःशासनस्य पापस्य यन्मया न हृतं शिरः ।तन्मे दहति कल्याणि हृदि शल्यमिवार्पितम् ।मा धर्मं जहि सुश्रोणि क्रोधं जहि महामते ॥४॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanasya pāpasya yanmayā na hṛtaṁ śiraḥ ,tanme dahati kalyāṇi hṛdi śalyamivārpitam ,mā dharmaṁ jahi suśroṇi krodhaṁ jahi mahāmate.
english: The fact that the head of that wicked Duḥśāsana was not severed by me  -  that, O blessed one (Kalyāṇī), burns me in my heart like an arrow (śalya) fixed within. Therefore, O fair-hipped one (Suśroṇī), do not abandon natural law (dharma); instead, abandon anger, O great-minded one (Mahāmate).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. The fact that the head of that wicked Duḥśāsana was not severed by me  -  that, O blessed one (Kalyāṇī), burns me in my heart like an arrow (śalya) fixed within. Therefore, O fair-hipped one (Suśroṇī), do not abandon natural law (dharma); instead, abandon anger, O great-minded one (Mahāmate).

sanskrit: इमं च समुपालम्भं त्वत्तो राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।शृणुयाद्यदि कल्याणि कृत्स्नं जह्यात्स जीवितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: imaṁ ca samupālambhaṁ tvatto rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,śṛṇuyādyadi kalyāṇi kṛtsnaṁ jahyātsa jīvitam.
english: O auspicious one, if King Yudhishthira were to hear this reproach from you, he would abandon his entire life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. O auspicious one, if King Yudhishthira were to hear this reproach from you, he would abandon his entire life.

sanskrit: धनंजयो वा सुश्रोणि यमौ वा तनुमध्यमे ।लोकान्तरगतेष्वेषु नाहं शक्ष्यामि जीवितुम् ॥६॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayo vā suśroṇi yamau vā tanumadhyame ,lokāntaragateṣveṣu nāhaṁ śakṣyāmi jīvitum.
english: O beautiful-hipped one, O slender-waisted one, if Dhananjaya or the twins were to depart for another world (die), I would not be able to live.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. O beautiful-hipped one, O slender-waisted one, if Dhananjaya or the twins were to depart for another world (die), I would not be able to live.

sanskrit: सुकन्या नाम शार्याती भार्गवं च्यवनं वने ।वल्मीकभूतं शाम्यन्तमन्वपद्यत भामिनी ॥७॥
transliteration: sukanyā nāma śāryātī bhārgavaṁ cyavanaṁ vane ,valmīkabhūtaṁ śāmyantamanvapadyata bhāminī.
english: The glorious lady named Sukanya, daughter of Saryati, attended upon the Bhargava (Chyavana) in the forest, who had become covered by an anthill and was meditating peacefully.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. The glorious lady named Sukanya, daughter of Saryati, attended upon the Bhargava (Chyavana) in the forest, who had become covered by an anthill and was meditating peacefully.

sanskrit: नाडायनी चेन्द्रसेना रूपेण यदि ते श्रुता ।पतिमन्वचरद्वृद्धं पुरा वर्षसहस्रिणम् ॥८॥
transliteration: nāḍāyanī cendrasenā rūpeṇa yadi te śrutā ,patimanvacaradvṛddhaṁ purā varṣasahasriṇam.
english: If you have heard of Nāḍāyanī, also called Indrasena, regarding her beauty, then know that she formerly served her ancient husband, who was a thousand years old.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. If you have heard of Nāḍāyanī, also called Indrasena, regarding her beauty, then know that she formerly served her ancient husband, who was a thousand years old.

sanskrit: दुहिता जनकस्यापि वैदेही यदि ते श्रुता ।पतिमन्वचरत्सीता महारण्यनिवासिनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: duhitā janakasyāpi vaidehī yadi te śrutā ,patimanvacaratsītā mahāraṇyanivāsinam.
english: If you have heard of Vaidehi, the daughter of Janaka, know that Sita followed her husband, who was dwelling in the great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. If you have heard of Vaidehi, the daughter of Janaka, know that Sita followed her husband, who was dwelling in the great forest.

sanskrit: रक्षसा निग्रहं प्राप्य रामस्य महिषी प्रिया ।क्लिश्यमानापि सुश्रोणी राममेवान्वपद्यत ॥१०॥
transliteration: rakṣasā nigrahaṁ prāpya rāmasya mahiṣī priyā ,kliśyamānāpi suśroṇī rāmamevānvapadyata.
english: Even though the beloved queen of Rama, the fair-hipped one, suffered capture by a demon (rakṣas), she still resorted to Rama alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. Even though the beloved queen of Rama, the fair-hipped one, suffered capture by a demon (rakṣas), she still resorted to Rama alone.

sanskrit: लोपामुद्रा तथा भीरु वयोरूपसमन्विता ।अगस्त्यमन्वयाद्धित्वा कामान्सर्वानमानुषान् ॥११॥
transliteration: lopāmudrā tathā bhīru vayorūpasamanvitā ,agastyamanvayāddhitvā kāmānsarvānamānuṣān.
english: Similarly, Lopamudra, though delicate and endowed with youth and beauty, followed Agastya, abandoning all superhuman desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. Similarly, Lopamudra, though delicate and endowed with youth and beauty, followed Agastya, abandoning all superhuman desires.

sanskrit: यथैताः कीर्तिता नार्यो रूपवत्यः पतिव्रताः ।तथा त्वमपि कल्याणि सर्वैः समुदिता गुणैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yathaitāḥ kīrtitā nāryo rūpavatyaḥ pativratāḥ ,tathā tvamapi kalyāṇi sarvaiḥ samuditā guṇaiḥ.
english: Just as these beautiful and devoted women have been celebrated, so too, O auspicious one, are you endowed with all good qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. Just as these beautiful and devoted women have been celebrated, so too, O auspicious one, are you endowed with all good qualities.

sanskrit: मादीर्घं क्षम कालं त्वं मासमध्यर्धसंमितम् ।पूर्णे त्रयोदशे वर्षे राज्ञो राज्ञी भविष्यसि ॥१३॥
transliteration: mādīrghaṁ kṣama kālaṁ tvaṁ māsamadhyardhasaṁmitam ,pūrṇe trayodaśe varṣe rājño rājñī bhaviṣyasi.
english: You should not endure this long period, not even for an additional month and a half. Upon the completion of the thirteenth year, you will again become the king's queen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. You should not endure this long period, not even for an additional month and a half. Upon the completion of the thirteenth year, you will again become the king's queen.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।आर्तयैतन्मया भीम कृतं बाष्पविमोक्षणम् ।अपारयन्त्या दुःखानि न राजानमुपालभे ॥१४॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,ārtayaitanmayā bhīma kṛtaṁ bāṣpavimokṣaṇam ,apārayantyā duḥkhāni na rājānamupālabhe.
english: Draupadi said: "O Bhima, I have shed these tears out of distress, being unable to endure these sorrows. I do not blame the king."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. Draupadi said: "O Bhima, I have shed these tears out of distress, being unable to endure these sorrows. I do not blame the king."

sanskrit: विमुक्तेन व्यतीतेन भीमसेन महाबल ।प्रत्युपस्थितकालस्य कार्यस्यानन्तरो भव ॥१५॥
transliteration: vimuktena vyatītena bhīmasena mahābala ,pratyupasthitakālasya kāryasyānantaro bhava.
english: O Bhimasena, mighty one, let go of what is past and gone. Be ready for the task whose time has now arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. O Bhimasena, mighty one, let go of what is past and gone. Be ready for the task whose time has now arrived.

sanskrit: ममेह भीम कैकेयी रूपाभिभवशङ्कया ।नित्यमुद्विजते राजा कथं नेयादिमामिति ॥१६॥
transliteration: mameha bhīma kaikeyī rūpābhibhavaśaṅkayā ,nityamudvijate rājā kathaṁ neyādimāmiti.
english: O Bhima, here, due to Kaikeyi's constant fear of being surpassed in beauty, the king always worries, thinking, 'How will this one (Draupadi) be dealt with?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. O Bhima, here, due to Kaikeyi's constant fear of being surpassed in beauty, the king always worries, thinking, 'How will this one (Draupadi) be dealt with?'

sanskrit: तस्या विदित्वा तं भावं स्वयं चानृतदर्शनः ।कीचकोऽयं सुदुष्टात्मा सदा प्रार्थयते हि माम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasyā viditvā taṁ bhāvaṁ svayaṁ cānṛtadarśanaḥ ,kīcako'yaṁ suduṣṭātmā sadā prārthayate hi mām.
english: Having understood his intention, and being myself a witness to his deceit, this Kīcaka, whose inner self (ātman) is exceedingly wicked, indeed always solicits me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. Having understood his intention, and being myself a witness to his deceit, this Kīcaka, whose inner self (ātman) is exceedingly wicked, indeed always solicits me.

sanskrit: तमहं कुपिता भीम पुनः कोपं नियम्य च ।अब्रुवं कामसंमूढमात्मानं रक्ष कीचक ॥१८॥
transliteration: tamahaṁ kupitā bhīma punaḥ kopaṁ niyamya ca ,abruvaṁ kāmasaṁmūḍhamātmānaṁ rakṣa kīcaka.
english: Though I was angered, O fearful Kīcaka, I again restrained my wrath and said to him, who was utterly bewildered by lust, 'Protect your own self (ātman)!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 18. Though I was angered, O fearful Kīcaka, I again restrained my wrath and said to him, who was utterly bewildered by lust, 'Protect your own self (ātman)!'

sanskrit: गन्धर्वाणामहं भार्या पञ्चानां महिषी प्रिया ।ते त्वां निहन्युर्दुर्धर्षाः शूराः साहसकारिणः ॥१९॥
transliteration: gandharvāṇāmahaṁ bhāryā pañcānāṁ mahiṣī priyā ,te tvāṁ nihanyurdurdharṣāḥ śūrāḥ sāhasakāriṇaḥ.
english: I am the beloved chief wife of five Gandharvas. Those invincible, brave, and valorous ones would kill you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 19. I am the beloved chief wife of five Gandharvas. Those invincible, brave, and valorous ones would kill you.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स दुष्टात्मा कीचकः प्रत्युवाच ह ।नाहं बिभेमि सैरन्ध्रि गन्धर्वाणां शुचिस्मिते ॥२०॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa duṣṭātmā kīcakaḥ pratyuvāca ha ,nāhaṁ bibhemi sairandhri gandharvāṇāṁ śucismite.
english: Thus addressed, that wicked-minded Kīcaka replied, 'O Sairandhrī, O pure-smiling one, I do not fear the Gandharvas!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 20. Thus addressed, that wicked-minded Kīcaka replied, 'O Sairandhrī, O pure-smiling one, I do not fear the Gandharvas!'

sanskrit: शतं सहस्रमपि वा गन्धर्वाणामहं रणे ।समागतं हनिष्यामि त्वं भीरु कुरु मे क्षणम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: śataṁ sahasramapi vā gandharvāṇāmahaṁ raṇe ,samāgataṁ haniṣyāmi tvaṁ bhīru kuru me kṣaṇam.
english: I will slay even a hundred or a thousand Gandharvas assembled in battle. You, timid one, just give me a moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 21. I will slay even a hundred or a thousand Gandharvas assembled in battle. You, timid one, just give me a moment.

sanskrit: इत्युक्ते चाब्रुवं सूतं कामातुरमहं पुनः ।न त्वं प्रतिबलस्तेषां गन्धर्वाणां यशस्विनाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: ityukte cābruvaṁ sūtaṁ kāmāturamahaṁ punaḥ ,na tvaṁ pratibalasteṣāṁ gandharvāṇāṁ yaśasvinām.
english: And after this was said, I again spoke to the charioteer (sūta), who was tormented by desire, saying, 'You are not an equal match for those renowned Gandharvas.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 22. And after this was said, I again spoke to the charioteer (sūta), who was tormented by desire, saying, 'You are not an equal match for those renowned Gandharvas.'

sanskrit: धर्मे स्थितास्मि सततं कुलशीलसमन्विता ।नेच्छामि कंचिद्वध्यन्तं तेन जीवसि कीचक ॥२३॥
transliteration: dharme sthitāsmi satataṁ kulaśīlasamanvitā ,necchāmi kaṁcidvadhyantaṁ tena jīvasi kīcaka.
english: I am always established in my natural law (dharma), endowed with good lineage and character. I do not wish for anyone to be killed; it is for that reason, Kīcaka, that you remain alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 23. I am always established in my natural law (dharma), endowed with good lineage and character. I do not wish for anyone to be killed; it is for that reason, Kīcaka, that you remain alive.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स दुष्टात्मा प्रहस्य स्वनवत्तदा ।न तिष्ठति स्म सन्मार्गे न च धर्मं बुभूषति ॥२४॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa duṣṭātmā prahasya svanavattadā ,na tiṣṭhati sma sanmārge na ca dharmaṁ bubhūṣati.
english: Thus addressed, that evil-minded one laughed loudly then. He did not remain on the right path, nor did he wish to uphold his natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 24. Thus addressed, that evil-minded one laughed loudly then. He did not remain on the right path, nor did he wish to uphold his natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: पापात्मा पापभावश्च कामरागवशानुगः ।अविनीतश्च दुष्टात्मा प्रत्याख्यातः पुनः पुनः ।दर्शने दर्शने हन्यात्तथा जह्यां च जीवितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: pāpātmā pāpabhāvaśca kāmarāgavaśānugaḥ ,avinītaśca duṣṭātmā pratyākhyātaḥ punaḥ punaḥ ,darśane darśane hanyāttathā jahyāṁ ca jīvitam.
english: He, the sinful-souled (pāpātman) one, with a sinful disposition, enslaved by desire and passion, undisciplined, and evil-minded, has been rejected repeatedly. I would kill him at every sight, and in doing so, I would even give up my own life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 25. He, the sinful-souled (pāpātman) one, with a sinful disposition, enslaved by desire and passion, undisciplined, and evil-minded, has been rejected repeatedly. I would kill him at every sight, and in doing so, I would even give up my own life.

sanskrit: तद्धर्मे यतमानानां महान्धर्मो नशिष्यति ।समयं रक्षमाणानां भार्या वो न भविष्यति ॥२६॥
transliteration: taddharme yatamānānāṁ mahāndharmo naśiṣyati ,samayaṁ rakṣamāṇānāṁ bhāryā vo na bhaviṣyati.
english: A great natural law (dharma) will be destroyed for those who strive for a specific natural law (dharma). And for those protecting their covenant (samaya), wives will not be available to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 26. A great natural law (dharma) will be destroyed for those who strive for a specific natural law (dharma). And for those protecting their covenant (samaya), wives will not be available to them.

sanskrit: भार्यायां रक्ष्यमाणायां प्रजा भवति रक्षिता ।प्रजायां रक्ष्यमाणायामात्मा भवति रक्षितः ॥२७॥
transliteration: bhāryāyāṁ rakṣyamāṇāyāṁ prajā bhavati rakṣitā ,prajāyāṁ rakṣyamāṇāyāmātmā bhavati rakṣitaḥ.
english: When the wife is protected, the progeny is protected. When the progeny is protected, the self (ātman) is protected.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 27. When the wife is protected, the progeny is protected. When the progeny is protected, the self (ātman) is protected.

sanskrit: वदतां वर्णधर्मांश्च ब्राह्मणानां हि मे श्रुतम् ।क्षत्रियस्य सदा धर्मो नान्यः शत्रुनिबर्हणात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vadatāṁ varṇadharmāṁśca brāhmaṇānāṁ hi me śrutam ,kṣatriyasya sadā dharmo nānyaḥ śatrunibarhaṇāt.
english: Indeed, I have heard from the Brahmins speaking of the duties (dharma) of the social classes (varṇa). For a Kṣatriya, the constant natural law (dharma) is none other than the destruction of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 28. Indeed, I have heard from the Brahmins speaking of the duties (dharma) of the social classes (varṇa). For a Kṣatriya, the constant natural law (dharma) is none other than the destruction of enemies.

sanskrit: पश्यतो धर्मराजस्य कीचको मां पदावधीत् ।तव चैव समक्षं वै भीमसेन महाबल ॥२९॥
transliteration: paśyato dharmarājasya kīcako māṁ padāvadhīt ,tava caiva samakṣaṁ vai bhīmasena mahābala.
english: Even while King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), was watching, Kīcaka kicked me. And indeed, O mighty Bhīmasena, it happened right before your very eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 29. Even while King Yudhiṣṭhira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), was watching, Kīcaka kicked me. And indeed, O mighty Bhīmasena, it happened right before your very eyes.

sanskrit: त्वया ह्यहं परित्राता तस्माद्घोराज्जटासुरात् ।जयद्रथं तथैव त्वमजैषीर्भ्रातृभिः सह ॥३०॥
transliteration: tvayā hyahaṁ paritrātā tasmādghorājjaṭāsurāt ,jayadrathaṁ tathaiva tvamajaiṣīrbhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: Indeed, you protected me from that terrible Jaṭāsura. And in the same way, you conquered Jayadratha along with your brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 30. Indeed, you protected me from that terrible Jaṭāsura. And in the same way, you conquered Jayadratha along with your brothers.

sanskrit: जहीममपि पापं त्वं योऽयं मामवमन्यते ।कीचको राजवाल्लभ्याच्छोककृन्मम भारत ॥३१॥
transliteration: jahīmamapi pāpaṁ tvaṁ yo'yaṁ māmavamanyate ,kīcako rājavāllabhyācchokakṛnmama bhārata.
english: You must kill this sinful one as well, this Kīcaka, who, due to the king's favor, disrespects me and causes me such sorrow, O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 31. You must kill this sinful one as well, this Kīcaka, who, due to the king's favor, disrespects me and causes me such sorrow, O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata).

sanskrit: तमेवं कामसंमत्तं भिन्धि कुम्भमिवाश्मनि ।यो निमित्तमनर्थानां बहूनां मम भारत ॥३२॥
transliteration: tamevaṁ kāmasaṁmattaṁ bhindhi kumbhamivāśmani ,yo nimittamanarthānāṁ bahūnāṁ mama bhārata.
english: Thus, break him, this Kīcaka, who is maddened by desire, just like you would break a pot on a stone. He is indeed the cause of my many misfortunes, O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 32. Thus, break him, this Kīcaka, who is maddened by desire, just like you would break a pot on a stone. He is indeed the cause of my many misfortunes, O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata).

sanskrit: तं चेज्जीवन्तमादित्यः प्रातरभ्युदयिष्यति ।विषमालोड्य पास्यामि मा कीचकवशं गमम् ।श्रेयो हि मरणं मह्यं भीमसेन तवाग्रतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: taṁ cejjīvantamādityaḥ prātarabhyudayiṣyati ,viṣamāloḍya pāsyāmi mā kīcakavaśaṁ gamam ,śreyo hi maraṇaṁ mahyaṁ bhīmasena tavāgrataḥ.
english: If the sun rises tomorrow morning finding me still alive, I will mix and drink poison. Let me not fall under Kīcaka's control. Indeed, Bhīmasena, death in your presence is far better for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 33. If the sun rises tomorrow morning finding me still alive, I will mix and drink poison. Let me not fall under Kīcaka's control. Indeed, Bhīmasena, death in your presence is far better for me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा प्रारुदत्कृष्णा भीमस्योरः समाश्रिता ।भीमश्च तां परिष्वज्य महत्सान्त्वं प्रयुज्य च ।कीचकं मनसागच्छत्सृक्किणी परिसंलिहन् ॥३४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā prārudatkṛṣṇā bhīmasyoraḥ samāśritā ,bhīmaśca tāṁ pariṣvajya mahatsāntvaṁ prayujya ca ,kīcakaṁ manasāgacchatsṛkkiṇī parisaṁlihan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) wept, having taken refuge on Bhīma's chest. Bhīma, embracing her and offering great comfort, mentally approached Kīcaka, licking the corners of his mouth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 20 Verse 34. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) wept, having taken refuge on Bhīma's chest. Bhīma, embracing her and offering great comfort, mentally approached Kīcaka, licking the corners of his mouth.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।तथा भद्रे करिष्यामि यथा त्वं भीरु भाषसे ।अद्य तं सूदयिष्यामि कीचकं सहबान्धवम् ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,tathā bhadre kariṣyāmi yathā tvaṁ bhīru bhāṣase ,adya taṁ sūdayiṣyāmi kīcakaṁ sahabāndhavam.
english: Bhīmasena said: O gentle one, I shall do as you, the timid one, say. Today, I will kill Kīcaka along with his relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. Bhīmasena said: O gentle one, I shall do as you, the timid one, say. Today, I will kill Kīcaka along with his relatives.

sanskrit: अस्याः प्रदोषे शर्वर्याः कुरुष्वानेन संगमम् ।दुःखं शोकं च निर्धूय याज्ञसेनि शुचिस्मिते ॥२॥
transliteration: asyāḥ pradoṣe śarvaryāḥ kuruṣvānena saṁgamam ,duḥkhaṁ śokaṁ ca nirdhūya yājñaseni śucismite.
english: In the early evening of this night, O Yaajnaseni, O pure-smiling one, arrange a meeting with him, having cast off your sorrow and grief.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. In the early evening of this night, O Yaajnaseni, O pure-smiling one, arrange a meeting with him, having cast off your sorrow and grief.

sanskrit: यैषा नर्तनशाला वै मत्स्यराजेन कारिता ।दिवात्र कन्या नृत्यन्ति रात्रौ यान्ति यथागृहम् ॥३॥
transliteration: yaiṣā nartanaśālā vai matsyarājena kāritā ,divātra kanyā nṛtyanti rātrau yānti yathāgṛham.
english: This is indeed the dance hall constructed by the King of Matsya. During the day, girls dance here, and at night, they return to their homes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. This is indeed the dance hall constructed by the King of Matsya. During the day, girls dance here, and at night, they return to their homes.

sanskrit: तत्रास्ति शयनं भीरु दृढाङ्गं सुप्रतिष्ठितम् ।तत्रास्य दर्शयिष्यामि पूर्वप्रेतान्पितामहान् ॥४॥
transliteration: tatrāsti śayanaṁ bhīru dṛḍhāṅgaṁ supratiṣṭhitam ,tatrāsya darśayiṣyāmi pūrvapretānpitāmahān.
english: O timid one, there is a sturdy and well-established bed. There, I will show him his departed ancestors and grandfathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. O timid one, there is a sturdy and well-established bed. There, I will show him his departed ancestors and grandfathers.

sanskrit: यथा च त्वां न पश्येयुः कुर्वाणां तेन संविदम् ।कुर्यास्तथा त्वं कल्याणि यथा संनिहितो भवेत् ॥५॥
transliteration: yathā ca tvāṁ na paśyeyuḥ kurvāṇāṁ tena saṁvidam ,kuryāstathā tvaṁ kalyāṇi yathā saṁnihito bhavet.
english: And so that they may not see you making an agreement with him, you, O auspicious one, should act in such a way that he remains close by.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. And so that they may not see you making an agreement with him, you, O auspicious one, should act in such a way that he remains close by.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा तौ कथयित्वा तु बाष्पमुत्सृज्य दुःखितौ ।रात्रिशेषं तदत्युग्रं धारयामासतुर्हृदा ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā tau kathayitvā tu bāṣpamutsṛjya duḥkhitau ,rātriśeṣaṁ tadatyugraṁ dhārayāmāsaturhṛdā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, and having shed tears, those two, in distress, endured the remainder of that exceedingly dreadful night within their hearts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, and having shed tears, those two, in distress, endured the remainder of that exceedingly dreadful night within their hearts.

sanskrit: तस्यां रात्र्यां व्यतीतायां प्रातरुत्थाय कीचकः ।गत्वा राजकुलायैव द्रौपदीमिदमब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ rātryāṁ vyatītāyāṁ prātarutthāya kīcakaḥ ,gatvā rājakulāyaiva draupadīmidamabravīt.
english: When that night had passed, Kīcaka rose in the morning, went directly to the royal palace, and said this to Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. When that night had passed, Kīcaka rose in the morning, went directly to the royal palace, and said this to Draupadī.

sanskrit: सभायां पश्यतो राज्ञः पातयित्वा पदाहनम् ।न चैवालभथास्त्राणमभिपन्ना बलीयसा ॥८॥
transliteration: sabhāyāṁ paśyato rājñaḥ pātayitvā padāhanam ,na caivālabhathāstrāṇamabhipannā balīyasā.
english: Though you were struck down by a kick from a powerful person in the assembly, before the king's very eyes, you did not obtain any protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. Though you were struck down by a kick from a powerful person in the assembly, before the king's very eyes, you did not obtain any protection.

sanskrit: प्रवादेन हि मत्स्यानां राजा नाम्नायमुच्यते ।अहमेव हि मत्स्यानां राजा वै वाहिनीपतिः ॥९॥
transliteration: pravādena hi matsyānāṁ rājā nāmnāyamucyate ,ahameva hi matsyānāṁ rājā vai vāhinīpatiḥ.
