english: Indeed, the king of the Matsyas is called by this name by reputation. But I myself am truly the king of the Matsyas and the commander of the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. Indeed, the king of the Matsyas is called by this name by reputation. But I myself am truly the king of the Matsyas and the commander of the army.

sanskrit: सा सुखं प्रतिपद्यस्व दासो भीरु भवामि ते ।अह्नाय तव सुश्रोणि शतं निष्कान्ददाम्यहम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sā sukhaṁ pratipadyasva dāso bhīru bhavāmi te ,ahnāya tava suśroṇi śataṁ niṣkāndadāmyaham.
english: Therefore, O timid one, accept happiness; I will become your servant. O beautiful-hipped one, I will immediately give you a hundred gold coins.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. Therefore, O timid one, accept happiness; I will become your servant. O beautiful-hipped one, I will immediately give you a hundred gold coins.

sanskrit: दासीशतं च ते दद्यां दासानामपि चापरम् ।रथं चाश्वतरीयुक्तमस्तु नौ भीरु संगमः ॥११॥
transliteration: dāsīśataṁ ca te dadyāṁ dāsānāmapi cāparam ,rathaṁ cāśvatarīyuktamastu nau bhīru saṁgamaḥ.
english: O timid one, I would give you a hundred maidservants, and another hundred male servants, as well as a chariot yoked with mules. Let our union take place.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. O timid one, I would give you a hundred maidservants, and another hundred male servants, as well as a chariot yoked with mules. Let our union take place.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।एकं मे समयं त्वद्य प्रतिपद्यस्व कीचक ।न त्वां सखा वा भ्राता वा जानीयात्संगतं मया ॥१२॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,ekaṁ me samayaṁ tvadya pratipadyasva kīcaka ,na tvāṁ sakhā vā bhrātā vā jānīyātsaṁgataṁ mayā.
english: Draupadi said, "O Kiicaka, accept one condition from me today: neither a friend nor a brother should know of your union with me."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. Draupadi said, "O Kiicaka, accept one condition from me today: neither a friend nor a brother should know of your union with me."

sanskrit: अवबोधाद्धि भीतास्मि गन्धर्वाणां यशस्विनाम् ।एवं मे प्रतिजानीहि ततोऽहं वशगा तव ॥१३॥
transliteration: avabodhāddhi bhītāsmi gandharvāṇāṁ yaśasvinām ,evaṁ me pratijānīhi tato'haṁ vaśagā tava.
english: Indeed, I am fearful of the glorious Gandharvas gaining knowledge (avabodha) [of our union]. Thus, promise me this, and then I shall be subject to your will.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. Indeed, I am fearful of the glorious Gandharvas gaining knowledge (avabodha) [of our union]. Thus, promise me this, and then I shall be subject to your will.

sanskrit: कीचक उवाच ।एवमेतत्करिष्यामि यथा सुश्रोणि भाषसे ।एको भद्रे गमिष्यामि शून्यमावसथं तव ॥१४॥
transliteration: kīcaka uvāca ,evametatkariṣyāmi yathā suśroṇi bhāṣase ,eko bhadre gamiṣyāmi śūnyamāvasathaṁ tava.
english: Kiicaka said, "O lady with beautiful hips, I shall do exactly as you say. O noble lady, I will go alone to your deserted dwelling."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 14. Kiicaka said, "O lady with beautiful hips, I shall do exactly as you say. O noble lady, I will go alone to your deserted dwelling."

sanskrit: समागमार्थं रम्भोरु त्वया मदनमोहितः ।यथा त्वां नावभोत्स्यन्ति गन्धर्वाः सूर्यवर्चसः ॥१५॥
transliteration: samāgamārthaṁ rambhoru tvayā madanamohitaḥ ,yathā tvāṁ nāvabhotsyanti gandharvāḥ sūryavarcasaḥ.
english: O beautiful-thighed one, infatuated by love for you, (I seek) a meeting, so that the Gandharvas, radiant like the sun, will not recognize you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 15. O beautiful-thighed one, infatuated by love for you, (I seek) a meeting, so that the Gandharvas, radiant like the sun, will not recognize you.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।यदिदं नर्तनागारं मत्स्यराजेन कारितम् ।दिवात्र कन्या नृत्यन्ति रात्रौ यान्ति यथागृहम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,yadidaṁ nartanāgāraṁ matsyarājena kāritam ,divātra kanyā nṛtyanti rātrau yānti yathāgṛham.
english: Draupadī said: "This dancing hall, which was built by the Matsya king - girls dance here during the day, and at night they return to their respective homes."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 16. Draupadī said: "This dancing hall, which was built by the Matsya king - girls dance here during the day, and at night they return to their respective homes."

sanskrit: तमिस्रे तत्र गच्छेथा गन्धर्वास्तन्न जानते ।तत्र दोषः परिहृतो भविष्यति न संशयः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tamisre tatra gacchethā gandharvāstanna jānate ,tatra doṣaḥ parihṛto bhaviṣyati na saṁśayaḥ.
english: You should go there in the darkness; the Gandharvas will not know that. In that case, the danger will undoubtedly be averted.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 17. You should go there in the darkness; the Gandharvas will not know that. In that case, the danger will undoubtedly be averted.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तमर्थं प्रतिजल्पन्त्याः कृष्णायाः कीचकेन ह ।दिवसार्धं समभवन्मासेनैव समं नृप ॥१८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tamarthaṁ pratijalpantyāḥ kṛṣṇāyāḥ kīcakena ha ,divasārdhaṁ samabhavanmāsenaiva samaṁ nṛpa.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, while Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was indeed replying to Kīcaka about that matter, half a day became equivalent to a whole month."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 18. Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, while Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was indeed replying to Kīcaka about that matter, half a day became equivalent to a whole month."

sanskrit: कीचकोऽथ गृहं गत्वा भृशं हर्षपरिप्लुतः ।सैरन्ध्रीरूपिणं मूढो मृत्युं तं नावबुद्धवान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: kīcako'tha gṛhaṁ gatvā bhṛśaṁ harṣapariplutaḥ ,sairandhrīrūpiṇaṁ mūḍho mṛtyuṁ taṁ nāvabuddhavān.
english: Then, Kīcaka, having gone home, was greatly overcome with joy. That deluded one did not realize that she, in the form of Sairandhrī, was his death (mṛtyu).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 19. Then, Kīcaka, having gone home, was greatly overcome with joy. That deluded one did not realize that she, in the form of Sairandhrī, was his death (mṛtyu).

sanskrit: गन्धाभरणमाल्येषु व्यासक्तः स विशेषतः ।अलंचकार सोऽऽत्मानं सत्वरः काममोहितः ॥२०॥
transliteration: gandhābharaṇamālyeṣu vyāsaktaḥ sa viśeṣataḥ ,alaṁcakāra so''tmānaṁ satvaraḥ kāmamohitaḥ.
english: Especially engrossed in perfumes, jewelry, and garlands, he quickly adorned himself, infatuated by passion (kāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 20. Especially engrossed in perfumes, jewelry, and garlands, he quickly adorned himself, infatuated by passion (kāma).

sanskrit: तस्य तत्कुर्वतः कर्म कालो दीर्घ इवाभवत् ।अनुचिन्तयतश्चापि तामेवायतलोचनाम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasya tatkurvataḥ karma kālo dīrgha ivābhavat ,anucintayataścāpi tāmevāyatalocanām.
english: For him, while he was engaged in that act, time seemed to stretch endlessly. This was also true as he reflected upon that very long-eyed woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 21. For him, while he was engaged in that act, time seemed to stretch endlessly. This was also true as he reflected upon that very long-eyed woman.

sanskrit: आसीदभ्यधिका चास्य श्रीः श्रियं प्रमुमुक्षतः ।निर्वाणकाले दीपस्य वर्तीमिव दिधक्षतः ॥२२॥
transliteration: āsīdabhyadhikā cāsya śrīḥ śriyaṁ pramumukṣataḥ ,nirvāṇakāle dīpasya vartīmiva didhakṣataḥ.
english: For him, as he was about to lose his splendor (śrī), there was actually even greater splendor, just like a lamp at the moment of flickering out appears to desire to consume its wick.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 22. For him, as he was about to lose his splendor (śrī), there was actually even greater splendor, just like a lamp at the moment of flickering out appears to desire to consume its wick.

sanskrit: कृतसंप्रत्ययस्तत्र कीचकः काममोहितः ।नाजानाद्दिवसं यान्तं चिन्तयानः समागमम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: kṛtasaṁpratyayastatra kīcakaḥ kāmamohitaḥ ,nājānāddivasaṁ yāntaṁ cintayānaḥ samāgamam.
english: Kīcaka, who was convinced there and deluded by desire, did not realize the day was passing, as he pondered the anticipated meeting.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 23. Kīcaka, who was convinced there and deluded by desire, did not realize the day was passing, as he pondered the anticipated meeting.

sanskrit: ततस्तु द्रौपदी गत्वा तदा भीमं महानसे ।उपातिष्ठत कल्याणी कौरव्यं पतिमन्तिकात् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatastu draupadī gatvā tadā bhīmaṁ mahānase ,upātiṣṭhata kalyāṇī kauravyaṁ patimantikāt.
english: Then, the auspicious (kalyāṇī) Draupadī went to the kitchen and approached her husband, Bhīma, the descendant of Kuru, from close by.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 24. Then, the auspicious (kalyāṇī) Draupadī went to the kitchen and approached her husband, Bhīma, the descendant of Kuru, from close by.

sanskrit: तमुवाच सुकेशान्ता कीचकस्य मया कृतः ।संगमो नर्तनागारे यथावोचः परंतप ॥२५॥
transliteration: tamuvāca sukeśāntā kīcakasya mayā kṛtaḥ ,saṁgamo nartanāgāre yathāvocaḥ paraṁtapa.
english: The one with beautiful hair (sukeśāntā) said to him, "I have arranged a meeting with Kīcaka in the dancing hall, just as you instructed, O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa)."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 25. The one with beautiful hair (sukeśāntā) said to him, "I have arranged a meeting with Kīcaka in the dancing hall, just as you instructed, O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa)."

sanskrit: शून्यं स नर्तनागारमागमिष्यति कीचकः ।एको निशि महाबाहो कीचकं तं निषूदय ॥२६॥
transliteration: śūnyaṁ sa nartanāgāramāgamiṣyati kīcakaḥ ,eko niśi mahābāho kīcakaṁ taṁ niṣūdaya.
english: Kīcaka will come alone to the empty dancing hall at night, O mighty-armed one (mahābāho). Slay that Kīcaka!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 26. Kīcaka will come alone to the empty dancing hall at night, O mighty-armed one (mahābāho). Slay that Kīcaka!

sanskrit: तं सूतपुत्रं कौन्तेय कीचकं मददर्पितम् ।गत्वा त्वं नर्तनागारं निर्जीवं कुरु पाण्डव ॥२७॥
transliteration: taṁ sūtaputraṁ kaunteya kīcakaṁ madadarpitam ,gatvā tvaṁ nartanāgāraṁ nirjīvaṁ kuru pāṇḍava.
english: O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), go to the dance hall and make that charioteer's son Kīcaka, who is intoxicated with arrogance, lifeless, O son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍava).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 27. O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), go to the dance hall and make that charioteer's son Kīcaka, who is intoxicated with arrogance, lifeless, O son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍava).

sanskrit: दर्पाच्च सूतपुत्रोऽसौ गन्धर्वानवमन्यते ।तं त्वं प्रहरतां श्रेष्ठ नडं नाग इवोद्धर ॥२८॥
transliteration: darpācca sūtaputro'sau gandharvānavamanyate ,taṁ tvaṁ praharatāṁ śreṣṭha naḍaṁ nāga ivoddhara.
english: And that son of the charioteer, due to his arrogance, disrespects the Gandharvas. O best among strikers, you should uproot him like an elephant pulls up a reed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 28. And that son of the charioteer, due to his arrogance, disrespects the Gandharvas. O best among strikers, you should uproot him like an elephant pulls up a reed.

sanskrit: अश्रु दुःखाभिभूताया मम मार्जस्व भारत ।आत्मनश्चैव भद्रं ते कुरु मानं कुलस्य च ॥२९॥
transliteration: aśru duḥkhābhibhūtāyā mama mārjasva bhārata ,ātmanaścaiva bhadraṁ te kuru mānaṁ kulasya ca.
english: O Bhārata, wipe away the tears of me, who am overwhelmed by sorrow. And for your own self (ātman), bring about good fortune, and for your lineage (kula) also, restore its honor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 29. O Bhārata, wipe away the tears of me, who am overwhelmed by sorrow. And for your own self (ātman), bring about good fortune, and for your lineage (kula) also, restore its honor.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।स्वागतं ते वरारोहे यन्मा वेदयसे प्रियम् ।न ह्यस्य कंचिदिच्छामि सहायं वरवर्णिनि ॥३०॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,svāgataṁ te varārohe yanmā vedayase priyam ,na hyasya kaṁcidicchāmi sahāyaṁ varavarṇini.
english: Bhīmasena said: "Welcome to you, O lady of beautiful hips, that you inform me of what is dear. Indeed, for him, I do not desire any helper, O beautiful lady."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 30. Bhīmasena said: "Welcome to you, O lady of beautiful hips, that you inform me of what is dear. Indeed, for him, I do not desire any helper, O beautiful lady."

sanskrit: या मे प्रीतिस्त्वयाख्याता कीचकस्य समागमे ।हत्वा हिडिम्बं सा प्रीतिर्ममासीद्वरवर्णिनि ॥३१॥
transliteration: yā me prītistvayākhyātā kīcakasya samāgame ,hatvā hiḍimbaṁ sā prītirmamāsīdvaravarṇini.
english: O beautiful woman, the delight you described regarding Kīcaka's encounter (with his fate) – that very delight was mine when I killed Hiḍimba.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 31. O beautiful woman, the delight you described regarding Kīcaka's encounter (with his fate) – that very delight was mine when I killed Hiḍimba.

sanskrit: सत्यं भ्रातॄंश्च धर्मं च पुरस्कृत्य ब्रवीमि ते ।कीचकं निहनिष्यामि वृत्रं देवपतिर्यथा ॥३२॥
transliteration: satyaṁ bhrātṝṁśca dharmaṁ ca puraskṛtya bravīmi te ,kīcakaṁ nihaniṣyāmi vṛtraṁ devapatiryathā.
english: I declare to you, holding truth, my brothers, and (natural law) dharma as paramount: I will kill Kīcaka, just as the lord of the gods (Indra) killed Vṛtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 32. I declare to you, holding truth, my brothers, and (natural law) dharma as paramount: I will kill Kīcaka, just as the lord of the gods (Indra) killed Vṛtra.

sanskrit: तं गह्वरे प्रकाशे वा पोथयिष्यामि कीचकम् ।अथ चेदवभोत्स्यन्ति हंस्ये मत्स्यानपि ध्रुवम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: taṁ gahvare prakāśe vā pothayiṣyāmi kīcakam ,atha cedavabhotsyanti haṁsye matsyānapi dhruvam.
english: I will crush Kīcaka, whether in a secret place or in the open. Furthermore, if they (the Matsyas) discover (my action), I will certainly kill even the Matsyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 33. I will crush Kīcaka, whether in a secret place or in the open. Furthermore, if they (the Matsyas) discover (my action), I will certainly kill even the Matsyas.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनं हत्वा प्रतिपत्स्ये वसुंधराम् ।कामं मत्स्यमुपास्तां हि कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ॥३४॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaṁ hatvā pratipatsye vasuṁdharām ,kāmaṁ matsyamupāstāṁ hi kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: After that, having killed Duryodhana, I will regain the earth. Let Yudhiṣṭhira, Kuntī's son, indeed serve the Matsya (king) as he desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 34. After that, having killed Duryodhana, I will regain the earth. Let Yudhiṣṭhira, Kuntī's son, indeed serve the Matsya (king) as he desires.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।यथा न संत्यजेथास्त्वं सत्यं वै मत्कृते विभो ।निगूढस्त्वं तथा वीर कीचकं विनिपातय ॥३५॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,yathā na saṁtyajethāstvaṁ satyaṁ vai matkṛte vibho ,nigūḍhastvaṁ tathā vīra kīcakaṁ vinipātaya.
english: Draupadī said: "Just as you would not forsake truth (satya) for my sake, O all-pervading one, in the same way, while remaining concealed, O hero, strike down Kīcaka!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 35. Draupadī said: "Just as you would not forsake truth (satya) for my sake, O all-pervading one, in the same way, while remaining concealed, O hero, strike down Kīcaka!"

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।एवमेतत्करिष्यामि यथा त्वं भीरु भाषसे ।अदृश्यमानस्तस्याद्य तमस्विन्यामनिन्दिते ॥३६॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,evametatkariṣyāmi yathā tvaṁ bhīru bhāṣase ,adṛśyamānastasyādya tamasvinyāmanindite.
english: Bhīmasena said: "I will certainly do this exactly as you, O timid one, instruct. Remaining unseen by him today, in the dark night, O blameless one."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 36. Bhīmasena said: "I will certainly do this exactly as you, O timid one, instruct. Remaining unseen by him today, in the dark night, O blameless one."

sanskrit: नागो बिल्वमिवाक्रम्य पोथयिष्याम्यहं शिरः ।अलभ्यामिच्छतस्तस्य कीचकस्य दुरात्मनः ॥३७॥
transliteration: nāgo bilvamivākramya pothayiṣyāmyahaṁ śiraḥ ,alabhyāmicchatastasya kīcakasya durātmanaḥ.
english: I will crush his head just as a serpent (nāga) crushes a bael fruit, the head of that evil-minded (durātman) Kīcaka who desires the unattainable.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 37. I will crush his head just as a serpent (nāga) crushes a bael fruit, the head of that evil-minded (durātman) Kīcaka who desires the unattainable.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।भीमोऽथ प्रथमं गत्वा रात्रौ छन्न उपाविशत् ।मृगं हरिरिवादृश्यः प्रत्याकाङ्क्षत्स कीचकम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,bhīmo'tha prathamaṁ gatvā rātrau channa upāviśat ,mṛgaṁ haririvādṛśyaḥ pratyākāṅkṣatsa kīcakam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Bhīma then went first and sat down hidden in the night. Unseen, like a lion (hari) awaiting its prey (mṛga), he waited for Kīcaka."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 38. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Bhīma then went first and sat down hidden in the night. Unseen, like a lion (hari) awaiting its prey (mṛga), he waited for Kīcaka."

sanskrit: कीचकश्चाप्यलंकृत्य यथाकाममुपाव्रजत् ।तां वेलां नर्तनागारे पाञ्चालीसंगमाशया ॥३९॥
transliteration: kīcakaścāpyalaṁkṛtya yathākāmamupāvrajat ,tāṁ velāṁ nartanāgāre pāñcālīsaṁgamāśayā.
english: Keycaka, having adorned himself as he wished, approached the dancing hall at that time, with the hope of uniting with Draupadī (Pāñcālī).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 39. Keycaka, having adorned himself as he wished, approached the dancing hall at that time, with the hope of uniting with Draupadī (Pāñcālī).

sanskrit: मन्यमानः स संकेतमागारं प्राविशच्च तम् ।प्रविश्य च स तद्वेश्म तमसा संवृतं महत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: manyamānaḥ sa saṁketamāgāraṁ prāviśacca tam ,praviśya ca sa tadveśma tamasā saṁvṛtaṁ mahat.
english: Believing it to be the appointed meeting place, he entered that building. And having entered that vast dwelling, which was enveloped in darkness, he then...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 40. Believing it to be the appointed meeting place, he entered that building. And having entered that vast dwelling, which was enveloped in darkness, he then...

sanskrit: पूर्वागतं ततस्तत्र भीममप्रतिमौजसम् ।एकान्तमास्थितं चैनमाससाद सुदुर्मतिः ॥४१॥
transliteration: pūrvāgataṁ tatastatra bhīmamapratimaujasam ,ekāntamāsthitaṁ cainamāsasāda sudurmatiḥ.
english: Then, the extremely evil-minded (sudurmatiḥ) Keycaka found there Bhīma, who had arrived earlier, possessing unequalled might, and who was situated in a secluded spot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 41. Then, the extremely evil-minded (sudurmatiḥ) Keycaka found there Bhīma, who had arrived earlier, possessing unequalled might, and who was situated in a secluded spot.

sanskrit: शयानं शयने तत्र मृत्युं सूतः परामृशत् ।जाज्वल्यमानं कोपेन कृष्णाधर्षणजेन ह ॥४२॥
transliteration: śayānaṁ śayane tatra mṛtyuṁ sūtaḥ parāmṛśat ,jājvalyamānaṁ kopena kṛṣṇādharṣaṇajena ha.
english: There, lying on a couch, the charioteer (sūtaḥ) (Keycaka) encountered (pāramṛśat) Death itself, blazing with the rage born from the insult to Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā). The particle "ha" indicates emphasis.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 42. There, lying on a couch, the charioteer (sūtaḥ) (Keycaka) encountered (pāramṛśat) Death itself, blazing with the rage born from the insult to Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā). The particle "ha" indicates emphasis.

sanskrit: उपसंगम्य चैवैनं कीचकः काममोहितः ।हर्षोन्मथितचित्तात्मा स्मयमानोऽभ्यभाषत ॥४३॥
transliteration: upasaṁgamya caivainaṁ kīcakaḥ kāmamohitaḥ ,harṣonmathitacittātmā smayamāno'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Keechaka, infatuated by desire (kāma) and with his mind and spirit (ātman) agitated by joy, approached her and, smiling, addressed her.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 43. Keechaka, infatuated by desire (kāma) and with his mind and spirit (ātman) agitated by joy, approached her and, smiling, addressed her.

sanskrit: प्रापितं ते मया वित्तं बहुरूपमनन्तकम् ।तत्सर्वं त्वां समुद्दिश्य सहसा समुपागतः ॥४४॥
transliteration: prāpitaṁ te mayā vittaṁ bahurūpamanantakam ,tatsarvaṁ tvāṁ samuddiśya sahasā samupāgataḥ.
english: I have acquired for you boundless and diverse wealth. I have swiftly approached (you) with all of that (wealth) intended for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 44. I have acquired for you boundless and diverse wealth. I have swiftly approached (you) with all of that (wealth) intended for your sake.

sanskrit: नाकस्मान्मां प्रशंसन्ति सदा गृहगताः स्त्रियः ।सुवासा दर्शनीयश्च नान्योऽस्ति त्वादृशः पुमान् ॥४५॥
transliteration: nākasmānmāṁ praśaṁsanti sadā gṛhagatāḥ striyaḥ ,suvāsā darśanīyaśca nānyo'sti tvādṛśaḥ pumān.
english: The women residing in my house always praise me, and not without cause. There is no other man as handsome and well-dressed as I am.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 45. The women residing in my house always praise me, and not without cause. There is no other man as handsome and well-dressed as I am.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।दिष्ट्या त्वं दर्शनीयोऽसि दिष्ट्यात्मानं प्रशंससि ।ईदृशस्तु त्वया स्पर्शः स्पृष्टपूर्वो न कर्हिचित् ॥४६॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,diṣṭyā tvaṁ darśanīyo'si diṣṭyātmānaṁ praśaṁsasi ,īdṛśastu tvayā sparśaḥ spṛṣṭapūrvo na karhicit.
english: Bhimasena said: "It is indeed fortunate that you are handsome! It is indeed fortunate that you praise your own self (ātman)! But a touch like this has surely never been experienced by you before."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 46. Bhimasena said: "It is indeed fortunate that you are handsome! It is indeed fortunate that you praise your own self (ātman)! But a touch like this has surely never been experienced by you before."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा तं महाबाहुर्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ।समुत्पत्य च कौन्तेयः प्रहस्य च नराधमम् ।भीमो जग्राह केशेषु माल्यवत्सु सुगन्धिषु ॥४७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā taṁ mahābāhurbhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ ,samutpatya ca kaunteyaḥ prahasya ca narādhamam ,bhīmo jagrāha keśeṣu mālyavatsu sugandhiṣu.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, the mighty-armed Bhīma, possessing terrible valor, sprang up, and that son of Kuntī (Bhīma), laughing, seized that wretch among men (Kīcaka) by his hair, which was adorned with garlands and fragrant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 47. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, the mighty-armed Bhīma, possessing terrible valor, sprang up, and that son of Kuntī (Bhīma), laughing, seized that wretch among men (Kīcaka) by his hair, which was adorned with garlands and fragrant.

sanskrit: स केशेषु परामृष्टो बलेन बलिनां वरः ।आक्षिप्य केशान्वेगेन बाह्वोर्जग्राह पाण्डवम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: sa keśeṣu parāmṛṣṭo balena balināṁ varaḥ ,ākṣipya keśānvegena bāhvorjagrāha pāṇḍavam.
english: He (Kīcaka), the best among the strong, being seized forcefully by the hair, swiftly pulled his hair away and grabbed the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) by both arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 48. He (Kīcaka), the best among the strong, being seized forcefully by the hair, swiftly pulled his hair away and grabbed the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma) by both arms.

sanskrit: बाहुयुद्धं तयोरासीत्क्रुद्धयोर्नरसिंहयोः ।वसन्ते वाशिताहेतोर्बलवद्गजयोरिव ॥४९॥
transliteration: bāhuyuddhaṁ tayorāsītkruddhayornarasiṁhayoḥ ,vasante vāśitāhetorbalavadgajayoriva.
english: A wrestling match ensued between those two enraged lion-like men, like that of two powerful elephants in spring, fighting over a female.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 49. A wrestling match ensued between those two enraged lion-like men, like that of two powerful elephants in spring, fighting over a female.

sanskrit: ईषदागलितं चापि क्रोधाच्चलपदं स्थितम् ।कीचको बलवान्भीमं जानुभ्यामाक्षिपद्भुवि ॥५०॥
transliteration: īṣadāgalitaṁ cāpi krodhāccalapadaṁ sthitam ,kīcako balavānbhīmaṁ jānubhyāmākṣipadbhuvi.
english: Kīcaka, who was strong, although somewhat loosened (from Bhīma's grip) and staggering from rage, then threw Bhīma to the ground with his knees.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 50. Kīcaka, who was strong, although somewhat loosened (from Bhīma's grip) and staggering from rage, then threw Bhīma to the ground with his knees.

sanskrit: पातितो भुवि भीमस्तु कीचकेन बलीयसा ।उत्पपाताथ वेगेन दण्डाहत इवोरगः ॥५१॥
transliteration: pātito bhuvi bhīmastu kīcakena balīyasā ,utpapātātha vegena daṇḍāhata ivoragaḥ.
english: Though thrown to the ground by the more powerful Kichaka, Bhima then sprang up swiftly, like a serpent struck by a stick.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 51. Though thrown to the ground by the more powerful Kichaka, Bhima then sprang up swiftly, like a serpent struck by a stick.

sanskrit: स्पर्धया च बलोन्मत्तौ तावुभौ सूतपाण्डवौ ।निशीथे पर्यकर्षेतां बलिनौ निशि निर्जने ॥५२॥
transliteration: spardhayā ca balonmattau tāvubhau sūtapāṇḍavau ,niśīthe paryakarṣetāṁ balinau niśi nirjane.
english: And those two, the charioteer's son (Kichaka) and the son of Pandu (Bhima), both powerful and maddened by rivalry and strength, wrestled with each other at midnight in a secluded place.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 52. And those two, the charioteer's son (Kichaka) and the son of Pandu (Bhima), both powerful and maddened by rivalry and strength, wrestled with each other at midnight in a secluded place.

sanskrit: ततस्तद्भवनश्रेष्ठं प्राकम्पत मुहुर्मुहुः ।बलवच्चापि संक्रुद्धावन्योन्यं तावगर्जताम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: tatastadbhavanaśreṣṭhaṁ prākampata muhurmuhuḥ ,balavaccāpi saṁkruddhāvanyonyaṁ tāvagarjatām.
english: Then, that magnificent palace trembled again and again. And those two, powerfully enraged, roared at each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 53. Then, that magnificent palace trembled again and again. And those two, powerfully enraged, roared at each other.

sanskrit: तलाभ्यां तु स भीमेन वक्षस्यभिहतो बली ।कीचको रोषसंतप्तः पदान्न चलितः पदम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: talābhyāṁ tu sa bhīmena vakṣasyabhihato balī ,kīcako roṣasaṁtaptaḥ padānna calitaḥ padam.
english: But Kichaka, that strong man, though struck on the chest by Bhima with his palms and burning with rage, did not move an inch from his place.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 54. But Kichaka, that strong man, though struck on the chest by Bhima with his palms and burning with rage, did not move an inch from his place.

sanskrit: मुहूर्तं तु स तं वेगं सहित्वा भुवि दुःसहम् ।बलादहीयत तदा सूतो भीमबलार्दितः ॥५५॥
transliteration: muhūrtaṁ tu sa taṁ vegaṁ sahitvā bhuvi duḥsaham ,balādahīyata tadā sūto bhīmabalārditaḥ.
english: For a moment, however, that charioteer (sūta), oppressed by Bhīma's strength, endured that unbearable force on the ground. Then, by force, he was overcome.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 55. For a moment, however, that charioteer (sūta), oppressed by Bhīma's strength, endured that unbearable force on the ground. Then, by force, he was overcome.

sanskrit: तं हीयमानं विज्ञाय भीमसेनो महाबलः ।वक्षस्यानीय वेगेन ममन्थैनं विचेतसम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: taṁ hīyamānaṁ vijñāya bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,vakṣasyānīya vegena mamanthainaṁ vicetasam.
english: Seeing him giving up, the mighty Bhīmasena quickly brought him to his chest and crushed that senseless man.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 56. Seeing him giving up, the mighty Bhīmasena quickly brought him to his chest and crushed that senseless man.

sanskrit: क्रोधाविष्टो विनिःश्वस्य पुनश्चैनं वृकोदरः ।जग्राह जयतां श्रेष्ठः केशेष्वेव तदा भृशम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: krodhāviṣṭo viniḥśvasya punaścainaṁ vṛkodaraḥ ,jagrāha jayatāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ keśeṣveva tadā bhṛśam.
english: Enraged, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) sighed heavily, and then that best among conquerors seized him again, very tightly by his hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 57. Enraged, Vṛkodara (Bhīma) sighed heavily, and then that best among conquerors seized him again, very tightly by his hair.

sanskrit: गृहीत्वा कीचकं भीमो विरुराव महाबलः ।शार्दूलः पिशिताकाङ्क्षी गृहीत्वेव महामृगम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: gṛhītvā kīcakaṁ bhīmo virurāva mahābalaḥ ,śārdūlaḥ piśitākāṅkṣī gṛhītveva mahāmṛgam.
english: Having seized Kīcaka, the mighty Bhīma roared, just like a tiger eager for flesh (piśita), having seized a large animal.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 58. Having seized Kīcaka, the mighty Bhīma roared, just like a tiger eager for flesh (piśita), having seized a large animal.

sanskrit: तस्य पादौ च पाणी च शिरो ग्रीवां च सर्वशः ।काये प्रवेशयामास पशोरिव पिनाकधृक् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tasya pādau ca pāṇī ca śiro grīvāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,kāye praveśayāmāsa paśoriva pinākadhṛk.
english: The wielder of Pinaka (Bhīma) completely inserted his feet, hands, head, and neck into [Kīcaka's] body, as one would do to an animal.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 59. The wielder of Pinaka (Bhīma) completely inserted his feet, hands, head, and neck into [Kīcaka's] body, as one would do to an animal.

sanskrit: तं संमथितसर्वाङ्गं मांसपिण्डोपमं कृतम् ।कृष्णायै दर्शयामास भीमसेनो महाबलः ॥६०॥
transliteration: taṁ saṁmathitasarvāṅgaṁ māṁsapiṇḍopamaṁ kṛtam ,kṛṣṇāyai darśayāmāsa bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ.
english: Bhīmasena, the immensely powerful one, then showed him (Kīcaka) to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who had been reduced to a shapeless mass of flesh with all his limbs crushed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 60. Bhīmasena, the immensely powerful one, then showed him (Kīcaka) to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who had been reduced to a shapeless mass of flesh with all his limbs crushed.

sanskrit: उवाच च महातेजा द्रौपदीं पाण्डुनन्दनः ।पश्यैनमेहि पाञ्चालि कामुकोऽयं यथा कृतः ॥६१॥
transliteration: uvāca ca mahātejā draupadīṁ pāṇḍunandanaḥ ,paśyainamehi pāñcāli kāmuko'yaṁ yathā kṛtaḥ.
english: And the greatly radiant son of Pāṇḍu (Bhīma) said to Draupadī, "Come, Pāñcālī, see how this lustful man has been dealt with (rendered)."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 61. And the greatly radiant son of Pāṇḍu (Bhīma) said to Draupadī, "Come, Pāñcālī, see how this lustful man has been dealt with (rendered)."

sanskrit: तथा स कीचकं हत्वा गत्वा रोषस्य वै शमम् ।आमन्त्र्य द्रौपदीं कृष्णां क्षिप्रमायान्महानसम् ॥६२॥
transliteration: tathā sa kīcakaṁ hatvā gatvā roṣasya vai śamam ,āmantrya draupadīṁ kṛṣṇāṁ kṣipramāyānmahānasam.
english: Having thus killed Kīcaka, and with his anger indeed pacified, he (Bhīma) bid farewell to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) and quickly went to the great kitchen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 62. Having thus killed Kīcaka, and with his anger indeed pacified, he (Bhīma) bid farewell to Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) and quickly went to the great kitchen.

sanskrit: कीचकं घातयित्वा तु द्रौपदी योषितां वरा ।प्रहृष्टा गतसंतापा सभापालानुवाच ह ॥६३॥
transliteration: kīcakaṁ ghātayitvā tu draupadī yoṣitāṁ varā ,prahṛṣṭā gatasaṁtāpā sabhāpālānuvāca ha.
english: The excellent Draupadi, foremost among women, having caused Kicaka to be killed, became greatly delighted and free from distress. She then spoke to the hall-guards.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 63. The excellent Draupadi, foremost among women, having caused Kicaka to be killed, became greatly delighted and free from distress. She then spoke to the hall-guards.

sanskrit: कीचकोऽयं हतः शेते गन्धर्वैः पतिभिर्मम ।परस्त्रीकामसंमत्तः समागच्छत पश्यत ॥६४॥
transliteration: kīcako'yaṁ hataḥ śete gandharvaiḥ patibhirmama ,parastrīkāmasaṁmattaḥ samāgacchata paśyata.
english: This Kicaka, who was killed by my Gandharva husbands, lies here, maddened by desire for another's wife. Come and see!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 64. This Kicaka, who was killed by my Gandharva husbands, lies here, maddened by desire for another's wife. Come and see!

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा भाषितं तस्या नर्तनागाररक्षिणः ।सहसैव समाजग्मुरादायोल्काः सहस्रशः ॥६५॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā bhāṣitaṁ tasyā nartanāgārarakṣiṇaḥ ,sahasaiva samājagmurādāyolkāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Upon hearing her words, the guards of the dancing hall immediately assembled, bringing thousands of torches.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 65. Upon hearing her words, the guards of the dancing hall immediately assembled, bringing thousands of torches.

sanskrit: ततो गत्वाथ तद्वेश्म कीचकं विनिपातितम् ।गतासुं ददृशुर्भूमौ रुधिरेण समुक्षितम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: tato gatvātha tadveśma kīcakaṁ vinipātitam ,gatāsuṁ dadṛśurbhūmau rudhireṇa samukṣitam.
english: Then, having gone to that house, they saw Kicaka, who had been killed and was lying lifeless on the ground, soaked in blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 66. Then, having gone to that house, they saw Kicaka, who had been killed and was lying lifeless on the ground, soaked in blood.

sanskrit: क्वास्य ग्रीवा क्व चरणौ क्व पाणी क्व शिरस्तथा ।इति स्म तं परीक्षन्ते गन्धर्वेण हतं तदा ॥६७॥
transliteration: kvāsya grīvā kva caraṇau kva pāṇī kva śirastathā ,iti sma taṁ parīkṣante gandharveṇa hataṁ tadā.
english: Then, they examined him, who had been slain by the Gandharva, asking, 'Where is his neck? Where are his feet? Where are his hands? Where is his head?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 21 Verse 67. Then, they examined him, who had been slain by the Gandharva, asking, 'Where is his neck? Where are his feet? Where are his hands? Where is his head?'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मिन्काले समागम्य सर्वे तत्रास्य बान्धवाः ।रुरुदुः कीचकं दृष्ट्वा परिवार्य समन्ततः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasminkāle samāgamya sarve tatrāsya bāndhavāḥ ,ruruduḥ kīcakaṁ dṛṣṭvā parivārya samantataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: At that time, all his relatives assembled there. Seeing Kīcaka, they surrounded him from all sides and wept.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: At that time, all his relatives assembled there. Seeing Kīcaka, they surrounded him from all sides and wept.

sanskrit: सर्वे संहृष्टरोमाणः संत्रस्ताः प्रेक्ष्य कीचकम् ।तथा सर्वाङ्गसंभुग्नं कूर्मं स्थल इवोद्धृतम् ॥२॥
transliteration: sarve saṁhṛṣṭaromāṇaḥ saṁtrastāḥ prekṣya kīcakam ,tathā sarvāṅgasaṁbhugnaṁ kūrmaṁ sthala ivoddhṛtam.
english: All of them, with their hair standing on end and greatly terrified, beheld Kīcaka, whose limbs were completely contorted, resembling a tortoise pulled out onto land.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. All of them, with their hair standing on end and greatly terrified, beheld Kīcaka, whose limbs were completely contorted, resembling a tortoise pulled out onto land.

sanskrit: पोथितं भीमसेनेन तमिन्द्रेणेव दानवम् ।संस्कारयितुमिच्छन्तो बहिर्नेतुं प्रचक्रमुः ॥३॥
transliteration: pothitaṁ bhīmasenena tamindreṇeva dānavam ,saṁskārayitumicchanto bahirnetuṁ pracakramuḥ.
english: Desiring to perform the funeral rites (saṃskāra), they began to carry outside him, who had been battered by Bhīmasena just as a Dānava demon is battered by Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. Desiring to perform the funeral rites (saṃskāra), they began to carry outside him, who had been battered by Bhīmasena just as a Dānava demon is battered by Indra.

sanskrit: ददृशुस्ते ततः कृष्णां सूतपुत्राः समागताः ।अदूरादनवद्याङ्गीं स्तम्भमालिङ्ग्य तिष्ठतीम् ॥४॥
transliteration: dadṛśuste tataḥ kṛṣṇāṁ sūtaputrāḥ samāgatāḥ ,adūrādanavadyāṅgīṁ stambhamāliṅgya tiṣṭhatīm.
english: Then, the assembled charioteers' sons saw Draupadī (kṛṣṇā), who had flawless limbs, standing nearby and embracing a pillar.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. Then, the assembled charioteers' sons saw Draupadī (kṛṣṇā), who had flawless limbs, standing nearby and embracing a pillar.

sanskrit: समवेतेषु सूतेषु तानुवाचोपकीचकः ।हन्यतां शीघ्रमसती यत्कृते कीचको हतः ॥५॥
transliteration: samaveteṣu sūteṣu tānuvācopakīcakaḥ ,hanyatāṁ śīghramasatī yatkṛte kīcako hataḥ.
english: When the charioteers had gathered, Upakīcaka spoke to them: "Let that unchaste woman be quickly slain, on account of whom Kīcaka was killed."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. When the charioteers had gathered, Upakīcaka spoke to them: "Let that unchaste woman be quickly slain, on account of whom Kīcaka was killed."

sanskrit: अथ वा नेह हन्तव्या दह्यतां कामिना सह ।मृतस्यापि प्रियं कार्यं सूतपुत्रस्य सर्वथा ॥६॥
transliteration: atha vā neha hantavyā dahyatāṁ kāminā saha ,mṛtasyāpi priyaṁ kāryaṁ sūtaputrasya sarvathā.
english: Alternatively, she should not be killed here. Let her be burned along with her lover. By all means, what is pleasing (priyam) to the dead charioteer's son must be accomplished (kāryam).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. Alternatively, she should not be killed here. Let her be burned along with her lover. By all means, what is pleasing (priyam) to the dead charioteer's son must be accomplished (kāryam).

sanskrit: ततो विराटमूचुस्ते कीचकोऽस्याः कृते हतः ।सहाद्यानेन दह्येत तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥७॥
transliteration: tato virāṭamūcuste kīcako'syāḥ kṛte hataḥ ,sahādyānena dahyeta tadanujñātumarhasi.
english: Then they (the charioteers' sons) said to King Virāṭa: "Kīcaka was killed because of her. She should be burned with him today. You should permit that."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. Then they (the charioteers' sons) said to King Virāṭa: "Kīcaka was killed because of her. She should be burned with him today. You should permit that."

sanskrit: पराक्रमं तु सूतानां मत्वा राजान्वमोदत ।सैरन्ध्र्याः सूतपुत्रेण सह दाहं विशां पते ॥८॥
transliteration: parākramaṁ tu sūtānāṁ matvā rājānvamodata ,sairandhryāḥ sūtaputreṇa saha dāhaṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: O lord of men, the king, having considered the might of the charioteers, consented to the burning of Sairandhrī along with the charioteer's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. O lord of men, the king, having considered the might of the charioteers, consented to the burning of Sairandhrī along with the charioteer's son.

sanskrit: तां समासाद्य वित्रस्तां कृष्णां कमललोचनाम् ।मोमुह्यमानां ते तत्र जगृहुः कीचका भृशम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tāṁ samāsādya vitrastāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ kamalalocanām ,momuhyamānāṁ te tatra jagṛhuḥ kīcakā bhṛśam.
english: Approaching her, the lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇā, who was terrified and greatly bewildered, the Kīcakas forcefully seized her there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. Approaching her, the lotus-eyed Kṛṣṇā, who was terrified and greatly bewildered, the Kīcakas forcefully seized her there.

sanskrit: ततस्तु तां समारोप्य निबध्य च सुमध्यमाम् ।जग्मुरुद्यम्य ते सर्वे श्मशानमभितस्तदा ॥१०॥
transliteration: tatastu tāṁ samāropya nibadhya ca sumadhyamām ,jagmurudyamya te sarve śmaśānamabhitastadā.
english: Then, having placed and bound her, the slender-waisted one, all of them, having lifted (the corpse), went towards the cremation ground at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. Then, having placed and bound her, the slender-waisted one, all of them, having lifted (the corpse), went towards the cremation ground at that time.

sanskrit: ह्रियमाणा तु सा राजन्सूतपुत्रैरनिन्दिता ।प्राक्रोशन्नाथमिच्छन्ती कृष्णा नाथवती सती ॥११॥
transliteration: hriyamāṇā tu sā rājansūtaputrairaninditā ,prākrośannāthamicchantī kṛṣṇā nāthavatī satī.
english: But O king, that blameless Kṛṣṇā, despite having protectors, cried out for a lord as she was being carried away by the charioteer's sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. But O king, that blameless Kṛṣṇā, despite having protectors, cried out for a lord as she was being carried away by the charioteer's sons.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।जयो जयन्तो विजयो जयत्सेनो जयद्बलः ।ते मे वाचं विजानन्तु सूतपुत्रा नयन्ति माम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,jayo jayanto vijayo jayatseno jayadbalaḥ ,te me vācaṁ vijānantu sūtaputrā nayanti mām.
english: Draupadi said, "May Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala hear my words, for the charioteers' sons are leading me away."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. Draupadi said, "May Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala hear my words, for the charioteers' sons are leading me away."

sanskrit: येषां ज्यातलनिर्घोषो विस्फूर्जितमिवाशनेः ।व्यश्रूयत महायुद्धे भीमघोषस्तरस्विनाम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: yeṣāṁ jyātalanirghoṣo visphūrjitamivāśaneḥ ,vyaśrūyata mahāyuddhe bhīmaghoṣastarasvinām.
english: In the great battle, the terrible roar of those mighty ones - whose bowstring twang was heard like a clap of thunder - resounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. In the great battle, the terrible roar of those mighty ones - whose bowstring twang was heard like a clap of thunder - resounded.

sanskrit: रथघोषश्च बलवान्गन्धर्वाणां यशस्विनाम् ।ते मे वाचं विजानन्तु सूतपुत्रा नयन्ति माम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: rathaghoṣaśca balavāngandharvāṇāṁ yaśasvinām ,te me vācaṁ vijānantu sūtaputrā nayanti mām.
english: And the powerful sound of the chariots of the glorious Gandharvas! May they understand my words, for the charioteers' sons are leading me away.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. And the powerful sound of the chariots of the glorious Gandharvas! May they understand my words, for the charioteers' sons are leading me away.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्यास्ताः कृपणा वाचः कृष्णायाः परिदेविताः ।श्रुत्वैवाभ्यपतद्भीमः शयनादविचारयन् ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasyāstāḥ kṛpaṇā vācaḥ kṛṣṇāyāḥ paridevitāḥ ,śrutvaivābhyapatadbhīmaḥ śayanādavicārayan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said, "Having immediately heard those piteous words and lamentations of Draupadi, Bhima rushed from his bed without a moment's hesitation."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. Vaiśampāyana said, "Having immediately heard those piteous words and lamentations of Draupadi, Bhima rushed from his bed without a moment's hesitation."

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।अहं शृणोमि ते वाचं त्वया सैरन्ध्रि भाषिताम् ।तस्मात्ते सूतपुत्रेभ्यो न भयं भीरु विद्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,ahaṁ śṛṇomi te vācaṁ tvayā sairandhri bhāṣitām ,tasmātte sūtaputrebhyo na bhayaṁ bhīru vidyate.
english: Bhīmasena said: "O Sairandhrī, I hear the words spoken by you. Therefore, timid one, you have no fear from the sons of the charioteer."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. Bhīmasena said: "O Sairandhrī, I hear the words spoken by you. Therefore, timid one, you have no fear from the sons of the charioteer."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा स महाबाहुर्विजजृम्भे जिघांसया ।ततः स व्यायतं कृत्वा वेषं विपरिवर्त्य च ।अद्वारेणाभ्यवस्कन्द्य निर्जगाम बहिस्तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā sa mahābāhurvijajṛmbhe jighāṁsayā ,tataḥ sa vyāyataṁ kṛtvā veṣaṁ viparivartya ca ,advāreṇābhyavaskandya nirjagāma bahistadā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having spoken thus, that mighty-armed one (Bhīmasena) stretched himself out, intent on killing. Then, making his body taut and changing his disguise, he went out by leaping through an unusual exit."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having spoken thus, that mighty-armed one (Bhīmasena) stretched himself out, intent on killing. Then, making his body taut and changing his disguise, he went out by leaping through an unusual exit."

sanskrit: स भीमसेनः प्राकारादारुज्य तरसा द्रुमम् ।श्मशानाभिमुखः प्रायाद्यत्र ते कीचका गताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa bhīmasenaḥ prākārādārujya tarasā drumam ,śmaśānābhimukhaḥ prāyādyatra te kīcakā gatāḥ.
english: That Bhīmasena, having forcibly torn a tree from the rampart, went towards the cremation ground where those Kīcakas had gone.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. That Bhīmasena, having forcibly torn a tree from the rampart, went towards the cremation ground where those Kīcakas had gone.

sanskrit: स तं वृक्षं दशव्यामं सस्कन्धविटपं बली ।प्रगृह्याभ्यद्रवत्सूतान्दण्डपाणिरिवान्तकः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa taṁ vṛkṣaṁ daśavyāmaṁ saskandhaviṭapaṁ balī ,pragṛhyābhyadravatsūtāndaṇḍapāṇirivāntakaḥ.
english: That powerful one (Bhīmasena), grasping that tree - ten arm-spans long, complete with its trunk and branches - rushed towards the charioteer's sons like Death (Antaka) holding his staff.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 19. That powerful one (Bhīmasena), grasping that tree - ten arm-spans long, complete with its trunk and branches - rushed towards the charioteer's sons like Death (Antaka) holding his staff.

sanskrit: ऊरुवेगेन तस्याथ न्यग्रोधाश्वत्थकिंशुकाः ।भूमौ निपतिता वृक्षाः संघशस्तत्र शेरते ॥२०॥
transliteration: ūruvegena tasyātha nyagrodhāśvatthakiṁśukāḥ ,bhūmau nipatitā vṛkṣāḥ saṁghaśastatra śerate.
english: Then, by the force of his thighs, banyan, holy fig, and palāśa trees fell to the ground and lay there in groups.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 20. Then, by the force of his thighs, banyan, holy fig, and palāśa trees fell to the ground and lay there in groups.

sanskrit: तं सिंहमिव संक्रुद्धं दृष्ट्वा गन्धर्वमागतम् ।वित्रेसुः सर्वतः सूता विषादभयकम्पिताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: taṁ siṁhamiva saṁkruddhaṁ dṛṣṭvā gandharvamāgatam ,vitresuḥ sarvataḥ sūtā viṣādabhayakampitāḥ.
english: Having seen that Gandharva, approaching like a greatly enraged lion, the charioteers, agitated by dejection and fear, trembled everywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 21. Having seen that Gandharva, approaching like a greatly enraged lion, the charioteers, agitated by dejection and fear, trembled everywhere.

sanskrit: तमन्तकमिवायान्तं गन्धर्वं प्रेक्ष्य ते तदा ।दिधक्षन्तस्तदा ज्येष्ठं भ्रातरं ह्युपकीचकाः ।परस्परमथोचुस्ते विषादभयकम्पिताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tamantakamivāyāntaṁ gandharvaṁ prekṣya te tadā ,didhakṣantastadā jyeṣṭhaṁ bhrātaraṁ hyupakīcakāḥ ,parasparamathocuste viṣādabhayakampitāḥ.
english: Then, having seen that Gandharva approaching like the god of death (antaka), those Upakīcakas, who were then about to cremate their elder brother, agitated by dejection and fear, spoke to each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 22. Then, having seen that Gandharva approaching like the god of death (antaka), those Upakīcakas, who were then about to cremate their elder brother, agitated by dejection and fear, spoke to each other.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वो बलवानेति क्रुद्ध उद्यम्य पादपम् ।सैरन्ध्री मुच्यतां शीघ्रं महन्नो भयमागतम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: gandharvo balavāneti kruddha udyamya pādapam ,sairandhrī mucyatāṁ śīghraṁ mahanno bhayamāgatam.
english: The powerful Gandharva is approaching, enraged and holding up a tree. Let Sairandhrī be released quickly! A great fear has indeed come upon us.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 23. The powerful Gandharva is approaching, enraged and holding up a tree. Let Sairandhrī be released quickly! A great fear has indeed come upon us.

sanskrit: ते तु दृष्ट्वा तमाविद्धं भीमसेनेन पादपम् ।विमुच्य द्रौपदीं तत्र प्राद्रवन्नगरं प्रति ॥२४॥
transliteration: te tu dṛṣṭvā tamāviddhaṁ bhīmasenena pādapam ,vimucya draupadīṁ tatra prādravannagaraṁ prati.
english: But they, having seen the tree struck down by Bhīmasena, released Draupadī there and fled towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 24. But they, having seen the tree struck down by Bhīmasena, released Draupadī there and fled towards the city.

sanskrit: द्रवतस्तांस्तु संप्रेक्ष्य स वज्री दानवानिव ।शतं पञ्चाधिकं भीमः प्राहिणोद्यमसादनम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: dravatastāṁstu saṁprekṣya sa vajrī dānavāniva ,śataṁ pañcādhikaṁ bhīmaḥ prāhiṇodyamasādanam.
english: Bhīma, upon clearly seeing them fleeing, sent 105 of them to the abode of Yama (death), just as Indra (vajrī) would send demons (dānava) to their doom.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 25. Bhīma, upon clearly seeing them fleeing, sent 105 of them to the abode of Yama (death), just as Indra (vajrī) would send demons (dānava) to their doom.

sanskrit: तत आश्वासयत्कृष्णां प्रविमुच्य विशां पते ।उवाच च महाबाहुः पाञ्चालीं तत्र द्रौपदीम् ।अश्रुपूर्णमुखीं दीनां दुर्धर्षः स वृकोदरः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tata āśvāsayatkṛṣṇāṁ pravimucya viśāṁ pate ,uvāca ca mahābāhuḥ pāñcālīṁ tatra draupadīm ,aśrupūrṇamukhīṁ dīnāṁ durdharṣaḥ sa vṛkodaraḥ.
english: Then, having completely freed Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), the mighty-armed, formidable Bhīma (vṛkodara), O lord of men (a conventional address by the narrator), spoke to that distressed Pāñcālī (Draupadī), whose face was tear-filled.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 26. Then, having completely freed Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), the mighty-armed, formidable Bhīma (vṛkodara), O lord of men (a conventional address by the narrator), spoke to that distressed Pāñcālī (Draupadī), whose face was tear-filled.

sanskrit: एवं ते भीरु वध्यन्ते ये त्वां क्लिश्यन्त्यनागसम् ।प्रैहि त्वं नगरं कृष्णे न भयं विद्यते तव ।अन्येनाहं गमिष्यामि विराटस्य महानसम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: evaṁ te bhīru vadhyante ye tvāṁ kliśyantyanāgasam ,praihi tvaṁ nagaraṁ kṛṣṇe na bhayaṁ vidyate tava ,anyenāhaṁ gamiṣyāmi virāṭasya mahānasam.
english: "Thus, O timid one, those who torment you, the innocent, are being killed. You, Kṛṣṇā, go to the city; there is no danger for you. I will go by another way to Virāṭa's kitchen."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 27. "Thus, O timid one, those who torment you, the innocent, are being killed. You, Kṛṣṇā, go to the city; there is no danger for you. I will go by another way to Virāṭa's kitchen."

sanskrit: पञ्चाधिकं शतं तच्च निहतं तत्र भारत ।महावनमिव छिन्नं शिश्ये विगलितद्रुमम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: pañcādhikaṁ śataṁ tacca nihataṁ tatra bhārata ,mahāvanamiva chinnaṁ śiśye vigalitadrumam.
english: And there, O Bhārata, those one hundred and five (Kīcakas) lay slain, just like a great forest that has been cut down, with its trees scattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 28. And there, O Bhārata, those one hundred and five (Kīcakas) lay slain, just like a great forest that has been cut down, with its trees scattered.

sanskrit: एवं ते निहता राजञ्शतं पञ्च च कीचकाः ।स च सेनापतिः पूर्वमित्येतत्सूतषट्शतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: evaṁ te nihatā rājañśataṁ pañca ca kīcakāḥ ,sa ca senāpatiḥ pūrvamityetatsūtaṣaṭśatam.
english: Thus, O King, those one hundred and five Kīcakas were slain. And that commander (Kīcaka) had been killed earlier. So, this accounts for six hundred charioteers in total.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 29. Thus, O King, those one hundred and five Kīcakas were slain. And that commander (Kīcaka) had been killed earlier. So, this accounts for six hundred charioteers in total.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा महदाश्चर्यं नरा नार्यश्च संगताः ।विस्मयं परमं गत्वा नोचुः किंचन भारत ॥३०॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā mahadāścaryaṁ narā nāryaśca saṁgatāḥ ,vismayaṁ paramaṁ gatvā nocuḥ kiṁcana bhārata.
english: And seeing that great marvel, the men and women who had gathered there, experiencing extreme astonishment, said absolutely nothing, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 22 Verse 30. And seeing that great marvel, the men and women who had gathered there, experiencing extreme astonishment, said absolutely nothing, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ते दृष्ट्वा निहतान्सूतान्राज्ञे गत्वा न्यवेदयन् ।गन्धर्वैर्निहता राजन्सूतपुत्राः परःशताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,te dṛṣṭvā nihatānsūtānrājñe gatvā nyavedayan ,gandharvairnihatā rājansūtaputrāḥ paraḥśatāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Seeing those slain charioteers, they went to the king and reported, 'O King, more than a hundred charioteers' sons have been killed by Gandharvas.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Seeing those slain charioteers, they went to the king and reported, 'O King, more than a hundred charioteers' sons have been killed by Gandharvas.'

sanskrit: यथा वज्रेण वै दीर्णं पर्वतस्य महच्छिरः ।विनिकीर्णं प्रदृश्येत तथा सूता महीतले ॥२॥
transliteration: yathā vajreṇa vai dīrṇaṁ parvatasya mahacchiraḥ ,vinikīrṇaṁ pradṛśyeta tathā sūtā mahītale.
english: Just as a great mountain peak, split by a thunderbolt, would appear shattered and scattered on the ground, so too would Sairandhrī (the charioteer's wife) appear on the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. Just as a great mountain peak, split by a thunderbolt, would appear shattered and scattered on the ground, so too would Sairandhrī (the charioteer's wife) appear on the earth.

sanskrit: सैरन्ध्री च विमुक्तासौ पुनरायाति ते गृहम् ।सर्वं संशयितं राजन्नगरं ते भविष्यति ॥३॥
transliteration: sairandhrī ca vimuktāsau punarāyāti te gṛham ,sarvaṁ saṁśayitaṁ rājannagaraṁ te bhaviṣyati.
english: And if that Sairandhrī, once released, returns to your house, O King, your entire city will be in jeopardy.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. And if that Sairandhrī, once released, returns to your house, O King, your entire city will be in jeopardy.

sanskrit: तथारूपा हि सैरन्ध्री गन्धर्वाश्च महाबलाः ।पुंसामिष्टश्च विषयो मैथुनाय न संशयः ॥४॥
transliteration: tathārūpā hi sairandhrī gandharvāśca mahābalāḥ ,puṁsāmiṣṭaśca viṣayo maithunāya na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Indeed, Sairandhrī possesses such beauty, and the Gandharvas are mighty. She is, moreover, a desired object for men for sexual union; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. Indeed, Sairandhrī possesses such beauty, and the Gandharvas are mighty. She is, moreover, a desired object for men for sexual union; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: यथा सैरन्ध्रिवेषेण न ते राजन्निदं पुरम् ।विनाशमेति वै क्षिप्रं तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥५॥
transliteration: yathā sairandhriveṣeṇa na te rājannidaṁ puram ,vināśameti vai kṣipraṁ tathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: O King, let such a policy (nīti) be enacted so that this city of yours does not swiftly come to ruin because of Sairandhrī's disguise.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. O King, let such a policy (nīti) be enacted so that this city of yours does not swiftly come to ruin because of Sairandhrī's disguise.

sanskrit: तेषां तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा विराटो वाहिनीपतिः ।अब्रवीत्क्रियतामेषां सूतानां परमक्रिया ॥६॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tadvacanaṁ śrutvā virāṭo vāhinīpatiḥ ,abravītkriyatāmeṣāṁ sūtānāṁ paramakriyā.
english: Hearing their words, Virāṭa, the commander of the army, said, "Let the final rites be performed for these charioteers."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. Hearing their words, Virāṭa, the commander of the army, said, "Let the final rites be performed for these charioteers."

sanskrit: एकस्मिन्नेव ते सर्वे सुसमिद्धे हुताशने ।दह्यन्तां कीचकाः शीघ्रं रत्नैर्गन्धैश्च सर्वशः ॥७॥
transliteration: ekasminneva te sarve susamiddhe hutāśane ,dahyantāṁ kīcakāḥ śīghraṁ ratnairgandhaiśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Let all those Kīcakas quickly be burned completely in one well-kindled fire, along with jewels and fragrances.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. Let all those Kīcakas quickly be burned completely in one well-kindled fire, along with jewels and fragrances.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णां चाब्रवीद्राजा महिषीं जातसाध्वसः ।सैरन्ध्रीमागतां ब्रूया ममैव वचनादिदम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇāṁ cābravīdrājā mahiṣīṁ jātasādhvasaḥ ,sairandhrīmāgatāṁ brūyā mamaiva vacanādidam.
english: The king, filled with apprehension, also spoke to Queen Sudeṣṇā: "You should tell this message to the Sairandhrī, who has returned, directly from my command."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. The king, filled with apprehension, also spoke to Queen Sudeṣṇā: "You should tell this message to the Sairandhrī, who has returned, directly from my command."

sanskrit: गच्छ सैरन्ध्रि भद्रं ते यथाकामं चराबले ।बिभेति राजा सुश्रोणि गन्धर्वेभ्यः पराभवात् ॥९॥
transliteration: gaccha sairandhri bhadraṁ te yathākāmaṁ carābale ,bibheti rājā suśroṇi gandharvebhyaḥ parābhavāt.
english: Go, Sairandhrī, may good fortune be upon you. Roam as you wish, O frail one. The king fears defeat from the Gandharvas, O beautiful-hipped lady.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. Go, Sairandhrī, may good fortune be upon you. Roam as you wish, O frail one. The king fears defeat from the Gandharvas, O beautiful-hipped lady.

sanskrit: न हि तामुत्सहे वक्तुं स्वयं गन्धर्वरक्षिताम् ।स्त्रियस्त्वदोषास्तां वक्तुमतस्त्वां प्रब्रवीम्यहम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: na hi tāmutsahe vaktuṁ svayaṁ gandharvarakṣitām ,striyastvadoṣāstāṁ vaktumatastvāṁ prabravīmyaham.
english: Indeed, I am unable to speak of her myself, as she is protected by Gandharvas. However, women are considered blameless when speaking of (other) women, therefore, I am confiding in you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. Indeed, I am unable to speak of her myself, as she is protected by Gandharvas. However, women are considered blameless when speaking of (other) women, therefore, I am confiding in you.

sanskrit: अथ मुक्ता भयात्कृष्णा सूतपुत्रान्निरस्य च ।मोक्षिता भीमसेनेन जगाम नगरं प्रति ॥११॥
transliteration: atha muktā bhayātkṛṣṇā sūtaputrānnirasya ca ,mokṣitā bhīmasenena jagāma nagaraṁ prati.
english: Then Draupadi, released from fear and having repelled the son of the charioteer, was liberated (mokṣita) by Bhimasena and went towards the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. Then Draupadi, released from fear and having repelled the son of the charioteer, was liberated (mokṣita) by Bhimasena and went towards the city.

sanskrit: त्रासितेव मृगी बाला शार्दूलेन मनस्विनी ।गात्राणि वाससी चैव प्रक्षाल्य सलिलेन सा ॥१२॥
transliteration: trāsiteva mṛgī bālā śārdūlena manasvinī ,gātrāṇi vāsasī caiva prakṣālya salilena sā.
english: Like a young doe frightened by a tiger, that high-minded woman, having washed her limbs and also her clothes with water...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. Like a young doe frightened by a tiger, that high-minded woman, having washed her limbs and also her clothes with water...

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा पुरुषा राजन्प्राद्रवन्त दिशो दश ।गन्धर्वाणां भयत्रस्ताः केचिद्दृष्टीर्न्यमीलयन् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā puruṣā rājanprādravanta diśo daśa ,gandharvāṇāṁ bhayatrastāḥ keciddṛṣṭīrnyamīlayan.
english: O King, upon seeing her, the men (puruṣa), terrified by the Gandharvas, ran away in all ten directions; some even closed their eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. O King, upon seeing her, the men (puruṣa), terrified by the Gandharvas, ran away in all ten directions; some even closed their eyes.

sanskrit: ततो महानसद्वारि भीमसेनमवस्थितम् ।ददर्श राजन्पाञ्चाली यथा मत्तं महाद्विपम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato mahānasadvāri bhīmasenamavasthitam ,dadarśa rājanpāñcālī yathā mattaṁ mahādvipam.
english: O King, Draupadi then saw Bhimasena standing at the kitchen door, resembling a great, intoxicated elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. O King, Draupadi then saw Bhimasena standing at the kitchen door, resembling a great, intoxicated elephant.

sanskrit: तं विस्मयन्ती शनकैः संज्ञाभिरिदमब्रवीत् ।गन्धर्वराजाय नमो येनास्मि परिमोचिता ॥१५॥
transliteration: taṁ vismayantī śanakaiḥ saṁjñābhiridamabravīt ,gandharvarājāya namo yenāsmi parimocitā.
english: Wondering, she slowly spoke to him with gestures, saying, 'Salutations to the king of Gandharvas, by whom I was liberated!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. Wondering, she slowly spoke to him with gestures, saying, 'Salutations to the king of Gandharvas, by whom I was liberated!'

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।ये यस्या विचरन्तीह पुरुषा वशवर्तिनः ।तस्यास्ते वचनं श्रुत्वा अनृणा विचरन्त्युत ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,ye yasyā vicarantīha puruṣā vaśavartinaḥ ,tasyāste vacanaṁ śrutvā anṛṇā vicarantyuta.
english: Bhimasena said: 'The persons who move about here, obedient to whosoever they may be, they, having heard her (Draupadi's) word, also act free from obligation (anṛṇāḥ).'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. Bhimasena said: 'The persons who move about here, obedient to whosoever they may be, they, having heard her (Draupadi's) word, also act free from obligation (anṛṇāḥ).'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः सा नर्तनागारे धनंजयमपश्यत ।राज्ञः कन्या विराटस्य नर्तयानं महाभुजम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ sā nartanāgāre dhanaṁjayamapaśyata ,rājñaḥ kanyā virāṭasya nartayānaṁ mahābhujam.
english: Vaishampayana said: 'Then she (Draupadi) saw Dhananjaya (Arjuna) in the dancing hall; he was the great-armed one, dancing, and King Virata's daughter (Uttaraa) was also present.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. Vaishampayana said: 'Then she (Draupadi) saw Dhananjaya (Arjuna) in the dancing hall; he was the great-armed one, dancing, and King Virata's daughter (Uttaraa) was also present.'

sanskrit: ततस्ता नर्तनागाराद्विनिष्क्रम्य सहार्जुनाः ।कन्या ददृशुरायान्तीं कृष्णां क्लिष्टामनागसम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatastā nartanāgārādviniṣkramya sahārjunāḥ ,kanyā dadṛśurāyāntīṁ kṛṣṇāṁ kliṣṭāmanāgasam.
english: Then, having emerged from the dancing hall with Arjuna, the maidens saw Krishna (Draupadi) approaching, distressed and blameless.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. Then, having emerged from the dancing hall with Arjuna, the maidens saw Krishna (Draupadi) approaching, distressed and blameless.

sanskrit: कन्या ऊचुः ।दिष्ट्या सैरन्ध्रि मुक्तासि दिष्ट्यासि पुनरागता ।दिष्ट्या विनिहताः सूता ये त्वां क्लिश्यन्त्यनागसम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: kanyā ūcuḥ ,diṣṭyā sairandhri muktāsi diṣṭyāsi punarāgatā ,diṣṭyā vinihatāḥ sūtā ye tvāṁ kliśyantyanāgasam.
english: The maidens exclaimed, "Fortunately, Sairandhri, you are free! Fortunately, you have returned! And fortunately, those charioteers who were tormenting you, the innocent one, have been slain!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. The maidens exclaimed, "Fortunately, Sairandhri, you are free! Fortunately, you have returned! And fortunately, those charioteers who were tormenting you, the innocent one, have been slain!"

sanskrit: बृहन्नडोवाच ।कथं सैरन्ध्रि मुक्तासि कथं पापाश्च ते हताः ।इच्छामि वै तव श्रोतुं सर्वमेव यथातथम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: bṛhannaḍovāca ,kathaṁ sairandhri muktāsi kathaṁ pāpāśca te hatāḥ ,icchāmi vai tava śrotuṁ sarvameva yathātatham.
english: Bṛhannalā asked, "Sairandhri, how were you freed? And how were those wicked individuals killed? I certainly wish to hear the entire account from you, just as it happened."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. Bṛhannalā asked, "Sairandhri, how were you freed? And how were those wicked individuals killed? I certainly wish to hear the entire account from you, just as it happened."

sanskrit: सैरन्ध्र्युवाच ।बृहन्नडे किं नु तव सैरन्ध्र्या कार्यमद्य वै ।या त्वं वससि कल्याणि सदा कन्यापुरे सुखम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sairandhryuvāca ,bṛhannaḍe kiṁ nu tava sairandhryā kāryamadya vai ,yā tvaṁ vasasi kalyāṇi sadā kanyāpure sukham.
english: Sairandhri replied, "O Bṛhannalā, what concern do you truly have for Sairandhri today? You, O benevolent one, always live comfortably in the women's quarters."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. Sairandhri replied, "O Bṛhannalā, what concern do you truly have for Sairandhri today? You, O benevolent one, always live comfortably in the women's quarters."

sanskrit: न हि दुःखं समाप्नोषि सैरन्ध्री यदुपाश्नुते ।तेन मां दुःखितामेवं पृच्छसे प्रहसन्निव ॥२२॥
transliteration: na hi duḥkhaṁ samāpnoṣi sairandhrī yadupāśnute ,tena māṁ duḥkhitāmevaṁ pṛcchase prahasanniva.
english: You certainly do not experience the kind of suffering that Sairandhrī undergoes. That is why you ask me, who am so distressed, as if you are laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 22. You certainly do not experience the kind of suffering that Sairandhrī undergoes. That is why you ask me, who am so distressed, as if you are laughing.

sanskrit: बृहन्नडोवाच ।बृहन्नडापि कल्याणि दुःखमाप्नोत्यनुत्तमम् ।तिर्यग्योनिगता बाले न चैनामवबुध्यसे ॥२३॥
transliteration: bṛhannaḍovāca ,bṛhannaḍāpi kalyāṇi duḥkhamāpnotyanuttamam ,tiryagyonigatā bāle na caināmavabudhyase.
english: Bṛhannadā said: 'O auspicious one, Bṛhannadā also experiences unsurpassed suffering. O child, you do not understand this (suffering) of hers, which is like being reduced to an animal state.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 23. Bṛhannadā said: 'O auspicious one, Bṛhannadā also experiences unsurpassed suffering. O child, you do not understand this (suffering) of hers, which is like being reduced to an animal state.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः सहैव कन्याभिर्द्रौपदी राजवेश्म तत् ।प्रविवेश सुदेष्णायाः समीपमपलायिनी ॥२४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ sahaiva kanyābhirdraupadī rājaveśma tat ,praviveśa sudeṣṇāyāḥ samīpamapalāyinī.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then Draupadī, accompanied by the maidens, entered that royal palace, approaching Sudeṣṇā, without trying to escape.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 24. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then Draupadī, accompanied by the maidens, entered that royal palace, approaching Sudeṣṇā, without trying to escape.

sanskrit: तामब्रवीद्राजपुत्री विराटवचनादिदम् ।सैरन्ध्रि गम्यतां शीघ्रं यत्र कामयसे गतिम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: tāmabravīdrājaputrī virāṭavacanādidam ,sairandhri gamyatāṁ śīghraṁ yatra kāmayase gatim.
english: The princess, at King Virāṭa's command, said this to her (Draupadī): 'O Sairandhrī, go quickly to wherever you wish.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 25. The princess, at King Virāṭa's command, said this to her (Draupadī): 'O Sairandhrī, go quickly to wherever you wish.'

sanskrit: राजा बिभेति भद्रं ते गन्धर्वेभ्यः पराभवात् ।त्वं चापि तरुणी सुभ्रु रूपेणाप्रतिमा भुवि ॥२६॥
transliteration: rājā bibheti bhadraṁ te gandharvebhyaḥ parābhavāt ,tvaṁ cāpi taruṇī subhru rūpeṇāpratimā bhuvi.
english: The king fears humiliation from the Gandharvas; may good fortune be with you. And you, O beautiful-browed woman, are also young and of unequalled beauty on earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 26. The king fears humiliation from the Gandharvas; may good fortune be with you. And you, O beautiful-browed woman, are also young and of unequalled beauty on earth.

sanskrit: सैरन्ध्र्युवाच ।त्रयोदशाहमात्रं मे राजा क्षमतु भामिनि ।कृतकृत्या भविष्यन्ति गन्धर्वास्ते न संशयः ॥२७॥
transliteration: sairandhryuvāca ,trayodaśāhamātraṁ me rājā kṣamatu bhāmini ,kṛtakṛtyā bhaviṣyanti gandharvāste na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Sairandhrī said: "O passionate lady, may the king tolerate me for only thirteen days. There is no doubt that your Gandharvas will achieve their goal."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 27. Sairandhrī said: "O passionate lady, may the king tolerate me for only thirteen days. There is no doubt that your Gandharvas will achieve their goal."

sanskrit: ततो मां तेऽपनेष्यन्ति करिष्यन्ति च ते प्रियम् ।ध्रुवं च श्रेयसा राजा योक्ष्यते सह बान्धवैः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato māṁ te'paneṣyanti kariṣyanti ca te priyam ,dhruvaṁ ca śreyasā rājā yokṣyate saha bāndhavaiḥ.
english: Then they (the Gandharvas) will take me away and will do what is dear to you. And certainly, the king, along with his relatives, will attain prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 23 Verse 28. Then they (the Gandharvas) will take me away and will do what is dear to you. And certainly, the king, along with his relatives, will attain prosperity.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।कीचकस्य तु घातेन सानुजस्य विशां पते ।अत्याहितं चिन्तयित्वा व्यस्मयन्त पृथग्जनाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,kīcakasya tu ghātena sānujasya viśāṁ pate ,atyāhitaṁ cintayitvā vyasmayanta pṛthagjanāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O lord of the people, considering the great danger caused by the slaying of Kīcaka and his younger brother, the various people were astonished."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "O lord of the people, considering the great danger caused by the slaying of Kīcaka and his younger brother, the various people were astonished."

sanskrit: तस्मिन्पुरे जनपदे संजल्पोऽभूच्च सर्वशः ।शौर्याद्धि वल्लभो राज्ञो महासत्त्वश्च कीचकः ॥२॥
transliteration: tasminpure janapade saṁjalpo'bhūcca sarvaśaḥ ,śauryāddhi vallabho rājño mahāsattvaśca kīcakaḥ.
english: In that city and province, there was widespread talk. Indeed, Kīcaka, valorous and exceptionally strong, was very dear to the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. In that city and province, there was widespread talk. Indeed, Kīcaka, valorous and exceptionally strong, was very dear to the king.

sanskrit: आसीत्प्रहर्ता च नृणां दारामर्शी च दुर्मतिः ।स हतः खलु पापात्मा गन्धर्वैर्दुष्टपूरुषः ॥३॥
transliteration: āsītprahartā ca nṛṇāṁ dārāmarśī ca durmatiḥ ,sa hataḥ khalu pāpātmā gandharvairduṣṭapūruṣaḥ.
english: He was also a tormentor of men and a villainous molester of women. That wicked man (duṣṭapuruṣa), that sinful soul (pāpātman), was indeed killed by the Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. He was also a tormentor of men and a villainous molester of women. That wicked man (duṣṭapuruṣa), that sinful soul (pāpātman), was indeed killed by the Gandharvas.

sanskrit: इत्यजल्पन्महाराज परानीकविशातनम् ।देशे देशे मनुष्याश्च कीचकं दुष्प्रधर्षणम् ॥४॥
transliteration: ityajalpanmahārāja parānīkaviśātanam ,deśe deśe manuṣyāśca kīcakaṁ duṣpradharṣaṇam.
english: Thus, O great king, people everywhere, in every region, spoke of Kīcaka, the destroyer of enemy armies, who was difficult to overpower.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. Thus, O great king, people everywhere, in every region, spoke of Kīcaka, the destroyer of enemy armies, who was difficult to overpower.

sanskrit: अथ वै धार्तराष्ट्रेण प्रयुक्ता ये बहिश्चराः ।मृगयित्वा बहून्ग्रामान्राष्ट्राणि नगराणि च ॥५॥
transliteration: atha vai dhārtarāṣṭreṇa prayuktā ye bahiścarāḥ ,mṛgayitvā bahūngrāmānrāṣṭrāṇi nagarāṇi ca.
english: Now, those scouts who were indeed dispatched by the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, after diligently searching many villages, kingdoms, and cities...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. Now, those scouts who were indeed dispatched by the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, after diligently searching many villages, kingdoms, and cities...

sanskrit: संविधाय यथादिष्टं यथादेशप्रदर्शनम् ।कृतचिन्ता न्यवर्तन्त ते च नागपुरं प्रति ॥६॥
transliteration: saṁvidhāya yathādiṣṭaṁ yathādeśapradarśanam ,kṛtacintā nyavartanta te ca nāgapuraṁ prati.
english: After executing the instructions and pointing out the locations as directed, and having completed their task, they returned to Nagapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. After executing the instructions and pointing out the locations as directed, and having completed their task, they returned to Nagapura.

sanskrit: तत्र दृष्ट्वा तु राजानं कौरव्यं धृतराष्ट्रजम् ।द्रोणकर्णकृपैः सार्धं भीष्मेण च महात्मना ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra dṛṣṭvā tu rājānaṁ kauravyaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrajam ,droṇakarṇakṛpaiḥ sārdhaṁ bhīṣmeṇa ca mahātmanā.
english: There, they saw the king, a descendant of Kuru (kauravya), the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with Droṇa, Karṇa, Kṛpa, and the great-souled (mahātman) Bhīṣma.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. There, they saw the king, a descendant of Kuru (kauravya), the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with Droṇa, Karṇa, Kṛpa, and the great-souled (mahātman) Bhīṣma.

sanskrit: संगतं भ्रातृभिश्चापि त्रिगर्तैश्च महारथैः ।दुर्योधनं सभामध्ये आसीनमिदमब्रुवन् ॥८॥
transliteration: saṁgataṁ bhrātṛbhiścāpi trigartaiśca mahārathaiḥ ,duryodhanaṁ sabhāmadhye āsīnamidamabruvan.
english: They spoke these words to Duryodhana, who was seated in the assembly hall, accompanied by his brothers and also by the great charioteers (mahāratha) of Trigarta.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. They spoke these words to Duryodhana, who was seated in the assembly hall, accompanied by his brothers and also by the great charioteers (mahāratha) of Trigarta.

sanskrit: कृतोऽस्माभिः परो यत्नस्तेषामन्वेषणे सदा ।पाण्डवानां मनुष्येन्द्र तस्मिन्महति कानने ॥९॥
transliteration: kṛto'smābhiḥ paro yatnasteṣāmanveṣaṇe sadā ,pāṇḍavānāṁ manuṣyendra tasminmahati kānane.
english: O king among men (manuṣyendra), we have always made a great effort in searching for those Pāṇḍavas in that great forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. O king among men (manuṣyendra), we have always made a great effort in searching for those Pāṇḍavas in that great forest.

sanskrit: निर्जने मृगसंकीर्णे नानाद्रुमलतावृते ।लताप्रतानबहुले नानागुल्मसमावृते ॥१०॥
transliteration: nirjane mṛgasaṁkīrṇe nānādrumalatāvṛte ,latāpratānabahule nānāgulmasamāvṛte.
english: In a desolate place, crowded with animals, covered with various trees and creepers, abundant with vine tendrils, and completely overgrown with various bushes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. In a desolate place, crowded with animals, covered with various trees and creepers, abundant with vine tendrils, and completely overgrown with various bushes.

sanskrit: न च विद्मो गता येन पार्थाः स्युर्दृढविक्रमाः ।मार्गमाणाः पदन्यासं तेषु तेषु तथा तथा ॥११॥
transliteration: na ca vidmo gatā yena pārthāḥ syurdṛḍhavikramāḥ ,mārgamāṇāḥ padanyāsaṁ teṣu teṣu tathā tathā.
english: We do not know by which path the firmly valiant Pandavas went, though we are searching for their footsteps in various places and in many ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. We do not know by which path the firmly valiant Pandavas went, though we are searching for their footsteps in various places and in many ways.

sanskrit: गिरिकूटेषु तुङ्गेषु नानाजनपदेषु च ।जनाकीर्णेषु देशेषु खर्वटेषु पुरेषु च ॥१२॥
transliteration: girikūṭeṣu tuṅgeṣu nānājanapadeṣu ca ,janākīrṇeṣu deśeṣu kharvaṭeṣu pureṣu ca.
english: They searched on lofty mountain peaks, in various populated countries, in populous regions, and in towns and cities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. They searched on lofty mountain peaks, in various populated countries, in populous regions, and in towns and cities.

sanskrit: नरेन्द्र बहुशोऽन्विष्टा नैव विद्मश्च पाण्डवान् ।अत्यन्तभावं नष्टास्ते भद्रं तुभ्यं नरर्षभ ॥१३॥
transliteration: narendra bahuśo'nviṣṭā naiva vidmaśca pāṇḍavān ,atyantabhāvaṁ naṣṭāste bhadraṁ tubhyaṁ nararṣabha.
english: O King, though we have searched repeatedly, we still do not know of the Pandavas. They are completely lost. May prosperity be to you, O best among men!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. O King, though we have searched repeatedly, we still do not know of the Pandavas. They are completely lost. May prosperity be to you, O best among men!

sanskrit: वर्त्मान्यन्विष्यमाणास्तु रथानां रथसत्तम ।कंचित्कालं मनुष्येन्द्र सूतानामनुगा वयम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vartmānyanviṣyamāṇāstu rathānāṁ rathasattama ,kaṁcitkālaṁ manuṣyendra sūtānāmanugā vayam.
english: O best among charioteers, O lord of men, we searched for the tracks of the chariots and charioteers, pursuing them for some time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. O best among charioteers, O lord of men, we searched for the tracks of the chariots and charioteers, pursuing them for some time.

sanskrit: मृगयित्वा यथान्यायं विदितार्थाः स्म तत्त्वतः ।प्राप्ता द्वारवतीं सूता ऋते पार्थैः परंतप ॥१५॥
transliteration: mṛgayitvā yathānyāyaṁ viditārthāḥ sma tattvataḥ ,prāptā dvāravatīṁ sūtā ṛte pārthaiḥ paraṁtapa.
english: O tormentor of foes, having searched properly, we truly understood the situation. We, the charioteers (sūtas), reached Dvārakā without the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. O tormentor of foes, having searched properly, we truly understood the situation. We, the charioteers (sūtas), reached Dvārakā without the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā).

sanskrit: न तत्र पाण्डवा राजन्नापि कृष्णा पतिव्रता ।सर्वथा विप्रनष्टास्ते नमस्ते भरतर्षभ ॥१६॥
transliteration: na tatra pāṇḍavā rājannāpi kṛṣṇā pativratā ,sarvathā vipranaṣṭāste namaste bharatarṣabha.
english: O king, the Pāṇḍavas were not there, nor was Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), the devoted wife. They are entirely lost. Homage to you, O best of the Bhāratas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. O king, the Pāṇḍavas were not there, nor was Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), the devoted wife. They are entirely lost. Homage to you, O best of the Bhāratas.

sanskrit: न हि विद्मो गतिं तेषां वासं वापि महात्मनाम् ।पाण्डवानां प्रवृत्तिं वा विद्मः कर्मापि वा कृतम् ।स नः शाधि मनुष्येन्द्र अत ऊर्ध्वं विशां पते ॥१७॥
transliteration: na hi vidmo gatiṁ teṣāṁ vāsaṁ vāpi mahātmanām ,pāṇḍavānāṁ pravṛttiṁ vā vidmaḥ karmāpi vā kṛtam ,sa naḥ śādhi manuṣyendra ata ūrdhvaṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: Indeed, we do not know their destination or even the dwelling of those great souls (mahātmanām). Nor do we know the activities of the Pāṇḍavas or any deeds (karma) they might have performed. Therefore, O lord of men, O lord of the people, instruct us on what to do hereafter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. Indeed, we do not know their destination or even the dwelling of those great souls (mahātmanām). Nor do we know the activities of the Pāṇḍavas or any deeds (karma) they might have performed. Therefore, O lord of men, O lord of the people, instruct us on what to do hereafter.

sanskrit: अन्वेषणे पाण्डवानां भूयः किं करवामहे ।इमां च नः प्रियामीक्ष वाचं भद्रवतीं शुभाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: anveṣaṇe pāṇḍavānāṁ bhūyaḥ kiṁ karavāmahe ,imāṁ ca naḥ priyāmīkṣa vācaṁ bhadravatīṁ śubhām.
english: What more should we do regarding the search for the Pāṇḍavas? And consider this dear, auspicious, and beautiful message of ours.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. What more should we do regarding the search for the Pāṇḍavas? And consider this dear, auspicious, and beautiful message of ours.

sanskrit: येन त्रिगर्ता निकृता बलेन महता नृप ।सूतेन राज्ञो मत्स्यस्य कीचकेन महात्मना ॥१९॥
transliteration: yena trigartā nikṛtā balena mahatā nṛpa ,sūtena rājño matsyasya kīcakena mahātmanā.
english: O King, by whom the Trigartas were greatly humiliated through immense strength - that Kīcaka, the great-souled charioteer of King Matsya...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. O King, by whom the Trigartas were greatly humiliated through immense strength - that Kīcaka, the great-souled charioteer of King Matsya...

sanskrit: स हतः पतितः शेते गन्धर्वैर्निशि भारत ।अदृश्यमानैर्दुष्टात्मा सह भ्रातृभिरच्युत ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa hataḥ patitaḥ śete gandharvairniśi bhārata ,adṛśyamānairduṣṭātmā saha bhrātṛbhiracyuta.
english: O Bhārata, O Acyuta, that evil-souled Kīcaka now lies slain and fallen at night, along with his brothers, by the invisible Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. O Bhārata, O Acyuta, that evil-souled Kīcaka now lies slain and fallen at night, along with his brothers, by the invisible Gandharvas.

sanskrit: प्रियमेतदुपश्रुत्य शत्रूणां तु पराभवम् ।कृतकृत्यश्च कौरव्य विधत्स्व यदनन्तरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: priyametadupaśrutya śatrūṇāṁ tu parābhavam ,kṛtakṛtyaśca kauravya vidhatsva yadanantaram.
english: O scion of Kuru (Kauravya), having heard this pleasing news of the enemies' defeat, and having thus accomplished your task, now perform what needs to be done next.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 24 Verse 21. O scion of Kuru (Kauravya), having heard this pleasing news of the enemies' defeat, and having thus accomplished your task, now perform what needs to be done next.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो दुर्योधनो राजा श्रुत्वा तेषां वचस्तदा ।चिरमन्तर्मना भूत्वा प्रत्युवाच सभासदः ॥१॥
