transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato duryodhano rājā śrutvā teṣāṁ vacastadā ,ciramantarmanā bhūtvā pratyuvāca sabhāsadaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, King Duryodhana, after hearing their words, pondered deeply for a long time within himself before replying to the assembly members.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, King Duryodhana, after hearing their words, pondered deeply for a long time within himself before replying to the assembly members.

sanskrit: सुदुःखा खलु कार्याणां गतिर्विज्ञातुमन्ततः ।तस्मात्सर्वे उदीक्षध्वं क्व नु स्युः पाण्डवा गताः ॥२॥
transliteration: suduḥkhā khalu kāryāṇāṁ gatirvijñātumantataḥ ,tasmātsarve udīkṣadhvaṁ kva nu syuḥ pāṇḍavā gatāḥ.
english: Indeed, it is very difficult to fully ascertain the course of affairs. Therefore, all of you should diligently search to find out where the Pāṇḍavas have gone.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. Indeed, it is very difficult to fully ascertain the course of affairs. Therefore, all of you should diligently search to find out where the Pāṇḍavas have gone.

sanskrit: अल्पावशिष्टं कालस्य गतभूयिष्ठमन्ततः ।तेषामज्ञातचर्यायामस्मिन्वर्षे त्रयोदशे ॥३॥
transliteration: alpāvaśiṣṭaṁ kālasya gatabhūyiṣṭhamantataḥ ,teṣāmajñātacaryāyāmasminvarṣe trayodaśe.
english: In this thirteenth year of their incognito (ajñātacaryā) period, only a small portion of the time remains, as the greater part has already passed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 3. In this thirteenth year of their incognito (ajñātacaryā) period, only a small portion of the time remains, as the greater part has already passed.

sanskrit: अस्य वर्षस्य शेषं चेद्व्यतीयुरिह पाण्डवाः ।निवृत्तसमयास्ते हि सत्यव्रतपरायणाः ॥४॥
transliteration: asya varṣasya śeṣaṁ cedvyatīyuriha pāṇḍavāḥ ,nivṛttasamayāste hi satyavrataparāyaṇāḥ.
english: If the Pāṇḍavas successfully complete the remainder of this year here, then their period of exile will have ended, for they are indeed dedicated to their vows of truth (satyavrata).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 4. If the Pāṇḍavas successfully complete the remainder of this year here, then their period of exile will have ended, for they are indeed dedicated to their vows of truth (satyavrata).

sanskrit: क्षरन्त इव नागेन्द्राः सर्व आशीविषोपमाः ।दुःखा भवेयुः संरब्धाः कौरवान्प्रति ते ध्रुवम् ॥५॥
transliteration: kṣaranta iva nāgendrāḥ sarva āśīviṣopamāḥ ,duḥkhā bhaveyuḥ saṁrabdhāḥ kauravānprati te dhruvam.
english: They (the Pāṇḍavas) would surely become miserable and enraged towards the Kauravas, resembling great serpents oozing venom, all of them like venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 5. They (the Pāṇḍavas) would surely become miserable and enraged towards the Kauravas, resembling great serpents oozing venom, all of them like venomous snakes.

sanskrit: अर्वाक्कालस्य विज्ञाताः कृच्छ्ररूपधराः पुनः ।प्रविशेयुर्जितक्रोधास्तावदेव पुनर्वनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: arvākkālasya vijñātāḥ kṛcchrarūpadharāḥ punaḥ ,praviśeyurjitakrodhāstāvadeva punarvanam.
english: Should they be recognized before the appointed time, they would, assuming difficult disguises again and having subdued their anger, re-enter the forest for that same duration.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 6. Should they be recognized before the appointed time, they would, assuming difficult disguises again and having subdued their anger, re-enter the forest for that same duration.

sanskrit: तस्मात्क्षिप्रं बुभुत्सध्वं यथा नोऽत्यन्तमव्ययम् ।राज्यं निर्द्वन्द्वमव्यग्रं निःसपत्नं चिरं भवेत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tasmātkṣipraṁ bubhutsadhvaṁ yathā no'tyantamavyayam ,rājyaṁ nirdvandvamavyagraṁ niḥsapatnaṁ ciraṁ bhavet.
english: Therefore, quickly seek to understand how our kingdom may become perpetually imperishable, free from conflict, undisturbed, and without rivals for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. Therefore, quickly seek to understand how our kingdom may become perpetually imperishable, free from conflict, undisturbed, and without rivals for a long time.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्ततः कर्णः क्षिप्रं गच्छन्तु भारत ।अन्ये धूर्ततरा दक्षा निभृताः साधुकारिणः ॥८॥
transliteration: athābravīttataḥ karṇaḥ kṣipraṁ gacchantu bhārata ,anye dhūrtatarā dakṣā nibhṛtāḥ sādhukāriṇaḥ.
english: Thereafter, Karṇa spoke: 'O Bhārata, let other agents go quickly, those who are exceedingly cunning, skillful, discreet, and act effectively.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. Thereafter, Karṇa spoke: 'O Bhārata, let other agents go quickly, those who are exceedingly cunning, skillful, discreet, and act effectively.'

sanskrit: चरन्तु देशान्संवीताः स्फीताञ्जनपदाकुलान् ।तत्र गोष्ठीष्वथान्यासु सिद्धप्रव्रजितेषु च ॥९॥
transliteration: carantu deśānsaṁvītāḥ sphītāñjanapadākulān ,tatra goṣṭhīṣvathānyāsu siddhapravrajiteṣu ca.
english: Let them, disguised, wander through prosperous lands that are crowded with people. Let them search there, among gatherings and other assemblies, and among perfected beings (siddhas) and ascetics.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. Let them, disguised, wander through prosperous lands that are crowded with people. Let them search there, among gatherings and other assemblies, and among perfected beings (siddhas) and ascetics.

sanskrit: परिचारेषु तीर्थेषु विविधेष्वाकरेषु च ।विज्ञातव्या मनुष्यैस्तैस्तर्कया सुविनीतया ॥१०॥
transliteration: paricāreṣu tīrtheṣu vividheṣvākareṣu ca ,vijñātavyā manuṣyaistaistarkayā suvinītayā.
english: A well-disciplined inquiry (tarkā) should be employed by those men in various services, sacred places (tīrthas), and mines.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. A well-disciplined inquiry (tarkā) should be employed by those men in various services, sacred places (tīrthas), and mines.

sanskrit: विविधैस्तत्परैः सम्यक्तज्ज्ञैर्निपुणसंवृतैः ।अन्वेष्टव्याश्च निपुणं पाण्डवाश्छन्नवासिनः ॥११॥
transliteration: vividhaistatparaiḥ samyaktajjñairnipuṇasaṁvṛtaiḥ ,anveṣṭavyāśca nipuṇaṁ pāṇḍavāśchannavāsinaḥ.
english: The Pandavas, living in disguise, should be skillfully sought by various, dedicated experts who are thoroughly and cleverly disguised.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. The Pandavas, living in disguise, should be skillfully sought by various, dedicated experts who are thoroughly and cleverly disguised.

sanskrit: नदीकुञ्जेषु तीर्थेषु ग्रामेषु नगरेषु च ।आश्रमेषु च रम्येषु पर्वतेषु गुहासु च ॥१२॥
transliteration: nadīkuñjeṣu tīrtheṣu grāmeṣu nagareṣu ca ,āśrameṣu ca ramyeṣu parvateṣu guhāsu ca.
english: (They should be sought) in river groves, in sacred places (tīrthas), in villages, and in cities; and in beautiful hermitages (āśramas), on mountains, and in caves.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 12. (They should be sought) in river groves, in sacred places (tīrthas), in villages, and in cities; and in beautiful hermitages (āśramas), on mountains, and in caves.

sanskrit: अथाग्रजानन्तरजः पापभावानुरागिणम् ।ज्येष्ठं दुःशासनस्तत्र भ्राता भ्रातरमब्रवीत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: athāgrajānantarajaḥ pāpabhāvānurāgiṇam ,jyeṣṭhaṁ duḥśāsanastatra bhrātā bhrātaramabravīt.
english: Then, Duḥśāsana, the younger brother, spoke there to his elder brother, who was inclined towards sinful thoughts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 13. Then, Duḥśāsana, the younger brother, spoke there to his elder brother, who was inclined towards sinful thoughts.

sanskrit: एतच्च कर्णो यत्प्राह सर्वमीक्षामहे तथा ।यथोद्दिष्टं चराः सर्वे मृगयन्तु ततस्ततः ।एते चान्ये च भूयांसो देशाद्देशं यथाविधि ॥१४॥
transliteration: etacca karṇo yatprāha sarvamīkṣāmahe tathā ,yathoddiṣṭaṁ carāḥ sarve mṛgayantu tatastataḥ ,ete cānye ca bhūyāṁso deśāddeśaṁ yathāvidhi.
english: And as for what Karṇa said, we shall observe all of it accordingly. Let all the spies, as instructed, search everywhere. And these and many others should proceed from country to country, following the proper procedure.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 14. And as for what Karṇa said, we shall observe all of it accordingly. Let all the spies, as instructed, search everywhere. And these and many others should proceed from country to country, following the proper procedure.

sanskrit: न तु तेषां गतिर्वासः प्रवृत्तिश्चोपलभ्यते ।अत्याहितं वा गूढास्ते पारं वोर्मिमतो गताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: na tu teṣāṁ gatirvāsaḥ pravṛttiścopalabhyate ,atyāhitaṁ vā gūḍhāste pāraṁ vormimato gatāḥ.
english: However, their movements, dwelling places, or activities are not being found. Or perhaps, having gone into hiding, they have met with great danger, or they have reached the far shore of the surging waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 15. However, their movements, dwelling places, or activities are not being found. Or perhaps, having gone into hiding, they have met with great danger, or they have reached the far shore of the surging waters.

sanskrit: व्यालैर्वापि महारण्ये भक्षिताः शूरमानिनः ।अथ वा विषमं प्राप्य विनष्टाः शाश्वतीः समाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vyālairvāpi mahāraṇye bhakṣitāḥ śūramāninaḥ ,atha vā viṣamaṁ prāpya vinaṣṭāḥ śāśvatīḥ samāḥ.
english: Or perhaps, considering themselves brave, they were devoured by wild animals in the great forest. Or else, having encountered great difficulty, they have perished forever.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 16. Or perhaps, considering themselves brave, they were devoured by wild animals in the great forest. Or else, having encountered great difficulty, they have perished forever.

sanskrit: तस्मान्मानसमव्यग्रं कृत्वा त्वं कुरुनन्दन ।कुरु कार्यं यथोत्साहं मन्यसे यन्नराधिप ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasmānmānasamavyagraṁ kṛtvā tvaṁ kurunandana ,kuru kāryaṁ yathotsāhaṁ manyase yannarādhipa.
english: Therefore, O Kuru scion, having made your mind unwavering, perform the duty with the enthusiasm that you, O king of men, deem appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 25 Verse 17. Therefore, O Kuru scion, having made your mind unwavering, perform the duty with the enthusiasm that you, O king of men, deem appropriate.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथाब्रवीन्महावीर्यो द्रोणस्तत्त्वार्थदर्शिवान् ।न तादृशा विनश्यन्ति नापि यान्ति पराभवम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,athābravīnmahāvīryo droṇastattvārthadarśivān ,na tādṛśā vinaśyanti nāpi yānti parābhavam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then the greatly valorous Droṇa, who perceived the true nature of reality, spoke: 'Such individuals do not perish, nor do they experience defeat.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then the greatly valorous Droṇa, who perceived the true nature of reality, spoke: 'Such individuals do not perish, nor do they experience defeat.'

sanskrit: शूराश्च कृतविद्याश्च बुद्धिमन्तो जितेन्द्रियाः ।धर्मज्ञाश्च कृतज्ञाश्च धर्मराजमनुव्रताः ॥२॥
transliteration: śūrāśca kṛtavidyāśca buddhimanto jitendriyāḥ ,dharmajñāśca kṛtajñāśca dharmarājamanuvratāḥ.
english: They are brave, accomplished in knowledge, intelligent, and have conquered their senses. They are also knowers of natural law (dharma), grateful, and devoted to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 2. They are brave, accomplished in knowledge, intelligent, and have conquered their senses. They are also knowers of natural law (dharma), grateful, and devoted to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: नीतिधर्मार्थतत्त्वज्ञं पितृवच्च समाहितम् ।धर्मे स्थितं सत्यधृतिं ज्येष्ठं ज्येष्ठापचायिनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: nītidharmārthatattvajñaṁ pitṛvacca samāhitam ,dharme sthitaṁ satyadhṛtiṁ jyeṣṭhaṁ jyeṣṭhāpacāyinam.
english: They are devoted to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), who understands the essence of policy, natural law (dharma), and worldly affairs; who is like a father and self-composed; who is established in (natural) law (dharma) and steadfast in truth; and who is the eldest and honors his elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 3. They are devoted to Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), who understands the essence of policy, natural law (dharma), and worldly affairs; who is like a father and self-composed; who is established in (natural) law (dharma) and steadfast in truth; and who is the eldest and honors his elders.

sanskrit: अनुव्रता महात्मानं भ्रातरं भ्रातरो नृप ।अजातशत्रुं ह्रीमन्तं तं च भ्रातॄननुव्रतम् ॥४॥
transliteration: anuvratā mahātmānaṁ bhrātaraṁ bhrātaro nṛpa ,ajātaśatruṁ hrīmantaṁ taṁ ca bhrātṝnanuvratam.
english: O King, the brothers are devoted to their great-souled (mahātman) brother, Yudhiṣṭhira, who has no enemies and is modest. And he, in turn, is devoted to his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 4. O King, the brothers are devoted to their great-souled (mahātman) brother, Yudhiṣṭhira, who has no enemies and is modest. And he, in turn, is devoted to his brothers.

sanskrit: तेषां तथा विधेयानां निभृतानां महात्मनाम् ।किमर्थं नीतिमान्पार्थः श्रेयो नैषां करिष्यति ॥५॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tathā vidheyānāṁ nibhṛtānāṁ mahātmanām ,kimarthaṁ nītimānpārthaḥ śreyo naiṣāṁ kariṣyati.
english: Why will the principled Pārtha (Arjuna) not secure the welfare of those Pāṇḍavas who are so obedient, humble, and great-souled (mahātman)?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 5. Why will the principled Pārtha (Arjuna) not secure the welfare of those Pāṇḍavas who are so obedient, humble, and great-souled (mahātman)?

sanskrit: तस्माद्यत्नात्प्रतीक्षन्ते कालस्योदयमागतम् ।न हि ते नाशमृच्छेयुरिति पश्याम्यहं धिया ॥६॥
transliteration: tasmādyatnātpratīkṣante kālasyodayamāgatam ,na hi te nāśamṛccheyuriti paśyāmyahaṁ dhiyā.
english: Therefore, they carefully await the arrival of the opportune time. For they will surely not meet destruction; this I understand with my intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. Therefore, they carefully await the arrival of the opportune time. For they will surely not meet destruction; this I understand with my intellect.

sanskrit: सांप्रतं चैव यत्कार्यं तच्च क्षिप्रमकालिकम् ।क्रियतां साधु संचिन्त्य वासश्चैषां प्रचिन्त्यताम् ॥७॥
transliteration: sāṁprataṁ caiva yatkāryaṁ tacca kṣipramakālikam ,kriyatāṁ sādhu saṁcintya vāsaścaiṣāṁ pracintyatām.
english: And indeed, whatever task is pressing now, let it be performed quickly and without delay, after careful consideration. And let their dwelling place also be arranged for them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. And indeed, whatever task is pressing now, let it be performed quickly and without delay, after careful consideration. And let their dwelling place also be arranged for them.

sanskrit: यथावत्पाण्डुपुत्राणां सर्वार्थेषु धृतात्मनाम् ।दुर्ज्ञेयाः खलु शूरास्ते अपापास्तपसा वृताः ॥८॥
transliteration: yathāvatpāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ sarvārtheṣu dhṛtātmanām ,durjñeyāḥ khalu śūrāste apāpāstapasā vṛtāḥ.
english: The sons of Pāṇḍu, who are resolute in all their endeavors, are indeed difficult to know. Those heroes are sinless and endowed with ascetic power (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. The sons of Pāṇḍu, who are resolute in all their endeavors, are indeed difficult to know. Those heroes are sinless and endowed with ascetic power (tapas).

sanskrit: शुद्धात्मा गुणवान्पार्थः सत्यवान्नीतिमाञ्शुचिः ।तेजोराशिरसंख्येयो गृह्णीयादपि चक्षुषी ॥९॥
transliteration: śuddhātmā guṇavānpārthaḥ satyavānnītimāñśuciḥ ,tejorāśirasaṁkhyeyo gṛhṇīyādapi cakṣuṣī.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha) has a pure inner self (ātman), is virtuous, truthful, righteous, and pure. He is an immeasurable mass of splendor, capable of seizing even one's eyes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. Arjuna (Pārtha) has a pure inner self (ātman), is virtuous, truthful, righteous, and pure. He is an immeasurable mass of splendor, capable of seizing even one's eyes.

sanskrit: विज्ञाय क्रियतां तस्माद्भूयश्च मृगयामहे ।ब्राह्मणैश्चारकैः सिद्धैर्ये चान्ये तद्विदो जनाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: vijñāya kriyatāṁ tasmādbhūyaśca mṛgayāmahe ,brāhmaṇaiścārakaiḥ siddhairye cānye tadvido janāḥ.
english: Therefore, let this be understood and acted upon. Moreover, we will search with Brahmins, spies, perfected beings (siddhas), and other persons who know this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. Therefore, let this be understood and acted upon. Moreover, we will search with Brahmins, spies, perfected beings (siddhas), and other persons who know this matter.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः शांतनवो भीष्मो भरतानां पितामहः ।श्रुतवान्देशकालज्ञस्तत्त्वज्ञः सर्वधर्मवित् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ śāṁtanavo bhīṣmo bharatānāṁ pitāmahaḥ ,śrutavāndeśakālajñastattvajñaḥ sarvadharmavit.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then Bhīṣma, the descendant of Śāntanu and the grandfather of the Bhāratas, who was learned, knowledgeable of place and time, a knower of truth, and conversant with all principles of natural law (dharma), (spoke).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then Bhīṣma, the descendant of Śāntanu and the grandfather of the Bhāratas, who was learned, knowledgeable of place and time, a knower of truth, and conversant with all principles of natural law (dharma), (spoke).

sanskrit: आचार्यवाक्योपरमे तद्वाक्यमभिसंदधत् ।हितार्थं स उवाचेमां भारतीं भारतान्प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: ācāryavākyoparame tadvākyamabhisaṁdadhat ,hitārthaṁ sa uvācemāṁ bhāratīṁ bhāratānprati.
english: When the preceptor's words concluded, he (Bhishma), bearing those words in mind for their welfare (hitārtham), spoke this address to the Bhāratas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 2. When the preceptor's words concluded, he (Bhishma), bearing those words in mind for their welfare (hitārtham), spoke this address to the Bhāratas.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरे समासक्तां धर्मज्ञे धर्मसंश्रिताम् ।असत्सु दुर्लभां नित्यं सतां चाभिमतां सदा ।भीष्मः समवदत्तत्र गिरं साधुभिरर्चिताम् ॥३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhire samāsaktāṁ dharmajñe dharmasaṁśritām ,asatsu durlabhāṁ nityaṁ satāṁ cābhimatāṁ sadā ,bhīṣmaḥ samavadattatra giraṁ sādhubhirarcitām.
english: There, Bhishma spoke a speech that was focused on Yudhishthira, the knower of natural law (dharma), and itself rooted in natural law (dharma). It is ever rare for the wicked to understand, but always cherished by the virtuous, and honored by good people.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 3. There, Bhishma spoke a speech that was focused on Yudhishthira, the knower of natural law (dharma), and itself rooted in natural law (dharma). It is ever rare for the wicked to understand, but always cherished by the virtuous, and honored by good people.

sanskrit: यथैष ब्राह्मणः प्राह द्रोणः सर्वार्थतत्त्ववित् ।सर्वलक्षणसंपन्ना नाशं नार्हन्ति पाण्डवाः ॥४॥
transliteration: yathaiṣa brāhmaṇaḥ prāha droṇaḥ sarvārthatattvavit ,sarvalakṣaṇasaṁpannā nāśaṁ nārhanti pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Just as this Brahmin (brāhmaṇa), Droṇa, the knower of the truth of all matters, has declared, "The Pāṇḍavas, who are endowed with all auspicious characteristics, do not deserve destruction."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 4. Just as this Brahmin (brāhmaṇa), Droṇa, the knower of the truth of all matters, has declared, "The Pāṇḍavas, who are endowed with all auspicious characteristics, do not deserve destruction."

sanskrit: श्रुतवृत्तोपसंपन्नाः साधुव्रतसमन्विताः ।वृद्धानुशासने मग्नाः सत्यव्रतपरायणाः ॥५॥
transliteration: śrutavṛttopasaṁpannāḥ sādhuvratasamanvitāḥ ,vṛddhānuśāsane magnāḥ satyavrataparāyaṇāḥ.
english: They are endowed with knowledge (śruta) and good conduct, committed to virtuous vows, absorbed in the instructions of their elders, and dedicated to the vow of truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 5. They are endowed with knowledge (śruta) and good conduct, committed to virtuous vows, absorbed in the instructions of their elders, and dedicated to the vow of truth.

sanskrit: समयं समयज्ञास्ते पालयन्तः शुचिव्रताः ।नावसीदितुमर्हन्ति उद्वहन्तः सतां धुरम् ॥६॥
transliteration: samayaṁ samayajñāste pālayantaḥ śucivratāḥ ,nāvasīditumarhanti udvahantaḥ satāṁ dhuram.
english: Those who understand the proper time and observe their pure vows, bearing the heavy responsibility of the virtuous, ought not to become distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 6. Those who understand the proper time and observe their pure vows, bearing the heavy responsibility of the virtuous, ought not to become distressed.

sanskrit: धर्मतश्चैव गुप्तास्ते स्ववीर्येण च पाण्डवाः ।न नाशमधिगच्छेयुरिति मे धीयते मतिः ॥७॥
transliteration: dharmataścaiva guptāste svavīryeṇa ca pāṇḍavāḥ ,na nāśamadhigaccheyuriti me dhīyate matiḥ.
english: Indeed, protected both by natural law (dharma) and by their own valor, the Pāṇḍavas should not meet with destruction - this is my firm belief.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 7. Indeed, protected both by natural law (dharma) and by their own valor, the Pāṇḍavas should not meet with destruction - this is my firm belief.

sanskrit: तत्र बुद्धिं प्रणेष्यामि पाण्डवान्प्रति भारत ।न तु नीतिः सुनीतस्य शक्यतेऽन्वेषितुं परैः ॥८॥
transliteration: tatra buddhiṁ praṇeṣyāmi pāṇḍavānprati bhārata ,na tu nītiḥ sunītasya śakyate'nveṣituṁ paraiḥ.
english: Therefore, O Bhārata, I will apply my intellect to the matter of the Pāṇḍavas. But the policy of a well-guided person cannot be easily discovered by opponents.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 8. Therefore, O Bhārata, I will apply my intellect to the matter of the Pāṇḍavas. But the policy of a well-guided person cannot be easily discovered by opponents.

sanskrit: यत्तु शक्यमिहास्माभिस्तान्वै संचिन्त्य पाण्डवान् ।बुद्ध्या प्रवक्तुं न द्रोहात्प्रवक्ष्यामि निबोध तत् ॥९॥
transliteration: yattu śakyamihāsmābhistānvai saṁcintya pāṇḍavān ,buddhyā pravaktuṁ na drohātpravakṣyāmi nibodha tat.
english: Therefore, whatever can be spoken by us here, having truly considered the Pāṇḍavas with wisdom, that I shall declare, not out of malice. Listen to it carefully.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 9. Therefore, whatever can be spoken by us here, having truly considered the Pāṇḍavas with wisdom, that I shall declare, not out of malice. Listen to it carefully.

sanskrit: सा त्वियं साधु वक्तव्या न त्वनीतिः कथंचन ।वृद्धानुशासने तात तिष्ठतः सत्यशीलिनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sā tviyaṁ sādhu vaktavyā na tvanītiḥ kathaṁcana ,vṛddhānuśāsane tāta tiṣṭhataḥ satyaśīlinaḥ.
english: O dear one, this matter must certainly be stated properly, and never by any means as an impropriety, for one who adheres to the instructions of elders and is devoted to truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 10. O dear one, this matter must certainly be stated properly, and never by any means as an impropriety, for one who adheres to the instructions of elders and is devoted to truth.

sanskrit: अवश्यं त्विह धीरेण सतां मध्ये विवक्षता ।यथामति विवक्तव्यं सर्वशो धर्मलिप्सया ॥११॥
transliteration: avaśyaṁ tviha dhīreṇa satāṁ madhye vivakṣatā ,yathāmati vivaktavyaṁ sarvaśo dharmalipsayā.
english: Indeed, here, a wise person, when wishing to speak among the virtuous, must express (their views) fully, according to their own understanding, with the sole desire to uphold natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 11. Indeed, here, a wise person, when wishing to speak among the virtuous, must express (their views) fully, according to their own understanding, with the sole desire to uphold natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: तत्र नाहं तथा मन्ये यथायमितरो जनः ।पुरे जनपदे वापि यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatra nāhaṁ tathā manye yathāyamitaro janaḥ ,pure janapade vāpi yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Regarding that matter, I do not think the same way as these other people do. This is especially true in any city or region where King Yudhishthira is the ruler.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 12. Regarding that matter, I do not think the same way as these other people do. This is especially true in any city or region where King Yudhishthira is the ruler.

sanskrit: नासूयको न चापीर्षुर्नातिवादी न मत्सरी ।भविष्यति जनस्तत्र स्वं स्वं धर्ममनुव्रतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nāsūyako na cāpīrṣurnātivādī na matsarī ,bhaviṣyati janastatra svaṁ svaṁ dharmamanuvrataḥ.
english: There, the populace will not be malicious, nor jealous, nor overly critical, nor miserly. Each will faithfully follow their own intrinsic nature (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 13. There, the populace will not be malicious, nor jealous, nor overly critical, nor miserly. Each will faithfully follow their own intrinsic nature (dharma).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मघोषाश्च भूयांसः पूर्णाहुत्यस्तथैव च ।क्रतवश्च भविष्यन्ति भूयांसो भूरिदक्षिणाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: brahmaghoṣāśca bhūyāṁsaḥ pūrṇāhutyastathaiva ca ,kratavaśca bhaviṣyanti bhūyāṁso bhūridakṣiṇāḥ.
english: There will be numerous recitations of the Vedas and, similarly, many final offerings. Additionally, there will be many Vedic rituals (kratu) accompanied by generous donations.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 14. There will be numerous recitations of the Vedas and, similarly, many final offerings. Additionally, there will be many Vedic rituals (kratu) accompanied by generous donations.

sanskrit: सदा च तत्र पर्जन्यः सम्यग्वर्षी न संशयः ।संपन्नसस्या च मही निरीतीका भविष्यति ॥१५॥
transliteration: sadā ca tatra parjanyaḥ samyagvarṣī na saṁśayaḥ ,saṁpannasasyā ca mahī nirītīkā bhaviṣyati.
english: And there, the rain will always fall properly, without a doubt. The earth will have flourishing crops and be free from calamities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 15. And there, the rain will always fall properly, without a doubt. The earth will have flourishing crops and be free from calamities.

sanskrit: रसवन्ति च धान्यानि गुणवन्ति फलानि च ।गन्धवन्ति च माल्यानि शुभशब्दा च भारती ॥१६॥
transliteration: rasavanti ca dhānyāni guṇavanti phalāni ca ,gandhavanti ca mālyāni śubhaśabdā ca bhāratī.
english: The grains will be flavorful, and the fruits will be wholesome. The garlands will be fragrant, and speech will be auspicious.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 16. The grains will be flavorful, and the fruits will be wholesome. The garlands will be fragrant, and speech will be auspicious.

sanskrit: वायुश्च सुखसंस्पर्शो निष्प्रतीपं च दर्शनम् ।भयं नाभ्याविशेत्तत्र यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vāyuśca sukhasaṁsparśo niṣpratīpaṁ ca darśanam ,bhayaṁ nābhyāviśettatra yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: The wind will be pleasant to the touch, and all sights will be unimpeded (niṣpratīpa). Fear will not enter there, where King Yudhiṣṭhira resides.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 17. The wind will be pleasant to the touch, and all sights will be unimpeded (niṣpratīpa). Fear will not enter there, where King Yudhiṣṭhira resides.

sanskrit: गावश्च बहुलास्तत्र न कृशा न च दुर्दुहाः ।पयांसि दधिसर्पींषि रसवन्ति हितानि च ॥१८॥
transliteration: gāvaśca bahulāstatra na kṛśā na ca durduhāḥ ,payāṁsi dadhisarpīṁṣi rasavanti hitāni ca.
english: There, the cows are numerous; they are neither emaciated nor difficult to milk. Their milk, yogurt, and ghee are flavorful and wholesome.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 18. There, the cows are numerous; they are neither emaciated nor difficult to milk. Their milk, yogurt, and ghee are flavorful and wholesome.

sanskrit: गुणवन्ति च पानानि भोज्यानि रसवन्ति च ।तत्र देशे भविष्यन्ति यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥१९॥
transliteration: guṇavanti ca pānāni bhojyāni rasavanti ca ,tatra deśe bhaviṣyanti yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Also, the drinks and foods in that land where King Yudhiṣṭhira rules will be of good quality and flavorful.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 19. Also, the drinks and foods in that land where King Yudhiṣṭhira rules will be of good quality and flavorful.

sanskrit: रसाः स्पर्शाश्च गन्धाश्च शब्दाश्चापि गुणान्विताः ।दृश्यानि च प्रसन्नानि यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: rasāḥ sparśāśca gandhāśca śabdāścāpi guṇānvitāḥ ,dṛśyāni ca prasannāni yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: In the land where King Yudhiṣṭhira rules, the tastes, touches, smells, and sounds will also be of excellent quality, and the sights will be pleasing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 20. In the land where King Yudhiṣṭhira rules, the tastes, touches, smells, and sounds will also be of excellent quality, and the sights will be pleasing.

sanskrit: स्वैः स्वैर्गुणैः सुसंयुक्तास्तस्मिन्वर्षे त्रयोदशे ।देशे तस्मिन्भविष्यन्ति तात पाण्डवसंयुते ॥२१॥
transliteration: svaiḥ svairguṇaiḥ susaṁyuktāstasminvarṣe trayodaśe ,deśe tasminbhaviṣyanti tāta pāṇḍavasaṁyute.
english: Dear one, in that land, allied with the Pāṇḍavas, during that thirteenth year, all these (attributes, discussed in previous verses) will be well-endowed with their own respective excellent qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 21. Dear one, in that land, allied with the Pāṇḍavas, during that thirteenth year, all these (attributes, discussed in previous verses) will be well-endowed with their own respective excellent qualities.

sanskrit: संप्रीतिमाञ्जनस्तत्र संतुष्टः शुचिरव्ययः ।देवतातिथिपूजासु सर्वभूतानुरागवान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: saṁprītimāñjanastatra saṁtuṣṭaḥ śuciravyayaḥ ,devatātithipūjāsu sarvabhūtānurāgavān.
english: There, the people will be full of affection, contented, pure, and steadfast. They will be devoted to worshipping gods and guests, and benevolent towards all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 22. There, the people will be full of affection, contented, pure, and steadfast. They will be devoted to worshipping gods and guests, and benevolent towards all beings.

sanskrit: इष्टदानो महोत्साहः शश्वद्धर्मपरायणः ।अशुभद्विट्शुभप्रेप्सुर्नित्ययज्ञः शुभव्रतः ।भविष्यति जनस्तत्र यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ॥२३॥
transliteration: iṣṭadāno mahotsāhaḥ śaśvaddharmaparāyaṇaḥ ,aśubhadviṭśubhaprepsurnityayajñaḥ śubhavrataḥ ,bhaviṣyati janastatra yatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Where King Yudhishthira rules, the people there will be generous with desired gifts, greatly enthusiastic, and always devoted to natural law (dharma). They will despise inauspicious things, desire prosperity, constantly perform sacred rituals (yajña), and observe virtuous vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 23. Where King Yudhishthira rules, the people there will be generous with desired gifts, greatly enthusiastic, and always devoted to natural law (dharma). They will despise inauspicious things, desire prosperity, constantly perform sacred rituals (yajña), and observe virtuous vows.

sanskrit: त्यक्तवाक्यानृतस्तात शुभकल्याणमङ्गलः ।शुभार्थेप्सुः शुभमतिर्यत्र राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।भविष्यति जनस्तत्र नित्यं चेष्टप्रियव्रतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tyaktavākyānṛtastāta śubhakalyāṇamaṅgalaḥ ,śubhārthepsuḥ śubhamatiryatra rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhaviṣyati janastatra nityaṁ ceṣṭapriyavrataḥ.
english: O dear one, where King Yudhishthira rules, the people there will be truthful in speech, auspicious, bringing well-being, desiring good outcomes, and of a good mind. They will always observe their cherished and desired vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 24. O dear one, where King Yudhishthira rules, the people there will be truthful in speech, auspicious, bringing well-being, desiring good outcomes, and of a good mind. They will always observe their cherished and desired vows.

sanskrit: धर्मात्मा स तदादृश्यः सोऽपि तात द्विजातिभिः ।किं पुनः प्राकृतैः पार्थः शक्यो विज्ञातुमन्ततः ॥२५॥
transliteration: dharmātmā sa tadādṛśyaḥ so'pi tāta dvijātibhiḥ ,kiṁ punaḥ prākṛtaiḥ pārthaḥ śakyo vijñātumantataḥ.
english: O dear one, that righteous-souled (dharmātmā) king, even by the twice-born, is difficult to perceive (adṛśyaḥ) or understand. How then can Pārtha (Yudhishthira) be truly known by ordinary people?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 25. O dear one, that righteous-souled (dharmātmā) king, even by the twice-born, is difficult to perceive (adṛśyaḥ) or understand. How then can Pārtha (Yudhishthira) be truly known by ordinary people?

sanskrit: यस्मिन्सत्यं धृतिर्दानं परा शान्तिर्ध्रुवा क्षमा ।ह्रीः श्रीः कीर्तिः परं तेज आनृशंस्यमथार्जवम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: yasminsatyaṁ dhṛtirdānaṁ parā śāntirdhruvā kṣamā ,hrīḥ śrīḥ kīrtiḥ paraṁ teja ānṛśaṁsyamathārjavam.
english: In whom (or in which situation) there is truth, resolve, generosity (dāna), supreme peace, constant forgiveness, modesty, splendor, fame, supreme brilliance, compassion, and moreover, integrity.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 26. In whom (or in which situation) there is truth, resolve, generosity (dāna), supreme peace, constant forgiveness, modesty, splendor, fame, supreme brilliance, compassion, and moreover, integrity.

sanskrit: तस्मात्तत्र निवासं तु छन्नं सत्रेण धीमतः ।गतिं वा परमां तस्य नोत्सहे वक्तुमन्यथा ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasmāttatra nivāsaṁ tu channaṁ satreṇa dhīmataḥ ,gatiṁ vā paramāṁ tasya notsahe vaktumanyathā.
english: Therefore, regarding his dwelling there, which is indeed concealed by a sacrificial session (satra) of the wise one, or his supreme destiny, I am unable to speak otherwise.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 27. Therefore, regarding his dwelling there, which is indeed concealed by a sacrificial session (satra) of the wise one, or his supreme destiny, I am unable to speak otherwise.

sanskrit: एवमेतत्तु संचिन्त्य यत्कृतं मन्यसे हितम् ।तत्क्षिप्रं कुरु कौरव्य यद्येवं श्रद्दधासि मे ॥२८॥
transliteration: evametattu saṁcintya yatkṛtaṁ manyase hitam ,tatkṣipraṁ kuru kauravya yadyevaṁ śraddadhāsi me.
english: Having thus considered this, whatever you deem beneficial, do that quickly, O Kauravya, if you have faith (śraddhā) in me in this way.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 27 Verse 28. Having thus considered this, whatever you deem beneficial, do that quickly, O Kauravya, if you have faith (śraddhā) in me in this way.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः शारद्वतो वाक्यमित्युवाच कृपस्तदा ।युक्तं प्राप्तं च वृद्धेन पाण्डवान्प्रति भाषितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ śāradvato vākyamityuvāca kṛpastadā ,yuktaṁ prāptaṁ ca vṛddhena pāṇḍavānprati bhāṣitam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Kṛpa Śāradvata spoke thus: "What has been spoken to the Pāṇḍavas by the elder (vṛddha) is proper and well-received."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Kṛpa Śāradvata spoke thus: "What has been spoken to the Pāṇḍavas by the elder (vṛddha) is proper and well-received."

sanskrit: धर्मार्थसहितं श्लक्ष्णं तत्त्वतश्च सहेतुमत् ।तत्रानुरूपं भीष्मेण ममाप्यत्र गिरं शृणु ॥२॥
transliteration: dharmārthasahitaṁ ślakṣṇaṁ tattvataśca sahetumat ,tatrānurūpaṁ bhīṣmeṇa mamāpyatra giraṁ śṛṇu.
english: Listen now to my words here, which are appropriate for this context, just like Bhishma's speech. My words are subtle, essentially true, well-reasoned, and pertain to both natural law (dharma) and practical prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 2. Listen now to my words here, which are appropriate for this context, just like Bhishma's speech. My words are subtle, essentially true, well-reasoned, and pertain to both natural law (dharma) and practical prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: तेषां चैव गतिस्तीर्थैर्वासश्चैषां प्रचिन्त्यताम् ।नीतिर्विधीयतां चापि सांप्रतं या हिता भवेत् ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ caiva gatistīrthairvāsaścaiṣāṁ pracintyatām ,nītirvidhīyatāṁ cāpi sāṁprataṁ yā hitā bhavet.
english: Their movements and dwelling places should indeed be thoroughly considered by appropriate means. Furthermore, let a policy (nīti) now be established that would prove beneficial.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 3. Their movements and dwelling places should indeed be thoroughly considered by appropriate means. Furthermore, let a policy (nīti) now be established that would prove beneficial.

sanskrit: नावज्ञेयो रिपुस्तात प्राकृतोऽपि बुभूषता ।किं पुनः पाण्डवास्तात सर्वास्त्रकुशला रणे ॥४॥
transliteration: nāvajñeyo ripustāta prākṛto'pi bubhūṣatā ,kiṁ punaḥ pāṇḍavāstāta sarvāstrakuśalā raṇe.
english: Dear son, one who desires to prosper should not despise an enemy, even an ordinary one. How much less should one despise the Pandavas, dear son, who are skilled in all weapons on the battlefield?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 4. Dear son, one who desires to prosper should not despise an enemy, even an ordinary one. How much less should one despise the Pandavas, dear son, who are skilled in all weapons on the battlefield?

sanskrit: तस्मात्सत्रं प्रविष्टेषु पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ।गूढभावेषु छन्नेषु काले चोदयमागते ॥५॥
transliteration: tasmātsatraṁ praviṣṭeṣu pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu ,gūḍhabhāveṣu channeṣu kāle codayamāgate.
english: Therefore, as the great-souled (mahātman) Pandavas, with their intentions concealed and themselves in hiding, have entered a retreat, and with the time for their emergence now at hand...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 5. Therefore, as the great-souled (mahātman) Pandavas, with their intentions concealed and themselves in hiding, have entered a retreat, and with the time for their emergence now at hand...

sanskrit: स्वराष्ट्रपरराष्ट्रेषु ज्ञातव्यं बलमात्मनः ।उदये पाण्डवानां च प्राप्ते काले न संशयः ॥६॥
transliteration: svarāṣṭrapararāṣṭreṣu jñātavyaṁ balamātmanaḥ ,udaye pāṇḍavānāṁ ca prāpte kāle na saṁśayaḥ.
english: One should ascertain their own strength (ātman) both within their own country and in foreign lands. There is no doubt that when the time for the rise of the Pāṇḍavas arrives, it will happen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 6. One should ascertain their own strength (ātman) both within their own country and in foreign lands. There is no doubt that when the time for the rise of the Pāṇḍavas arrives, it will happen.

sanskrit: निवृत्तसमयाः पार्था महात्मानो महाबलाः ।महोत्साहा भविष्यन्ति पाण्डवा ह्यतितेजसः ॥७॥
transliteration: nivṛttasamayāḥ pārthā mahātmāno mahābalāḥ ,mahotsāhā bhaviṣyanti pāṇḍavā hyatitejasaḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, sons of Pṛthā, having completed their term, will indeed become great-souled (mahātman), immensely powerful, and filled with great enthusiasm, possessing exceptional vigor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 7. The Pāṇḍavas, sons of Pṛthā, having completed their term, will indeed become great-souled (mahātman), immensely powerful, and filled with great enthusiasm, possessing exceptional vigor.

sanskrit: तस्माद्बलं च कोशं च नीतिश्चापि विधीयताम् ।यथा कालोदये प्राप्ते सम्यक्तैः संदधामहे ॥८॥
transliteration: tasmādbalaṁ ca kośaṁ ca nītiścāpi vidhīyatām ,yathā kālodaye prāpte samyaktaiḥ saṁdadhāmahe.
english: Therefore, let resources (bala), the treasury (kośa), and political strategy (nīti) be duly prepared, so that when the opportune time arrives, we may properly reconcile with them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 8. Therefore, let resources (bala), the treasury (kośa), and political strategy (nīti) be duly prepared, so that when the opportune time arrives, we may properly reconcile with them.

sanskrit: तात मन्यामि तत्सर्वं बुध्यस्व बलमात्मनः ।नियतं सर्वमित्रेषु बलवत्स्वबलेषु च ॥९॥
transliteration: tāta manyāmi tatsarvaṁ budhyasva balamātmanaḥ ,niyataṁ sarvamitreṣu balavatsvabaleṣu ca.
english: Father, I consider all this (to be true). Understand your own strength (ātman) consistently, both among all allies and among those who are strong or weak.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 9. Father, I consider all this (to be true). Understand your own strength (ātman) consistently, both among all allies and among those who are strong or weak.

sanskrit: उच्चावचं बलं ज्ञात्वा मध्यस्थं चापि भारत ।प्रहृष्टमप्रहृष्टं च संदधाम तथा परैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: uccāvacaṁ balaṁ jñātvā madhyasthaṁ cāpi bhārata ,prahṛṣṭamaprahṛṣṭaṁ ca saṁdadhāma tathā paraiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, after assessing the various types of military strength (bala), including neutral ones, and those that are enthusiastic and unenthusiastic, we should therefore make alliances with others.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 10. O Bhārata, after assessing the various types of military strength (bala), including neutral ones, and those that are enthusiastic and unenthusiastic, we should therefore make alliances with others.

sanskrit: साम्ना भेदेन दानेन दण्डेन बलिकर्मणा ।न्यायेनानम्य च परान्बलाच्चानम्य दुर्बलान् ॥११॥
transliteration: sāmnā bhedena dānena daṇḍena balikarmaṇā ,nyāyenānamya ca parānbalāccānamya durbalān.
english: By means of conciliation, by causing division, by offering gifts (dāna), by applying punishment, and by exacting tribute, one should subdue enemies justly; and also forcibly subdue the weak.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 11. By means of conciliation, by causing division, by offering gifts (dāna), by applying punishment, and by exacting tribute, one should subdue enemies justly; and also forcibly subdue the weak.

sanskrit: सान्त्वयित्वा च मित्राणि बलं चाभाष्यतां सुखम् ।सकोशबलसंवृद्धः सम्यक्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: sāntvayitvā ca mitrāṇi balaṁ cābhāṣyatāṁ sukham ,sakośabalasaṁvṛddhaḥ samyaksiddhimavāpsyasi.
english: And after conciliating your allies, and ensuring the army is addressed pleasantly, you, having been thoroughly enriched with treasury and military strength (bala), will attain complete success.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 12. And after conciliating your allies, and ensuring the army is addressed pleasantly, you, having been thoroughly enriched with treasury and military strength (bala), will attain complete success.

sanskrit: योत्स्यसे चापि बलिभिररिभिः प्रत्युपस्थितैः ।अन्यैस्त्वं पाण्डवैर्वापि हीनस्वबलवाहनैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yotsyase cāpi balibhiraribhiḥ pratyupasthitaiḥ ,anyaistvaṁ pāṇḍavairvāpi hīnasvabalavāhanaiḥ.
english: You will fight with powerful enemies who are confronting you. And you will also fight with other opponents, or even with the Pāṇḍavas, who are diminished in their own strength (bala) and vehicles.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 13. You will fight with powerful enemies who are confronting you. And you will also fight with other opponents, or even with the Pāṇḍavas, who are diminished in their own strength (bala) and vehicles.

sanskrit: एवं सर्वं विनिश्चित्य व्यवसायं स्वधर्मतः ।यथाकालं मनुष्येन्द्र चिरं सुखमवाप्स्यसि ॥१४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sarvaṁ viniścitya vyavasāyaṁ svadharmataḥ ,yathākālaṁ manuṣyendra ciraṁ sukhamavāpsyasi.
english: Having thus determined everything and your undertaking in accordance with your own natural law (dharma), O lord of men, you will obtain lasting happiness at the appropriate time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 28 Verse 14. Having thus determined everything and your undertaking in accordance with your own natural law (dharma), O lord of men, you will obtain lasting happiness at the appropriate time.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथ राजा त्रिगर्तानां सुशर्मा रथयूथपः ।प्राप्तकालमिदं वाक्यमुवाच त्वरितो भृशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,atha rājā trigartānāṁ suśarmā rathayūthapaḥ ,prāptakālamidaṁ vākyamuvāca tvarito bhṛśam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Suśarman, the king of the Trigartas and a commander of chariot divisions, spoke this opportune statement, very quickly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Suśarman, the king of the Trigartas and a commander of chariot divisions, spoke this opportune statement, very quickly.

sanskrit: असकृन्निकृतः पूर्वं मत्स्यैः साल्वेयकैः सह ।सूतेन चैव मत्स्यस्य कीचकेन पुनः पुनः ॥२॥
transliteration: asakṛnnikṛtaḥ pūrvaṁ matsyaiḥ sālveyakaiḥ saha ,sūtena caiva matsyasya kīcakena punaḥ punaḥ.
english: He was repeatedly humiliated before, by the Matsyas along with the Sālveyakas, and indeed, again and again, by Kīcaka, the charioteer of King Matsya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 2. He was repeatedly humiliated before, by the Matsyas along with the Sālveyakas, and indeed, again and again, by Kīcaka, the charioteer of King Matsya.

sanskrit: बाधितो बन्धुभिः सार्धं बलाद्बलवता विभो ।स कर्णमभ्युदीक्ष्याथ दुर्योधनमभाषत ॥३॥
transliteration: bādhito bandhubhiḥ sārdhaṁ balādbalavatā vibho ,sa karṇamabhyudīkṣyātha duryodhanamabhāṣata.
english: O mighty one, having been forcibly afflicted along with his kinsmen by that strong one, he then, after looking at Karṇa, addressed Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 3. O mighty one, having been forcibly afflicted along with his kinsmen by that strong one, he then, after looking at Karṇa, addressed Duryodhana.

sanskrit: असकृन्मत्स्यराज्ञा मे राष्ट्रं बाधितमोजसा ।प्रणेता कीचकश्चास्य बलवानभवत्पुरा ॥४॥
transliteration: asakṛnmatsyarājñā me rāṣṭraṁ bādhitamojasā ,praṇetā kīcakaścāsya balavānabhavatpurā.
english: My kingdom has often been oppressed by the Matsya king through his power. Previously, Kicaka was also his powerful leader.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 4. My kingdom has often been oppressed by the Matsya king through his power. Previously, Kicaka was also his powerful leader.

sanskrit: क्रूरोऽमर्षी स दुष्टात्मा भुवि प्रख्यातविक्रमः ।निहतस्तत्र गन्धर्वैः पापकर्मा नृशंसवान् ॥५॥
transliteration: krūro'marṣī sa duṣṭātmā bhuvi prakhyātavikramaḥ ,nihatastatra gandharvaiḥ pāpakarmā nṛśaṁsavān.
english: That cruel, wrathful, wicked-souled man (ātman), renowned for his valor on earth, that evil-doer (karma), that merciless one, was slain there by the Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 5. That cruel, wrathful, wicked-souled man (ātman), renowned for his valor on earth, that evil-doer (karma), that merciless one, was slain there by the Gandharvas.

sanskrit: तस्मिंश्च निहते राजन्हीनदर्पो निराश्रयः ।भविष्यति निरुत्साहो विराट इति मे मतिः ॥६॥
transliteration: tasmiṁśca nihate rājanhīnadarpo nirāśrayaḥ ,bhaviṣyati nirutsāho virāṭa iti me matiḥ.
english: And when he is killed, O king, Virata will become humbled, helpless, and devoid of enthusiasm. This is my conviction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 6. And when he is killed, O king, Virata will become humbled, helpless, and devoid of enthusiasm. This is my conviction.

sanskrit: तत्र यात्रा मम मता यदि ते रोचतेऽनघ ।कौरवाणां च सर्वेषां कर्णस्य च महात्मनः ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra yātrā mama matā yadi te rocate'nagha ,kauravāṇāṁ ca sarveṣāṁ karṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ.
english: Therefore, O sinless one, an expedition (against Virata) is my proposal, if it is agreeable to you, and to all the Kauravas, and to the great-souled (ātman) Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 7. Therefore, O sinless one, an expedition (against Virata) is my proposal, if it is agreeable to you, and to all the Kauravas, and to the great-souled (ātman) Karna.

sanskrit: एतत्प्राप्तमहं मन्ये कार्यमात्ययिकं हितम् ।राष्ट्रं तस्याभियात्वाशु बहुधान्यसमाकुलम् ॥८॥
transliteration: etatprāptamahaṁ manye kāryamātyayikaṁ hitam ,rāṣṭraṁ tasyābhiyātvāśu bahudhānyasamākulam.
english: I believe that this urgent and beneficial task has fallen to us: we should swiftly attack his kingdom, which is abundant in grains.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 8. I believe that this urgent and beneficial task has fallen to us: we should swiftly attack his kingdom, which is abundant in grains.

sanskrit: आददामोऽस्य रत्नानि विविधानि वसूनि च ।ग्रामान्राष्ट्राणि वा तस्य हरिष्यामो विभागशः ॥९॥
transliteration: ādadāmo'sya ratnāni vividhāni vasūni ca ,grāmānrāṣṭrāṇi vā tasya hariṣyāmo vibhāgaśaḥ.
english: We shall seize his diverse jewels and wealth, and we will take his villages or kingdoms, portion by portion.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 9. We shall seize his diverse jewels and wealth, and we will take his villages or kingdoms, portion by portion.

sanskrit: अथ वा गोसहस्राणि बहूनि च शुभानि च ।विविधानि हरिष्यामः प्रतिपीड्य पुरं बलात् ॥१०॥
transliteration: atha vā gosahasrāṇi bahūni ca śubhāni ca ,vividhāni hariṣyāmaḥ pratipīḍya puraṁ balāt.
english: Alternatively, after having forcefully oppressed the city, we shall seize thousands of many, auspicious, and diverse cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 10. Alternatively, after having forcefully oppressed the city, we shall seize thousands of many, auspicious, and diverse cows.

sanskrit: कौरवैः सह संगम्य त्रिगर्तैश्च विशां पते ।गास्तस्यापहरामाशु सह सर्वैः सुसंहताः ॥११॥
transliteration: kauravaiḥ saha saṁgamya trigartaiśca viśāṁ pate ,gāstasyāpaharāmāśu saha sarvaiḥ susaṁhatāḥ.
english: O Lord of the people, having allied with the Kauravas and the Trigartas, let us, all of us firmly united, swiftly seize his cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 11. O Lord of the people, having allied with the Kauravas and the Trigartas, let us, all of us firmly united, swiftly seize his cows.

sanskrit: संधिं वा तेन कृत्वा तु निबध्नीमोऽस्य पौरुषम् ।हत्वा चास्य चमूं कृत्स्नां वशमन्वानयामहे ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁdhiṁ vā tena kṛtvā tu nibadhnīmo'sya pauruṣam ,hatvā cāsya camūṁ kṛtsnāṁ vaśamanvānayāmahe.
english: Alternatively, by forming an alliance with him, we can restrain his prowess. Or, by destroying his entire army, we can bring him under our control.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 12. Alternatively, by forming an alliance with him, we can restrain his prowess. Or, by destroying his entire army, we can bring him under our control.

sanskrit: तं वशे न्यायतः कृत्वा सुखं वत्स्यामहे वयम् ।भवतो बलवृद्धिश्च भविष्यति न संशयः ॥१३॥
transliteration: taṁ vaśe nyāyataḥ kṛtvā sukhaṁ vatsyāmahe vayam ,bhavato balavṛddhiśca bhaviṣyati na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Having justly brought him under our control, we shall live happily. And, without a doubt, your strength will also increase.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 13. Having justly brought him under our control, we shall live happily. And, without a doubt, your strength will also increase.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं तस्य कर्णो राजानमब्रवीत् ।सूक्तं सुशर्मणा वाक्यं प्राप्तकालं हितं च नः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā vacanaṁ tasya karṇo rājānamabravīt ,sūktaṁ suśarmaṇā vākyaṁ prāptakālaṁ hitaṁ ca naḥ.
english: Upon hearing his words, Karṇa spoke to the king: 'The statement made by Suśarman is well-said, timely, and beneficial for us.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 14. Upon hearing his words, Karṇa spoke to the king: 'The statement made by Suśarman is well-said, timely, and beneficial for us.'

sanskrit: तस्मात्क्षिप्रं विनिर्यामो योजयित्वा वरूथिनीम् ।विभज्य चाप्यनीकानि यथा वा मन्यसेऽनघ ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasmātkṣipraṁ viniryāmo yojayitvā varūthinīm ,vibhajya cāpyanīkāni yathā vā manyase'nagha.
english: Therefore, let us quickly march forth after assembling the army. Or, after dividing the troops, as you deem appropriate, O sinless one.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 15. Therefore, let us quickly march forth after assembling the army. Or, after dividing the troops, as you deem appropriate, O sinless one.

sanskrit: प्रज्ञावान्कुरुवृद्धोऽयं सर्वेषां नः पितामहः ।आचार्यश्च तथा द्रोणः कृपः शारद्वतस्तथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: prajñāvānkuruvṛddho'yaṁ sarveṣāṁ naḥ pitāmahaḥ ,ācāryaśca tathā droṇaḥ kṛpaḥ śāradvatastathā.
english: This wise elder of the Kurus is our grandfather (Bhishma). Also present are the teacher Drona and Kripa, the son of Śaradvan.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 16. This wise elder of the Kurus is our grandfather (Bhishma). Also present are the teacher Drona and Kripa, the son of Śaradvan.

sanskrit: मन्यन्ते ते यथा सर्वे तथा यात्रा विधीयताम् ।संमन्त्र्य चाशु गच्छामः साधनार्थं महीपतेः ॥१७॥
transliteration: manyante te yathā sarve tathā yātrā vidhīyatām ,saṁmantrya cāśu gacchāmaḥ sādhanārthaṁ mahīpateḥ.
english: Let the expedition be arranged just as all of them deem appropriate. And having deliberated quickly, let us go forth to secure the king's objective.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 17. Let the expedition be arranged just as all of them deem appropriate. And having deliberated quickly, let us go forth to secure the king's objective.

sanskrit: किं च नः पाण्डवैः कार्यं हीनार्थबलपौरुषैः ।अत्यर्थं वा प्रनष्टास्ते प्राप्ता वापि यमक्षयम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: kiṁ ca naḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ kāryaṁ hīnārthabalapauruṣaiḥ ,atyarthaṁ vā pranaṣṭāste prāptā vāpi yamakṣayam.
english: And what concern do we have with the Pāṇḍavas, who are devoid of wealth, strength, and valor? They are either utterly ruined, or they have even reached the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 18. And what concern do we have with the Pāṇḍavas, who are devoid of wealth, strength, and valor? They are either utterly ruined, or they have even reached the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: यामो राजन्ननुद्विग्ना विराटविषयं वयम् ।आदास्यामो हि गास्तस्य विविधानि वसूनि च ॥१९॥
transliteration: yāmo rājannanudvignā virāṭaviṣayaṁ vayam ,ādāsyāmo hi gāstasya vividhāni vasūni ca.
english: O King, let us go unperturbed to the region of Virāṭa. Indeed, we will seize his cows and various riches.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 19. O King, let us go unperturbed to the region of Virāṭa. Indeed, we will seize his cows and various riches.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनो राजा वाक्यमादाय तस्य तत् ।वैकर्तनस्य कर्णस्य क्षिप्रमाज्ञापयत्स्वयम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato duryodhano rājā vākyamādāya tasya tat ,vaikartanasya karṇasya kṣipramājñāpayatsvayam.
english: Then King Duryodhana, having received that instruction from Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana, quickly gave the order himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 20. Then King Duryodhana, having received that instruction from Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana, quickly gave the order himself.

sanskrit: शासने नित्यसंयुक्तं दुःशासनमनन्तरम् ।सह वृद्धैस्तु संमन्त्र्य क्षिप्रं योजय वाहिनीम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: śāsane nityasaṁyuktaṁ duḥśāsanamanantaram ,saha vṛddhaistu saṁmantrya kṣipraṁ yojaya vāhinīm.
english: Immediately afterwards, [involve] Duḥśāsana, who is constantly dedicated to command. But, having consulted with the elders, quickly arrange the army.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 21. Immediately afterwards, [involve] Duḥśāsana, who is constantly dedicated to command. But, having consulted with the elders, quickly arrange the army.

sanskrit: यथोद्देशं च गच्छामः सहिताः सर्वकौरवैः ।सुशर्मा तु यथोद्दिष्टं देशं यातु महारथः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yathoddeśaṁ ca gacchāmaḥ sahitāḥ sarvakauravaiḥ ,suśarmā tu yathoddiṣṭaṁ deśaṁ yātu mahārathaḥ.
english: And let us go, accompanied by all the Kauravas, to the designated location. As for Suśarmā, the great warrior, let him go to the place as instructed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 22. And let us go, accompanied by all the Kauravas, to the designated location. As for Suśarmā, the great warrior, let him go to the place as instructed.

sanskrit: त्रिगर्तैः सहितो राजा समग्रबलवाहनः ।प्रागेव हि सुसंवीतो मत्स्यस्य विषयं प्रति ॥२३॥
transliteration: trigartaiḥ sahito rājā samagrabalavāhanaḥ ,prāgeva hi susaṁvīto matsyasya viṣayaṁ prati.
english: The king (Suśarmā), accompanied by the Trigartas, with his entire army and vehicles, and indeed well-prepared, should proceed ahead towards the Matsya territory.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 23. The king (Suśarmā), accompanied by the Trigartas, with his entire army and vehicles, and indeed well-prepared, should proceed ahead towards the Matsya territory.

sanskrit: जघन्यतो वयं तत्र यास्यामो दिवसान्तरम् ।विषयं मत्स्यराजस्य सुसमृद्धं सुसंहताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: jaghanyato vayaṁ tatra yāsyāmo divasāntaram ,viṣayaṁ matsyarājasya susamṛddhaṁ susaṁhatāḥ.
english: We, well-united, will go there later, after a day, to the very prosperous territory of the Matsya king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 24. We, well-united, will go there later, after a day, to the very prosperous territory of the Matsya king.

sanskrit: ते यात्वा सहसा तत्र विराटनगरं प्रति ।क्षिप्रं गोपान्समासाद्य गृह्णन्तु विपुलं धनम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: te yātvā sahasā tatra virāṭanagaraṁ prati ,kṣipraṁ gopānsamāsādya gṛhṇantu vipulaṁ dhanam.
english: Having quickly gone there to the city of Virāṭa, they should swiftly approach the cowherds and seize abundant wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 25. Having quickly gone there to the city of Virāṭa, they should swiftly approach the cowherds and seize abundant wealth.

sanskrit: गवां शतसहस्राणि श्रीमन्ति गुणवन्ति च ।वयमपि निगृह्णीमो द्विधा कृत्वा वरूथिनीम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: gavāṁ śatasahasrāṇi śrīmanti guṇavanti ca ,vayamapi nigṛhṇīmo dvidhā kṛtvā varūthinīm.
english: We too will seize hundreds of thousands of splendid and excellent cows, having divided our army into two parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 26. We too will seize hundreds of thousands of splendid and excellent cows, having divided our army into two parts.

sanskrit: स स्म गत्वा यथोद्दिष्टां दिशं वह्नेर्महीपतिः ।आदत्त गाः सुशर्माथ घर्मपक्षस्य सप्तमीम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa sma gatvā yathoddiṣṭāṁ diśaṁ vahnermahīpatiḥ ,ādatta gāḥ suśarmātha gharmapakṣasya saptamīm.
english: Then King Suśarmā, having gone to the previously indicated direction of Agni (South-East), seized the cows on the seventh day of the hot season.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 27. Then King Suśarmā, having gone to the previously indicated direction of Agni (South-East), seized the cows on the seventh day of the hot season.

sanskrit: अपरं दिवसं सर्वे राजन्संभूय कौरवाः ।अष्टम्यां तान्यगृह्णन्त गोकुलानि सहस्रशः ॥२८॥
transliteration: aparaṁ divasaṁ sarve rājansaṁbhūya kauravāḥ ,aṣṭamyāṁ tānyagṛhṇanta gokulāni sahasraśaḥ.
english: O King, on the following day, all the Kauravas, having assembled, seized those thousands of herds of cattle on the eighth day.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 29 Verse 28. O King, on the following day, all the Kauravas, having assembled, seized those thousands of herds of cattle on the eighth day.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्तेषां महाराज तत्रैवामिततेजसाम् ।छद्मलिङ्गप्रविष्टानां पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatasteṣāṁ mahārāja tatraivāmitatejasām ,chadmaliṅgapraviṣṭānāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Then, O great king, concerning those great-souled Pāṇḍavas, who possessed immeasurable splendor and had entered there in disguised forms."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "Then, O great king, concerning those great-souled Pāṇḍavas, who possessed immeasurable splendor and had entered there in disguised forms."

sanskrit: व्यतीतः समयः सम्यग्वसतां वै पुरोत्तमे ।कुर्वतां तस्य कर्माणि विराटस्य महीपतेः ॥२॥
transliteration: vyatītaḥ samayaḥ samyagvasatāṁ vai purottame ,kurvatāṁ tasya karmāṇi virāṭasya mahīpateḥ.
english: Indeed, their time had properly passed while they were dwelling in the excellent city and performing the tasks of that King Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 2. Indeed, their time had properly passed while they were dwelling in the excellent city and performing the tasks of that King Virāṭa.

sanskrit: ततस्त्रयोदशस्यान्ते तस्य वर्षस्य भारत ।सुशर्मणा गृहीतं तु गोधनं तरसा बहु ॥३॥
transliteration: tatastrayodaśasyānte tasya varṣasya bhārata ,suśarmaṇā gṛhītaṁ tu godhanaṁ tarasā bahu.
english: Then, O Bhārata, at the conclusion of that thirteenth year, a large amount of cattle wealth was indeed seized by force by Suśarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 3. Then, O Bhārata, at the conclusion of that thirteenth year, a large amount of cattle wealth was indeed seized by force by Suśarman.

sanskrit: ततो जवेन महता गोपाः पुरमथाव्रजत् ।अपश्यन्मत्स्यराजं च रथात्प्रस्कन्द्य कुण्डली ॥४॥
transliteration: tato javena mahatā gopāḥ puramathāvrajat ,apaśyanmatsyarājaṁ ca rathātpraskandya kuṇḍalī.
english: Then, with great speed, the cowherd (Bhallava) approached the city. Having leaped down from the chariot, wearing earrings, he saw the king of Matsya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 4. Then, with great speed, the cowherd (Bhallava) approached the city. Having leaped down from the chariot, wearing earrings, he saw the king of Matsya.

sanskrit: शूरैः परिवृतं योधैः कुण्डलाङ्गदधारिभिः ।सद्भिश्च मन्त्रिभिः सार्धं पाण्डवैश्च नरर्षभैः ॥५॥
transliteration: śūraiḥ parivṛtaṁ yodhaiḥ kuṇḍalāṅgadadhāribhiḥ ,sadbhiśca mantribhiḥ sārdhaṁ pāṇḍavaiśca nararṣabhaiḥ.
english: He (the Matsya king) was surrounded by brave warriors wearing earrings and armlets, and by good ministers, as well as by the Pāṇḍavas, who were foremost among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 5. He (the Matsya king) was surrounded by brave warriors wearing earrings and armlets, and by good ministers, as well as by the Pāṇḍavas, who were foremost among men.

sanskrit: तं सभायां महाराजमासीनं राष्ट्रवर्धनम् ।सोऽब्रवीदुपसंगम्य विराटं प्रणतस्तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: taṁ sabhāyāṁ mahārājamāsīnaṁ rāṣṭravardhanam ,so'bravīdupasaṁgamya virāṭaṁ praṇatastadā.
english: Then, having approached King Virata, the great king who was seated in the assembly and enhanced the prosperity of his kingdom, the cowherd bowed down and spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 6. Then, having approached King Virata, the great king who was seated in the assembly and enhanced the prosperity of his kingdom, the cowherd bowed down and spoke to him.

sanskrit: अस्मान्युधि विनिर्जित्य परिभूय सबान्धवान् ।गवां शतसहस्राणि त्रिगर्ताः कालयन्ति ते ।तान्परीप्स मनुष्येन्द्र मा नेशुः पशवस्तव ॥७॥
transliteration: asmānyudhi vinirjitya paribhūya sabāndhavān ,gavāṁ śatasahasrāṇi trigartāḥ kālayanti te ,tānparīpsa manuṣyendra mā neśuḥ paśavastava.
english: The Trigartas, having completely conquered and humiliated us and our kinsmen in battle, are driving away a hundred thousand of your cows. O king of men, protect them, lest your cattle perish.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 7. The Trigartas, having completely conquered and humiliated us and our kinsmen in battle, are driving away a hundred thousand of your cows. O king of men, protect them, lest your cattle perish.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा नृपतिः सेनां मत्स्यानां समयोजयत् ।रथनागाश्वकलिलां पत्तिध्वजसमाकुलाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā nṛpatiḥ senāṁ matsyānāṁ samayojayat ,rathanāgāśvakalilāṁ pattidhvajasamākulām.
english: Having heard that, the king mobilized the army of the Matsyas, which was teeming with chariots, elephants, and horses, and crowded with infantry and banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 8. Having heard that, the king mobilized the army of the Matsyas, which was teeming with chariots, elephants, and horses, and crowded with infantry and banners.

sanskrit: राजानो राजपुत्राश्च तनुत्राण्यत्र भेजिरे ।भानुमन्ति विचित्राणि सूपसेव्यानि भागशः ॥९॥
transliteration: rājāno rājaputrāśca tanutrāṇyatra bhejire ,bhānumanti vicitrāṇi sūpasevyāni bhāgaśaḥ.
english: Here, kings and princes donned their armors, which were radiant, varied, and well-fitting, each according to their share.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 9. Here, kings and princes donned their armors, which were radiant, varied, and well-fitting, each according to their share.

sanskrit: सवज्रायसगर्भं तु कवचं तप्तकाञ्चनम् ।विराटस्य प्रियो भ्राता शतानीकोऽभ्यहारयत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: savajrāyasagarbhaṁ tu kavacaṁ taptakāñcanam ,virāṭasya priyo bhrātā śatānīko'bhyahārayat.
english: But Virāṭa's dear brother, Śatānīka, brought forth the armor of gleaming gold, which was embedded with adamantine iron (steel).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 10. But Virāṭa's dear brother, Śatānīka, brought forth the armor of gleaming gold, which was embedded with adamantine iron (steel).

sanskrit: सर्वपारसवं वर्म कल्याणपटलं दृढम् ।शतानीकादवरजो मदिराश्वोऽभ्यहारयत् ॥११॥
transliteration: sarvapārasavaṁ varma kalyāṇapaṭalaṁ dṛḍham ,śatānīkādavarajo madirāśvo'bhyahārayat.
english: Madirāśva, who was younger than Śatānīka, brought forth the strong armor, entirely made of excellent steel, with auspicious layers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 11. Madirāśva, who was younger than Śatānīka, brought forth the strong armor, entirely made of excellent steel, with auspicious layers.

sanskrit: शतसूर्यं शतावर्तं शतबिन्दु शताक्षिमत् ।अभेद्यकल्पं मत्स्यानां राजा कवचमाहरत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: śatasūryaṁ śatāvartaṁ śatabindu śatākṣimat ,abhedyakalpaṁ matsyānāṁ rājā kavacamāharat.
english: The king acquired an armor that shone like a hundred suns, featured a hundred intricate patterns, a hundred dots, and a hundred eye-like embellishments. It was almost impenetrable for the Matsyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 12. The king acquired an armor that shone like a hundred suns, featured a hundred intricate patterns, a hundred dots, and a hundred eye-like embellishments. It was almost impenetrable for the Matsyas.

sanskrit: उत्सेधे यस्य पद्मानि शतं सौगन्धिकानि च ।सुवर्णपृष्ठं सूर्याभं सूर्यदत्तोऽभ्यहारयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: utsedhe yasya padmāni śataṁ saugandhikāni ca ,suvarṇapṛṣṭhaṁ sūryābhaṁ sūryadatto'bhyahārayat.
english: Upon its raised surface were a hundred fragrant lotuses, and it had a golden back, shining like the sun. Sūryadatta wore it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 13. Upon its raised surface were a hundred fragrant lotuses, and it had a golden back, shining like the sun. Sūryadatta wore it.

sanskrit: दृढमायसगर्भं तु श्वेतं वर्म शताक्षिमत् ।विराटस्य सुतो ज्येष्ठो वीरः शङ्खोऽभ्यहारयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dṛḍhamāyasagarbhaṁ tu śvetaṁ varma śatākṣimat ,virāṭasya suto jyeṣṭho vīraḥ śaṅkho'bhyahārayat.
english: Virāṭa's eldest heroic son, Śaṅkha, wore this strong, iron-cored, yet white armor, which was adorned with a hundred eye-like designs.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 14. Virāṭa's eldest heroic son, Śaṅkha, wore this strong, iron-cored, yet white armor, which was adorned with a hundred eye-like designs.

sanskrit: शतशश्च तनुत्राणि यथास्वानि महारथाः ।योत्स्यमानाभ्यनह्यन्त देवरूपाः प्रहारिणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: śataśaśca tanutrāṇi yathāsvāni mahārathāḥ ,yotsyamānābhyanahyanta devarūpāḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: And then, hundreds of great chariot-warriors, who were god-like and skilled in striking, fastened on their own armors, preparing to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 15. And then, hundreds of great chariot-warriors, who were god-like and skilled in striking, fastened on their own armors, preparing to fight.

sanskrit: सूपस्करेषु शुभ्रेषु महत्सु च महारथाः ।पृथक्काञ्चनसंनाहान्रथेष्वश्वानयोजयन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: sūpaskareṣu śubhreṣu mahatsu ca mahārathāḥ ,pṛthakkāñcanasaṁnāhānratheṣvaśvānayojayan.
english: The great charioteers (mahārathāḥ) harnessed horses, each adorned with golden armor, to their splendid, grand, and well-equipped chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 16. The great charioteers (mahārathāḥ) harnessed horses, each adorned with golden armor, to their splendid, grand, and well-equipped chariots.

sanskrit: सूर्यचन्द्रप्रतीकाशो रथे दिव्ये हिरण्मयः ।महानुभावो मत्स्यस्य ध्वज उच्छिश्रिये तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: sūryacandrapratīkāśo rathe divye hiraṇmayaḥ ,mahānubhāvo matsyasya dhvaja ucchiśriye tadā.
english: Then, on the divine chariot, the flag of Matsya, golden and radiant like the sun and moon, and possessing great splendor, was hoisted.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 17. Then, on the divine chariot, the flag of Matsya, golden and radiant like the sun and moon, and possessing great splendor, was hoisted.

sanskrit: अथान्यान्विविधाकारान्ध्वजान्हेमविभूषितान् ।यथास्वं क्षत्रियाः शूरा रथेषु समयोजयन् ॥१८॥
transliteration: athānyānvividhākārāndhvajānhemavibhūṣitān ,yathāsvaṁ kṣatriyāḥ śūrā ratheṣu samayojayan.
english: Then, the brave warriors (kṣatriyāḥ) fixed other flags of various designs, adorned with gold, onto their chariots, each according to their own emblems.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 18. Then, the brave warriors (kṣatriyāḥ) fixed other flags of various designs, adorned with gold, onto their chariots, each according to their own emblems.

sanskrit: अथ मत्स्योऽब्रवीद्राजा शतानीकं जघन्यजम् ।कङ्कबल्लवगोपाला दामग्रन्थिश्च वीर्यवान् ।युध्येयुरिति मे बुद्धिर्वर्तते नात्र संशयः ॥१९॥
transliteration: atha matsyo'bravīdrājā śatānīkaṁ jaghanyajam ,kaṅkaballavagopālā dāmagranthiśca vīryavān ,yudhyeyuriti me buddhirvartate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: Then King Matsya spoke to Śatānīka, his youngest brother: "My belief is that Kaṅka, Vallava, Gopāla, and the powerful Dāmagranthi should fight; there is no doubt about this."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 19. Then King Matsya spoke to Śatānīka, his youngest brother: "My belief is that Kaṅka, Vallava, Gopāla, and the powerful Dāmagranthi should fight; there is no doubt about this."

sanskrit: एतेषामपि दीयन्तां रथा ध्वजपताकिनः ।कवचानि विचित्राणि दृढानि च मृदूनि च ।प्रतिमुञ्चन्तु गात्रेषु दीयन्तामायुधानि च ॥२०॥
transliteration: eteṣāmapi dīyantāṁ rathā dhvajapatākinaḥ ,kavacāni vicitrāṇi dṛḍhāni ca mṛdūni ca ,pratimuñcantu gātreṣu dīyantāmāyudhāni ca.
english: Let chariots, complete with flags and banners, be provided for these men. Also, let them be given diverse armors, both strong and soft. They should put these on their limbs, and weapons should also be supplied.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 20. Let chariots, complete with flags and banners, be provided for these men. Also, let them be given diverse armors, both strong and soft. They should put these on their limbs, and weapons should also be supplied.

sanskrit: वीराङ्गरूपाः पुरुषा नागराजकरोपमाः ।नेमे जातु न युध्येरन्निति मे धीयते मतिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: vīrāṅgarūpāḥ puruṣā nāgarājakaropamāḥ ,neme jātu na yudhyeranniti me dhīyate matiḥ.
english: These men possess heroic forms, resembling the mighty arms of a serpent king. My conviction is that such individuals would certainly never refuse to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 21. These men possess heroic forms, resembling the mighty arms of a serpent king. My conviction is that such individuals would certainly never refuse to fight.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा तु नृपतेर्वाक्यं त्वरितमानसः ।शतानीकस्तु पार्थेभ्यो रथान्राजन्समादिशत् ।सहदेवाय राज्ञे च भीमाय नकुलाय च ॥२२॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā tu nṛpatervākyaṁ tvaritamānasaḥ ,śatānīkastu pārthebhyo rathānrājansamādiśat ,sahadevāya rājñe ca bhīmāya nakulāya ca.
english: Upon hearing this statement from the king, Śatānīka, with an eager mind, O King, immediately assigned chariots to the Pārthas: specifically to Sahadeva, to King Yudhiṣṭhira, to Bhīma, and to Nakula.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 22. Upon hearing this statement from the king, Śatānīka, with an eager mind, O King, immediately assigned chariots to the Pārthas: specifically to Sahadeva, to King Yudhiṣṭhira, to Bhīma, and to Nakula.

sanskrit: तान्प्रहृष्टास्ततः सूता राजभक्तिपुरस्कृताः ।निर्दिष्टान्नरदेवेन रथाञ्शीघ्रमयोजयन् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tānprahṛṣṭāstataḥ sūtā rājabhaktipuraskṛtāḥ ,nirdiṣṭānnaradevena rathāñśīghramayojayan.
english: Then, the charioteers, who were greatly pleased and honored by their devotion (bhakti) to the king, swiftly prepared the chariots as instructed by the lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 23. Then, the charioteers, who were greatly pleased and honored by their devotion (bhakti) to the king, swiftly prepared the chariots as instructed by the lord of men.

sanskrit: कवचानि विचित्राणि दृढानि च मृदूनि च ।विराटः प्रादिशद्यानि तेषामक्लिष्टकर्मणाम् ।तान्यामुच्य शरीरेषु दंशितास्ते परंतपाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: kavacāni vicitrāṇi dṛḍhāni ca mṛdūni ca ,virāṭaḥ prādiśadyāni teṣāmakliṣṭakarmaṇām ,tānyāmucya śarīreṣu daṁśitāste paraṁtapāḥ.
english: King Virāṭa had provided various armors, both strong and soft, to those whose actions (karma) were unwearied. Having put these on their bodies, those tormentors of enemies were fully armored.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 24. King Virāṭa had provided various armors, both strong and soft, to those whose actions (karma) were unwearied. Having put these on their bodies, those tormentors of enemies were fully armored.

sanskrit: तरस्विनश्छन्नरूपाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ।विराटमन्वयुः पश्चात्सहिताः कुरुपुंगवाः ।चत्वारो भ्रातरः शूराः पाण्डवाः सत्यविक्रमाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tarasvinaśchannarūpāḥ sarve yuddhaviśāradāḥ ,virāṭamanvayuḥ paścātsahitāḥ kurupuṁgavāḥ ,catvāro bhrātaraḥ śūrāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ satyavikramāḥ.
english: All of them - powerful, disguised in form, and skilled in warfare - followed King Virāṭa afterwards, accompanied by the best among the Kurus. These were the four valiant Pāṇḍava brothers, whose valor (vikrama) was true.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 25. All of them - powerful, disguised in form, and skilled in warfare - followed King Virāṭa afterwards, accompanied by the best among the Kurus. These were the four valiant Pāṇḍava brothers, whose valor (vikrama) was true.

sanskrit: भीमाश्च मत्तमातङ्गाः प्रभिन्नकरटामुखाः ।क्षरन्त इव जीमूताः सुदन्ताः षष्टिहायनाः ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhīmāśca mattamātaṅgāḥ prabhinnakaraṭāmukhāḥ ,kṣaranta iva jīmūtāḥ sudantāḥ ṣaṣṭihāyanāḥ.
english: They (the Pāṇḍavas) were dreadful, like rutting elephants with burst temples, raining (fluid) like clouds, well-tusked, and sixty years old.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 26. They (the Pāṇḍavas) were dreadful, like rutting elephants with burst temples, raining (fluid) like clouds, well-tusked, and sixty years old.

sanskrit: स्वारूढा युद्धकुशलैः शिक्षितैर्हस्तिसादिभिः ।राजानमन्वयुः पश्चाच्चलन्त इव पर्वताः ॥२७॥
transliteration: svārūḍhā yuddhakuśalaiḥ śikṣitairhastisādibhiḥ ,rājānamanvayuḥ paścāccalanta iva parvatāḥ.
english: Like (those majestic elephants) that are well-mounted by battle-skilled and trained elephant-riders, they (the Pāṇḍavas) followed the king afterwards, moving like mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 27. Like (those majestic elephants) that are well-mounted by battle-skilled and trained elephant-riders, they (the Pāṇḍavas) followed the king afterwards, moving like mountains.

sanskrit: विशारदानां वश्यानां हृष्टानां चानुयायिनाम् ।अष्टौ रथसहस्राणि दश नागशतानि च ।षष्टिश्चाश्वसहस्राणि मत्स्यानामभिनिर्ययुः ॥२८॥
transliteration: viśāradānāṁ vaśyānāṁ hṛṣṭānāṁ cānuyāyinām ,aṣṭau rathasahasrāṇi daśa nāgaśatāni ca ,ṣaṣṭiścāśvasahasrāṇi matsyānāmabhiniryayuḥ.
english: Eight thousand chariots, one thousand elephants, and sixty thousand horses, belonging to the skilled, obedient, and joyful followers of the Matsyas, sallied forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 28. Eight thousand chariots, one thousand elephants, and sixty thousand horses, belonging to the skilled, obedient, and joyful followers of the Matsyas, sallied forth.

sanskrit: तदनीकं विराटस्य शुशुभे भरतर्षभ ।संप्रयातं महाराज निनीषन्तं गवां पदम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tadanīkaṁ virāṭasya śuśubhe bharatarṣabha ,saṁprayātaṁ mahārāja ninīṣantaṁ gavāṁ padam.
english: O best of Bharatas, O great king, that army of Virāṭa, as it marched forth desiring to recover the cattle, appeared splendid.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 29. O best of Bharatas, O great king, that army of Virāṭa, as it marched forth desiring to recover the cattle, appeared splendid.

sanskrit: तद्बलाग्र्यं विराटस्य संप्रस्थितमशोभत ।दृढायुधजनाकीर्णं गजाश्वरथसंकुलम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tadbalāgryaṁ virāṭasya saṁprasthitamaśobhata ,dṛḍhāyudhajanākīrṇaṁ gajāśvarathasaṁkulam.
english: That excellent army of Virāṭa, as it set forth, appeared splendid, filled with men holding strong weapons, and crowded with elephants, horses, and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 30 Verse 30. That excellent army of Virāṭa, as it set forth, appeared splendid, filled with men holding strong weapons, and crowded with elephants, horses, and chariots.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।निर्याय नगराच्छूरा व्यूढानीकाः प्रहारिणः ।त्रिगर्तानस्पृशन्मत्स्याः सूर्ये परिणते सति ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,niryāya nagarācchūrā vyūḍhānīkāḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,trigartānaspṛśanmatsyāḥ sūrye pariṇate sati.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having sallied forth from the city, the brave Matsyas, with their armies arrayed and acting as attackers, reached the Trigartas as the sun was setting.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having sallied forth from the city, the brave Matsyas, with their armies arrayed and acting as attackers, reached the Trigartas as the sun was setting.

sanskrit: ते त्रिगर्ताश्च मत्स्याश्च संरब्धा युद्धदुर्मदाः ।अन्योन्यमभिगर्जन्तो गोषु गृद्धा महाबलाः ॥२॥
transliteration: te trigartāśca matsyāśca saṁrabdhā yuddhadurmadāḥ ,anyonyamabhigarjanto goṣu gṛddhā mahābalāḥ.
english: Those Trigartas and Matsyas, enraged and fierce in battle, were immensely powerful and roared at each other, eager for the cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 2. Those Trigartas and Matsyas, enraged and fierce in battle, were immensely powerful and roared at each other, eager for the cows.

sanskrit: भीमाश्च मत्तमातङ्गास्तोमराङ्कुशचोदिताः ।ग्रामणीयैः समारूढाः कुशलैर्हस्तिसादिभिः ॥३॥
transliteration: bhīmāśca mattamātaṅgāstomarāṅkuśacoditāḥ ,grāmaṇīyaiḥ samārūḍhāḥ kuśalairhastisādibhiḥ.
english: And formidable, frenzied elephants, spurred on by javelins and goads, were mounted by skillful elephant riders and chieftains.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 3. And formidable, frenzied elephants, spurred on by javelins and goads, were mounted by skillful elephant riders and chieftains.

sanskrit: तेषां समागमो घोरस्तुमुलो लोमहर्षणः ।देवासुरसमो राजन्नासीत्सूर्ये विलम्बति ॥४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ samāgamo ghorastumulo lomaharṣaṇaḥ ,devāsurasamo rājannāsītsūrye vilambati.
english: O King, their terrible, tumultuous, and hair-raising encounter, which resembled a battle between gods and demons, took place as the sun was setting.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 4. O King, their terrible, tumultuous, and hair-raising encounter, which resembled a battle between gods and demons, took place as the sun was setting.

sanskrit: उदतिष्ठद्रजो भौमं न प्रज्ञायत किंचन ।पक्षिणश्चापतन्भूमौ सैन्येन रजसावृताः ॥५॥
transliteration: udatiṣṭhadrajo bhaumaṁ na prajñāyata kiṁcana ,pakṣiṇaścāpatanbhūmau sainyena rajasāvṛtāḥ.
english: Ground-dust rose up, and nothing could be seen. Birds, enveloped by the dust raised by the army, fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 5. Ground-dust rose up, and nothing could be seen. Birds, enveloped by the dust raised by the army, fell to the ground.

sanskrit: इषुभिर्व्यतिसंयद्भिरादित्योऽन्तरधीयत ।खद्योतैरिव संयुक्तमन्तरिक्षं व्यराजत ॥६॥
transliteration: iṣubhirvyatisaṁyadbhirādityo'ntaradhīyata ,khadyotairiva saṁyuktamantarikṣaṁ vyarājata.
english: The sun disappeared due to the clashing arrows. The sky shone as if it were filled with fireflies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 6. The sun disappeared due to the clashing arrows. The sky shone as if it were filled with fireflies.
