sanskrit: रुक्मपृष्ठानि चापानि व्यतिषक्तानि धन्विनाम् ।पततां लोकवीराणां सव्यदक्षिणमस्यताम् ॥७॥
transliteration: rukmapṛṣṭhāni cāpāni vyatiṣaktāni dhanvinām ,patatāṁ lokavīrāṇāṁ savyadakṣiṇamasyatām.
english: The gold-backed bows of the archers became intermingled, as those warriors, the heroes of the world, were falling (in battle) and shooting (arrows) to the left and right.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 7. The gold-backed bows of the archers became intermingled, as those warriors, the heroes of the world, were falling (in battle) and shooting (arrows) to the left and right.

sanskrit: रथा रथैः समाजग्मुः पादातैश्च पदातयः ।सादिभिः सादिनश्चैव गजैश्चापि महागजाः ॥८॥
transliteration: rathā rathaiḥ samājagmuḥ pādātaiśca padātayaḥ ,sādibhiḥ sādinaścaiva gajaiścāpi mahāgajāḥ.
english: Chariots collided with chariots, foot soldiers with foot soldiers, horsemen with horsemen, and great elephants also with elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 8. Chariots collided with chariots, foot soldiers with foot soldiers, horsemen with horsemen, and great elephants also with elephants.

sanskrit: असिभिः पट्टिशैः प्रासैः शक्तिभिस्तोमरैरपि ।संरब्धाः समरे राजन्निजघ्नुरितरेतरम् ॥९॥
transliteration: asibhiḥ paṭṭiśaiḥ prāsaiḥ śaktibhistomarairapi ,saṁrabdhāḥ samare rājannijaghnuritaretaram.
english: O King, furious in battle, they struck each other down with swords, paṭṭiśas (blunt weapons/spears), spears, lances, and javelins.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 9. O King, furious in battle, they struck each other down with swords, paṭṭiśas (blunt weapons/spears), spears, lances, and javelins.

sanskrit: निघ्नन्तः समरेऽन्योन्यं शूराः परिघबाहवः ।न शेकुरभिसंरब्धाः शूरान्कर्तुं पराङ्मुखान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: nighnantaḥ samare'nyonyaṁ śūrāḥ parighabāhavaḥ ,na śekurabhisaṁrabdhāḥ śūrānkartuṁ parāṅmukhān.
english: In battle, the brave heroes, whose arms were like iron bars, struck each other; yet, even though they were greatly enraged, they could not make other heroes turn away and flee.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 10. In battle, the brave heroes, whose arms were like iron bars, struck each other; yet, even though they were greatly enraged, they could not make other heroes turn away and flee.

sanskrit: कॢप्तोत्तरोष्ठं सुनसं कॢप्तकेशमलंकृतम् ।अदृश्यत शिरश्छिन्नं रजोध्वस्तं सकुण्डलम् ॥११॥
transliteration: kḷptottaroṣṭhaṁ sunasaṁ kḷptakeśamalaṁkṛtam ,adṛśyata śiraśchinnaṁ rajodhvastaṁ sakuṇḍalam.
english: A severed head, with its upper lip well-formed, a fine nose, well-arranged hair, adorned, covered with dust, and still wearing its earrings, was seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 11. A severed head, with its upper lip well-formed, a fine nose, well-arranged hair, adorned, covered with dust, and still wearing its earrings, was seen.

sanskrit: अदृश्यंस्तत्र गात्राणि शरैश्छिन्नानि भागशः ।शालस्कन्धनिकाशानि क्षत्रियाणां महामृधे ॥१२॥
transliteration: adṛśyaṁstatra gātrāṇi śaraiśchinnāni bhāgaśaḥ ,śālaskandhanikāśāni kṣatriyāṇāṁ mahāmṛdhe.
english: There, in the great battle, the limbs of the warriors (kṣatriyas)  -  severed into pieces by arrows and resembling the trunks of śāla trees  -  were seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 12. There, in the great battle, the limbs of the warriors (kṣatriyas)  -  severed into pieces by arrows and resembling the trunks of śāla trees  -  were seen.

sanskrit: नागभोगनिकाशैश्च बाहुभिश्चन्दनोक्षितैः ।आकीर्णा वसुधा तत्र शिरोभिश्च सकुण्डलैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nāgabhoganikāśaiśca bāhubhiścandanokṣitaiḥ ,ākīrṇā vasudhā tatra śirobhiśca sakuṇḍalaiḥ.
english: There, the earth was strewn with arms, resembling the hoods of serpents (nāgas) and anointed with sandalwood paste, as well as with heads still adorned with earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 13. There, the earth was strewn with arms, resembling the hoods of serpents (nāgas) and anointed with sandalwood paste, as well as with heads still adorned with earrings.

sanskrit: उपशाम्यद्रजो भौमं रुधिरेण प्रसर्पता ।कश्मलं प्राविशद्घोरं निर्मर्यादमवर्तत ॥१४॥
transliteration: upaśāmyadrajo bhaumaṁ rudhireṇa prasarpatā ,kaśmalaṁ prāviśadghoraṁ nirmaryādamavartata.
english: As the earthly dust settled, subdued by the spreading blood, a terrible confusion (kaśmala) enveloped everything, and lawlessness prevailed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 14. As the earthly dust settled, subdued by the spreading blood, a terrible confusion (kaśmala) enveloped everything, and lawlessness prevailed.

sanskrit: शतानीकः शतं हत्वा विशालाक्षश्चतुःशतम् ।प्रविष्टौ महतीं सेनां त्रिगर्तानां महारथौ ।आर्च्छेतां बहुसंरब्धौ केशाकेशि नखानखि ॥१५॥
transliteration: śatānīkaḥ śataṁ hatvā viśālākṣaścatuḥśatam ,praviṣṭau mahatīṁ senāṁ trigartānāṁ mahārathau ,ārcchetāṁ bahusaṁrabdhau keśākeśi nakhānakhi.
english: Having slain a hundred (foes), Śatānīka, and four hundred, Viśālākṣa, these two great warriors (mahāratha) entered the vast army of the Trigartas. Greatly enraged, they fought in close combat, pulling hair (keśākeśi) and tearing with nails (nakhānakhi).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 15. Having slain a hundred (foes), Śatānīka, and four hundred, Viśālākṣa, these two great warriors (mahāratha) entered the vast army of the Trigartas. Greatly enraged, they fought in close combat, pulling hair (keśākeśi) and tearing with nails (nakhānakhi).

sanskrit: लक्षयित्वा त्रिगर्तानां तौ प्रविष्टौ रथव्रजम् ।जग्मतुः सूर्यदत्तश्च मदिराश्वश्च पृष्ठतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: lakṣayitvā trigartānāṁ tau praviṣṭau rathavrajam ,jagmatuḥ sūryadattaśca madirāśvaśca pṛṣṭhataḥ.
english: Having observed those two (Śatānīka and Viśālākṣa) who had entered the multitude of Trigarta chariots, Sūryadatta and Madirāśva followed from behind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 16. Having observed those two (Śatānīka and Viśālākṣa) who had entered the multitude of Trigarta chariots, Sūryadatta and Madirāśva followed from behind.

sanskrit: विराटस्तत्र संग्रामे हत्वा पञ्चशतान्रथान् ।हयानां च शतान्यत्र हत्वा पञ्च महारथान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: virāṭastatra saṁgrāme hatvā pañcaśatānrathān ,hayānāṁ ca śatānyatra hatvā pañca mahārathān.
english: In that battle (saṃgrāma), Virāṭa, having killed five hundred charioteers, and here also having killed hundreds of horses and five great warriors (mahāratha).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 17. In that battle (saṃgrāma), Virāṭa, having killed five hundred charioteers, and here also having killed hundreds of horses and five great warriors (mahāratha).

sanskrit: चरन्स विविधान्मार्गान्रथेषु रथयूथपः ।त्रिगर्तानां सुशर्माणमार्च्छद्रुक्मरथं रणे ॥१८॥
transliteration: caransa vividhānmārgānratheṣu rathayūthapaḥ ,trigartānāṁ suśarmāṇamārcchadrukmarathaṁ raṇe.
english: That leader of chariot divisions, traversing various paths on his chariots, attacked Suśarman of the Trigartas, who rode a golden chariot, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 18. That leader of chariot divisions, traversing various paths on his chariots, attacked Suśarman of the Trigartas, who rode a golden chariot, in battle.

sanskrit: तौ व्यावहरतां तत्र महात्मानौ महाबलौ ।अन्योन्यमभिगर्जन्तौ गोष्ठे गोवृषभाविव ॥१९॥
transliteration: tau vyāvaharatāṁ tatra mahātmānau mahābalau ,anyonyamabhigarjantau goṣṭhe govṛṣabhāviva.
english: There, those two great-souled and mighty individuals fought, roaring at each other like two bulls in a cow-pen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 19. There, those two great-souled and mighty individuals fought, roaring at each other like two bulls in a cow-pen.

sanskrit: ततो रथाभ्यां रथिनौ व्यतियाय समन्ततः ।शरान्व्यसृजतां शीघ्रं तोयधारा घनाविव ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato rathābhyāṁ rathinau vyatiyāya samantataḥ ,śarānvyasṛjatāṁ śīghraṁ toyadhārā ghanāviva.
english: Then, those two charioteers passed by each other in their chariots, moving all around. They quickly discharged arrows, just as clouds release torrents of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 20. Then, those two charioteers passed by each other in their chariots, moving all around. They quickly discharged arrows, just as clouds release torrents of water.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यं चातिसंरब्धौ विचेरतुरमर्षणौ ।कृतास्त्रौ निशितैर्बाणैरसिशक्तिगदाभृतौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: anyonyaṁ cātisaṁrabdhau viceraturamarṣaṇau ,kṛtāstrau niśitairbāṇairasiśaktigadābhṛtau.
english: And those two, exceedingly enraged and intolerant, adept with weapons, moved about engaging each other with sharpened arrows. They were armed with swords, spears, and maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 21. And those two, exceedingly enraged and intolerant, adept with weapons, moved about engaging each other with sharpened arrows. They were armed with swords, spears, and maces.

sanskrit: ततो राजा सुशर्माणं विव्याध दशभिः शरैः ।पञ्चभिः पञ्चभिश्चास्य विव्याध चतुरो हयान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato rājā suśarmāṇaṁ vivyādha daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,pañcabhiḥ pañcabhiścāsya vivyādha caturo hayān.
english: Then the king struck Suśarman with ten arrows. And with five and five (arrows), he pierced his four horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 22. Then the king struck Suśarman with ten arrows. And with five and five (arrows), he pierced his four horses.

sanskrit: तथैव मत्स्यराजानं सुशर्मा युद्धदुर्मदः ।पञ्चाशता शितैर्बाणैर्विव्याध परमास्त्रवित् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathaiva matsyarājānaṁ suśarmā yuddhadurmadaḥ ,pañcāśatā śitairbāṇairvivyādha paramāstravit.
english: Similarly, Suśarman, who was intoxicated with battle and skilled in supreme weapons, pierced King Matsya with fifty sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 23. Similarly, Suśarman, who was intoxicated with battle and skilled in supreme weapons, pierced King Matsya with fifty sharp arrows.

sanskrit: ततः सैन्यं समावृत्य मत्स्यराजसुशर्मणोः ।नाभ्यजानंस्तदान्योन्यं प्रदोषे रजसावृते ॥२४॥
transliteration: tataḥ sainyaṁ samāvṛtya matsyarājasuśarmaṇoḥ ,nābhyajānaṁstadānyonyaṁ pradoṣe rajasāvṛte.
english: Then, with the armies of King Matsya and Suśarman surrounding them, they could not recognize each other in the twilight covered by dust.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 31 Verse 24. Then, with the armies of King Matsya and Suśarman surrounding them, they could not recognize each other in the twilight covered by dust.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तमसाभिप्लुते लोके रजसा चैव भारत ।व्यतिष्ठन्वै मुहूर्तं तु व्यूढानीकाः प्रहारिणः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tamasābhiplute loke rajasā caiva bhārata ,vyatiṣṭhanvai muhūrtaṁ tu vyūḍhānīkāḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O Bhārata, when the world was enveloped by darkness and also by dust, the warriors with arranged battle formations paused for a moment."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: "O Bhārata, when the world was enveloped by darkness and also by dust, the warriors with arranged battle formations paused for a moment."

sanskrit: ततोऽन्धकारं प्रणुदन्नुदतिष्ठत चन्द्रमाः ।कुर्वाणो विमलां रात्रिं नन्दयन्क्षत्रियान्युधि ॥२॥
transliteration: tato'ndhakāraṁ praṇudannudatiṣṭhata candramāḥ ,kurvāṇo vimalāṁ rātriṁ nandayankṣatriyānyudhi.
english: Then the moon rose, dispelling the darkness and making the night bright, thus delighting the warriors in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 2. Then the moon rose, dispelling the darkness and making the night bright, thus delighting the warriors in battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रकाशमासाद्य पुनर्युद्धमवर्तत ।घोररूपं ततस्ते स्म नावेक्षन्त परस्परम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ prakāśamāsādya punaryuddhamavartata ,ghorarūpaṁ tataste sma nāvekṣanta parasparam.
english: Then, with the return of light, the battle began again. Such was its terrible nature that they did not look at each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 3. Then, with the return of light, the battle began again. Such was its terrible nature that they did not look at each other.

sanskrit: ततः सुशर्मा त्रैगर्तः सह भ्रात्रा यवीयसा ।अभ्यद्रवन्मत्स्यराजं रथव्रातेन सर्वशः ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ suśarmā traigartaḥ saha bhrātrā yavīyasā ,abhyadravanmatsyarājaṁ rathavrātena sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then Suśarman, the Trigarta king, along with his younger brother, attacked the Matsya king with a multitude of chariots from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 4. Then Suśarman, the Trigarta king, along with his younger brother, attacked the Matsya king with a multitude of chariots from all sides.

sanskrit: ततो रथाभ्यां प्रस्कन्द्य भ्रातरौ क्षत्रियर्षभौ ।गदापाणी सुसंरब्धौ समभ्यद्रवतां हयान् ॥५॥
transliteration: tato rathābhyāṁ praskandya bhrātarau kṣatriyarṣabhau ,gadāpāṇī susaṁrabdhau samabhyadravatāṁ hayān.
english: Then the two brothers, those excellent Kṣatriyas, highly enraged and holding maces in their hands, jumped from their two chariots and charged towards the horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 5. Then the two brothers, those excellent Kṣatriyas, highly enraged and holding maces in their hands, jumped from their two chariots and charged towards the horses.

sanskrit: तथैव तेषां तु बलानि तानि क्रुद्धान्यथान्योन्यमभिद्रवन्ति ।गदासिखड्गैश्च परश्वधैश्च प्रासैश्च तीक्ष्णाग्रसुपीतधारैः ॥६॥
transliteration: tathaiva teṣāṁ tu balāni tāni; kruddhānyathānyonyamabhidravanti ,gadāsikhaḍgaiśca paraśvadhaiśca; prāsaiśca tīkṣṇāgrasupītadhāraiḥ.
english: In the same manner, those enraged forces then charged at each other with maces, swords, battle-axes, and spears that had sharp points and well-sharpened edges.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 6. In the same manner, those enraged forces then charged at each other with maces, swords, battle-axes, and spears that had sharp points and well-sharpened edges.

sanskrit: बलं तु मत्स्यस्य बलेन राजा सर्वं त्रिगर्ताधिपतिः सुशर्मा ।प्रमथ्य जित्वा च प्रसह्य मत्स्यं विराटमोजस्विनमभ्यधावत् ॥७॥
transliteration: balaṁ tu matsyasya balena rājā; sarvaṁ trigartādhipatiḥ suśarmā ,pramathya jitvā ca prasahya matsyaṁ; virāṭamojasvinamabhyadhāvat.
english: But King Suśarman, the lord of Trigarta, with his army, having utterly crushed and conquered all of King Matsya's forces, and having forcibly overcome the mighty King Virāṭa (Matsya), then attacked him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 7. But King Suśarman, the lord of Trigarta, with his army, having utterly crushed and conquered all of King Matsya's forces, and having forcibly overcome the mighty King Virāṭa (Matsya), then attacked him.

sanskrit: तौ निहत्य पृथग्धुर्यावुभौ च पार्ष्णिसारथी ।विरथं मत्स्यराजानं जीवग्राहमगृह्णताम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tau nihatya pṛthagdhuryāvubhau ca pārṣṇisārathī ,virathaṁ matsyarājānaṁ jīvagrāhamagṛhṇatām.
english: Having separately struck down both the two draught animals and the two charioteers, they captured King Matsya alive, after rendering him chariot-less.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 8. Having separately struck down both the two draught animals and the two charioteers, they captured King Matsya alive, after rendering him chariot-less.

sanskrit: तमुन्मथ्य सुशर्मा तु रुदतीं वधुकामिव ।स्यन्दनं स्वं समारोप्य प्रययौ शीघ्रवाहनः ॥९॥
transliteration: tamunmathya suśarmā tu rudatīṁ vadhukāmiva ,syandanaṁ svaṁ samāropya prayayau śīghravāhanaḥ.
english: But Suśarman, having completely overpowered him (King Matsya) and treating him like a weeping bride, placed him onto his own chariot and then swiftly departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 9. But Suśarman, having completely overpowered him (King Matsya) and treating him like a weeping bride, placed him onto his own chariot and then swiftly departed.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्गृहीते विरथे विराटे बलवत्तरे ।प्राद्रवन्त भयान्मत्स्यास्त्रिगर्तैरर्दिता भृशम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasmingṛhīte virathe virāṭe balavattare ,prādravanta bhayānmatsyāstrigartairarditā bhṛśam.
english: When that powerful Virāṭa was captured and rendered chariotless, the Matsyas fled in great fear, severely afflicted by the Trigartas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 10. When that powerful Virāṭa was captured and rendered chariotless, the Matsyas fled in great fear, severely afflicted by the Trigartas.

sanskrit: तेषु संत्रास्यमानेषु कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।अभ्यभाषन्महाबाहुं भीमसेनमरिंदमम् ॥११॥
transliteration: teṣu saṁtrāsyamāneṣu kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,abhyabhāṣanmahābāhuṁ bhīmasenamariṁdamam.
english: While they were thus being terrified, Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, addressed the mighty-armed Bhimasena, the subduer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 11. While they were thus being terrified, Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, addressed the mighty-armed Bhimasena, the subduer of enemies.

sanskrit: मत्स्यराजः परामृष्टस्त्रिगर्तेन सुशर्मणा ।तं मोक्षय महाबाहो न गच्छेद्द्विषतां वशम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: matsyarājaḥ parāmṛṣṭastrigartena suśarmaṇā ,taṁ mokṣaya mahābāho na gaccheddviṣatāṁ vaśam.
english: The King of Matsya has been seized by Suśarman of Trigarta. O mighty-armed one, release him! He should not fall into the power of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 12. The King of Matsya has been seized by Suśarman of Trigarta. O mighty-armed one, release him! He should not fall into the power of enemies.

sanskrit: उषिताः स्मः सुखं सर्वे सर्वकामैः सुपूजिताः ।भीमसेन त्वया कार्या तस्य वासस्य निष्कृतिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: uṣitāḥ smaḥ sukhaṁ sarve sarvakāmaiḥ supūjitāḥ ,bhīmasena tvayā kāryā tasya vāsasya niṣkṛtiḥ.
english: We have all lived happily, honored with all our desires fulfilled. O Bhimasena, you must perform an act of gratitude (niṣkṛti) for that residence.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 13. We have all lived happily, honored with all our desires fulfilled. O Bhimasena, you must perform an act of gratitude (niṣkṛti) for that residence.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।अहमेनं परित्रास्ये शासनात्तव पार्थिव ।पश्य मे सुमहत्कर्म युध्यतः सह शत्रुभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,ahamenaṁ paritrāsye śāsanāttava pārthiva ,paśya me sumahatkarma yudhyataḥ saha śatrubhiḥ.
english: Bhīmasena said: "O king, I will protect him according to your command. Behold my mighty deed as I fight along with the enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 14. Bhīmasena said: "O king, I will protect him according to your command. Behold my mighty deed as I fight along with the enemies."

sanskrit: स्वबाहुबलमाश्रित्य तिष्ठ त्वं भ्रातृभिः सह ।एकान्तमाश्रितो राजन्पश्य मेऽद्य पराक्रमम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: svabāhubalamāśritya tiṣṭha tvaṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,ekāntamāśrito rājanpaśya me'dya parākramam.
english: "You (O king) should remain with your brothers, relying on the strength of your own arms. O king, stay in a secluded spot and today behold my prowess."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 15. "You (O king) should remain with your brothers, relying on the strength of your own arms. O king, stay in a secluded spot and today behold my prowess."

sanskrit: सुस्कन्धोऽयं महावृक्षो गदारूप इव स्थितः ।एनमेव समारुज्य द्रावयिष्यामि शात्रवान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: suskandho'yaṁ mahāvṛkṣo gadārūpa iva sthitaḥ ,enameva samārujya drāvayiṣyāmi śātravān.
english: "This large tree with a sturdy trunk stands like my mace. By breaking this very tree, I will rout the enemies."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 16. "This large tree with a sturdy trunk stands like my mace. By breaking this very tree, I will rout the enemies."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तं मत्तमिव मातङ्गं वीक्षमाणं वनस्पतिम् ।अब्रवीद्भ्रातरं वीरं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taṁ mattamiva mātaṅgaṁ vīkṣamāṇaṁ vanaspatim ,abravīdbhrātaraṁ vīraṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), spoke to his heroic brother, who was observing the tree like an intoxicated elephant."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 17. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), spoke to his heroic brother, who was observing the tree like an intoxicated elephant."

sanskrit: मा भीम साहसं कार्षीस्तिष्ठत्वेष वनस्पतिः ।मा त्वा वृक्षेण कर्माणि कुर्वाणमतिमानुषम् ।जनाः समवबुध्येरन्भीमोऽयमिति भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: mā bhīma sāhasaṁ kārṣīstiṣṭhatveṣa vanaspatiḥ ,mā tvā vṛkṣeṇa karmāṇi kurvāṇamatimānuṣam ,janāḥ samavabudhyeranbhīmo'yamiti bhārata.
english: O Bhima, do not undertake such a daring act. Let this tree remain where it is. O Bharata, people should not realize that you are performing superhuman (atimānuṣam) deeds with a tree, [leading them to think] 'This is Bhima!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 18. O Bhima, do not undertake such a daring act. Let this tree remain where it is. O Bharata, people should not realize that you are performing superhuman (atimānuṣam) deeds with a tree, [leading them to think] 'This is Bhima!'

sanskrit: अन्यदेवायुधं किंचित्प्रतिपद्यस्व मानुषम् ।चापं वा यदि वा शक्तिं निस्त्रिंशं वा परश्वधम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: anyadevāyudhaṁ kiṁcitpratipadyasva mānuṣam ,cāpaṁ vā yadi vā śaktiṁ nistriṁśaṁ vā paraśvadham.
english: Acquire some other human (mānuṣam) weapon, such as a bow, or a spear (śakti), or a sword, or an axe.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 19. Acquire some other human (mānuṣam) weapon, such as a bow, or a spear (śakti), or a sword, or an axe.

sanskrit: यदेव मानुषं भीम भवेदन्यैरलक्षितम् ।तदेवायुधमादाय मोक्षयाशु महीपतिम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yadeva mānuṣaṁ bhīma bhavedanyairalakṣitam ,tadevāyudhamādāya mokṣayāśu mahīpatim.
english: O Bhima, whatever human (mānuṣam) weapon would remain unseen by others, taking precisely that weapon, quickly liberate (mokṣa) the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 20. O Bhima, whatever human (mānuṣam) weapon would remain unseen by others, taking precisely that weapon, quickly liberate (mokṣa) the king.

sanskrit: यमौ च चक्ररक्षौ ते भवितारौ महाबलौ ।व्यूहतः समरे तात मत्स्यराजं परीप्सतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yamau ca cakrarakṣau te bhavitārau mahābalau ,vyūhataḥ samare tāta matsyarājaṁ parīpsataḥ.
english: And the two mighty (mahābalau) Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) will be your wheel-guards (cakrarakṣau). Dear one (tāta), they will be arraying formations in battle (samare), desirous of protecting the Matsya king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 21. And the two mighty (mahābalau) Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) will be your wheel-guards (cakrarakṣau). Dear one (tāta), they will be arraying formations in battle (samare), desirous of protecting the Matsya king.

sanskrit: ततः समस्तास्ते सर्वे तुरगानभ्यचोदयन् ।दिव्यमस्त्रं विकुर्वाणास्त्रिगर्तान्प्रत्यमर्षणाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tataḥ samastāste sarve turagānabhyacodayan ,divyamastraṁ vikurvāṇāstrigartānpratyamarṣaṇāḥ.
english: Then, all of them, enraged, spurred on their horses, wielding divine weapons against the Trigartas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 22. Then, all of them, enraged, spurred on their horses, wielding divine weapons against the Trigartas.

sanskrit: तान्निवृत्तरथान्दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवान्सा महाचमूः ।वैराटी परमक्रुद्धा युयुधे परमाद्भुतम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tānnivṛttarathāndṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavānsā mahācamūḥ ,vairāṭī paramakruddhā yuyudhe paramādbhutam.
english: Seeing those Pandavas whose chariots had retreated, that great army (mahācamūḥ) of Virata, exceedingly enraged, fought with astonishing prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 23. Seeing those Pandavas whose chariots had retreated, that great army (mahācamūḥ) of Virata, exceedingly enraged, fought with astonishing prowess.

sanskrit: सहस्रं न्यवधीत्तत्र कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमः सप्तशतान्योधान्परलोकमदर्शयत् ।नकुलश्चापि सप्तैव शतानि प्राहिणोच्छरैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: sahasraṁ nyavadhīttatra kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmaḥ saptaśatānyodhānparalokamadarśayat ,nakulaścāpi saptaiva śatāni prāhiṇoccharaiḥ.
english: There, Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, killed a thousand (warriors). Bhima sent seven hundred warriors to the other world. And Nakula also dispatched exactly seven hundred (warriors) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 24. There, Yudhishthira, son of Kunti, killed a thousand (warriors). Bhima sent seven hundred warriors to the other world. And Nakula also dispatched exactly seven hundred (warriors) with arrows.

sanskrit: शतानि त्रीणि शूराणां सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।युधिष्ठिरसमादिष्टो निजघ्ने पुरुषर्षभः ।भित्त्वा तां महतीं सेनां त्रिगर्तानां नरर्षभ ॥२५॥
transliteration: śatāni trīṇi śūrāṇāṁ sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,yudhiṣṭhirasamādiṣṭo nijaghne puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,bhittvā tāṁ mahatīṁ senāṁ trigartānāṁ nararṣabha.
english: Commanded by Yudhishthira, the valiant (pratāpavān) Sahadeva, a bull among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ), killed three hundred heroes. Having pierced that great army (senām) of the Trigartas, he achieved this, O bull among men (nararṣabha)!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 25. Commanded by Yudhishthira, the valiant (pratāpavān) Sahadeva, a bull among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ), killed three hundred heroes. Having pierced that great army (senām) of the Trigartas, he achieved this, O bull among men (nararṣabha)!

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा त्वरमाणो महारथः ।अभिद्रुत्य सुशर्माणं शरैरभ्यतुदद्भृशम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā tvaramāṇo mahārathaḥ ,abhidrutya suśarmāṇaṁ śarairabhyatudadbhṛśam.
english: Then King Yudhishthira, the great warrior, swiftly rushed towards Susharma and pierced him severely with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 26. Then King Yudhishthira, the great warrior, swiftly rushed towards Susharma and pierced him severely with arrows.

sanskrit: सुशर्मापि सुसंक्रुद्धस्त्वरमाणो युधिष्ठिरम् ।अविध्यन्नवभिर्बाणैश्चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: suśarmāpi susaṁkruddhastvaramāṇo yudhiṣṭhiram ,avidhyannavabhirbāṇaiścaturbhiścaturo hayān.
english: Susharma, greatly enraged, also swiftly pierced Yudhishthira with nine arrows, and with four (arrows, he pierced) his four horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 27. Susharma, greatly enraged, also swiftly pierced Yudhishthira with nine arrows, and with four (arrows, he pierced) his four horses.

sanskrit: ततो राजन्नाशुकारी कुन्तीपुत्रो वृकोदरः ।समासाद्य सुशर्माणमश्वानस्य व्यपोथयत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tato rājannāśukārī kuntīputro vṛkodaraḥ ,samāsādya suśarmāṇamaśvānasya vyapothayat.
english: Then, O King, the swift-acting Vrikodara, son of Kunti, having reached Susharma, destroyed his horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 28. Then, O King, the swift-acting Vrikodara, son of Kunti, having reached Susharma, destroyed his horses.

sanskrit: पृष्ठगोपौ च तस्याथ हत्वा परमसायकैः ।अथास्य सारथिं क्रुद्धो रथोपस्थादपाहरत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭhagopau ca tasyātha hatvā paramasāyakaiḥ ,athāsya sārathiṁ kruddho rathopasthādapāharat.
english: Then, having killed his two rear guards with excellent arrows, the enraged (Bhima) pulled his charioteer from the chariot's seat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 29. Then, having killed his two rear guards with excellent arrows, the enraged (Bhima) pulled his charioteer from the chariot's seat.

sanskrit: चक्ररक्षश्च शूरश्च शोणाश्वो नाम विश्रुतः ।स भयाद्द्वैरथं दृष्ट्वा त्रैगर्तं प्राजहत्तदा ॥३०॥
transliteration: cakrarakṣaśca śūraśca śoṇāśvo nāma viśrutaḥ ,sa bhayāddvairathaṁ dṛṣṭvā traigartaṁ prājahattadā.
english: The brave and renowned protector of the wheels, named Shoṇāśva, then abandoned the King of Trigarta, having seen the chariot duel, out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 30. The brave and renowned protector of the wheels, named Shoṇāśva, then abandoned the King of Trigarta, having seen the chariot duel, out of fear.

sanskrit: ततो विराटः प्रस्कन्द्य रथादथ सुशर्मणः ।गदामस्य परामृश्य तमेवाजघ्निवान्बली ।स चचार गदापाणिर्वृद्धोऽपि तरुणो यथा ॥३१॥
transliteration: tato virāṭaḥ praskandya rathādatha suśarmaṇaḥ ,gadāmasya parāmṛśya tamevājaghnivānbalī ,sa cacāra gadāpāṇirvṛddho'pi taruṇo yathā.
english: Then, the powerful King Virāṭa, having jumped down from Suśarman's chariot and seized his mace, struck Suśarman. Though old, he moved about, mace in hand, like a young man.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 31. Then, the powerful King Virāṭa, having jumped down from Suśarman's chariot and seized his mace, struck Suśarman. Though old, he moved about, mace in hand, like a young man.

sanskrit: भीमस्तु भीमसंकाशो रथात्प्रस्कन्द्य कुण्डली ।त्रिगर्तराजमादत्त सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा ॥३२॥
transliteration: bhīmastu bhīmasaṁkāśo rathātpraskandya kuṇḍalī ,trigartarājamādatta siṁhaḥ kṣudramṛgaṁ yathā.
english: Bhīma, however, formidable in appearance and wearing earrings, having jumped down from his chariot, seized the King of Trigarta, just as a lion seizes a small animal.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 32. Bhīma, however, formidable in appearance and wearing earrings, having jumped down from his chariot, seized the King of Trigarta, just as a lion seizes a small animal.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्गृहीते विरथे त्रिगर्तानां महारथे ।अभज्यत बलं सर्वं त्रैगर्तं तद्भयातुरम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasmingṛhīte virathe trigartānāṁ mahārathe ,abhajyata balaṁ sarvaṁ traigartaṁ tadbhayāturam.
english: When that great charioteer of the Trigartas was seized and made chariotless, the entire Trigarta army, terrified by that (fear), scattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 33. When that great charioteer of the Trigartas was seized and made chariotless, the entire Trigarta army, terrified by that (fear), scattered.

sanskrit: निवर्त्य गास्ततः सर्वाः पाण्डुपुत्रा महाबलाः ।अवजित्य सुशर्माणं धनं चादाय सर्वशः ॥३४॥
transliteration: nivartya gāstataḥ sarvāḥ pāṇḍuputrā mahābalāḥ ,avajitya suśarmāṇaṁ dhanaṁ cādāya sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, the mighty sons of Pāṇḍu, having brought back all the cows and having defeated Suśarman, completely seized all the wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 34. Then, the mighty sons of Pāṇḍu, having brought back all the cows and having defeated Suśarman, completely seized all the wealth.

sanskrit: स्वबाहुबलसंपन्ना ह्रीनिषेधा यतव्रताः ।संग्रामशिरसो मध्ये तां रात्रिं सुखिनोऽवसन् ॥३५॥
transliteration: svabāhubalasaṁpannā hrīniṣedhā yatavratāḥ ,saṁgrāmaśiraso madhye tāṁ rātriṁ sukhino'vasan.
english: Endowed with the strength of their own arms, having overcome any sense of modesty, and adhering to their vows, they happily spent that night in the heart of the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 35. Endowed with the strength of their own arms, having overcome any sense of modesty, and adhering to their vows, they happily spent that night in the heart of the battlefield.

sanskrit: ततो विराटः कौन्तेयानतिमानुषविक्रमान् ।अर्चयामास वित्तेन मानेन च महारथान् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tato virāṭaḥ kaunteyānatimānuṣavikramān ,arcayāmāsa vittena mānena ca mahārathān.
english: Then, King Virāṭa honored the Pāṇḍavas (kaunteyān), who possessed superhuman valor and were great charioteers, with wealth and respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 36. Then, King Virāṭa honored the Pāṇḍavas (kaunteyān), who possessed superhuman valor and were great charioteers, with wealth and respect.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।यथैव मम रत्नानि युष्माकं तानि वै तथा ।कार्यं कुरुत तैः सर्वे यथाकामं यथासुखम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,yathaiva mama ratnāni yuṣmākaṁ tāni vai tathā ,kāryaṁ kuruta taiḥ sarve yathākāmaṁ yathāsukham.
english: Virāṭa said: "Just as these jewels are mine, so indeed are they yours. All of you, use them as you desire and as you please."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 37. Virāṭa said: "Just as these jewels are mine, so indeed are they yours. All of you, use them as you desire and as you please."

sanskrit: ददान्यलंकृताः कन्या वसूनि विविधानि च ।मनसश्चाप्यभिप्रेतं यद्वः शत्रुनिबर्हणाः ॥३८॥
transliteration: dadānyalaṁkṛtāḥ kanyā vasūni vividhāni ca ,manasaścāpyabhipretaṁ yadvaḥ śatrunibarhaṇāḥ.
english: O destroyers of enemies! I shall give decorated maidens, various riches, and whatever else is desired by your hearts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 38. O destroyers of enemies! I shall give decorated maidens, various riches, and whatever else is desired by your hearts.

sanskrit: युष्माकं विक्रमादद्य मुक्तोऽहं स्वस्तिमानिह ।तस्माद्भवन्तो मत्स्यानामीश्वराः सर्व एव हि ॥३९॥
transliteration: yuṣmākaṁ vikramādadya mukto'haṁ svastimāniha ,tasmādbhavanto matsyānāmīśvarāḥ sarva eva hi.
english: By your prowess, I am today freed and safe here. Therefore, all of you are indeed the lords of the Matsyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 39. By your prowess, I am today freed and safe here. Therefore, all of you are indeed the lords of the Matsyas.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथाभिवादिनं मत्स्यं कौरवेयाः पृथक्पृथक् ।ऊचुः प्राञ्जलयः सर्वे युधिष्ठिरपुरोगमाः ॥४०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathābhivādinaṁ matsyaṁ kauraveyāḥ pṛthakpṛthak ,ūcuḥ prāñjalayaḥ sarve yudhiṣṭhirapurogamāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The Kuru princes, with Yudhiṣṭhira as their leader, all individually and with folded hands, spoke to Matsya (King Virāṭa) who was greeting them thus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 40. Vaiśampāyana said: The Kuru princes, with Yudhiṣṭhira as their leader, all individually and with folded hands, spoke to Matsya (King Virāṭa) who was greeting them thus.

sanskrit: प्रतिनन्दाम ते वाक्यं सर्वं चैव विशां पते ।एतेनैव प्रतीताः स्मो यत्त्वं मुक्तोऽद्य शत्रुभिः ॥४१॥
transliteration: pratinandāma te vākyaṁ sarvaṁ caiva viśāṁ pate ,etenaiva pratītāḥ smo yattvaṁ mukto'dya śatrubhiḥ.
english: O lord of the people! We indeed welcome all your words. We are truly satisfied by this, that you are freed from your enemies today.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 41. O lord of the people! We indeed welcome all your words. We are truly satisfied by this, that you are freed from your enemies today.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्प्रीतमना मत्स्यराजो युधिष्ठिरम् ।पुनरेव महाबाहुर्विराटो राजसत्तमः ।एहि त्वामभिषेक्ष्यामि मत्स्यराजोऽस्तु नो भवान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: athābravītprītamanā matsyarājo yudhiṣṭhiram ,punareva mahābāhurvirāṭo rājasattamaḥ ,ehi tvāmabhiṣekṣyāmi matsyarājo'stu no bhavān.
english: Then, the mighty-armed Virāṭa, the best among kings and the delighted king of Matsya, spoke again to Yudhiṣṭhira: 'Come, I shall anoint you. Let your honor be our king of Matsya.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 42. Then, the mighty-armed Virāṭa, the best among kings and the delighted king of Matsya, spoke again to Yudhiṣṭhira: 'Come, I shall anoint you. Let your honor be our king of Matsya.'

sanskrit: मनसश्चाप्यभिप्रेतं यत्ते शत्रुनिबर्हण ।तत्तेऽहं संप्रदास्यामि सर्वमर्हति नो भवान् ॥४३॥
transliteration: manasaścāpyabhipretaṁ yatte śatrunibarhaṇa ,tatte'haṁ saṁpradāsyāmi sarvamarhati no bhavān.
english: O destroyer of enemies, whatever is desired by your mind, that I shall give completely to you. Your honor is worthy of everything from us.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 43. O destroyer of enemies, whatever is desired by your mind, that I shall give completely to you. Your honor is worthy of everything from us.

sanskrit: रत्नानि गाः सुवर्णं च मणिमुक्तमथापि वा ।वैयाघ्रपद्य विप्रेन्द्र सर्वथैव नमोऽस्तु ते ॥४४॥
transliteration: ratnāni gāḥ suvarṇaṁ ca maṇimuktamathāpi vā ,vaiyāghrapadya viprendra sarvathaiva namo'stu te.
english: Jewels, cows, and gold, or even pearls and gems – O descendant of Vyāghrapāda, O chief of Brahmins, homage be to you in every way.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 44. Jewels, cows, and gold, or even pearls and gems – O descendant of Vyāghrapāda, O chief of Brahmins, homage be to you in every way.

sanskrit: त्वत्कृते ह्यद्य पश्यामि राज्यमात्मानमेव च ।यतश्च जातः संरम्भः स च शत्रुर्वशं गतः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tvatkṛte hyadya paśyāmi rājyamātmānameva ca ,yataśca jātaḥ saṁrambhaḥ sa ca śatrurvaśaṁ gataḥ.
english: Indeed, today, for your sake, I see my kingdom and myself saved. And that enemy from whom this conflict arose has been subdued.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 45. Indeed, today, for your sake, I see my kingdom and myself saved. And that enemy from whom this conflict arose has been subdued.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो मत्स्यं पुनरेवाभ्यभाषत ।प्रतिनन्दामि ते वाक्यं मनोज्ञं मत्स्य भाषसे ॥४६॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro matsyaṁ punarevābhyabhāṣata ,pratinandāmi te vākyaṁ manojñaṁ matsya bhāṣase.
english: Then Yudhishthira spoke to King Matsya again: 'O Matsya, I truly appreciate your agreeable words.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 46. Then Yudhishthira spoke to King Matsya again: 'O Matsya, I truly appreciate your agreeable words.'

sanskrit: आनृशंस्यपरो नित्यं सुसुखः सततं भव ।गच्छन्तु दूतास्त्वरितं नगरं तव पार्थिव ।सुहृदां प्रियमाख्यातुं घोषयन्तु च ते जयम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: ānṛśaṁsyaparo nityaṁ susukhaḥ satataṁ bhava ,gacchantu dūtāstvaritaṁ nagaraṁ tava pārthiva ,suhṛdāṁ priyamākhyātuṁ ghoṣayantu ca te jayam.
english: O King, may you always be devoted to kindness and perpetually very happy. Let your messengers swiftly go to your city to announce the good news to your friends and proclaim your victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 47. O King, may you always be devoted to kindness and perpetually very happy. Let your messengers swiftly go to your city to announce the good news to your friends and proclaim your victory.

sanskrit: ततस्तद्वचनान्मत्स्यो दूतान्राजा समादिशत् ।आचक्षध्वं पुरं गत्वा संग्रामे विजयं मम ॥४८॥
transliteration: tatastadvacanānmatsyo dūtānrājā samādiśat ,ācakṣadhvaṁ puraṁ gatvā saṁgrāme vijayaṁ mama.
english: Then, at Yudhishthira's word, King Matsya commanded the messengers: 'Go to the city and announce my victory in battle!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 48. Then, at Yudhishthira's word, King Matsya commanded the messengers: 'Go to the city and announce my victory in battle!'

sanskrit: कुमाराः समलंकृत्य पर्यागच्छन्तु मे पुरात् ।वादित्राणि च सर्वाणि गणिकाश्च स्वलंकृताः ॥४९॥
transliteration: kumārāḥ samalaṁkṛtya paryāgacchantu me purāt ,vāditrāṇi ca sarvāṇi gaṇikāśca svalaṁkṛtāḥ.
english: Let the princes, having adorned themselves, come forth from my city. And let all musical instruments and well-decorated courtesans also come.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 49. Let the princes, having adorned themselves, come forth from my city. And let all musical instruments and well-decorated courtesans also come.

sanskrit: ते गत्वा केवलां रात्रिमथ सूर्योदयं प्रति ।विराटस्य पुराभ्याशे दूता जयमघोषयन् ॥५०॥
transliteration: te gatvā kevalāṁ rātrimatha sūryodayaṁ prati ,virāṭasya purābhyāśe dūtā jayamaghoṣayan.
english: Having traveled throughout the entire night, then, towards the sunrise, the messengers announced victory near Virāṭa's city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 32 Verse 50. Having traveled throughout the entire night, then, towards the sunrise, the messengers announced victory near Virāṭa's city.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।याते त्रिगर्तं मत्स्ये तु पशूंस्तान्स्वान्परीप्सति ।दुर्योधनः सहामात्यो विराटमुपयादथ ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,yāte trigartaṁ matsye tu paśūṁstānsvānparīpsati ,duryodhanaḥ sahāmātyo virāṭamupayādatha.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: When Matsya (King Virāṭa) had gone to Trigarta, intent on recovering his own cattle, then Duryodhana, accompanied by his ministers, approached Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: When Matsya (King Virāṭa) had gone to Trigarta, intent on recovering his own cattle, then Duryodhana, accompanied by his ministers, approached Virāṭa.

sanskrit: भीष्मो द्रोणश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च परमास्त्रवित् ।द्रौणिश्च सौबलश्चैव तथा दुःशासनः प्रभुः ॥२॥
transliteration: bhīṣmo droṇaśca karṇaśca kṛpaśca paramāstravit ,drauṇiśca saubalaścaiva tathā duḥśāsanaḥ prabhuḥ.
english: Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Karṇa, and Kṛpa, the master of supreme weapons; and Drauṇi, and Saubala (Śakuni); as well as the powerful Duḥśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 2. Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Karṇa, and Kṛpa, the master of supreme weapons; and Drauṇi, and Saubala (Śakuni); as well as the powerful Duḥśāsana.

sanskrit: विविंशतिर्विकर्णश्च चित्रसेनश्च वीर्यवान् ।दुर्मुखो दुःसहश्चैव ये चैवान्ये महारथाः ॥३॥
transliteration: viviṁśatirvikarṇaśca citrasenaśca vīryavān ,durmukho duḥsahaścaiva ye caivānye mahārathāḥ.
english: Viviṃśati, Vikarṇa, and the valiant Citrasena; Durmukha and Duḥsaha also; and whatever other great charioteers (mahārathas) were there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 3. Viviṃśati, Vikarṇa, and the valiant Citrasena; Durmukha and Duḥsaha also; and whatever other great charioteers (mahārathas) were there.

sanskrit: एते मत्स्यानुपागम्य विराटस्य महीपतेः ।घोषान्विद्राव्य तरसा गोधनं जह्रुरोजसा ॥४॥
transliteration: ete matsyānupāgamya virāṭasya mahīpateḥ ,ghoṣānvidrāvya tarasā godhanaṁ jahrurojasā.
english: These (Kauravas), having approached the Matsya kingdom of King Virāṭa, swiftly scattered the cowherds' settlements and forcibly plundered their cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 4. These (Kauravas), having approached the Matsya kingdom of King Virāṭa, swiftly scattered the cowherds' settlements and forcibly plundered their cattle.

sanskrit: षष्टिं गवां सहस्राणि कुरवः कालयन्ति ते ।महता रथवंशेन परिवार्य समन्ततः ॥५॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭiṁ gavāṁ sahasrāṇi kuravaḥ kālayanti te ,mahatā rathavaṁśena parivārya samantataḥ.
english: Those Kurus drive away sixty thousand cows, having surrounded them from all sides with a great array of chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 5. Those Kurus drive away sixty thousand cows, having surrounded them from all sides with a great array of chariots.

sanskrit: गोपालानां तु घोषेषु हन्यतां तैर्महारथैः ।आरावः सुमहानासीत्संप्रहारे भयंकरे ॥६॥
transliteration: gopālānāṁ tu ghoṣeṣu hanyatāṁ tairmahārathaiḥ ,ārāvaḥ sumahānāsītsaṁprahāre bhayaṁkare.
english: Indeed, amidst the cowherds' settlements, as they were being struck down by those great charioteers, a very great uproar arose in that terrifying conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 6. Indeed, amidst the cowherds' settlements, as they were being struck down by those great charioteers, a very great uproar arose in that terrifying conflict.

sanskrit: गवाध्यक्षस्तु संत्रस्तो रथमास्थाय सत्वरः ।जगाम नगरायैव परिक्रोशंस्तदार्तवत् ॥७॥
transliteration: gavādhyakṣastu saṁtrasto rathamāsthāya satvaraḥ ,jagāma nagarāyaiva parikrośaṁstadārtavat.
english: However, the terrified superintendent of cows, having swiftly mounted a chariot, went directly to the city, lamenting like one in distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 7. However, the terrified superintendent of cows, having swiftly mounted a chariot, went directly to the city, lamenting like one in distress.

sanskrit: स प्रविश्य पुरं राज्ञो नृपवेश्माभ्ययात्ततः ।अवतीर्य रथात्तूर्णमाख्यातुं प्रविवेश ह ॥८॥
transliteration: sa praviśya puraṁ rājño nṛpaveśmābhyayāttataḥ ,avatīrya rathāttūrṇamākhyātuṁ praviveśa ha.
english: Having entered the king's city, he then approached the royal palace. Quickly dismounting from the chariot, he indeed entered to report the news.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 8. Having entered the king's city, he then approached the royal palace. Quickly dismounting from the chariot, he indeed entered to report the news.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा भूमिंजयं नाम पुत्रं मत्स्यस्य मानिनम् ।तस्मै तत्सर्वमाचष्ट राष्ट्रस्य पशुकर्षणम् ॥९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā bhūmiṁjayaṁ nāma putraṁ matsyasya māninam ,tasmai tatsarvamācaṣṭa rāṣṭrasya paśukarṣaṇam.
english: Having seen King Matsya's proud son named Bhumijaya (Uttara), he reported everything to him, specifically the plundering of the kingdom's cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 9. Having seen King Matsya's proud son named Bhumijaya (Uttara), he reported everything to him, specifically the plundering of the kingdom's cattle.

sanskrit: षष्टिं गवां सहस्राणि कुरवः कालयन्ति ते ।तद्विजेतुं समुत्तिष्ठ गोधनं राष्ट्रवर्धनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭiṁ gavāṁ sahasrāṇi kuravaḥ kālayanti te ,tadvijetuṁ samuttiṣṭha godhanaṁ rāṣṭravardhanam.
english: The Kurus are driving away sixty thousand cows. Therefore, arise to conquer that herd of cattle, which enhances the wealth of our kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 10. The Kurus are driving away sixty thousand cows. Therefore, arise to conquer that herd of cattle, which enhances the wealth of our kingdom.

sanskrit: राजपुत्र हितप्रेप्सुः क्षिप्रं निर्याहि वै स्वयम् ।त्वां हि मत्स्यो महीपालः शून्यपालमिहाकरोत् ॥११॥
transliteration: rājaputra hitaprepsuḥ kṣipraṁ niryāhi vai svayam ,tvāṁ hi matsyo mahīpālaḥ śūnyapālamihākarot.
english: O Prince, desiring your own welfare, you should quickly go forth yourself. For King Matsya, the protector of the earth, has made you the guardian of this empty city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 11. O Prince, desiring your own welfare, you should quickly go forth yourself. For King Matsya, the protector of the earth, has made you the guardian of this empty city.

sanskrit: त्वया परिषदो मध्ये श्लाघते स नराधिपः ।पुत्रो ममानुरूपश्च शूरश्चेति कुलोद्वहः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tvayā pariṣado madhye ślāghate sa narādhipaḥ ,putro mamānurūpaśca śūraśceti kulodvahaḥ.
english: That king (Duryodhana) boasts about you in the assembly, saying, 'My son is worthy, brave, and the upholder of the lineage!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 12. That king (Duryodhana) boasts about you in the assembly, saying, 'My son is worthy, brave, and the upholder of the lineage!'

sanskrit: इष्वस्त्रे निपुणो योधः सदा वीरश्च मे सुतः ।तस्य तत्सत्यमेवास्तु मनुष्येन्द्रस्य भाषितम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: iṣvastre nipuṇo yodhaḥ sadā vīraśca me sutaḥ ,tasya tatsatyamevāstu manuṣyendrasya bhāṣitam.
english: My son is a warrior skilled in archery and always brave. May that statement of the king (Duryodhana) truly come to pass.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 13. My son is a warrior skilled in archery and always brave. May that statement of the king (Duryodhana) truly come to pass.

sanskrit: आवर्तय कुरूञ्जित्वा पशून्पशुमतां वर ।निर्दहैषामनीकानि भीमेन शरतेजसा ॥१४॥
transliteration: āvartaya kurūñjitvā paśūnpaśumatāṁ vara ,nirdahaiṣāmanīkāni bhīmena śaratejasā.
english: O best among those who possess cattle, having conquered the Kurus, bring back the cattle! Burn their armies completely with the terrifying power of your arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 14. O best among those who possess cattle, having conquered the Kurus, bring back the cattle! Burn their armies completely with the terrifying power of your arrows.

sanskrit: धनुश्च्युतै रुक्मपुङ्खैः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।द्विषतां भिन्ध्यनीकानि गजानामिव यूथपः ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhanuścyutai rukmapuṅkhaiḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,dviṣatāṁ bhindhyanīkāni gajānāmiva yūthapaḥ.
english: Shatter the armies of your enemies with arrows released from your bow, which have golden shafts and well-formed joints, just as a leader of elephants shatters other herds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 15. Shatter the armies of your enemies with arrows released from your bow, which have golden shafts and well-formed joints, just as a leader of elephants shatters other herds.

sanskrit: पाशोपधानां ज्यातन्त्रीं चापदण्डां महास्वनाम् ।शरवर्णां धनुर्वीणां शत्रुमध्ये प्रवादय ॥१६॥
transliteration: pāśopadhānāṁ jyātantrīṁ cāpadaṇḍāṁ mahāsvanām ,śaravarṇāṁ dhanurvīṇāṁ śatrumadhye pravādaya.
english: In the midst of the enemies, play your bow-lute (dhanurvīṇā), which has nooses as its rests, bowstrings as its strings, bow-staves as its frame, produces a mighty sound, and has the appearance of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 16. In the midst of the enemies, play your bow-lute (dhanurvīṇā), which has nooses as its rests, bowstrings as its strings, bow-staves as its frame, produces a mighty sound, and has the appearance of arrows.

sanskrit: श्वेता रजतसंकाशा रथे युज्यन्तु ते हयाः ।ध्वजं च सिंहं सौवर्णमुच्छ्रयन्तु तवाभिभोः ॥१७॥
transliteration: śvetā rajatasaṁkāśā rathe yujyantu te hayāḥ ,dhvajaṁ ca siṁhaṁ sauvarṇamucchrayantu tavābhibhoḥ.
english: O lord (abhibhoḥ), let your white, silver-like horses be yoked to the chariot. And let your golden lion banner be raised.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 17. O lord (abhibhoḥ), let your white, silver-like horses be yoked to the chariot. And let your golden lion banner be raised.

sanskrit: रुक्मपुङ्खाः प्रसन्नाग्रा मुक्ता हस्तवता त्वया ।छादयन्तु शराः सूर्यं राज्ञामायुर्निरोधिनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: rukmapuṅkhāḥ prasannāgrā muktā hastavatā tvayā ,chādayantu śarāḥ sūryaṁ rājñāmāyurnirodhinaḥ.
english: Let these arrows, which have golden shafts and sharp tips, released by your skillful hand, cover the sun and obstruct the lives of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 18. Let these arrows, which have golden shafts and sharp tips, released by your skillful hand, cover the sun and obstruct the lives of kings.

sanskrit: रणे जित्वा कुरून्सर्वान्वज्रपाणिरिवासुरान् ।यशो महदवाप्य त्वं प्रविशेदं पुरं पुनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: raṇe jitvā kurūnsarvānvajrapāṇirivāsurān ,yaśo mahadavāpya tvaṁ praviśedaṁ puraṁ punaḥ.
english: Having conquered all the Kurus in battle, just as Indra (Vajrapāṇiḥ) conquered the asuras, and having achieved great fame, you should enter this city again.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 19. Having conquered all the Kurus in battle, just as Indra (Vajrapāṇiḥ) conquered the asuras, and having achieved great fame, you should enter this city again.

sanskrit: त्वं हि राष्ट्रस्य परमा गतिर्मत्स्यपतेः सुतः ।गतिमन्तो भवन्त्वद्य सर्वे विषयवासिनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hi rāṣṭrasya paramā gatirmatsyapateḥ sutaḥ ,gatimanto bhavantvadya sarve viṣayavāsinaḥ.
english: Indeed, you are the supreme protector of the kingdom, son of the Matsya king. May all inhabitants of this region find protection today.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 20. Indeed, you are the supreme protector of the kingdom, son of the Matsya king. May all inhabitants of this region find protection today.

sanskrit: स्त्रीमध्य उक्तस्तेनासौ तद्वाक्यमभयंकरम् ।अन्तःपुरे श्लाघमान इदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: strīmadhya uktastenāsau tadvākyamabhayaṁkaram ,antaḥpure ślāghamāna idaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Addressed among the women, he (that person) received that reassuring statement. Praising himself in the inner chambers, he then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 33 Verse 21. Addressed among the women, he (that person) received that reassuring statement. Praising himself in the inner chambers, he then spoke these words.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।अद्याहमनुगच्छेयं दृढधन्वा गवां पदम् ।यदि मे सारथिः कश्चिद्भवेदश्वेषु कोविदः ॥१॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,adyāhamanugaccheyaṁ dṛḍhadhanvā gavāṁ padam ,yadi me sārathiḥ kaścidbhavedaśveṣu kovidaḥ.
english: Uttara said: 'Today I would pursue the cows' path, being a strong bowman, if only I had a charioteer who is expert with horses.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 1. Uttara said: 'Today I would pursue the cows' path, being a strong bowman, if only I had a charioteer who is expert with horses.'

sanskrit: तमेव नाधिगच्छामि यो मे यन्ता भवेन्नरः ।पश्यध्वं सारथिं क्षिप्रं मम युक्तं प्रयास्यतः ॥२॥
transliteration: tameva nādhigacchāmi yo me yantā bhavennaraḥ ,paśyadhvaṁ sārathiṁ kṣipraṁ mama yuktaṁ prayāsyataḥ.
english: I cannot find that very person who would be my charioteer - a man suitable for me. You all must quickly find a charioteer for me, as I am ready to depart.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 2. I cannot find that very person who would be my charioteer - a man suitable for me. You all must quickly find a charioteer for me, as I am ready to depart.

sanskrit: अष्टाविंशतिरात्रं वा मासं वा नूनमन्ततः ।यत्तदासीन्महद्युद्धं तत्र मे सारथिर्हतः ॥३॥
transliteration: aṣṭāviṁśatirātraṁ vā māsaṁ vā nūnamantataḥ ,yattadāsīnmahadyuddhaṁ tatra me sārathirhataḥ.
english: Surely, in that great war which lasted for at least twenty-eight nights or a month, my charioteer was killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 3. Surely, in that great war which lasted for at least twenty-eight nights or a month, my charioteer was killed.

sanskrit: स लभेयं यदि त्वन्यं हययानविदं नरम् ।त्वरावानद्य यात्वाहं समुच्छ्रितमहाध्वजम् ॥४॥
transliteration: sa labheyaṁ yadi tvanyaṁ hayayānavidaṁ naram ,tvarāvānadya yātvāhaṁ samucchritamahādhvajam.
english: If I can indeed obtain another man skilled in horses and chariots, then I, being swift, will go today towards that (enemy) with the high, great banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 4. If I can indeed obtain another man skilled in horses and chariots, then I, being swift, will go today towards that (enemy) with the high, great banner.

sanskrit: विगाह्य तत्परानीकं गजवाजिरथाकुलम् ।शस्त्रप्रतापनिर्वीर्यान्कुरूञ्जित्वानये पशून् ॥५॥
transliteration: vigāhya tatparānīkaṁ gajavājirathākulam ,śastrapratāpanirvīryānkurūñjitvānaye paśūn.
english: Having penetrated that enemy army, which is teeming with elephants, horses, and chariots, and having conquered the Kurus - whose valor has been drained by the might of weapons - I shall bring them back like cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 5. Having penetrated that enemy army, which is teeming with elephants, horses, and chariots, and having conquered the Kurus - whose valor has been drained by the might of weapons - I shall bring them back like cattle.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं शांतनवं कर्णं वैकर्तनं कृपम् ।द्रोणं च सह पुत्रेण महेष्वासान्समागतान् ॥६॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ śāṁtanavaṁ karṇaṁ vaikartanaṁ kṛpam ,droṇaṁ ca saha putreṇa maheṣvāsānsamāgatān.
english: I shall bring back like cattle Duryodhana, Bhishma (the son of Shantanu), Karṇa (the son of Vikartana), Kṛpa, and Droṇa together with his son - all those great archers who have assembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 6. I shall bring back like cattle Duryodhana, Bhishma (the son of Shantanu), Karṇa (the son of Vikartana), Kṛpa, and Droṇa together with his son - all those great archers who have assembled.

sanskrit: वित्रासयित्वा संग्रामे दानवानिव वज्रभृत् ।अनेनैव मुहूर्तेन पुनः प्रत्यानये पशून् ॥७॥
transliteration: vitrāsayitvā saṁgrāme dānavāniva vajrabhṛt ,anenaiva muhūrtena punaḥ pratyānaye paśūn.
english: Having terrified (them) in battle, just like Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrabhṛt), I will bring back the cattle again in this very moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 7. Having terrified (them) in battle, just like Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrabhṛt), I will bring back the cattle again in this very moment.

sanskrit: शून्यमासाद्य कुरवः प्रयान्त्यादाय गोधनम् ।किं नु शक्यं मया कर्तुं यदहं तत्र नाभवम् ॥८॥
transliteration: śūnyamāsādya kuravaḥ prayāntyādāya godhanam ,kiṁ nu śakyaṁ mayā kartuṁ yadahaṁ tatra nābhavam.
english: Having found (our grazing grounds) deserted, the Kurus are leaving, having taken the cattle. What indeed can I do, since I was not there?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 8. Having found (our grazing grounds) deserted, the Kurus are leaving, having taken the cattle. What indeed can I do, since I was not there?

sanskrit: पश्येयुरद्य मे वीर्यं कुरवस्ते समागताः ।किं नु पार्थोऽर्जुनः साक्षादयमस्मान्प्रबाधते ॥९॥
transliteration: paśyeyuradya me vīryaṁ kuravaste samāgatāḥ ,kiṁ nu pārtho'rjunaḥ sākṣādayamasmānprabādhate.
english: May those assembled Kurus see my valor today! Is it indeed that Partha, Arjuna himself, is directly oppressing us?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 9. May those assembled Kurus see my valor today! Is it indeed that Partha, Arjuna himself, is directly oppressing us?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य तद्वचनं स्त्रीषु भाषतः स्म पुनः पुनः ।नामर्षयत पाञ्चाली बीभत्सोः परिकीर्तनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya tadvacanaṁ strīṣu bhāṣataḥ sma punaḥ punaḥ ,nāmarṣayata pāñcālī bībhatsoḥ parikīrtanam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Pañcālī (Draupadi) did not tolerate that speech of his, spoken again and again among the women, nor the praise (parikīrtanam) of Bībhatsu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 10. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Pañcālī (Draupadi) did not tolerate that speech of his, spoken again and again among the women, nor the praise (parikīrtanam) of Bībhatsu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अथैनमुपसंगम्य स्त्रीमध्यात्सा तपस्विनी ।व्रीडमानेव शनकैरिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥११॥
transliteration: athainamupasaṁgamya strīmadhyātsā tapasvinī ,vrīḍamāneva śanakairidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Then, that ascetic woman, having approached him from amidst the women, slowly spoke these words as if feeling shy.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 11. Then, that ascetic woman, having approached him from amidst the women, slowly spoke these words as if feeling shy.

sanskrit: योऽसौ बृहद्वारणाभो युवा सुप्रियदर्शनः ।बृहन्नडेति विख्यातः पार्थस्यासीत्स सारथिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yo'sau bṛhadvāraṇābho yuvā supriyadarśanaḥ ,bṛhannaḍeti vikhyātaḥ pārthasyāsītsa sārathiḥ.
english: That young man, who had the appearance of a great elephant and a very pleasing look, renowned by the name Bṛhannaḍā, he was Arjuna's (Pārtha's) charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 12. That young man, who had the appearance of a great elephant and a very pleasing look, renowned by the name Bṛhannaḍā, he was Arjuna's (Pārtha's) charioteer.

sanskrit: धनुष्यनवरश्चासीत्तस्य शिष्यो महात्मनः ।दृष्टपूर्वो मया वीर चरन्त्या पाण्डवान्प्रति ॥१३॥
transliteration: dhanuṣyanavaraścāsīttasya śiṣyo mahātmanaḥ ,dṛṣṭapūrvo mayā vīra carantyā pāṇḍavānprati.
english: And he was an excellent disciple of that great-souled (mahātman) individual in archery. O hero, I had seen him before when I was traveling towards the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 13. And he was an excellent disciple of that great-souled (mahātman) individual in archery. O hero, I had seen him before when I was traveling towards the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: यदा तत्पावको दावमदहत्खाण्डवं महत् ।अर्जुनस्य तदानेन संगृहीता हयोत्तमाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: yadā tatpāvako dāvamadahatkhāṇḍavaṁ mahat ,arjunasya tadānena saṁgṛhītā hayottamāḥ.
english: When that fire (pāvaka) burned the great Khāṇḍava forest, then the excellent horses were controlled by him (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 14. When that fire (pāvaka) burned the great Khāṇḍava forest, then the excellent horses were controlled by him (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तेन सारथिना पार्थः सर्वभूतानि सर्वशः ।अजयत्खाण्डवप्रस्थे न हि यन्तास्ति तादृशः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tena sārathinā pārthaḥ sarvabhūtāni sarvaśaḥ ,ajayatkhāṇḍavaprasthe na hi yantāsti tādṛśaḥ.
english: With that charioteer, Arjuna (pārtha) completely conquered all creatures in Khāṇḍavaprastha. Truly, there is no other charioteer like him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 15. With that charioteer, Arjuna (pārtha) completely conquered all creatures in Khāṇḍavaprastha. Truly, there is no other charioteer like him.

sanskrit: येयं कुमारी सुश्रोणी भगिनी ते यवीयसी ।अस्याः स वचनं वीर करिष्यति न संशयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: yeyaṁ kumārī suśroṇī bhaginī te yavīyasī ,asyāḥ sa vacanaṁ vīra kariṣyati na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O hero, he will certainly fulfill the word of this beautifully-hipped young maiden, your younger sister; there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 16. O hero, he will certainly fulfill the word of this beautifully-hipped young maiden, your younger sister; there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: यदि वै सारथिः स स्यात्कुरून्सर्वानसंशयम् ।जित्वा गाश्च समादाय ध्रुवमागमनं भवेत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadi vai sārathiḥ sa syātkurūnsarvānasaṁśayam ,jitvā gāśca samādāya dhruvamāgamanaṁ bhavet.
english: If he were indeed the charioteer, then, having conquered all the Kurus and retrieved the cows, his return would certainly be assured, without a doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 17. If he were indeed the charioteer, then, having conquered all the Kurus and retrieved the cows, his return would certainly be assured, without a doubt.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स सैरन्ध्र्या भगिनीं प्रत्यभाषत ।गच्छ त्वमनवद्याङ्गि तामानय बृहन्नडाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa sairandhryā bhaginīṁ pratyabhāṣata ,gaccha tvamanavadyāṅgi tāmānaya bṛhannaḍām.
english: Thus spoken to by Sairandhrī, he replied to his sister: 'O you of faultless limbs, go and bring Bṛhannalā!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 18. Thus spoken to by Sairandhrī, he replied to his sister: 'O you of faultless limbs, go and bring Bṛhannalā!'

sanskrit: सा भ्रात्रा प्रेषिता शीघ्रमगच्छन्नर्तनागृहम् ।यत्रास्ते स महाबाहुश्छन्नः सत्रेण पाण्डवः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sā bhrātrā preṣitā śīghramagacchannartanāgṛham ,yatrāste sa mahābāhuśchannaḥ satreṇa pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Sent by her brother, she quickly went to the dancing hall where that mighty-armed Pandava (Arjuna) was staying, hidden in his disguise.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 34 Verse 19. Sent by her brother, she quickly went to the dancing hall where that mighty-armed Pandava (Arjuna) was staying, hidden in his disguise.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स तां दृष्ट्वा विशालाक्षीं राजपुत्रीं सखीं सखा ।प्रहसन्नब्रवीद्राजन्कुत्रागमनमित्युत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa tāṁ dṛṣṭvā viśālākṣīṁ rājaputrīṁ sakhīṁ sakhā ,prahasannabravīdrājankutrāgamanamityuta.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Seeing that wide-eyed princess, his friend, he (Arjuna) laughed and said, 'O King, what brings you here?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Seeing that wide-eyed princess, his friend, he (Arjuna) laughed and said, 'O King, what brings you here?'

sanskrit: तमब्रवीद्राजपुत्री समुपेत्य नरर्षभम् ।प्रणयं भावयन्ती स्म सखीमध्य इदं वचः ॥२॥
transliteration: tamabravīdrājaputrī samupetya nararṣabham ,praṇayaṁ bhāvayantī sma sakhīmadhya idaṁ vacaḥ.
english: The princess, having approached that best of men (Arjuna), spoke these words to him, showing affection amidst her female companions.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 2. The princess, having approached that best of men (Arjuna), spoke these words to him, showing affection amidst her female companions.

sanskrit: गावो राष्ट्रस्य कुरुभिः काल्यन्ते नो बृहन्नडे ।तान्विजेतुं मम भ्राता प्रयास्यति धनुर्धरः ॥३॥
transliteration: gāvo rāṣṭrasya kurubhiḥ kālyante no bṛhannaḍe ,tānvijetuṁ mama bhrātā prayāsyati dhanurdharaḥ.
english: O Bṛhannaḷā, the Kurus are driving away the cows of our kingdom. My brother, the archer, will set out to defeat them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 3. O Bṛhannaḷā, the Kurus are driving away the cows of our kingdom. My brother, the archer, will set out to defeat them.

sanskrit: नचिरं च हतस्तस्य संग्रामे रथसारथिः ।तेन नास्ति समः सूतो योऽस्य सारथ्यमाचरेत् ॥४॥
transliteration: naciraṁ ca hatastasya saṁgrāme rathasārathiḥ ,tena nāsti samaḥ sūto yo'sya sārathyamācaret.
english: His charioteer was killed in battle not long ago. Consequently, there is no charioteer equal to him who can perform charioteering for this prince.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 4. His charioteer was killed in battle not long ago. Consequently, there is no charioteer equal to him who can perform charioteering for this prince.

sanskrit: तस्मै प्रयतमानाय सारथ्यर्थं बृहन्नडे ।आचचक्षे हयज्ञाने सैरन्ध्री कौशलं तव ॥५॥
transliteration: tasmai prayatamānāya sārathyarthaṁ bṛhannaḍe ,ācacakṣe hayajñāne sairandhrī kauśalaṁ tava.
english: O Brihannalā, when (the prince) was striving for a charioteer, Sairandhrī (Draupadī) spoke of your skill in horse management.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 5. O Brihannalā, when (the prince) was striving for a charioteer, Sairandhrī (Draupadī) spoke of your skill in horse management.

sanskrit: सा सारथ्यं मम भ्रातुः कुरु साधु बृहन्नडे ।पुरा दूरतरं गावो ह्रियन्ते कुरुभिर्हि नः ॥६॥
transliteration: sā sārathyaṁ mama bhrātuḥ kuru sādhu bṛhannaḍe ,purā dūrataraṁ gāvo hriyante kurubhirhi naḥ.
english: O Brihannalā, please perform the charioteering for my brother well. Indeed, our cows are being driven much farther away by the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 6. O Brihannalā, please perform the charioteering for my brother well. Indeed, our cows are being driven much farther away by the Kauravas.

sanskrit: अथैतद्वचनं मेऽद्य नियुक्ता न करिष्यसि ।प्रणयादुच्यमाना त्वं परित्यक्ष्यामि जीवितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: athaitadvacanaṁ me'dya niyuktā na kariṣyasi ,praṇayāducyamānā tvaṁ parityakṣyāmi jīvitam.
english: Now, if you, though commanded by me and spoken to affectionately, do not perform this task today, I will abandon my life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 7. Now, if you, though commanded by me and spoken to affectionately, do not perform this task today, I will abandon my life.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु सुश्रोण्या तया सख्या परंतपः ।जगाम राजपुत्रस्य सकाशममितौजसः ॥८॥
transliteration: evamuktastu suśroṇyā tayā sakhyā paraṁtapaḥ ,jagāma rājaputrasya sakāśamamitaujasaḥ.
english: Thus addressed by that beautiful-hipped female companion (Draupadi), the tormentor of foes (Arjuna/Bhima) went to the presence of the prince (Yudhishthira), who possessed immense vigor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 8. Thus addressed by that beautiful-hipped female companion (Draupadi), the tormentor of foes (Arjuna/Bhima) went to the presence of the prince (Yudhishthira), who possessed immense vigor.

sanskrit: तं सा व्रजन्तं त्वरितं प्रभिन्नमिव कुञ्जरम् ।अन्वगच्छद्विशालाक्षी शिशुर्गजवधूरिव ॥९॥
transliteration: taṁ sā vrajantaṁ tvaritaṁ prabhinnamiva kuñjaram ,anvagacchadviśālākṣī śiśurgajavadhūriva.
english: She, the wide-eyed one (Draupadi), followed him as he went swiftly, like a young female elephant following a rutting elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 9. She, the wide-eyed one (Draupadi), followed him as he went swiftly, like a young female elephant following a rutting elephant.

sanskrit: दूरादेव तु तं प्रेक्ष्य राजपुत्रोऽभ्यभाषत ।त्वया सारथिना पार्थः खाण्डवेऽग्निमतर्पयत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dūrādeva tu taṁ prekṣya rājaputro'bhyabhāṣata ,tvayā sārathinā pārthaḥ khāṇḍave'gnimatarpayat.
english: Indeed, having seen him from afar, the prince (Yudhishthira) addressed him: 'With you as his charioteer, Arjuna (Pārtha) satisfied the fire (Agni) in the Khāṇḍava forest.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 10. Indeed, having seen him from afar, the prince (Yudhishthira) addressed him: 'With you as his charioteer, Arjuna (Pārtha) satisfied the fire (Agni) in the Khāṇḍava forest.'

sanskrit: पृथिवीमजयत्कृत्स्नां कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ।सैरन्ध्री त्वां समाचष्ट सा हि जानाति पाण्डवान् ॥११॥
transliteration: pṛthivīmajayatkṛtsnāṁ kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ ,sairandhrī tvāṁ samācaṣṭa sā hi jānāti pāṇḍavān.
english: The son of Kuntī, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), conquered the entire earth. Sairandhrī (Draupadi) has spoken about you; indeed, she knows the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 11. The son of Kuntī, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), conquered the entire earth. Sairandhrī (Draupadi) has spoken about you; indeed, she knows the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: संयच्छ मामकानश्वांस्तथैव त्वं बृहन्नडे ।कुरुभिर्योत्स्यमानस्य गोधनानि परीप्सतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁyaccha māmakānaśvāṁstathaiva tvaṁ bṛhannaḍe ,kurubhiryotsyamānasya godhanāni parīpsataḥ.
english: O Bṛhannadā, you must control my horses in the same manner, as I am about to fight the Kurus and desire to recover the cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 12. O Bṛhannadā, you must control my horses in the same manner, as I am about to fight the Kurus and desire to recover the cattle.

sanskrit: अर्जुनस्य किलासीस्त्वं सारथिर्दयितः पुरा ।त्वयाजयत्सहायेन पृथिवीं पाण्डवर्षभः ॥१३॥
transliteration: arjunasya kilāsīstvaṁ sārathirdayitaḥ purā ,tvayājayatsahāyena pṛthivīṁ pāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ.
english: Indeed, it is said that you were formerly Arjuna's beloved charioteer. With your assistance, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas conquered the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 13. Indeed, it is said that you were formerly Arjuna's beloved charioteer. With your assistance, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas conquered the earth.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ता प्रत्युवाच राजपुत्रं बृहन्नडा ।का शक्तिर्मम सारथ्यं कर्तुं संग्राममूर्धनि ॥१४॥
transliteration: evamuktā pratyuvāca rājaputraṁ bṛhannaḍā ,kā śaktirmama sārathyaṁ kartuṁ saṁgrāmamūrdhani.
english: Having been addressed in this manner, Bṛhannadā replied to the prince, 'What ability do I possess to perform charioteering at the forefront of battle?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 14. Having been addressed in this manner, Bṛhannadā replied to the prince, 'What ability do I possess to perform charioteering at the forefront of battle?'

sanskrit: गीतं वा यदि वा नृत्तं वादित्रं वा पृथग्विधम् ।तत्करिष्यामि भद्रं ते सारथ्यं तु कुतो मयि ॥१५॥
transliteration: gītaṁ vā yadi vā nṛttaṁ vāditraṁ vā pṛthagvidham ,tatkariṣyāmi bhadraṁ te sārathyaṁ tu kuto mayi.
english: Singing, or dancing, or instrumental music of various kinds - that I will do. May it be well with you! But how can charioteering be found in me?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 15. Singing, or dancing, or instrumental music of various kinds - that I will do. May it be well with you! But how can charioteering be found in me?

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।बृहन्नडे गायनो वा नर्तनो वा पुनर्भव ।क्षिप्रं मे रथमास्थाय निगृह्णीष्व हयोत्तमान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,bṛhannaḍe gāyano vā nartano vā punarbhava ,kṣipraṁ me rathamāsthāya nigṛhṇīṣva hayottamān.
english: Uttara said: "Bṛhannadā, whether you are a singer or a dancer, become a charioteer again! Quickly mount my chariot and rein in the best horses."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 16. Uttara said: "Bṛhannadā, whether you are a singer or a dancer, become a charioteer again! Quickly mount my chariot and rein in the best horses."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स तत्र नर्मसंयुक्तमकरोत्पाण्डवो बहु ।उत्तरायाः प्रमुखतः सर्वं जानन्नरिंदम ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa tatra narmasaṁyuktamakarotpāṇḍavo bahu ,uttarāyāḥ pramukhataḥ sarvaṁ jānannariṁdama.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "There, the son of Pāṇḍu, Arjuna, knowing everything and facing Uttara, performed many playful (narmasaṃyukta) actions."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 17. Vaiśampāyana said: "There, the son of Pāṇḍu, Arjuna, knowing everything and facing Uttara, performed many playful (narmasaṃyukta) actions."

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वमुत्क्षिप्य कवचं शरीरे प्रत्यमुञ्चत ।कुमार्यस्तत्र तं दृष्ट्वा प्राहसन्पृथुलोचनाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: ūrdhvamutkṣipya kavacaṁ śarīre pratyamuñcata ,kumāryastatra taṁ dṛṣṭvā prāhasanpṛthulocanāḥ.
english: Having thrown his armor upwards, he put it on his body. Seeing him there, the wide-eyed maidens laughed aloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 18. Having thrown his armor upwards, he put it on his body. Seeing him there, the wide-eyed maidens laughed aloud.

sanskrit: स तु दृष्ट्वा विमुह्यन्तं स्वयमेवोत्तरस्ततः ।कवचेन महार्हेण समनह्यद्बृहन्नडाम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa tu dṛṣṭvā vimuhyantaṁ svayamevottarastataḥ ,kavacena mahārheṇa samanahyadbṛhannaḍām.
english: But Uttara himself, seeing him (Bṛhannadā) becoming bewildered, then fastened Bṛhannadā with a very valuable armor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 19. But Uttara himself, seeing him (Bṛhannadā) becoming bewildered, then fastened Bṛhannadā with a very valuable armor.

sanskrit: स बिभ्रत्कवचं चाग्र्यं स्वयमप्यंशुमत्प्रभम् ।ध्वजं च सिंहमुच्छ्रित्य सारथ्ये समकल्पयत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa bibhratkavacaṁ cāgryaṁ svayamapyaṁśumatprabham ,dhvajaṁ ca siṁhamucchritya sārathye samakalpayat.
english: He, wearing the excellent armor that shone like the sun, and having hoisted a lion banner, prepared himself for charioteering.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 20. He, wearing the excellent armor that shone like the sun, and having hoisted a lion banner, prepared himself for charioteering.

sanskrit: धनूंषि च महार्हाणि बाणांश्च रुचिरान्बहून् ।आदाय प्रययौ वीरः स बृहन्नडसारथिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: dhanūṁṣi ca mahārhāṇi bāṇāṁśca rucirānbahūn ,ādāya prayayau vīraḥ sa bṛhannaḍasārathiḥ.
english: That hero, whose charioteer was Brihannala, taking many valuable bows and beautiful arrows, went forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 21. That hero, whose charioteer was Brihannala, taking many valuable bows and beautiful arrows, went forth.

sanskrit: अथोत्तरा च कन्याश्च सख्यस्तामब्रुवंस्तदा ।बृहन्नडे आनयेथा वासांसि रुचिराणि नः ॥२२॥
transliteration: athottarā ca kanyāśca sakhyastāmabruvaṁstadā ,bṛhannaḍe ānayethā vāsāṁsi rucirāṇi naḥ.
english: Then Uttara and her female friends said to her (Brihannala), "O Brihannala, please bring beautiful clothes for us."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 22. Then Uttara and her female friends said to her (Brihannala), "O Brihannala, please bring beautiful clothes for us."

sanskrit: पाञ्चालिकार्थं सूक्ष्माणि चित्राणि विविधानि च ।विजित्य संग्रामगतान्भीष्मद्रोणमुखान्कुरून् ॥२३॥
transliteration: pāñcālikārthaṁ sūkṣmāṇi citrāṇi vividhāni ca ,vijitya saṁgrāmagatānbhīṣmadroṇamukhānkurūn.
english: ...fine, colorful, and diverse clothes for our dolls, after you have defeated the Kurus led by Bhishma and Drona, who have gone to battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 23. ...fine, colorful, and diverse clothes for our dolls, after you have defeated the Kurus led by Bhishma and Drona, who have gone to battle.

sanskrit: अथ ता ब्रुवतीः कन्याः सहिताः पाण्डुनन्दनः ।प्रत्युवाच हसन्पार्थो मेघदुन्दुभिनिःस्वनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: atha tā bruvatīḥ kanyāḥ sahitāḥ pāṇḍunandanaḥ ,pratyuvāca hasanpārtho meghadundubhiniḥsvanaḥ.
english: Then Pārtha, the son of Pāṇḍu, whose voice resonated like a thunder-drum, laughed and replied to those maidens who were speaking together.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 24. Then Pārtha, the son of Pāṇḍu, whose voice resonated like a thunder-drum, laughed and replied to those maidens who were speaking together.

sanskrit: यद्युत्तरोऽयं संग्रामे विजेष्यति महारथान् ।अथाहरिष्ये वासांसि दिव्यानि रुचिराणि च ॥२५॥
transliteration: yadyuttaro'yaṁ saṁgrāme vijeṣyati mahārathān ,athāhariṣye vāsāṁsi divyāni rucirāṇi ca.
english: If this Uttara conquers the great charioteers in battle, then I will procure splendid and beautiful garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 25. If this Uttara conquers the great charioteers in battle, then I will procure splendid and beautiful garments.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु बीभत्सुस्ततः प्राचोदयद्धयान् ।कुरूनभिमुखाञ्शूरो नानाध्वजपताकिनः ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tu bībhatsustataḥ prācodayaddhayān ,kurūnabhimukhāñśūro nānādhvajapatākinaḥ.
english: Having thus spoken, the hero Bībhatsu, adorned with various banners and flags, then urged his horses forward, towards the Kurus who were facing them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 35 Verse 26. Having thus spoken, the hero Bībhatsu, adorned with various banners and flags, then urged his horses forward, towards the Kurus who were facing them.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स राजधान्या निर्याय वैराटिः पृथिवींजयः ।प्रयाहीत्यब्रवीत्सूतं यत्र ते कुरवो गताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa rājadhānyā niryāya vairāṭiḥ pṛthivīṁjayaḥ ,prayāhītyabravītsūtaṁ yatra te kuravo gatāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The son of Virāṭa, Pṛthivīṃjaya, having exited the capital, told his charioteer, "Go where those Kurus have gone!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: The son of Virāṭa, Pṛthivīṃjaya, having exited the capital, told his charioteer, "Go where those Kurus have gone!"

sanskrit: समवेतान्कुरून्यावज्जिगीषूनवजित्य वै ।गाश्चैषां क्षिप्रमादाय पुनरायामि स्वं पुरम् ॥२॥
transliteration: samavetānkurūnyāvajjigīṣūnavajitya vai ,gāścaiṣāṁ kṣipramādāya punarāyāmi svaṁ puram.
english: Having indeed conquered all these assembled Kurus who are eager for battle, I will quickly seize their cows and return to my own city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 2. Having indeed conquered all these assembled Kurus who are eager for battle, I will quickly seize their cows and return to my own city.

sanskrit: ततस्तांश्चोदयामास सदश्वान्पाण्डुनन्दनः ।ते हया नरसिंहेन चोदिता वातरंहसः ।आलिखन्त इवाकाशमूहुः काञ्चनमालिनः ॥३॥
transliteration: tatastāṁścodayāmāsa sadaśvānpāṇḍunandanaḥ ,te hayā narasiṁhena coditā vātaraṁhasaḥ ,ālikhanta ivākāśamūhuḥ kāñcanamālinaḥ.
english: Thereupon, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) urged those excellent horses. Those steeds, driven by the lion among men (Arjuna) and swift as the wind, soared through the sky as if scraping it, adorned with golden harnesses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 3. Thereupon, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) urged those excellent horses. Those steeds, driven by the lion among men (Arjuna) and swift as the wind, soared through the sky as if scraping it, adorned with golden harnesses.

sanskrit: नातिदूरमथो यात्वा मत्स्यपुत्रधनंजयौ ।अवेक्षेताममित्रघ्नौ कुरूणां बलिनां बलम् ।श्मशानमभितो गत्वा आससाद कुरूनथ ॥४॥
transliteration: nātidūramatho yātvā matsyaputradhanaṁjayau ,avekṣetāmamitraghnau kurūṇāṁ balināṁ balam ,śmaśānamabhito gatvā āsasāda kurūnatha.
english: Not much further, having proceeded, the son of Matsya (Uttara) and Dhananjaya (Arjuna), those destroyers of foes, observed the mighty army of the Kurus. Then, having approached the cremation ground, he (Arjuna) finally reached the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 4. Not much further, having proceeded, the son of Matsya (Uttara) and Dhananjaya (Arjuna), those destroyers of foes, observed the mighty army of the Kurus. Then, having approached the cremation ground, he (Arjuna) finally reached the Kurus.

sanskrit: तदनीकं महत्तेषां विबभौ सागरस्वनम् ।सर्पमाणमिवाकाशे वनं बहुलपादपम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tadanīkaṁ mahatteṣāṁ vibabhau sāgarasvanam ,sarpamāṇamivākāśe vanaṁ bahulapādapam.
english: Their vast army, roaring like the ocean, appeared. It resembled a dense forest, abundant with trees, seemingly spreading across the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 5. Their vast army, roaring like the ocean, appeared. It resembled a dense forest, abundant with trees, seemingly spreading across the sky.

sanskrit: ददृशे पार्थिवो रेणुर्जनितस्तेन सर्पता ।दृष्टिप्रणाशो भूतानां दिवस्पृङ्नरसत्तम ॥६॥
transliteration: dadṛśe pārthivo reṇurjanitastena sarpatā ,dṛṣṭipraṇāśo bhūtānāṁ divaspṛṅnarasattama.
english: O best among men (narasattama), an earthly dust (pārthiva reṇu) was seen, generated by their movement, which touched the sky and destroyed the sight (dṛṣṭipraṇāśa) of all beings.
