search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 6. O best among men (narasattama), an earthly dust (pārthiva reṇu) was seen, generated by their movement, which touched the sky and destroyed the sight (dṛṣṭipraṇāśa) of all beings.

sanskrit: तदनीकं महद्दृष्ट्वा गजाश्वरथसंकुलम् ।कर्णदुर्योधनकृपैर्गुप्तं शांतनवेन च ॥७॥
transliteration: tadanīkaṁ mahaddṛṣṭvā gajāśvarathasaṁkulam ,karṇaduryodhanakṛpairguptaṁ śāṁtanavena ca.
english: Having seen that great army, crowded with elephants, horses, and chariots, protected by Karṇa, Duryodhana, Kṛpa, and the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma)...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 7. Having seen that great army, crowded with elephants, horses, and chariots, protected by Karṇa, Duryodhana, Kṛpa, and the son of Śāntanu (Bhīṣma)...

sanskrit: द्रोणेन च सपुत्रेण महेष्वासेन धीमता ।हृष्टरोमा भयोद्विग्नः पार्थं वैराटिरब्रवीत् ॥८॥
transliteration: droṇena ca saputreṇa maheṣvāsena dhīmatā ,hṛṣṭaromā bhayodvignaḥ pārthaṁ vairāṭirabravīt.
english: And by Droṇa, accompanied by his son, a great archer and wise man. The son of Virāṭa (vairāṭi), with hair standing on end and agitated by fear, then spoke to Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 8. And by Droṇa, accompanied by his son, a great archer and wise man. The son of Virāṭa (vairāṭi), with hair standing on end and agitated by fear, then spoke to Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: नोत्सहे कुरुभिर्योद्धुं रोमहर्षं हि पश्य मे ।बहुप्रवीरमत्युग्रं देवैरपि दुरासदम् ।प्रतियोद्धुं न शक्ष्यामि कुरुसैन्यमनन्तकम् ॥९॥
transliteration: notsahe kurubhiryoddhuṁ romaharṣaṁ hi paśya me ,bahupravīramatyugraṁ devairapi durāsadam ,pratiyoddhuṁ na śakṣyāmi kurusainyamanantakam.
english: I am unable to fight against the Kurus. Indeed, look at my hair standing on end (romaharṣa). I will not be able to fight back against this Kuru army (kurusainyam), which has many great heroes (bahupravīra), is extremely formidable, is invincible even by the gods, and is limitless (anantaka).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 9. I am unable to fight against the Kurus. Indeed, look at my hair standing on end (romaharṣa). I will not be able to fight back against this Kuru army (kurusainyam), which has many great heroes (bahupravīra), is extremely formidable, is invincible even by the gods, and is limitless (anantaka).

sanskrit: नाशंसे भारतीं सेनां प्रवेष्टुं भीमकार्मुकाम् ।रथनागाश्वकलिलां पत्तिध्वजसमाकुलाम् ।दृष्ट्वैव हि परानाजावात्मा प्रव्यथतीव मे ॥१०॥
transliteration: nāśaṁse bhāratīṁ senāṁ praveṣṭuṁ bhīmakārmukām ,rathanāgāśvakalilāṁ pattidhvajasamākulām ,dṛṣṭvaiva hi parānājāvātmā pravyathatīva me.
english: I do not wish to engage with the Bhārata army, which consists of formidable archers, is thronged with chariots, elephants, and horses, and is densely packed with infantry and banners. Indeed, merely seeing these adversaries on the battlefield, my inner being (ātman) becomes distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 10. I do not wish to engage with the Bhārata army, which consists of formidable archers, is thronged with chariots, elephants, and horses, and is densely packed with infantry and banners. Indeed, merely seeing these adversaries on the battlefield, my inner being (ātman) becomes distressed.

sanskrit: यत्र द्रोणश्च भीष्मश्च कृपः कर्णो विविंशतिः ।अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सोमदत्तोऽथ बाह्लिकः ॥११॥
transliteration: yatra droṇaśca bhīṣmaśca kṛpaḥ karṇo viviṁśatiḥ ,aśvatthāmā vikarṇaśca somadatto'tha bāhlikaḥ.
english: Where there are Drona, Bhishma, Kripa, Karna, Vivimshati, Ashwatthama, Vikarna, and then Somadatta and Balhika.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 11. Where there are Drona, Bhishma, Kripa, Karna, Vivimshati, Ashwatthama, Vikarna, and then Somadatta and Balhika.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तथा वीरो राजा च रथिनां वरः ।द्युतिमन्तो महेष्वासाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: duryodhanastathā vīro rājā ca rathināṁ varaḥ ,dyutimanto maheṣvāsāḥ sarve yuddhaviśāradāḥ.
english: Also, there is the heroic King Duryodhana, the best among charioteers. All of them are illustrious great archers, skilled in warfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 12. Also, there is the heroic King Duryodhana, the best among charioteers. All of them are illustrious great archers, skilled in warfare.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वैव हि कुरूनेतान्व्यूढानीकान्प्रहारिणः ।हृषितानि च रोमाणि कश्मलं चागतं मम ॥१३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvaiva hi kurūnetānvyūḍhānīkānprahāriṇaḥ ,hṛṣitāni ca romāṇi kaśmalaṁ cāgataṁ mama.
english: Indeed, merely seeing these Kurus, whose battle formations are arrayed and who are ready to strike, my body hairs have bristled, and bewilderment has overcome me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 13. Indeed, merely seeing these Kurus, whose battle formations are arrayed and who are ready to strike, my body hairs have bristled, and bewilderment has overcome me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अवियातो वियातस्य मौर्ख्याद्धूर्तस्य पश्यतः ।परिदेवयते मन्दः सकाशे सव्यसाचिनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,aviyāto viyātasya maurkhyāddhūrtasya paśyataḥ ,paridevayate mandaḥ sakāśe savyasācinaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The dull-witted Uttara, though himself (currently) unscattered, laments over the scattering (of his forces), which he attributes to the foolishness of the rogue (Duryodhana), in the presence of Arjuna (Savyasācin) who is observing (the situation).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 14. Vaiśampāyana said: The dull-witted Uttara, though himself (currently) unscattered, laments over the scattering (of his forces), which he attributes to the foolishness of the rogue (Duryodhana), in the presence of Arjuna (Savyasācin) who is observing (the situation).

sanskrit: त्रिगर्तान्मे पिता यातः शून्ये संप्रणिधाय माम् ।सर्वां सेनामुपादाय न मे सन्तीह सैनिकाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: trigartānme pitā yātaḥ śūnye saṁpraṇidhāya mām ,sarvāṁ senāmupādāya na me santīha sainikāḥ.
english: My father has gone to Trigarta, leaving me here alone in this deserted place. He took the entire army with him. Now I have no soldiers here.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 15. My father has gone to Trigarta, leaving me here alone in this deserted place. He took the entire army with him. Now I have no soldiers here.

sanskrit: सोऽहमेको बहून्बालः कृतास्त्रानकृतश्रमः ।प्रतियोद्धुं न शक्ष्यामि निवर्तस्व बृहन्नडे ॥१६॥
transliteration: so'hameko bahūnbālaḥ kṛtāstrānakṛtaśramaḥ ,pratiyoddhuṁ na śakṣyāmi nivartasva bṛhannaḍe.
english: Therefore, I, a mere boy, alone and inexperienced, will not be able to fight against so many who are skilled in weapons. Turn back, O Bṛhannalā!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 16. Therefore, I, a mere boy, alone and inexperienced, will not be able to fight against so many who are skilled in weapons. Turn back, O Bṛhannalā!

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।भयेन दीनरूपोऽसि द्विषतां हर्षवर्धनः ।न च तावत्कृतं किंचित्परैः कर्म रणाजिरे ॥१७॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,bhayena dīnarūpo'si dviṣatāṁ harṣavardhanaḥ ,na ca tāvatkṛtaṁ kiṁcitparaiḥ karma raṇājire.
english: Arjuna said: Due to fear, you appear miserable, thus increasing the joy of your enemies. Moreover, the enemies have not yet performed any deed on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 17. Arjuna said: Due to fear, you appear miserable, thus increasing the joy of your enemies. Moreover, the enemies have not yet performed any deed on the battlefield.

sanskrit: स्वयमेव च मामात्थ वह मां कौरवान्प्रति ।सोऽहं त्वां तत्र नेष्यामि यत्रैते बहुला ध्वजाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: svayameva ca māmāttha vaha māṁ kauravānprati ,so'haṁ tvāṁ tatra neṣyāmi yatraite bahulā dhvajāḥ.
english: You yourself have indeed told me, 'Drive me towards the Kauravas.' So, I will take you there, where these numerous banners stand.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 18. You yourself have indeed told me, 'Drive me towards the Kauravas.' So, I will take you there, where these numerous banners stand.

sanskrit: मध्यमामिषगृध्राणां कुरूणामाततायिनाम् ।नेष्यामि त्वां महाबाहो पृथिव्यामपि युध्यताम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: madhyamāmiṣagṛdhrāṇāṁ kurūṇāmātatāyinām ,neṣyāmi tvāṁ mahābāho pṛthivyāmapi yudhyatām.
english: O mighty-armed one, I will lead you to the Kauravas, those aggressors who are like vultures greedy for their central share, even as they fight on this earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 19. O mighty-armed one, I will lead you to the Kauravas, those aggressors who are like vultures greedy for their central share, even as they fight on this earth.

sanskrit: तथा स्त्रीषु प्रतिश्रुत्य पौरुषं पुरुषेषु च ।कत्थमानोऽभिनिर्याय किमर्थं न युयुत्ससे ॥२०॥
transliteration: tathā strīṣu pratiśrutya pauruṣaṁ puruṣeṣu ca ,katthamāno'bhiniryāya kimarthaṁ na yuyutsase.
english: Having thus promised your valor (pauruṣa) to women and boasted of it among men (puruṣa), and having come forth (to battle), why do you not desire to fight?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 20. Having thus promised your valor (pauruṣa) to women and boasted of it among men (puruṣa), and having come forth (to battle), why do you not desire to fight?

sanskrit: न चेद्विजित्य गास्तास्त्वं गृहान्वै प्रतियास्यसि ।प्रहसिष्यन्ति वीर त्वां नरा नार्यश्च संगताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: na cedvijitya gāstāstvaṁ gṛhānvai pratiyāsyasi ,prahasiṣyanti vīra tvāṁ narā nāryaśca saṁgatāḥ.
english: O hero, if you do not conquer those cows and return home, men and women, having gathered, will indeed laugh at you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 21. O hero, if you do not conquer those cows and return home, men and women, having gathered, will indeed laugh at you.

sanskrit: अहमप्यत्र सैरन्ध्र्या स्तुतः सारथ्यकर्मणि ।न हि शक्ष्याम्यनिर्जित्य गाः प्रयातुं पुरं प्रति ॥२२॥
transliteration: ahamapyatra sairandhryā stutaḥ sārathyakarmaṇi ,na hi śakṣyāmyanirjitya gāḥ prayātuṁ puraṁ prati.
english: Here, I have also been praised by Sairandhrī for my charioteering (karma). Indeed, I will not be able to return to the city without conquering the cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 22. Here, I have also been praised by Sairandhrī for my charioteering (karma). Indeed, I will not be able to return to the city without conquering the cows.

sanskrit: स्तोत्रेण चैव सैरन्ध्र्यास्तव वाक्येन तेन च ।कथं न युध्येयमहं कुरून्सर्वान्स्थिरो भव ॥२३॥
transliteration: stotreṇa caiva sairandhryāstava vākyena tena ca ,kathaṁ na yudhyeyamahaṁ kurūnsarvānsthiro bhava.
english: With the praise of Sairandhrī and with that statement of yours, how can I not fight all the Kauravas? Be firm!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 23. With the praise of Sairandhrī and with that statement of yours, how can I not fight all the Kauravas? Be firm!

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।कामं हरन्तु मत्स्यानां भूयांसं कुरवो धनम् ।प्रहसन्तु च मां नार्यो नरा वापि बृहन्नडे ॥२४॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,kāmaṁ harantu matsyānāṁ bhūyāṁsaṁ kuravo dhanam ,prahasantu ca māṁ nāryo narā vāpi bṛhannaḍe.
english: Uttara said: 'Let the Kauravas freely carry away a great deal of wealth from the Matsyas. And let women or even men laugh at me, O Bṛhannalā!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 24. Uttara said: 'Let the Kauravas freely carry away a great deal of wealth from the Matsyas. And let women or even men laugh at me, O Bṛhannalā!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा प्राद्रवद्भीतो रथात्प्रस्कन्द्य कुण्डली ।त्यक्त्वा मानं स मन्दात्मा विसृज्य सशरं धनुः ॥२५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā prādravadbhīto rathātpraskandya kuṇḍalī ,tyaktvā mānaṁ sa mandātmā visṛjya saśaraṁ dhanuḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Having spoken thus, that fearful one, still wearing his earrings (kuṇḍalī), leaped down from the chariot and fled. That faint-hearted person (manda-ātman), abandoning his dignity, cast aside his bow with arrows.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 25. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Having spoken thus, that fearful one, still wearing his earrings (kuṇḍalī), leaped down from the chariot and fled. That faint-hearted person (manda-ātman), abandoning his dignity, cast aside his bow with arrows.'

sanskrit: बृहन्नडोवाच ।नैष पूर्वैः स्मृतो धर्मः क्षत्रियस्य पलायनम् ।श्रेयस्ते मरणं युद्धे न भीतस्य पलायनम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: bṛhannaḍovāca ,naiṣa pūrvaiḥ smṛto dharmaḥ kṣatriyasya palāyanam ,śreyaste maraṇaṁ yuddhe na bhītasya palāyanam.
english: Bṛhannalā said: The elders have not considered this flight to be the natural law (dharma) for a warrior (kṣatriya). It is better for you to die in battle, rather than to flee out of fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 26. Bṛhannalā said: The elders have not considered this flight to be the natural law (dharma) for a warrior (kṣatriya). It is better for you to die in battle, rather than to flee out of fear.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा तु कौन्तेयः सोऽवप्लुत्य रथोत्तमात् ।तमन्वधावद्धावन्तं राजपुत्रं धनंजयः ।दीर्घां वेणीं विधुन्वानः साधु रक्ते च वाससी ॥२७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā tu kaunteyaḥ so'vaplutya rathottamāt ,tamanvadhāvaddhāvantaṁ rājaputraṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,dīrghāṁ veṇīṁ vidhunvānaḥ sādhu rakte ca vāsasī.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, the son of Kunti (Arjuna) leapt down from the excellent chariot. Dhanañjaya then pursued the fleeing prince, shaking his long braid and his very red garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 27. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having spoken thus, the son of Kunti (Arjuna) leapt down from the excellent chariot. Dhanañjaya then pursued the fleeing prince, shaking his long braid and his very red garments.

sanskrit: विधूय वेणीं धावन्तमजानन्तोऽर्जुनं तदा ।सैनिकाः प्राहसन्केचित्तथारूपमवेक्ष्य तम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: vidhūya veṇīṁ dhāvantamajānanto'rjunaṁ tadā ,sainikāḥ prāhasankecittathārūpamavekṣya tam.
english: At that time, seeing him running and shaking his braid in that particular form, some soldiers laughed aloud, not knowing that it was Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 28. At that time, seeing him running and shaking his braid in that particular form, some soldiers laughed aloud, not knowing that it was Arjuna.

sanskrit: तं शीघ्रमभिधावन्तं संप्रेक्ष्य कुरवोऽब्रुवन् ।क एष वेषप्रच्छन्नो भस्मनेव हुताशनः ॥२९॥
transliteration: taṁ śīghramabhidhāvantaṁ saṁprekṣya kuravo'bruvan ,ka eṣa veṣapracchanno bhasmaneva hutāśanaḥ.
english: Having observed him (Arjuna) running swiftly, the Kurus asked, "Who is this person hidden in disguise, like a fire concealed by ashes?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 29. Having observed him (Arjuna) running swiftly, the Kurus asked, "Who is this person hidden in disguise, like a fire concealed by ashes?"

sanskrit: किंचिदस्य यथा पुंसः किंचिदस्य यथा स्त्रियः ।सारूप्यमर्जुनस्येव क्लीबरूपं बिभर्ति च ॥३०॥
transliteration: kiṁcidasya yathā puṁsaḥ kiṁcidasya yathā striyaḥ ,sārūpyamarjunasyeva klībarūpaṁ bibharti ca.
english: Some of his features are like a man's, and some are like a woman's. Indeed, his resemblance is like Arjuna's, yet he also maintains the form of a eunuch.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 30. Some of his features are like a man's, and some are like a woman's. Indeed, his resemblance is like Arjuna's, yet he also maintains the form of a eunuch.

sanskrit: तदेवैतच्छिरोग्रीवं तौ बाहू परिघोपमौ ।तद्वदेवास्य विक्रान्तं नायमन्यो धनंजयात् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tadevaitacchirogrīvaṁ tau bāhū parighopamau ,tadvadevāsya vikrāntaṁ nāyamanyo dhanaṁjayāt.
english: His head and neck are indeed just like that, and his two arms resemble maces. Similarly, his valor is exactly the same; this person is none other than Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 31. His head and neck are indeed just like that, and his two arms resemble maces. Similarly, his valor is exactly the same; this person is none other than Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अमरेष्विव देवेन्द्रो मानुषेषु धनंजयः ।एकः कोऽस्मानुपायायादन्यो लोके धनंजयात् ॥३२॥
transliteration: amareṣviva devendro mānuṣeṣu dhanaṁjayaḥ ,ekaḥ ko'smānupāyāyādanyo loke dhanaṁjayāt.
english: Among the immortals, he is like Devendra (Indra); among humans, he is Dhanañjaya (Arjuna). Who else in this world, acting alone, could approach us other than Dhanañjaya (Arjuna)?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 32. Among the immortals, he is like Devendra (Indra); among humans, he is Dhanañjaya (Arjuna). Who else in this world, acting alone, could approach us other than Dhanañjaya (Arjuna)?

sanskrit: एकः पुत्रो विराटस्य शून्ये संनिहितः पुरे ।स एष किल निर्यातो बालभावान्न पौरुषात् ॥३३॥
transliteration: ekaḥ putro virāṭasya śūnye saṁnihitaḥ pure ,sa eṣa kila niryāto bālabhāvānna pauruṣāt.
english: The single son of Virata, who remained in the empty city, has indeed come out, driven by childishness and not by true valor (pauruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 33. The single son of Virata, who remained in the empty city, has indeed come out, driven by childishness and not by true valor (pauruṣa).

sanskrit: सत्रेण नूनं छन्नं हि चरन्तं पार्थमर्जुनम् ।उत्तरः सारथिं कृत्वा निर्यातो नगराद्बहिः ॥३४॥
transliteration: satreṇa nūnaṁ channaṁ hi carantaṁ pārthamarjunam ,uttaraḥ sārathiṁ kṛtvā niryāto nagarādbahiḥ.
english: Uttara certainly went out of the city, having made Arjuna, who was indeed moving about hidden by his disguise, his charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 34. Uttara certainly went out of the city, having made Arjuna, who was indeed moving about hidden by his disguise, his charioteer.

sanskrit: स नो मन्ये ध्वजान्दृष्ट्वा भीत एष पलायति ।तं नूनमेष धावन्तं जिघृक्षति धनंजयः ॥३५॥
transliteration: sa no manye dhvajāndṛṣṭvā bhīta eṣa palāyati ,taṁ nūnameṣa dhāvantaṁ jighṛkṣati dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: “I think this Uttara, having seen our banners, is afraid and is fleeing. Surely, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) wishes to seize him as he runs.”
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 35. “I think this Uttara, having seen our banners, is afraid and is fleeing. Surely, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) wishes to seize him as he runs.”

sanskrit: इति स्म कुरवः सर्वे विमृशन्तः पृथक्पृथक् ।न च व्यवसितुं किंचिदुत्तरं शक्नुवन्ति ते ।छन्नं तथा तं सत्रेण पाण्डवं प्रेक्ष्य भारत ॥३६॥
transliteration: iti sma kuravaḥ sarve vimṛśantaḥ pṛthakpṛthak ,na ca vyavasituṁ kiṁciduttaraṁ śaknuvanti te ,channaṁ tathā taṁ satreṇa pāṇḍavaṁ prekṣya bhārata.
english: Thus, all the Kauravas, deliberating individually, were unable to decide anything about Uttara, O Bhārata, after seeing that son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) thus concealed by his disguise.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 36. Thus, all the Kauravas, deliberating individually, were unable to decide anything about Uttara, O Bhārata, after seeing that son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) thus concealed by his disguise.

sanskrit: उत्तरं तु प्रधावन्तमनुद्रुत्य धनंजयः ।गत्वा पदशतं तूर्णं केशपक्षे परामृशत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: uttaraṁ tu pradhāvantamanudrutya dhanaṁjayaḥ ,gatvā padaśataṁ tūrṇaṁ keśapakṣe parāmṛśat.
english: But Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), having swiftly pursued Uttara as he ran, went a hundred steps and quickly seized him by his lock of hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 37. But Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), having swiftly pursued Uttara as he ran, went a hundred steps and quickly seized him by his lock of hair.

sanskrit: सोऽर्जुनेन परामृष्टः पर्यदेवयदार्तवत् ।बहुलं कृपणं चैव विराटस्य सुतस्तदा ॥३८॥
transliteration: so'rjunena parāmṛṣṭaḥ paryadevayadārtavat ,bahulaṁ kṛpaṇaṁ caiva virāṭasya sutastadā.
english: Then, the son of Virāṭa, seized by Arjuna, lamented profusely and piteously, as if in great distress.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 38. Then, the son of Virāṭa, seized by Arjuna, lamented profusely and piteously, as if in great distress.

sanskrit: शातकुम्भस्य शुद्धस्य शतं निष्कान्ददामि ते ।मणीनष्टौ च वैडूर्यान्हेमबद्धान्महाप्रभान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: śātakumbhasya śuddhasya śataṁ niṣkāndadāmi te ,maṇīnaṣṭau ca vaiḍūryānhemabaddhānmahāprabhān.
english: I will give you a hundred pure gold (śātakhumbha) coins (niṣka) and eight cat's eye gems (vaiḍūrya), set in gold and greatly lustrous.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 39. I will give you a hundred pure gold (śātakhumbha) coins (niṣka) and eight cat's eye gems (vaiḍūrya), set in gold and greatly lustrous.

sanskrit: हेमदण्डप्रतिच्छन्नं रथं युक्तं च सुव्रजैः ।मत्तांश्च दश मातङ्गान्मुञ्च मां त्वं बृहन्नडे ॥४०॥
transliteration: hemadaṇḍapraticchannaṁ rathaṁ yuktaṁ ca suvrajaiḥ ,mattāṁśca daśa mātaṅgānmuñca māṁ tvaṁ bṛhannaḍe.
english: O Bṛhannalā, release me (and I will give you) a chariot adorned with golden shafts and equipped with excellent horses, and ten intoxicated elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 40. O Bṛhannalā, release me (and I will give you) a chariot adorned with golden shafts and equipped with excellent horses, and ten intoxicated elephants.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमादीनि वाक्यानि विलपन्तमचेतसम् ।प्रहस्य पुरुषव्याघ्रो रथस्यान्तिकमानयत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamādīni vākyāni vilapantamacetasam ,prahasya puruṣavyāghro rathasyāntikamānayat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: As [the prince] was uttering such words, lamenting and bewildered, the tiger among men (Arjuna) laughed heartily and brought him near the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 41. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: As [the prince] was uttering such words, lamenting and bewildered, the tiger among men (Arjuna) laughed heartily and brought him near the chariot.

sanskrit: अथैनमब्रवीत्पार्थो भयार्तं नष्टचेतसम् ।यदि नोत्सहसे योद्धुं शत्रुभिः शत्रुकर्शन ।एहि मे त्वं हयान्यच्छ युध्यमानस्य शत्रुभिः ॥४२॥
transliteration: athainamabravītpārtho bhayārtaṁ naṣṭacetasam ,yadi notsahase yoddhuṁ śatrubhiḥ śatrukarśana ,ehi me tvaṁ hayānyaccha yudhyamānasya śatrubhiḥ.
english: Then Arjuna (Pārtha) spoke to him, who was tormented by fear and had lost his composure: "If you are unable to fight the enemies, O vanquisher of foes, then come and drive my horses while I engage in battle with them."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 42. Then Arjuna (Pārtha) spoke to him, who was tormented by fear and had lost his composure: "If you are unable to fight the enemies, O vanquisher of foes, then come and drive my horses while I engage in battle with them."

sanskrit: प्रयाह्येतद्रथानीकं मद्बाहुबलरक्षितः ।अप्रधृष्यतमं घोरं गुप्तं वीरैर्महारथैः ॥४३॥
transliteration: prayāhyetadrathānīkaṁ madbāhubalarakṣitaḥ ,apradhṛṣyatamaṁ ghoraṁ guptaṁ vīrairmahārathaiḥ.
english: You, protected by the strength of my arms, proceed into this chariot division, which is most invincible, dreadful, and guarded by great warriors (mahārathas).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 43. You, protected by the strength of my arms, proceed into this chariot division, which is most invincible, dreadful, and guarded by great warriors (mahārathas).

sanskrit: मा भैस्त्वं राजपुत्राग्र्य क्षत्रियोऽसि परंतप ।अहं वै कुरुभिर्योत्स्याम्यवजेष्यामि ते पशून् ॥४४॥
transliteration: mā bhaistvaṁ rājaputrāgrya kṣatriyo'si paraṁtapa ,ahaṁ vai kurubhiryotsyāmyavajeṣyāmi te paśūn.
english: Do not fear, O foremost among princes! You are a warrior (kṣatriya), O scorcher of foes. I will indeed fight with the Kurus and will recover your cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 44. Do not fear, O foremost among princes! You are a warrior (kṣatriya), O scorcher of foes. I will indeed fight with the Kurus and will recover your cattle.

sanskrit: प्रविश्यैतद्रथानीकमप्रधृष्यं दुरासदम् ।यन्ता भूस्त्वं नरश्रेष्ठ योत्स्येऽहं कुरुभिः सह ॥४५॥
transliteration: praviśyaitadrathānīkamapradhṛṣyaṁ durāsadam ,yantā bhūstvaṁ naraśreṣṭha yotsye'haṁ kurubhiḥ saha.
english: Having entered this chariot division, which is unassailable and difficult to approach, you, O best among men, become the charioteer. I will fight with the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 45. Having entered this chariot division, which is unassailable and difficult to approach, you, O best among men, become the charioteer. I will fight with the Kurus.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवाणो बीभत्सुर्वैराटिमपराजितः ।समाश्वास्य मुहूर्तं तमुत्तरं भरतर्षभ ॥४६॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvāṇo bībhatsurvairāṭimaparājitaḥ ,samāśvāsya muhūrtaṁ tamuttaraṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Arjuna (Bībhatsu), the undefeated one, speaking thus, consoled Uttara, the son of Virāṭa (Vairāṭi), for a moment, O best of Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 46. Arjuna (Bībhatsu), the undefeated one, speaking thus, consoled Uttara, the son of Virāṭa (Vairāṭi), for a moment, O best of Bharatas.

sanskrit: तत एनं विचेष्टन्तमकामं भयपीडितम् ।रथमारोपयामास पार्थः प्रहरतां वरः ॥४७॥
transliteration: tata enaṁ viceṣṭantamakāmaṁ bhayapīḍitam ,rathamāropayāmāsa pārthaḥ praharatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna (Pārtha), the foremost among warriors, mounted him – struggling, unwilling, and afflicted by fear – onto the chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 36 Verse 47. Then, Arjuna (Pārtha), the foremost among warriors, mounted him – struggling, unwilling, and afflicted by fear – onto the chariot.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तं दृष्ट्वा क्लीबवेषेण रथस्थं नरपुंगवम् ।शमीमभिमुखं यान्तं रथमारोप्य चोत्तरम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taṁ dṛṣṭvā klībaveṣeṇa rathasthaṁ narapuṁgavam ,śamīmabhimukhaṁ yāntaṁ rathamāropya cottaram.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: And, after mounting Uttara onto the chariot, (Arjuna), that best of men, was seen in a eunuch's guise on the chariot, going towards the śamī tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: And, after mounting Uttara onto the chariot, (Arjuna), that best of men, was seen in a eunuch's guise on the chariot, going towards the śamī tree.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणमुखास्तत्र कुरूणां रथसत्तमाः ।वित्रस्तमनसः सर्वे धनंजयकृताद्भयात् ॥२॥
transliteration: bhīṣmadroṇamukhāstatra kurūṇāṁ rathasattamāḥ ,vitrastamanasaḥ sarve dhanaṁjayakṛtādbhayāt.
english: There, all the foremost chariot-warriors of the Kurus, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, had their minds terrified by the fear caused by Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 2. There, all the foremost chariot-warriors of the Kurus, led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa, had their minds terrified by the fear caused by Dhanañjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तानवेक्ष्य हतोत्साहानुत्पातानपि चाद्भुतान् ।गुरुः शस्त्रभृतां श्रेष्ठो भारद्वाजोऽभ्यभाषत ॥३॥
transliteration: tānavekṣya hatotsāhānutpātānapi cādbhutān ,guruḥ śastrabhṛtāṁ śreṣṭho bhāradvājo'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Seeing them dispirited and also the strange portents, Droṇa, the preceptor (guru) and foremost among weapon-bearers, spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 3. Seeing them dispirited and also the strange portents, Droṇa, the preceptor (guru) and foremost among weapon-bearers, spoke.

sanskrit: चलाश्च वाताः संवान्ति रूक्षाः परुषनिःस्वनाः ।भस्मवर्णप्रकाशेन तमसा संवृतं नभः ॥४॥
transliteration: calāśca vātāḥ saṁvānti rūkṣāḥ paruṣaniḥsvanāḥ ,bhasmavarṇaprakāśena tamasā saṁvṛtaṁ nabhaḥ.
english: The winds blow unsteadily, rough and with harsh sounds. The sky is enveloped in darkness with an ash-colored glow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 4. The winds blow unsteadily, rough and with harsh sounds. The sky is enveloped in darkness with an ash-colored glow.

sanskrit: रूक्षवर्णाश्च जलदा दृश्यन्तेऽद्भुतदर्शनाः ।निःसरन्ति च कोशेभ्यः शस्त्राणि विविधानि च ॥५॥
transliteration: rūkṣavarṇāśca jaladā dṛśyante'dbhutadarśanāḥ ,niḥsaranti ca kośebhyaḥ śastrāṇi vividhāni ca.
english: The clouds appear of rough color and strange appearance. And various weapons emerge from their scabbards.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 5. The clouds appear of rough color and strange appearance. And various weapons emerge from their scabbards.

sanskrit: शिवाश्च विनदन्त्येता दीप्तायां दिशि दारुणाः ।हयाश्चाश्रूणि मुञ्चन्ति ध्वजाः कम्पन्त्यकम्पिताः ॥६॥
transliteration: śivāśca vinadantyetā dīptāyāṁ diśi dāruṇāḥ ,hayāścāśrūṇi muñcanti dhvajāḥ kampantyakampitāḥ.
english: And these terrifying jackals howl in the blazing direction. Horses shed tears, and banners, though unshaken (by wind), tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 6. And these terrifying jackals howl in the blazing direction. Horses shed tears, and banners, though unshaken (by wind), tremble.

sanskrit: यादृशान्यत्र रूपाणि संदृश्यन्ते बहून्यपि ।यत्ता भवन्तस्तिष्ठन्तु स्याद्युद्धं समुपस्थितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: yādṛśānyatra rūpāṇi saṁdṛśyante bahūnyapi ,yattā bhavantastiṣṭhantu syādyuddhaṁ samupasthitam.
english: Even though many such forms are visible here, all of you should remain vigilant, for war might be at hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 7. Even though many such forms are visible here, all of you should remain vigilant, for war might be at hand.

sanskrit: रक्षध्वमपि चात्मानं व्यूहध्वं वाहिनीमपि ।वैशसं च प्रतीक्षध्वं रक्षध्वं चापि गोधनम् ॥८॥
transliteration: rakṣadhvamapi cātmānaṁ vyūhadhvaṁ vāhinīmapi ,vaiśasaṁ ca pratīkṣadhvaṁ rakṣadhvaṁ cāpi godhanam.
english: Protect yourselves (ātman) and also deploy the army. Await the impending slaughter, and also guard the cattle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 8. Protect yourselves (ātman) and also deploy the army. Await the impending slaughter, and also guard the cattle.

sanskrit: एष वीरो महेष्वासः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।आगतः क्लीबवेषेण पार्थो नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥९॥
transliteration: eṣa vīro maheṣvāsaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,āgataḥ klībaveṣeṇa pārtho nāstyatra saṁśayaḥ.
english: This hero, a great archer, the foremost among all weapon-bearers, has arrived in the disguise of a eunuch. This is Arjuna (Pārtha); there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 9. This hero, a great archer, the foremost among all weapon-bearers, has arrived in the disguise of a eunuch. This is Arjuna (Pārtha); there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: स एष पार्थो विक्रान्तः सव्यसाची परंतपः ।नायुद्धेन निवर्तेत सर्वैरपि मरुद्गणैः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sa eṣa pārtho vikrāntaḥ savyasācī paraṁtapaḥ ,nāyuddhena nivarteta sarvairapi marudgaṇaiḥ.
english: That very Arjuna (Pārtha) is mighty, ambidextrous (Savyasācī), and a tormentor of foes (Parantapa). He would not retreat without a battle, even if he were to face all the Maruts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 10. That very Arjuna (Pārtha) is mighty, ambidextrous (Savyasācī), and a tormentor of foes (Parantapa). He would not retreat without a battle, even if he were to face all the Maruts.

sanskrit: क्लेशितश्च वने शूरो वासवेन च शिक्षितः ।अमर्षवशमापन्नो योत्स्यते नात्र संशयः ॥११॥
transliteration: kleśitaśca vane śūro vāsavena ca śikṣitaḥ ,amarṣavaśamāpanno yotsyate nātra saṁśayaḥ.
english: That hero, who was tormented in the forest and trained by Indra, has now become overcome by indignation. He will certainly fight; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 11. That hero, who was tormented in the forest and trained by Indra, has now become overcome by indignation. He will certainly fight; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: नेहास्य प्रतियोद्धारमहं पश्यामि कौरवाः ।महादेवोऽपि पार्थेन श्रूयते युधि तोषितः ॥१२॥
transliteration: nehāsya pratiyoddhāramahaṁ paśyāmi kauravāḥ ,mahādevo'pi pārthena śrūyate yudhi toṣitaḥ.
english: O Kauravas, I do not see anyone who can contend with him here. It is said that even Mahadeva was propitiated by Partha in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 12. O Kauravas, I do not see anyone who can contend with him here. It is said that even Mahadeva was propitiated by Partha in battle.

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।सदा भवान्फल्गुनस्य गुणैरस्मान्विकत्थसे ।न चार्जुनः कला पूर्णा मम दुर्योधनस्य वा ॥१३॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,sadā bhavānphalgunasya guṇairasmānvikatthase ,na cārjunaḥ kalā pūrṇā mama duryodhanasya vā.
english: Karṇa said: You always boast to us about Arjuna's qualities. But Arjuna's abilities are not even a complete fraction of mine or Duryodhana's.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 13. Karṇa said: You always boast to us about Arjuna's qualities. But Arjuna's abilities are not even a complete fraction of mine or Duryodhana's.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।यद्येष पार्थो राधेय कृतं कार्यं भवेन्मम ।ज्ञाताः पुनश्चरिष्यन्ति द्वादशान्यान्हि वत्सरान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,yadyeṣa pārtho rādheya kṛtaṁ kāryaṁ bhavenmama ,jñātāḥ punaścariṣyanti dvādaśānyānhi vatsarān.
english: Duryodhana said: "O son of Radha (Karṇa), if this Partha were to achieve my purpose, then my relatives (the Pandavas) would indeed wander for another twelve years."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 14. Duryodhana said: "O son of Radha (Karṇa), if this Partha were to achieve my purpose, then my relatives (the Pandavas) would indeed wander for another twelve years."

sanskrit: अथैष कश्चिदेवान्यः क्लीबवेषेण मानवः ।शरैरेनं सुनिशितैः पातयिष्यामि भूतले ॥१५॥
transliteration: athaiṣa kaścidevānyaḥ klībaveṣeṇa mānavaḥ ,śarairenaṁ suniśitaiḥ pātayiṣyāmi bhūtale.
english: Now, this other person, who is merely a man disguised as an eunuch, I will make him fall to the ground with very sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 15. Now, this other person, who is merely a man disguised as an eunuch, I will make him fall to the ground with very sharp arrows.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मिन्ब्रुवति तद्वाक्यं धार्तराष्ट्रे परंतपे ।भीष्मो द्रोणः कृपो द्रौणिः पौरुषं तदपूजयन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasminbruvati tadvākyaṁ dhārtarāṣṭre paraṁtape ,bhīṣmo droṇaḥ kṛpo drauṇiḥ pauruṣaṁ tadapūjayan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When that son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), the tormentor of foes, was speaking those words, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kṛpa, and Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) praised his valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 37 Verse 16. Vaiśampāyana said: When that son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), the tormentor of foes, was speaking those words, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kṛpa, and Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā) praised his valor.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तां शमीमुपसंगम्य पार्थो वैराटिमब्रवीत् ।सुकुमारं समाज्ञातं संग्रामे नातिकोविदम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tāṁ śamīmupasaṁgamya pārtho vairāṭimabravīt ,sukumāraṁ samājñātaṁ saṁgrāme nātikovidam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having approached that śami tree, Pārtha (Arjuna) said to the son of Virāṭa (Uttara), who was considered very delicate and not very skilled in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having approached that śami tree, Pārtha (Arjuna) said to the son of Virāṭa (Uttara), who was considered very delicate and not very skilled in battle.

sanskrit: समादिष्टो मया क्षिप्रं धनूंष्यवहरोत्तर ।नेमानि हि त्वदीयानि सोढुं शक्ष्यन्ति मे बलम् ॥२॥
transliteration: samādiṣṭo mayā kṣipraṁ dhanūṁṣyavaharottara ,nemāni hi tvadīyāni soḍhuṁ śakṣyanti me balam.
english: O Uttara! You have been commanded by me to quickly take away these bows. Indeed, these bows, which belong to you, will not be able to bear my strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 2. O Uttara! You have been commanded by me to quickly take away these bows. Indeed, these bows, which belong to you, will not be able to bear my strength.

sanskrit: भारं वापि गुरुं हर्तुं कुञ्जरं वा प्रमर्दितुम् ।मम वा बाहुविक्षेपं शत्रूनिह विजेष्यतः ॥३॥
transliteration: bhāraṁ vāpi guruṁ hartuṁ kuñjaraṁ vā pramarditum ,mama vā bāhuvikṣepaṁ śatrūniha vijeṣyataḥ.
english: My two (companions), by the exertion of their arms, will here conquer enemies, or carry a heavy burden, or crush an elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 3. My two (companions), by the exertion of their arms, will here conquer enemies, or carry a heavy burden, or crush an elephant.

sanskrit: तस्माद्भूमिंजयारोह शमीमेतां पलाशिनीम् ।अस्यां हि पाण्डुपुत्राणां धनूंषि निहितान्युत ॥४॥
transliteration: tasmādbhūmiṁjayāroha śamīmetāṁ palāśinīm ,asyāṁ hi pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ dhanūṁṣi nihitānyuta.
english: Therefore, O Bhumijaya, climb this leafy śamī tree, for indeed, the bows of the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas) are deposited in it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 4. Therefore, O Bhumijaya, climb this leafy śamī tree, for indeed, the bows of the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas) are deposited in it.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्य भीमस्य बीभत्सोर्यमयोस्तथा ।ध्वजाः शराश्च शूराणां दिव्यानि कवचानि च ॥५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasya bhīmasya bībhatsoryamayostathā ,dhvajāḥ śarāśca śūrāṇāṁ divyāni kavacāni ca.
english: Here are the banners, arrows, and divine armours of Yudhishthira, Bhima, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), and the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva)  -  these heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 5. Here are the banners, arrows, and divine armours of Yudhishthira, Bhima, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), and the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva)  -  these heroes.

sanskrit: अत्र चैतन्महावीर्यं धनुः पार्थस्य गाण्डिवम् ।एकं शतसहस्रेण संमितं राष्ट्रवर्धनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: atra caitanmahāvīryaṁ dhanuḥ pārthasya gāṇḍivam ,ekaṁ śatasahasreṇa saṁmitaṁ rāṣṭravardhanam.
english: And here is this greatly powerful bow of Partha (Arjuna), the Gaṇḍīva, which alone is comparable to a hundred thousand (other bows) and enhances the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 6. And here is this greatly powerful bow of Partha (Arjuna), the Gaṇḍīva, which alone is comparable to a hundred thousand (other bows) and enhances the kingdom.

sanskrit: व्यायामसहमत्यर्थं तृणराजसमं महत् ।सर्वायुधमहामात्रं शत्रुसंबाधकारकम् ॥७॥
transliteration: vyāyāmasahamatyarthaṁ tṛṇarājasamaṁ mahat ,sarvāyudhamahāmātraṁ śatrusaṁbādhakārakam.
english: It was suitable for intense practice, as vast as a royal palm, containing all great weapons, and causing distress to enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 7. It was suitable for intense practice, as vast as a royal palm, containing all great weapons, and causing distress to enemies.

sanskrit: सुवर्णविकृतं दिव्यं श्लक्ष्णमायतमव्रणम् ।अलं भारं गुरुं वोढुं दारुणं चारुदर्शनम् ।तादृशान्येव सर्वाणि बलवन्ति दृढानि च ॥८॥
transliteration: suvarṇavikṛtaṁ divyaṁ ślakṣṇamāyatamavraṇam ,alaṁ bhāraṁ guruṁ voḍhuṁ dāruṇaṁ cārudarśanam ,tādṛśānyeva sarvāṇi balavanti dṛḍhāni ca.
english: It was crafted from gold, divine, smooth, long, and without blemish. Capable of bearing a heavy weight, formidable yet beautiful to behold. All of them were similarly powerful and firm.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 8. It was crafted from gold, divine, smooth, long, and without blemish. Capable of bearing a heavy weight, formidable yet beautiful to behold. All of them were similarly powerful and firm.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।अस्मिन्वृक्षे किलोद्बद्धं शरीरमिति नः श्रुतम् ।तदहं राजपुत्रः सन्स्पृशेयं पाणिना कथम् ॥९॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,asminvṛkṣe kilodbaddhaṁ śarīramiti naḥ śrutam ,tadahaṁ rājaputraḥ sanspṛśeyaṁ pāṇinā katham.
english: Uttara said: "Indeed, we have heard that a body (corpse) is tied up in this tree. How, then, can I, being a prince, touch it with my hand?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 9. Uttara said: "Indeed, we have heard that a body (corpse) is tied up in this tree. How, then, can I, being a prince, touch it with my hand?"

sanskrit: नैवंविधं मया युक्तमालब्धुं क्षत्रयोनिना ।महता राजपुत्रेण मन्त्रयज्ञविदा सता ॥१०॥
transliteration: naivaṁvidhaṁ mayā yuktamālabdhuṁ kṣatrayoninā ,mahatā rājaputreṇa mantrayajñavidā satā.
english: It is not proper for me, who am of a warrior origin (kṣatriya) and a great prince, conversant with sacred utterances (mantra) and rituals (yajña), to touch such a thing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 10. It is not proper for me, who am of a warrior origin (kṣatriya) and a great prince, conversant with sacred utterances (mantra) and rituals (yajña), to touch such a thing.

sanskrit: स्पृष्टवन्तं शरीरं मां शववाहमिवाशुचिम् ।कथं वा व्यवहार्यं वै कुर्वीथास्त्वं बृहन्नडे ॥११॥
transliteration: spṛṣṭavantaṁ śarīraṁ māṁ śavavāhamivāśucim ,kathaṁ vā vyavahāryaṁ vai kurvīthāstvaṁ bṛhannaḍe.
english: O Bṛhannadā, how can you consider me, whose body has touched (the corpse) and is therefore impure (aśucim) like a corpse-bearer, to be fit for interaction?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 11. O Bṛhannadā, how can you consider me, whose body has touched (the corpse) and is therefore impure (aśucim) like a corpse-bearer, to be fit for interaction?

sanskrit: बृहन्नडोवाच ।व्यवहार्यश्च राजेन्द्र शुचिश्चैव भविष्यसि ।धनूंष्येतानि मा भैस्त्वं शरीरं नात्र विद्यते ॥१२॥
transliteration: bṛhannaḍovāca ,vyavahāryaśca rājendra śuciścaiva bhaviṣyasi ,dhanūṁṣyetāni mā bhaistvaṁ śarīraṁ nātra vidyate.
english: Bṛhannadā said: 'O King (rājendra), you will indeed be worthy of interaction and pure (śuci). Do not fear these bows; (the impure) body (śarīra) is not here (to contaminate you or be a source of fear).'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 12. Bṛhannadā said: 'O King (rājendra), you will indeed be worthy of interaction and pure (śuci). Do not fear these bows; (the impure) body (śarīra) is not here (to contaminate you or be a source of fear).'

sanskrit: दायादं मत्स्यराजस्य कुले जातं मनस्विनम् ।कथं त्वा निन्दितं कर्म कारयेयं नृपात्मज ॥१३॥
transliteration: dāyādaṁ matsyarājasya kule jātaṁ manasvinam ,kathaṁ tvā ninditaṁ karma kārayeyaṁ nṛpātmaja.
english: O Prince (nṛpātmaja), how can I make you, who are a noble (manasvinam) heir born in the lineage of the Matsya king, perform a blameworthy action (karma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 13. O Prince (nṛpātmaja), how can I make you, who are a noble (manasvinam) heir born in the lineage of the Matsya king, perform a blameworthy action (karma)?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तः स पार्थेन रथात्प्रस्कन्द्य कुण्डली ।आरुरोह शमीवृक्षं वैराटिरवशस्तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktaḥ sa pārthena rathātpraskandya kuṇḍalī ,āruroha śamīvṛkṣaṁ vairāṭiravaśastadā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Thus addressed by Pārtha (Arjuna), Uttara (vairāṭi), adorned with earrings (kuṇḍalī), then helplessly (avaśaḥ) jumped down from the chariot and climbed the śamī tree.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 14. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Thus addressed by Pārtha (Arjuna), Uttara (vairāṭi), adorned with earrings (kuṇḍalī), then helplessly (avaśaḥ) jumped down from the chariot and climbed the śamī tree.'

sanskrit: तमन्वशासच्छत्रुघ्नो रथे तिष्ठन्धनंजयः ।परिवेष्टनमेतेषां क्षिप्रं चैव व्यपानुद ॥१५॥
transliteration: tamanvaśāsacchatrughno rathe tiṣṭhandhanaṁjayaḥ ,pariveṣṭanameteṣāṁ kṣipraṁ caiva vyapānuda.
english: Shatrughna, standing in the chariot, instructed Dhananjaya (Arjuna): "Quickly undo the wrappings of these."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 15. Shatrughna, standing in the chariot, instructed Dhananjaya (Arjuna): "Quickly undo the wrappings of these."

sanskrit: तथा संनहनान्येषां परिमुच्य समन्ततः ।अपश्यद्गाण्डिवं तत्र चतुर्भिरपरैः सह ॥१६॥
transliteration: tathā saṁnahanānyeṣāṁ parimucya samantataḥ ,apaśyadgāṇḍivaṁ tatra caturbhiraparaiḥ saha.
english: Thus, having untied the wrappings of these (bows) from all sides, he saw the (bow) Gandiva there along with four other (bows).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 16. Thus, having untied the wrappings of these (bows) from all sides, he saw the (bow) Gandiva there along with four other (bows).

sanskrit: तेषां विमुच्यमानानां धनुषामर्कवर्चसाम् ।विनिश्चेरुः प्रभा दिव्या ग्रहाणामुदयेष्विव ॥१७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ vimucyamānānāṁ dhanuṣāmarkavarcasām ,viniśceruḥ prabhā divyā grahāṇāmudayeṣviva.
english: As those bows, shining with the brilliance of the sun, were being untied, divine splendors emerged from them, similar to (the light) at the rising of planets.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 17. As those bows, shining with the brilliance of the sun, were being untied, divine splendors emerged from them, similar to (the light) at the rising of planets.

sanskrit: स तेषां रूपमालोक्य भोगिनामिव जृम्भताम् ।हृष्टरोमा भयोद्विग्नः क्षणेन समपद्यत ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa teṣāṁ rūpamālokya bhogināmiva jṛmbhatām ,hṛṣṭaromā bhayodvignaḥ kṣaṇena samapadyata.
english: He, having observed their appearance, like that of stretching serpents, instantly became one whose hairs stood on end, agitated by fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 18. He, having observed their appearance, like that of stretching serpents, instantly became one whose hairs stood on end, agitated by fear.

sanskrit: संस्पृश्य तानि चापानि भानुमन्ति बृहन्ति च ।वैराटिरर्जुनं राजन्निदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁspṛśya tāni cāpāni bhānumanti bṛhanti ca ,vairāṭirarjunaṁ rājannidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Touching those resplendent and mighty bows, the son of Virāṭa, O King, spoke these words to Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 19. Touching those resplendent and mighty bows, the son of Virāṭa, O King, spoke these words to Arjuna.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।बिन्दवो जातरूपस्य शतं यस्मिन्निपातिताः ।सहस्रकोटि सौवर्णाः कस्यैतद्धनुरुत्तमम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,bindavo jātarūpasya śataṁ yasminnipātitāḥ ,sahasrakoṭi sauvarṇāḥ kasyaitaddhanuruttamam.
english: Uttara said: "On which bow are a hundred drops of pure gold inlaid, and a thousand crores of golden ornaments? Whose is this excellent bow?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 20. Uttara said: "On which bow are a hundred drops of pure gold inlaid, and a thousand crores of golden ornaments? Whose is this excellent bow?"

sanskrit: वारणा यस्य सौवर्णाः पृष्ठे भासन्ति दंशिताः ।सुपार्श्वं सुग्रहं चैव कस्यैतद्धनुरुत्तमम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: vāraṇā yasya sauvarṇāḥ pṛṣṭhe bhāsanti daṁśitāḥ ,supārśvaṁ sugrahaṁ caiva kasyaitaddhanuruttamam.
english: Whose bow has golden protective coverings shining on its back, and is both well-formed and easy to grip? Whose is this excellent bow?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 21. Whose bow has golden protective coverings shining on its back, and is both well-formed and easy to grip? Whose is this excellent bow?

sanskrit: तपनीयस्य शुद्धस्य षष्टिर्यस्येन्द्रगोपकाः ।पृष्ठे विभक्ताः शोभन्ते कस्यैतद्धनुरुत्तमम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tapanīyasya śuddhasya ṣaṣṭiryasyendragopakāḥ ,pṛṣṭhe vibhaktāḥ śobhante kasyaitaddhanuruttamam.
english: On whose bow do sixty 'indragopaka' gems of pure refined gold (tapanīya) shine, beautifully arranged on its back? Whose is this excellent bow?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 22. On whose bow do sixty 'indragopaka' gems of pure refined gold (tapanīya) shine, beautifully arranged on its back? Whose is this excellent bow?

sanskrit: सूर्या यत्र च सौवर्णास्त्रयो भासन्ति दंशिताः ।तेजसा प्रज्वलन्तो हि कस्यैतद्धनुरुत्तमम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: sūryā yatra ca sauvarṇāstrayo bhāsanti daṁśitāḥ ,tejasā prajvalanto hi kasyaitaddhanuruttamam.
english: Whose is this excellent bow, on which three golden suns, splendidly adorned, shine, truly blazing with radiance?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 23. Whose is this excellent bow, on which three golden suns, splendidly adorned, shine, truly blazing with radiance?

sanskrit: शालभा यत्र सौवर्णास्तपनीयविचित्रिताः ।सुवर्णमणिचित्रं च कस्यैतद्धनुरुत्तमम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: śālabhā yatra sauvarṇāstapanīyavicitritāḥ ,suvarṇamaṇicitraṁ ca kasyaitaddhanuruttamam.
english: Whose is this excellent bow, on which there are golden moth-like figures adorned with refined gold, and variegated with gold and jewels?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 24. Whose is this excellent bow, on which there are golden moth-like figures adorned with refined gold, and variegated with gold and jewels?

sanskrit: इमे च कस्य नाराचाः सहस्रा लोमवाहिनः ।समन्तात्कलधौताग्रा उपासङ्गे हिरण्मये ॥२५॥
transliteration: ime ca kasya nārācāḥ sahasrā lomavāhinaḥ ,samantātkaladhautāgrā upāsaṅge hiraṇmaye.
english: And whose are these thousands of iron arrows, finely feathered, with tips of pure gold all around, in this golden quiver?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 25. And whose are these thousands of iron arrows, finely feathered, with tips of pure gold all around, in this golden quiver?

sanskrit: विपाठाः पृथवः कस्य गार्ध्रपत्राः शिलाशिताः ।हारिद्रवर्णाः सुनसाः पीताः सर्वायसाः शराः ॥२६॥
transliteration: vipāṭhāḥ pṛthavaḥ kasya gārdhrapatrāḥ śilāśitāḥ ,hāridravarṇāḥ sunasāḥ pītāḥ sarvāyasāḥ śarāḥ.
english: Whose are these wide, yellow, entirely iron arrows, broad-headed, feathered with eagle wings, sharpened on stone, and with keen points?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 26. Whose are these wide, yellow, entirely iron arrows, broad-headed, feathered with eagle wings, sharpened on stone, and with keen points?

sanskrit: कस्यायमसितावापः पञ्चशार्दूललक्षणः ।वराहकर्णव्यामिश्रः शरान्धारयते दश ॥२७॥
transliteration: kasyāyamasitāvāpaḥ pañcaśārdūlalakṣaṇaḥ ,varāhakarṇavyāmiśraḥ śarāndhārayate daśa.
english: Whose is this dark quiver, marked with five tigers and adorned with boar's ears, that holds ten arrows?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 27. Whose is this dark quiver, marked with five tigers and adorned with boar's ears, that holds ten arrows?

sanskrit: कस्येमे पृथवो दीर्घाः सर्वपारशवाः शराः ।शतानि सप्त तिष्ठन्ति नाराचा रुधिराशनाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: kasyeme pṛthavo dīrghāḥ sarvapāraśavāḥ śarāḥ ,śatāni sapta tiṣṭhanti nārācā rudhirāśanāḥ.
english: Whose are these broad, long, and entirely metallic arrows? There are seven hundred blood-drinking iron darts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 28. Whose are these broad, long, and entirely metallic arrows? There are seven hundred blood-drinking iron darts.

sanskrit: कस्येमे शुकपत्राभैः पूर्वैरर्धैः सुवाससः ।उत्तरैरायसैः पीतैर्हेमपुङ्खैः शिलाशितैः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kasyeme śukapatrābhaiḥ pūrvairardhaiḥ suvāsasaḥ ,uttarairāyasaiḥ pītairhemapuṅkhaiḥ śilāśitaiḥ.
english: Whose are these arrows, whose front parts are well-fletched like parrot feathers, and whose rear parts are yellow, made of iron, with golden nocks, and sharpened on stone?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 29. Whose are these arrows, whose front parts are well-fletched like parrot feathers, and whose rear parts are yellow, made of iron, with golden nocks, and sharpened on stone?

sanskrit: कस्यायं सायको दीर्घः शिलीपृष्ठः शिलीमुखः ।वैयाघ्रकोशे निहितो हेमचित्रत्सरुर्महान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: kasyāyaṁ sāyako dīrghaḥ śilīpṛṣṭhaḥ śilīmukhaḥ ,vaiyāghrakośe nihito hemacitratsarurmahān.
english: Whose is this long, mighty arrow, with a slender shaft and a sharp point, kept in a tiger-skin quiver, and adorned with a golden and variegated grip?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 30. Whose is this long, mighty arrow, with a slender shaft and a sharp point, kept in a tiger-skin quiver, and adorned with a golden and variegated grip?

sanskrit: सुफलश्चित्रकोशश्च किङ्किणीसायको महान् ।कस्य हेमत्सरुर्दिव्यः खड्गः परमनिर्व्रणः ॥३१॥
transliteration: suphalaścitrakośaśca kiṅkiṇīsāyako mahān ,kasya hematsarurdivyaḥ khaḍgaḥ paramanirvraṇaḥ.
english: Whose is this great sword, which is effective, has a wonderfully decorated sheath, and is adorned with tinkling bells? Whose is this divine sword, possessing a golden hilt and utterly flawless?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 31. Whose is this great sword, which is effective, has a wonderfully decorated sheath, and is adorned with tinkling bells? Whose is this divine sword, possessing a golden hilt and utterly flawless?

sanskrit: कस्यायं विमलः खड्गो गव्ये कोशे समर्पितः ।हेमत्सरुरनाधृष्यो नैषध्यो भारसाधनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: kasyāyaṁ vimalaḥ khaḍgo gavye kośe samarpitaḥ ,hematsaruranādhṛṣyo naiṣadhyo bhārasādhanaḥ.
english: Whose is this spotless sword, which is kept in a cowhide sheath? It has a golden hilt, is irresistible, belongs to the king of Niṣadha, and accomplishes great tasks.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 32. Whose is this spotless sword, which is kept in a cowhide sheath? It has a golden hilt, is irresistible, belongs to the king of Niṣadha, and accomplishes great tasks.

sanskrit: कस्य पाञ्चनखे कोशे सायको हेमविग्रहः ।प्रमाणरूपसंपन्नः पीत आकाशसंनिभः ॥३३॥
transliteration: kasya pāñcanakhe kośe sāyako hemavigrahaḥ ,pramāṇarūpasaṁpannaḥ pīta ākāśasaṁnibhaḥ.
english: Whose is this arrow (sāyaka), with a golden body, kept in a sheath made from the hide of a five-clawed animal? It is endowed with perfect measure and form, is yellow-golden, and shines like the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 33. Whose is this arrow (sāyaka), with a golden body, kept in a sheath made from the hide of a five-clawed animal? It is endowed with perfect measure and form, is yellow-golden, and shines like the sky.

sanskrit: कस्य हेममये कोशे सुतप्ते पावकप्रभे ।निस्त्रिंशोऽयं गुरुः पीतः सैक्यः परमनिर्व्रणः ॥३४॥
transliteration: kasya hemamaye kośe sutapte pāvakaprabhe ,nistriṁśo'yaṁ guruḥ pītaḥ saikyaḥ paramanirvraṇaḥ.
english: Whose is this sword (nistriṃśa), kept in a golden sheath, which is intensely brilliant and shines like fire? This heavy, yellow-golden, iron-made sword is utterly flawless.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 34. Whose is this sword (nistriṃśa), kept in a golden sheath, which is intensely brilliant and shines like fire? This heavy, yellow-golden, iron-made sword is utterly flawless.

sanskrit: निर्दिशस्व यथातत्त्वं मया पृष्टा बृहन्नडे ।विस्मयो मे परो जातो दृष्ट्वा सर्वमिदं महत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: nirdiśasva yathātattvaṁ mayā pṛṣṭā bṛhannaḍe ,vismayo me paro jāto dṛṣṭvā sarvamidaṁ mahat.
english: Bṛhannalā, as I have asked you, please explain the truth. Great astonishment has arisen in me after seeing all this magnificent display.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 35. Bṛhannalā, as I have asked you, please explain the truth. Great astonishment has arisen in me after seeing all this magnificent display.

sanskrit: बृहन्नडोवाच ।यन्मां पूर्वमिहापृच्छः शत्रुसेनानिबर्हणम् ।गाण्डीवमेतत्पार्थस्य लोकेषु विदितं धनुः ॥३६॥
transliteration: bṛhannaḍovāca ,yanmāṁ pūrvamihāpṛcchaḥ śatrusenānibarhaṇam ,gāṇḍīvametatpārthasya lokeṣu viditaṁ dhanuḥ.
english: Bṛhannalā said, "O destroyer of enemy armies, what you previously asked me about here  -  this Gāṇḍīva is Pārtha's (Arjuna's) bow, renowned in all the worlds."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 36. Bṛhannalā said, "O destroyer of enemy armies, what you previously asked me about here  -  this Gāṇḍīva is Pārtha's (Arjuna's) bow, renowned in all the worlds."

sanskrit: सर्वायुधमहामात्रं शातकुम्भपरिष्कृतम् ।एतत्तदर्जुनस्यासीद्गाण्डीवं परमायुधम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sarvāyudhamahāmātraṁ śātakumbhapariṣkṛtam ,etattadarjunasyāsīdgāṇḍīvaṁ paramāyudham.
english: Adorned with pure gold, this Gāṇḍīva was Arjuna's supreme weapon, the foremost of all weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 37. Adorned with pure gold, this Gāṇḍīva was Arjuna's supreme weapon, the foremost of all weapons.

sanskrit: यत्तच्छतसहस्रेण संमितं राष्ट्रवर्धनम् ।येन देवान्मनुष्यांश्च पार्थो विषहते मृधे ॥३८॥
transliteration: yattacchatasahasreṇa saṁmitaṁ rāṣṭravardhanam ,yena devānmanuṣyāṁśca pārtho viṣahate mṛdhe.
english: That bow, which is equivalent to a hundred thousand (weapons) and enhances the kingdom, is what Pārtha (Arjuna) uses to conquer both gods and humans in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 38. That bow, which is equivalent to a hundred thousand (weapons) and enhances the kingdom, is what Pārtha (Arjuna) uses to conquer both gods and humans in battle.

sanskrit: देवदानवगन्धर्वैः पूजितं शाश्वतीः समाः ।एतद्वर्षसहस्रं तु ब्रह्मा पूर्वमधारयत् ॥३९॥
transliteration: devadānavagandharvaiḥ pūjitaṁ śāśvatīḥ samāḥ ,etadvarṣasahasraṁ tu brahmā pūrvamadhārayat.
english: Worshipped by gods, demons, and celestial musicians (gandharvas) for perpetual years, Brahmā (brahman) formerly held this (bow) for a thousand years.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 39. Worshipped by gods, demons, and celestial musicians (gandharvas) for perpetual years, Brahmā (brahman) formerly held this (bow) for a thousand years.

sanskrit: ततोऽनन्तरमेवाथ प्रजापतिरधारयत् ।त्रीणि पञ्चशतं चैव शक्रोऽशीति च पञ्च च ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato'nantaramevātha prajāpatiradhārayat ,trīṇi pañcaśataṁ caiva śakro'śīti ca pañca ca.
english: Thereafter, Prajāpati (lord of creatures) immediately held it for three times five hundred (1500) years. And indeed, Śakra (Indra) held it for eighty-five years.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 40. Thereafter, Prajāpati (lord of creatures) immediately held it for three times five hundred (1500) years. And indeed, Śakra (Indra) held it for eighty-five years.

sanskrit: सोमः पञ्चशतं राजा तथैव वरुणः शतम् ।पार्थः पञ्च च षष्टिं च वर्षाणि श्वेतवाहनः ॥४१॥
transliteration: somaḥ pañcaśataṁ rājā tathaiva varuṇaḥ śatam ,pārthaḥ pañca ca ṣaṣṭiṁ ca varṣāṇi śvetavāhanaḥ.
english: King Soma held it for five hundred (years). Likewise, Varuṇa (held it for) a hundred (years). And Pārtha (Arjuna), also known as Śvetavāhana, held it for sixty-five years.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 41. King Soma held it for five hundred (years). Likewise, Varuṇa (held it for) a hundred (years). And Pārtha (Arjuna), also known as Śvetavāhana, held it for sixty-five years.

sanskrit: महावीर्यं महद्दिव्यमेतत्तद्धनुरुत्तमम् ।पूजितं सुरमर्त्येषु बिभर्ति परमं वपुः ॥४२॥
transliteration: mahāvīryaṁ mahaddivyametattaddhanuruttamam ,pūjitaṁ suramartyeṣu bibharti paramaṁ vapuḥ.
english: This great, mighty, divine, and excellent bow, which is revered by both gods and mortals, possesses a supreme form.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 42. This great, mighty, divine, and excellent bow, which is revered by both gods and mortals, possesses a supreme form.

sanskrit: सुपार्श्वं भीमसेनस्य जातरूपग्रहं धनुः ।येन पार्थोऽजयत्कृत्स्नां दिशं प्राचीं परंतपः ॥४३॥
transliteration: supārśvaṁ bhīmasenasya jātarūpagrahaṁ dhanuḥ ,yena pārtho'jayatkṛtsnāṁ diśaṁ prācīṁ paraṁtapaḥ.
english: This is Bhīmasena's well-proportioned bow, which has a golden grip, and with which Arjuna, the tormentor of foes, conquered the entire eastern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 43. This is Bhīmasena's well-proportioned bow, which has a golden grip, and with which Arjuna, the tormentor of foes, conquered the entire eastern direction.

sanskrit: इन्द्रगोपकचित्रं च यदेतच्चारुविग्रहम् ।राज्ञो युधिष्ठिरस्यैतद्वैराटे धनुरुत्तमम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: indragopakacitraṁ ca yadetaccāruvigraham ,rājño yudhiṣṭhirasyaitadvairāṭe dhanuruttamam.
english: And this excellent bow, beautiful in form and adorned with the colors of cochineals, belongs to King Yudhiṣṭhira, (which is currently) in the kingdom of Virāṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 44. And this excellent bow, beautiful in form and adorned with the colors of cochineals, belongs to King Yudhiṣṭhira, (which is currently) in the kingdom of Virāṭa.

sanskrit: सूर्या यस्मिंस्तु सौवर्णाः प्रभासन्ते प्रभासिनः ।तेजसा प्रज्वलन्तो वै नकुलस्यैतदायुधम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: sūryā yasmiṁstu sauvarṇāḥ prabhāsante prabhāsinaḥ ,tejasā prajvalanto vai nakulasyaitadāyudham.
english: This is Nakula's weapon, on which golden suns, brilliant and blazing with splendor, indeed shine forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 45. This is Nakula's weapon, on which golden suns, brilliant and blazing with splendor, indeed shine forth.

sanskrit: शलभा यत्र सौवर्णास्तपनीयविचित्रिताः ।एतन्माद्रीसुतस्यापि सहदेवस्य कार्मुकम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: śalabhā yatra sauvarṇāstapanīyavicitritāḥ ,etanmādrīsutasyāpi sahadevasya kārmukam.
english: This is also the bow of Sahadeva, the son of Mādrī, on which golden figures resembling locusts, splendidly variegated with refined gold, are depicted.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 46. This is also the bow of Sahadeva, the son of Mādrī, on which golden figures resembling locusts, splendidly variegated with refined gold, are depicted.

sanskrit: ये त्विमे क्षुरसंकाशाः सहस्रा लोमवाहिनः ।एतेऽर्जुनस्य वैराटे शराः सर्पविषोपमाः ॥४७॥
transliteration: ye tvime kṣurasaṁkāśāḥ sahasrā lomavāhinaḥ ,ete'rjunasya vairāṭe śarāḥ sarpaviṣopamāḥ.
english: These thousands of Arjuna's arrows, which are sharp like razors and carry away hair (of the enemy), are like snake venom in the Virāṭa war.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 47. These thousands of Arjuna's arrows, which are sharp like razors and carry away hair (of the enemy), are like snake venom in the Virāṭa war.

sanskrit: एते ज्वलन्तः संग्रामे तेजसा शीघ्रगामिनः ।भवन्ति वीरस्याक्षय्या व्यूहतः समरे रिपून् ॥४८॥
transliteration: ete jvalantaḥ saṁgrāme tejasā śīghragāminaḥ ,bhavanti vīrasyākṣayyā vyūhataḥ samare ripūn.
english: These (arrows), blazing in battle with their brilliance and moving swiftly, become inexhaustible for the hero, routing enemies in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 48. These (arrows), blazing in battle with their brilliance and moving swiftly, become inexhaustible for the hero, routing enemies in combat.

sanskrit: ये चेमे पृथवो दीर्घाश्चन्द्रबिम्बार्धदर्शनाः ।एते भीमस्य निशिता रिपुक्षयकराः शराः ॥४९॥
transliteration: ye ceme pṛthavo dīrghāścandrabimbārdhadarśanāḥ ,ete bhīmasya niśitā ripukṣayakarāḥ śarāḥ.
english: And these broad, long arrows, which appear like a half-moon disc, are Bhīma's sharpened arrows that destroy enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 49. And these broad, long arrows, which appear like a half-moon disc, are Bhīma's sharpened arrows that destroy enemies.

sanskrit: हारिद्रवर्णा ये त्वेते हेमपुङ्खाः शिलाशिताः ।नकुलस्य कलापोऽयं पञ्चशार्दूललक्षणः ॥५०॥
transliteration: hāridravarṇā ye tvete hemapuṅkhāḥ śilāśitāḥ ,nakulasya kalāpo'yaṁ pañcaśārdūlalakṣaṇaḥ.
english: These arrows, yellow like turmeric, with golden shafts, and sharpened on stone, constitute Nakula's quiver, which is marked with five tigers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 50. These arrows, yellow like turmeric, with golden shafts, and sharpened on stone, constitute Nakula's quiver, which is marked with five tigers.

sanskrit: येनासौ व्यजयत्कृत्स्नां प्रतीचीं दिशमाहवे ।कलापो ह्येष तस्यासीन्माद्रीपुत्रस्य धीमतः ॥५१॥
transliteration: yenāsau vyajayatkṛtsnāṁ pratīcīṁ diśamāhave ,kalāpo hyeṣa tasyāsīnmādrīputrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Indeed, this quiver belonged to that intelligent son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), with which he conquered the entire western direction in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 51. Indeed, this quiver belonged to that intelligent son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), with which he conquered the entire western direction in battle.

sanskrit: ये त्विमे भास्कराकाराः सर्वपारशवाः शराः ।एते चित्राः क्रियोपेताः सहदेवस्य धीमतः ॥५२॥
transliteration: ye tvime bhāskarākārāḥ sarvapāraśavāḥ śarāḥ ,ete citrāḥ kriyopetāḥ sahadevasya dhīmataḥ.
english: But these arrows, which shine like the sun and are all made of steel, these wonderful and effective ones belong to intelligent Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 52. But these arrows, which shine like the sun and are all made of steel, these wonderful and effective ones belong to intelligent Sahadeva.

sanskrit: ये त्विमे निशिताः पीताः पृथवो दीर्घवाससः ।हेमपुङ्खास्त्रिपर्वाणो राज्ञ एते महाशराः ॥५३॥
transliteration: ye tvime niśitāḥ pītāḥ pṛthavo dīrghavāsasaḥ ,hemapuṅkhāstriparvāṇo rājña ete mahāśarāḥ.
english: But these sharpened, yellow, broad, and long-feathered arrows, with golden fletching and three sections, these great arrows belong to the King.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 53. But these sharpened, yellow, broad, and long-feathered arrows, with golden fletching and three sections, these great arrows belong to the King.

sanskrit: यस्त्वयं सायको दीर्घः शिलीपृष्ठः शिलीमुखः ।अर्जुनस्यैष संग्रामे गुरुभारसहो दृढः ॥५४॥
transliteration: yastvayaṁ sāyako dīrghaḥ śilīpṛṣṭhaḥ śilīmukhaḥ ,arjunasyaiṣa saṁgrāme gurubhārasaho dṛḍhaḥ.
english: But this long arrow, sharp-backed and sharp-pointed, is Arjuna's; it is firm and capable of enduring a great burden in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 54. But this long arrow, sharp-backed and sharp-pointed, is Arjuna's; it is firm and capable of enduring a great burden in battle.

sanskrit: वैयाघ्रकोशस्तु महान्भीमसेनस्य सायकः ।गुरुभारसहो दिव्यः शात्रवाणां भयंकरः ॥५५॥
transliteration: vaiyāghrakośastu mahānbhīmasenasya sāyakaḥ ,gurubhārasaho divyaḥ śātravāṇāṁ bhayaṁkaraḥ.
english: Bhimasena's great weapon, which is housed in a tiger-skin sheath, is divine, capable of enduring heavy burdens, and terrifying to enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 55. Bhimasena's great weapon, which is housed in a tiger-skin sheath, is divine, capable of enduring heavy burdens, and terrifying to enemies.

sanskrit: सुफलश्चित्रकोशश्च हेमत्सरुरनुत्तमः ।निस्त्रिंशः कौरवस्यैष धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥५६॥
transliteration: suphalaścitrakośaśca hematsaruranuttamaḥ ,nistriṁśaḥ kauravasyaiṣa dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: This sword (nistriṃśa) belonging to the wise king of righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira, a Kuru prince, is exceedingly effective, beautifully sheathed, gold-hilted, and unsurpassed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 56. This sword (nistriṃśa) belonging to the wise king of righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira, a Kuru prince, is exceedingly effective, beautifully sheathed, gold-hilted, and unsurpassed.

sanskrit: यस्तु पाञ्चनखे कोशे निहितश्चित्रसेवने ।नकुलस्यैष निस्त्रिंशो गुरुभारसहो दृढः ॥५७॥
transliteration: yastu pāñcanakhe kośe nihitaścitrasevane ,nakulasyaiṣa nistriṁśo gurubhārasaho dṛḍhaḥ.
english: This sword (nistriṃśa) of Nakula, which is housed in a sheath made of the skin of a five-clawed animal and also has a beautifully adorned covering, is strong and capable of enduring heavy burdens.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 57. This sword (nistriṃśa) of Nakula, which is housed in a sheath made of the skin of a five-clawed animal and also has a beautifully adorned covering, is strong and capable of enduring heavy burdens.

sanskrit: यस्त्वयं विमलः खड्गो गव्ये कोशे समर्पितः ।सहदेवस्य विद्ध्येनं सर्वभारसहं दृढम् ॥५८॥
transliteration: yastvayaṁ vimalaḥ khaḍgo gavye kośe samarpitaḥ ,sahadevasya viddhyenaṁ sarvabhārasahaṁ dṛḍham.
english: This spotless sword, which is placed in a cow-hide sheath, know it to be Sahadeva's; it is strong and capable of enduring all burdens.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 38 Verse 58. This spotless sword, which is placed in a cow-hide sheath, know it to be Sahadeva's; it is strong and capable of enduring all burdens.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।सुवर्णविकृतानीमान्यायुधानि महात्मनाम् ।रुचिराणि प्रकाशन्ते पार्थानामाशुकारिणाम् ॥१॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,suvarṇavikṛtānīmānyāyudhāni mahātmanām ,rucirāṇi prakāśante pārthānāmāśukāriṇām.
english: Uttara said: These brilliant weapons, adorned with gold, belonging to those swift-acting Pārthas, the great souls, shine brightly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 1. Uttara said: These brilliant weapons, adorned with gold, belonging to those swift-acting Pārthas, the great souls, shine brightly.

sanskrit: क्व नु स्विदर्जुनः पार्थः कौरव्यो वा युधिष्ठिरः ।नकुलः सहदेवश्च भीमसेनश्च पाण्डवः ॥२॥
transliteration: kva nu svidarjunaḥ pārthaḥ kauravyo vā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,nakulaḥ sahadevaśca bhīmasenaśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Where, I wonder, are Arjuna, the son of Pṛthā, or Yudhiṣṭhira, the prince of the Kurus, along with Nakula, Sahadeva, and Bhīmasena, the sons of Pāṇḍu?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 2. Where, I wonder, are Arjuna, the son of Pṛthā, or Yudhiṣṭhira, the prince of the Kurus, along with Nakula, Sahadeva, and Bhīmasena, the sons of Pāṇḍu?

sanskrit: सर्व एव महात्मानः सर्वामित्रविनाशनाः ।राज्यमक्षैः पराकीर्य न श्रूयन्ते कदाचन ॥३॥
transliteration: sarva eva mahātmānaḥ sarvāmitravināśanāḥ ,rājyamakṣaiḥ parākīrya na śrūyante kadācana.
english: All of them are great souls (mahātman), destroyers of all enemies. Yet, having gambled away their kingdom with dice, they are never heard of anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 3. All of them are great souls (mahātman), destroyers of all enemies. Yet, having gambled away their kingdom with dice, they are never heard of anywhere.

sanskrit: द्रौपदी क्व च पाञ्चाली स्त्रीरत्नमिति विश्रुता ।जितानक्षैस्तदा कृष्णा तानेवान्वगमद्वनम् ॥४॥
transliteration: draupadī kva ca pāñcālī strīratnamiti viśrutā ,jitānakṣaistadā kṛṣṇā tānevānvagamadvanam.
english: And where is Draupadī, the princess of Pañcāla, renowned as a jewel among women? Kṛṣṇā, having been lost by dice at that time, indeed followed them into the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 4. And where is Draupadī, the princess of Pañcāla, renowned as a jewel among women? Kṛṣṇā, having been lost by dice at that time, indeed followed them into the forest.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।अहमस्म्यर्जुनः पार्थः सभास्तारो युधिष्ठिरः ।बल्लवो भीमसेनस्तु पितुस्ते रसपाचकः ॥५॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,ahamasmyarjunaḥ pārthaḥ sabhāstāro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ballavo bhīmasenastu pituste rasapācakaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: 'I am Arjuna, the son of Pritha. Yudhiṣṭhira is the courtier. Bhīmasena, however, is Ballava, your father's cook.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 5. Arjuna said: 'I am Arjuna, the son of Pritha. Yudhiṣṭhira is the courtier. Bhīmasena, however, is Ballava, your father's cook.'

sanskrit: अश्वबन्धोऽथ नकुलः सहदेवस्तु गोकुले ।सैरन्ध्रीं द्रौपदीं विद्धि यत्कृते कीचका हताः ॥६॥
transliteration: aśvabandho'tha nakulaḥ sahadevastu gokule ,sairandhrīṁ draupadīṁ viddhi yatkṛte kīcakā hatāḥ.
english: And Nakula is the horse-keeper. Sahadeva, however, is in the cow-pen. Know Draupadī as Sairandhrī, for whose sake the Kīcakas were killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 6. And Nakula is the horse-keeper. Sahadeva, however, is in the cow-pen. Know Draupadī as Sairandhrī, for whose sake the Kīcakas were killed.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।दश पार्थस्य नामानि यानि पूर्वं श्रुतानि मे ।प्रब्रूयास्तानि यदि मे श्रद्दध्यां सर्वमेव ते ॥७॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,daśa pārthasya nāmāni yāni pūrvaṁ śrutāni me ,prabrūyāstāni yadi me śraddadhyāṁ sarvameva te.
english: Uttara said: 'If you would tell me the ten names of Partha (Arjuna) which I have heard before, I would indeed believe all you say.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 7. Uttara said: 'If you would tell me the ten names of Partha (Arjuna) which I have heard before, I would indeed believe all you say.'

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।हन्त तेऽहं समाचक्षे दश नामानि यानि मे ।अर्जुनः फल्गुनो जिष्णुः किरीटी श्वेतवाहनः ।बीभत्सुर्विजयः कृष्णः सव्यसाची धनंजयः ॥८॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,hanta te'haṁ samācakṣe daśa nāmāni yāni me ,arjunaḥ phalguno jiṣṇuḥ kirīṭī śvetavāhanaḥ ,bībhatsurvijayaḥ kṛṣṇaḥ savyasācī dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: 'Indeed, I will tell you my ten names: Arjuna, Phalguna, Jiṣṇu, Kirīṭī, Śvetavāhana, Bībhatsu, Vijaya, Kṛṣṇa, Savyasācī, and Dhanañjaya.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 8. Arjuna said: 'Indeed, I will tell you my ten names: Arjuna, Phalguna, Jiṣṇu, Kirīṭī, Śvetavāhana, Bībhatsu, Vijaya, Kṛṣṇa, Savyasācī, and Dhanañjaya.'

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।केनासि विजयो नाम केनासि श्वेतवाहनः ।किरीटी नाम केनासि सव्यसाची कथं भवान् ॥९॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,kenāsi vijayo nāma kenāsi śvetavāhanaḥ ,kirīṭī nāma kenāsi savyasācī kathaṁ bhavān.
english: Uttara said: "By what reason are you named Vijaya? By what reason are you called Śvetavāhana? By what reason are you named Kiriṭī? How are you Savyasācī (ambidextrous)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 9. Uttara said: "By what reason are you named Vijaya? By what reason are you called Śvetavāhana? By what reason are you named Kiriṭī? How are you Savyasācī (ambidextrous)?"

sanskrit: अर्जुनः फल्गुनो जिष्णुः कृष्णो बीभत्सुरेव च ।धनंजयश्च केनासि प्रब्रूहि मम तत्त्वतः ।श्रुता मे तस्य वीरस्य केवला नामहेतवः ॥१०॥
transliteration: arjunaḥ phalguno jiṣṇuḥ kṛṣṇo bībhatsureva ca ,dhanaṁjayaśca kenāsi prabrūhi mama tattvataḥ ,śrutā me tasya vīrasya kevalā nāmahetavaḥ.
english: You are Arjuna, Phalguna, Jiṣṇu, Kṛṣṇa, and Bibhatsu, as well as Dhanañjaya. By what reasons are you called these? Please explain fully to me, for I have only heard the mere causes of that hero's names.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 10. You are Arjuna, Phalguna, Jiṣṇu, Kṛṣṇa, and Bibhatsu, as well as Dhanañjaya. By what reasons are you called these? Please explain fully to me, for I have only heard the mere causes of that hero's names.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।सर्वाञ्जनपदाञ्जित्वा वित्तमाच्छिद्य केवलम् ।मध्ये धनस्य तिष्ठामि तेनाहुर्मां धनंजयम् ॥११॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,sarvāñjanapadāñjitvā vittamācchidya kevalam ,madhye dhanasya tiṣṭhāmi tenāhurmāṁ dhanaṁjayam.
english: Arjuna said: "Having conquered all provinces and taken only their wealth, I stand amidst riches. Therefore, people call me Dhanañjaya."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 11. Arjuna said: "Having conquered all provinces and taken only their wealth, I stand amidst riches. Therefore, people call me Dhanañjaya."

sanskrit: अभिप्रयामि संग्रामे यदहं युद्धदुर्मदान् ।नाजित्वा विनिवर्तामि तेन मां विजयं विदुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: abhiprayāmi saṁgrāme yadahaṁ yuddhadurmadān ,nājitvā vinivartāmi tena māṁ vijayaṁ viduḥ.
english: Since I advance into battle against those fiercely arrogant in combat and do not return without conquering them, for that reason they call me Vijaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 12. Since I advance into battle against those fiercely arrogant in combat and do not return without conquering them, for that reason they call me Vijaya.

sanskrit: श्वेताः काञ्चनसंनाहा रथे युज्यन्ति मे हयाः ।संग्रामे युध्यमानस्य तेनाहं श्वेतवाहनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: śvetāḥ kāñcanasaṁnāhā rathe yujyanti me hayāḥ ,saṁgrāme yudhyamānasya tenāhaṁ śvetavāhanaḥ.
english: My white horses, adorned with golden harnesses, are yoked to my chariot. Therefore, because I fight in battle, I am known as Śvetavāhana (one whose steeds are white).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 13. My white horses, adorned with golden harnesses, are yoked to my chariot. Therefore, because I fight in battle, I am known as Śvetavāhana (one whose steeds are white).

sanskrit: उत्तराभ्यां च पूर्वाभ्यां फल्गुनीभ्यामहं दिवा ।जातो हिमवतः पृष्ठे तेन मां फल्गुनं विदुः ॥१४॥
transliteration: uttarābhyāṁ ca pūrvābhyāṁ phalgunībhyāmahaṁ divā ,jāto himavataḥ pṛṣṭhe tena māṁ phalgunaṁ viduḥ.
english: I was born on the peak of the Himalayas during the day, under the influence of both the earlier and later Phalguni constellations (nakṣatras). For this reason, they know me as Phalguna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 14. I was born on the peak of the Himalayas during the day, under the influence of both the earlier and later Phalguni constellations (nakṣatras). For this reason, they know me as Phalguna.

sanskrit: पुरा शक्रेण मे दत्तं युध्यतो दानवर्षभैः ।किरीटं मूर्ध्नि सूर्याभं तेन माहुः किरीटिनम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: purā śakreṇa me dattaṁ yudhyato dānavarṣabhaiḥ ,kirīṭaṁ mūrdhni sūryābhaṁ tena māhuḥ kirīṭinam.
english: Formerly, when I was fighting the foremost Dānavas (demons), Indra (Śakra) gave me a sun-like diadem for my head. Therefore, they call me Kirīṭin (the crowned one).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 15. Formerly, when I was fighting the foremost Dānavas (demons), Indra (Śakra) gave me a sun-like diadem for my head. Therefore, they call me Kirīṭin (the crowned one).

sanskrit: न कुर्यां कर्म बीभत्सं युध्यमानः कथंचन ।तेन देवमनुष्येषु बीभत्सुरिति मां विदुः ॥१६॥
transliteration: na kuryāṁ karma bībhatsaṁ yudhyamānaḥ kathaṁcana ,tena devamanuṣyeṣu bībhatsuriti māṁ viduḥ.
english: While fighting, I would never do any repulsive deed (karma) by any means. Therefore, among gods and humans, they know me as Bibhatsu (the one who abhors vile deeds).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 16. While fighting, I would never do any repulsive deed (karma) by any means. Therefore, among gods and humans, they know me as Bibhatsu (the one who abhors vile deeds).

sanskrit: उभौ मे दक्षिणौ पाणी गाण्डीवस्य विकर्षणे ।तेन देवमनुष्येषु सव्यसाचीति मां विदुः ॥१७॥
transliteration: ubhau me dakṣiṇau pāṇī gāṇḍīvasya vikarṣaṇe ,tena devamanuṣyeṣu savyasācīti māṁ viduḥ.
english: Both my hands are equally adept at drawing the Gaṇḍīva bow. Therefore, among gods and humans, they know me as Savyasācī.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 17. Both my hands are equally adept at drawing the Gaṇḍīva bow. Therefore, among gods and humans, they know me as Savyasācī.

sanskrit: पृथिव्यां चतुरन्तायां वर्णो मे दुर्लभः समः ।करोमि कर्म शुक्लं च तेन मामर्जुनं विदुः ॥१८॥
transliteration: pṛthivyāṁ caturantāyāṁ varṇo me durlabhaḥ samaḥ ,karomi karma śuklaṁ ca tena māmarjunaṁ viduḥ.
english: On this four-cornered earth, a complexion matching mine is rare. Also, I perform pure actions (karma). Therefore, they know me as Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 18. On this four-cornered earth, a complexion matching mine is rare. Also, I perform pure actions (karma). Therefore, they know me as Arjuna.

sanskrit: अहं दुरापो दुर्धर्षो दमनः पाकशासनिः ।तेन देवमनुष्येषु जिष्णुनामास्मि विश्रुतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ahaṁ durāpo durdharṣo damanaḥ pākaśāsaniḥ ,tena devamanuṣyeṣu jiṣṇunāmāsmi viśrutaḥ.
english: I am difficult to approach, unassailable, and a subduer; I am also the son of Indra (Pākaśāsana). Therefore, among gods and humans, I am renowned by the name Jiṣṇu.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 19. I am difficult to approach, unassailable, and a subduer; I am also the son of Indra (Pākaśāsana). Therefore, among gods and humans, I am renowned by the name Jiṣṇu.

sanskrit: कृष्ण इत्येव दशमं नाम चक्रे पिता मम ।कृष्णावदातस्य सतः प्रियत्वाद्बालकस्य वै ॥२०॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa ityeva daśamaṁ nāma cakre pitā mama ,kṛṣṇāvadātasya sataḥ priyatvādbālakasya vai.
english: My father indeed gave me the tenth name, 'Kṛṣṇa,' due to his affection for the boy who possessed both dark and fair qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 20. My father indeed gave me the tenth name, 'Kṛṣṇa,' due to his affection for the boy who possessed both dark and fair qualities.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः पार्थं स वैराटिरभ्यवादयदन्तिकात् ।अहं भूमिंजयो नाम नाम्नाहमपि चोत्तरः ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ pārthaṁ sa vairāṭirabhyavādayadantikāt ,ahaṁ bhūmiṁjayo nāma nāmnāhamapi cottaraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, that son of Virāṭa (Uttara) saluted Arjuna (Pārtha) from nearby. He said, "My name is Bhūmiñjaya, and I am also known by the name Uttara."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 21. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, that son of Virāṭa (Uttara) saluted Arjuna (Pārtha) from nearby. He said, "My name is Bhūmiñjaya, and I am also known by the name Uttara."
