sanskrit: दिष्ट्या त्वां पार्थ पश्यामि स्वागतं ते धनंजय ।लोहिताक्ष महाबाहो नागराजकरोपम ।यदज्ञानादवोचं त्वां क्षन्तुमर्हसि तन्मम ॥२२॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā tvāṁ pārtha paśyāmi svāgataṁ te dhanaṁjaya ,lohitākṣa mahābāho nāgarājakaropama ,yadajñānādavocaṁ tvāṁ kṣantumarhasi tanmama.
english: Fortunately, O Pārtha, I see you! Welcome to you, O Dhanañjaya! O red-eyed one, O mighty-armed one, whose arms are like the trunks of a lordly elephant! Please forgive me for what I said to you out of ignorance.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 22. Fortunately, O Pārtha, I see you! Welcome to you, O Dhanañjaya! O red-eyed one, O mighty-armed one, whose arms are like the trunks of a lordly elephant! Please forgive me for what I said to you out of ignorance.

sanskrit: यतस्त्वया कृतं पूर्वं विचित्रं कर्म दुष्करम् ।अतो भयं व्यतीतं मे प्रीतिश्च परमा त्वयि ॥२३॥
transliteration: yatastvayā kṛtaṁ pūrvaṁ vicitraṁ karma duṣkaram ,ato bhayaṁ vyatītaṁ me prītiśca paramā tvayi.
english: Since you have previously performed such a wonderful and arduous deed (karma), my fear has therefore vanished, and I have the highest affection for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 39 Verse 23. Since you have previously performed such a wonderful and arduous deed (karma), my fear has therefore vanished, and I have the highest affection for you.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।आस्थाय विपुलं वीर रथं सारथिना मया ।कतमं यास्यसेऽनीकमुक्तो यास्याम्यहं त्वया ॥१॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,āsthāya vipulaṁ vīra rathaṁ sārathinā mayā ,katamaṁ yāsyase'nīkamukto yāsyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Uttara said: "O hero, having mounted this mighty chariot, with me as your charioteer, to which division (anīka) of the army will you proceed? I will drive as instructed by you."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 1. Uttara said: "O hero, having mounted this mighty chariot, with me as your charioteer, to which division (anīka) of the army will you proceed? I will drive as instructed by you."

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।प्रीतोऽस्मि पुरुषव्याघ्र न भयं विद्यते तव ।सर्वान्नुदामि ते शत्रून्रणे रणविशारद ॥२॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,prīto'smi puruṣavyāghra na bhayaṁ vidyate tava ,sarvānnudāmi te śatrūnraṇe raṇaviśārada.
english: Arjuna said: O best among men, I am pleased. You have no cause for fear. O expert in battle, I will drive away all your enemies in the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 2. Arjuna said: O best among men, I am pleased. You have no cause for fear. O expert in battle, I will drive away all your enemies in the fight.

sanskrit: स्वस्थो भव महाबुद्धे पश्य मां शत्रुभिः सह ।युध्यमानं विमर्देऽस्मिन्कुर्वाणं भैरवं महत् ॥३॥
transliteration: svastho bhava mahābuddhe paśya māṁ śatrubhiḥ saha ,yudhyamānaṁ vimarde'sminkurvāṇaṁ bhairavaṁ mahat.
english: O great-minded one, be at ease! Behold me fighting with the enemies in this conflict, creating great terror!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 3. O great-minded one, be at ease! Behold me fighting with the enemies in this conflict, creating great terror!

sanskrit: एतान्सर्वानुपासङ्गान्क्षिप्रं बध्नीहि मे रथे ।एतं चाहर निस्त्रिंशं जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ।अहं वै कुरुभिर्योत्स्याम्यवजेष्यामि ते पशून् ॥४॥
transliteration: etānsarvānupāsaṅgānkṣipraṁ badhnīhi me rathe ,etaṁ cāhara nistriṁśaṁ jātarūpapariṣkṛtam ,ahaṁ vai kurubhiryotsyāmyavajeṣyāmi te paśūn.
english: Quickly attach all these quivers to my chariot, and bring this gold-adorned sword! I will indeed fight with the Kurus, and I will conquer your adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 4. Quickly attach all these quivers to my chariot, and bring this gold-adorned sword! I will indeed fight with the Kurus, and I will conquer your adversaries.

sanskrit: संकल्पपक्षविक्षेपं बाहुप्राकारतोरणम् ।त्रिदण्डतूणसंबाधमनेकध्वजसंकुलम् ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁkalpapakṣavikṣepaṁ bāhuprākāratoraṇam ,tridaṇḍatūṇasaṁbādhamanekadhvajasaṁkulam.
english: It will be characterized by a will that spreads like wings, with arms like fortress walls and archways, filled with quivers containing three-shafted arrows, and crowded with many banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 5. It will be characterized by a will that spreads like wings, with arms like fortress walls and archways, filled with quivers containing three-shafted arrows, and crowded with many banners.

sanskrit: ज्याक्षेपणं क्रोधकृतं नेमीनिनददुन्दुभि ।नगरं ते मया गुप्तं रथोपस्थं भविष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: jyākṣepaṇaṁ krodhakṛtaṁ nemīninadadundubhi ,nagaraṁ te mayā guptaṁ rathopasthaṁ bhaviṣyati.
english: The twanging of the bowstring, done in anger, and the sound of the chariot wheels, like a battle drum, will guard your city, protected by me from this chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 6. The twanging of the bowstring, done in anger, and the sound of the chariot wheels, like a battle drum, will guard your city, protected by me from this chariot.

sanskrit: अधिष्ठितो मया संख्ये रथो गाण्डीवधन्वना ।अजेयः शत्रुसैन्यानां वैराटे व्येतु ते भयम् ॥७॥
transliteration: adhiṣṭhito mayā saṁkhye ratho gāṇḍīvadhanvanā ,ajeyaḥ śatrusainyānāṁ vairāṭe vyetu te bhayam.
english: Let your fear depart, O son of Virāṭa! This chariot, driven by me, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva (bow), will be invincible to enemy armies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 7. Let your fear depart, O son of Virāṭa! This chariot, driven by me, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva (bow), will be invincible to enemy armies in battle.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।बिभेमि नाहमेतेषां जानामि त्वां स्थिरं युधि ।केशवेनापि संग्रामे साक्षादिन्द्रेण वा समम् ॥८॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,bibhemi nāhameteṣāṁ jānāmi tvāṁ sthiraṁ yudhi ,keśavenāpi saṁgrāme sākṣādindreṇa vā samam.
english: Uttara said: "I do not fear these (enemies). I know you to be steadfast in battle, equal to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) himself or even Indra in combat."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 8. Uttara said: "I do not fear these (enemies). I know you to be steadfast in battle, equal to Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) himself or even Indra in combat."

sanskrit: इदं तु चिन्तयन्नेव परिमुह्यामि केवलम् ।निश्चयं चापि दुर्मेधा न गच्छामि कथंचन ॥९॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu cintayanneva parimuhyāmi kevalam ,niścayaṁ cāpi durmedhā na gacchāmi kathaṁcana.
english: However, merely by reflecting on this, I become utterly bewildered. And being dull-witted (durmedhā), I am unable to reach any certain decision.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 9. However, merely by reflecting on this, I become utterly bewildered. And being dull-witted (durmedhā), I am unable to reach any certain decision.

sanskrit: एवं वीराङ्गरूपस्य लक्षणैरुचितस्य च ।केन कर्मविपाकेन क्लीबत्वमिदमागतम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: evaṁ vīrāṅgarūpasya lakṣaṇairucitasya ca ,kena karmavipākena klībatvamidamāgatam.
english: Considering this heroic physique, adorned with appropriate characteristics, by what ripening of past actions (karma) has this effeminate state come about?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 10. Considering this heroic physique, adorned with appropriate characteristics, by what ripening of past actions (karma) has this effeminate state come about?

sanskrit: मन्ये त्वां क्लीबवेषेण चरन्तं शूलपाणिनम् ।गन्धर्वराजप्रतिमं देवं वापि शतक्रतुम् ॥११॥
transliteration: manye tvāṁ klībaveṣeṇa carantaṁ śūlapāṇinam ,gandharvarājapratimaṁ devaṁ vāpi śatakratum.
english: I consider you, disguised as a eunuch, to be Śūlapāṇi (Lord Shiva), or even a god resembling the king of the Gandharvas, or Śatakratu (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 11. I consider you, disguised as a eunuch, to be Śūlapāṇi (Lord Shiva), or even a god resembling the king of the Gandharvas, or Śatakratu (Indra).

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।भ्रातुर्नियोगाज्ज्येष्ठस्य संवत्सरमिदं व्रतम् ।चरामि ब्रह्मचर्यं वै सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥१२॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,bhrāturniyogājjyeṣṭhasya saṁvatsaramidaṁ vratam ,carāmi brahmacaryaṁ vai satyametadbravīmi te.
english: Arjuna said: "By the command of my eldest brother, I am observing this vow of celibacy (brahmacarya) for a year. Indeed, I tell you this truth."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 12. Arjuna said: "By the command of my eldest brother, I am observing this vow of celibacy (brahmacarya) for a year. Indeed, I tell you this truth."

sanskrit: नास्मि क्लीबो महाबाहो परवान्धर्मसंयुतः ।समाप्तव्रतमुत्तीर्णं विद्धि मां त्वं नृपात्मज ॥१३॥
transliteration: nāsmi klībo mahābāho paravāndharmasaṁyutaḥ ,samāptavratamuttīrṇaṁ viddhi māṁ tvaṁ nṛpātmaja.
english: O mighty-armed one, I am neither effeminate nor dependent on others; rather, I am devoted to my own natural law (dharma). O prince, know that I have completed my vow and emerged (from this state).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 13. O mighty-armed one, I am neither effeminate nor dependent on others; rather, I am devoted to my own natural law (dharma). O prince, know that I have completed my vow and emerged (from this state).

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।परमोऽनुग्रहो मेऽद्य यत्प्रतर्को न मे वृथा ।न हीदृशाः क्लीबरूपा भवन्तीह नरोत्तमाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,paramo'nugraho me'dya yatpratarko na me vṛthā ,na hīdṛśāḥ klībarūpā bhavantīha narottamāḥ.
english: Uttara said: This is a supreme favor for me today, because my conjecture was not in vain. Indeed, excellent men (puruṣa) do not assume such an effeminate appearance here.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 14. Uttara said: This is a supreme favor for me today, because my conjecture was not in vain. Indeed, excellent men (puruṣa) do not assume such an effeminate appearance here.

sanskrit: सहायवानस्मि रणे युध्येयममरैरपि ।साध्वसं तत्प्रनष्टं मे किं करोमि ब्रवीहि मे ॥१५॥
transliteration: sahāyavānasmi raṇe yudhyeyamamarairapi ,sādhvasaṁ tatpranaṣṭaṁ me kiṁ karomi bravīhi me.
english: I am now assisted in battle; I could fight even with the immortals. My fear has completely vanished. What should I do? Tell me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 15. I am now assisted in battle; I could fight even with the immortals. My fear has completely vanished. What should I do? Tell me.

sanskrit: अहं ते संग्रहीष्यामि हयाञ्शत्रुरथारुजः ।शिक्षितो ह्यस्मि सारथ्ये तीर्थतः पुरुषर्षभ ॥१६॥
transliteration: ahaṁ te saṁgrahīṣyāmi hayāñśatrurathārujaḥ ,śikṣito hyasmi sārathye tīrthataḥ puruṣarṣabha.
english: I, who am the destroyer of enemy chariots, will take control of your horses. For I am indeed trained in charioteering by a proper teacher, O best among men (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 16. I, who am the destroyer of enemy chariots, will take control of your horses. For I am indeed trained in charioteering by a proper teacher, O best among men (puruṣa).

sanskrit: दारुको वासुदेवस्य यथा शक्रस्य मातलिः ।तथा मां विद्धि सारथ्ये शिक्षितं नरपुंगव ॥१७॥
transliteration: dāruko vāsudevasya yathā śakrasya mātaliḥ ,tathā māṁ viddhi sārathye śikṣitaṁ narapuṁgava.
english: Just as Daruka serves Vasudeva (Krishna) and Matali serves Shakra (Indra), so know me, who is trained in charioteering, O excellent man (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 17. Just as Daruka serves Vasudeva (Krishna) and Matali serves Shakra (Indra), so know me, who is trained in charioteering, O excellent man (puruṣa).

sanskrit: यस्य याते न पश्यन्ति भूमौ प्राप्तं पदं पदम् ।दक्षिणं यो धुरं युक्तः सुग्रीवसदृशो हयः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yasya yāte na paśyanti bhūmau prāptaṁ padaṁ padam ,dakṣiṇaṁ yo dhuraṁ yuktaḥ sugrīvasadṛśo hayaḥ.
english: That horse, yoked to the right side (of the chariot) and resembling Sugrīva, moves with such speed that when it goes, people do not see its footprint (pada) on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 18. That horse, yoked to the right side (of the chariot) and resembling Sugrīva, moves with such speed that when it goes, people do not see its footprint (pada) on the ground.

sanskrit: योऽयं धुरं धुर्यवरो वामं वहति शोभनः ।तं मन्ये मेघपुष्पस्य जवेन सदृशं हयम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: yo'yaṁ dhuraṁ dhuryavaro vāmaṁ vahati śobhanaḥ ,taṁ manye meghapuṣpasya javena sadṛśaṁ hayam.
english: I consider this excellent and beautiful horse, the best among the draught animals, which pulls the left side (of the yoke), to be similar in speed to Meghapuṣpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 19. I consider this excellent and beautiful horse, the best among the draught animals, which pulls the left side (of the yoke), to be similar in speed to Meghapuṣpa.

sanskrit: योऽयं काञ्चनसंनाहः पार्ष्णिं वहति शोभनः ।वामं सैन्यस्य मन्ये तं जवेन बलवत्तरम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: yo'yaṁ kāñcanasaṁnāhaḥ pārṣṇiṁ vahati śobhanaḥ ,vāmaṁ sainyasya manye taṁ javena balavattaram.
english: I consider this splendid horse, adorned with golden armor, which pulls the rear flank of the army to the left, to be very powerful in speed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 20. I consider this splendid horse, adorned with golden armor, which pulls the rear flank of the army to the left, to be very powerful in speed.

sanskrit: योऽयं वहति ते पार्ष्णिं दक्षिणामञ्चितोद्यतः ।बलाहकादपि मतः स जवे वीर्यवत्तरः ॥२१॥
transliteration: yo'yaṁ vahati te pārṣṇiṁ dakṣiṇāmañcitodyataḥ ,balāhakādapi mataḥ sa jave vīryavattaraḥ.
english: This horse, which is gracefully poised and pulls your right rear flank, is considered even more powerful in speed than Balāhaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 21. This horse, which is gracefully poised and pulls your right rear flank, is considered even more powerful in speed than Balāhaka.

sanskrit: त्वामेवायं रथो वोढुं संग्रामेऽर्हति धन्विनम् ।त्वं चेमं रथमास्थाय योद्धुमर्हो मतो मम ॥२२॥
transliteration: tvāmevāyaṁ ratho voḍhuṁ saṁgrāme'rhati dhanvinam ,tvaṁ cemaṁ rathamāsthāya yoddhumarho mato mama.
english: This chariot is capable of carrying only you, the archer, in battle. And it is my opinion that you are fit to fight having mounted this chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 22. This chariot is capable of carrying only you, the archer, in battle. And it is my opinion that you are fit to fight having mounted this chariot.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो निर्मुच्य बाहुभ्यां वलयानि स वीर्यवान् ।चित्रे दुन्दुभिसंनादे प्रत्यमुञ्चत्तले शुभे ॥२३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato nirmucya bāhubhyāṁ valayāni sa vīryavān ,citre dundubhisaṁnāde pratyamuñcattale śubhe.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, that mighty one, having removed the bracelets from his two arms, fastened the auspicious armguards amidst the varied sound of the drums.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 23. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, that mighty one, having removed the bracelets from his two arms, fastened the auspicious armguards amidst the varied sound of the drums.

sanskrit: कृष्णान्भङ्गीमतः केशाञ्श्वेतेनोद्ग्रथ्य वाससा ।अधिज्यं तरसा कृत्वा गाण्डीवं व्याक्षिपद्धनुः ॥२४॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇānbhaṅgīmataḥ keśāñśvetenodgrathya vāsasā ,adhijyaṁ tarasā kṛtvā gāṇḍīvaṁ vyākṣipaddhanuḥ.
english: Having tied up his black, curly hair with a white cloth, he forcefully strung the Gāṇḍīva bow and drew it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 24. Having tied up his black, curly hair with a white cloth, he forcefully strung the Gāṇḍīva bow and drew it.

sanskrit: तस्य विक्षिप्यमाणस्य धनुषोऽभून्महास्वनः ।यथा शैलस्य महतः शैलेनैवाभिजघ्नुषः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasya vikṣipyamāṇasya dhanuṣo'bhūnmahāsvanaḥ ,yathā śailasya mahataḥ śailenaivābhijaghnuṣaḥ.
english: As his bow was being drawn, a tremendous sound emanated, like that of a huge mountain striking another mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 25. As his bow was being drawn, a tremendous sound emanated, like that of a huge mountain striking another mountain.

sanskrit: सनिर्घाताभवद्भूमिर्दिक्षु वायुर्ववौ भृशम् ।भ्रान्तद्विजं खं तदासीत्प्रकम्पितमहाद्रुमम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sanirghātābhavadbhūmirdikṣu vāyurvavau bhṛśam ,bhrāntadvijaṁ khaṁ tadāsītprakampitamahādrumam.
english: The earth became thunderous, and the wind blew fiercely in all directions. At that time, the sky was filled with agitated birds, and the mighty trees trembled greatly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 26. The earth became thunderous, and the wind blew fiercely in all directions. At that time, the sky was filled with agitated birds, and the mighty trees trembled greatly.

sanskrit: तं शब्दं कुरवोऽजानन्विस्फोटमशनेरिव ।यदर्जुनो धनुःश्रेष्ठं बाहुभ्यामाक्षिपद्रथे ॥२७॥
transliteration: taṁ śabdaṁ kuravo'jānanvisphoṭamaśaneriva ,yadarjuno dhanuḥśreṣṭhaṁ bāhubhyāmākṣipadrathe.
english: The Kurus knew that sound to be like the explosion of a thunderbolt, because Arjuna was twanging his excellent bow with his two arms while in his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 40 Verse 27. The Kurus knew that sound to be like the explosion of a thunderbolt, because Arjuna was twanging his excellent bow with his two arms while in his chariot.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उत्तरं सारथिं कृत्वा शमीं कृत्वा प्रदक्षिणम् ।आयुधं सर्वमादाय ततः प्रायाद्धनंजयः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,uttaraṁ sārathiṁ kṛtvā śamīṁ kṛtvā pradakṣiṇam ,āyudhaṁ sarvamādāya tataḥ prāyāddhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having made Uttara his charioteer and having circumambulated the śamī tree, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) then took all his weapons and set out.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having made Uttara his charioteer and having circumambulated the śamī tree, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) then took all his weapons and set out.

sanskrit: ध्वजं सिंहं रथात्तस्मादपनीय महारथः ।प्रणिधाय शमीमूले प्रायादुत्तरसारथिः ॥२॥
transliteration: dhvajaṁ siṁhaṁ rathāttasmādapanīya mahārathaḥ ,praṇidhāya śamīmūle prāyāduttarasārathiḥ.
english: The great charioteer (Arjuna), after taking down that lion banner from the chariot and placing it at the base of the śamī tree, then departed with Uttara as his charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 2. The great charioteer (Arjuna), after taking down that lion banner from the chariot and placing it at the base of the śamī tree, then departed with Uttara as his charioteer.

sanskrit: दैवीं मायां रथे युक्त्वा विहितां विश्वकर्मणा ।काञ्चनं सिंहलाङ्गूलं ध्वजं वानरलक्षणम् ॥३॥
transliteration: daivīṁ māyāṁ rathe yuktvā vihitāṁ viśvakarmaṇā ,kāñcanaṁ siṁhalāṅgūlaṁ dhvajaṁ vānaralakṣaṇam.
english: Having attached to the chariot a divine, creative power (māyā) fashioned by Viśvakarma, along with a golden lion's tail, a banner, and a monkey emblem.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 3. Having attached to the chariot a divine, creative power (māyā) fashioned by Viśvakarma, along with a golden lion's tail, a banner, and a monkey emblem.

sanskrit: मनसा चिन्तयामास प्रसादं पावकस्य च ।स च तच्चिन्तितं ज्ञात्वा ध्वजे भूतान्यचोदयत् ॥४॥
transliteration: manasā cintayāmāsa prasādaṁ pāvakasya ca ,sa ca taccintitaṁ jñātvā dhvaje bhūtānyacodayat.
english: He (Arjuna) meditated in his mind on the favor of Agni. And [Hanumān], knowing what had been thought, impelled [various] beings onto the banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 4. He (Arjuna) meditated in his mind on the favor of Agni. And [Hanumān], knowing what had been thought, impelled [various] beings onto the banner.

sanskrit: सपताकं विचित्राङ्गं सोपासङ्गं महारथः ।रथमास्थाय बीभत्सुः कौन्तेयः श्वेतवाहनः ॥५॥
transliteration: sapatākaṁ vicitrāṅgaṁ sopāsaṅgaṁ mahārathaḥ ,rathamāsthāya bībhatsuḥ kaunteyaḥ śvetavāhanaḥ.
english: The great warrior (mahāratha), Bibhatsu (bībhatsu), the son of Kunti (kaunteya), whose steeds were white (śvetavāhana), having ascended his chariot - which was adorned with flags, of intricate design, and equipped with quivers -
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 5. The great warrior (mahāratha), Bibhatsu (bībhatsu), the son of Kunti (kaunteya), whose steeds were white (śvetavāhana), having ascended his chariot - which was adorned with flags, of intricate design, and equipped with quivers -

sanskrit: बद्धासिः सतनुत्राणः प्रगृहीतशरासनः ।ततः प्रायादुदीचीं स कपिप्रवरकेतनः ॥६॥
transliteration: baddhāsiḥ satanutrāṇaḥ pragṛhītaśarāsanaḥ ,tataḥ prāyādudīcīṁ sa kapipravaraketanaḥ.
english: Having his sword fastened, wearing armor, and with his bow in hand, he (Arjuna) - whose banner bore the chief of monkeys (Hanumān) - then proceeded towards the north.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 6. Having his sword fastened, wearing armor, and with his bow in hand, he (Arjuna) - whose banner bore the chief of monkeys (Hanumān) - then proceeded towards the north.

sanskrit: स्वनवन्तं महाशङ्खं बलवानरिमर्दनः ।प्राधमद्बलमास्थाय द्विषतां लोमहर्षणम् ॥७॥
transliteration: svanavantaṁ mahāśaṅkhaṁ balavānarimardanaḥ ,prādhamadbalamāsthāya dviṣatāṁ lomaharṣaṇam.
english: The strong subduer of enemies (arimardanaḥ), employing his full strength, blew the resounding great conch shell, which was hair-raising for the adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 7. The strong subduer of enemies (arimardanaḥ), employing his full strength, blew the resounding great conch shell, which was hair-raising for the adversaries.

sanskrit: ततस्ते जवना धुर्या जानुभ्यामगमन्महीम् ।उत्तरश्चापि संत्रस्तो रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataste javanā dhuryā jānubhyāmagamanmahīm ,uttaraścāpi saṁtrasto rathopastha upāviśat.
english: Then, those swift leading horses collapsed to the ground on their knees. And Uttara, greatly terrified, sat down in the chariot's seat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 8. Then, those swift leading horses collapsed to the ground on their knees. And Uttara, greatly terrified, sat down in the chariot's seat.

sanskrit: संस्थाप्य चाश्वान्कौन्तेयः समुद्यम्य च रश्मिभिः ।उत्तरं च परिष्वज्य समाश्वासयदर्जुनः ॥९॥
transliteration: saṁsthāpya cāśvānkaunteyaḥ samudyamya ca raśmibhiḥ ,uttaraṁ ca pariṣvajya samāśvāsayadarjunaḥ.
english: Having stopped the horses and reined them in with the reins, Arjuna (Kaunteya) embraced Uttara and then comforted him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 9. Having stopped the horses and reined them in with the reins, Arjuna (Kaunteya) embraced Uttara and then comforted him.

sanskrit: मा भैस्त्वं राजपुत्राग्र्य क्षत्रियोऽसि परंतप ।कथं पुरुषशार्दूल शत्रुमध्ये विषीदसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: mā bhaistvaṁ rājaputrāgrya kṣatriyo'si paraṁtapa ,kathaṁ puruṣaśārdūla śatrumadhye viṣīdasi.
english: "Do not fear, O foremost among princes! You are a warrior (kṣatriya), O tormentor of enemies (paraṃtapa)! Why, O tiger among men (puruṣaśārdūla), do you despair in the midst of your foes?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 10. "Do not fear, O foremost among princes! You are a warrior (kṣatriya), O tormentor of enemies (paraṃtapa)! Why, O tiger among men (puruṣaśārdūla), do you despair in the midst of your foes?"

sanskrit: श्रुतास्ते शङ्खशब्दाश्च भेरीशब्दाश्च पुष्कलाः ।कुञ्जराणां च नदतां व्यूढानीकेषु तिष्ठताम् ॥११॥
transliteration: śrutāste śaṅkhaśabdāśca bherīśabdāśca puṣkalāḥ ,kuñjarāṇāṁ ca nadatāṁ vyūḍhānīkeṣu tiṣṭhatām.
english: You have heard the mighty sounds of conches and drums, and the roars of elephants standing within the arrayed armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 11. You have heard the mighty sounds of conches and drums, and the roars of elephants standing within the arrayed armies.

sanskrit: स त्वं कथमिहानेन शङ्खशब्देन भीषितः ।विषण्णरूपो वित्रस्तः पुरुषः प्राकृतो यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ kathamihānena śaṅkhaśabdena bhīṣitaḥ ,viṣaṇṇarūpo vitrastaḥ puruṣaḥ prākṛto yathā.
english: How is it that you are now frightened by this conch sound, appearing dejected and utterly terrified, just like an ordinary man?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 12. How is it that you are now frightened by this conch sound, appearing dejected and utterly terrified, just like an ordinary man?

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।श्रुता मे शङ्खशब्दाश्च भेरीशब्दाश्च पुष्कलाः ।कुञ्जराणां च निनदा व्यूढानीकेषु तिष्ठताम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,śrutā me śaṅkhaśabdāśca bherīśabdāśca puṣkalāḥ ,kuñjarāṇāṁ ca ninadā vyūḍhānīkeṣu tiṣṭhatām.
english: Uttara replied: I have indeed heard the mighty sounds of conches and drums, and the roars of elephants standing within the arrayed armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 13. Uttara replied: I have indeed heard the mighty sounds of conches and drums, and the roars of elephants standing within the arrayed armies.

sanskrit: नैवंविधः शङ्खशब्दः पुरा जातु मया श्रुतः ।ध्वजस्य चापि रूपं मे दृष्टपूर्वं न हीदृशम् ।धनुषश्चैव निर्घोषः श्रुतपूर्वो न मे क्वचित् ॥१४॥
transliteration: naivaṁvidhaḥ śaṅkhaśabdaḥ purā jātu mayā śrutaḥ ,dhvajasya cāpi rūpaṁ me dṛṣṭapūrvaṁ na hīdṛśam ,dhanuṣaścaiva nirghoṣaḥ śrutapūrvo na me kvacit.
english: I have never before heard a conch sound of this kind. Indeed, such a form of a banner has also never been seen by me previously. And the twang of a bow like this has never been heard by me anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 14. I have never before heard a conch sound of this kind. Indeed, such a form of a banner has also never been seen by me previously. And the twang of a bow like this has never been heard by me anywhere.

sanskrit: अस्य शङ्खस्य शब्देन धनुषो निस्वनेन च ।रथस्य च निनादेन मनो मुह्यति मे भृशम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: asya śaṅkhasya śabdena dhanuṣo nisvanena ca ,rathasya ca ninādena mano muhyati me bhṛśam.
english: By the sound of this conch, by the twang of the bow, and by the roar of the chariot, my mind is greatly bewildered.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 15. By the sound of this conch, by the twang of the bow, and by the roar of the chariot, my mind is greatly bewildered.

sanskrit: व्याकुलाश्च दिशः सर्वा हृदयं व्यथतीव मे ।ध्वजेन पिहिताः सर्वा दिशो न प्रतिभान्ति मे ।गाण्डीवस्य च शब्देन कर्णौ मे बधिरीकृतौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: vyākulāśca diśaḥ sarvā hṛdayaṁ vyathatīva me ,dhvajena pihitāḥ sarvā diśo na pratibhānti me ,gāṇḍīvasya ca śabdena karṇau me badhirīkṛtau.
english: And all directions are agitated, and my heart feels pain. All directions, concealed by the banner, do not appear to me. And by the sound of the Gāṇḍīva (bow), my ears have been deafened.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 16. And all directions are agitated, and my heart feels pain. All directions, concealed by the banner, do not appear to me. And by the sound of the Gāṇḍīva (bow), my ears have been deafened.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।एकान्ते रथमास्थाय पद्भ्यां त्वमवपीडय ।दृढं च रश्मीन्संयच्छ शङ्खं ध्मास्याम्यहं पुनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,ekānte rathamāsthāya padbhyāṁ tvamavapīḍaya ,dṛḍhaṁ ca raśmīnsaṁyaccha śaṅkhaṁ dhmāsyāmyahaṁ punaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: "Having placed the chariot aside in a secluded spot, press down with your feet. And firmly hold the reins. I will blow the conch again."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 17. Arjuna said: "Having placed the chariot aside in a secluded spot, press down with your feet. And firmly hold the reins. I will blow the conch again."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य शङ्खस्य शब्देन रथनेमिस्वनेन च ।गाण्डीवस्य च घोषेण पृथिवी समकम्पत ॥१८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya śaṅkhasya śabdena rathanemisvanena ca ,gāṇḍīvasya ca ghoṣeṇa pṛthivī samakampata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "By the sound of that conch, by the noise of the chariot wheels, and by the roar of the Gāṇḍīva (bow), the earth trembled."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 18. Vaiśampāyana said: "By the sound of that conch, by the noise of the chariot wheels, and by the roar of the Gāṇḍīva (bow), the earth trembled."

sanskrit: द्रोण उवाच ।यथा रथस्य निर्घोषो यथा शङ्ख उदीर्यते ।कम्पते च यथा भूमिर्नैषोऽन्यः सव्यसाचिनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: droṇa uvāca ,yathā rathasya nirghoṣo yathā śaṅkha udīryate ,kampate ca yathā bhūmirnaiṣo'nyaḥ savyasācinaḥ.
english: Droṇa said: The roar of the chariot, the way the conch is blown, and how the earth trembles  -  this can be no one else but the ambidextrous one (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 19. Droṇa said: The roar of the chariot, the way the conch is blown, and how the earth trembles  -  this can be no one else but the ambidextrous one (Arjuna).

sanskrit: शस्त्राणि न प्रकाशन्ते न प्रहृष्यन्ति वाजिनः ।अग्नयश्च न भासन्ते समिद्धास्तन्न शोभनम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: śastrāṇi na prakāśante na prahṛṣyanti vājinaḥ ,agnayaśca na bhāsante samiddhāstanna śobhanam.
english: Our weapons do not shine, the horses do not neigh joyfully, and even the blazing fires do not glow brightly. This is not a good sign.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 20. Our weapons do not shine, the horses do not neigh joyfully, and even the blazing fires do not glow brightly. This is not a good sign.

sanskrit: प्रत्यादित्यं च नः सर्वे मृगा घोरप्रवादिनः ।ध्वजेषु च निलीयन्ते वायसास्तन्न शोभनम् ।शकुनाश्चापसव्या नो वेदयन्ति महद्भयम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: pratyādityaṁ ca naḥ sarve mṛgā ghorapravādinaḥ ,dhvajeṣu ca nilīyante vāyasāstanna śobhanam ,śakunāścāpasavyā no vedayanti mahadbhayam.
english: All the fierce-sounding animals are facing the sun, and crows are perching on our banners. This is not a good sign. Furthermore, ill-omened birds (śakunā) on our left are indicating great fear for us.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 21. All the fierce-sounding animals are facing the sun, and crows are perching on our banners. This is not a good sign. Furthermore, ill-omened birds (śakunā) on our left are indicating great fear for us.

sanskrit: गोमायुरेष सेनाया रुवन्मध्येऽनुधावति ।अनाहतश्च निष्क्रान्तो महद्वेदयते भयम् ।भवतां रोमकूपाणि प्रहृष्टान्युपलक्षये ॥२२॥
transliteration: gomāyureṣa senāyā ruvanmadhye'nudhāvati ,anāhataśca niṣkrānto mahadvedayate bhayam ,bhavatāṁ romakūpāṇi prahṛṣṭānyupalakṣaye.
english: This jackal is howling and running through the middle of the army. It has come out unprovoked, indicating great fear. I observe your body hairs standing on end.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 22. This jackal is howling and running through the middle of the army. It has come out unprovoked, indicating great fear. I observe your body hairs standing on end.

sanskrit: पराभूता च वः सेना न कश्चिद्योद्धुमिच्छति ।विवर्णमुखभूयिष्ठाः सर्वे योधा विचेतसः ।गाः संप्रस्थाप्य तिष्ठामो व्यूढानीकाः प्रहारिणः ॥२३॥
transliteration: parābhūtā ca vaḥ senā na kaścidyoddhumicchati ,vivarṇamukhabhūyiṣṭhāḥ sarve yodhā vicetasaḥ ,gāḥ saṁprasthāpya tiṣṭhāmo vyūḍhānīkāḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: Your army is defeated, and no one wishes to fight. All the warriors are mostly pale-faced and bewildered. We will remain here, having sent the cows ahead, with our ranks arrayed and ready to attack.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 41 Verse 23. Your army is defeated, and no one wishes to fight. All the warriors are mostly pale-faced and bewildered. We will remain here, having sent the cows ahead, with our ranks arrayed and ready to attack.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथ दुर्योधनो राजा समरे भीष्ममब्रवीत् ।द्रोणं च रथशार्दूलं कृपं च सुमहारथम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,atha duryodhano rājā samare bhīṣmamabravīt ,droṇaṁ ca rathaśārdūlaṁ kṛpaṁ ca sumahāratham.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Duryodhana spoke in battle to Bhishma, and to Drona, that foremost of charioteers, and to Kripa, the great general.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Duryodhana spoke in battle to Bhishma, and to Drona, that foremost of charioteers, and to Kripa, the great general.

sanskrit: उक्तोऽयमर्थ आचार्यो मया कर्णेन चासकृत् ।पुनरेव च वक्ष्यामि न हि तृप्यामि तं ब्रुवन् ॥२॥
transliteration: ukto'yamartha ācāryo mayā karṇena cāsakṛt ,punareva ca vakṣyāmi na hi tṛpyāmi taṁ bruvan.
english: This matter, O Teacher, has been stated by Karna and me repeatedly. And I will speak of it again, for I am certainly not satisfied while speaking it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 2. This matter, O Teacher, has been stated by Karna and me repeatedly. And I will speak of it again, for I am certainly not satisfied while speaking it.

sanskrit: पराजितैर्हि वस्तव्यं तैश्च द्वादश वत्सरान् ।वने जनपदेऽज्ञातैरेष एव पणो हि नः ॥३॥
transliteration: parājitairhi vastavyaṁ taiśca dvādaśa vatsarān ,vane janapade'jñātaireṣa eva paṇo hi naḥ.
english: Indeed, it is stipulated that the defeated ones must dwell for twelve years, in the forest and in populated areas, unrecognized. This is certainly our wager.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 3. Indeed, it is stipulated that the defeated ones must dwell for twelve years, in the forest and in populated areas, unrecognized. This is certainly our wager.

sanskrit: तेषां न तावन्निर्वृत्तं वर्तते तु त्रयोदशम् ।अज्ञातवासं बीभत्सुरथास्माभिः समागतः ॥४॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ na tāvannirvṛttaṁ vartate tu trayodaśam ,ajñātavāsaṁ bībhatsurathāsmābhiḥ samāgataḥ.
english: Their thirteenth year is not yet completed. Yet, Arjuna (Bībhatsu) has now come among us during his period of living in disguise (ajñātavāsa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 4. Their thirteenth year is not yet completed. Yet, Arjuna (Bībhatsu) has now come among us during his period of living in disguise (ajñātavāsa).

sanskrit: अनिवृत्ते तु निर्वासे यदि बीभत्सुरागतः ।पुनर्द्वादश वर्षाणि वने वत्स्यन्ति पाण्डवाः ॥५॥
transliteration: anivṛtte tu nirvāse yadi bībhatsurāgataḥ ,punardvādaśa varṣāṇi vane vatsyanti pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: If Arjuna (Bībhatsu) has returned before the completion of their period of exile, then the Pāṇḍavas will have to dwell in the forest for another twelve years.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 5. If Arjuna (Bībhatsu) has returned before the completion of their period of exile, then the Pāṇḍavas will have to dwell in the forest for another twelve years.

sanskrit: लोभाद्वा ते न जानीयुरस्मान्वा मोह आविशत् ।हीनातिरिक्तमेतेषां भीष्मो वेदितुमर्हति ॥६॥
transliteration: lobhādvā te na jānīyurasmānvā moha āviśat ,hīnātiriktameteṣāṁ bhīṣmo veditumarhati.
english: Perhaps they do not recognize us due to greed, or perhaps delusion has overcome us. Bhishma is certainly capable of knowing what is deficient or excessive in their claim.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 6. Perhaps they do not recognize us due to greed, or perhaps delusion has overcome us. Bhishma is certainly capable of knowing what is deficient or excessive in their claim.

sanskrit: अर्थानां तु पुनर्द्वैधे नित्यं भवति संशयः ।अन्यथा चिन्तितो ह्यर्थः पुनर्भवति चान्यथा ॥७॥
transliteration: arthānāṁ tu punardvaidhe nityaṁ bhavati saṁśayaḥ ,anyathā cintito hyarthaḥ punarbhavati cānyathā.
english: Indeed, when there is a duality or dispute concerning matters, doubt always arises. A thing thought to be one way actually turns out to be another.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 7. Indeed, when there is a duality or dispute concerning matters, doubt always arises. A thing thought to be one way actually turns out to be another.

sanskrit: उत्तरं मार्गमाणानां मत्स्यसेनां युयुत्सताम् ।यदि बीभत्सुरायातस्तेषां कः स्यात्पराङ्मुखः ॥८॥
transliteration: uttaraṁ mārgamāṇānāṁ matsyasenāṁ yuyutsatām ,yadi bībhatsurāyātasteṣāṁ kaḥ syātparāṅmukhaḥ.
english: The Matsyas are seeking a northern route and are eager to fight the Matsya army. If Arjuna (Bhībhatsu) has arrived, who among them would turn away in retreat?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 8. The Matsyas are seeking a northern route and are eager to fight the Matsya army. If Arjuna (Bhībhatsu) has arrived, who among them would turn away in retreat?

sanskrit: त्रिगर्तानां वयं हेतोर्मत्स्यान्योद्धुमिहागताः ।मत्स्यानां विप्रकारांस्ते बहूनस्मानकीर्तयन् ॥९॥
transliteration: trigartānāṁ vayaṁ hetormatsyānyoddhumihāgatāḥ ,matsyānāṁ viprakārāṁste bahūnasmānakīrtayan.
english: For the sake of the Trigartas, we have come here to fight the Matsyas. They recounted to us many offenses (viprakāra) committed by the Matsyas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 9. For the sake of the Trigartas, we have come here to fight the Matsyas. They recounted to us many offenses (viprakāra) committed by the Matsyas.

sanskrit: तेषां भयाभिपन्नानां तदस्माभिः प्रतिश्रुतम् ।प्रथमं तैर्ग्रहीतव्यं मत्स्यानां गोधनं महत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ bhayābhipannānāṁ tadasmābhiḥ pratiśrutam ,prathamaṁ tairgrahītavyaṁ matsyānāṁ godhanaṁ mahat.
english: To those (Trigartas) who were struck with fear, this was promised by us: first, their great wealth of cattle (godhana) belonging to the Matsyas should be seized by them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 10. To those (Trigartas) who were struck with fear, this was promised by us: first, their great wealth of cattle (godhana) belonging to the Matsyas should be seized by them.

sanskrit: सप्तमीमपराह्णे वै तथा नस्तैः समाहितम् ।अष्टम्यां पुनरस्माभिरादित्यस्योदयं प्रति ॥११॥
transliteration: saptamīmaparāhṇe vai tathā nastaiḥ samāhitam ,aṣṭamyāṁ punarasmābhirādityasyodayaṁ prati.
english: Thus, on the afternoon of the seventh day, it was settled by them (the Trigartas) for us; and then, on the eighth day, by us, toward the rising of the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 11. Thus, on the afternoon of the seventh day, it was settled by them (the Trigartas) for us; and then, on the eighth day, by us, toward the rising of the sun.

sanskrit: ते वा गावो न पश्यन्ति यदि व स्युः पराजिताः ।अस्मान्वाप्यतिसंधाय कुर्युर्मत्स्येन संगतम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: te vā gāvo na paśyanti yadi va syuḥ parājitāḥ ,asmānvāpyatisaṁdhāya kuryurmatsyena saṁgatam.
english: Or perhaps they (our enemies) have not yet sighted the cows, or they might be defeated. Or, having outmaneuvered us, they could form an alliance with King Matsya (Virāṭa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 12. Or perhaps they (our enemies) have not yet sighted the cows, or they might be defeated. Or, having outmaneuvered us, they could form an alliance with King Matsya (Virāṭa).

sanskrit: अथ वा तानुपायातो मत्स्यो जानपदैः सह ।सर्वया सेनया सार्धमस्मान्योद्धुमुपागतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: atha vā tānupāyāto matsyo jānapadaiḥ saha ,sarvayā senayā sārdhamasmānyoddhumupāgataḥ.
english: Or else, King Matsya (Virāṭa) himself, having approached his people, has arrived with his countrymen and his entire army to fight us.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 13. Or else, King Matsya (Virāṭa) himself, having approached his people, has arrived with his countrymen and his entire army to fight us.

sanskrit: तेषामेव महावीर्यः कश्चिदेव पुरःसरः ।अस्माञ्जेतुमिहायातो मत्स्यो वापि स्वयं भवेत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: teṣāmeva mahāvīryaḥ kaścideva puraḥsaraḥ ,asmāñjetumihāyāto matsyo vāpi svayaṁ bhavet.
english: Indeed, some greatly powerful leader from among them has come here to conquer us, or it might even be King Matsya (Virāṭa) himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 14. Indeed, some greatly powerful leader from among them has come here to conquer us, or it might even be King Matsya (Virāṭa) himself.

sanskrit: यद्येष राजा मत्स्यानां यदि बीभत्सुरागतः ।सर्वैर्योद्धव्यमस्माभिरिति नः समयः कृतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: yadyeṣa rājā matsyānāṁ yadi bībhatsurāgataḥ ,sarvairyoddhavyamasmābhiriti naḥ samayaḥ kṛtaḥ.
english: If this is the King of the Matsyas, or if Bibhatsu (Arjuna) has arrived, then it is our agreement that all of us must fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 15. If this is the King of the Matsyas, or if Bibhatsu (Arjuna) has arrived, then it is our agreement that all of us must fight.

sanskrit: अथ कस्मात्स्थिता ह्येते रथेषु रथसत्तमाः ।भीष्मो द्रोणः कृपश्चैव विकर्णो द्रौणिरेव च ॥१६॥
transliteration: atha kasmātsthitā hyete ratheṣu rathasattamāḥ ,bhīṣmo droṇaḥ kṛpaścaiva vikarṇo drauṇireva ca.
english: Now, why are these foremost chariot-warriors - Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Vikarna, and Drona's son - standing in their chariots?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 16. Now, why are these foremost chariot-warriors - Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Vikarna, and Drona's son - standing in their chariots?

sanskrit: संभ्रान्तमनसः सर्वे काले ह्यस्मिन्महारथाः ।नान्यत्र युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽस्ति तथात्मा प्रणिधीयताम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: saṁbhrāntamanasaḥ sarve kāle hyasminmahārathāḥ ,nānyatra yuddhācchreyo'sti tathātmā praṇidhīyatām.
english: Indeed, all these great chariot-warriors are bewildered in mind at this moment. There is no greater good (śreyas) than battle, so let the self (ātman) be steadfastly applied to it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 17. Indeed, all these great chariot-warriors are bewildered in mind at this moment. There is no greater good (śreyas) than battle, so let the self (ātman) be steadfastly applied to it.

sanskrit: आच्छिन्ने गोधनेऽस्माकमपि देवेन वज्रिणा ।यमेन वापि संग्रामे को हास्तिनपुरं व्रजेत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ācchinne godhane'smākamapi devena vajriṇā ,yamena vāpi saṁgrāme ko hāstinapuraṁ vrajet.
english: If our cattle-wealth were to be seized, even by the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrin) or by Yama himself in battle, who indeed would return to Hastinapura?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 18. If our cattle-wealth were to be seized, even by the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrin) or by Yama himself in battle, who indeed would return to Hastinapura?

sanskrit: शरैरभिप्रणुन्नानां भग्नानां गहने वने ।को हि जीवेत्पदातीनां भवेदश्वेषु संशयः ।आचार्यं पृष्ठतः कृत्वा तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: śarairabhipraṇunnānāṁ bhagnānāṁ gahane vane ,ko hi jīvetpadātīnāṁ bhavedaśveṣu saṁśayaḥ ,ācāryaṁ pṛṣṭhataḥ kṛtvā tathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: Who, among foot soldiers, would survive if they were thoroughly repelled by arrows and routed in a dense forest? Even for those on horseback, there would be doubt. Therefore, let a strategy (nīti) be arranged with the preceptor (ācārya) kept safe behind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 19. Who, among foot soldiers, would survive if they were thoroughly repelled by arrows and routed in a dense forest? Even for those on horseback, there would be doubt. Therefore, let a strategy (nīti) be arranged with the preceptor (ācārya) kept safe behind.

sanskrit: जानाति हि मतं तेषामतस्त्रासयतीव नः ।अर्जुनेनास्य संप्रीतिमधिकामुपलक्षये ॥२०॥
transliteration: jānāti hi mataṁ teṣāmatastrāsayatīva naḥ ,arjunenāsya saṁprītimadhikāmupalakṣaye.
english: Indeed, he knows their intention, and therefore he seems to frighten us. I observe his great affection for Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 20. Indeed, he knows their intention, and therefore he seems to frighten us. I observe his great affection for Arjuna.

sanskrit: तथा हि दृष्ट्वा बीभत्सुमुपायान्तं प्रशंसति ।यथा सेना न भज्येत तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathā hi dṛṣṭvā bībhatsumupāyāntaṁ praśaṁsati ,yathā senā na bhajyeta tathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: Indeed, upon seeing Bībhatsu (Arjuna) approaching, he praises him. Therefore, let a strategy (nīti) be devised so that the army may not be broken.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 21. Indeed, upon seeing Bībhatsu (Arjuna) approaching, he praises him. Therefore, let a strategy (nīti) be devised so that the army may not be broken.

sanskrit: अदेशिका महारण्ये ग्रीष्मे शत्रुवशं गता ।यथा न विभ्रमेत्सेना तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: adeśikā mahāraṇye grīṣme śatruvaśaṁ gatā ,yathā na vibhrametsenā tathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: Being without a guide in a great forest, and having fallen into the enemy's power during summer, let a strategy (nīti) be devised so that the army does not become disoriented.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 22. Being without a guide in a great forest, and having fallen into the enemy's power during summer, let a strategy (nīti) be devised so that the army does not become disoriented.

sanskrit: अश्वानां हेषितं श्रुत्वा का प्रशंसा भवेत्परे ।स्थाने वापि व्रजन्तो वा सदा हेषन्ति वाजिनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: aśvānāṁ heṣitaṁ śrutvā kā praśaṁsā bhavetpare ,sthāne vāpi vrajanto vā sadā heṣanti vājinaḥ.
english: Having heard the neighing of horses, what praise can there be for the enemy? For horses always neigh, whether they are standing or moving.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 23. Having heard the neighing of horses, what praise can there be for the enemy? For horses always neigh, whether they are standing or moving.

sanskrit: सदा च वायवो वान्ति नित्यं वर्षति वासवः ।स्तनयित्नोश्च निर्घोषः श्रूयते बहुशस्तथा ॥२४॥
transliteration: sadā ca vāyavo vānti nityaṁ varṣati vāsavaḥ ,stanayitnośca nirghoṣaḥ śrūyate bahuśastathā.
english: The winds always blow, and Indra continually sends rain. Similarly, the roar of thunder is heard frequently.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 24. The winds always blow, and Indra continually sends rain. Similarly, the roar of thunder is heard frequently.

sanskrit: किमत्र कार्यं पार्थस्य कथं वा स प्रशस्यते ।अन्यत्र कामाद्द्वेषाद्वा रोषाद्वास्मासु केवलात् ॥२५॥
transliteration: kimatra kāryaṁ pārthasya kathaṁ vā sa praśasyate ,anyatra kāmāddveṣādvā roṣādvāsmāsu kevalāt.
english: What is Arjuna's business here, or how is he praised, except out of mere desire, hatred, or anger directed solely at us?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 25. What is Arjuna's business here, or how is he praised, except out of mere desire, hatred, or anger directed solely at us?

sanskrit: आचार्या वै कारुणिकाः प्राज्ञाश्चापायदर्शिनः ।नैते महाभये प्राप्ते संप्रष्टव्याः कथंचन ॥२६॥
transliteration: ācāryā vai kāruṇikāḥ prājñāścāpāyadarśinaḥ ,naite mahābhaye prāpte saṁpraṣṭavyāḥ kathaṁcana.
english: Indeed, teachers are compassionate and wise, foreseeing dangers. However, when a great danger has arrived, they should never be consulted.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 26. Indeed, teachers are compassionate and wise, foreseeing dangers. However, when a great danger has arrived, they should never be consulted.

sanskrit: प्रासादेषु विचित्रेषु गोष्ठीष्वावसथेषु च ।कथा विचित्राः कुर्वाणाः पण्डितास्तत्र शोभनाः ॥२७॥
transliteration: prāsādeṣu vicitreṣu goṣṭhīṣvāvasatheṣu ca ,kathā vicitrāḥ kurvāṇāḥ paṇḍitāstatra śobhanāḥ.
english: In splendid palaces, gatherings, and residences, scholars who tell fascinating stories appear brilliant there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 27. In splendid palaces, gatherings, and residences, scholars who tell fascinating stories appear brilliant there.

sanskrit: बहून्याश्चर्यरूपाणि कुर्वन्तो जनसंसदि ।इष्वस्त्रे चारुसंधाने पण्डितास्तत्र शोभनाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: bahūnyāścaryarūpāṇi kurvanto janasaṁsadi ,iṣvastre cārusaṁdhāne paṇḍitāstatra śobhanāḥ.
english: Experts who perform many wondrous feats in public gatherings, especially regarding the skillful aiming and fitting of arrows, are brilliant in that area.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 28. Experts who perform many wondrous feats in public gatherings, especially regarding the skillful aiming and fitting of arrows, are brilliant in that area.

sanskrit: परेषां विवरज्ञाने मनुष्याचरितेषु च ।अन्नसंस्कारदोषेषु पण्डितास्तत्र शोभनाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: pareṣāṁ vivarajñāne manuṣyācariteṣu ca ,annasaṁskāradoṣeṣu paṇḍitāstatra śobhanāḥ.
english: Experts are brilliant in understanding the weak points of others, discerning human conduct, and identifying faults in food preparation.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 29. Experts are brilliant in understanding the weak points of others, discerning human conduct, and identifying faults in food preparation.

sanskrit: पण्डितान्पृष्ठतः कृत्वा परेषां गुणवादिनः ।विधीयतां तथा नीतिर्यथा वध्येत वै परः ॥३०॥
transliteration: paṇḍitānpṛṣṭhataḥ kṛtvā pareṣāṁ guṇavādinaḥ ,vidhīyatāṁ tathā nītiryathā vadhyeta vai paraḥ.
english: Setting aside the learned (paṇḍita) and those who praise the virtues of others, let such a policy (nīti) be enacted that the enemy may indeed be annihilated.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 30. Setting aside the learned (paṇḍita) and those who praise the virtues of others, let such a policy (nīti) be enacted that the enemy may indeed be annihilated.

sanskrit: गावश्चैव प्रतिष्ठन्तां सेनां व्यूहन्तु माचिरम् ।आरक्षाश्च विधीयन्तां यत्र योत्स्यामहे परान् ॥३१॥
transliteration: gāvaścaiva pratiṣṭhantāṁ senāṁ vyūhantu māciram ,ārakṣāśca vidhīyantāṁ yatra yotsyāmahe parān.
english: Let the cows be secured, and let the army be arrayed without delay. Guards should also be appointed where we are to fight the enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 42 Verse 31. Let the cows be secured, and let the army be arrayed without delay. Guards should also be appointed where we are to fight the enemies.

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।सर्वानायुष्मतो भीतान्संत्रस्तानिव लक्षये ।अयुद्धमनसश्चैव सर्वांश्चैवानवस्थितान् ॥१॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,sarvānāyuṣmato bhītānsaṁtrastāniva lakṣaye ,ayuddhamanasaścaiva sarvāṁścaivānavasthitān.
english: Karṇa said: "I see all these long-lived warriors, fearful and as if terrified. Furthermore, all of them are disinclined to fight and appear agitated."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 1. Karṇa said: "I see all these long-lived warriors, fearful and as if terrified. Furthermore, all of them are disinclined to fight and appear agitated."

sanskrit: यद्येष राजा मत्स्यानां यदि बीभत्सुरागतः ।अहमावारयिष्यामि वेलेव मकरालयम् ॥२॥
transliteration: yadyeṣa rājā matsyānāṁ yadi bībhatsurāgataḥ ,ahamāvārayiṣyāmi veleva makarālayam.
english: Whether this is the king of the Matsyas or if Bhībhantsu (Arjuna) has arrived, I will hold him back, just as a shore holds back the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 2. Whether this is the king of the Matsyas or if Bhībhantsu (Arjuna) has arrived, I will hold him back, just as a shore holds back the ocean.

sanskrit: मम चापप्रमुक्तानां शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ।नावृत्तिर्गच्छतामस्ति सर्पाणामिव सर्पताम् ॥३॥
transliteration: mama cāpapramuktānāṁ śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām ,nāvṛttirgacchatāmasti sarpāṇāmiva sarpatām.
english: My arrows, released from the bow, with their bent shafts, have no return journey, just like serpents that are moving (forward).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 3. My arrows, released from the bow, with their bent shafts, have no return journey, just like serpents that are moving (forward).

sanskrit: रुक्मपुङ्खाः सुतीक्ष्णाग्रा मुक्ता हस्तवता मया ।छादयन्तु शराः पार्थं शलभा इव पादपम् ॥४॥
transliteration: rukmapuṅkhāḥ sutīkṣṇāgrā muktā hastavatā mayā ,chādayantu śarāḥ pārthaṁ śalabhā iva pādapam.
english: My arrows, which have golden shafts and very sharp points, released by my skilled hand, shall cover Pārtha (Arjuna) just as locusts cover a tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 4. My arrows, which have golden shafts and very sharp points, released by my skilled hand, shall cover Pārtha (Arjuna) just as locusts cover a tree.

sanskrit: शराणां पुङ्खसक्तानां मौर्व्याभिहतया दृढम् ।श्रूयतां तलयोः शब्दो भेर्योराहतयोरिव ॥५॥
transliteration: śarāṇāṁ puṅkhasaktānāṁ maurvyābhihatayā dṛḍham ,śrūyatāṁ talayoḥ śabdo bheryorāhatayoriva.
english: Let the strong sound of the palms, struck firmly by the bowstring as arrows are nocked, be heard, like the sound of two beaten drums.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 5. Let the strong sound of the palms, struck firmly by the bowstring as arrows are nocked, be heard, like the sound of two beaten drums.

sanskrit: समाहितो हि बीभत्सुर्वर्षाण्यष्टौ च पञ्च च ।जातस्नेहश्च युद्धस्य मयि संप्रहरिष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: samāhito hi bībhatsurvarṣāṇyaṣṭau ca pañca ca ,jātasnehaśca yuddhasya mayi saṁprahariṣyati.
english: Indeed, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), having been attentive for thirteen (eight and five) years and having developed a fondness for battle, will certainly strike against me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 6. Indeed, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), having been attentive for thirteen (eight and five) years and having developed a fondness for battle, will certainly strike against me.

sanskrit: पात्रीभूतश्च कौन्तेयो ब्राह्मणो गुणवानिव ।शरौघान्प्रतिगृह्णातु मया मुक्तान्सहस्रशः ॥७॥
transliteration: pātrībhūtaśca kaunteyo brāhmaṇo guṇavāniva ,śaraughānpratigṛhṇātu mayā muktānsahasraśaḥ.
english: Let Kaunteya (Arjuna), who has become a worthy recipient like a virtuous Brahmin, accept the thousands of arrow-streams released by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 7. Let Kaunteya (Arjuna), who has become a worthy recipient like a virtuous Brahmin, accept the thousands of arrow-streams released by me.

sanskrit: एष चैव महेष्वासस्त्रिषु लोकेषु विश्रुतः ।अहं चापि कुरुश्रेष्ठा अर्जुनान्नावरः क्वचित् ॥८॥
transliteration: eṣa caiva maheṣvāsastriṣu lokeṣu viśrutaḥ ,ahaṁ cāpi kuruśreṣṭhā arjunānnāvaraḥ kvacit.
english: And this great archer (Arjuna) is indeed renowned in the three worlds. Yet, O best of the Kurus, I am also certainly not inferior to Arjuna anywhere at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 8. And this great archer (Arjuna) is indeed renowned in the three worlds. Yet, O best of the Kurus, I am also certainly not inferior to Arjuna anywhere at all.

sanskrit: इतश्चेतश्च निर्मुक्तैः काञ्चनैर्गार्ध्रवाजितैः ।दृश्यतामद्य वै व्योम खद्योतैरिव संवृतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: itaścetaśca nirmuktaiḥ kāñcanairgārdhravājitaiḥ ,dṛśyatāmadya vai vyoma khadyotairiva saṁvṛtam.
english: Behold, today the sky appears covered, here and there, by released golden arrows feathered with vulture feathers, looking like fireflies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 9. Behold, today the sky appears covered, here and there, by released golden arrows feathered with vulture feathers, looking like fireflies.

sanskrit: अद्याहमृणमक्षय्यं पुरा वाचा प्रतिश्रुतम् ।धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दास्यामि निहत्य समरेऽर्जुनम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: adyāhamṛṇamakṣayyaṁ purā vācā pratiśrutam ,dhārtarāṣṭrasya dāsyāmi nihatya samare'rjunam.
english: Today, I will repay the inexhaustible debt that I formerly promised by word to the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, after killing Arjuna in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 10. Today, I will repay the inexhaustible debt that I formerly promised by word to the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, after killing Arjuna in battle.

sanskrit: अन्तरा छिद्यमानानां पुङ्खानां व्यतिशीर्यताम् ।शलभानामिवाकाशे प्रचारः संप्रदृश्यताम् ॥११॥
transliteration: antarā chidyamānānāṁ puṅkhānāṁ vyatiśīryatām ,śalabhānāmivākāśe pracāraḥ saṁpradṛśyatām.
english: Observe the movement, in the sky, of the arrow shafts being cut and scattered, just like the flight of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 11. Observe the movement, in the sky, of the arrow shafts being cut and scattered, just like the flight of locusts.

sanskrit: इन्द्राशनिसमस्पर्शं महेन्द्रसमतेजसम् ।अर्दयिष्याम्यहं पार्थमुल्काभिरिव कुञ्जरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: indrāśanisamasparśaṁ mahendrasamatejasam ,ardayiṣyāmyahaṁ pārthamulkābhiriva kuñjaram.
english: I will torment Pārtha, whose touch is like Indra's thunderbolt and whose splendor equals the great Indra, just as one would torment an elephant with firebrands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 12. I will torment Pārtha, whose touch is like Indra's thunderbolt and whose splendor equals the great Indra, just as one would torment an elephant with firebrands.

sanskrit: तमग्निमिव दुर्धर्षमसिशक्तिशरेन्धनम् ।पाण्डवाग्निमहं दीप्तं प्रदहन्तमिवाहितान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tamagnimiva durdharṣamasiśaktiśarendhanam ,pāṇḍavāgnimahaṁ dīptaṁ pradahantamivāhitān.
english: I behold that blazing Pāṇḍava-fire, which is as irresistible as fire itself, its fuel consisting of swords, spears, and arrows, and which appears to be consuming enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 13. I behold that blazing Pāṇḍava-fire, which is as irresistible as fire itself, its fuel consisting of swords, spears, and arrows, and which appears to be consuming enemies.

sanskrit: अश्ववेगपुरोवातो रथौघस्तनयित्नुमान् ।शरधारो महामेघः शमयिष्यामि पाण्डवम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: aśvavegapurovāto rathaughastanayitnumān ,śaradhāro mahāmeghaḥ śamayiṣyāmi pāṇḍavam.
english: I will extinguish the Pāṇḍava, who is like a great cloud with the speed of horses as its forefront wind, with a multitude of chariots as its thunder, and with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 14. I will extinguish the Pāṇḍava, who is like a great cloud with the speed of horses as its forefront wind, with a multitude of chariots as its thunder, and with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: मत्कार्मुकविनिर्मुक्ताः पार्थमाशीविषोपमाः ।शराः समभिसर्पन्तु वल्मीकमिव पन्नगाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: matkārmukavinirmuktāḥ pārthamāśīviṣopamāḥ ,śarāḥ samabhisarpantu valmīkamiva pannagāḥ.
english: Let the arrows, released from my bow and resembling venomous serpents, creep towards Pārtha (Arjuna) just as snakes creep towards an anthill.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 15. Let the arrows, released from my bow and resembling venomous serpents, creep towards Pārtha (Arjuna) just as snakes creep towards an anthill.

sanskrit: जामदग्न्यान्मया ह्यस्त्रं यत्प्राप्तमृषिसत्तमात् ।तदुपाश्रित्य वीर्यं च युध्येयमपि वासवम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: jāmadagnyānmayā hyastraṁ yatprāptamṛṣisattamāt ,tadupāśritya vīryaṁ ca yudhyeyamapi vāsavam.
english: Indeed, relying on that missile (astra) which I received from Jamadagni's son (Paraśurāma), the foremost of sages, and [relying on my] valor (vīrya), I would fight even Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 16. Indeed, relying on that missile (astra) which I received from Jamadagni's son (Paraśurāma), the foremost of sages, and [relying on my] valor (vīrya), I would fight even Indra.

sanskrit: ध्वजाग्रे वानरस्तिष्ठन्भल्लेन निहतो मया ।अद्यैव पततां भूमौ विनदन्भैरवान्रवान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: dhvajāgre vānarastiṣṭhanbhallena nihato mayā ,adyaiva patatāṁ bhūmau vinadanbhairavānravān.
english: Let the monkey (Hanuman), standing at the top of the banner, struck down by my arrow, fall to the ground today itself, emitting terrifying roars.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 17. Let the monkey (Hanuman), standing at the top of the banner, struck down by my arrow, fall to the ground today itself, emitting terrifying roars.

sanskrit: शत्रोर्मयाभिपन्नानां भूतानां ध्वजवासिनाम् ।दिशः प्रतिष्ठमानानामस्तु शब्दो दिवं गतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śatrormayābhipannānāṁ bhūtānāṁ dhvajavāsinām ,diśaḥ pratiṣṭhamānānāmastu śabdo divaṁ gataḥ.
english: May the sound of those beings dwelling on the enemy's banner, who have taken refuge with him, as they flee in all directions, reach the heavens (divam), [caused] by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 18. May the sound of those beings dwelling on the enemy's banner, who have taken refuge with him, as they flee in all directions, reach the heavens (divam), [caused] by me.

sanskrit: अद्य दुर्योधनस्याहं शल्यं हृदि चिरस्थितम् ।समूलमुद्धरिष्यामि बीभत्सुं पातयन्रथात् ॥१९॥
transliteration: adya duryodhanasyāhaṁ śalyaṁ hṛdi cirasthitam ,samūlamuddhariṣyāmi bībhatsuṁ pātayanrathāt.
english: Today I shall completely extract the long-standing thorn (śalya) from Duryodhana's heart, by throwing Arjuna (Bībhatsu) from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 19. Today I shall completely extract the long-standing thorn (śalya) from Duryodhana's heart, by throwing Arjuna (Bībhatsu) from his chariot.

sanskrit: हताश्वं विरथं पार्थं पौरुषे पर्यवस्थितम् ।निःश्वसन्तं यथा नागमद्य पश्यन्तु कौरवाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: hatāśvaṁ virathaṁ pārthaṁ pauruṣe paryavasthitam ,niḥśvasantaṁ yathā nāgamadya paśyantu kauravāḥ.
english: Let the Kauravas see Arjuna (Pārtha) today: his horses slain, his chariot lost, relying solely on his valor (pauruṣa), and hissing like a serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 20. Let the Kauravas see Arjuna (Pārtha) today: his horses slain, his chariot lost, relying solely on his valor (pauruṣa), and hissing like a serpent.

sanskrit: कामं गच्छन्तु कुरवो धनमादाय केवलम् ।रथेषु वापि तिष्ठन्तो युद्धं पश्यन्तु मामकम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: kāmaṁ gacchantu kuravo dhanamādāya kevalam ,ratheṣu vāpi tiṣṭhanto yuddhaṁ paśyantu māmakam.
english: The Kurus may indeed go, taking only their wealth, or they may even remain in their chariots and simply observe my battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 43 Verse 21. The Kurus may indeed go, taking only their wealth, or they may even remain in their chariots and simply observe my battle.

sanskrit: कृप उवाच ।सदैव तव राधेय युद्धे क्रूरतरा मतिः ।नार्थानां प्रकृतिं वेत्थ नानुबन्धमवेक्षसे ॥१॥
transliteration: kṛpa uvāca ,sadaiva tava rādheya yuddhe krūratarā matiḥ ,nārthānāṁ prakṛtiṁ vettha nānubandhamavekṣase.
english: Kṛpa said: O son of Rādhā, your mind (mati) is always exceedingly cruel in battle. You neither know the true nature (prakṛti) of matters nor do you consider their consequences.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 1. Kṛpa said: O son of Rādhā, your mind (mati) is always exceedingly cruel in battle. You neither know the true nature (prakṛti) of matters nor do you consider their consequences.

sanskrit: नया हि बहवः सन्ति शास्त्राण्याश्रित्य चिन्तिताः ।तेषां युद्धं तु पापिष्ठं वेदयन्ति पुराविदः ॥२॥
transliteration: nayā hi bahavaḥ santi śāstrāṇyāśritya cintitāḥ ,teṣāṁ yuddhaṁ tu pāpiṣṭhaṁ vedayanti purāvidaḥ.
english: Indeed, many policies (naya) are devised by relying on the scriptures (śāstra). But among these, ancient scholars (purāvida) declare war to be the most sinful (pāpiṣṭha).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 2. Indeed, many policies (naya) are devised by relying on the scriptures (śāstra). But among these, ancient scholars (purāvida) declare war to be the most sinful (pāpiṣṭha).

sanskrit: देशकालेन संयुक्तं युद्धं विजयदं भवेत् ।हीनकालं तदेवेह फलवन्न भवत्युत ।देशे काले च विक्रान्तं कल्याणाय विधीयते ॥३॥
transliteration: deśakālena saṁyuktaṁ yuddhaṁ vijayadaṁ bhavet ,hīnakālaṁ tadeveha phalavanna bhavatyuta ,deśe kāle ca vikrāntaṁ kalyāṇāya vidhīyate.
english: A battle (yuddha) that is aligned with the proper place and time (deśakāla) may bring victory. But that very battle, if ill-timed, is not fruitful here. And a valiant act (vikrānta), when carried out at the right place and time, is conducive to well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 3. A battle (yuddha) that is aligned with the proper place and time (deśakāla) may bring victory. But that very battle, if ill-timed, is not fruitful here. And a valiant act (vikrānta), when carried out at the right place and time, is conducive to well-being.

sanskrit: आनुकूल्येन कार्याणामन्तरं संविधीयताम् ।भारं हि रथकारस्य न व्यवस्यन्ति पण्डिताः ॥४॥
transliteration: ānukūlyena kāryāṇāmantaraṁ saṁvidhīyatām ,bhāraṁ hi rathakārasya na vyavasyanti paṇḍitāḥ.
english: One should manage the intricacies of tasks favorably. Indeed, wise people (paṇḍitāḥ) do not undertake the burden of a chariot-maker.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 4. One should manage the intricacies of tasks favorably. Indeed, wise people (paṇḍitāḥ) do not undertake the burden of a chariot-maker.

sanskrit: परिचिन्त्य तु पार्थेन संनिपातो न नः क्षमः ।एकः कुरूनभ्यरक्षदेकश्चाग्निमतर्पयत् ॥५॥
transliteration: paricintya tu pārthena saṁnipāto na naḥ kṣamaḥ ,ekaḥ kurūnabhyarakṣadekaścāgnimatarpayat.
english: However, having considered by Arjuna (Pārtha), a confrontation is not suitable for us. One (Bhīma) protected the Kurus, and another (Arjuna) satisfied Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 5. However, having considered by Arjuna (Pārtha), a confrontation is not suitable for us. One (Bhīma) protected the Kurus, and another (Arjuna) satisfied Agni.

sanskrit: एकश्च पञ्च वर्षाणि ब्रह्मचर्यमधारयत् ।एकः सुभद्रामारोप्य द्वैरथे कृष्णमाह्वयत् ।अस्मिन्नेव वने कृष्णो हृतां कृष्णामवाजयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: ekaśca pañca varṣāṇi brahmacaryamadhārayat ,ekaḥ subhadrāmāropya dvairathe kṛṣṇamāhvayat ,asminneva vane kṛṣṇo hṛtāṁ kṛṣṇāmavājayat.
english: One (Arjuna) observed celibacy (brahmacarya) for five years. One (Arjuna), having placed Subhadrā (on his chariot), challenged Kṛṣṇa to a chariot-borne combat. In this very forest, Kṛṣṇa recovered the abducted Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 6. One (Arjuna) observed celibacy (brahmacarya) for five years. One (Arjuna), having placed Subhadrā (on his chariot), challenged Kṛṣṇa to a chariot-borne combat. In this very forest, Kṛṣṇa recovered the abducted Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā).

sanskrit: एकश्च पञ्च वर्षाणि शक्रादस्त्राण्यशिक्षत ।एकः सांयमिनीं जित्वा कुरूणामकरोद्यशः ॥७॥
transliteration: ekaśca pañca varṣāṇi śakrādastrāṇyaśikṣata ,ekaḥ sāṁyaminīṁ jitvā kurūṇāmakarodyaśaḥ.
english: And one (Arjuna) learned divine weapons (astras) from Indra (Śakra) for five years. One (Bhīma), having conquered Saṃyaminī, brought fame to the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 7. And one (Arjuna) learned divine weapons (astras) from Indra (Śakra) for five years. One (Bhīma), having conquered Saṃyaminī, brought fame to the Kurus.

sanskrit: एको गन्धर्वराजानं चित्रसेनमरिंदमः ।विजिग्ये तरसा संख्ये सेनां चास्य सुदुर्जयाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: eko gandharvarājānaṁ citrasenamariṁdamaḥ ,vijigye tarasā saṁkhye senāṁ cāsya sudurjayām.
english: The subduer of enemies (ariṃdama), he alone, conquered King Citrasena of the Gandharvas and his very formidable army with great force in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 8. The subduer of enemies (ariṃdama), he alone, conquered King Citrasena of the Gandharvas and his very formidable army with great force in battle.

sanskrit: तथा निवातकवचाः कालखञ्जाश्च दानवाः ।दैवतैरप्यवध्यास्ते एकेन युधि पातिताः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathā nivātakavacāḥ kālakhañjāśca dānavāḥ ,daivatairapyavadhyāste ekena yudhi pātitāḥ.
english: Similarly, the Dānavas known as Nivātakavacas and Kālakhañjas, who were impossible to kill even for the gods, were struck down in battle by one person (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 9. Similarly, the Dānavas known as Nivātakavacas and Kālakhañjas, who were impossible to kill even for the gods, were struck down in battle by one person (Arjuna).

sanskrit: एकेन हि त्वया कर्ण किं नामेह कृतं पुरा ।एकैकेन यथा तेषां भूमिपाला वशीकृताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ekena hi tvayā karṇa kiṁ nāmeha kṛtaṁ purā ,ekaikena yathā teṣāṁ bhūmipālā vaśīkṛtāḥ.
english: O Karṇa, what indeed has been accomplished by you alone in this world previously? Consider how by each one of them (Arjuna and the other Pāṇḍavas), the kings of the earth were subdued.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 10. O Karṇa, what indeed has been accomplished by you alone in this world previously? Consider how by each one of them (Arjuna and the other Pāṇḍavas), the kings of the earth were subdued.

sanskrit: इन्द्रोऽपि हि न पार्थेन संयुगे योद्धुमर्हति ।यस्तेनाशंसते योद्धुं कर्तव्यं तस्य भेषजम् ॥११॥
transliteration: indro'pi hi na pārthena saṁyuge yoddhumarhati ,yastenāśaṁsate yoddhuṁ kartavyaṁ tasya bheṣajam.
english: Indeed, not even Indra is capable of fighting Pārtha (Arjuna) in battle. For anyone who desires to fight him, a remedy (bheṣajam) should be administered to that person.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 11. Indeed, not even Indra is capable of fighting Pārtha (Arjuna) in battle. For anyone who desires to fight him, a remedy (bheṣajam) should be administered to that person.

sanskrit: आशीविषस्य क्रुद्धस्य पाणिमुद्यम्य दक्षिणम् ।अविमृश्य प्रदेशिन्या दंष्ट्रामादातुमिच्छसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: āśīviṣasya kruddhasya pāṇimudyamya dakṣiṇam ,avimṛśya pradeśinyā daṁṣṭrāmādātumicchasi.
english: You wish to grasp the fangs of a furious venomous snake with your right hand, without considering the danger, by using your index finger.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 12. You wish to grasp the fangs of a furious venomous snake with your right hand, without considering the danger, by using your index finger.

sanskrit: अथ वा कुञ्जरं मत्तमेक एव चरन्वने ।अनङ्कुशं समारुह्य नगरं गन्तुमिच्छसि ॥१३॥
transliteration: atha vā kuñjaraṁ mattameka eva caranvane ,anaṅkuśaṁ samāruhya nagaraṁ gantumicchasi.
english: Or else, wandering alone in the forest, you desire to ride an un-goaded mad elephant into the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 13. Or else, wandering alone in the forest, you desire to ride an un-goaded mad elephant into the city.

sanskrit: समिद्धं पावकं वापि घृतमेदोवसाहुतम् ।घृताक्तश्चीरवासास्त्वं मध्येनोत्तर्तुमिच्छसि ॥१४॥
transliteration: samiddhaṁ pāvakaṁ vāpi ghṛtamedovasāhutam ,ghṛtāktaścīravāsāstvaṁ madhyenottartumicchasi.
english: Or else, you, smeared with ghee and dressed in bark-garments, wish to cross through the middle of a blazing fire, which has been offered ghee, fat, and marrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 14. Or else, you, smeared with ghee and dressed in bark-garments, wish to cross through the middle of a blazing fire, which has been offered ghee, fat, and marrow.

sanskrit: आत्मानं यः समुद्बध्य कण्ठे बद्ध्वा महाशिलाम् ।समुद्रं प्रतरेद्दोर्भ्यां तत्र किं नाम पौरुषम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ātmānaṁ yaḥ samudbadhya kaṇṭhe baddhvā mahāśilām ,samudraṁ pratareddorbhyāṁ tatra kiṁ nāma pauruṣam.
english: What kind of heroism (pauruṣam) is it for someone who, having bound their own self (ātman) by the neck with a great stone, then attempts to cross the ocean using only their arms?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 15. What kind of heroism (pauruṣam) is it for someone who, having bound their own self (ātman) by the neck with a great stone, then attempts to cross the ocean using only their arms?

sanskrit: अकृतास्त्रः कृतास्त्रं वै बलवन्तं सुदुर्बलः ।तादृशं कर्ण यः पार्थं योद्धुमिच्छेत्स दुर्मतिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: akṛtāstraḥ kṛtāstraṁ vai balavantaṁ sudurbalaḥ ,tādṛśaṁ karṇa yaḥ pārthaṁ yoddhumicchetsa durmatiḥ.
english: O Karṇa, a person who is unaccomplished in weaponry and very weak, yet desires to fight such a powerful and skilled Arjuna (Pārtha), is indeed foolish.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 16. O Karṇa, a person who is unaccomplished in weaponry and very weak, yet desires to fight such a powerful and skilled Arjuna (Pārtha), is indeed foolish.

sanskrit: अस्माभिरेष निकृतो वर्षाणीह त्रयोदश ।सिंहः पाशविनिर्मुक्तो न नः शेषं करिष्यति ॥१७॥
transliteration: asmābhireṣa nikṛto varṣāṇīha trayodaśa ,siṁhaḥ pāśavinirmukto na naḥ śeṣaṁ kariṣyati.
english: This one (Arjuna) has been tormented by us for thirteen years. Now, like a lion freed from a noose, he will not leave any of us remaining.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 17. This one (Arjuna) has been tormented by us for thirteen years. Now, like a lion freed from a noose, he will not leave any of us remaining.

sanskrit: एकान्ते पार्थमासीनं कूपेऽग्निमिव संवृतम् ।अज्ञानादभ्यवस्कन्द्य प्राप्ताः स्मो भयमुत्तमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ekānte pārthamāsīnaṁ kūpe'gnimiva saṁvṛtam ,ajñānādabhyavaskandya prāptāḥ smo bhayamuttamam.
english: Because of our ignorance, we assailed Arjuna (Pārtha) while he was seated in solitude, like a fire concealed in a well. As a result, we have now incurred great fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 18. Because of our ignorance, we assailed Arjuna (Pārtha) while he was seated in solitude, like a fire concealed in a well. As a result, we have now incurred great fear.

sanskrit: सह युध्यामहे पार्थमागतं युद्धदुर्मदम् ।सैन्यास्तिष्ठन्तु संनद्धा व्यूढानीकाः प्रहारिणः ॥१९॥
transliteration: saha yudhyāmahe pārthamāgataṁ yuddhadurmadam ,sainyāstiṣṭhantu saṁnaddhā vyūḍhānīkāḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: We shall fight alongside Arjuna (Pārtha), who has arrived, intoxicated with the fervor of battle. Let the armies stand ready, with their battle formations arrayed, prepared to strike.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 19. We shall fight alongside Arjuna (Pārtha), who has arrived, intoxicated with the fervor of battle. Let the armies stand ready, with their battle formations arrayed, prepared to strike.

sanskrit: द्रोणो दुर्योधनो भीष्मो भवान्द्रौणिस्तथा वयम् ।सर्वे युध्यामहे पार्थं कर्ण मा साहसं कृथाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: droṇo duryodhano bhīṣmo bhavāndrauṇistathā vayam ,sarve yudhyāmahe pārthaṁ karṇa mā sāhasaṁ kṛthāḥ.
english: Droṇa, Duryodhana, Bhīṣma, you, Drauṇi, and all of us are fighting Pārtha. Karṇa, do not act so rashly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 20. Droṇa, Duryodhana, Bhīṣma, you, Drauṇi, and all of us are fighting Pārtha. Karṇa, do not act so rashly.

sanskrit: वयं व्यवसितं पार्थं वज्रपाणिमिवोद्यतम् ।षड्रथाः प्रतियुध्येम तिष्ठेम यदि संहताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: vayaṁ vyavasitaṁ pārthaṁ vajrapāṇimivodyatam ,ṣaḍrathāḥ pratiyudhyema tiṣṭhema yadi saṁhatāḥ.
english: We, the six charioteers, could fight against Pārtha, who is resolute and ready like Indra with his uplifted thunderbolt, if we remain united.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 21. We, the six charioteers, could fight against Pārtha, who is resolute and ready like Indra with his uplifted thunderbolt, if we remain united.

sanskrit: व्यूढानीकानि सैन्यानि यत्ताः परमधन्विनः ।युध्यामहेऽर्जुनं संख्ये दानवा वासवं यथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: vyūḍhānīkāni sainyāni yattāḥ paramadhanvinaḥ ,yudhyāmahe'rjunaṁ saṁkhye dānavā vāsavaṁ yathā.
english: Our armies are arrayed in battle formations, and the supreme archers are ready. We will fight Arjuna in battle, just as the Dānavas (demons) fight Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 44 Verse 22. Our armies are arrayed in battle formations, and the supreme archers are ready. We will fight Arjuna in battle, just as the Dānavas (demons) fight Indra.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामोवाच ।न च तावज्जिता गावो न च सीमान्तरं गताः ।न हास्तिनपुरं प्राप्तास्त्वं च कर्ण विकत्थसे ॥१॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmovāca ,na ca tāvajjitā gāvo na ca sīmāntaraṁ gatāḥ ,na hāstinapuraṁ prāptāstvaṁ ca karṇa vikatthase.
english: Aśvatthāmā said: 'The cows have not yet been won, nor have the borders been crossed. Hastināpura has not been reached, and yet, Karṇa, you are boasting!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 1. Aśvatthāmā said: 'The cows have not yet been won, nor have the borders been crossed. Hastināpura has not been reached, and yet, Karṇa, you are boasting!'

sanskrit: संग्रामान्सुबहूञ्जित्वा लब्ध्वा च विपुलं धनम् ।विजित्य च परां भूमिं नाहुः किंचन पौरुषम् ॥२॥
transliteration: saṁgrāmānsubahūñjitvā labdhvā ca vipulaṁ dhanam ,vijitya ca parāṁ bhūmiṁ nāhuḥ kiṁcana pauruṣam.
english: Having conquered many battles, acquired vast wealth, and overcome great lands, they (the wise) do not declare any of this to be true valor (pauruṣa) whatsoever.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 2. Having conquered many battles, acquired vast wealth, and overcome great lands, they (the wise) do not declare any of this to be true valor (pauruṣa) whatsoever.

sanskrit: पचत्यग्निरवाक्यस्तु तूष्णीं भाति दिवाकरः ।तूष्णीं धारयते लोकान्वसुधा सचराचरान् ॥३॥
transliteration: pacatyagniravākyastu tūṣṇīṁ bhāti divākaraḥ ,tūṣṇīṁ dhārayate lokānvasudhā sacarācarān.
english: Fire cooks silently, and the sun shines quietly. The earth (vasudhā) silently sustains all beings, both moving and non-moving.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 3. Fire cooks silently, and the sun shines quietly. The earth (vasudhā) silently sustains all beings, both moving and non-moving.

sanskrit: चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य कर्माणि विहितानि मनीषिभिः ।धनं यैरधिगन्तव्यं यच्च कुर्वन्न दुष्यति ॥४॥
transliteration: cāturvarṇyasya karmāṇi vihitāni manīṣibhiḥ ,dhanaṁ yairadhigantavyaṁ yacca kurvanna duṣyati.
english: The duties (karma) of the four social classes (cāturvarṇya) were prescribed by the wise, and these are the duties by which wealth is to be acquired, and performing which one does not become tainted.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 4. The duties (karma) of the four social classes (cāturvarṇya) were prescribed by the wise, and these are the duties by which wealth is to be acquired, and performing which one does not become tainted.

sanskrit: अधीत्य ब्राह्मणो वेदान्याजयेत यजेत च ।क्षत्रियो धनुराश्रित्य यजेतैव न याजयेत् ।वैश्योऽधिगम्य द्रव्याणि ब्रह्मकर्माणि कारयेत् ॥५॥
transliteration: adhītya brāhmaṇo vedānyājayeta yajeta ca ,kṣatriyo dhanurāśritya yajetaiva na yājayet ,vaiśyo'dhigamya dravyāṇi brahmakarmāṇi kārayet.
english: Having studied the Vedas, a Brahmin should perform sacrifices and also officiate them. A Kshatriya, relying on his bow (i.e., ruling/protecting), should perform sacrifices himself, but should certainly not officiate them for others. A Vaishya, having acquired wealth, should cause priestly duties to be performed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 5. Having studied the Vedas, a Brahmin should perform sacrifices and also officiate them. A Kshatriya, relying on his bow (i.e., ruling/protecting), should perform sacrifices himself, but should certainly not officiate them for others. A Vaishya, having acquired wealth, should cause priestly duties to be performed.

sanskrit: वर्तमाना यथाशास्त्रं प्राप्य चापि महीमिमाम् ।सत्कुर्वन्ति महाभागा गुरून्सुविगुणानपि ॥६॥
transliteration: vartamānā yathāśāstraṁ prāpya cāpi mahīmimām ,satkurvanti mahābhāgā gurūnsuviguṇānapi.
english: Even after obtaining this earth, noble persons who act according to the scriptures honor highly virtuous teachers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 6. Even after obtaining this earth, noble persons who act according to the scriptures honor highly virtuous teachers.

sanskrit: प्राप्य द्यूतेन को राज्यं क्षत्रियस्तोष्टुमर्हति ।तथा नृशंसरूपेण यथान्यः प्राकृतो जनः ॥७॥
transliteration: prāpya dyūtena ko rājyaṁ kṣatriyastoṣṭumarhati ,tathā nṛśaṁsarūpeṇa yathānyaḥ prākṛto janaḥ.
english: What kṣatriya (warrior) would be pleased to gain a kingdom through gambling, especially when obtained by cruel means, just like any common person would?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 7. What kṣatriya (warrior) would be pleased to gain a kingdom through gambling, especially when obtained by cruel means, just like any common person would?

sanskrit: तथावाप्तेषु वित्तेषु को विकत्थेद्विचक्षणः ।निकृत्या वञ्चनायोगैश्चरन्वैतंसिको यथा ॥८॥
transliteration: tathāvāpteṣu vitteṣu ko vikatthedvicakṣaṇaḥ ,nikṛtyā vañcanāyogaiścaranvaitaṁsiko yathā.
english: Which discerning person would boast about wealth obtained through fraud and deceptive means, just like a fowler (hunter) who acts deceitfully?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 8. Which discerning person would boast about wealth obtained through fraud and deceptive means, just like a fowler (hunter) who acts deceitfully?

sanskrit: कतमद्द्वैरथं युद्धं यत्राजैषीर्धनंजयम् ।नकुलं सहदेवं च धनं येषां त्वया हृतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: katamaddvairathaṁ yuddhaṁ yatrājaiṣīrdhanaṁjayam ,nakulaṁ sahadevaṁ ca dhanaṁ yeṣāṁ tvayā hṛtam.
english: Which single combat was it, which battle, where you defeated Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva, whose wealth you have seized?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 9. Which single combat was it, which battle, where you defeated Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva, whose wealth you have seized?
