sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरो जितः कस्मिन्भीमश्च बलिनां वरः ।इन्द्रप्रस्थं त्वया कस्मिन्संग्रामे निर्जितं पुरा ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiro jitaḥ kasminbhīmaśca balināṁ varaḥ ,indraprasthaṁ tvayā kasminsaṁgrāme nirjitaṁ purā.
english: In which battle was Yudhiṣṭhira conquered, and Bhīma, the best of the strong? And in which battle was Indraprastha conquered by you previously?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 10. In which battle was Yudhiṣṭhira conquered, and Bhīma, the best of the strong? And in which battle was Indraprastha conquered by you previously?

sanskrit: तथैव कतमं युद्धं यस्मिन्कृष्णा जिता त्वया ।एकवस्त्रा सभां नीता दुष्टकर्मन्रजस्वला ॥११॥
transliteration: tathaiva katamaṁ yuddhaṁ yasminkṛṣṇā jitā tvayā ,ekavastrā sabhāṁ nītā duṣṭakarmanrajasvalā.
english: Likewise, in which battle was Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) conquered by you? O evil-doer, she was brought to the assembly wearing only one garment, while she was menstruating.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 11. Likewise, in which battle was Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) conquered by you? O evil-doer, she was brought to the assembly wearing only one garment, while she was menstruating.

sanskrit: मूलमेषां महत्कृत्तं सारार्थी चन्दनं यथा ।कर्म कारयिथाः शूर तत्र किं विदुरोऽब्रवीत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: mūlameṣāṁ mahatkṛttaṁ sārārthī candanaṁ yathā ,karma kārayithāḥ śūra tatra kiṁ viduro'bravīt.
english: Their great root was severed, just as sandalwood is cut for its essence. O hero, you caused these deeds (karma) to be done. What did Vidura say there?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 12. Their great root was severed, just as sandalwood is cut for its essence. O hero, you caused these deeds (karma) to be done. What did Vidura say there?

sanskrit: यथाशक्ति मनुष्याणां शममालक्षयामहे ।अन्येषां चैव सत्त्वानामपि कीटपिपीलिके ॥१३॥
transliteration: yathāśakti manuṣyāṇāṁ śamamālakṣayāmahe ,anyeṣāṁ caiva sattvānāmapi kīṭapipīlike.
english: We observe tranquility (śama) in humans according to their capacity, and indeed, also in other living beings (sattva), even in insects and ants.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 13. We observe tranquility (śama) in humans according to their capacity, and indeed, also in other living beings (sattva), even in insects and ants.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्यास्तं परिक्लेशं न क्षन्तुं पाण्डवोऽर्हति ।दुःखाय धार्तराष्ट्राणां प्रादुर्भूतो धनंजयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: draupadyāstaṁ parikleśaṁ na kṣantuṁ pāṇḍavo'rhati ,duḥkhāya dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ prādurbhūto dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) should not forgive that great suffering and humiliation of Draupadī. Dhananjaya (Arjuna) has manifested to bring sorrow to the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 14. The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) should not forgive that great suffering and humiliation of Draupadī. Dhananjaya (Arjuna) has manifested to bring sorrow to the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas).

sanskrit: त्वं पुनः पण्डितो भूत्वा वाचं वक्तुमिहेच्छसि ।वैरान्तकरणो जिष्णुर्न नः शेषं करिष्यति ॥१५॥
transliteration: tvaṁ punaḥ paṇḍito bhūtvā vācaṁ vaktumihecchasi ,vairāntakaraṇo jiṣṇurna naḥ śeṣaṁ kariṣyati.
english: But you, pretending to be a wise man, wish to speak here. Jiṣṇu (Arjuna), the one who puts an end to hostility, will not leave any of our enemies remaining.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 15. But you, pretending to be a wise man, wish to speak here. Jiṣṇu (Arjuna), the one who puts an end to hostility, will not leave any of our enemies remaining.

sanskrit: नैष देवान्न गन्धर्वान्नासुरान्न च राक्षसान् ।भयादिह न युध्येत कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ॥१६॥
transliteration: naiṣa devānna gandharvānnāsurānna ca rākṣasān ,bhayādiha na yudhyeta kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: This Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the son of Kuntī, would not, out of fear, refrain from fighting here even against the gods, Gandharvas, Asuras, or Rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 16. This Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the son of Kuntī, would not, out of fear, refrain from fighting here even against the gods, Gandharvas, Asuras, or Rākṣasas.

sanskrit: यं यमेषोऽभिसंक्रुद्धः संग्रामेऽभिपतिष्यति ।वृक्षं गरुडवेगेन विनिहत्य तमेष्यति ॥१७॥
transliteration: yaṁ yameṣo'bhisaṁkruddhaḥ saṁgrāme'bhipatiṣyati ,vṛkṣaṁ garuḍavegena vinihatya tameṣyati.
english: Whomsoever this enraged (Arjuna) attacks in battle, he will certainly strike him down with the speed of Garuḍa, just as Garuḍa fells a tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 17. Whomsoever this enraged (Arjuna) attacks in battle, he will certainly strike him down with the speed of Garuḍa, just as Garuḍa fells a tree.

sanskrit: त्वत्तो विशिष्टं वीर्येण धनुष्यमरराट्समम् ।वासुदेवसमं युद्धे तं पार्थं को न पूजयेत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tvatto viśiṣṭaṁ vīryeṇa dhanuṣyamararāṭsamam ,vāsudevasamaṁ yuddhe taṁ pārthaṁ ko na pūjayet.
english: Who would not honor that Arjuna (Pārtha), who is superior to you in valor, equal to Indra (amararāṭ) in archery, and equal to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 18. Who would not honor that Arjuna (Pārtha), who is superior to you in valor, equal to Indra (amararāṭ) in archery, and equal to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) in battle?

sanskrit: दैवं दैवेन युध्येत मानुषेण च मानुषम् ।अस्त्रेणास्त्रं समाहन्यात्कोऽर्जुनेन समः पुमान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: daivaṁ daivena yudhyeta mānuṣeṇa ca mānuṣam ,astreṇāstraṁ samāhanyātko'rjunena samaḥ pumān.
english: A divine power should be fought with a divine power, and a human power with a human power. A weapon (astram) should be counteracted with a weapon. What man is equal to Arjuna?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 19. A divine power should be fought with a divine power, and a human power with a human power. A weapon (astram) should be counteracted with a weapon. What man is equal to Arjuna?

sanskrit: पुत्रादनन्तरः शिष्य इति धर्मविदो विदुः ।एतेनापि निमित्तेन प्रियो द्रोणस्य पाण्डवः ॥२०॥
transliteration: putrādanantaraḥ śiṣya iti dharmavido viduḥ ,etenāpi nimittena priyo droṇasya pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Experts in natural law (dharma) know that a disciple is considered to be next only to a son. For this very reason, Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, is dear to Droṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 20. Experts in natural law (dharma) know that a disciple is considered to be next only to a son. For this very reason, Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, is dear to Droṇa.

sanskrit: यथा त्वमकरोर्द्यूतमिन्द्रप्रस्थं यथाहरः ।यथानैषीः सभां कृष्णां तथा युध्यस्व पाण्डवम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: yathā tvamakarordyūtamindraprasthaṁ yathāharaḥ ,yathānaiṣīḥ sabhāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ tathā yudhyasva pāṇḍavam.
english: Just as you arranged the gambling game, just as you seized Indrapraṣṭha, and just as you led Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) to the assembly, so now you should fight the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 21. Just as you arranged the gambling game, just as you seized Indrapraṣṭha, and just as you led Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) to the assembly, so now you should fight the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अयं ते मातुलः प्राज्ञः क्षत्रधर्मस्य कोविदः ।दुर्द्यूतदेवी गान्धारः शकुनिर्युध्यतामिह ॥२२॥
transliteration: ayaṁ te mātulaḥ prājñaḥ kṣatradharmasya kovidaḥ ,durdyūtadevī gāndhāraḥ śakuniryudhyatāmiha.
english: This is your wise maternal uncle, an expert in the natural law (dharma) of a warrior. Let Śakuni of Gāndhāra, who delights in evil gambling, fight here.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 22. This is your wise maternal uncle, an expert in the natural law (dharma) of a warrior. Let Śakuni of Gāndhāra, who delights in evil gambling, fight here.

sanskrit: नाक्षान्क्षिपति गाण्डीवं न कृतं द्वापरं न च ।ज्वलतो निशितान्बाणांस्तीक्ष्णान्क्षिपति गाण्डिवम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: nākṣānkṣipati gāṇḍīvaṁ na kṛtaṁ dvāparaṁ na ca ,jvalato niśitānbāṇāṁstīkṣṇānkṣipati gāṇḍivam.
english: Gāṇḍīva does not throw dice, nor the 'kṛta' (four-point throw in dice), nor the 'dvāpara' (two-point throw in dice). Instead, Gāṇḍīva throws blazing, sharpened, and piercing arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 23. Gāṇḍīva does not throw dice, nor the 'kṛta' (four-point throw in dice), nor the 'dvāpara' (two-point throw in dice). Instead, Gāṇḍīva throws blazing, sharpened, and piercing arrows.

sanskrit: न हि गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्ता गार्ध्रपत्राः सुतेजनाः ।अन्तरेष्ववतिष्ठन्ति गिरीणामपि दारणाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: na hi gāṇḍīvanirmuktā gārdhrapatrāḥ sutejanāḥ ,antareṣvavatiṣṭhanti girīṇāmapi dāraṇāḥ.
english: Indeed, arrows released from Gāṇḍīva, fletched with vulture feathers and extremely keen, do not stop even within mountains, but rather shatter them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 24. Indeed, arrows released from Gāṇḍīva, fletched with vulture feathers and extremely keen, do not stop even within mountains, but rather shatter them.

sanskrit: अन्तकः शमनो मृत्युस्तथाग्निर्वडवामुखः ।कुर्युरेते क्वचिच्छेषं न तु क्रुद्धो धनंजयः ॥२५॥
transliteration: antakaḥ śamano mṛtyustathāgnirvaḍavāmukhaḥ ,kuryurete kvaciccheṣaṁ na tu kruddho dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Antaka (Yama), Śamana (Yama), Mṛtyu (Death), and even Agni (fire) with Vaḍavāmukha (the submarine fire), might sometimes leave something remaining. But an enraged Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) will certainly not.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 25. Antaka (Yama), Śamana (Yama), Mṛtyu (Death), and even Agni (fire) with Vaḍavāmukha (the submarine fire), might sometimes leave something remaining. But an enraged Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) will certainly not.

sanskrit: युध्यतां काममाचार्यो नाहं योत्स्ये धनंजयम् ।मत्स्यो ह्यस्माभिरायोध्यो यद्यागच्छेद्गवां पदम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: yudhyatāṁ kāmamācāryo nāhaṁ yotsye dhanaṁjayam ,matsyo hyasmābhirāyodhyo yadyāgacchedgavāṁ padam.
english: Let the preceptor fight as he wishes; I will not fight Dhanañjaya. Indeed, we must fight Matsya if he enters the cattle's grazing grounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 45 Verse 26. Let the preceptor fight as he wishes; I will not fight Dhanañjaya. Indeed, we must fight Matsya if he enters the cattle's grazing grounds.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।साधु पश्यति वै द्रोणः कृपः साध्वनुपश्यति ।कर्णस्तु क्षत्रधर्मेण यथावद्योद्धुमिच्छति ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,sādhu paśyati vai droṇaḥ kṛpaḥ sādhvanupaśyati ,karṇastu kṣatradharmeṇa yathāvadyoddhumicchati.
english: Bhishma said: Drona indeed perceives rightly, and Kripa also perceives rightly. But Karna wishes to fight appropriately, in accordance with the warrior's natural law (kṣatradharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 1. Bhishma said: Drona indeed perceives rightly, and Kripa also perceives rightly. But Karna wishes to fight appropriately, in accordance with the warrior's natural law (kṣatradharma).

sanskrit: आचार्यो नाभिषक्तव्यः पुरुषेण विजानता ।देशकालौ तु संप्रेक्ष्य योद्धव्यमिति मे मतिः ॥२॥
transliteration: ācāryo nābhiṣaktavyaḥ puruṣeṇa vijānatā ,deśakālau tu saṁprekṣya yoddhavyamiti me matiḥ.
english: A discerning person should not attack a preceptor. However, having properly assessed the place and time, one should fight; this is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 2. A discerning person should not attack a preceptor. However, having properly assessed the place and time, one should fight; this is my opinion.

sanskrit: यस्य सूर्यसमाः पञ्च सपत्नाः स्युः प्रहारिणः ।कथमभ्युदये तेषां न प्रमुह्येत पण्डितः ॥३॥
transliteration: yasya sūryasamāḥ pañca sapatnāḥ syuḥ prahāriṇaḥ ,kathamabhyudaye teṣāṁ na pramuhyeta paṇḍitaḥ.
english: For whom there are five formidable enemies, radiant like the sun and capable of striking, how could a wise man (paṇḍita) not be bewildered by their prosperity?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 3. For whom there are five formidable enemies, radiant like the sun and capable of striking, how could a wise man (paṇḍita) not be bewildered by their prosperity?

sanskrit: स्वार्थे सर्वे विमुह्यन्ति येऽपि धर्मविदो जनाः ।तस्माद्राजन्ब्रवीम्येष वाक्यं ते यदि रोचते ॥४॥
transliteration: svārthe sarve vimuhyanti ye'pi dharmavido janāḥ ,tasmādrājanbravīmyeṣa vākyaṁ te yadi rocate.
english: Everyone becomes deluded regarding their own self-interest, even those people who understand natural law (dharma). Therefore, O King, I speak this statement to you, if it pleases you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 4. Everyone becomes deluded regarding their own self-interest, even those people who understand natural law (dharma). Therefore, O King, I speak this statement to you, if it pleases you.

sanskrit: कर्णो यदभ्यवोचन्नस्तेजःसंजननाय तत् ।आचार्यपुत्रः क्षमतां महत्कार्यमुपस्थितम् ॥५॥
transliteration: karṇo yadabhyavocannastejaḥsaṁjananāya tat ,ācāryaputraḥ kṣamatāṁ mahatkāryamupasthitam.
english: Whatever Karṇa spoke to us for the generation of our valor, may the preceptor's (guru) son forgive it, for a great task has presented itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 5. Whatever Karṇa spoke to us for the generation of our valor, may the preceptor's (guru) son forgive it, for a great task has presented itself.

sanskrit: नायं कालो विरोधस्य कौन्तेये समुपस्थिते ।क्षन्तव्यं भवता सर्वमाचार्येण कृपेण च ॥६॥
transliteration: nāyaṁ kālo virodhasya kaunteye samupasthite ,kṣantavyaṁ bhavatā sarvamācāryeṇa kṛpeṇa ca.
english: This is not the time for conflict, now that the son of Kuntī (Arjuna) has arrived. Everything must be forgiven by you, and by the preceptor (ācārya) and Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 6. This is not the time for conflict, now that the son of Kuntī (Arjuna) has arrived. Everything must be forgiven by you, and by the preceptor (ācārya) and Kṛpa.

sanskrit: भवतां हि कृतास्त्रत्वं यथादित्ये प्रभा तथा ।यथा चन्द्रमसो लक्ष्म सर्वथा नापकृष्यते ।एवं भवत्सु ब्राह्मण्यं ब्रह्मास्त्रं च प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: bhavatāṁ hi kṛtāstratvaṁ yathāditye prabhā tathā ,yathā candramaso lakṣma sarvathā nāpakṛṣyate ,evaṁ bhavatsu brāhmaṇyaṁ brahmāstraṁ ca pratiṣṭhitam.
english: Indeed, your mastery of divine weapons is like the radiance inherent in the sun. Just as the mark on the moon is never diminished in any way, similarly, among you, priestly status (brāhmaṇya) and the divine weapon (brahmāstra) are firmly established.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 7. Indeed, your mastery of divine weapons is like the radiance inherent in the sun. Just as the mark on the moon is never diminished in any way, similarly, among you, priestly status (brāhmaṇya) and the divine weapon (brahmāstra) are firmly established.

sanskrit: चत्वार एकतो वेदाः क्षात्रमेकत्र दृश्यते ।नैतत्समस्तमुभयं कस्मिंश्चिदनुशुश्रुमः ॥८॥
transliteration: catvāra ekato vedāḥ kṣātramekatra dṛśyate ,naitatsamastamubhayaṁ kasmiṁścidanuśuśrumaḥ.
english: The four Vedas are on one side, and the martial spirit (kṣātram) is seen on the other. We have never heard of these two qualities existing completely together in anyone.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 8. The four Vedas are on one side, and the martial spirit (kṣātram) is seen on the other. We have never heard of these two qualities existing completely together in anyone.

sanskrit: अन्यत्र भारताचार्यात्सपुत्रादिति मे मतिः ।ब्रह्मास्त्रं चैव वेदाश्च नैतदन्यत्र दृश्यते ॥९॥
transliteration: anyatra bhāratācāryātsaputrāditi me matiḥ ,brahmāstraṁ caiva vedāśca naitadanyatra dṛśyate.
english: My opinion is that this (combination) is not found anywhere else except in the preceptor (guru) of the Bhāratas along with his son. The Brahmāstra weapon and also the Vedas - this is not seen elsewhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 9. My opinion is that this (combination) is not found anywhere else except in the preceptor (guru) of the Bhāratas along with his son. The Brahmāstra weapon and also the Vedas - this is not seen elsewhere.

sanskrit: आचार्यपुत्रः क्षमतां नायं कालः स्वभेदने ।सर्वे संहत्य युध्यामः पाकशासनिमागतम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: ācāryaputraḥ kṣamatāṁ nāyaṁ kālaḥ svabhedane ,sarve saṁhatya yudhyāmaḥ pākaśāsanimāgatam.
english: O son of the preceptor, please forgive! This is not the time for our own internal division. Let us all unite and fight against the son of Pākaśāsana (Arjuna), who has arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 10. O son of the preceptor, please forgive! This is not the time for our own internal division. Let us all unite and fight against the son of Pākaśāsana (Arjuna), who has arrived.

sanskrit: बलस्य व्यसनानीह यान्युक्तानि मनीषिभिः ।मुख्यो भेदो हि तेषां वै पापिष्ठो विदुषां मतः ॥११॥
transliteration: balasya vyasanānīha yānyuktāni manīṣibhiḥ ,mukhyo bhedo hi teṣāṁ vai pāpiṣṭho viduṣāṁ mataḥ.
english: Among the calamities (vyasanāni) of an army that have been enumerated by the wise, internal division (bheda) is indeed the chief, considered the most sinful (pāpiṣṭha) by scholars.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 11. Among the calamities (vyasanāni) of an army that have been enumerated by the wise, internal division (bheda) is indeed the chief, considered the most sinful (pāpiṣṭha) by scholars.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामोवाच ।आचार्य एव क्षमतां शान्तिरत्र विधीयताम् ।अभिषज्यमाने हि गुरौ तद्वृत्तं रोषकारितम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmovāca ,ācārya eva kṣamatāṁ śāntiratra vidhīyatām ,abhiṣajyamāne hi gurau tadvṛttaṁ roṣakāritam.
english: Aśvatthāmā said: The preceptor (ācārya) himself should forgive, and peace should be established here. Indeed, when a teacher (guru) is being attacked, such behavior is caused by anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 12. Aśvatthāmā said: The preceptor (ācārya) himself should forgive, and peace should be established here. Indeed, when a teacher (guru) is being attacked, such behavior is caused by anger.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो दुर्योधनो द्रोणं क्षमयामास भारत ।सह कर्णेन भीष्मेण कृपेण च महात्मना ॥१३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato duryodhano droṇaṁ kṣamayāmāsa bhārata ,saha karṇena bhīṣmeṇa kṛpeṇa ca mahātmanā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), Duryodhana appeased Droṇa, accompanied by Karṇa, Bhīṣma, and the great-souled (mahātman) Kṛpa.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 13. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), Duryodhana appeased Droṇa, accompanied by Karṇa, Bhīṣma, and the great-souled (mahātman) Kṛpa.

sanskrit: द्रोण उवाच ।यदेव प्रथमं वाक्यं भीष्मः शांतनवोऽब्रवीत् ।तेनैवाहं प्रसन्नो वै परमत्र विधीयताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: droṇa uvāca ,yadeva prathamaṁ vākyaṁ bhīṣmaḥ śāṁtanavo'bravīt ,tenaivāhaṁ prasanno vai paramatra vidhīyatām.
english: Droṇa said: Indeed, I am pleased by that very first statement which Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu (śāntanava), uttered. Now, let the highest (parama) be done in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 14. Droṇa said: Indeed, I am pleased by that very first statement which Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu (śāntanava), uttered. Now, let the highest (parama) be done in this matter.

sanskrit: यथा दुर्योधनेऽयत्ते नागः स्पृशति सैनिकान् ।साहसाद्यदि वा मोहात्तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yathā duryodhane'yatte nāgaḥ spṛśati sainikān ,sāhasādyadi vā mohāttathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: Just as a rogue elephant (nāga) might attack soldiers when Duryodhana is unwary, so too should a strategy (nīti) be enacted, whether due to impulsiveness or delusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 15. Just as a rogue elephant (nāga) might attack soldiers when Duryodhana is unwary, so too should a strategy (nīti) be enacted, whether due to impulsiveness or delusion.

sanskrit: वनवासे ह्यनिर्वृत्ते दर्शयेन्न धनंजयः ।धनं वालभमानोऽत्र नाद्य नः क्षन्तुमर्हति ॥१६॥
transliteration: vanavāse hyanirvṛtte darśayenna dhanaṁjayaḥ ,dhanaṁ vālabhamāno'tra nādya naḥ kṣantumarhati.
english: Certainly, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) must not reveal himself while his period of forest exile is still unfulfilled. If he were to obtain his wealth (or claim to it) here, he would not tolerate us today.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 16. Certainly, Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) must not reveal himself while his period of forest exile is still unfulfilled. If he were to obtain his wealth (or claim to it) here, he would not tolerate us today.

sanskrit: यथा नायं समायुज्याद्धार्तराष्ट्रान्कथंचन ।यथा च न पराजय्यात्तथा नीतिर्विधीयताम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yathā nāyaṁ samāyujyāddhārtarāṣṭrānkathaṁcana ,yathā ca na parājayyāttathā nītirvidhīyatām.
english: A policy (nīti) should be devised in such a way that he (Arjuna) does not, by any means, join the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas), and also that he is not defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 17. A policy (nīti) should be devised in such a way that he (Arjuna) does not, by any means, join the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas), and also that he is not defeated.

sanskrit: उक्तं दुर्योधनेनापि पुरस्ताद्वाक्यमीदृशम् ।तदनुस्मृत्य गाङ्गेय यथावद्वक्तुमर्हसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: uktaṁ duryodhanenāpi purastādvākyamīdṛśam ,tadanusmṛtya gāṅgeya yathāvadvaktumarhasi.
english: Such a statement was also made by Duryodhana previously. Therefore, O son of Gaṅgā (Bhīṣma), remembering that, you should speak appropriately.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 46 Verse 18. Such a statement was also made by Duryodhana previously. Therefore, O son of Gaṅgā (Bhīṣma), remembering that, you should speak appropriately.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।कलांशास्तात युज्यन्ते मुहूर्ताश्च दिनानि च ।अर्धमासाश्च मासाश्च नक्षत्राणि ग्रहास्तथा ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,kalāṁśāstāta yujyante muhūrtāśca dināni ca ,ardhamāsāśca māsāśca nakṣatrāṇi grahāstathā.
english: Bhīṣma said: 'My dear one, kalā-portions (fractions of time), muhūrtas (units of 48 minutes), days, half-months, months, constellations, and planets are all reckoned together.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 1. Bhīṣma said: 'My dear one, kalā-portions (fractions of time), muhūrtas (units of 48 minutes), days, half-months, months, constellations, and planets are all reckoned together.'

sanskrit: ऋतवश्चापि युज्यन्ते तथा संवत्सरा अपि ।एवं कालविभागेन कालचक्रं प्रवर्तते ॥२॥
transliteration: ṛtavaścāpi yujyante tathā saṁvatsarā api ,evaṁ kālavibhāgena kālacakraṁ pravartate.
english: The seasons are connected, and so too are the years. In this way, through the division of time, the wheel of time (kālacakra) revolves.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 2. The seasons are connected, and so too are the years. In this way, through the division of time, the wheel of time (kālacakra) revolves.

sanskrit: तेषां कालातिरेकेण ज्योतिषां च व्यतिक्रमात् ।पञ्चमे पञ्चमे वर्षे द्वौ मासावुपजायतः ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ kālātirekeṇa jyotiṣāṁ ca vyatikramāt ,pañcame pañcame varṣe dvau māsāvupajāyataḥ.
english: Due to the accumulation of time and the deviation of the luminaries (jyotiṣām) from their usual course, two months are produced in every fifth year.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 3. Due to the accumulation of time and the deviation of the luminaries (jyotiṣām) from their usual course, two months are produced in every fifth year.

sanskrit: तेषामभ्यधिका मासाः पञ्च द्वादश च क्षपाः ।त्रयोदशानां वर्षाणामिति मे वर्तते मतिः ॥४॥
transliteration: teṣāmabhyadhikā māsāḥ pañca dvādaśa ca kṣapāḥ ,trayodaśānāṁ varṣāṇāmiti me vartate matiḥ.
english: Regarding these [cycles], there are five months and twelve additional nights over a period of thirteen years. This is my understanding (mati).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 4. Regarding these [cycles], there are five months and twelve additional nights over a period of thirteen years. This is my understanding (mati).

sanskrit: सर्वं यथावच्चरितं यद्यदेभिः परिश्रुतम् ।एवमेतद्ध्रुवं ज्ञात्वा ततो बीभत्सुरागतः ॥५॥
transliteration: sarvaṁ yathāvaccaritaṁ yadyadebhiḥ pariśrutam ,evametaddhruvaṁ jñātvā tato bībhatsurāgataḥ.
english: Whatever was thoroughly understood by these means, all of it (the calendrical divisions) proceeded accurately. Thus, having known this to be certain, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) then arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 5. Whatever was thoroughly understood by these means, all of it (the calendrical divisions) proceeded accurately. Thus, having known this to be certain, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) then arrived.

sanskrit: सर्वे चैव महात्मानः सर्वे धर्मार्थकोविदाः ।येषां युधिष्ठिरो राजा कस्माद्धर्मेऽपराध्नुयुः ॥६॥
transliteration: sarve caiva mahātmānaḥ sarve dharmārthakovidāḥ ,yeṣāṁ yudhiṣṭhiro rājā kasmāddharme'parādhnuyuḥ.
english: All of them are indeed great-souled individuals, and all are experts in matters of natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha). With Yudhishthira as their king, why would they ever transgress the natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 6. All of them are indeed great-souled individuals, and all are experts in matters of natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha). With Yudhishthira as their king, why would they ever transgress the natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: अलुब्धाश्चैव कौन्तेयाः कृतवन्तश्च दुष्करम् ।न चापि केवलं राज्यमिच्छेयुस्तेऽनुपायतः ॥७॥
transliteration: alubdhāścaiva kaunteyāḥ kṛtavantaśca duṣkaram ,na cāpi kevalaṁ rājyamiccheyuste'nupāyataḥ.
english: The sons of Kunti (Kaunteyas) are certainly not greedy, and they have performed incredibly difficult deeds. Furthermore, they would not desire merely a kingdom obtained through improper means.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 7. The sons of Kunti (Kaunteyas) are certainly not greedy, and they have performed incredibly difficult deeds. Furthermore, they would not desire merely a kingdom obtained through improper means.

sanskrit: तदैव ते हि विक्रान्तुमीषुः कौरवनन्दनाः ।धर्मपाशनिबद्धास्तु न चेलुः क्षत्रियव्रतात् ॥८॥
transliteration: tadaiva te hi vikrāntumīṣuḥ kauravanandanāḥ ,dharmapāśanibaddhāstu na celuḥ kṣatriyavratāt.
english: At that very moment, the delight of the Kurus (Kauravanandanāḥ) indeed desired to display their prowess. Yet, bound by the fetters of natural law (dharma), they did not deviate from the warrior's (kṣatriya) vow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 8. At that very moment, the delight of the Kurus (Kauravanandanāḥ) indeed desired to display their prowess. Yet, bound by the fetters of natural law (dharma), they did not deviate from the warrior's (kṣatriya) vow.

sanskrit: यच्चानृत इति ख्यायेद्यच्च गच्छेत्पराभवम् ।वृणुयुर्मरणं पार्था नानृतत्वं कथंचन ॥९॥
transliteration: yaccānṛta iti khyāyedyacca gacchetparābhavam ,vṛṇuyurmaraṇaṁ pārthā nānṛtatvaṁ kathaṁcana.
english: And whatever would be known as untrue, or whatever would lead to disgrace, the sons of Pritha (Pārthas) would choose death, but never falsehood (anṛtatva) under any circumstances.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 9. And whatever would be known as untrue, or whatever would lead to disgrace, the sons of Pritha (Pārthas) would choose death, but never falsehood (anṛtatva) under any circumstances.

sanskrit: प्राप्ते तु काले प्राप्तव्यं नोत्सृजेयुर्नरर्षभाः ।अपि वज्रभृता गुप्तं तथावीर्या हि पाण्डवाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: prāpte tu kāle prāptavyaṁ notsṛjeyurnararṣabhāḥ ,api vajrabhṛtā guptaṁ tathāvīryā hi pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Indeed, the Pandavas, these foremost among men, possess such prowess that when the opportune moment arrives, they would not relinquish what is to be gained, even if it were protected by Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 10. Indeed, the Pandavas, these foremost among men, possess such prowess that when the opportune moment arrives, they would not relinquish what is to be gained, even if it were protected by Indra, the wielder of the thunderbolt.

sanskrit: प्रतियुध्याम समरे सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरम् ।तस्माद्यदत्र कल्याणं लोके सद्भिरनुष्ठितम् ।तत्संविधीयतां क्षिप्रं मा नो ह्यर्थोऽतिगात्परान् ॥११॥
transliteration: pratiyudhyāma samare sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varam ,tasmādyadatra kalyāṇaṁ loke sadbhiranuṣṭhitam ,tatsaṁvidhīyatāṁ kṣipraṁ mā no hyartho'tigātparān.
english: Let us engage in battle against the foremost among all weapon-wielders. Therefore, whatever course of action is auspicious here and is followed by the righteous in the world, let that be swiftly implemented. May our objective not pass into the hands of our enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 11. Let us engage in battle against the foremost among all weapon-wielders. Therefore, whatever course of action is auspicious here and is followed by the righteous in the world, let that be swiftly implemented. May our objective not pass into the hands of our enemies.

sanskrit: न हि पश्यामि संग्रामे कदाचिदपि कौरव ।एकान्तसिद्धिं राजेन्द्र संप्राप्तश्च धनंजयः ॥१२॥
transliteration: na hi paśyāmi saṁgrāme kadācidapi kaurava ,ekāntasiddhiṁ rājendra saṁprāptaśca dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Indeed, O scion of Kuru, O king of kings, I never perceive absolute success in battle, especially now that Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) has arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 12. Indeed, O scion of Kuru, O king of kings, I never perceive absolute success in battle, especially now that Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) has arrived.

sanskrit: संप्रवृत्ते तु संग्रामे भावाभावौ जयाजयौ ।अवश्यमेकं स्पृशतो दृष्टमेतदसंशयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: saṁpravṛtte tu saṁgrāme bhāvābhāvau jayājayau ,avaśyamekaṁ spṛśato dṛṣṭametadasaṁśayam.
english: Indeed, once a battle has fully commenced, the dualities of life and death, and victory and defeat, will inevitably pertain to one side. This is observed beyond a doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 13. Indeed, once a battle has fully commenced, the dualities of life and death, and victory and defeat, will inevitably pertain to one side. This is observed beyond a doubt.

sanskrit: तस्माद्युद्धावचरिकं कर्म वा धर्मसंहितम् ।क्रियतामाशु राजेन्द्र संप्राप्तो हि धनंजयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasmādyuddhāvacarikaṁ karma vā dharmasaṁhitam ,kriyatāmāśu rājendra saṁprāpto hi dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Therefore, O king of kings (rājendra), let an action related to battle, or one consistent with natural law (dharma), be undertaken quickly, for Arjuna has indeed arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 14. Therefore, O king of kings (rājendra), let an action related to battle, or one consistent with natural law (dharma), be undertaken quickly, for Arjuna has indeed arrived.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।नाहं राज्यं प्रदास्यामि पाण्डवानां पितामह ।युद्धावचारिकं यत्तु तच्छीघ्रं संविधीयताम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,nāhaṁ rājyaṁ pradāsyāmi pāṇḍavānāṁ pitāmaha ,yuddhāvacārikaṁ yattu tacchīghraṁ saṁvidhīyatām.
english: Duryodhana said: 'O grandfather, I will not give the kingdom to the Pāṇḍavas. As for what is related to battle, let that be quickly prepared.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 15. Duryodhana said: 'O grandfather, I will not give the kingdom to the Pāṇḍavas. As for what is related to battle, let that be quickly prepared.'

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।अत्र या मामकी बुद्धिः श्रूयतां यदि रोचते ।क्षिप्रं बलचतुर्भागं गृह्य गच्छ पुरं प्रति ।ततोऽपरश्चतुर्भागो गाः समादाय गच्छतु ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,atra yā māmakī buddhiḥ śrūyatāṁ yadi rocate ,kṣipraṁ balacaturbhāgaṁ gṛhya gaccha puraṁ prati ,tato'paraścaturbhāgo gāḥ samādāya gacchatu.
english: Bhīṣma said: 'Listen to my advice on this matter, if it is agreeable to you. Quickly take a quarter of the army and proceed towards the city. Then, let another quarter of the army depart, taking the cows.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 16. Bhīṣma said: 'Listen to my advice on this matter, if it is agreeable to you. Quickly take a quarter of the army and proceed towards the city. Then, let another quarter of the army depart, taking the cows.'

sanskrit: वयं त्वर्धेन सैन्येन प्रतियोत्स्याम पाण्डवम् ।मत्स्यं वा पुनरायातमथ वापि शतक्रतुम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: vayaṁ tvardhena sainyena pratiyotsyāma pāṇḍavam ,matsyaṁ vā punarāyātamatha vāpi śatakratum.
english: We, however, will fight against the Pandava with just half our army, whether it be Matsya (the king of Virata) returned, or even Indra himself (Śatakratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 17. We, however, will fight against the Pandava with just half our army, whether it be Matsya (the king of Virata) returned, or even Indra himself (Śatakratu).

sanskrit: आचार्यो मध्यतस्तिष्ठत्वश्वत्थामा तु सव्यतः ।कृपः शारद्वतो धीमान्पार्श्वं रक्षतु दक्षिणम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ācāryo madhyatastiṣṭhatvaśvatthāmā tu savyataḥ ,kṛpaḥ śāradvato dhīmānpārśvaṁ rakṣatu dakṣiṇam.
english: Let the preceptor (Ācārya) stand in the middle, and Ashvatthama on the left. Let the wise Kripa, son of Sharadvat, protect the right flank.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 18. Let the preceptor (Ācārya) stand in the middle, and Ashvatthama on the left. Let the wise Kripa, son of Sharadvat, protect the right flank.

sanskrit: अग्रतः सूतपुत्रस्तु कर्णस्तिष्ठतु दंशितः ।अहं सर्वस्य सैन्यस्य पश्चात्स्थास्यामि पालयन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: agrataḥ sūtaputrastu karṇastiṣṭhatu daṁśitaḥ ,ahaṁ sarvasya sainyasya paścātsthāsyāmi pālayan.
english: Let Karna, the son of Suta, stand fully armored in the front. I will stand behind the entire army, protecting it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 47 Verse 19. Let Karna, the son of Suta, stand fully armored in the front. I will stand behind the entire army, protecting it.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा व्यूढेष्वनीकेषु कौरवेयैर्महारथैः ।उपायादर्जुनस्तूर्णं रथघोषेण नादयन् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā vyūḍheṣvanīkeṣu kauraveyairmahārathaiḥ ,upāyādarjunastūrṇaṁ rathaghoṣeṇa nādayan.
english: Vaishampayana said: When the armies had thus been arrayed by the great charioteers of the Kauravas, Arjuna swiftly approached, making the ground reverberate with the roar of his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: When the armies had thus been arrayed by the great charioteers of the Kauravas, Arjuna swiftly approached, making the ground reverberate with the roar of his chariot.

sanskrit: ददृशुस्ते ध्वजाग्रं वै शुश्रुवुश्च रथस्वनम् ।दोधूयमानस्य भृशं गाण्डीवस्य च निस्वनम् ॥२॥
transliteration: dadṛśuste dhvajāgraṁ vai śuśruvuśca rathasvanam ,dodhūyamānasya bhṛśaṁ gāṇḍīvasya ca nisvanam.
english: They indeed saw his banner-top and heard the sound of his chariot, and the intense twang of his greatly vibrating Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 2. They indeed saw his banner-top and heard the sound of his chariot, and the intense twang of his greatly vibrating Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: ततस्तत्सर्वमालोक्य द्रोणो वचनमब्रवीत् ।महारथमनुप्राप्तं दृष्ट्वा गाण्डीवधन्विनम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tatastatsarvamālokya droṇo vacanamabravīt ,mahārathamanuprāptaṁ dṛṣṭvā gāṇḍīvadhanvinam.
english: Then, observing all that, and seeing the great chariot-warrior, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna), Drona spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 3. Then, observing all that, and seeing the great chariot-warrior, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna), Drona spoke.

sanskrit: एतद्ध्वजाग्रं पार्थस्य दूरतः संप्रकाशते ।एष घोषः सजलदो रोरवीति च वानरः ॥४॥
transliteration: etaddhvajāgraṁ pārthasya dūrataḥ saṁprakāśate ,eṣa ghoṣaḥ sajalado roravīti ca vānaraḥ.
english: From a distance, the banner-top of Partha (Arjuna) is clearly visible. And this monkey (Hanuman), whose roar is like a thunderous cloud, bellows loudly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 4. From a distance, the banner-top of Partha (Arjuna) is clearly visible. And this monkey (Hanuman), whose roar is like a thunderous cloud, bellows loudly.

sanskrit: एष तिष्ठन्रथश्रेष्ठो रथे रथवरप्रणुत् ।उत्कर्षति धनुःश्रेष्ठं गाण्डीवमशनिस्वनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: eṣa tiṣṭhanrathaśreṣṭho rathe rathavarapraṇut ,utkarṣati dhanuḥśreṣṭhaṁ gāṇḍīvamaśanisvanam.
english: Standing in his chariot, this foremost charioteer, who skillfully drives the excellent chariot, now draws the supreme Gāṇḍīva bow, which thunders like lightning.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 5. Standing in his chariot, this foremost charioteer, who skillfully drives the excellent chariot, now draws the supreme Gāṇḍīva bow, which thunders like lightning.

sanskrit: इमौ हि बाणौ सहितौ पादयोर्मे व्यवस्थितौ ।अपरौ चाप्यतिक्रान्तौ कर्णौ संस्पृश्य मे शरौ ॥६॥
transliteration: imau hi bāṇau sahitau pādayorme vyavasthitau ,aparau cāpyatikrāntau karṇau saṁspṛśya me śarau.
english: Indeed, these two arrows are lodged at my feet. And two other arrows have passed, grazing my ears.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 6. Indeed, these two arrows are lodged at my feet. And two other arrows have passed, grazing my ears.

sanskrit: निरुष्य हि वने वासं कृत्वा कर्मातिमानुषम् ।अभिवादयते पार्थः श्रोत्रे च परिपृच्छति ॥७॥
transliteration: niruṣya hi vane vāsaṁ kṛtvā karmātimānuṣam ,abhivādayate pārthaḥ śrotre ca paripṛcchati.
english: Indeed, after residing in the forest and performing superhuman deeds (karma), Arjuna (Pārtha) offers his salutations and then makes inquiries of the listeners.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 7. Indeed, after residing in the forest and performing superhuman deeds (karma), Arjuna (Pārtha) offers his salutations and then makes inquiries of the listeners.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।इषुपाते च सेनाया हयान्संयच्छ सारथे ।यावत्समीक्षे सैन्येऽस्मिन्क्वासौ कुरुकुलाधमः ॥८॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,iṣupāte ca senāyā hayānsaṁyaccha sārathe ,yāvatsamīkṣe sainye'sminkvāsau kurukulādhamaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: 'O charioteer! Hold the horses within the range of the army's arrows, until I can observe in this army where that disgrace of the Kuru family is.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 8. Arjuna said: 'O charioteer! Hold the horses within the range of the army's arrows, until I can observe in this army where that disgrace of the Kuru family is.'

sanskrit: सर्वानन्याननादृत्य दृष्ट्वा तमतिमानिनम् ।तस्य मूर्ध्नि पतिष्यामि तत एते पराजिताः ॥९॥
transliteration: sarvānanyānanādṛtya dṛṣṭvā tamatimāninam ,tasya mūrdhni patiṣyāmi tata ete parājitāḥ.
english: Ignoring all others, and having seen that excessively arrogant one, I shall strike him on the head; thereupon, they will be defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 9. Ignoring all others, and having seen that excessively arrogant one, I shall strike him on the head; thereupon, they will be defeated.

sanskrit: एष व्यवस्थितो द्रोणो द्रौणिश्च तदनन्तरम् ।भीष्मः कृपश्च कर्णश्च महेष्वासा व्यवस्थिताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: eṣa vyavasthito droṇo drauṇiśca tadanantaram ,bhīṣmaḥ kṛpaśca karṇaśca maheṣvāsā vyavasthitāḥ.
english: Here stands Droṇa, and immediately after him, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā). Bhīṣma, Kṛpa, and Karṇa, all great archers, are also arrayed there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 10. Here stands Droṇa, and immediately after him, Drauṇi (Aśvatthāmā). Bhīṣma, Kṛpa, and Karṇa, all great archers, are also arrayed there.

sanskrit: राजानं नात्र पश्यामि गाः समादाय गच्छति ।दक्षिणं मार्गमास्थाय शङ्के जीवपरायणः ॥११॥
transliteration: rājānaṁ nātra paśyāmi gāḥ samādāya gacchati ,dakṣiṇaṁ mārgamāsthāya śaṅke jīvaparāyaṇaḥ.
english: I do not see the king here; he is leaving, taking the cows. I suspect he has taken the southern path, intent on saving his life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 11. I do not see the king here; he is leaving, taking the cows. I suspect he has taken the southern path, intent on saving his life.

sanskrit: उत्सृज्यैतद्रथानीकं गच्छ यत्र सुयोधनः ।तत्रैव योत्स्ये वैराटे नास्ति युद्धं निरामिषम् ।तं जित्वा विनिवर्तिष्ये गाः समादाय वै पुनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: utsṛjyaitadrathānīkaṁ gaccha yatra suyodhanaḥ ,tatraiva yotsye vairāṭe nāsti yuddhaṁ nirāmiṣam ,taṁ jitvā vinivartiṣye gāḥ samādāya vai punaḥ.
english: Abandon this chariot army and go to where Suyodhana is, O son of Virāṭa. There alone I will fight, for there is no battle without a true objective. Having conquered him, I will surely return, bringing the cows back again.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 12. Abandon this chariot army and go to where Suyodhana is, O son of Virāṭa. There alone I will fight, for there is no battle without a true objective. Having conquered him, I will surely return, bringing the cows back again.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तः स वैराटिर्हयान्संयम्य यत्नतः ।नियम्य च ततो रश्मीन्यत्र ते कुरुपुंगवाः ।अचोदयत्ततो वाहान्यतो दुर्योधनस्ततः ॥१३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktaḥ sa vairāṭirhayānsaṁyamya yatnataḥ ,niyamya ca tato raśmīnyatra te kurupuṁgavāḥ ,acodayattato vāhānyato duryodhanastataḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus addressed, the son of Virāṭa, Uttara, carefully restraining the horses and then controlling the reins, drove the steeds towards where those foremost of Kurus and Duryodhana were.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 13. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Thus addressed, the son of Virāṭa, Uttara, carefully restraining the horses and then controlling the reins, drove the steeds towards where those foremost of Kurus and Duryodhana were.

sanskrit: उत्सृज्य रथवंशं तु प्रयाते श्वेतवाहने ।अभिप्रायं विदित्वास्य द्रोणो वचनमब्रवीत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: utsṛjya rathavaṁśaṁ tu prayāte śvetavāhane ,abhiprāyaṁ viditvāsya droṇo vacanamabravīt.
english: But when Śvetavāhana (Arjuna) had departed, abandoning the main line of chariots, Droṇa, having understood his intention, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 14. But when Śvetavāhana (Arjuna) had departed, abandoning the main line of chariots, Droṇa, having understood his intention, spoke these words.

sanskrit: नैषोऽन्तरेण राजानं बीभत्सुः स्थातुमिच्छति ।तस्य पार्ष्णिं ग्रहीष्यामो जवेनाभिप्रयास्यतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: naiṣo'ntareṇa rājānaṁ bībhatsuḥ sthātumicchati ,tasya pārṣṇiṁ grahīṣyāmo javenābhiprayāsyataḥ.
english: This Bibhatsu (Arjuna) does not wish to stand idly without confronting the king. We will swiftly strike his rear flank as he advances.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 15. This Bibhatsu (Arjuna) does not wish to stand idly without confronting the king. We will swiftly strike his rear flank as he advances.

sanskrit: न ह्येनमभिसंक्रुद्धमेको युध्येत संयुगे ।अन्यो देवात्सहस्राक्षात्कृष्णाद्वा देवकीसुतात् ॥१६॥
transliteration: na hyenamabhisaṁkruddhameko yudhyeta saṁyuge ,anyo devātsahasrākṣātkṛṣṇādvā devakīsutāt.
english: Indeed, no single person could fight him (Arjuna) in battle when he is greatly enraged, except for another god, either the thousand-eyed (Indra) or Krishna, Devaki's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 16. Indeed, no single person could fight him (Arjuna) in battle when he is greatly enraged, except for another god, either the thousand-eyed (Indra) or Krishna, Devaki's son.

sanskrit: किं नो गावः करिष्यन्ति धनं वा विपुलं तथा ।दुर्योधनः पार्थजले पुरा नौरिव मज्जति ॥१७॥
transliteration: kiṁ no gāvaḥ kariṣyanti dhanaṁ vā vipulaṁ tathā ,duryodhanaḥ pārthajale purā nauriva majjati.
english: What use will cows be to us, or even abundant wealth? Duryodhana will surely sink like a boat in the ocean (of prowess) of Partha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 17. What use will cows be to us, or even abundant wealth? Duryodhana will surely sink like a boat in the ocean (of prowess) of Partha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तथैव गत्वा बीभत्सुर्नाम विश्राव्य चात्मनः ।शलभैरिव तां सेनां शरैः शीघ्रमवाकिरत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathaiva gatvā bībhatsurnāma viśrāvya cātmanaḥ ,śalabhairiva tāṁ senāṁ śaraiḥ śīghramavākirat.
english: Indeed, having gone there, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), after announcing his own name (ātman), quickly showered that army with arrows, as if with a swarm of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 18. Indeed, having gone there, Bibhatsu (Arjuna), after announcing his own name (ātman), quickly showered that army with arrows, as if with a swarm of locusts.

sanskrit: कीर्यमाणाः शरौघैस्तु योधास्ते पार्थचोदितैः ।नापश्यन्नावृतां भूमिमन्तरिक्षं च पत्रिभिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: kīryamāṇāḥ śaraughaistu yodhāste pārthacoditaiḥ ,nāpaśyannāvṛtāṁ bhūmimantarikṣaṁ ca patribhiḥ.
english: Being overwhelmed by the showers of arrows unleashed by Partha (Arjuna), those warriors could not see any part of the earth or the sky that was not covered by arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 19. Being overwhelmed by the showers of arrows unleashed by Partha (Arjuna), those warriors could not see any part of the earth or the sky that was not covered by arrows.

sanskrit: तेषां नात्मनिनो युद्धे नापयानेऽभवन्मतिः ।शीघ्रत्वमेव पार्थस्य पूजयन्ति स्म चेतसा ॥२०॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ nātmanino yuddhe nāpayāne'bhavanmatiḥ ,śīghratvameva pārthasya pūjayanti sma cetasā.
english: They had no thought (mati) for their own self-preservation in battle, nor for retreat. Instead, they could only mentally praise Partha's (Arjuna's) exceptional swiftness.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 20. They had no thought (mati) for their own self-preservation in battle, nor for retreat. Instead, they could only mentally praise Partha's (Arjuna's) exceptional swiftness.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खं प्रदध्मौ स द्विषतां लोमहर्षणम् ।विस्फार्य च धनुःश्रेष्ठं ध्वजे भूतान्यचोदयत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhaṁ pradadhmau sa dviṣatāṁ lomaharṣaṇam ,visphārya ca dhanuḥśreṣṭhaṁ dhvaje bhūtānyacodayat.
english: Then, he (Arjuna) blew his conch, which caused the enemies' hair to stand on end. And stretching his supreme bow, he animated the beings (spirits) on his banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 21. Then, he (Arjuna) blew his conch, which caused the enemies' hair to stand on end. And stretching his supreme bow, he animated the beings (spirits) on his banner.

sanskrit: तस्य शङ्खस्य शब्देन रथनेमिस्वनेन च ।अमानुषाणां तेषां च भूतानां ध्वजवासिनाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tasya śaṅkhasya śabdena rathanemisvanena ca ,amānuṣāṇāṁ teṣāṁ ca bhūtānāṁ dhvajavāsinām.
english: By the sound of his conch, and by the rumble of the chariot wheels, and by the (sound/cries) of those non-human beings (spirits) residing on the banner,
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 22. By the sound of his conch, and by the rumble of the chariot wheels, and by the (sound/cries) of those non-human beings (spirits) residing on the banner,

sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं पुच्छान्विधुन्वाना रेभमाणाः समन्ततः ।गावः प्रतिन्यवर्तन्त दिशमास्थाय दक्षिणाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: ūrdhvaṁ pucchānvidhunvānā rebhamāṇāḥ samantataḥ ,gāvaḥ pratinyavartanta diśamāsthāya dakṣiṇām.
english: The cows, shaking their tails upwards and bellowing all around, turned back, heading towards the southern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 48 Verse 23. The cows, shaking their tails upwards and bellowing all around, turned back, heading towards the southern direction.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स शत्रुसेनां तरसा प्रणुद्य गास्ता विजित्याथ धनुर्धराग्र्यः ।दुर्योधनायाभिमुखं प्रयातो भूयोऽर्जुनः प्रियमाजौ चिकीर्षन् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa śatrusenāṁ tarasā praṇudya; gāstā vijityātha dhanurdharāgryaḥ ,duryodhanāyābhimukhaṁ prayāto; bhūyo'rjunaḥ priyamājau cikīrṣan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Arjuna, the foremost among archers, having swiftly repelled the enemy army and recovered those cows, then advanced again towards Duryodhana, intending to accomplish a pleasing feat in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Arjuna, the foremost among archers, having swiftly repelled the enemy army and recovered those cows, then advanced again towards Duryodhana, intending to accomplish a pleasing feat in battle.

sanskrit: गोषु प्रयातासु जवेन मत्स्यान्किरीटिनं कृतकार्यं च मत्वा ।दुर्योधनायाभिमुखं प्रयान्तं कुरुप्रवीराः सहसाभिपेतुः ॥२॥
transliteration: goṣu prayātāsu javena matsyā;nkirīṭinaṁ kṛtakāryaṁ ca matvā ,duryodhanāyābhimukhaṁ prayāntaṁ; kurupravīrāḥ sahasābhipetuḥ.
english: When the cows had swiftly departed, and recognizing that the Matsyas and Arjuna (Kirīṭin) had accomplished their task, the foremost Kuru warriors suddenly attacked Arjuna as he advanced towards Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 2. When the cows had swiftly departed, and recognizing that the Matsyas and Arjuna (Kirīṭin) had accomplished their task, the foremost Kuru warriors suddenly attacked Arjuna as he advanced towards Duryodhana.

sanskrit: तेषामनीकानि बहूनि गाढं व्यूढानि दृष्ट्वा बहुलध्वजानि ।मत्स्यस्य पुत्रं द्विषतां निहन्ता वैराटिमामन्त्र्य ततोऽभ्युवाच ॥३॥
transliteration: teṣāmanīkāni bahūni gāḍhaṁ; vyūḍhāni dṛṣṭvā bahuladhvajāni ,matsyasya putraṁ dviṣatāṁ nihantā; vairāṭimāmantrya tato'bhyuvāca.
english: Having seen their many armies, densely arrayed and bearing numerous banners, Arjuna, the slayer of enemies, then addressed Uttarā's brother (the son of Virāṭa and Matsya), and spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 3. Having seen their many armies, densely arrayed and bearing numerous banners, Arjuna, the slayer of enemies, then addressed Uttarā's brother (the son of Virāṭa and Matsya), and spoke.

sanskrit: एतेन तूर्णं प्रतिपादयेमाञ्श्वेतान्हयान्काञ्चनरश्मियोक्त्रान् ।जवेन सर्वेण कुरु प्रयत्नमासादयैतद्रथसिंहवृन्दम् ॥४॥
transliteration: etena tūrṇaṁ pratipādayemā;ñśvetānhayānkāñcanaraśmiyoktrān ,javena sarveṇa kuru prayatna;māsādayaitadrathasiṁhavṛndam.
english: With this, quickly bring forth these white horses, harnessed with golden reins. With all speed, make an effort and approach this multitude of lion-like chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 4. With this, quickly bring forth these white horses, harnessed with golden reins. With all speed, make an effort and approach this multitude of lion-like chariots.

sanskrit: गजो गजेनेव मया दुरात्मा यो योद्धुमाकाङ्क्षति सूतपुत्रः ।तमेव मां प्रापय राजपुत्र दुर्योधनापाश्रयजातदर्पम् ॥५॥
transliteration: gajo gajeneva mayā durātmā; yo yoddhumākāṅkṣati sūtaputraḥ ,tameva māṁ prāpaya rājaputra; duryodhanāpāśrayajātadarpam.
english: O Prince (rājaputra), lead me to that evil-minded son of a charioteer (sūtaputra) who desires to fight, and whose arrogance has arisen from his reliance on Duryodhana. I am ready to engage him, just as one elephant (gaja) would confront another, (and he will be vanquished) by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 5. O Prince (rājaputra), lead me to that evil-minded son of a charioteer (sūtaputra) who desires to fight, and whose arrogance has arisen from his reliance on Duryodhana. I am ready to engage him, just as one elephant (gaja) would confront another, (and he will be vanquished) by me.

sanskrit: स तैर्हयैर्वातजवैर्बृहद्भिः पुत्रो विराटस्य सुवर्णकक्ष्यैः ।विध्वंसयंस्तद्रथिनामनीकं ततोऽवहत्पाण्डवमाजिमध्ये ॥६॥
transliteration: sa tairhayairvātajavairbṛhadbhiḥ; putro virāṭasya suvarṇakakṣyaiḥ ,vidhvaṁsayaṁstadrathināmanīkaṁ; tato'vahatpāṇḍavamājimadhye.
english: He, the son of Virāṭa (putraḥ virāṭasya), with those mighty, wind-swift horses (vātajavaiḥ bṛhadbhiḥ) having golden harnesses (suvarṇakakṣyaiḥ), then, destroying that army (anīkam) of charioteers (rathinām), carried the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍavam) into the midst of the battle (ājimadhye).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 6. He, the son of Virāṭa (putraḥ virāṭasya), with those mighty, wind-swift horses (vātajavaiḥ bṛhadbhiḥ) having golden harnesses (suvarṇakakṣyaiḥ), then, destroying that army (anīkam) of charioteers (rathinām), carried the Pāṇḍava (pāṇḍavam) into the midst of the battle (ājimadhye).

sanskrit: तं चित्रसेनो विशिखैर्विपाठैः संग्रामजिच्छत्रुसहो जयश्च ।प्रत्युद्ययुर्भारतमापतन्तं महारथाः कर्णमभीप्समानाः ॥७॥
transliteration: taṁ citraseno viśikhairvipāṭhaiḥ; saṁgrāmajicchatrusaho jayaśca ,pratyudyayurbhāratamāpatantaṁ; mahārathāḥ karṇamabhīpsamānāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, Citrasena, Saṅgrāmajit, Śatrusaha, and Jaya – these great chariot-warriors (mahārathāḥ), desiring Karṇa (karṇam abhīpsamānāḥ), advanced to meet him (tam) as he was approaching (āpatantam).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 7. O Bhārata, Citrasena, Saṅgrāmajit, Śatrusaha, and Jaya – these great chariot-warriors (mahārathāḥ), desiring Karṇa (karṇam abhīpsamānāḥ), advanced to meet him (tam) as he was approaching (āpatantam).

sanskrit: ततः स तेषां पुरुषप्रवीरः शरासनार्चिः शरवेगतापः ।व्रातान्रथानामदहत्स मन्युर्वनं यथाग्निः कुरुपुंगवानाम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa teṣāṁ puruṣapravīraḥ; śarāsanārciḥ śaravegatāpaḥ ,vrātānrathānāmadahatsa manyu;rvanaṁ yathāgniḥ kurupuṁgavānām.
english: Then that hero among men, whose bow was like a flame and whose arrows' speed caused intense heat, consumed the arrays of chariots belonging to those Kuru chiefs, just as fire consumes a forest with its fury.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 8. Then that hero among men, whose bow was like a flame and whose arrows' speed caused intense heat, consumed the arrays of chariots belonging to those Kuru chiefs, just as fire consumes a forest with its fury.

sanskrit: तस्मिंस्तु युद्धे तुमुले प्रवृत्ते पार्थं विकर्णोऽतिरथं रथेन ।विपाठवर्षेण कुरुप्रवीरो भीमेन भीमानुजमाससाद ॥९॥
transliteration: tasmiṁstu yuddhe tumule pravṛtte; pārthaṁ vikarṇo'tirathaṁ rathena ,vipāṭhavarṣeṇa kurupravīro; bhīmena bhīmānujamāsasāda.
english: Indeed, when that tumultuous battle began, Vikarna, a hero of the Kurus, assailed Arjuna (pārtha)  -  who was a great charioteer (maharathi) and Bhima's younger brother  -  fiercely with a shower of broad-headed arrows from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 9. Indeed, when that tumultuous battle began, Vikarna, a hero of the Kurus, assailed Arjuna (pārtha)  -  who was a great charioteer (maharathi) and Bhima's younger brother  -  fiercely with a shower of broad-headed arrows from his chariot.

sanskrit: ततो विकर्णस्य धनुर्विकृष्य जाम्बूनदाग्र्योपचितं दृढज्यम् ।अपातयद्ध्वजमस्य प्रमथ्य छिन्नध्वजः सोऽप्यपयाज्जवेन ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato vikarṇasya dhanurvikṛṣya; jāmbūnadāgryopacitaṁ dṛḍhajyam ,apātayaddhvajamasya pramathya; chinnadhvajaḥ so'pyapayājjavena.
english: Then, having broken Vikarna's bow, which was adorned with excellent gold and had a strong string, and having destroyed his banner, (Arjuna) struck it down. Deprived of his banner, Vikarna himself quickly retreated.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 10. Then, having broken Vikarna's bow, which was adorned with excellent gold and had a strong string, and having destroyed his banner, (Arjuna) struck it down. Deprived of his banner, Vikarna himself quickly retreated.

sanskrit: तं शात्रवाणां गणबाधितारं कर्माणि कुर्वाणममानुषाणि ।शत्रुंतपः कोपममृष्यमाणः समर्पयत्कूर्मनखेन पार्थम् ॥११॥
transliteration: taṁ śātravāṇāṁ gaṇabādhitāraṁ; karmāṇi kurvāṇamamānuṣāṇi ,śatruṁtapaḥ kopamamṛṣyamāṇaḥ; samarpayatkūrmanakhena pārtham.
english: Unable to tolerate the anger, the tormentor of enemies attacked Arjuna (pārtha)  -  who was harassing the hosts of foes and performing superhuman deeds (karma)  -  with a tortoise-claw (weapon).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 11. Unable to tolerate the anger, the tormentor of enemies attacked Arjuna (pārtha)  -  who was harassing the hosts of foes and performing superhuman deeds (karma)  -  with a tortoise-claw (weapon).

sanskrit: स तेन राज्ञातिरथेन विद्धो विगाहमानो ध्वजिनीं कुरूणाम् ।शत्रुंतपं पञ्चभिराशु विद्ध्वा ततोऽस्य सूतं दशभिर्जघान ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa tena rājñātirathena viddho; vigāhamāno dhvajinīṁ kurūṇām ,śatruṁtapaṁ pañcabhirāśu viddhvā; tato'sya sūtaṁ daśabhirjaghāna.
english: He, though pierced by that great chariot-warrior king, entered the Kuru army. Having quickly pierced Shatruṃtapa with five (arrows), he then struck his charioteer with ten.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 12. He, though pierced by that great chariot-warrior king, entered the Kuru army. Having quickly pierced Shatruṃtapa with five (arrows), he then struck his charioteer with ten.

sanskrit: ततः स विद्धो भरतर्षभेण बाणेन गात्रावरणातिगेन ।गतासुराजौ निपपात भूमौ नगो नगाग्रादिव वातरुग्णः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa viddho bharatarṣabheṇa; bāṇena gātrāvaraṇātigena ,gatāsurājau nipapāta bhūmau; nago nagāgrādiva vātarugṇaḥ.
english: Then he, pierced by the best of Bharatas (Abhimanyu) with an arrow that penetrated his armor, his life having departed, fell to the ground in battle, just like a tree broken by the wind falls from a mountain peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 13. Then he, pierced by the best of Bharatas (Abhimanyu) with an arrow that penetrated his armor, his life having departed, fell to the ground in battle, just like a tree broken by the wind falls from a mountain peak.

sanskrit: रथर्षभास्ते तु रथर्षभेण वीरा रणे वीरतरेण भग्नाः ।चकम्पिरे वातवशेन काले प्रकम्पितानीव महावनानि ॥१४॥
transliteration: ratharṣabhāste tu ratharṣabheṇa; vīrā raṇe vīratareṇa bhagnāḥ ,cakampire vātavaśena kāle; prakampitānīva mahāvanāni.
english: Those excellent chariot-warriors, indeed, those heroes, were broken in battle by that exceedingly heroic best of chariot-warriors (Abhimanyu). They trembled, just as vast forests are shaken by the power of the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 14. Those excellent chariot-warriors, indeed, those heroes, were broken in battle by that exceedingly heroic best of chariot-warriors (Abhimanyu). They trembled, just as vast forests are shaken by the power of the wind.

sanskrit: हतास्तु पार्थेन नरप्रवीरा भूमौ युवानः सुषुपुः सुवेषाः ।वसुप्रदा वासवतुल्यवीर्याः पराजिता वासवजेन संख्ये ।सुवर्णकार्ष्णायसवर्मनद्धा नागा यथा हैमवताः प्रवृद्धाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: hatāstu pārthena narapravīrā; bhūmau yuvānaḥ suṣupuḥ suveṣāḥ ,vasupradā vāsavatulyavīryāḥ; parājitā vāsavajena saṁkhye ,suvarṇakārṣṇāyasavarmanaddhā; nāgā yathā haimavatāḥ pravṛddhāḥ.
english: Indeed, those excellent young warriors, finely attired, liberal bestowers of wealth, and possessing valor equal to Indra, were slain by the son of Pṛthā (Abhimanyu) and defeated in battle by the son of Vāsava (Abhimanyu). They lay on the ground, armored in gold and black iron, like colossal Himalayan elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 15. Indeed, those excellent young warriors, finely attired, liberal bestowers of wealth, and possessing valor equal to Indra, were slain by the son of Pṛthā (Abhimanyu) and defeated in battle by the son of Vāsava (Abhimanyu). They lay on the ground, armored in gold and black iron, like colossal Himalayan elephants.

sanskrit: तथा स शत्रून्समरे विनिघ्नन्गाण्डीवधन्वा पुरुषप्रवीरः ।चचार संख्ये प्रदिशो दिशश्च दहन्निवाग्निर्वनमातपान्ते ॥१६॥
transliteration: tathā sa śatrūnsamare vinighna;ngāṇḍīvadhanvā puruṣapravīraḥ ,cacāra saṁkhye pradiśo diśaśca; dahannivāgnirvanamātapānte.
english: Similarly, that foremost hero among men, Arjuna (Gāṇḍīvadhanvā), striking down his enemies in battle, moved about in the combat zone, covering all main and intermediate directions, just as a fire burns down a forest at the end of the dry season.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 16. Similarly, that foremost hero among men, Arjuna (Gāṇḍīvadhanvā), striking down his enemies in battle, moved about in the combat zone, covering all main and intermediate directions, just as a fire burns down a forest at the end of the dry season.

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णपर्णानि यथा वसन्ते विशातयित्वात्यनिलो नुदन्खे ।तथा सपत्नान्विकिरन्किरीटी चचार संख्येऽतिरथो रथेन ॥१७॥
transliteration: prakīrṇaparṇāni yathā vasante; viśātayitvātyanilo nudankhe ,tathā sapatnānvikirankirīṭī; cacāra saṁkhye'tiratho rathena.
english: Just as a mighty wind in spring scatters torn leaves, pushing them into the sky, so too did the crowned one (Arjuna), a great warrior, move about in battle with his chariot, scattering his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 17. Just as a mighty wind in spring scatters torn leaves, pushing them into the sky, so too did the crowned one (Arjuna), a great warrior, move about in battle with his chariot, scattering his enemies.

sanskrit: शोणाश्ववाहस्य हयान्निहत्य वैकर्तनभ्रातुरदीनसत्त्वः ।एकेन संग्रामजितः शरेण शिरो जहाराथ किरीटमाली ॥१८॥
transliteration: śoṇāśvavāhasya hayānnihatya; vaikartanabhrāturadīnasattvaḥ ,ekena saṁgrāmajitaḥ śareṇa; śiro jahārātha kirīṭamālī.
english: The crowned one (Arjuna), of an unyielding spirit, first struck down the horses of the driver of red horses and those of Vaikartana's (Karna's) brother. Then, with a single arrow, he severed the head of Jayadratha, the conqueror of battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 18. The crowned one (Arjuna), of an unyielding spirit, first struck down the horses of the driver of red horses and those of Vaikartana's (Karna's) brother. Then, with a single arrow, he severed the head of Jayadratha, the conqueror of battles.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्हते भ्रातरि सूतपुत्रो वैकर्तनो वीर्यमथाददानः ।प्रगृह्य दन्ताविव नागराजो महर्षभं व्याघ्र इवाभ्यधावत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tasminhate bhrātari sūtaputro; vaikartano vīryamathādadānaḥ ,pragṛhya dantāviva nāgarājo; maharṣabhaṁ vyāghra ivābhyadhāvat.
english: When his brother (Jayadratha) was killed, Karna, the son of the charioteer and Vaikartana, then taking up his valor, rushed forth like a king of elephants seizing with its tusks, or like a tiger attacking a great bull.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 19. When his brother (Jayadratha) was killed, Karna, the son of the charioteer and Vaikartana, then taking up his valor, rushed forth like a king of elephants seizing with its tusks, or like a tiger attacking a great bull.

sanskrit: स पाण्डवं द्वादशभिः पृषत्कैर्वैकर्तनः शीघ्रमुपाजघान ।विव्याध गात्रेषु हयांश्च सर्वान्विराटपुत्रं च शरैर्निजघ्ने ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa pāṇḍavaṁ dvādaśabhiḥ pṛṣatkai;rvaikartanaḥ śīghramupājaghāna ,vivyādha gātreṣu hayāṁśca sarvā;nvirāṭaputraṁ ca śarairnijaghne.
english: The son of Vikaṛta (Karṇa) quickly struck the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with twelve arrows. He pierced all the horses in their limbs and also struck Virāṭa's son (Uttara) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 20. The son of Vikaṛta (Karṇa) quickly struck the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with twelve arrows. He pierced all the horses in their limbs and also struck Virāṭa's son (Uttara) with arrows.

sanskrit: स हस्तिनेवाभिहतो गजेन्द्रः प्रगृह्य भल्लान्निशितान्निषङ्गात् ।आकर्णपूर्णं च धनुर्विकृष्य विव्याध बाणैरथ सूतपुत्रम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa hastinevābhihato gajendraḥ; pragṛhya bhallānniśitānniṣaṅgāt ,ākarṇapūrṇaṁ ca dhanurvikṛṣya; vivyādha bāṇairatha sūtaputram.
english: Like a great elephant struck by another elephant, he (Arjuna) took sharp arrows from his quiver. Then, drawing his bow fully to his ear, he pierced the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 21. Like a great elephant struck by another elephant, he (Arjuna) took sharp arrows from his quiver. Then, drawing his bow fully to his ear, he pierced the son of the charioteer (Karṇa) with arrows.

sanskrit: अथास्य बाहूरुशिरोललाटं ग्रीवां रथाङ्गानि परावमर्दी ।स्थितस्य बाणैर्युधि निर्बिभेद गाण्डीवमुक्तैरशनिप्रकाशैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: athāsya bāhūruśirolalāṭaṁ; grīvāṁ rathāṅgāni parāvamardī ,sthitasya bāṇairyudhi nirbibheda; gāṇḍīvamuktairaśaniprakāśaiḥ.
english: Then he, the tormentor of enemies, (Arjuna) pierced his (Karṇa's) arms, thighs, head, forehead, neck, and chariot parts with arrows that were released from the Gāṇḍīva bow and shone like lightning, while he (Karṇa) was standing in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 22. Then he, the tormentor of enemies, (Arjuna) pierced his (Karṇa's) arms, thighs, head, forehead, neck, and chariot parts with arrows that were released from the Gāṇḍīva bow and shone like lightning, while he (Karṇa) was standing in battle.

sanskrit: स पार्थमुक्तैर्विशिखैः प्रणुन्नो गजो गजेनेव जितस्तरस्वी ।विहाय संग्रामशिरः प्रयातो वैकर्तनः पाण्डवबाणतप्तः ॥२३॥
transliteration: sa pārthamuktairviśikhaiḥ praṇunno; gajo gajeneva jitastarasvī ,vihāya saṁgrāmaśiraḥ prayāto; vaikartanaḥ pāṇḍavabāṇataptaḥ.
english: That mighty son of Vikaṛta (Karṇa), struck by the arrows released by Pārtha (Arjuna) and overcome as an elephant by another elephant, abandoned the forefront of the battle, scorched by the Pāṇḍava's arrows, and departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 49 Verse 23. That mighty son of Vikaṛta (Karṇa), struck by the arrows released by Pārtha (Arjuna) and overcome as an elephant by another elephant, abandoned the forefront of the battle, scorched by the Pāṇḍava's arrows, and departed.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अपयाते तु राधेये दुर्योधनपुरोगमाः ।अनीकेन यथास्वेन शरैरार्च्छन्त पाण्डवम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,apayāte tu rādheye duryodhanapurogamāḥ ,anīkena yathāsvena śarairārcchanta pāṇḍavam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When Rādhā's son (Karṇa) had departed, Duryodhana and his foremost warriors, along with their respective divisions, attacked the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When Rādhā's son (Karṇa) had departed, Duryodhana and his foremost warriors, along with their respective divisions, attacked the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with arrows.

sanskrit: बहुधा तस्य सैन्यस्य व्यूढस्यापततः शरैः ।अभियानीयमाज्ञाय वैराटिरिदमब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: bahudhā tasya sainyasya vyūḍhasyāpatataḥ śaraiḥ ,abhiyānīyamājñāya vairāṭiridamabravīt.
english: Realizing this attack, which involved numerous arrows coming from their well-arranged and advancing army, Uttara, the son of Virāṭa, said this.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 2. Realizing this attack, which involved numerous arrows coming from their well-arranged and advancing army, Uttara, the son of Virāṭa, said this.

sanskrit: आस्थाय रुचिरं जिष्णो रथं सारथिना मया ।कतमद्यास्यसेऽनीकमुक्तो यास्याम्यहं त्वया ॥३॥
transliteration: āsthāya ruciraṁ jiṣṇo rathaṁ sārathinā mayā ,katamadyāsyase'nīkamukto yāsyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: O Jishṇu (Arjuna), after ascending this splendid chariot with me as your charioteer, which division will you attack today? I will go wherever you instruct me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 3. O Jishṇu (Arjuna), after ascending this splendid chariot with me as your charioteer, which division will you attack today? I will go wherever you instruct me.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।लोहिताक्षमरिष्टं यं वैयाघ्रमनुपश्यसि ।नीलां पताकामाश्रित्य रथे तिष्ठन्तमुत्तर ॥४॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,lohitākṣamariṣṭaṁ yaṁ vaiyāghramanupaśyasi ,nīlāṁ patākāmāśritya rathe tiṣṭhantamuttara.
english: Arjuna said: "O Uttara, you see that red-eyed, uninjured warrior, who is wearing a tiger-skin and standing in his chariot with a blue banner."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 4. Arjuna said: "O Uttara, you see that red-eyed, uninjured warrior, who is wearing a tiger-skin and standing in his chariot with a blue banner."

sanskrit: कृपस्यैतद्रथानीकं प्रापयस्वैतदेव माम् ।एतस्य दर्शयिष्यामि शीघ्रास्त्रं दृढधन्विनः ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛpasyaitadrathānīkaṁ prāpayasvaitadeva mām ,etasya darśayiṣyāmi śīghrāstraṁ dṛḍhadhanvinaḥ.
english: Lead me to this very chariot army of Kṛpa. I will show my swift weapons to this firm-bowed warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 5. Lead me to this very chariot army of Kṛpa. I will show my swift weapons to this firm-bowed warrior.

sanskrit: कमण्डलुर्ध्वजे यस्य शातकुम्भमयः शुभः ।आचार्य एष वै द्रोणः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ॥६॥
transliteration: kamaṇḍalurdhvaje yasya śātakumbhamayaḥ śubhaḥ ,ācārya eṣa vai droṇaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ.
english: He whose banner bears an auspicious water-pot (kamaṇḍalu) made of pure gold – this indeed is preceptor Droṇa, the foremost among all weapon-bearers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 6. He whose banner bears an auspicious water-pot (kamaṇḍalu) made of pure gold – this indeed is preceptor Droṇa, the foremost among all weapon-bearers.

sanskrit: सुप्रसन्नमना वीर कुरुष्वैनं प्रदक्षिणम् ।अत्रैव चाविरोधेन एष धर्मः सनातनः ॥७॥
transliteration: suprasannamanā vīra kuruṣvainaṁ pradakṣiṇam ,atraiva cāvirodhena eṣa dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: O hero, with a very joyful mind, circumambulate him clockwise. And right here, without any conflict, this is the eternal natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 7. O hero, with a very joyful mind, circumambulate him clockwise. And right here, without any conflict, this is the eternal natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: यदि मे प्रथमं द्रोणः शरीरे प्रहरिष्यति ।ततोऽस्य प्रहरिष्यामि नास्य कोपो भविष्यति ॥८॥
transliteration: yadi me prathamaṁ droṇaḥ śarīre prahariṣyati ,tato'sya prahariṣyāmi nāsya kopo bhaviṣyati.
english: If Droṇa strikes my body first, then I will strike him back. His anger will not arise.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 8. If Droṇa strikes my body first, then I will strike him back. His anger will not arise.

sanskrit: अस्याविदूरे तु धनुर्ध्वजाग्रे यस्य दृश्यते ।आचार्यस्यैष पुत्रो वै अश्वत्थामा महारथः ॥९॥
transliteration: asyāvidūre tu dhanurdhvajāgre yasya dṛśyate ,ācāryasyaiṣa putro vai aśvatthāmā mahārathaḥ.
english: Not far from him, at the top of whose banner a bow is seen, is indeed this son of the preceptor (guru), Ashvatthama, the great chariot-warrior.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 9. Not far from him, at the top of whose banner a bow is seen, is indeed this son of the preceptor (guru), Ashvatthama, the great chariot-warrior.

sanskrit: सदा ममैष मान्यश्च सर्वशस्त्रभृतामपि ।एतस्य त्वं रथं प्राप्य निवर्तेथाः पुनः पुनः ॥१०॥
transliteration: sadā mamaiṣa mānyaśca sarvaśastrabhṛtāmapi ,etasya tvaṁ rathaṁ prāpya nivartethāḥ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: He is always honored by me, and indeed by all those who bear weapons. After reaching his chariot, you should repeatedly turn back.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 10. He is always honored by me, and indeed by all those who bear weapons. After reaching his chariot, you should repeatedly turn back.

sanskrit: य एष तु रथानीके सुवर्णकवचावृतः ।सेनाग्र्येण तृतीयेन व्यवहार्येण तिष्ठति ॥११॥
transliteration: ya eṣa tu rathānīke suvarṇakavacāvṛtaḥ ,senāgryeṇa tṛtīyena vyavahāryeṇa tiṣṭhati.
english: He who, indeed, is in the chariot array, covered with golden armor, stands with the third vanguard of the army, which is fit for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 11. He who, indeed, is in the chariot array, covered with golden armor, stands with the third vanguard of the army, which is fit for battle.

sanskrit: यस्य नागो ध्वजाग्रे वै हेमकेतनसंश्रितः ।धृतराष्ट्रात्मजः श्रीमानेष राजा सुयोधनः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yasya nāgo dhvajāgre vai hemaketanasaṁśritaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭrātmajaḥ śrīmāneṣa rājā suyodhanaḥ.
english: An elephant rests at the top of whose banner, supported by a golden emblem; this is indeed the glorious son of Dhritarashtra, King Suyodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 12. An elephant rests at the top of whose banner, supported by a golden emblem; this is indeed the glorious son of Dhritarashtra, King Suyodhana.

sanskrit: एतस्याभिमुखं वीर रथं पररथारुजः ।प्रापयस्वैष तेजोभिप्रमाथी युद्धदुर्मदः ॥१३॥
transliteration: etasyābhimukhaṁ vīra rathaṁ pararathārujaḥ ,prāpayasvaiṣa tejobhipramāthī yuddhadurmadaḥ.
english: O hero, destroyer of enemy chariots, drive the chariot to face him. This man is overwhelming with his prowess and unconquerable in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 13. O hero, destroyer of enemy chariots, drive the chariot to face him. This man is overwhelming with his prowess and unconquerable in battle.

sanskrit: एष द्रोणस्य शिष्याणां शीघ्रास्त्रः प्रथमो मतः ।एतस्य दर्शयिष्यामि शीघ्रास्त्रं विपुलं शरैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: eṣa droṇasya śiṣyāṇāṁ śīghrāstraḥ prathamo mataḥ ,etasya darśayiṣyāmi śīghrāstraṁ vipulaṁ śaraiḥ.
english: He is considered the foremost among Droṇa's disciples in the swift use of weapons. I will demonstrate my vast skill in swift archery to him with my arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 14. He is considered the foremost among Droṇa's disciples in the swift use of weapons. I will demonstrate my vast skill in swift archery to him with my arrows.

sanskrit: नागकक्ष्या तु रुचिरा ध्वजाग्रे यस्य तिष्ठति ।एष वैकर्तनः कर्णो विदितः पूर्वमेव ते ॥१५॥
transliteration: nāgakakṣyā tu rucirā dhvajāgre yasya tiṣṭhati ,eṣa vaikartanaḥ karṇo viditaḥ pūrvameva te.
english: A splendid serpent ensign stands at the top of his banner. This is Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana, who is already known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 15. A splendid serpent ensign stands at the top of his banner. This is Karṇa, the son of Vīkartana, who is already known to you.

sanskrit: एतस्य रथमास्थाय राधेयस्य दुरात्मनः ।यत्तो भवेथाः संग्रामे स्पर्धत्येष मया सदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: etasya rathamāsthāya rādheyasya durātmanaḥ ,yatto bhavethāḥ saṁgrāme spardhatyeṣa mayā sadā.
english: Mounting the chariot of this wicked-souled Radheya (Karṇa), you should be prepared in battle. He always challenges me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 16. Mounting the chariot of this wicked-souled Radheya (Karṇa), you should be prepared in battle. He always challenges me.

sanskrit: यस्तु नीलानुसारेण पञ्चतारेण केतुना ।हस्तावापी बृहद्धन्वा रथे तिष्ठति वीर्यवान् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yastu nīlānusāreṇa pañcatāreṇa ketunā ,hastāvāpī bṛhaddhanvā rathe tiṣṭhati vīryavān.
english: And he who, valorous, stands in his chariot, with a great bow and a quiver at hand, and whose flag is blue and bears five stars...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 17. And he who, valorous, stands in his chariot, with a great bow and a quiver at hand, and whose flag is blue and bears five stars...

sanskrit: यस्य तारार्कचित्रोऽसौ रथे ध्वजवरः स्थितः ।यस्यैतत्पाण्डुरं छत्रं विमलं मूर्ध्नि तिष्ठति ॥१८॥
transliteration: yasya tārārkacitro'sau rathe dhvajavaraḥ sthitaḥ ,yasyaitatpāṇḍuraṁ chatraṁ vimalaṁ mūrdhni tiṣṭhati.
english: Whose excellent banner, adorned with stars and the sun, is situated on his chariot, and whose spotless white umbrella rests over his head.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 18. Whose excellent banner, adorned with stars and the sun, is situated on his chariot, and whose spotless white umbrella rests over his head.

sanskrit: महतो रथवंशस्य नानाध्वजपताकिनः ।बलाहकाग्रे सूर्यो वा य एष प्रमुखे स्थितः ॥१९॥
transliteration: mahato rathavaṁśasya nānādhvajapatākinaḥ ,balāhakāgre sūryo vā ya eṣa pramukhe sthitaḥ.
english: This very person who stands prominently, like the sun at the forefront of the clouds, amidst a great multitude of chariots adorned with various banners and flags...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 19. This very person who stands prominently, like the sun at the forefront of the clouds, amidst a great multitude of chariots adorned with various banners and flags...

sanskrit: हैमं चन्द्रार्कसंकाशं कवचं यस्य दृश्यते ।जातरूपशिरस्त्राणस्त्रासयन्निव मे मनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: haimaṁ candrārkasaṁkāśaṁ kavacaṁ yasya dṛśyate ,jātarūpaśirastrāṇastrāsayanniva me manaḥ.
english: Whose golden armor shines like the moon and sun, and whose golden helmet seems to strike fear into my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 20. Whose golden armor shines like the moon and sun, and whose golden helmet seems to strike fear into my mind.

sanskrit: एष शांतनवो भीष्मः सर्वेषां नः पितामहः ।राजश्रियावबद्धस्तु दुर्योधनवशानुगः ॥२१॥
transliteration: eṣa śāṁtanavo bhīṣmaḥ sarveṣāṁ naḥ pitāmahaḥ ,rājaśriyāvabaddhastu duryodhanavaśānugaḥ.
english: This Bhīṣma, the son of Śantanu, is the grandfather of all of us. However, he is bound by royal power and follows Duryodhana's command.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 21. This Bhīṣma, the son of Śantanu, is the grandfather of all of us. However, he is bound by royal power and follows Duryodhana's command.

sanskrit: पश्चादेष प्रयातव्यो न मे विघ्नकरो भवेत् ।एतेन युध्यमानस्य यत्तः संयच्छ मे हयान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: paścādeṣa prayātavyo na me vighnakaro bhavet ,etena yudhyamānasya yattaḥ saṁyaccha me hayān.
english: He should be dealt with later; he should not become an obstacle for me now. When I am fighting against him, you must be careful and restrain my horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 22. He should be dealt with later; he should not become an obstacle for me now. When I am fighting against him, you must be careful and restrain my horses.

sanskrit: ततोऽभ्यवहदव्यग्रो वैराटिः सव्यसाचिनम् ।यत्रातिष्ठत्कृपो राजन्योत्स्यमानो धनंजयम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato'bhyavahadavyagro vairāṭiḥ savyasācinam ,yatrātiṣṭhatkṛpo rājanyotsyamāno dhanaṁjayam.
english: Then, O King, the unagitated son of Virāṭa (Uttara) drove towards the ambidextrous Arjuna, where Kṛpa stood, intending to fight against Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 50 Verse 23. Then, O King, the unagitated son of Virāṭa (Uttara) drove towards the ambidextrous Arjuna, where Kṛpa stood, intending to fight against Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तान्यनीकान्यदृश्यन्त कुरूणामुग्रधन्विनाम् ।संसर्पन्तो यथा मेघा घर्मान्ते मन्दमारुताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tānyanīkānyadṛśyanta kurūṇāmugradhanvinām ,saṁsarpanto yathā meghā gharmānte mandamārutāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: The armies of those Kuru warriors, fierce with their bows, were seen advancing like clouds moved by gentle breezes at the end of the hot season.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: The armies of those Kuru warriors, fierce with their bows, were seen advancing like clouds moved by gentle breezes at the end of the hot season.

sanskrit: अभ्याशे वाजिनस्तस्थुः समारूढाः प्रहारिभिः ।भीमरूपाश्च मातङ्गास्तोमराङ्कुशचोदिताः ॥२॥
transliteration: abhyāśe vājinastasthuḥ samārūḍhāḥ prahāribhiḥ ,bhīmarūpāśca mātaṅgāstomarāṅkuśacoditāḥ.
english: Mounted by warriors, horses stood nearby, and fierce-looking elephants, spurred on by lances and goads, were also present.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 2. Mounted by warriors, horses stood nearby, and fierce-looking elephants, spurred on by lances and goads, were also present.

sanskrit: ततः शक्रः सुरगणैः समारुह्य सुदर्शनम् ।सहोपायात्तदा राजन्विश्वाश्विमरुतां गणैः ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śakraḥ suragaṇaiḥ samāruhya sudarśanam ,sahopāyāttadā rājanviśvāśvimarutāṁ gaṇaiḥ.
english: Then, O king, Indra, accompanied by the hosts of gods and having mounted Sudarśana, arrived along with the hosts of Viśvas, Aśvins, and Maruts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 3. Then, O king, Indra, accompanied by the hosts of gods and having mounted Sudarśana, arrived along with the hosts of Viśvas, Aśvins, and Maruts.

sanskrit: तद्देवयक्षगन्धर्वमहोरगसमाकुलम् ।शुशुभेऽभ्रविनिर्मुक्तं ग्रहैरिव नभस्तलम् ॥४॥
transliteration: taddevayakṣagandharvamahoragasamākulam ,śuśubhe'bhravinirmuktaṁ grahairiva nabhastalam.
english: That expanse, crowded with gods, yakshas, gandharvas, and great serpents, shone splendidly, just like a cloudless sky illuminated by planets.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 4. That expanse, crowded with gods, yakshas, gandharvas, and great serpents, shone splendidly, just like a cloudless sky illuminated by planets.
