sanskrit: अस्त्राणां च बलं तेषां मानुषेषु प्रयुज्यताम् ।तच्च घोरं महद्युद्धं भीष्मार्जुनसमागमे ॥५॥
transliteration: astrāṇāṁ ca balaṁ teṣāṁ mānuṣeṣu prayujyatām ,tacca ghoraṁ mahadyuddhaṁ bhīṣmārjunasamāgame.
english: And let the power of their celestial weapons be employed against humans. That great and terrible battle occurred at the encounter of Bhishma and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 5. And let the power of their celestial weapons be employed against humans. That great and terrible battle occurred at the encounter of Bhishma and Arjuna.

sanskrit: शतं शतसहस्राणां यत्र स्थूणा हिरण्मयाः ।मणिरत्नमयाश्चान्याः प्रासादमुपधारयन् ॥६॥
transliteration: śataṁ śatasahasrāṇāṁ yatra sthūṇā hiraṇmayāḥ ,maṇiratnamayāścānyāḥ prāsādamupadhārayan.
english: Where millions of golden pillars, and other pillars adorned with jewels and gems, supported the palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 6. Where millions of golden pillars, and other pillars adorned with jewels and gems, supported the palace.

sanskrit: तत्र कामगमं दिव्यं सर्वरत्नविभूषितम् ।विमानं देवराजस्य शुशुभे खेचरं तदा ॥७॥
transliteration: tatra kāmagamaṁ divyaṁ sarvaratnavibhūṣitam ,vimānaṁ devarājasya śuśubhe khecaraṁ tadā.
english: There, at that time, the divine aerial car (vimāna) of the king of gods, which was adorned with all jewels and could move wherever desired, shone brilliantly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 7. There, at that time, the divine aerial car (vimāna) of the king of gods, which was adorned with all jewels and could move wherever desired, shone brilliantly.

sanskrit: तत्र देवास्त्रयस्त्रिंशत्तिष्ठन्ति सहवासवाः ।गन्धर्वा राक्षसाः सर्पाः पितरश्च महर्षिभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: tatra devāstrayastriṁśattiṣṭhanti sahavāsavāḥ ,gandharvā rākṣasāḥ sarpāḥ pitaraśca maharṣibhiḥ.
english: There reside the thirty-three gods along with Indra, as well as Gandharvas, Rakshasas, Serpents, and the Pitṛs (ancestors) with the great sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 8. There reside the thirty-three gods along with Indra, as well as Gandharvas, Rakshasas, Serpents, and the Pitṛs (ancestors) with the great sages.

sanskrit: तथा राजा वसुमना बलाक्षः सुप्रतर्दनः ।अष्टकश्च शिबिश्चैव ययातिर्नहुषो गयः ॥९॥
transliteration: tathā rājā vasumanā balākṣaḥ supratardanaḥ ,aṣṭakaśca śibiścaiva yayātirnahuṣo gayaḥ.
english: And similarly, King Vasumanas, Balākṣa, Supratardana, and also Aṣṭaka, Śibi, Yayāti, Nahuṣa, and Gaya were present.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 9. And similarly, King Vasumanas, Balākṣa, Supratardana, and also Aṣṭaka, Śibi, Yayāti, Nahuṣa, and Gaya were present.

sanskrit: मनुः क्षुपो रघुर्भानुः कृशाश्वः सगरः शलः ।विमाने देवराजस्य समदृश्यन्त सुप्रभाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: manuḥ kṣupo raghurbhānuḥ kṛśāśvaḥ sagaraḥ śalaḥ ,vimāne devarājasya samadṛśyanta suprabhāḥ.
english: Manu, Kṣupa, Raghu, Bhānu, Kṛśāśva, Sagara, and Śala appeared exceedingly radiant in the aerial chariot (vimāna) of the king of gods (devarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 10. Manu, Kṣupa, Raghu, Bhānu, Kṛśāśva, Sagara, and Śala appeared exceedingly radiant in the aerial chariot (vimāna) of the king of gods (devarāja).

sanskrit: अग्नेरीशस्य सोमस्य वरुणस्य प्रजापतेः ।तथा धातुर्विधातुश्च कुबेरस्य यमस्य च ॥११॥
transliteration: agnerīśasya somasya varuṇasya prajāpateḥ ,tathā dhāturvidhātuśca kuberasya yamasya ca.
english: (Similarly, the aerial chariots) of Agni, Īśa, Soma, Varuṇa, Prajāpati, and also of Dhātṛ, Vidhātṛ, Kubera, and Yama (shone).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 11. (Similarly, the aerial chariots) of Agni, Īśa, Soma, Varuṇa, Prajāpati, and also of Dhātṛ, Vidhātṛ, Kubera, and Yama (shone).

sanskrit: अलम्बुसोग्रसेनस्य गन्धर्वस्य च तुम्बुरोः ।यथाभागं यथोद्देशं विमानानि चकाशिरे ॥१२॥
transliteration: alambusograsenasya gandharvasya ca tumburoḥ ,yathābhāgaṁ yathoddeśaṁ vimānāni cakāśire.
english: The aerial chariots (vimānas) of Alambuṣa and Ugrasena, and of the gandharva Tumburu, shone according to their respective shares and designated places.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 12. The aerial chariots (vimānas) of Alambuṣa and Ugrasena, and of the gandharva Tumburu, shone according to their respective shares and designated places.

sanskrit: सर्वदेवनिकायाश्च सिद्धाश्च परमर्षयः ।अर्जुनस्य कुरूणां च द्रष्टुं युद्धमुपागताः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sarvadevanikāyāśca siddhāśca paramarṣayaḥ ,arjunasya kurūṇāṁ ca draṣṭuṁ yuddhamupāgatāḥ.
english: And all the hosts of gods, the perfected beings (siddhas), and the great sages (paramarṣis) arrived to witness the battle between Arjuna and the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 13. And all the hosts of gods, the perfected beings (siddhas), and the great sages (paramarṣis) arrived to witness the battle between Arjuna and the Kurus.

sanskrit: दिव्यानां तत्र माल्यानां गन्धः पुण्योऽथ सर्वशः ।प्रससार वसन्ताग्रे वनानामिव पुष्पताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: divyānāṁ tatra mālyānāṁ gandhaḥ puṇyo'tha sarvaśaḥ ,prasasāra vasantāgre vanānāmiva puṣpatām.
english: There, the sacred fragrance of divine garlands spread everywhere, just as the blossoming of forests does at the onset of spring.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 14. There, the sacred fragrance of divine garlands spread everywhere, just as the blossoming of forests does at the onset of spring.

sanskrit: रक्तारक्तानि देवानां समदृश्यन्त तिष्ठताम् ।आतपत्राणि वासांसि स्रजश्च व्यजनानि च ॥१५॥
transliteration: raktāraktāni devānāṁ samadṛśyanta tiṣṭhatām ,ātapatrāṇi vāsāṁsi srajaśca vyajanāni ca.
english: The variously red parasols, garments, garlands, and fans of the gods who were standing there were seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 15. The variously red parasols, garments, garlands, and fans of the gods who were standing there were seen.

sanskrit: उपशाम्यद्रजो भौमं सर्वं व्याप्तं मरीचिभिः ।दिव्यान्गन्धानुपादाय वायुर्योधानसेवत ॥१६॥
transliteration: upaśāmyadrajo bhaumaṁ sarvaṁ vyāptaṁ marīcibhiḥ ,divyāngandhānupādāya vāyuryodhānasevata.
english: All the earthly dust was subsiding, pervaded by rays. The wind, carrying divine fragrances, attended to the warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 16. All the earthly dust was subsiding, pervaded by rays. The wind, carrying divine fragrances, attended to the warriors.

sanskrit: प्रभासितमिवाकाशं चित्ररूपमलंकृतम् ।संपतद्भिः स्थितैश्चैव नानारत्नावभासितैः ।विमानैर्विविधैश्चित्रैरुपानीतैः सुरोत्तमैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: prabhāsitamivākāśaṁ citrarūpamalaṁkṛtam ,saṁpatadbhiḥ sthitaiścaiva nānāratnāvabhāsitaiḥ ,vimānairvividhaiścitrairupānītaiḥ surottamaiḥ.
english: The sky appeared as if illuminated, adorned with diverse forms, by the flying and stationary celestial cars. These celestial cars, of various kinds and brilliant, were illuminated by diverse jewels and were brought by the best of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 51 Verse 17. The sky appeared as if illuminated, adorned with diverse forms, by the flying and stationary celestial cars. These celestial cars, of various kinds and brilliant, were illuminated by diverse jewels and were brought by the best of the gods.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतस्मिन्नन्तरे तत्र महावीर्यपराक्रमः ।आजगाम महासत्त्वः कृपः शस्त्रभृतां वरः ।अर्जुनं प्रति संयोद्धुं युद्धार्थी स महारथः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etasminnantare tatra mahāvīryaparākramaḥ ,ājagāma mahāsattvaḥ kṛpaḥ śastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,arjunaṁ prati saṁyoddhuṁ yuddhārthī sa mahārathaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: At that moment, Kṛpa arrived there, a man of great fortitude and immense valor and might, the foremost among those who bear weapons. That great chariot-warrior, eager for battle, came to fight against Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: At that moment, Kṛpa arrived there, a man of great fortitude and immense valor and might, the foremost among those who bear weapons. That great chariot-warrior, eager for battle, came to fight against Arjuna.

sanskrit: तौ रथौ सूर्यसंकाशौ योत्स्यमानौ महाबलौ ।शारदाविव जीमूतौ व्यरोचेतां व्यवस्थितौ ॥२॥
transliteration: tau rathau sūryasaṁkāśau yotsyamānau mahābalau ,śāradāviva jīmūtau vyarocetāṁ vyavasthitau.
english: Those two chariots, resembling the sun, mighty and about to engage in battle, shone beautifully as they stood positioned, like two clouds in the autumn season.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 2. Those two chariots, resembling the sun, mighty and about to engage in battle, shone beautifully as they stood positioned, like two clouds in the autumn season.

sanskrit: पार्थोऽपि विश्रुतं लोके गाण्डीवं परमायुधम् ।विकृष्य चिक्षेप बहून्नाराचान्मर्मभेदिनः ॥३॥
transliteration: pārtho'pi viśrutaṁ loke gāṇḍīvaṁ paramāyudham ,vikṛṣya cikṣepa bahūnnārācānmarmabhedinaḥ.
english: Pārtha (Arjuna) also, drawing the Gāṇḍīva, his supreme weapon renowned throughout the world, discharged many arrows that pierced vital spots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 3. Pārtha (Arjuna) also, drawing the Gāṇḍīva, his supreme weapon renowned throughout the world, discharged many arrows that pierced vital spots.

sanskrit: तानप्राप्ताञ्शितैर्बाणैर्नाराचान्रक्तभोजनान् ।कृपश्चिच्छेद पार्थस्य शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥४॥
transliteration: tānaprāptāñśitairbāṇairnārācānraktabhojanān ,kṛpaściccheda pārthasya śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Kṛpa, with his sharp arrows, cut down Pārtha's arrows, which were designed to drink blood and had not yet reached their mark, into hundreds and thousands of pieces.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 4. Kṛpa, with his sharp arrows, cut down Pārtha's arrows, which were designed to drink blood and had not yet reached their mark, into hundreds and thousands of pieces.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थश्च संक्रुद्धश्चित्रान्मार्गान्प्रदर्शयन् ।दिशः संछादयन्बाणैः प्रदिशश्च महारथः ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthaśca saṁkruddhaścitrānmārgānpradarśayan ,diśaḥ saṁchādayanbāṇaiḥ pradiśaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, the great warrior (mahāratha) Arjuna, greatly enraged, displayed diverse methods of attack, completely covering the cardinal and intermediate directions with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 5. Then, the great warrior (mahāratha) Arjuna, greatly enraged, displayed diverse methods of attack, completely covering the cardinal and intermediate directions with his arrows.

sanskrit: एकच्छायमिवाकाशं प्रकुर्वन्सर्वतः प्रभुः ।प्रच्छादयदमेयात्मा पार्थः शरशतैः कृपम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ekacchāyamivākāśaṁ prakurvansarvataḥ prabhuḥ ,pracchādayadameyātmā pārthaḥ śaraśataiḥ kṛpam.
english: From all sides, the mighty Arjuna (prabhu), whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable (ameyātmā), covered Kṛpa with hundreds of arrows, as if making the sky uniformly shaded.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 6. From all sides, the mighty Arjuna (prabhu), whose spirit (ātman) was immeasurable (ameyātmā), covered Kṛpa with hundreds of arrows, as if making the sky uniformly shaded.

sanskrit: स शरैरर्पितः क्रुद्धः शितैरग्निशिखोपमैः ।तूर्णं शरसहस्रेण पार्थमप्रतिमौजसम् ।अर्पयित्वा महात्मानं ननाद समरे कृपः ॥७॥
transliteration: sa śarairarpitaḥ kruddhaḥ śitairagniśikhopamaiḥ ,tūrṇaṁ śarasahasreṇa pārthamapratimaujasam ,arpayitvā mahātmānaṁ nanāda samare kṛpaḥ.
english: Enraged by Arjuna's sharp arrows, which were like tongues of fire, Kṛpa, the great-souled (mahātman) warrior, swiftly assailed Arjuna, who possessed unequalled might, with a thousand arrows in battle, and then roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 7. Enraged by Arjuna's sharp arrows, which were like tongues of fire, Kṛpa, the great-souled (mahātman) warrior, swiftly assailed Arjuna, who possessed unequalled might, with a thousand arrows in battle, and then roared.

sanskrit: ततः कनकपुङ्खाग्रैर्वीरः संनतपर्वभिः ।त्वरन्गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैरर्जुनस्तस्य वाजिनः ।चतुर्भिश्चतुरस्तीक्ष्णैरविध्यत्परमेषुभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kanakapuṅkhāgrairvīraḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,tvarangāṇḍīvanirmuktairarjunastasya vājinaḥ ,caturbhiścaturastīkṣṇairavidhyatparameṣubhiḥ.
english: Then, the hero Arjuna, acting swiftly, pierced Kṛpa's four horses with four sharp, excellent arrows. These arrows, released from his gāṇḍīva bow, had golden shafts and perfectly joined nodes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 8. Then, the hero Arjuna, acting swiftly, pierced Kṛpa's four horses with four sharp, excellent arrows. These arrows, released from his gāṇḍīva bow, had golden shafts and perfectly joined nodes.

sanskrit: ते हया निशितैर्विद्धा ज्वलद्भिरिव पन्नगैः ।उत्पेतुः सहसा सर्वे कृपः स्थानादथाच्यवत् ॥९॥
transliteration: te hayā niśitairviddhā jvaladbhiriva pannagaiḥ ,utpetuḥ sahasā sarve kṛpaḥ sthānādathācyavat.
english: Those horses, struck by sharp (arrows) like blazing snakes, suddenly all sprang up. Then Kripa was dislodged from his position.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 9. Those horses, struck by sharp (arrows) like blazing snakes, suddenly all sprang up. Then Kripa was dislodged from his position.

sanskrit: च्युतं तु गौतमं स्थानात्समीक्ष्य कुरुनन्दनः ।नाविध्यत्परवीरघ्नो रक्षमाणोऽस्य गौरवम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: cyutaṁ tu gautamaṁ sthānātsamīkṣya kurunandanaḥ ,nāvidhyatparavīraghno rakṣamāṇo'sya gauravam.
english: But seeing Gautama (Kripa) dislodged from his position, Arjuna, the delight of the Kurus and slayer of enemy heroes, did not strike him, out of respect for his honor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 10. But seeing Gautama (Kripa) dislodged from his position, Arjuna, the delight of the Kurus and slayer of enemy heroes, did not strike him, out of respect for his honor.

sanskrit: स तु लब्ध्वा पुनः स्थानं गौतमः सव्यसाचिनम् ।विव्याध दशभिर्बाणैस्त्वरितः कङ्कपत्रिभिः ॥११॥
transliteration: sa tu labdhvā punaḥ sthānaṁ gautamaḥ savyasācinam ,vivyādha daśabhirbāṇaistvaritaḥ kaṅkapatribhiḥ.
english: But Gautama (Kripa), having quickly regained his position, then struck Savyasachin (Arjuna) with ten arrows, feathered with kanka.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 11. But Gautama (Kripa), having quickly regained his position, then struck Savyasachin (Arjuna) with ten arrows, feathered with kanka.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थो धनुस्तस्य भल्लेन निशितेन च ।चिच्छेदैकेन भूयश्च हस्ताच्चापमथाहरत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārtho dhanustasya bhallena niśitena ca ,cicchedaikena bhūyaśca hastāccāpamathāharat.
english: Then Partha (Arjuna) severed Kripa's bow with a single sharp bhalla arrow, and moreover, he snatched the bow from his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 12. Then Partha (Arjuna) severed Kripa's bow with a single sharp bhalla arrow, and moreover, he snatched the bow from his hand.

sanskrit: अथास्य कवचं बाणैर्निशितैर्मर्मभेदिभिः ।व्यधमन्न च पार्थोऽस्य शरीरमवपीडयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: athāsya kavacaṁ bāṇairniśitairmarmabhedibhiḥ ,vyadhamanna ca pārtho'sya śarīramavapīḍayat.
english: Then, his armor was pierced by sharp, vital-spot-piercing arrows. Yet, Arjuna (pārtha) was unable to inflict harm upon his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 13. Then, his armor was pierced by sharp, vital-spot-piercing arrows. Yet, Arjuna (pārtha) was unable to inflict harm upon his body.

sanskrit: तस्य निर्मुच्यमानस्य कवचात्काय आबभौ ।समये मुच्यमानस्य सर्पस्येव तनुर्यथा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasya nirmucyamānasya kavacātkāya ābabhau ,samaye mucyamānasya sarpasyeva tanuryathā.
english: As his armor was being shed, his body shone forth, just like the skin of a serpent (sarpa) being shed in due season.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 14. As his armor was being shed, his body shone forth, just like the skin of a serpent (sarpa) being shed in due season.

sanskrit: छिन्ने धनुषि पार्थेन सोऽन्यदादाय कार्मुकम् ।चकार गौतमः सज्यं तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: chinne dhanuṣi pārthena so'nyadādāya kārmukam ,cakāra gautamaḥ sajyaṁ tadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: When his bow was broken by Arjuna (pārtha), he (Gautama) took another bow and strung it. That event seemed astonishing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 15. When his bow was broken by Arjuna (pārtha), he (Gautama) took another bow and strung it. That event seemed astonishing.

sanskrit: स तदप्यस्य कौन्तेयश्चिच्छेद नतपर्वणा ।एवमन्यानि चापानि बहूनि कृतहस्तवत् ।शारद्वतस्य चिच्छेद पाण्डवः परवीरहा ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa tadapyasya kaunteyaściccheda nataparvaṇā ,evamanyāni cāpāni bahūni kṛtahastavat ,śāradvatasya ciccheda pāṇḍavaḥ paravīrahā.
english: Even that bow of his (Gautama's) was broken by Arjuna (Kaunteya) with bent-jointed arrows. In this way, Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), the destroyer of hostile heroes (paravīrahā), like an expert (kṛtahastavat), broke many other bows of Śāradvata (Kripa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 16. Even that bow of his (Gautama's) was broken by Arjuna (Kaunteya) with bent-jointed arrows. In this way, Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), the destroyer of hostile heroes (paravīrahā), like an expert (kṛtahastavat), broke many other bows of Śāradvata (Kripa).

sanskrit: स छिन्नधनुरादाय अथ शक्तिं प्रतापवान् ।प्राहिणोत्पाण्डुपुत्राय प्रदीप्तामशनीमिव ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa chinnadhanurādāya atha śaktiṁ pratāpavān ,prāhiṇotpāṇḍuputrāya pradīptāmaśanīmiva.
english: Then, that mighty warrior (Kripa), whose bow was broken, took up a spear (śakti) and hurled it, blazing like a thunderbolt, at the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 17. Then, that mighty warrior (Kripa), whose bow was broken, took up a spear (śakti) and hurled it, blazing like a thunderbolt, at the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तामर्जुनस्तदायान्तीं शक्तिं हेमविभूषिताम् ।वियद्गतां महोल्काभां चिच्छेद दशभिः शरैः ।सापतद्दशधा छिन्ना भूमौ पार्थेन धीमता ॥१८॥
transliteration: tāmarjunastadāyāntīṁ śaktiṁ hemavibhūṣitām ,viyadgatāṁ maholkābhāṁ ciccheda daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ ,sāpataddaśadhā chinnā bhūmau pārthena dhīmatā.
english: Then Arjuna cut that approaching spear (śakti), which was adorned with gold, moving through the sky, and appearing like a great meteor, into ten pieces with ten arrows. Severed into ten parts, it fell to the ground, struck down by the intelligent Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 18. Then Arjuna cut that approaching spear (śakti), which was adorned with gold, moving through the sky, and appearing like a great meteor, into ten pieces with ten arrows. Severed into ten parts, it fell to the ground, struck down by the intelligent Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: युगमध्ये तु भल्लैस्तु ततः स सधनुः कृपः ।तमाशु निशितैः पार्थं बिभेद दशभिः शरैः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yugamadhye tu bhallaistu tataḥ sa sadhanuḥ kṛpaḥ ,tamāśu niśitaiḥ pārthaṁ bibheda daśabhiḥ śaraiḥ.
english: Then, Kripa, with his bow, quickly pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) in the center of the chariot with ten sharp, broad-headed arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 19. Then, Kripa, with his bow, quickly pierced Pārtha (Arjuna) in the center of the chariot with ten sharp, broad-headed arrows.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थो महातेजा विशिखानग्नितेजसः ।चिक्षेप समरे क्रुद्धस्त्रयोदश शिलाशितान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārtho mahātejā viśikhānagnitejasaḥ ,cikṣepa samare kruddhastrayodaśa śilāśitān.
english: Then, the greatly effulgent Pārtha (Arjuna), enraged in battle, hurled thirteen fiery arrows, sharpened on stone.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 20. Then, the greatly effulgent Pārtha (Arjuna), enraged in battle, hurled thirteen fiery arrows, sharpened on stone.

sanskrit: अथास्य युगमेकेन चतुर्भिश्चतुरो हयान् ।षष्ठेन च शिरः कायाच्छरेण रथसारथेः ॥२१॥
transliteration: athāsya yugamekena caturbhiścaturo hayān ,ṣaṣṭhena ca śiraḥ kāyācchareṇa rathasāratheḥ.
english: Then, with one arrow, he severed his opponent's yoke, and with four arrows, his four horses. With the sixth arrow, he cut off the charioteer's head from his body.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 21. Then, with one arrow, he severed his opponent's yoke, and with four arrows, his four horses. With the sixth arrow, he cut off the charioteer's head from his body.

sanskrit: त्रिभिस्त्रिवेणुं समरे द्वाभ्यामक्षौ महाबलः ।द्वादशेन तु भल्लेन चकर्तास्य ध्वजं तथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: tribhistriveṇuṁ samare dvābhyāmakṣau mahābalaḥ ,dvādaśena tu bhallena cakartāsya dhvajaṁ tathā.
english: Furthermore, the mighty one, using three arrows, severed the triple-staff (triveṇu) of the chariot in battle, and with two arrows, he cut its two axles. Then, with the twelfth *bhalla* arrow, he likewise chopped off his banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 22. Furthermore, the mighty one, using three arrows, severed the triple-staff (triveṇu) of the chariot in battle, and with two arrows, he cut its two axles. Then, with the twelfth *bhalla* arrow, he likewise chopped off his banner.

sanskrit: ततो वज्रनिकाशेन फल्गुनः प्रहसन्निव ।त्रयोदशेनेन्द्रसमः कृपं वक्षस्यताडयत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tato vajranikāśena phalgunaḥ prahasanniva ,trayodaśenendrasamaḥ kṛpaṁ vakṣasyatāḍayat.
english: Then Phalguna (Arjuna), laughing as if in sport, and equal to Indra in prowess, struck Kripa on the chest with a thirteenth arrow, which was like a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 23. Then Phalguna (Arjuna), laughing as if in sport, and equal to Indra in prowess, struck Kripa on the chest with a thirteenth arrow, which was like a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: स छिन्नधन्वा विरथो हताश्वो हतसारथिः ।गदापाणिरवप्लुत्य तूर्णं चिक्षेप तां गदाम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa chinnadhanvā viratho hatāśvo hatasārathiḥ ,gadāpāṇiravaplutya tūrṇaṁ cikṣepa tāṁ gadām.
english: With his bow severed, his chariot destroyed, his horses slain, and his charioteer killed, he (Kripa), holding a mace in his hand, quickly jumped down and hurled that mace.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 24. With his bow severed, his chariot destroyed, his horses slain, and his charioteer killed, he (Kripa), holding a mace in his hand, quickly jumped down and hurled that mace.

sanskrit: सा तु मुक्ता गदा गुर्वी कृपेण सुपरिष्कृता ।अर्जुनेन शरैर्नुन्ना प्रतिमार्गमथागमत् ॥२५॥
transliteration: sā tu muktā gadā gurvī kṛpeṇa supariṣkṛtā ,arjunena śarairnunnā pratimārgamathāgamat.
english: But that heavy, well-adorned mace, which had been released by Kṛpa, was then struck by Arjuna's arrows and returned along the path it had come.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 25. But that heavy, well-adorned mace, which had been released by Kṛpa, was then struck by Arjuna's arrows and returned along the path it had come.

sanskrit: ततो योधाः परीप्सन्तः शारद्वतममर्षणम् ।सर्वतः समरे पार्थं शरवर्षैरवाकिरन् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato yodhāḥ parīpsantaḥ śāradvatamamarṣaṇam ,sarvataḥ samare pārthaṁ śaravarṣairavākiran.
english: Then, desiring to protect Śāradvata (Kṛpa), who was furious, the warriors showered Pārtha (Arjuna) from all sides in battle with volleys of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 26. Then, desiring to protect Śāradvata (Kṛpa), who was furious, the warriors showered Pārtha (Arjuna) from all sides in battle with volleys of arrows.

sanskrit: ततो विराटस्य सुतः सव्यमावृत्य वाजिनः ।यमकं मण्डलं कृत्वा तान्योधान्प्रत्यवारयत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tato virāṭasya sutaḥ savyamāvṛtya vājinaḥ ,yamakaṁ maṇḍalaṁ kṛtvā tānyodhānpratyavārayat.
english: Then, the son of Virāṭa, turning his horses to the left, performed a 'Yamaka' circular maneuver and repelled those warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 27. Then, the son of Virāṭa, turning his horses to the left, performed a 'Yamaka' circular maneuver and repelled those warriors.

sanskrit: ततः कृपमुपादाय विरथं ते नरर्षभाः ।अपाजह्रुर्महावेगाः कुन्तीपुत्राद्धनंजयात् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛpamupādāya virathaṁ te nararṣabhāḥ ,apājahrurmahāvegāḥ kuntīputrāddhanaṁjayāt.
english: Then those swift and mighty warriors, having rescued Kṛpa, who had been deprived of his chariot, carried him away from Kuntīputra (Arjuna), also known as Dhanaṃjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 52 Verse 28. Then those swift and mighty warriors, having rescued Kṛpa, who had been deprived of his chariot, carried him away from Kuntīputra (Arjuna), also known as Dhanaṃjaya.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।यत्रैषा काञ्चनी वेदी प्रदीप्ताग्निशिखोपमा ।उच्छ्रिता काञ्चने दण्डे पताकाभिरलंकृता ।तत्र मां वह भद्रं ते द्रोणानीकाय मारिष ॥१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,yatraiṣā kāñcanī vedī pradīptāgniśikhopamā ,ucchritā kāñcane daṇḍe patākābhiralaṁkṛtā ,tatra māṁ vaha bhadraṁ te droṇānīkāya māriṣa.
english: Arjuna said: "O venerable one, carry me to Drona's army where this golden altar, resembling a blazing fire-flame, is raised on a golden staff and adorned with banners. May good fortune be with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 1. Arjuna said: "O venerable one, carry me to Drona's army where this golden altar, resembling a blazing fire-flame, is raised on a golden staff and adorned with banners. May good fortune be with you."

sanskrit: अश्वाः शोणाः प्रकाशन्ते बृहन्तश्चारुवाहिनः ।स्निग्धविद्रुमसंकाशास्ताम्रास्याः प्रियदर्शनाः ।युक्ता रथवरे यस्य सर्वशिक्षाविशारदाः ॥२॥
transliteration: aśvāḥ śoṇāḥ prakāśante bṛhantaścāruvāhinaḥ ,snigdhavidrumasaṁkāśāstāmrāsyāḥ priyadarśanāḥ ,yuktā rathavare yasya sarvaśikṣāviśāradāḥ.
english: Whose (Drona's) excellent chariot is yoked with large, red, beautifully-pulling horses that shine, are smooth like coral, have coppery mouths, are pleasant to behold, and are skilled in all kinds of training.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 2. Whose (Drona's) excellent chariot is yoked with large, red, beautifully-pulling horses that shine, are smooth like coral, have coppery mouths, are pleasant to behold, and are skilled in all kinds of training.

sanskrit: दीर्घबाहुर्महातेजा बलरूपसमन्वितः ।सर्वलोकेषु विख्यातो भारद्वाजः प्रतापवान् ॥३॥
transliteration: dīrghabāhurmahātejā balarūpasamanvitaḥ ,sarvalokeṣu vikhyāto bhāradvājaḥ pratāpavān.
english: He, the long-armed, greatly radiant, endowed with strength and beauty, is the famous Bharadvaja, majestic throughout all worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 3. He, the long-armed, greatly radiant, endowed with strength and beauty, is the famous Bharadvaja, majestic throughout all worlds.

sanskrit: बुद्ध्या तुल्यो ह्युशनसा बृहस्पतिसमो नये ।वेदास्तथैव चत्वारो ब्रह्मचर्यं तथैव च ॥४॥
transliteration: buddhyā tulyo hyuśanasā bṛhaspatisamo naye ,vedāstathaiva catvāro brahmacaryaṁ tathaiva ca.
english: Indeed, he is equal in intellect to Uśanas and like Bṛhaspati in policy. Moreover, he has mastered the four Vedas and also practiced celibacy (brahmacarya).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 4. Indeed, he is equal in intellect to Uśanas and like Bṛhaspati in policy. Moreover, he has mastered the four Vedas and also practiced celibacy (brahmacarya).

sanskrit: ससंहाराणि दिव्यानि सर्वाण्यस्त्राणि मारिष ।धनुर्वेदश्च कार्त्स्न्येन यस्मिन्नित्यं प्रतिष्ठितः ॥५॥
transliteration: sasaṁhārāṇi divyāni sarvāṇyastrāṇi māriṣa ,dhanurvedaśca kārtsnyena yasminnityaṁ pratiṣṭhitaḥ.
english: O venerable one, in him are all divine weapons along with their powers of withdrawal, and the entire science of archery is always established.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 5. O venerable one, in him are all divine weapons along with their powers of withdrawal, and the entire science of archery is always established.

sanskrit: क्षमा दमश्च सत्यं च आनृशंस्यमथार्जवम् ।एते चान्ये च बहवो गुणा यस्मिन्द्विजोत्तमे ॥६॥
transliteration: kṣamā damaśca satyaṁ ca ānṛśaṁsyamathārjavam ,ete cānye ca bahavo guṇā yasmindvijottame.
english: Forgiveness, self-control, truthfulness, compassion, and straightforwardness - these and many other qualities reside in that best of Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 6. Forgiveness, self-control, truthfulness, compassion, and straightforwardness - these and many other qualities reside in that best of Brahmins.

sanskrit: तेनाहं योद्धुमिच्छामि महाभागेन संयुगे ।तस्मात्त्वं प्रापयाचार्यं क्षिप्रमुत्तर वाहय ॥७॥
transliteration: tenāhaṁ yoddhumicchāmi mahābhāgena saṁyuge ,tasmāttvaṁ prāpayācāryaṁ kṣipramuttara vāhaya.
english: I desire to fight with that illustrious one in battle. Therefore, O Uttara, quickly take me to the preceptor! Drive [the chariot]!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 7. I desire to fight with that illustrious one in battle. Therefore, O Uttara, quickly take me to the preceptor! Drive [the chariot]!

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अर्जुनेनैवमुक्तस्तु वैराटिर्हेमभूषितान् ।चोदयामास तानश्वान्भारद्वाजरथं प्रति ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,arjunenaivamuktastu vairāṭirhemabhūṣitān ,codayāmāsa tānaśvānbhāradvājarathaṁ prati.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed by Arjuna, Uttara, the son of Virāṭa, urged those gold-adorned horses towards the chariot of Bhāradvāja.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 8. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed by Arjuna, Uttara, the son of Virāṭa, urged those gold-adorned horses towards the chariot of Bhāradvāja.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं वेगेन पाण्डवं रथिनां वरम् ।द्रोणः प्रत्युद्ययौ पार्थं मत्तो मत्तमिव द्विपम् ॥९॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ vegena pāṇḍavaṁ rathināṁ varam ,droṇaḥ pratyudyayau pārthaṁ matto mattamiva dvipam.
english: Drona advanced to meet that Pandava, the foremost of charioteers, who was swiftly approaching, just as one enraged elephant charges towards another enraged elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 9. Drona advanced to meet that Pandava, the foremost of charioteers, who was swiftly approaching, just as one enraged elephant charges towards another enraged elephant.

sanskrit: ततः प्राध्मापयच्छङ्खं भेरीशतनिनादितम् ।प्रचुक्षुभे बलं सर्वमुद्धूत इव सागरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ prādhmāpayacchaṅkhaṁ bherīśatanināditam ,pracukṣubhe balaṁ sarvamuddhūta iva sāgaraḥ.
english: Then he blew the conch, which resounded with the sound of hundreds of drums. The entire army was greatly agitated, like a stirred-up ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 10. Then he blew the conch, which resounded with the sound of hundreds of drums. The entire army was greatly agitated, like a stirred-up ocean.

sanskrit: अथ शोणान्सदश्वांस्तान्हंसवर्णैर्मनोजवैः ।मिश्रितान्समरे दृष्ट्वा व्यस्मयन्त रणे जनाः ॥११॥
transliteration: atha śoṇānsadaśvāṁstānhaṁsavarṇairmanojavaiḥ ,miśritānsamare dṛṣṭvā vyasmayanta raṇe janāḥ.
english: Then, seeing those reddish-brown excellent horses mingled with the mind-swift, swan-colored ones on the battlefield, people were astonished in the conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 11. Then, seeing those reddish-brown excellent horses mingled with the mind-swift, swan-colored ones on the battlefield, people were astonished in the conflict.

sanskrit: तौ रथौ वीर्यसंपन्नौ दृष्ट्वा संग्राममूर्धनि ।आचार्यशिष्यावजितौ कृतविद्यौ मनस्विनौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: tau rathau vīryasaṁpannau dṛṣṭvā saṁgrāmamūrdhani ,ācāryaśiṣyāvajitau kṛtavidyau manasvinau.
english: Having seen those two chariots, both endowed with valor, at the forefront of battle, (belonging to) the unconquered teacher and disciple, who were accomplished in knowledge and high-minded.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 12. Having seen those two chariots, both endowed with valor, at the forefront of battle, (belonging to) the unconquered teacher and disciple, who were accomplished in knowledge and high-minded.

sanskrit: समाश्लिष्टौ तदान्योन्यं द्रोणपार्थौ महाबलौ ।दृष्ट्वा प्राकम्पत मुहुर्भरतानां महद्बलम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: samāśliṣṭau tadānyonyaṁ droṇapārthau mahābalau ,dṛṣṭvā prākampata muhurbharatānāṁ mahadbalam.
english: Then, seeing the mighty Droṇa and Arjuna (Pārtha) embracing each other, the great army (mahā-balam) of the Bhāratas trembled repeatedly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 13. Then, seeing the mighty Droṇa and Arjuna (Pārtha) embracing each other, the great army (mahā-balam) of the Bhāratas trembled repeatedly.

sanskrit: हर्षयुक्तस्तथा पार्थः प्रहसन्निव वीर्यवान् ।रथं रथेन द्रोणस्य समासाद्य महारथः ॥१४॥
transliteration: harṣayuktastathā pārthaḥ prahasanniva vīryavān ,rathaṁ rathena droṇasya samāsādya mahārathaḥ.
english: The valiant Arjuna (Pārtha), filled with joy and as if laughing, then, being a great warrior (mahāratha), approached Droṇa's chariot with his own chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 14. The valiant Arjuna (Pārtha), filled with joy and as if laughing, then, being a great warrior (mahāratha), approached Droṇa's chariot with his own chariot.

sanskrit: अभिवाद्य महाबाहुः सान्त्वपूर्वमिदं वचः ।उवाच श्लक्ष्णया वाचा कौन्तेयः परवीरहा ॥१५॥
transliteration: abhivādya mahābāhuḥ sāntvapūrvamidaṁ vacaḥ ,uvāca ślakṣṇayā vācā kaunteyaḥ paravīrahā.
english: Having paid his respects, the mighty-armed Arjuna (Kaunteya), the slayer of hostile heroes, spoke these conciliatory words with a gentle voice.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 15. Having paid his respects, the mighty-armed Arjuna (Kaunteya), the slayer of hostile heroes, spoke these conciliatory words with a gentle voice.

sanskrit: उषिताः स्म वने वासं प्रतिकर्म चिकीर्षवः ।कोपं नार्हसि नः कर्तुं सदा समरदुर्जय ॥१६॥
transliteration: uṣitāḥ sma vane vāsaṁ pratikarma cikīrṣavaḥ ,kopaṁ nārhasi naḥ kartuṁ sadā samaradurjaya.
english: We have fulfilled our residence in the forest, desiring to take revenge. You, who are ever unconquerable in battle, ought not to direct your anger towards us.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 16. We have fulfilled our residence in the forest, desiring to take revenge. You, who are ever unconquerable in battle, ought not to direct your anger towards us.

sanskrit: अहं तु प्रहृते पूर्वं प्रहरिष्यामि तेऽनघ ।इति मे वर्तते बुद्धिस्तद्भवान्कर्तुमर्हति ॥१७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tu prahṛte pūrvaṁ prahariṣyāmi te'nagha ,iti me vartate buddhistadbhavānkartumarhati.
english: O sinless one, if I am struck first, I will certainly retaliate against you. This is my firm resolve, and you should act upon it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 17. O sinless one, if I am struck first, I will certainly retaliate against you. This is my firm resolve, and you should act upon it.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्मै प्राहिणोद्द्रोणः शरानधिकविंशतिम् ।अप्राप्तांश्चैव तान्पार्थश्चिच्छेद कृतहस्तवत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato'smai prāhiṇoddroṇaḥ śarānadhikaviṁśatim ,aprāptāṁścaiva tānpārthaściccheda kṛtahastavat.
english: Thereupon, Drona discharged more than twenty arrows at him. But Arjuna, with the skill of a practiced hand, intercepted and cut them all down before they could reach their target.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 18. Thereupon, Drona discharged more than twenty arrows at him. But Arjuna, with the skill of a practiced hand, intercepted and cut them all down before they could reach their target.

sanskrit: ततः शरसहस्रेण रथं पार्थस्य वीर्यवान् ।अवाकिरत्ततो द्रोणः शीघ्रमस्त्रं विदर्शयन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ śarasahasreṇa rathaṁ pārthasya vīryavān ,avākirattato droṇaḥ śīghramastraṁ vidarśayan.
english: Then, the powerful Drona, swiftly demonstrating his skill in weaponry, showered Arjuna's chariot with a thousand arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 19. Then, the powerful Drona, swiftly demonstrating his skill in weaponry, showered Arjuna's chariot with a thousand arrows.

sanskrit: एवं प्रववृते युद्धं भारद्वाजकिरीटिनोः ।समं विमुञ्चतोः संख्ये विशिखान्दीप्ततेजसः ॥२०॥
transliteration: evaṁ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ bhāradvājakirīṭinoḥ ,samaṁ vimuñcatoḥ saṁkhye viśikhāndīptatejasaḥ.
english: Thus began the battle between Drona (the son of Bharadvāja) and Arjuna (the crowned one), as both, with equal prowess, unleashed arrows of blazing splendor in the combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 20. Thus began the battle between Drona (the son of Bharadvāja) and Arjuna (the crowned one), as both, with equal prowess, unleashed arrows of blazing splendor in the combat.

sanskrit: तावुभौ ख्यातकर्माणावुभौ वायुसमौ जवे ।उभौ दिव्यास्त्रविदुषावुभावुत्तमतेजसौ ।क्षिपन्तौ शरजालानि मोहयामासतुर्नृपान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tāvubhau khyātakarmāṇāvubhau vāyusamau jave ,ubhau divyāstraviduṣāvubhāvuttamatejasau ,kṣipantau śarajālāni mohayāmāsaturnṛpān.
english: Both of them, renowned for their deeds, were equal to the wind in speed. Both were experts in divine weapons and possessed excellent prowess. As they hurled showers of arrows, they bewildered the kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 21. Both of them, renowned for their deeds, were equal to the wind in speed. Both were experts in divine weapons and possessed excellent prowess. As they hurled showers of arrows, they bewildered the kings.

sanskrit: व्यस्मयन्त ततो योधाः सर्वे तत्र समागताः ।शरान्विसृजतोस्तूर्णं साधु साध्विति पूजयन् ॥२२॥
transliteration: vyasmayanta tato yodhāḥ sarve tatra samāgatāḥ ,śarānvisṛjatostūrṇaṁ sādhu sādhviti pūjayan.
english: All the warriors assembled there were then amazed. They praised the two, saying "Excellent! Excellent!" as they quickly released arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 22. All the warriors assembled there were then amazed. They praised the two, saying "Excellent! Excellent!" as they quickly released arrows.

sanskrit: द्रोणं हि समरे कोऽन्यो योद्धुमर्हति फल्गुनात् ।रौद्रः क्षत्रियधर्मोऽयं गुरुणा यदयुध्यत ।इत्यब्रुवञ्जनास्तत्र संग्रामशिरसि स्थिताः ॥२३॥
transliteration: droṇaṁ hi samare ko'nyo yoddhumarhati phalgunāt ,raudraḥ kṣatriyadharmo'yaṁ guruṇā yadayudhyata ,ityabruvañjanāstatra saṁgrāmaśirasi sthitāḥ.
english: "Indeed, who else in battle is capable of fighting Drona apart from Arjuna? This is a fierce duty (dharma) for a warrior (kṣatriya), that one should fight one's preceptor (guru)." Thus spoke the people stationed there at the forefront of the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 23. "Indeed, who else in battle is capable of fighting Drona apart from Arjuna? This is a fierce duty (dharma) for a warrior (kṣatriya), that one should fight one's preceptor (guru)." Thus spoke the people stationed there at the forefront of the battle.

sanskrit: वीरौ तावपि संरब्धौ संनिकृष्टौ महारथौ ।छादयेतां शरव्रातैरन्योन्यमपराजितौ ॥२४॥
transliteration: vīrau tāvapi saṁrabdhau saṁnikṛṣṭau mahārathau ,chādayetāṁ śaravrātairanyonyamaparājitau.
english: Even those two enraged heroes, those great charioteers who had approached each other, covered one another with multitudes of arrows, both remaining undefeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 24. Even those two enraged heroes, those great charioteers who had approached each other, covered one another with multitudes of arrows, both remaining undefeated.

sanskrit: विस्फार्य सुमहच्चापं हेमपृष्ठं दुरासदम् ।संरब्धोऽथ भरद्वाजः फल्गुनं प्रत्ययुध्यत ॥२५॥
transliteration: visphārya sumahaccāpaṁ hemapṛṣṭhaṁ durāsadam ,saṁrabdho'tha bharadvājaḥ phalgunaṁ pratyayudhyata.
english: Then, Bharadvaja, enraged, stretched his very great, gold-backed, formidable bow and fought against Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 25. Then, Bharadvaja, enraged, stretched his very great, gold-backed, formidable bow and fought against Arjuna.

sanskrit: स सायकमयैर्जालैरर्जुनस्य रथं प्रति ।भानुमद्भिः शिलाधौतैर्भानोः प्रच्छादयत्प्रभाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa sāyakamayairjālairarjunasya rathaṁ prati ,bhānumadbhiḥ śilādhautairbhānoḥ pracchādayatprabhām.
english: He obscured the splendor of the sun with radiant, stone-polished nets of arrows directed at Arjuna's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 26. He obscured the splendor of the sun with radiant, stone-polished nets of arrows directed at Arjuna's chariot.

sanskrit: पार्थं च स महाबाहुर्महावेगैर्महारथः ।विव्याध निशितैर्बाणैर्मेघो वृष्ट्येव पर्वतम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: pārthaṁ ca sa mahābāhurmahāvegairmahārathaḥ ,vivyādha niśitairbāṇairmegho vṛṣṭyeva parvatam.
english: And that mighty-armed, great warrior, with sharp arrows and great force, pierced Arjuna, just as a cloud showers a mountain with rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 27. And that mighty-armed, great warrior, with sharp arrows and great force, pierced Arjuna, just as a cloud showers a mountain with rain.

sanskrit: तथैव दिव्यं गाण्डीवं धनुरादाय पाण्डवः ।शत्रुघ्नं वेगवद्धृष्टो भारसाधनमुत्तमम् ।विससर्ज शरांश्चित्रान्सुवर्णविकृतान्बहून् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tathaiva divyaṁ gāṇḍīvaṁ dhanurādāya pāṇḍavaḥ ,śatrughnaṁ vegavaddhṛṣṭo bhārasādhanamuttamam ,visasarja śarāṁścitrānsuvarṇavikṛtānbahūn.
english: In the same way, the emboldened son of Pandu (Arjuna), taking up his divine Gandiva bow - which was foe-slaying, swift, excellent, and capable of accomplishing great tasks - discharged many variegated arrows adorned with gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 28. In the same way, the emboldened son of Pandu (Arjuna), taking up his divine Gandiva bow - which was foe-slaying, swift, excellent, and capable of accomplishing great tasks - discharged many variegated arrows adorned with gold.

sanskrit: नाशयञ्शरवर्षाणि भारद्वाजस्य वीर्यवान् ।तूर्णं चापविनिर्मुक्तैस्तदद्भुतमिवाभवत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: nāśayañśaravarṣāṇi bhāradvājasya vīryavān ,tūrṇaṁ cāpavinirmuktaistadadbhutamivābhavat.
english: The valorous one (Arjuna), swiftly destroying Dronācārya's showers of arrows with those released from his own bow, it was truly astonishing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 29. The valorous one (Arjuna), swiftly destroying Dronācārya's showers of arrows with those released from his own bow, it was truly astonishing.

sanskrit: स रथेन चरन्पार्थः प्रेक्षणीयो धनंजयः ।युगपद्दिक्षु सर्वासु सर्वशस्त्राण्यदर्शयत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa rathena caranpārthaḥ prekṣaṇīyo dhanaṁjayaḥ ,yugapaddikṣu sarvāsu sarvaśastrāṇyadarśayat.
english: That admirable Pārtha (Arjuna), Dhanañjaya, moving in his chariot, simultaneously displayed all types of weapons in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 30. That admirable Pārtha (Arjuna), Dhanañjaya, moving in his chariot, simultaneously displayed all types of weapons in all directions.

sanskrit: एकच्छायमिवाकाशं बाणैश्चक्रे समन्ततः ।नादृश्यत तदा द्रोणो नीहारेणेव संवृतः ॥३१॥
transliteration: ekacchāyamivākāśaṁ bāṇaiścakre samantataḥ ,nādṛśyata tadā droṇo nīhāreṇeva saṁvṛtaḥ.
english: With arrows, he made the entire sky uniformly covered, as if it had a single shade. Then, Drona was no longer visible, as if enveloped by a dense fog.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 31. With arrows, he made the entire sky uniformly covered, as if it had a single shade. Then, Drona was no longer visible, as if enveloped by a dense fog.

sanskrit: तस्याभवत्तदा रूपं संवृतस्य शरोत्तमैः ।जाज्वल्यमानस्य यथा पर्वतस्येव सर्वतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tasyābhavattadā rūpaṁ saṁvṛtasya śarottamaiḥ ,jājvalyamānasya yathā parvatasyeva sarvataḥ.
english: Then, Drona's form, (though) covered by excellent arrows, became radiant, just like a blazing mountain (shining) on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 32. Then, Drona's form, (though) covered by excellent arrows, became radiant, just like a blazing mountain (shining) on all sides.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु पार्थस्य रणे शरैः स्वरथमावृतम् ।स विस्फार्य धनुश्चित्रं मेघस्तनितनिस्वनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu pārthasya raṇe śaraiḥ svarathamāvṛtam ,sa visphārya dhanuścitraṁ meghastanitanisvanam.
english: Having seen Pārtha's chariot covered with arrows in battle, he (Droṇa) then stretched his wondrous bow, which resonated with a thunderous roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 33. Having seen Pārtha's chariot covered with arrows in battle, he (Droṇa) then stretched his wondrous bow, which resonated with a thunderous roar.

sanskrit: अग्निचक्रोपमं घोरं विकर्षन्परमायुधम् ।व्यशातयच्छरांस्तांस्तु द्रोणः समितिशोभनः ।महानभूत्ततः शब्दो वंशानामिव दह्यताम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: agnicakropamaṁ ghoraṁ vikarṣanparamāyudham ,vyaśātayaccharāṁstāṁstu droṇaḥ samitiśobhanaḥ ,mahānabhūttataḥ śabdo vaṁśānāmiva dahyatām.
english: Drawing back that terrible, supreme weapon, which resembled a wheel of fire, Droṇa, who shone gloriously in battle, shattered those arrows. Then, a great sound arose, like that of burning bamboos.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 34. Drawing back that terrible, supreme weapon, which resembled a wheel of fire, Droṇa, who shone gloriously in battle, shattered those arrows. Then, a great sound arose, like that of burning bamboos.

sanskrit: जाम्बूनदमयैः पुङ्खैश्चित्रचापवरातिगैः ।प्राच्छादयदमेयात्मा दिशः सूर्यस्य च प्रभाम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: jāmbūnadamayaiḥ puṅkhaiścitracāpavarātigaiḥ ,prācchādayadameyātmā diśaḥ sūryasya ca prabhām.
english: That one of boundless spirit (ātman) then covered the directions and the sun's splendor with golden-shafted arrows that flew exceptionally far from his wondrous, excellent bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 35. That one of boundless spirit (ātman) then covered the directions and the sun's splendor with golden-shafted arrows that flew exceptionally far from his wondrous, excellent bow.

sanskrit: ततः कनकपुङ्खानां शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ।वियच्चराणां वियति दृश्यन्ते बहुशः प्रजाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kanakapuṅkhānāṁ śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām ,viyaccarāṇāṁ viyati dṛśyante bahuśaḥ prajāḥ.
english: Then, in the sky, countless multitudes of golden-shafted arrows, with well-notched ends and traversing the atmosphere, were seen.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 36. Then, in the sky, countless multitudes of golden-shafted arrows, with well-notched ends and traversing the atmosphere, were seen.

sanskrit: द्रोणस्य पुङ्खसक्ताश्च प्रभवन्तः शरासनात् ।एको दीर्घ इवादृश्यदाकाशे संहतः शरः ॥३७॥
transliteration: droṇasya puṅkhasaktāśca prabhavantaḥ śarāsanāt ,eko dīrgha ivādṛśyadākāśe saṁhataḥ śaraḥ.
english: Drona's arrows, which were fixed to the string by their feathered ends and issued from his bow, appeared in the sky as if they were a single, long, dense arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 37. Drona's arrows, which were fixed to the string by their feathered ends and issued from his bow, appeared in the sky as if they were a single, long, dense arrow.

sanskrit: एवं तौ स्वर्णविकृतान्विमुञ्चन्तौ महाशरान् ।आकाशं संवृतं वीरावुल्काभिरिव चक्रतुः ॥३८॥
transliteration: evaṁ tau svarṇavikṛtānvimuñcantau mahāśarān ,ākāśaṁ saṁvṛtaṁ vīrāvulkābhiriva cakratuḥ.
english: Thus, those two heroes, releasing great arrows adorned with gold, made the sky covered as if with meteors.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 38. Thus, those two heroes, releasing great arrows adorned with gold, made the sky covered as if with meteors.

sanskrit: शरास्तयोश्च विबभुः कङ्कबर्हिणवाससः ।पङ्क्त्यः शरदि खस्थानां हंसानां चरतामिव ॥३९॥
transliteration: śarāstayośca vibabhuḥ kaṅkabarhiṇavāsasaḥ ,paṅktyaḥ śaradi khasthānāṁ haṁsānāṁ caratāmiva.
english: And the arrows of those two, which had vulture and peacock feathers, shone like lines of swans flying in the autumn sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 39. And the arrows of those two, which had vulture and peacock feathers, shone like lines of swans flying in the autumn sky.

sanskrit: युद्धं समभवत्तत्र सुसंरब्धं महात्मनोः ।द्रोणपाण्डवयोर्घोरं वृत्रवासवयोरिव ॥४०॥
transliteration: yuddhaṁ samabhavattatra susaṁrabdhaṁ mahātmanoḥ ,droṇapāṇḍavayorghoraṁ vṛtravāsavayoriva.
english: There, a fierce and terrible battle erupted between the two great souls, Drona and the Pandava (Arjuna), just like the one between Vritra and Vasava (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 40. There, a fierce and terrible battle erupted between the two great souls, Drona and the Pandava (Arjuna), just like the one between Vritra and Vasava (Indra).

sanskrit: तौ गजाविव चासाद्य विषाणाग्रैः परस्परम् ।शरैः पूर्णायतोत्सृष्टैरन्योन्यमभिजघ्नतुः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tau gajāviva cāsādya viṣāṇāgraiḥ parasparam ,śaraiḥ pūrṇāyatotsṛṣṭairanyonyamabhijaghnatuḥ.
english: They attacked each other, like two elephants striking with the tips of their tusks, and assailed each other with arrows shot after being drawn to their full extent.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 41. They attacked each other, like two elephants striking with the tips of their tusks, and assailed each other with arrows shot after being drawn to their full extent.

sanskrit: तौ व्यवाहरतां शूरौ संरब्धौ रणशोभिनौ ।उदीरयन्तौ समरे दिव्यान्यस्त्राणि भागशः ॥४२॥
transliteration: tau vyavāharatāṁ śūrau saṁrabdhau raṇaśobhinau ,udīrayantau samare divyānyastrāṇi bhāgaśaḥ.
english: Those two heroes, enraged and glorious in battle, fought, releasing divine weapons one after another in the engagement.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 42. Those two heroes, enraged and glorious in battle, fought, releasing divine weapons one after another in the engagement.

sanskrit: अथ त्वाचार्यमुख्येन शरान्सृष्टाञ्शिलाशितान् ।न्यवारयच्छितैर्बाणैरर्जुनो जयतां वरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: atha tvācāryamukhyena śarānsṛṣṭāñśilāśitān ,nyavārayacchitairbāṇairarjuno jayatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna, the foremost among victors, repelled those stone-sharpened arrows, which had been discharged by the chief preceptor (guru), using his own sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 43. Then, Arjuna, the foremost among victors, repelled those stone-sharpened arrows, which had been discharged by the chief preceptor (guru), using his own sharp arrows.

sanskrit: दर्शयन्नैन्द्रिरात्मानमुग्रमुग्रपराक्रमः ।इषुभिस्तूर्णमाकाशं बहुभिश्च समावृणोत् ॥४४॥
transliteration: darśayannaindrirātmānamugramugraparākramaḥ ,iṣubhistūrṇamākāśaṁ bahubhiśca samāvṛṇot.
english: Displaying his formidable self (ātman) and fierce valor, Arjuna, the son of Indra, quickly covered the sky with many arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 44. Displaying his formidable self (ātman) and fierce valor, Arjuna, the son of Indra, quickly covered the sky with many arrows.

sanskrit: जिघांसन्तं नरव्याघ्रमर्जुनं तिग्मतेजसम् ।आचार्यमुख्यः समरे द्रोणः शस्त्रभृतां वरः ।अर्जुनेन सहाक्रीडच्छरैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥४५॥
transliteration: jighāṁsantaṁ naravyāghramarjunaṁ tigmatejasam ,ācāryamukhyaḥ samare droṇaḥ śastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,arjunena sahākrīḍaccharaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: Drona, the pre-eminent guru (ācārya) and the foremost among weapon-wielders, engaged in battle with Arjuna, that tiger among men, who was fiercely energetic and eager to strike him. Drona, in turn, fought with Arjuna using arrows with well-formed shafts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 45. Drona, the pre-eminent guru (ācārya) and the foremost among weapon-wielders, engaged in battle with Arjuna, that tiger among men, who was fiercely energetic and eager to strike him. Drona, in turn, fought with Arjuna using arrows with well-formed shafts.

sanskrit: दिव्यान्यस्त्राणि मुञ्चन्तं भारद्वाजं महारणे ।अस्त्रैरस्त्राणि संवार्य फल्गुनः समयोधयत् ॥४६॥
transliteration: divyānyastrāṇi muñcantaṁ bhāradvājaṁ mahāraṇe ,astrairastrāṇi saṁvārya phalgunaḥ samayodhayat.
english: In the great battle, Arjuna (phalguna), having repelled the divine weapons that Drona (bhāradvāja) was continuously discharging, then fought against him with his own weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 46. In the great battle, Arjuna (phalguna), having repelled the divine weapons that Drona (bhāradvāja) was continuously discharging, then fought against him with his own weapons.

sanskrit: तयोरासीत्संप्रहारः क्रुद्धयोर्नरसिंहयोः ।अमर्षिणोस्तदान्योन्यं देवदानवयोरिव ॥४७॥
transliteration: tayorāsītsaṁprahāraḥ kruddhayornarasiṁhayoḥ ,amarṣiṇostadānyonyaṁ devadānavayoriva.
english: Then, a fierce and mutual combat took place between those two enraged and intolerant lions among men, just like the battle between the gods and the dānavas (demons).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 47. Then, a fierce and mutual combat took place between those two enraged and intolerant lions among men, just like the battle between the gods and the dānavas (demons).

sanskrit: ऐन्द्रं वायव्यमाग्नेयमस्त्रमस्त्रेण पाण्डवः ।द्रोणेन मुक्तं मुक्तं तु ग्रसते स्म पुनः पुनः ॥४८॥
transliteration: aindraṁ vāyavyamāgneyamastramastreṇa pāṇḍavaḥ ,droṇena muktaṁ muktaṁ tu grasate sma punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Moreover, Arjuna (pāṇḍava), using his own weapons, repeatedly neutralized (literally, 'swallowed') each and every weapon, be it aindra (Indra's weapon), vāyavya (Vāyu's weapon), or āgneya (Agni's weapon), that was released by Drona.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 48. Moreover, Arjuna (pāṇḍava), using his own weapons, repeatedly neutralized (literally, 'swallowed') each and every weapon, be it aindra (Indra's weapon), vāyavya (Vāyu's weapon), or āgneya (Agni's weapon), that was released by Drona.

sanskrit: एवं शूरौ महेष्वासौ विसृजन्तौ शिताञ्शरान् ।एकच्छायं चक्रतुस्तावाकाशं शरवृष्टिभिः ॥४९॥
transliteration: evaṁ śūrau maheṣvāsau visṛjantau śitāñśarān ,ekacchāyaṁ cakratustāvākāśaṁ śaravṛṣṭibhiḥ.
english: Thus, those two valiant warriors, mighty archers, releasing sharp arrows, made the sky appear as a single shade with showers of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 49. Thus, those two valiant warriors, mighty archers, releasing sharp arrows, made the sky appear as a single shade with showers of arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनेन मुक्तानां पततां च शरीरिषु ।पर्वतेष्विव वज्राणां शराणां श्रूयते स्वनः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tato'rjunena muktānāṁ patatāṁ ca śarīriṣu ,parvateṣviva vajrāṇāṁ śarāṇāṁ śrūyate svanaḥ.
english: Then, a roar of arrows, released by Arjuna and falling upon the bodies of the warriors, was heard, like the sound of thunderbolts striking mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 50. Then, a roar of arrows, released by Arjuna and falling upon the bodies of the warriors, was heard, like the sound of thunderbolts striking mountains.

sanskrit: ततो नागा रथाश्चैव सादिनश्च विशां पते ।शोणिताक्ता व्यदृश्यन्त पुष्पिता इव किंशुकाः ॥५१॥
transliteration: tato nāgā rathāścaiva sādinaśca viśāṁ pate ,śoṇitāktā vyadṛśyanta puṣpitā iva kiṁśukāḥ.
english: Then, O lord of the people, elephants, chariots, and horsemen, smeared with blood, appeared like blossoming kiṃśuka trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 51. Then, O lord of the people, elephants, chariots, and horsemen, smeared with blood, appeared like blossoming kiṃśuka trees.

sanskrit: बाहुभिश्च सकेयूरैर्विचित्रैश्च महारथैः ।सुवर्णचित्रैः कवचैर्ध्वजैश्च विनिपातितैः ॥५२॥
transliteration: bāhubhiśca sakeyūrairvicitraiśca mahārathaiḥ ,suvarṇacitraiḥ kavacairdhvajaiśca vinipātitaiḥ.
english: And with arms adorned with armlets, with wonderfully equipped great chariots, with golden-adorned armors, and with fallen banners.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 52. And with arms adorned with armlets, with wonderfully equipped great chariots, with golden-adorned armors, and with fallen banners.

sanskrit: योधैश्च निहतैस्तत्र पार्थबाणप्रपीडितैः ।बलमासीत्समुद्भ्रान्तं द्रोणार्जुनसमागमे ॥५३॥
transliteration: yodhaiśca nihataistatra pārthabāṇaprapīḍitaiḥ ,balamāsītsamudbhrāntaṁ droṇārjunasamāgame.
english: There, with warriors slain and oppressed by Arjuna's arrows, the army became utterly bewildered during the encounter between Droṇa and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 53. There, with warriors slain and oppressed by Arjuna's arrows, the army became utterly bewildered during the encounter between Droṇa and Arjuna.

sanskrit: विधुन्वानौ तु तौ वीरौ धनुषी भारसाधने ।आच्छादयेतामन्योन्यं तितक्षन्तौ रणेषुभिः ॥५४॥
transliteration: vidhunvānau tu tau vīrau dhanuṣī bhārasādhane ,ācchādayetāmanyonyaṁ titakṣantau raṇeṣubhiḥ.
english: Indeed, those two heroes, wielding their bows which were instruments of great tasks, covered each other with battle-arrows, both desirous of overcoming.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 54. Indeed, those two heroes, wielding their bows which were instruments of great tasks, covered each other with battle-arrows, both desirous of overcoming.

sanskrit: अथान्तरिक्षे नादोऽभूद्द्रोणं तत्र प्रशंसताम् ।दुष्करं कृतवान्द्रोणो यदर्जुनमयोधयत् ॥५५॥
transliteration: athāntarikṣe nādo'bhūddroṇaṁ tatra praśaṁsatām ,duṣkaraṁ kṛtavāndroṇo yadarjunamayodhayat.
english: Then, a cry arose in the sky from those praising Droṇa, saying, 'Droṇa has accomplished a difficult feat by fighting Arjuna!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 55. Then, a cry arose in the sky from those praising Droṇa, saying, 'Droṇa has accomplished a difficult feat by fighting Arjuna!'

sanskrit: प्रमाथिनं महावीर्यं दृढमुष्टिं दुरासदम् ।जेतारं देवदैत्यानां सर्पाणां च महारथम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: pramāthinaṁ mahāvīryaṁ dṛḍhamuṣṭiṁ durāsadam ,jetāraṁ devadaityānāṁ sarpāṇāṁ ca mahāratham.
english: (They praised Droṇa for fighting Arjuna,) the destroyer, the greatly valorous, the firm-fisted, the unapproachable, the conqueror of gods, demons, and serpents, and the great charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 56. (They praised Droṇa for fighting Arjuna,) the destroyer, the greatly valorous, the firm-fisted, the unapproachable, the conqueror of gods, demons, and serpents, and the great charioteer.

sanskrit: अविश्रमं च शिक्षां च लाघवं दूरपातिताम् ।पार्थस्य समरे दृष्ट्वा द्रोणस्याभूच्च विस्मयः ॥५७॥
transliteration: aviśramaṁ ca śikṣāṁ ca lāghavaṁ dūrapātitām ,pārthasya samare dṛṣṭvā droṇasyābhūcca vismayaḥ.
english: Having observed Pārtha's (Arjuna's) tireless dedication, skill, swiftness, and long-range accuracy in battle, Droṇa was filled with astonishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 57. Having observed Pārtha's (Arjuna's) tireless dedication, skill, swiftness, and long-range accuracy in battle, Droṇa was filled with astonishment.

sanskrit: अथ गाण्डीवमुद्यम्य दिव्यं धनुरमर्षणः ।विचकर्ष रणे पार्थो बाहुभ्यां भरतर्षभ ॥५८॥
transliteration: atha gāṇḍīvamudyamya divyaṁ dhanuramarṣaṇaḥ ,vicakarṣa raṇe pārtho bāhubhyāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Then, O best among Bhāratas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the indignant Pārtha (Arjuna), having raised his divine bow Gāṇḍīva, drew it back with both his arms in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 58. Then, O best among Bhāratas (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), the indignant Pārtha (Arjuna), having raised his divine bow Gāṇḍīva, drew it back with both his arms in battle.

sanskrit: तस्य बाणमयं वर्षं शलभानामिवायतम् ।न च बाणान्तरे वायुरस्य शक्नोति सर्पितुम् ॥५९॥
transliteration: tasya bāṇamayaṁ varṣaṁ śalabhānāmivāyatam ,na ca bāṇāntare vāyurasya śaknoti sarpitum.
english: His (Arjuna's) shower of arrows, vast like a swarm of locusts, was such that not even the wind could penetrate the spaces between the arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 59. His (Arjuna's) shower of arrows, vast like a swarm of locusts, was such that not even the wind could penetrate the spaces between the arrows.

sanskrit: अनिशं संदधानस्य शरानुत्सृजतस्तदा ।ददृशे नान्तरं किंचित्पार्थस्याददतोऽपि च ॥६०॥
transliteration: aniśaṁ saṁdadhānasya śarānutsṛjatastadā ,dadṛśe nāntaraṁ kiṁcitpārthasyādadato'pi ca.
english: Then, as Pārtha (Arjuna) was ceaselessly fitting arrows to the bow, releasing them, and even taking up more, no interval whatsoever could be perceived.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 60. Then, as Pārtha (Arjuna) was ceaselessly fitting arrows to the bow, releasing them, and even taking up more, no interval whatsoever could be perceived.

sanskrit: तथा शीघ्रास्त्रयुद्धे तु वर्तमाने सुदारुणे ।शीघ्राच्छीघ्रतरं पार्थः शरानन्यानुदीरयत् ॥६१॥
transliteration: tathā śīghrāstrayuddhe tu vartamāne sudāruṇe ,śīghrācchīghrataraṁ pārthaḥ śarānanyānudīrayat.
english: As that extremely fierce battle involving swift missiles (śīghrāstra-yuddha) raged on, Arjuna (Pārtha) discharged other arrows, each swifter than the last.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 61. As that extremely fierce battle involving swift missiles (śīghrāstra-yuddha) raged on, Arjuna (Pārtha) discharged other arrows, each swifter than the last.

sanskrit: ततः शतसहस्राणि शराणां नतपर्वणाम् ।युगपत्प्रापतंस्तत्र द्रोणस्य रथमन्तिकात् ॥६२॥
transliteration: tataḥ śatasahasrāṇi śarāṇāṁ nataparvaṇām ,yugapatprāpataṁstatra droṇasya rathamantikāt.
english: Then, hundreds of thousands of arrows with bent shafts simultaneously rained down there near Droṇa's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 62. Then, hundreds of thousands of arrows with bent shafts simultaneously rained down there near Droṇa's chariot.

sanskrit: अवकीर्यमाणे द्रोणे तु शरैर्गाण्डीवधन्वना ।हाहाकारो महानासीत्सैन्यानां भरतर्षभ ॥६३॥
transliteration: avakīryamāṇe droṇe tu śarairgāṇḍīvadhanvanā ,hāhākāro mahānāsītsainyānāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Indeed, as Droṇa was being covered by arrows from the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Gāṇḍīvadhanvan), a great cry of distress (hāhākāra) arose among the armies, O best of the Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 63. Indeed, as Droṇa was being covered by arrows from the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Gāṇḍīvadhanvan), a great cry of distress (hāhākāra) arose among the armies, O best of the Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha).

sanskrit: पाण्डवस्य तु शीघ्रास्त्रं मघवान्समपूजयत् ।गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव ये च तत्र समागताः ॥६४॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavasya tu śīghrāstraṁ maghavānsamapūjayat ,gandharvāpsarasaścaiva ye ca tatra samāgatāḥ.
english: Indeed, Indra (Maghavan) praised the swift missile (śīghrāstra) of Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), as did the Gandharvas, Apsaras, and all others who had gathered there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 64. Indeed, Indra (Maghavan) praised the swift missile (śīghrāstra) of Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), as did the Gandharvas, Apsaras, and all others who had gathered there.

sanskrit: ततो वृन्देन महता रथानां रथयूथपः ।आचार्यपुत्रः सहसा पाण्डवं प्रत्यवारयत् ॥६५॥
transliteration: tato vṛndena mahatā rathānāṁ rathayūthapaḥ ,ācāryaputraḥ sahasā pāṇḍavaṁ pratyavārayat.
english: Then, the preceptor's son, the commander of chariot divisions, suddenly blocked the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a large multitude of chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 65. Then, the preceptor's son, the commander of chariot divisions, suddenly blocked the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) with a large multitude of chariots.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थामा तु तत्कर्म हृदयेन महात्मनः ।पूजयामास पार्थस्य कोपं चास्याकरोद्भृशम् ॥६६॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmā tu tatkarma hṛdayena mahātmanaḥ ,pūjayāmāsa pārthasya kopaṁ cāsyākarodbhṛśam.
english: Ashwatthama, however, deep in his heart, honored that deed of the noble (Arjuna), and he also greatly intensified Arjuna's anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 66. Ashwatthama, however, deep in his heart, honored that deed of the noble (Arjuna), and he also greatly intensified Arjuna's anger.

sanskrit: स मन्युवशमापन्नः पार्थमभ्यद्रवद्रणे ।किरञ्शरसहस्राणि पर्जन्य इव वृष्टिमान् ॥६७॥
transliteration: sa manyuvaśamāpannaḥ pārthamabhyadravadraṇe ,kirañśarasahasrāṇi parjanya iva vṛṣṭimān.
english: Having fallen under the sway of wrath, he (Ashwatthama) rushed towards Pārtha (Arjuna) in battle, showering thousands of arrows like a rain-cloud that showers rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 67. Having fallen under the sway of wrath, he (Ashwatthama) rushed towards Pārtha (Arjuna) in battle, showering thousands of arrows like a rain-cloud that showers rain.

sanskrit: आवृत्य तु महाबाहुर्यतो द्रौणिस्ततो हयान् ।अन्तरं प्रददौ पार्थो द्रोणस्य व्यपसर्पितुम् ॥६८॥
transliteration: āvṛtya tu mahābāhuryato drauṇistato hayān ,antaraṁ pradadau pārtho droṇasya vyapasarpitum.
english: But the mighty-armed (Arjuna), having turned his horses away from where Drouṇi (Ashwatthama) was, gave Drona an opportunity to withdraw.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 68. But the mighty-armed (Arjuna), having turned his horses away from where Drouṇi (Ashwatthama) was, gave Drona an opportunity to withdraw.

sanskrit: स तु लब्ध्वान्तरं तूर्णमपायाज्जवनैर्हयैः ।छिन्नवर्मध्वजः शूरो निकृत्तः परमेषुभिः ॥६९॥
transliteration: sa tu labdhvāntaraṁ tūrṇamapāyājjavanairhayaiḥ ,chinnavarmadhvajaḥ śūro nikṛttaḥ parameṣubhiḥ.
english: But that hero, whose armor and banner had been cut and who was severely wounded by excellent arrows, quickly found an opportunity and escaped with swift horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 53 Verse 69. But that hero, whose armor and banner had been cut and who was severely wounded by excellent arrows, quickly found an opportunity and escaped with swift horses.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तं पार्थः प्रतिजग्राह वायुवेगमिवोद्धतम् ।शरजालेन महता वर्षमाणमिवाम्बुदम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taṁ pārthaḥ pratijagrāha vāyuvegamivoddhatam ,śarajālena mahatā varṣamāṇamivāmbudam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Pārtha (Arjuna) met him, who was as impetuous as the force of the wind and raining down a great multitude of arrows like a cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Pārtha (Arjuna) met him, who was as impetuous as the force of the wind and raining down a great multitude of arrows like a cloud.

sanskrit: तयोर्देवासुरसमः संनिपातो महानभूत् ।किरतोः शरजालानि वृत्रवासवयोरिव ॥२॥
transliteration: tayordevāsurasamaḥ saṁnipāto mahānabhūt ,kiratoḥ śarajālāni vṛtravāsavayoriva.
english: A great battle, resembling the clash between devas and asuras, occurred between those two, as they showered multitudes of arrows like Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 2. A great battle, resembling the clash between devas and asuras, occurred between those two, as they showered multitudes of arrows like Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra).

sanskrit: न स्म सूर्यस्तदा भाति न च वाति समीरणः ।शरगाढे कृते व्योम्नि छायाभूते समन्ततः ॥३॥
transliteration: na sma sūryastadā bhāti na ca vāti samīraṇaḥ ,śaragāḍhe kṛte vyomni chāyābhūte samantataḥ.
english: Then the sun did not shine, nor did the wind blow, as the sky was completely filled with arrows and had become shadowy all around.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 3. Then the sun did not shine, nor did the wind blow, as the sky was completely filled with arrows and had become shadowy all around.

sanskrit: महांश्चटचटाशब्दो योधयोर्हन्यमानयोः ।दह्यतामिव वेणूनामासीत्परपुरंजय ॥४॥
transliteration: mahāṁścaṭacaṭāśabdo yodhayorhanyamānayoḥ ,dahyatāmiva veṇūnāmāsītparapuraṁjaya.
english: O conqueror of enemy cities, there was a great crackling sound, like that of burning bamboos, as the two warriors were being struck.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 4. O conqueror of enemy cities, there was a great crackling sound, like that of burning bamboos, as the two warriors were being struck.

sanskrit: हयानस्यार्जुनः सर्वान्कृतवानल्पजीवितान् ।स राजन्न प्रजानाति दिशं कांचन मोहितः ॥५॥
transliteration: hayānasyārjunaḥ sarvānkṛtavānalpajīvitān ,sa rājanna prajānāti diśaṁ kāṁcana mohitaḥ.
english: Arjuna rendered all of his (opponent's) horses short-lived (killed or incapacitated them). O king, being bewildered, he (the opponent) did not know any direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 5. Arjuna rendered all of his (opponent's) horses short-lived (killed or incapacitated them). O king, being bewildered, he (the opponent) did not know any direction.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिर्महावीर्यः पार्थस्य विचरिष्यतः ।विवरं सूक्ष्ममालोक्य ज्यां चिच्छेद क्षुरेण ह ।तदस्यापूजयन्देवाः कर्म दृष्ट्वातिमानुषम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tato drauṇirmahāvīryaḥ pārthasya vicariṣyataḥ ,vivaraṁ sūkṣmamālokya jyāṁ ciccheda kṣureṇa ha ,tadasyāpūjayandevāḥ karma dṛṣṭvātimānuṣam.
english: Then, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), the mighty hero, having observed a small opening (vulnerability) as Pārtha (Arjuna) was moving about, indeed cut his bowstring with an arrow. Having seen that superhuman deed of his, the gods praised it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 6. Then, Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), the mighty hero, having observed a small opening (vulnerability) as Pārtha (Arjuna) was moving about, indeed cut his bowstring with an arrow. Having seen that superhuman deed of his, the gods praised it.

sanskrit: ततो द्रौणिर्धनूंष्यष्टौ व्यपक्रम्य नरर्षभम् ।पुनरभ्याहनत्पार्थं हृदये कङ्कपत्रिभिः ॥७॥
transliteration: tato drauṇirdhanūṁṣyaṣṭau vyapakramya nararṣabham ,punarabhyāhanatpārthaṁ hṛdaye kaṅkapatribhiḥ.
english: Then Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), after moving away from the best of men (Arjuna) and taking up eight bows, again struck Pārtha (Arjuna) in the heart with arrows feathered with heron feathers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 7. Then Droṇi (Aśvatthāmā), after moving away from the best of men (Arjuna) and taking up eight bows, again struck Pārtha (Arjuna) in the heart with arrows feathered with heron feathers.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थो महाबाहुः प्रहस्य स्वनवत्तदा ।योजयामास नवया मौर्व्या गाण्डीवमोजसा ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārtho mahābāhuḥ prahasya svanavattadā ,yojayāmāsa navayā maurvyā gāṇḍīvamojasā.
english: Then, the mighty-armed Arjuna, laughing sonorously at that time, strung his Gāṇḍīva bow with a new bowstring using his great might.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 8. Then, the mighty-armed Arjuna, laughing sonorously at that time, strung his Gāṇḍīva bow with a new bowstring using his great might.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्धचन्द्रमावृत्य तेन पार्थः समागमत् ।वारणेनेव मत्तेन मत्तो वारणयूथपः ॥९॥
transliteration: tato'rdhacandramāvṛtya tena pārthaḥ samāgamat ,vāraṇeneva mattena matto vāraṇayūthapaḥ.
english: Then, having drawn (his bow into) a crescent shape, Arjuna encountered him, just as a rutting leader of an elephant herd encounters a rutting elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 9. Then, having drawn (his bow into) a crescent shape, Arjuna encountered him, just as a rutting leader of an elephant herd encounters a rutting elephant.

sanskrit: ततः प्रववृते युद्धं पृथिव्यामेकवीरयोः ।रणमध्ये द्वयोरेव सुमहल्लोमहर्षणम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁ pṛthivyāmekavīrayoḥ ,raṇamadhye dvayoreva sumahallomaharṣaṇam.
english: Then, a very great and hair-raising battle commenced on the earth between those two sole heroes, right in the midst of the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 10. Then, a very great and hair-raising battle commenced on the earth between those two sole heroes, right in the midst of the battlefield.

sanskrit: तौ वीरौ कुरवः सर्वे ददृशुर्विस्मयान्विताः ।युध्यमानौ महात्मानौ यूथपाविव संगतौ ॥११॥
transliteration: tau vīrau kuravaḥ sarve dadṛśurvismayānvitāḥ ,yudhyamānau mahātmānau yūthapāviva saṁgatau.
english: All the Kurus, filled with wonder, watched those two great-souled heroes fighting, who were like two herd leaders engaged in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 11. All the Kurus, filled with wonder, watched those two great-souled heroes fighting, who were like two herd leaders engaged in combat.

sanskrit: तौ समाजघ्नतुर्वीरावन्योन्यं पुरुषर्षभौ ।शरैराशीविषाकारैर्ज्वलद्भिरिव पन्नगैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tau samājaghnaturvīrāvanyonyaṁ puruṣarṣabhau ,śarairāśīviṣākārairjvaladbhiriva pannagaiḥ.
english: Those two heroes, chief among men, struck each other with arrows that were like blazing, venomous snakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 12. Those two heroes, chief among men, struck each other with arrows that were like blazing, venomous snakes.

sanskrit: अक्षय्याविषुधी दिव्यौ पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ।तेन पार्थो रणे शूरस्तस्थौ गिरिरिवाचलः ॥१३॥
transliteration: akṣayyāviṣudhī divyau pāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ ,tena pārtho raṇe śūrastasthau giririvācalaḥ.
english: The great-souled Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) possessed two divine, inexhaustible quivers. Due to this, Arjuna, a hero in battle, stood firm like an immovable mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 13. The great-souled Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) possessed two divine, inexhaustible quivers. Due to this, Arjuna, a hero in battle, stood firm like an immovable mountain.

sanskrit: अश्वत्थाम्नः पुनर्बाणाः क्षिप्रमभ्यस्यतो रणे ।जग्मुः परिक्षयं शीघ्रमभूत्तेनाधिकोऽर्जुनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: aśvatthāmnaḥ punarbāṇāḥ kṣipramabhyasyato raṇe ,jagmuḥ parikṣayaṁ śīghramabhūttenādhiko'rjunaḥ.
english: But the arrows of Aśvatthāman, who was swiftly shooting them in battle, quickly reached exhaustion. Due to this, Arjuna became superior.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 14. But the arrows of Aśvatthāman, who was swiftly shooting them in battle, quickly reached exhaustion. Due to this, Arjuna became superior.

sanskrit: ततः कर्णो महच्चापं विकृष्याभ्यधिकं रुषा ।अवाक्षिपत्ततः शब्दो हाहाकारो महानभूत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ karṇo mahaccāpaṁ vikṛṣyābhyadhikaṁ ruṣā ,avākṣipattataḥ śabdo hāhākāro mahānabhūt.
english: Then Karṇa, having drawn his great bow with excessive anger, shot (arrows). Thereupon, a great cry of distress arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 15. Then Karṇa, having drawn his great bow with excessive anger, shot (arrows). Thereupon, a great cry of distress arose.

sanskrit: तत्र चक्षुर्दधे पार्थो यत्र विस्फार्यते धनुः ।ददर्श तत्र राधेयं तस्य कोपोऽत्यवीवृधत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatra cakṣurdadhe pārtho yatra visphāryate dhanuḥ ,dadarśa tatra rādheyaṁ tasya kopo'tyavīvṛdhat.
english: Arjuna fixed his gaze there, where the bow was being strung. There he saw Karna, and his anger greatly intensified.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 16. Arjuna fixed his gaze there, where the bow was being strung. There he saw Karna, and his anger greatly intensified.

sanskrit: स रोषवशमापन्नः कर्णमेव जिघांसया ।अवैक्षत विवृत्ताभ्यां नेत्राभ्यां कुरुपुंगवः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa roṣavaśamāpannaḥ karṇameva jighāṁsayā ,avaikṣata vivṛttābhyāṁ netrābhyāṁ kurupuṁgavaḥ.
english: Overcome by anger, that best of the Kurus (Arjuna) looked only at Karna with a desire to kill, his eyes wide-open.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 17. Overcome by anger, that best of the Kurus (Arjuna) looked only at Karna with a desire to kill, his eyes wide-open.

sanskrit: तथा तु विमुखे पार्थे द्रोणपुत्रस्य सायकान् ।त्वरिताः पुरुषा राजन्नुपाजह्रुः सहस्रशः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathā tu vimukhe pārthe droṇaputrasya sāyakān ,tvaritāḥ puruṣā rājannupājahruḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: But when Arjuna had thus turned his face away, O King, swift men brought thousands of arrows for Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 18. But when Arjuna had thus turned his face away, O King, swift men brought thousands of arrows for Drona's son (Aśvatthāmā).

sanskrit: उत्सृज्य च महाबाहुर्द्रोणपुत्रं धनंजयः ।अभिदुद्राव सहसा कर्णमेव सपत्नजित् ॥१९॥
transliteration: utsṛjya ca mahābāhurdroṇaputraṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,abhidudrāva sahasā karṇameva sapatnajit.
english: And having left Drona's son, the mighty-armed Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the conqueror of foes, suddenly rushed solely towards Karna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 19. And having left Drona's son, the mighty-armed Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the conqueror of foes, suddenly rushed solely towards Karna.

sanskrit: तमभिद्रुत्य कौन्तेयः क्रोधसंरक्तलोचनः ।कामयन्द्वैरथे युद्धमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tamabhidrutya kaunteyaḥ krodhasaṁraktalocanaḥ ,kāmayandvairathe yuddhamidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Arjuna, his eyes reddened with anger, rushed towards him, desiring a duel, and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 54 Verse 20. Arjuna, his eyes reddened with anger, rushed towards him, desiring a duel, and spoke these words.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।कर्ण यत्ते सभामध्ये बहु वाचा विकत्थितम् ।न मे युधि समोऽस्तीति तदिदं प्रत्युपस्थितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,karṇa yatte sabhāmadhye bahu vācā vikatthitam ,na me yudhi samo'stīti tadidaṁ pratyupasthitam.
english: Arjuna said: "O Karna, that which you boasted so much with words in the assembly, saying, 'No one is my equal in battle,' that very situation has now presented itself."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 1. Arjuna said: "O Karna, that which you boasted so much with words in the assembly, saying, 'No one is my equal in battle,' that very situation has now presented itself."

sanskrit: अवोचः परुषा वाचो धर्ममुत्सृज्य केवलम् ।इदं तु दुष्करं मन्ये यदिदं ते चिकीर्षितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: avocaḥ paruṣā vāco dharmamutsṛjya kevalam ,idaṁ tu duṣkaraṁ manye yadidaṁ te cikīrṣitam.
english: You spoke harsh words, entirely abandoning natural law (dharma). But this, which is intended by you, I consider difficult to achieve.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 2. You spoke harsh words, entirely abandoning natural law (dharma). But this, which is intended by you, I consider difficult to achieve.

sanskrit: यत्त्वया कथितं पूर्वं मामनासाद्य किंचन ।तदद्य कुरु राधेय कुरुमध्ये मया सह ॥३॥
transliteration: yattvayā kathitaṁ pūrvaṁ māmanāsādya kiṁcana ,tadadya kuru rādheya kurumadhye mayā saha.
english: Whatever you declared previously without ever encountering me, O son of Radha, do that now in the midst of the Kurus, along with me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 3. Whatever you declared previously without ever encountering me, O son of Radha, do that now in the midst of the Kurus, along with me.

sanskrit: यत्सभायां स्म पाञ्चालीं क्लिश्यमानां दुरात्मभिः ।दृष्टवानसि तस्याद्य फलमाप्नुहि केवलम् ॥४॥
transliteration: yatsabhāyāṁ sma pāñcālīṁ kliśyamānāṁ durātmabhiḥ ,dṛṣṭavānasi tasyādya phalamāpnuhi kevalam.
english: The consequence of merely having witnessed Draupadi being tormented by wicked people in that assembly – that alone you shall now obtain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 4. The consequence of merely having witnessed Draupadi being tormented by wicked people in that assembly – that alone you shall now obtain.

sanskrit: धर्मपाशनिबद्धेन यन्मया मर्षितं पुरा ।तस्य राधेय कोपस्य विजयं पश्य मे मृधे ॥५॥
transliteration: dharmapāśanibaddhena yanmayā marṣitaṁ purā ,tasya rādheya kopasya vijayaṁ paśya me mṛdhe.
english: O son of Rādhā (Radheya), behold in battle my victory, which stems from the anger that I formerly suppressed, bound as I was by the noose of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 5. O son of Rādhā (Radheya), behold in battle my victory, which stems from the anger that I formerly suppressed, bound as I was by the noose of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: एहि कर्ण मया सार्धं प्रतिपद्यस्व संगरम् ।प्रेक्षकाः कुरवः सर्वे भवन्तु सहसैनिकाः ॥६॥
transliteration: ehi karṇa mayā sārdhaṁ pratipadyasva saṁgaram ,prekṣakāḥ kuravaḥ sarve bhavantu sahasainikāḥ.
english: Come, Karna, engage in battle with me! Let all the Kurus, along with their soldiers, be spectators.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 6. Come, Karna, engage in battle with me! Let all the Kurus, along with their soldiers, be spectators.

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।ब्रवीषि वाचा यत्पार्थ कर्मणा तत्समाचर ।अतिशेते हि वै वाचं कर्मेति प्रथितं भुवि ॥७॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,bravīṣi vācā yatpārtha karmaṇā tatsamācara ,atiśete hi vai vācaṁ karmeti prathitaṁ bhuvi.
