english: Karna said: O son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), whatever you speak with words, perform it with action (karma)! For it is indeed renowned on earth that action (karma) surpasses speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 7. Karna said: O son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), whatever you speak with words, perform it with action (karma)! For it is indeed renowned on earth that action (karma) surpasses speech.

sanskrit: यत्त्वया मर्षितं पूर्वं तदशक्तेन मर्षितम् ।इति गृह्णामि तत्पार्थ तव दृष्ट्वापराक्रमम् ॥८॥
transliteration: yattvayā marṣitaṁ pūrvaṁ tadaśaktena marṣitam ,iti gṛhṇāmi tatpārtha tava dṛṣṭvāparākramam.
english: Whatever you tolerated before, I then considered it to have been endured by one who was powerless. But now, O Pārtha, having witnessed your valor, I understand the true meaning of that tolerance.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 8. Whatever you tolerated before, I then considered it to have been endured by one who was powerless. But now, O Pārtha, having witnessed your valor, I understand the true meaning of that tolerance.

sanskrit: धर्मपाशनिबद्धेन यदि ते मर्षितं पुरा ।तथैव बद्धमात्मानमबद्धमिव मन्यसे ॥९॥
transliteration: dharmapāśanibaddhena yadi te marṣitaṁ purā ,tathaiva baddhamātmānamabaddhamiva manyase.
english: If you tolerated it in the past, being bound by the fetters of natural law (dharma), then you consider your self (ātman) as if unbound, even though it is (truly) bound.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 9. If you tolerated it in the past, being bound by the fetters of natural law (dharma), then you consider your self (ātman) as if unbound, even though it is (truly) bound.

sanskrit: यदि तावद्वने वासो यथोक्तश्चरितस्त्वया ।तत्त्वं धर्मार्थवित्क्लिष्टः समयं भेत्तुमिच्छसि ॥१०॥
transliteration: yadi tāvadvane vāso yathoktaścaritastvayā ,tattvaṁ dharmārthavitkliṣṭaḥ samayaṁ bhettumicchasi.
english: If your dwelling in the forest was indeed carried out by you exactly as prescribed, then you, though distressed and a knower of natural law (dharma) and purpose, now wish to break the agreement.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 10. If your dwelling in the forest was indeed carried out by you exactly as prescribed, then you, though distressed and a knower of natural law (dharma) and purpose, now wish to break the agreement.

sanskrit: यदि शक्रः स्वयं पार्थ युध्यते तव कारणात् ।तथापि न व्यथा काचिन्मम स्याद्विक्रमिष्यतः ॥११॥
transliteration: yadi śakraḥ svayaṁ pārtha yudhyate tava kāraṇāt ,tathāpi na vyathā kācinmama syādvikramiṣyataḥ.
english: Even if Indra himself, O Pārtha, fights on your behalf, still there would be no distress for me, who am prepared to demonstrate my prowess.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 11. Even if Indra himself, O Pārtha, fights on your behalf, still there would be no distress for me, who am prepared to demonstrate my prowess.

sanskrit: अयं कौन्तेय कामस्ते नचिरात्समुपस्थितः ।योत्स्यसे त्वं मया सार्धमद्य द्रक्ष्यसि मे बलम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: ayaṁ kaunteya kāmaste nacirātsamupasthitaḥ ,yotsyase tvaṁ mayā sārdhamadya drakṣyasi me balam.
english: O son of Kunti, this desire of yours will soon be fulfilled. You will fight with me, and today you will witness my strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 12. O son of Kunti, this desire of yours will soon be fulfilled. You will fight with me, and today you will witness my strength.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।इदानीमेव तावत्त्वमपयातो रणान्मम ।तेन जीवसि राधेय निहतस्त्वनुजस्तव ॥१३॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,idānīmeva tāvattvamapayāto raṇānmama ,tena jīvasi rādheya nihatastvanujastava.
english: Arjuna said: 'Indeed, just now you retreated from my battle. That is why you are alive, O son of Rādhā, while your younger brother has been killed.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 13. Arjuna said: 'Indeed, just now you retreated from my battle. That is why you are alive, O son of Rādhā, while your younger brother has been killed.'

sanskrit: भ्रातरं घातयित्वा च त्यक्त्वा रणशिरश्च कः ।त्वदन्यः पुरुषः सत्सु ब्रूयादेवं व्यवस्थितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhrātaraṁ ghātayitvā ca tyaktvā raṇaśiraśca kaḥ ,tvadanyaḥ puruṣaḥ satsu brūyādevaṁ vyavasthitaḥ.
english: And who else, having caused his brother to be killed and having abandoned the forefront of battle, would speak thus among respectable people while situated in such a position? No other person (puruṣa) besides you would.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 14. And who else, having caused his brother to be killed and having abandoned the forefront of battle, would speak thus among respectable people while situated in such a position? No other person (puruṣa) besides you would.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इति कर्णं ब्रुवन्नेव बीभत्सुरपराजितः ।अभ्ययाद्विसृजन्बाणान्कायावरणभेदिनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,iti karṇaṁ bruvanneva bībhatsuraparājitaḥ ,abhyayādvisṛjanbāṇānkāyāvaraṇabhedinaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Even while speaking thus to Karna, the undefeated Arjuna (Bībhatsu) advanced, releasing arrows that pierced through armor.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 15. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Even while speaking thus to Karna, the undefeated Arjuna (Bībhatsu) advanced, releasing arrows that pierced through armor.'

sanskrit: प्रतिजग्राह तान्कर्णः शरानग्निशिखोपमान् ।शरवर्षेण महता वर्षमाण इवाम्बुदः ॥१६॥
transliteration: pratijagrāha tānkarṇaḥ śarānagniśikhopamān ,śaravarṣeṇa mahatā varṣamāṇa ivāmbudaḥ.
english: Karṇa received those arrows, which were like flames of fire. He then resembled a cloud, raining down a great shower of arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 16. Karṇa received those arrows, which were like flames of fire. He then resembled a cloud, raining down a great shower of arrows.

sanskrit: उत्पेतुः शरजालानि घोररूपाणि सर्वशः ।अविध्यदश्वान्बाह्वोश्च हस्तावापं पृथक्पृथक् ॥१७॥
transliteration: utpetuḥ śarajālāni ghorarūpāṇi sarvaśaḥ ,avidhyadaśvānbāhvośca hastāvāpaṁ pṛthakpṛthak.
english: Frightful volleys of arrows shot forth from all directions. (Arjuna) pierced the horses and, separately, struck the arm-guards on both (Karṇa's) arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 17. Frightful volleys of arrows shot forth from all directions. (Arjuna) pierced the horses and, separately, struck the arm-guards on both (Karṇa's) arms.

sanskrit: सोऽमृष्यमाणः कर्णस्य निषङ्गस्यावलम्बनम् ।चिच्छेद निशिताग्रेण शरेण नतपर्वणा ॥१८॥
transliteration: so'mṛṣyamāṇaḥ karṇasya niṣaṅgasyāvalambanam ,ciccheda niśitāgreṇa śareṇa nataparvaṇā.
english: Unable to tolerate (Karṇa's actions), he (Arjuna) cut the strap of Karṇa's quiver with a sharp-tipped, well-feathered arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 18. Unable to tolerate (Karṇa's actions), he (Arjuna) cut the strap of Karṇa's quiver with a sharp-tipped, well-feathered arrow.

sanskrit: उपासङ्गादुपादाय कर्णो बाणानथापरान् ।विव्याध पाण्डवं हस्ते तस्य मुष्टिरशीर्यत ॥१९॥
transliteration: upāsaṅgādupādāya karṇo bāṇānathāparān ,vivyādha pāṇḍavaṁ haste tasya muṣṭiraśīryata.
english: Then Karṇa, taking other arrows from his quiver, pierced the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) in the hand. As a result, his (Arjuna's) grip became loose.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 19. Then Karṇa, taking other arrows from his quiver, pierced the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) in the hand. As a result, his (Arjuna's) grip became loose.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थो महाबाहुः कर्णस्य धनुरच्छिनत् ।स शक्तिं प्राहिणोत्तस्मै तां पार्थो व्यधमच्छरैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārtho mahābāhuḥ karṇasya dhanuracchinat ,sa śaktiṁ prāhiṇottasmai tāṁ pārtho vyadhamaccharaiḥ.
english: Then, the mighty-armed Arjuna cut off Karṇa's bow. Karṇa then hurled a spear (śakti) at Arjuna, but Arjuna shattered it with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 20. Then, the mighty-armed Arjuna cut off Karṇa's bow. Karṇa then hurled a spear (śakti) at Arjuna, but Arjuna shattered it with his arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽभिपेतुर्बहवो राधेयस्य पदानुगाः ।तांश्च गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्तैः प्राहिणोद्यमसादनम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tato'bhipeturbahavo rādheyasya padānugāḥ ,tāṁśca gāṇḍīvanirmuktaiḥ prāhiṇodyamasādanam.
english: Then, many followers of Radheya (Karṇa) rushed towards Arjuna. He sent those (followers) to the abode of Yama (death) with arrows released from his Gaṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 21. Then, many followers of Radheya (Karṇa) rushed towards Arjuna. He sent those (followers) to the abode of Yama (death) with arrows released from his Gaṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्याश्वाञ्शरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्बीभत्सुर्भारसाधनैः ।आकर्णमुक्तैरभ्यघ्नंस्ते हताः प्रापतन्भुवि ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato'syāśvāñśaraistīkṣṇairbībhatsurbhārasādhanaiḥ ,ākarṇamuktairabhyaghnaṁste hatāḥ prāpatanbhuvi.
english: Then, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) struck Karṇa's horses with sharp arrows, shot with full force (ākarṇamuktaiḥ) and effective in battle (bhārasādhanaiḥ). Killed, those horses fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 22. Then, Bibhatsu (Arjuna) struck Karṇa's horses with sharp arrows, shot with full force (ākarṇamuktaiḥ) and effective in battle (bhārasādhanaiḥ). Killed, those horses fell to the ground.

sanskrit: अथापरेण बाणेन ज्वलितेन महाभुजः ।विव्याध कर्णं कौन्तेयस्तीक्ष्णेनोरसि वीर्यवान् ॥२३॥
transliteration: athāpareṇa bāṇena jvalitena mahābhujaḥ ,vivyādha karṇaṁ kaunteyastīkṣṇenorasi vīryavān.
english: Then, the valorous (vīryavān) and mighty-armed (mahābhujaḥ) Kaunteya (Arjuna) pierced Karṇa in the chest with another sharp, blazing arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 23. Then, the valorous (vīryavān) and mighty-armed (mahābhujaḥ) Kaunteya (Arjuna) pierced Karṇa in the chest with another sharp, blazing arrow.

sanskrit: तस्य भित्त्वा तनुत्राणं कायमभ्यपतच्छरः ।ततः स तमसाविष्टो न स्म किंचित्प्रजज्ञिवान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tasya bhittvā tanutrāṇaṁ kāyamabhyapataccharaḥ ,tataḥ sa tamasāviṣṭo na sma kiṁcitprajajñivān.
english: The arrow, having pierced his armor, struck his body. Consequently, enveloped by darkness, he could not perceive anything at all.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 24. The arrow, having pierced his armor, struck his body. Consequently, enveloped by darkness, he could not perceive anything at all.

sanskrit: स गाढवेदनो हित्वा रणं प्रायादुदङ्मुखः ।ततोऽर्जुन उपाक्रोशदुत्तरश्च महारथः ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa gāḍhavedano hitvā raṇaṁ prāyādudaṅmukhaḥ ,tato'rjuna upākrośaduttaraśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Suffering intense pain, he left the battlefield and went northwards. Then Arjuna and the great chariot warrior Uttara cried out.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 55 Verse 25. Suffering intense pain, he left the battlefield and went northwards. Then Arjuna and the great chariot warrior Uttara cried out.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो वैकर्तनं जित्वा पार्थो वैराटिमब्रवीत् ।एतन्मां प्रापयानीकं यत्र तालो हिरण्मयः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato vaikartanaṁ jitvā pārtho vairāṭimabravīt ,etanmāṁ prāpayānīkaṁ yatra tālo hiraṇmayaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Pārtha, having conquered Vaikartana (Karṇa), spoke to the son of Virāṭa (Uttara): "Lead me to that army where the golden palm tree emblem is located."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Pārtha, having conquered Vaikartana (Karṇa), spoke to the son of Virāṭa (Uttara): "Lead me to that army where the golden palm tree emblem is located."

sanskrit: अत्र शांतनवो भीष्मो रथेऽस्माकं पितामहः ।काङ्क्षमाणो मया युद्धं तिष्ठत्यमरदर्शनः ।आदास्याम्यहमेतस्य धनुर्ज्यामपि चाहवे ॥२॥
transliteration: atra śāṁtanavo bhīṣmo rathe'smākaṁ pitāmahaḥ ,kāṅkṣamāṇo mayā yuddhaṁ tiṣṭhatyamaradarśanaḥ ,ādāsyāmyahametasya dhanurjyāmapi cāhave.
english: Here, on a chariot, stands Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, our grandfather, looking like an immortal, eagerly desiring battle with me. I will indeed seize his bowstring in this very conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 2. Here, on a chariot, stands Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, our grandfather, looking like an immortal, eagerly desiring battle with me. I will indeed seize his bowstring in this very conflict.

sanskrit: अस्यन्तं दिव्यमस्त्रं मां चित्रमद्य निशामय ।शतह्रदामिवायान्तीं स्तनयित्नोरिवाम्बरे ॥३॥
transliteration: asyantaṁ divyamastraṁ māṁ citramadya niśāmaya ,śatahradāmivāyāntīṁ stanayitnorivāmbare.
english: Today, observe me as I wonderfully discharge a divine weapon, like approaching flashes of lightning or a thunderclap in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 3. Today, observe me as I wonderfully discharge a divine weapon, like approaching flashes of lightning or a thunderclap in the sky.

sanskrit: सुवर्णपृष्ठं गाण्डीवं द्रक्ष्यन्ति कुरवो मम ।दक्षिणेनाथ वामेन कतरेण स्विदस्यति ।इति मां संगताः सर्वे तर्कयिष्यन्ति शत्रवः ॥४॥
transliteration: suvarṇapṛṣṭhaṁ gāṇḍīvaṁ drakṣyanti kuravo mama ,dakṣiṇenātha vāmena katareṇa svidasyati ,iti māṁ saṁgatāḥ sarve tarkayiṣyanti śatravaḥ.
english: The Kurus will see my golden-backed bow, the Gāṇḍīva. All my assembled enemies will then speculate about me, wondering, 'Does he shoot with his right or his left hand?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 4. The Kurus will see my golden-backed bow, the Gāṇḍīva. All my assembled enemies will then speculate about me, wondering, 'Does he shoot with his right or his left hand?'

sanskrit: शोणितोदां रथावर्तां नागनक्रां दुरत्ययाम् ।नदीं प्रस्यन्दयिष्यामि परलोकप्रवाहिनीम् ॥५॥
transliteration: śoṇitodāṁ rathāvartāṁ nāganakrāṁ duratyayām ,nadīṁ prasyandayiṣyāmi paralokapravāhinīm.
english: I will make a river flow, difficult to cross, with blood for its water, chariots for its eddies, and elephants and crocodiles for its aquatic creatures, a river that carries (souls) to the other world.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 5. I will make a river flow, difficult to cross, with blood for its water, chariots for its eddies, and elephants and crocodiles for its aquatic creatures, a river that carries (souls) to the other world.

sanskrit: पाणिपादशिरःपृष्ठबाहुशाखानिरन्तरम् ।वनं कुरूणां छेत्स्यामि भल्लैः संनतपर्वभिः ॥६॥
transliteration: pāṇipādaśiraḥpṛṣṭhabāhuśākhānirantaram ,vanaṁ kurūṇāṁ chetsyāmi bhallaiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ.
english: I will cut down the Kuru army, which is like a dense forest of hands, feet, heads, backs, and arm-branches, using my sharp-pointed arrows (bhallaiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 6. I will cut down the Kuru army, which is like a dense forest of hands, feet, heads, backs, and arm-branches, using my sharp-pointed arrows (bhallaiḥ).

sanskrit: जयतः कौरवीं सेनामेकस्य मम धन्विनः ।शतं मार्गा भविष्यन्ति पावकस्येव कानने ।मया चक्रमिवाविद्धं सैन्यं द्रक्ष्यसि केवलम् ॥७॥
transliteration: jayataḥ kauravīṁ senāmekasya mama dhanvinaḥ ,śataṁ mārgā bhaviṣyanti pāvakasyeva kānane ,mayā cakramivāviddhaṁ sainyaṁ drakṣyasi kevalam.
english: As a lone archer, I will conquer the Kaurava army. A hundred paths will open up, just as fire spreads in a forest. You will see the army scattered by me, just like a wheel.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 7. As a lone archer, I will conquer the Kaurava army. A hundred paths will open up, just as fire spreads in a forest. You will see the army scattered by me, just like a wheel.

sanskrit: असंभ्रान्तो रथे तिष्ठ समेषु विषमेषु च ।दिवमावृत्य तिष्ठन्तं गिरिं भेत्स्यामि धारिभिः ॥८॥
transliteration: asaṁbhrānto rathe tiṣṭha sameṣu viṣameṣu ca ,divamāvṛtya tiṣṭhantaṁ giriṁ bhetsyāmi dhāribhiḥ.
english: Remain undisturbed (asambhānta) in your chariot, whether on even or uneven ground. I will pierce (bhetsyāmi) the mountain standing, covering the sky, with my arrows (dhāribhiḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 8. Remain undisturbed (asambhānta) in your chariot, whether on even or uneven ground. I will pierce (bhetsyāmi) the mountain standing, covering the sky, with my arrows (dhāribhiḥ).

sanskrit: अहमिन्द्रस्य वचनात्संग्रामेऽभ्यहनं पुरा ।पौलोमान्कालखञ्जांश्च सहस्राणि शतानि च ॥९॥
transliteration: ahamindrasya vacanātsaṁgrāme'bhyahanaṁ purā ,paulomānkālakhañjāṁśca sahasrāṇi śatāni ca.
english: By the command of Indra, I previously slew the Paulomas and the Kālakhañjas, thousands upon hundreds (of them), in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 9. By the command of Indra, I previously slew the Paulomas and the Kālakhañjas, thousands upon hundreds (of them), in battle.

sanskrit: अहमिन्द्राद्दृढां मुष्टिं ब्रह्मणः कृतहस्तताम् ।प्रगाढं तुमुलं चित्रमतिविद्धं प्रजापतेः ॥१०॥
transliteration: ahamindrāddṛḍhāṁ muṣṭiṁ brahmaṇaḥ kṛtahastatām ,pragāḍhaṁ tumulaṁ citramatividdhaṁ prajāpateḥ.
english: From Indra, I received a firm fist (dṛḍhām muṣṭim), and from Brahmā, dexterity (kṛtahastatām). From Prajāpati, I received intensely fierce, varied, and extremely precise archery (atividdham).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 10. From Indra, I received a firm fist (dṛḍhām muṣṭim), and from Brahmā, dexterity (kṛtahastatām). From Prajāpati, I received intensely fierce, varied, and extremely precise archery (atividdham).

sanskrit: अहं पारे समुद्रस्य हिरण्यपुरमारुजम् ।जित्वा षष्टिसहस्राणि रथिनामुग्रधन्विनाम् ॥११॥
transliteration: ahaṁ pāre samudrasya hiraṇyapuramārujam ,jitvā ṣaṣṭisahasrāṇi rathināmugradhanvinām.
english: I attacked Hiranyapura beyond the ocean, having conquered sixty thousand fierce bowmen who were charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 11. I attacked Hiranyapura beyond the ocean, having conquered sixty thousand fierce bowmen who were charioteers.

sanskrit: ध्वजवृक्षं पत्तितृणं रथसिंहगणायुतम् ।वनमादीपयिष्यामि कुरूणामस्त्रतेजसा ॥१२॥
transliteration: dhvajavṛkṣaṁ pattitṛṇaṁ rathasiṁhagaṇāyutam ,vanamādīpayiṣyāmi kurūṇāmastratejasā.
english: I will set ablaze the forest of the Kurus, which is filled with banner-trees, fallen grass, and throngs of lion-like chariots, using the power of my weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 12. I will set ablaze the forest of the Kurus, which is filled with banner-trees, fallen grass, and throngs of lion-like chariots, using the power of my weapons.

sanskrit: तानहं रथनीडेभ्यः शरैः संनतपर्वभिः ।एकः संकालयिष्यामि वज्रपाणिरिवासुरान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tānahaṁ rathanīḍebhyaḥ śaraiḥ saṁnataparvabhiḥ ,ekaḥ saṁkālayiṣyāmi vajrapāṇirivāsurān.
english: I alone will drive them out from their chariot-seats with arrows that have bent joints, just as Vajrapani (Indra) drives out the Asuras.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 13. I alone will drive them out from their chariot-seats with arrows that have bent joints, just as Vajrapani (Indra) drives out the Asuras.

sanskrit: रौद्रं रुद्रादहं ह्यस्त्रं वारुणं वरुणादपि ।अस्त्रमाग्नेयमग्नेश्च वायव्यं मातरिश्वनः ।वज्रादीनि तथास्त्राणि शक्रादहमवाप्तवान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: raudraṁ rudrādahaṁ hyastraṁ vāruṇaṁ varuṇādapi ,astramāgneyamagneśca vāyavyaṁ mātariśvanaḥ ,vajrādīni tathāstrāṇi śakrādahamavāptavān.
english: Indeed, I obtained the Raudra weapon from Rudra, and the Varuna weapon also from Varuna. I also obtained the Agni weapon from Agni, the Vayu weapon from Matarishvan (Vayu), and similarly, I obtained the thunderbolt and other weapons from Shakra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 14. Indeed, I obtained the Raudra weapon from Rudra, and the Varuna weapon also from Varuna. I also obtained the Agni weapon from Agni, the Vayu weapon from Matarishvan (Vayu), and similarly, I obtained the thunderbolt and other weapons from Shakra (Indra).

sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्रवनं घोरं नरसिंहाभिरक्षितम् ।अहमुत्पाटयिष्यामि वैराटे व्येतु ते भयम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: dhārtarāṣṭravanaṁ ghoraṁ narasiṁhābhirakṣitam ,ahamutpāṭayiṣyāmi vairāṭe vyetu te bhayam.
english: I will uproot this terrible forest of the Dhārtarāṣṭras, which is guarded by lion-like warriors. O son of Virāṭa, let your fear subside.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 15. I will uproot this terrible forest of the Dhārtarāṣṭras, which is guarded by lion-like warriors. O son of Virāṭa, let your fear subside.

sanskrit: एवमाश्वासितस्तेन वैराटिः सव्यसाचिना ।व्यगाहत रथानीकं भीमं भीष्मस्य धीमतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: evamāśvāsitastena vairāṭiḥ savyasācinā ,vyagāhata rathānīkaṁ bhīmaṁ bhīṣmasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Thus reassured by Arjuna (savyasācin), the son of Virāṭa penetrated the formidable chariot formation of the wise Bhīṣma.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 16. Thus reassured by Arjuna (savyasācin), the son of Virāṭa penetrated the formidable chariot formation of the wise Bhīṣma.

sanskrit: तमायान्तं महाबाहुं जिगीषन्तं रणे परान् ।अभ्यवारयदव्यग्रः क्रूरकर्मा धनंजयम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tamāyāntaṁ mahābāhuṁ jigīṣantaṁ raṇe parān ,abhyavārayadavyagraḥ krūrakarmā dhanaṁjayam.
english: The unperturbed one, known for cruel deeds (krūrakarmā), intercepted the mighty-armed Dhananjaya (Arjuna) who was approaching, eager to conquer the enemies in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 17. The unperturbed one, known for cruel deeds (krūrakarmā), intercepted the mighty-armed Dhananjaya (Arjuna) who was approaching, eager to conquer the enemies in battle.

sanskrit: तं चित्रमाल्याभरणाः कृतविद्या मनस्विनः ।आगच्छन्भीमधन्वानं मौर्वीं पर्यस्य बाहुभिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: taṁ citramālyābharaṇāḥ kṛtavidyā manasvinaḥ ,āgacchanbhīmadhanvānaṁ maurvīṁ paryasya bāhubhiḥ.
english: The skilled and resolute warriors, adorned with splendid garlands and ornaments, approached the formidable bowman (Arjuna), drawing back their bowstrings with their arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 18. The skilled and resolute warriors, adorned with splendid garlands and ornaments, approached the formidable bowman (Arjuna), drawing back their bowstrings with their arms.

sanskrit: दुःशासनो विकर्णश्च दुःसहोऽथ विविंशतिः ।आगत्य भीमधन्वानं बीभत्सुं पर्यवारयन् ॥१९॥
transliteration: duḥśāsano vikarṇaśca duḥsaho'tha viviṁśatiḥ ,āgatya bhīmadhanvānaṁ bībhatsuṁ paryavārayan.
english: Duḥśāsana, Vikaṛṇa, Duḥsaha, and Viviṃśati, having arrived, surrounded the formidable archer, Bibhatsu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 19. Duḥśāsana, Vikaṛṇa, Duḥsaha, and Viviṃśati, having arrived, surrounded the formidable archer, Bibhatsu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: दुःशासनस्तु भल्लेन विद्ध्वा वैराटिमुत्तरम् ।द्वितीयेनार्जुनं वीरः प्रत्यविध्यत्स्तनान्तरे ॥२०॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanastu bhallena viddhvā vairāṭimuttaram ,dvitīyenārjunaṁ vīraḥ pratyavidhyatstanāntare.
english: But the heroic Duḥśāsana, having pierced Uttara, the son of Virāṭa, with a broad-headed arrow, then pierced Arjuna in the chest with a second one.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 20. But the heroic Duḥśāsana, having pierced Uttara, the son of Virāṭa, with a broad-headed arrow, then pierced Arjuna in the chest with a second one.

sanskrit: तस्य जिष्णुरुपावृत्य पृथुधारेण कार्मुकम् ।चकर्त गार्ध्रपत्रेण जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasya jiṣṇurupāvṛtya pṛthudhāreṇa kārmukam ,cakarta gārdhrapatreṇa jātarūpapariṣkṛtam.
english: Then Jishṇu (Arjuna), turning swiftly, cut his bow, which was adorned with gold (jātarūpa), with a broad-bladed (arrow) and a vulture-feathered (arrow).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 21. Then Jishṇu (Arjuna), turning swiftly, cut his bow, which was adorned with gold (jātarūpa), with a broad-bladed (arrow) and a vulture-feathered (arrow).

sanskrit: अथैनं पञ्चभिः पश्चात्प्रत्यविध्यत्स्तनान्तरे ।सोऽपयातो रणं हित्वा पार्थबाणप्रपीडितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: athainaṁ pañcabhiḥ paścātpratyavidhyatstanāntare ,so'payāto raṇaṁ hitvā pārthabāṇaprapīḍitaḥ.
english: Then, after that, he (Arjuna) pierced him (Duḥśāsana) in the chest with five (arrows). Oppressed by Pārtha's (Arjuna's) arrows, he (Duḥśāsana) then retreated, abandoning the battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 22. Then, after that, he (Arjuna) pierced him (Duḥśāsana) in the chest with five (arrows). Oppressed by Pārtha's (Arjuna's) arrows, he (Duḥśāsana) then retreated, abandoning the battle.

sanskrit: तं विकर्णः शरैस्तीक्ष्णैर्गार्ध्रपत्रैरजिह्मगैः ।विव्याध परवीरघ्नमर्जुनं धृतराष्ट्रजः ॥२३॥
transliteration: taṁ vikarṇaḥ śaraistīkṣṇairgārdhrapatrairajihmagaiḥ ,vivyādha paravīraghnamarjunaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrajaḥ.
english: Vikarna, the son of Dhritarashtra, pierced Arjuna, who slays hostile heroes, with sharp, straight-flying, eagle-feathered arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 23. Vikarna, the son of Dhritarashtra, pierced Arjuna, who slays hostile heroes, with sharp, straight-flying, eagle-feathered arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तमपि कौन्तेयः शरेणानतपर्वणा ।ललाटेऽभ्यहनत्तूर्णं स विद्धः प्रापतद्रथात् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tatastamapi kaunteyaḥ śareṇānataparvaṇā ,lalāṭe'bhyahanattūrṇaṁ sa viddhaḥ prāpatadrathāt.
english: Then Arjuna, the son of Kunti, quickly struck him (Vikarna) on the forehead with a well-aimed arrow. He, thus pierced, fell from his chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 24. Then Arjuna, the son of Kunti, quickly struck him (Vikarna) on the forehead with a well-aimed arrow. He, thus pierced, fell from his chariot.

sanskrit: ततः पार्थमभिद्रुत्य दुःसहः सविविंशतिः ।अवाकिरच्छरैस्तीक्ष्णैः परीप्सन्भ्रातरं रणे ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārthamabhidrutya duḥsahaḥ saviviṁśatiḥ ,avākiraccharaistīkṣṇaiḥ parīpsanbhrātaraṁ raṇe.
english: Then Dussaha, accompanied by Vivimshati, attacked Arjuna and showered him with sharp arrows, intending to protect their brother in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 25. Then Dussaha, accompanied by Vivimshati, attacked Arjuna and showered him with sharp arrows, intending to protect their brother in battle.

sanskrit: तावुभौ गार्ध्रपत्राभ्यां निशिताभ्यां धनंजयः ।विद्ध्वा युगपदव्यग्रस्तयोर्वाहानसूदयत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tāvubhau gārdhrapatrābhyāṁ niśitābhyāṁ dhanaṁjayaḥ ,viddhvā yugapadavyagrastayorvāhānasūdayat.
english: Unperturbed, Arjuna (Dhananjaya) simultaneously struck both of them (Dussaha and Vivimshati) with sharp, eagle-feathered arrows and then destroyed their horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 26. Unperturbed, Arjuna (Dhananjaya) simultaneously struck both of them (Dussaha and Vivimshati) with sharp, eagle-feathered arrows and then destroyed their horses.

sanskrit: तौ हताश्वौ विविद्धाङ्गौ धृतराष्ट्रात्मजावुभौ ।अभिपत्य रथैरन्यैरपनीतौ पदानुगैः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tau hatāśvau vividdhāṅgau dhṛtarāṣṭrātmajāvubhau ,abhipatya rathairanyairapanītau padānugaiḥ.
english: Both of Dhritarashtra's sons, whose horses had been killed and whose bodies were pierced, were rushed towards and carried away by other chariots and foot-soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 27. Both of Dhritarashtra's sons, whose horses had been killed and whose bodies were pierced, were rushed towards and carried away by other chariots and foot-soldiers.

sanskrit: सर्वा दिशश्चाभ्यपतद्बीभत्सुरपराजितः ।किरीटमाली कौन्तेयो लब्धलक्षो महाबलः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sarvā diśaścābhyapatadbībhatsuraparājitaḥ ,kirīṭamālī kaunteyo labdhalakṣo mahābalaḥ.
english: The unconquered Bibhatsu (Arjuna), adorned with a crown and garlands, the son of Kunti, who had achieved his aim and possessed great strength, attacked all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 56 Verse 28. The unconquered Bibhatsu (Arjuna), adorned with a crown and garlands, the son of Kunti, who had achieved his aim and possessed great strength, attacked all directions.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथ संगम्य सर्वे तु कौरवाणां महारथाः ।अर्जुनं सहिता यत्ताः प्रत्ययुध्यन्त भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,atha saṁgamya sarve tu kauravāṇāṁ mahārathāḥ ,arjunaṁ sahitā yattāḥ pratyayudhyanta bhārata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, O Bhārata, all the great chariot warriors of the Kauravas, having gathered together and prepared, fought against Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, O Bhārata, all the great chariot warriors of the Kauravas, having gathered together and prepared, fought against Arjuna.

sanskrit: स सायकमयैर्जालैः सर्वतस्तान्महारथान् ।प्राच्छादयदमेयात्मा नीहार इव पर्वतान् ॥२॥
transliteration: sa sāyakamayairjālaiḥ sarvatastānmahārathān ,prācchādayadameyātmā nīhāra iva parvatān.
english: He, whose spirit was immeasurable, covered all those great chariot warriors on every side with networks made of arrows, just as fog covers mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 2. He, whose spirit was immeasurable, covered all those great chariot warriors on every side with networks made of arrows, just as fog covers mountains.

sanskrit: नदद्भिश्च महानागैर्हेषमाणैश्च वाजिभिः ।भेरीशङ्खनिनादैश्च स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥३॥
transliteration: nadadbhiśca mahānāgairheṣamāṇaiśca vājibhiḥ ,bherīśaṅkhaninādaiśca sa śabdastumulo'bhavat.
english: And by the roaring great elephants, by the neighing horses, and by the sounds of drums and conches, that sound became tumultuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 3. And by the roaring great elephants, by the neighing horses, and by the sounds of drums and conches, that sound became tumultuous.

sanskrit: नराश्वकायान्निर्भिद्य लोहानि कवचानि च ।पार्थस्य शरजालानि विनिष्पेतुः सहस्रशः ॥४॥
transliteration: narāśvakāyānnirbhidya lohāni kavacāni ca ,pārthasya śarajālāni viniṣpetuḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Arjuna's volleys of arrows, having pierced the bodies of men and horses as well as their iron armors, flew forth by the thousands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 4. Arjuna's volleys of arrows, having pierced the bodies of men and horses as well as their iron armors, flew forth by the thousands.

sanskrit: त्वरमाणः शरानस्यन्पाण्डवः स बभौ रणे ।मध्यंदिनगतोऽर्चिष्माञ्शरदीव दिवाकरः ॥५॥
transliteration: tvaramāṇaḥ śarānasyanpāṇḍavaḥ sa babhau raṇe ,madhyaṁdinagato'rciṣmāñśaradīva divākaraḥ.
english: That Pāṇḍava, quickly shooting arrows in battle, shone like the radiant sun at midday in autumn.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 5. That Pāṇḍava, quickly shooting arrows in battle, shone like the radiant sun at midday in autumn.

sanskrit: उपप्लवन्त वित्रस्ता रथेभ्यो रथिनस्तदा ।सादिनश्चाश्वपृष्ठेभ्यो भूमौ चापि पदातयः ॥६॥
transliteration: upaplavanta vitrastā rathebhyo rathinastadā ,sādinaścāśvapṛṣṭhebhyo bhūmau cāpi padātayaḥ.
english: Then, terrified, the chariot-warriors jumped from their chariots, the cavalrymen from their horses' backs, and even the foot soldiers (fell) to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 6. Then, terrified, the chariot-warriors jumped from their chariots, the cavalrymen from their horses' backs, and even the foot soldiers (fell) to the ground.

sanskrit: शरैः संताड्यमानानां कवचानां महात्मनाम् ।ताम्रराजतलोहानां प्रादुरासीन्महास्वनः ॥७॥
transliteration: śaraiḥ saṁtāḍyamānānāṁ kavacānāṁ mahātmanām ,tāmrarājatalohānāṁ prādurāsīnmahāsvanaḥ.
english: From the armors of the great warriors (mahātmanām), which were being struck by arrows, and which were made of copper, silver, and iron, a tremendous roar arose.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 7. From the armors of the great warriors (mahātmanām), which were being struck by arrows, and which were made of copper, silver, and iron, a tremendous roar arose.

sanskrit: छन्नमायोधनं सर्वं शरीरैर्गतचेतसाम् ।गजाश्वसादिभिस्तत्र शितबाणात्तजीवितैः ॥८॥
transliteration: channamāyodhanaṁ sarvaṁ śarīrairgatacetasām ,gajāśvasādibhistatra śitabāṇāttajīvitaiḥ.
english: The entire battlefield was covered there with the bodies of those whose consciousness had departed, and also by elephants, horses, and their riders, whose lives had been taken by sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 8. The entire battlefield was covered there with the bodies of those whose consciousness had departed, and also by elephants, horses, and their riders, whose lives had been taken by sharp arrows.

sanskrit: रथोपस्थाभिपतितैरास्तृता मानवैर्मही ।प्रनृत्यदिव संग्रामे चापहस्तो धनंजयः ॥९॥
transliteration: rathopasthābhipatitairāstṛtā mānavairmahī ,pranṛtyadiva saṁgrāme cāpahasto dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: The earth (mahī) was covered with men who had fallen from their chariot platforms. It was as if Dhananjaya (Arjuna), bow in hand, was dancing on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 9. The earth (mahī) was covered with men who had fallen from their chariot platforms. It was as if Dhananjaya (Arjuna), bow in hand, was dancing on the battlefield.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा गाण्डीवनिर्घोषं विस्फूर्जितमिवाशनेः ।त्रस्तानि सर्वभूतानि व्यगच्छन्त महाहवात् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śrutvā gāṇḍīvanirghoṣaṁ visphūrjitamivāśaneḥ ,trastāni sarvabhūtāni vyagacchanta mahāhavāt.
english: Hearing the tremendous roar of the Gaṇḍīva bow, which was like the pealing of a thunderbolt, all creatures (sarvabhūtāni) became terrified and fled from the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 10. Hearing the tremendous roar of the Gaṇḍīva bow, which was like the pealing of a thunderbolt, all creatures (sarvabhūtāni) became terrified and fled from the great battle.

sanskrit: कुण्डलोष्णीषधारीणि जातरूपस्रजानि च ।पतितानि स्म दृश्यन्ते शिरांसि रणमूर्धनि ॥११॥
transliteration: kuṇḍaloṣṇīṣadhārīṇi jātarūpasrajāni ca ,patitāni sma dṛśyante śirāṁsi raṇamūrdhani.
english: Heads, adorned with earrings and turbans, and wearing golden garlands, are indeed seen fallen on the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 11. Heads, adorned with earrings and turbans, and wearing golden garlands, are indeed seen fallen on the battlefield.

sanskrit: विशिखोन्मथितैर्गात्रैर्बाहुभिश्च सकार्मुकैः ।सहस्ताभरणैश्चान्यैः प्रच्छन्ना भाति मेदिनी ॥१२॥
transliteration: viśikhonmathitairgātrairbāhubhiśca sakārmukaiḥ ,sahastābharaṇaiścānyaiḥ pracchannā bhāti medinī.
english: The earth appears completely covered by bodies shattered by arrows, by arms still holding their bows, and by other limbs adorned with hand ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 12. The earth appears completely covered by bodies shattered by arrows, by arms still holding their bows, and by other limbs adorned with hand ornaments.

sanskrit: शिरसां पात्यमानानामन्तरा निशितैः शरैः ।अश्मवृष्टिरिवाकाशादभवद्भरतर्षभ ॥१३॥
transliteration: śirasāṁ pātyamānānāmantarā niśitaiḥ śaraiḥ ,aśmavṛṣṭirivākāśādabhavadbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, amidst the falling heads, it was as if a shower of stones descended from the sky due to the sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 13. O best of Bharatas, amidst the falling heads, it was as if a shower of stones descended from the sky due to the sharp arrows.

sanskrit: दर्शयित्वा तथात्मानं रौद्रं रुद्रपराक्रमः ।अवरुद्धश्चरन्पार्थो दशवर्षाणि त्रीणि च ।क्रोधाग्निमुत्सृजद्घोरं धार्तराष्ट्रेषु पाण्डवः ॥१४॥
transliteration: darśayitvā tathātmānaṁ raudraṁ rudraparākramaḥ ,avaruddhaścaranpārtho daśavarṣāṇi trīṇi ca ,krodhāgnimutsṛjadghoraṁ dhārtarāṣṭreṣu pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), who possessed the valor of Rudra, having thus displayed his fierce self (ātman) after being obstructed and living in exile for thirteen years (ten and three), was now unleashing a dreadful fire of anger upon the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 14. The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), who possessed the valor of Rudra, having thus displayed his fierce self (ātman) after being obstructed and living in exile for thirteen years (ten and three), was now unleashing a dreadful fire of anger upon the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्दहतः सैन्यं दृष्ट्वा चैव पराक्रमम् ।सर्वे शान्तिपरा योधा धार्तराष्ट्रस्य पश्यतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasya taddahataḥ sainyaṁ dṛṣṭvā caiva parākramam ,sarve śāntiparā yodhā dhārtarāṣṭrasya paśyataḥ.
english: Having witnessed his prowess and that destruction of the army by him, all the warriors of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son became inclined to peace, even as Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son himself watched.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 15. Having witnessed his prowess and that destruction of the army by him, all the warriors of Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son became inclined to peace, even as Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son himself watched.

sanskrit: वित्रासयित्वा तत्सैन्यं द्रावयित्वा महारथान् ।अर्जुनो जयतां श्रेष्ठः पर्यवर्तत भारत ॥१६॥
transliteration: vitrāsayitvā tatsainyaṁ drāvayitvā mahārathān ,arjuno jayatāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ paryavartata bhārata.
english: Having terrified that army and put the great charioteers to flight, Arjuna, the foremost among conquerors, turned back, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 16. Having terrified that army and put the great charioteers to flight, Arjuna, the foremost among conquerors, turned back, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: प्रावर्तयन्नदीं घोरां शोणितौघतरङ्गिणीम् ।अस्थिशैवलसंबाधां युगान्ते कालनिर्मिताम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: prāvartayannadīṁ ghorāṁ śoṇitaughataraṅgiṇīm ,asthiśaivalasaṁbādhāṁ yugānte kālanirmitām.
english: He caused a dreadful river to flow, its waves being torrents of blood, and its flow obstructed by moss-like bones, resembling one created by Time (Kāla) at the dissolution of an eon (yuga).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 17. He caused a dreadful river to flow, its waves being torrents of blood, and its flow obstructed by moss-like bones, resembling one created by Time (Kāla) at the dissolution of an eon (yuga).

sanskrit: शरचापप्लवां घोरां मांसशोणितकर्दमाम् ।महारथमहाद्वीपां शङ्खदुन्दुभिनिस्वनाम् ।चकार महतीं पार्थो नदीमुत्तरशोणिताम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: śaracāpaplavāṁ ghorāṁ māṁsaśoṇitakardamām ,mahārathamahādvīpāṁ śaṅkhadundubhinisvanām ,cakāra mahatīṁ pārtho nadīmuttaraśoṇitām.
english: Partha (Arjuna) created a vast, dreadful river, with overflowing blood, having arrows and bows as its floats, flesh and blood as its mire, great chariots as its mighty islands, and echoing with the sounds of conches and drums.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 18. Partha (Arjuna) created a vast, dreadful river, with overflowing blood, having arrows and bows as its floats, flesh and blood as its mire, great chariots as its mighty islands, and echoing with the sounds of conches and drums.

sanskrit: आददानस्य हि शरान्संधाय च विमुञ्चतः ।विकर्षतश्च गाण्डीवं न किंचिद्दृश्यतेऽन्तरम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: ādadānasya hi śarānsaṁdhāya ca vimuñcataḥ ,vikarṣataśca gāṇḍīvaṁ na kiṁciddṛśyate'ntaram.
english: Indeed, as he took the arrows, fixed them, released them, and drew the Gaṇḍīva bow, no interval was perceptible.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 57 Verse 19. Indeed, as he took the arrows, fixed them, released them, and drew the Gaṇḍīva bow, no interval was perceptible.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथ दुर्योधनः कर्णो दुःशासनविविंशती ।द्रोणश्च सह पुत्रेण कृपश्चातिरथो रणे ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,atha duryodhanaḥ karṇo duḥśāsanaviviṁśatī ,droṇaśca saha putreṇa kṛpaścātiratho raṇe.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Duryodhana, Karṇa, Duḥśāsana, Viviṃśati, Droṇa with his son, and Kṛpa, the great warrior, in the battle -
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Duryodhana, Karṇa, Duḥśāsana, Viviṃśati, Droṇa with his son, and Kṛpa, the great warrior, in the battle -

sanskrit: पुनरीयुः सुसंरब्धा धनंजयजिघांसया ।विस्फारयन्तश्चापानि बलवन्ति दृढानि च ॥२॥
transliteration: punarīyuḥ susaṁrabdhā dhanaṁjayajighāṁsayā ,visphārayantaścāpāni balavanti dṛḍhāni ca.
english: Highly enraged, they again advanced with the intent to kill Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), drawing their strong and sturdy bows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 2. Highly enraged, they again advanced with the intent to kill Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), drawing their strong and sturdy bows.

sanskrit: तान्प्रकीर्णपताकेन रथेनादित्यवर्चसा ।प्रत्युद्ययौ महाराज समस्तान्वानरध्वजः ॥३॥
transliteration: tānprakīrṇapatākena rathenādityavarcasā ,pratyudyayau mahārāja samastānvānaradhvajaḥ.
english: O great king, the one with the monkey banner (Arjuna) advanced to meet all of them with his chariot, which bore a fluttering banner and shone with sun-like splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 3. O great king, the one with the monkey banner (Arjuna) advanced to meet all of them with his chariot, which bore a fluttering banner and shone with sun-like splendor.

sanskrit: ततः कृपश्च कर्णश्च द्रोणश्च रथिनां वरः ।तं महास्त्रैर्महावीर्यं परिवार्य धनंजयम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛpaśca karṇaśca droṇaśca rathināṁ varaḥ ,taṁ mahāstrairmahāvīryaṁ parivārya dhanaṁjayam.
english: Then Kṛpa, Karṇa, and Droṇa, the best among charioteers, having surrounded that mighty Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) with great weapons,
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 4. Then Kṛpa, Karṇa, and Droṇa, the best among charioteers, having surrounded that mighty Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) with great weapons,

sanskrit: शरौघान्सम्यगस्यन्तो जीमूता इव वार्षिकाः ।ववर्षुः शरवर्षाणि प्रपतन्तं किरीटिनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: śaraughānsamyagasyanto jīmūtā iva vārṣikāḥ ,vavarṣuḥ śaravarṣāṇi prapatantaṁ kirīṭinam.
english: like rainy clouds, they showered torrents of arrows, properly unleashing continuous streams of shafts upon the advancing Kiriṭin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 5. like rainy clouds, they showered torrents of arrows, properly unleashing continuous streams of shafts upon the advancing Kiriṭin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: इषुभिर्बहुभिस्तूर्णं समरे लोमवाहिभिः ।अदूरात्पर्यवस्थाय पूरयामासुरादृताः ॥६॥
transliteration: iṣubhirbahubhistūrṇaṁ samare lomavāhibhiḥ ,adūrātparyavasthāya pūrayāmāsurādṛtāḥ.
english: From a short distance, having diligently taken up positions around him in battle, they quickly covered him with many piercing arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 6. From a short distance, having diligently taken up positions around him in battle, they quickly covered him with many piercing arrows.

sanskrit: तथावकीर्णस्य हि तैर्दिव्यैरस्त्रैः समन्ततः ।न तस्य द्व्यङ्गुलमपि विवृतं समदृश्यत ॥७॥
transliteration: tathāvakīrṇasya hi tairdivyairastraiḥ samantataḥ ,na tasya dvyaṅgulamapi vivṛtaṁ samadṛśyata.
english: Indeed, of him who was thus covered from all sides by those divine weapons, not even two fingers' breadth of his body was seen exposed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 7. Indeed, of him who was thus covered from all sides by those divine weapons, not even two fingers' breadth of his body was seen exposed.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्य बीभत्सुर्दिव्यमैन्द्रं महारथः ।अस्त्रमादित्यसंकाशं गाण्डीवे समयोजयत् ॥८॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasya bībhatsurdivyamaindraṁ mahārathaḥ ,astramādityasaṁkāśaṁ gāṇḍīve samayojayat.
english: Then, the great warrior (mahārathaḥ) Bhībhatsu, smiling (prahasya), affixed the divine (divyam) weapon (astra) of Indra (aindram), which shone like the sun (ādityasaṃkāśam), to his Gaṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 8. Then, the great warrior (mahārathaḥ) Bhībhatsu, smiling (prahasya), affixed the divine (divyam) weapon (astra) of Indra (aindram), which shone like the sun (ādityasaṃkāśam), to his Gaṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: स रश्मिभिरिवादित्यः प्रतपन्समरे बली ।किरीटमाली कौन्तेयः सर्वान्प्राच्छादयत्कुरून् ॥९॥
transliteration: sa raśmibhirivādityaḥ pratapansamare balī ,kirīṭamālī kaunteyaḥ sarvānprācchādayatkurūn.
english: Like the sun (ādityaḥ) with its rays (raśmibhiḥ), that powerful (balī) Kaunteya, adorned with a diadem and a garland (kirīṭamālī), shone brilliantly (pratapan) in battle (samare) and completely overwhelmed all the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 9. Like the sun (ādityaḥ) with its rays (raśmibhiḥ), that powerful (balī) Kaunteya, adorned with a diadem and a garland (kirīṭamālī), shone brilliantly (pratapan) in battle (samare) and completely overwhelmed all the Kurus.

sanskrit: यथा बलाहके विद्युत्पावको वा शिलोच्चये ।तथा गाण्डीवमभवदिन्द्रायुधमिवाततम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yathā balāhake vidyutpāvako vā śiloccaye ,tathā gāṇḍīvamabhavadindrāyudhamivātatam.
english: Just as lightning (vidyut) appears in a cloud (balāhake), or fire (pāvakaḥ) blazes on a mountain peak (śiloccaye), so too did Gaṇḍīva become vast (ātatam) and resemble Indra's bow (indrāyudham), the rainbow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 10. Just as lightning (vidyut) appears in a cloud (balāhake), or fire (pāvakaḥ) blazes on a mountain peak (śiloccaye), so too did Gaṇḍīva become vast (ātatam) and resemble Indra's bow (indrāyudham), the rainbow.

sanskrit: यथा वर्षति पर्जन्ये विद्युद्विभ्राजते दिवि ।तथा दश दिशः सर्वाः पतद्गाण्डीवमावृणोत् ॥११॥
transliteration: yathā varṣati parjanye vidyudvibhrājate divi ,tathā daśa diśaḥ sarvāḥ patadgāṇḍīvamāvṛṇot.
english: Just as lightning (vidyut) flashes in the sky (divi) when the rain-cloud (parjanye) rains (varṣati), so too did the flying Gaṇḍīva (patatgāṇḍīvam) cover all ten directions (diśaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 11. Just as lightning (vidyut) flashes in the sky (divi) when the rain-cloud (parjanye) rains (varṣati), so too did the flying Gaṇḍīva (patatgāṇḍīvam) cover all ten directions (diśaḥ).

sanskrit: त्रस्ताश्च रथिनः सर्वे बभूवुस्तत्र सर्वशः ।सर्वे शान्तिपरा भूत्वा स्वचित्तानि न लेभिरे ।संग्रामविमुखाः सर्वे योधास्ते हतचेतसः ॥१२॥
transliteration: trastāśca rathinaḥ sarve babhūvustatra sarvaśaḥ ,sarve śāntiparā bhūtvā svacittāni na lebhire ,saṁgrāmavimukhāḥ sarve yodhāste hatacetasaḥ.
english: All the charioteers there became utterly terrified. Everyone, yearning for peace, could not regain their composure. All those warriors, having turned away from battle, were utterly disheartened.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 12. All the charioteers there became utterly terrified. Everyone, yearning for peace, could not regain their composure. All those warriors, having turned away from battle, were utterly disheartened.

sanskrit: एवं सर्वाणि सैन्यानि भग्नानि भरतर्षभ ।प्राद्रवन्त दिशः सर्वा निराशानि स्वजीविते ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁ sarvāṇi sainyāni bhagnāni bharatarṣabha ,prādravanta diśaḥ sarvā nirāśāni svajīvite.
english: In this way, O best of Bharatas, all the armies were completely routed. They fled in every direction, having lost all hope for their own lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 58 Verse 13. In this way, O best of Bharatas, all the armies were completely routed. They fled in every direction, having lost all hope for their own lives.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः शांतनवो भीष्मो दुराधर्षः प्रतापवान् ।वध्यमानेषु योधेषु धनंजयमुपाद्रवत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ śāṁtanavo bhīṣmo durādharṣaḥ pratāpavān ,vadhyamāneṣu yodheṣu dhanaṁjayamupādravat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Bhishma, the son of Śāntanu, who was both unassailable and valorous, rushed towards Dhananjaya (Arjuna) while the warriors were being slaughtered.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, Bhishma, the son of Śāntanu, who was both unassailable and valorous, rushed towards Dhananjaya (Arjuna) while the warriors were being slaughtered.

sanskrit: प्रगृह्य कार्मुकश्रेष्ठं जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ।शरानादाय तीक्ष्णाग्रान्मर्मभेदप्रमाथिनः ॥२॥
transliteration: pragṛhya kārmukaśreṣṭhaṁ jātarūpapariṣkṛtam ,śarānādāya tīkṣṇāgrānmarmabhedapramāthinaḥ.
english: Grasping his finest bow, which was embellished with gold, and taking up sharp-tipped arrows that pierce vital spots and cause destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 2. Grasping his finest bow, which was embellished with gold, and taking up sharp-tipped arrows that pierce vital spots and cause destruction.

sanskrit: पाण्डुरेणातपत्रेण ध्रियमाणेन मूर्धनि ।शुशुभे स नरव्याघ्रो गिरिः सूर्योदये यथा ॥३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍureṇātapatreṇa dhriyamāṇena mūrdhani ,śuśubhe sa naravyāghro giriḥ sūryodaye yathā.
english: That tiger among men appeared splendid, with a white parasol held over his head, just like a mountain at sunrise.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 3. That tiger among men appeared splendid, with a white parasol held over his head, just like a mountain at sunrise.

sanskrit: प्रध्माय शङ्खं गाङ्गेयो धार्तराष्ट्रान्प्रहर्षयन् ।प्रदक्षिणमुपावृत्य बीभत्सुं समवारयत् ॥४॥
transliteration: pradhmāya śaṅkhaṁ gāṅgeyo dhārtarāṣṭrānpraharṣayan ,pradakṣiṇamupāvṛtya bībhatsuṁ samavārayat.
english: Having loudly blown his conch and delighted the sons of Dhritarashtra, Bhishma, the son of Ganga, circled clockwise and then checked Bibhatsu (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 4. Having loudly blown his conch and delighted the sons of Dhritarashtra, Bhishma, the son of Ganga, circled clockwise and then checked Bibhatsu (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तमुद्वीक्ष्य तथायान्तं कौन्तेयः परवीरहा ।प्रत्यगृह्णात्प्रहृष्टात्मा धाराधरमिवाचलः ॥५॥
transliteration: tamudvīkṣya tathāyāntaṁ kaunteyaḥ paravīrahā ,pratyagṛhṇātprahṛṣṭātmā dhārādharamivācalaḥ.
english: Having seen him thus approaching, Kaunteya (Arjuna), the slayer of enemy heroes, whose spirit (ātman) was delighted, faced him like an immovable mountain receiving a cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 5. Having seen him thus approaching, Kaunteya (Arjuna), the slayer of enemy heroes, whose spirit (ātman) was delighted, faced him like an immovable mountain receiving a cloud.

sanskrit: ततो भीष्मः शरानष्टौ ध्वजे पार्थस्य वीर्यवान् ।समपर्यन्महावेगाञ्श्वसमानानिवोरगान् ॥६॥
transliteration: tato bhīṣmaḥ śarānaṣṭau dhvaje pārthasya vīryavān ,samaparyanmahāvegāñśvasamānānivoragān.
english: Then the valiant Bhishma struck eight swiftly moving arrows, hissing like snakes, into Partha (Arjuna)'s banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 6. Then the valiant Bhishma struck eight swiftly moving arrows, hissing like snakes, into Partha (Arjuna)'s banner.

sanskrit: ते ध्वजं पाण्डुपुत्रस्य समासाद्य पतत्रिणः ।ज्वलन्तः कपिमाजघ्नुर्ध्वजाग्रनिलयांश्च तान् ॥७॥
transliteration: te dhvajaṁ pāṇḍuputrasya samāsādya patatriṇaḥ ,jvalantaḥ kapimājaghnurdhvajāgranilayāṁśca tān.
english: Those blazing, flying arrows, having reached the banner of Arjuna (Pāṇḍuputra), struck the ape (kapi) and also those dwelling at the top of the banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 7. Those blazing, flying arrows, having reached the banner of Arjuna (Pāṇḍuputra), struck the ape (kapi) and also those dwelling at the top of the banner.

sanskrit: ततो भल्लेन महता पृथुधारेण पाण्डवः ।छत्रं चिच्छेद भीष्मस्य तूर्णं तदपतद्भुवि ॥८॥
transliteration: tato bhallena mahatā pṛthudhāreṇa pāṇḍavaḥ ,chatraṁ ciccheda bhīṣmasya tūrṇaṁ tadapatadbhuvi.
english: Then, Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), with a great, broad-edged dart, swiftly cut off Bhishma's parasol, and it immediately fell to the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 8. Then, Arjuna (Pāṇḍava), with a great, broad-edged dart, swiftly cut off Bhishma's parasol, and it immediately fell to the ground.

sanskrit: ध्वजं चैवास्य कौन्तेयः शरैरभ्यहनद्दृढम् ।शीघ्रकृद्रथवाहांश्च तथोभौ पार्ष्णिसारथी ॥९॥
transliteration: dhvajaṁ caivāsya kaunteyaḥ śarairabhyahanaddṛḍham ,śīghrakṛdrathavāhāṁśca tathobhau pārṣṇisārathī.
english: And indeed, Arjuna (Kaunteya) firmly struck his (Bhishma's) banner with arrows. Swiftly acting, he also struck the chariot horses and both the charioteers (pārṣṇisārathi) at the rear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 9. And indeed, Arjuna (Kaunteya) firmly struck his (Bhishma's) banner with arrows. Swiftly acting, he also struck the chariot horses and both the charioteers (pārṣṇisārathi) at the rear.

sanskrit: तयोस्तदभवद्युद्धं तुमुलं लोमहर्षणम् ।भीष्मस्य सह पार्थेन बलिवासवयोरिव ॥१०॥
transliteration: tayostadabhavadyuddhaṁ tumulaṁ lomaharṣaṇam ,bhīṣmasya saha pārthena balivāsavayoriva.
english: That tumultuous, hair-raising battle (yuddha) took place between those two, Bhishma and Arjuna (Pārtha), just like the one between Bali and Indra (Vāsava).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 10. That tumultuous, hair-raising battle (yuddha) took place between those two, Bhishma and Arjuna (Pārtha), just like the one between Bali and Indra (Vāsava).

sanskrit: भल्लैर्भल्लाः समागम्य भीष्मपाण्डवयोर्युधि ।अन्तरिक्षे व्यराजन्त खद्योताः प्रावृषीव हि ॥११॥
transliteration: bhallairbhallāḥ samāgamya bhīṣmapāṇḍavayoryudhi ,antarikṣe vyarājanta khadyotāḥ prāvṛṣīva hi.
english: In the battle between Bhishma and the Pandavas, spears clashed with spears in the sky, shining brilliantly just like fireflies in the rainy season.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 11. In the battle between Bhishma and the Pandavas, spears clashed with spears in the sky, shining brilliantly just like fireflies in the rainy season.

sanskrit: अग्निचक्रमिवाविद्धं सव्यदक्षिणमस्यतः ।गाण्डीवमभवद्राजन्पार्थस्य सृजतः शरान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: agnicakramivāviddhaṁ savyadakṣiṇamasyataḥ ,gāṇḍīvamabhavadrājanpārthasya sṛjataḥ śarān.
english: O King, as Partha (Arjuna) discharged arrows, shooting them ambidextrously, his bow Gandiva appeared like a revolving wheel of fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 12. O King, as Partha (Arjuna) discharged arrows, shooting them ambidextrously, his bow Gandiva appeared like a revolving wheel of fire.

sanskrit: स तैः संछादयामास भीष्मं शरशतैः शितैः ।पर्वतं वारिधाराभिश्छादयन्निव तोयदः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ saṁchādayāmāsa bhīṣmaṁ śaraśataiḥ śitaiḥ ,parvataṁ vāridhārābhiśchādayanniva toyadaḥ.
english: He (Arjuna) completely covered Bhishma with hundreds of sharp arrows, just as a cloud covers a mountain with streams of rain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 13. He (Arjuna) completely covered Bhishma with hundreds of sharp arrows, just as a cloud covers a mountain with streams of rain.

sanskrit: तां स वेलामिवोद्धूतां शरवृष्टिं समुत्थिताम् ।व्यधमत्सायकैर्भीष्मो अर्जुनं संनिवारयत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: tāṁ sa velāmivoddhūtāṁ śaravṛṣṭiṁ samutthitām ,vyadhamatsāyakairbhīṣmo arjunaṁ saṁnivārayat.
english: Bhishma, restraining Arjuna, dispersed that raised shower of arrows, which was like an agitated tide, with his own arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 14. Bhishma, restraining Arjuna, dispersed that raised shower of arrows, which was like an agitated tide, with his own arrows.

sanskrit: ततस्तानि निकृत्तानि शरजालानि भागशः ।समरेऽभिव्यशीर्यन्त फल्गुनस्य रथं प्रति ॥१५॥
transliteration: tatastāni nikṛttāni śarajālāni bhāgaśaḥ ,samare'bhivyaśīryanta phalgunasya rathaṁ prati.
english: Then, those volleys of arrows, having been cut down into pieces in battle, were scattered towards Phalguna's (Arjuna's) chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 15. Then, those volleys of arrows, having been cut down into pieces in battle, were scattered towards Phalguna's (Arjuna's) chariot.

sanskrit: ततः कनकपुङ्खानां शरवृष्टिं समुत्थिताम् ।पाण्डवस्य रथात्तूर्णं शलभानामिवायतिम् ।व्यधमत्तां पुनस्तस्य भीष्मः शरशतैः शितैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kanakapuṅkhānāṁ śaravṛṣṭiṁ samutthitām ,pāṇḍavasya rathāttūrṇaṁ śalabhānāmivāyatim ,vyadhamattāṁ punastasya bhīṣmaḥ śaraśataiḥ śitaiḥ.
english: Then, Bhishma, with hundreds of sharp arrows, quickly dispersed that shower of golden-feathered arrows which had risen from Pāṇḍava's (Arjuna's) chariot, just like a swarm of locusts.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 16. Then, Bhishma, with hundreds of sharp arrows, quickly dispersed that shower of golden-feathered arrows which had risen from Pāṇḍava's (Arjuna's) chariot, just like a swarm of locusts.

sanskrit: ततस्ते कुरवः सर्वे साधु साध्विति चाब्रुवन् ।दुष्करं कृतवान्भीष्मो यदर्जुनमयोधयत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataste kuravaḥ sarve sādhu sādhviti cābruvan ,duṣkaraṁ kṛtavānbhīṣmo yadarjunamayodhayat.
english: Then all those Kurus exclaimed, "Excellent! Excellent!" and said that Bhishma had performed a difficult feat by fighting Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 17. Then all those Kurus exclaimed, "Excellent! Excellent!" and said that Bhishma had performed a difficult feat by fighting Arjuna.

sanskrit: बलवांस्तरुणो दक्षः क्षिप्रकारी च पाण्डवः ।कोऽन्यः समर्थः पार्थस्य वेगं धारयितुं रणे ॥१८॥
transliteration: balavāṁstaruṇo dakṣaḥ kṣiprakārī ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,ko'nyaḥ samarthaḥ pārthasya vegaṁ dhārayituṁ raṇe.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) is strong, young, skillful, and swift in action. Who else is capable of checking Pārtha's (Arjuna's) impetuosity in battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 18. The Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) is strong, young, skillful, and swift in action. Who else is capable of checking Pārtha's (Arjuna's) impetuosity in battle?

sanskrit: ऋते शांतनवाद्भीष्मात्कृष्णाद्वा देवकीसुतात् ।आचार्यप्रवराद्वापि भारद्वाजान्महाबलात् ॥१९॥
transliteration: ṛte śāṁtanavādbhīṣmātkṛṣṇādvā devakīsutāt ,ācāryapravarādvāpi bhāradvājānmahābalāt.
english: Except for Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu; or Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī; or even the mighty Droṇa, the foremost of teachers and descendant of Bharadvāja.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 19. Except for Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu; or Kṛṣṇa, the son of Devakī; or even the mighty Droṇa, the foremost of teachers and descendant of Bharadvāja.

sanskrit: अस्त्रैरस्त्राणि संवार्य क्रीडतः पुरुषर्षभौ ।चक्षूंषि सर्वभूतानां मोहयन्तौ महाबलौ ॥२०॥
transliteration: astrairastrāṇi saṁvārya krīḍataḥ puruṣarṣabhau ,cakṣūṁṣi sarvabhūtānāṁ mohayantau mahābalau.
english: Having parried other weapons with their own weapons, the two best among men play, bewildering the eyes of all beings, those two mighty ones.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 20. Having parried other weapons with their own weapons, the two best among men play, bewildering the eyes of all beings, those two mighty ones.

sanskrit: प्राजापत्यं तथैवैन्द्रमाग्नेयं च सुदारुणम् ।कौबेरं वारुणं चैव याम्यं वायव्यमेव च ।प्रयुञ्जानौ महात्मानौ समरे तौ विचेरतुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: prājāpatyaṁ tathaivaindramāgneyaṁ ca sudāruṇam ,kauberaṁ vāruṇaṁ caiva yāmyaṁ vāyavyameva ca ,prayuñjānau mahātmānau samare tau viceratuḥ.
english: Employing the weapons of Prajāpati, and similarly the very dreadful ones of Indra and Agni, as well as those of Kubera, Varuṇa, Yama, and Vāyu, those two great-souled individuals (mahātmānau) moved about in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 21. Employing the weapons of Prajāpati, and similarly the very dreadful ones of Indra and Agni, as well as those of Kubera, Varuṇa, Yama, and Vāyu, those two great-souled individuals (mahātmānau) moved about in battle.

sanskrit: विस्मितान्यथ भूतानि तौ दृष्ट्वा संयुगे तदा ।साधु पार्थ महाबाहो साधु भीष्मेति चाब्रुवन् ॥२२॥
transliteration: vismitānyatha bhūtāni tau dṛṣṭvā saṁyuge tadā ,sādhu pārtha mahābāho sādhu bhīṣmeti cābruvan.
english: Then, having seen those two in battle, all the astonished beings exclaimed, 'Well done, O Pārtha, mighty-armed one! Well done, O Bhīṣma!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 22. Then, having seen those two in battle, all the astonished beings exclaimed, 'Well done, O Pārtha, mighty-armed one! Well done, O Bhīṣma!'

sanskrit: नेदं युक्तं मनुष्येषु योऽयं संदृश्यते महान् ।महास्त्राणां संप्रयोगः समरे भीष्मपार्थयोः ॥२३॥
transliteration: nedaṁ yuktaṁ manuṣyeṣu yo'yaṁ saṁdṛśyate mahān ,mahāstrāṇāṁ saṁprayogaḥ samare bhīṣmapārthayoḥ.
english: This great engagement of powerful weapons, which is observed in the battle between Bhishma and Arjuna, is not proper among humans.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 23. This great engagement of powerful weapons, which is observed in the battle between Bhishma and Arjuna, is not proper among humans.

sanskrit: एवं सर्वास्त्रविदुषोरस्त्रयुद्धमवर्तत ।अथ जिष्णुरुपावृत्य पृथुधारेण कार्मुकम् ।चकर्त भीष्मस्य तदा जातरूपपरिष्कृतम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ sarvāstraviduṣorastrayuddhamavartata ,atha jiṣṇurupāvṛtya pṛthudhāreṇa kārmukam ,cakarta bhīṣmasya tadā jātarūpapariṣkṛtam.
english: Thus, a weapon-duel took place between the two who were skilled in all types of weapons. Then, Jishnu (Arjuna), having turned, instantly cut Bhishma's gold-adorned bow with a broad-edged (arrow).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 24. Thus, a weapon-duel took place between the two who were skilled in all types of weapons. Then, Jishnu (Arjuna), having turned, instantly cut Bhishma's gold-adorned bow with a broad-edged (arrow).

sanskrit: निमेषान्तरमात्रेण भीष्मोऽन्यत्कार्मुकं रणे ।समादाय महाबाहुः सज्यं चक्रे महाबलः ।शरांश्च सुबहून्क्रुद्धो मुमोचाशु धनंजये ॥२५॥
transliteration: nimeṣāntaramātreṇa bhīṣmo'nyatkārmukaṁ raṇe ,samādāya mahābāhuḥ sajyaṁ cakre mahābalaḥ ,śarāṁśca subahūnkruddho mumocāśu dhanaṁjaye.
english: In just a moment, the mighty-armed and very powerful Bhishma, taking another bow on the battlefield, strung it. Then, enraged, he swiftly released many arrows at Dhananjaya (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 25. In just a moment, the mighty-armed and very powerful Bhishma, taking another bow on the battlefield, strung it. Then, enraged, he swiftly released many arrows at Dhananjaya (Arjuna).

sanskrit: अर्जुनोऽपि शरांश्चित्रान्भीष्माय निशितान्बहून् ।चिक्षेप सुमहातेजास्तथा भीष्मश्च पाण्डवे ॥२६॥
transliteration: arjuno'pi śarāṁścitrānbhīṣmāya niśitānbahūn ,cikṣepa sumahātejāstathā bhīṣmaśca pāṇḍave.
english: The exceedingly mighty and radiant Arjuna also discharged many colorful and sharp arrows at Bhishma. Similarly, Bhishma (discharged arrows) at the Pandava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 26. The exceedingly mighty and radiant Arjuna also discharged many colorful and sharp arrows at Bhishma. Similarly, Bhishma (discharged arrows) at the Pandava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: तयोर्दिव्यास्त्रविदुषोरस्यतोरनिशं शरान् ।न विशेषस्तदा राजँल्लक्ष्यते स्म महात्मनोः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tayordivyāstraviduṣorasyatoraniśaṁ śarān ,na viśeṣastadā rājaँllakṣyate sma mahātmanoḥ.
english: O king, at that time, no distinction could be observed between those two great souls (mahātmanoḥ) who were skilled in divine weapons and constantly discharging arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 27. O king, at that time, no distinction could be observed between those two great souls (mahātmanoḥ) who were skilled in divine weapons and constantly discharging arrows.

sanskrit: अथावृणोद्दश दिशः शरैरतिरथस्तदा ।किरीटमाली कौन्तेयः शूरः शांतनवस्तथा ॥२८॥
transliteration: athāvṛṇoddaśa diśaḥ śarairatirathastadā ,kirīṭamālī kaunteyaḥ śūraḥ śāṁtanavastathā.
english: Then, at that time, the great charioteer (atirathaḥ) Arjuna, the son of Kunti (Kaunteya), who wore a crown and garland, covered the ten directions with arrows. Likewise, the brave warrior Bhishma, son of Shantanu (Śāntanava), also did the same.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 28. Then, at that time, the great charioteer (atirathaḥ) Arjuna, the son of Kunti (Kaunteya), who wore a crown and garland, covered the ten directions with arrows. Likewise, the brave warrior Bhishma, son of Shantanu (Śāntanava), also did the same.

sanskrit: अतीव पाण्डवो भीष्मं भीष्मश्चातीव पाण्डवम् ।बभूव तस्मिन्संग्रामे राजँल्लोके तदद्भुतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: atīva pāṇḍavo bhīṣmaṁ bhīṣmaścātīva pāṇḍavam ,babhūva tasminsaṁgrāme rājaँlloke tadadbhutam.
english: O king, in that battle, it was a great wonder (adbhutam) in the world (loke) how exceedingly Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, afflicted Bhishma, and Bhishma, in turn, exceedingly afflicted Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 29. O king, in that battle, it was a great wonder (adbhutam) in the world (loke) how exceedingly Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, afflicted Bhishma, and Bhishma, in turn, exceedingly afflicted Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: पाण्डवेन हताः शूरा भीष्मस्य रथरक्षिणः ।शेरते स्म तदा राजन्कौन्तेयस्याभितो रथम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavena hatāḥ śūrā bhīṣmasya ratharakṣiṇaḥ ,śerate sma tadā rājankaunteyasyābhito ratham.
english: O king, at that time, the brave chariot-guards of Bhishma, who were slain by Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, lay all around the chariot of Arjuna, the son of Kunti (Kaunteya).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 30. O king, at that time, the brave chariot-guards of Bhishma, who were slain by Arjuna, the Pāṇḍava, lay all around the chariot of Arjuna, the son of Kunti (Kaunteya).

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवनिर्मुक्ता निरमित्रं चिकीर्षवः ।आगच्छन्पुङ्खसंश्लिष्टाः श्वेतवाहनपत्रिणः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvanirmuktā niramitraṁ cikīrṣavaḥ ,āgacchanpuṅkhasaṁśliṣṭāḥ śvetavāhanapatriṇaḥ.
english: Then, the feathered arrows of Arjuna (Śvetavāhana), released from his Gāṇḍīva bow, intent on making the enemy disappear, came forth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 31. Then, the feathered arrows of Arjuna (Śvetavāhana), released from his Gāṇḍīva bow, intent on making the enemy disappear, came forth.

sanskrit: निष्पतन्तो रथात्तस्य धौता हैरण्यवाससः ।आकाशे समदृश्यन्त हंसानामिव पङ्क्तयः ॥३२॥
transliteration: niṣpatanto rathāttasya dhautā hairaṇyavāsasaḥ ,ākāśe samadṛśyanta haṁsānāmiva paṅktayaḥ.
english: Issuing forth from his chariot, those (arrows), shining and with golden coverings, appeared in the sky like rows of swans.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 32. Issuing forth from his chariot, those (arrows), shining and with golden coverings, appeared in the sky like rows of swans.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्दिव्यमस्त्रं हि प्रगाढं चित्रमस्यतः ।प्रेक्षन्ते स्मान्तरिक्षस्थाः सर्वे देवाः सवासवाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasya taddivyamastraṁ hi pragāḍhaṁ citramasyataḥ ,prekṣante smāntarikṣasthāḥ sarve devāḥ savāsavāḥ.
english: Indeed, all the gods, along with Indra (Vāsava), who were situated in the sky, observed him as he discharged that mighty and wondrous divine weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 33. Indeed, all the gods, along with Indra (Vāsava), who were situated in the sky, observed him as he discharged that mighty and wondrous divine weapon.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा परमप्रीतो गन्धर्वश्चित्रमद्भुतम् ।शशंस देवराजाय चित्रसेनः प्रतापवान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā paramaprīto gandharvaścitramadbhutam ,śaśaṁsa devarājāya citrasenaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Having seen that wondrous and astonishing thing, the mighty Gandharva Citrasena, who was exceedingly pleased, reported it to the king of gods (Devarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 34. Having seen that wondrous and astonishing thing, the mighty Gandharva Citrasena, who was exceedingly pleased, reported it to the king of gods (Devarāja).

sanskrit: पश्येमानरिनिर्दारान्संसक्तानिव गच्छतः ।चित्ररूपमिदं जिष्णोर्दिव्यमस्त्रमुदीर्यतः ॥३५॥
transliteration: paśyemānarinirdārānsaṁsaktāniva gacchataḥ ,citrarūpamidaṁ jiṣṇordivyamastramudīryataḥ.
english: Behold these destroyers of enemies, moving as if joined together. This is the wondrous, divine weapon of Jishnu (Arjuna) being unleashed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 35. Behold these destroyers of enemies, moving as if joined together. This is the wondrous, divine weapon of Jishnu (Arjuna) being unleashed.

sanskrit: नेदं मनुष्याः श्रद्दध्युर्न हीदं तेषु विद्यते ।पौराणानां महास्त्राणां विचित्रोऽयं समागमः ॥३६॥
transliteration: nedaṁ manuṣyāḥ śraddadhyurna hīdaṁ teṣu vidyate ,paurāṇānāṁ mahāstrāṇāṁ vicitro'yaṁ samāgamaḥ.
english: Humans would not believe this, for indeed, such power does not exist among them. This is a marvelous convergence of ancient, mighty weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 36. Humans would not believe this, for indeed, such power does not exist among them. This is a marvelous convergence of ancient, mighty weapons.

sanskrit: मध्यंदिनगतं सूर्यं प्रतपन्तमिवाम्बरे ।न शक्नुवन्ति सैन्यानि पाण्डवं प्रतिवीक्षितुम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: madhyaṁdinagataṁ sūryaṁ pratapantamivāmbare ,na śaknuvanti sainyāni pāṇḍavaṁ prativīkṣitum.
english: The armies are unable to gaze upon Pāṇḍu's son (Arjuna), who is like the midday sun intensely shining in the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 37. The armies are unable to gaze upon Pāṇḍu's son (Arjuna), who is like the midday sun intensely shining in the sky.

sanskrit: उभौ विश्रुतकर्माणावुभौ युद्धविशारदौ ।उभौ सदृशकर्माणावुभौ युधि दुरासदौ ॥३८॥
transliteration: ubhau viśrutakarmāṇāvubhau yuddhaviśāradau ,ubhau sadṛśakarmāṇāvubhau yudhi durāsadau.
english: Both are famous for their exploits, both are experts in warfare. Both perform comparable deeds, and both are unconquerable in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 38. Both are famous for their exploits, both are experts in warfare. Both perform comparable deeds, and both are unconquerable in battle.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो देवराजस्तु पार्थभीष्मसमागमम् ।पूजयामास दिव्येन पुष्पवर्षेण भारत ॥३९॥
transliteration: ityukto devarājastu pārthabhīṣmasamāgamam ,pūjayāmāsa divyena puṣpavarṣeṇa bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having been addressed thus, the king of the gods, Indra, then honored the meeting of Arjuna (Pārtha) and Bhishma with a divine shower of flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 39. O Bhārata, having been addressed thus, the king of the gods, Indra, then honored the meeting of Arjuna (Pārtha) and Bhishma with a divine shower of flowers.

sanskrit: ततो भीष्मः शांतनवो वामे पार्श्वे समर्पयत् ।अस्यतः प्रतिसंधाय विवृतं सव्यसाचिनः ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato bhīṣmaḥ śāṁtanavo vāme pārśve samarpayat ,asyataḥ pratisaṁdhāya vivṛtaṁ savyasācinaḥ.
english: Then Bhishma, the son of Shantanu, having fitted an arrow, struck an exposed point on the left side of the shooting Savyasācin (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 40. Then Bhishma, the son of Shantanu, having fitted an arrow, struck an exposed point on the left side of the shooting Savyasācin (Arjuna).

sanskrit: ततः प्रहस्य बीभत्सुः पृथुधारेण कार्मुकम् ।न्यकृन्तद्गार्ध्रपत्रेण भीष्मस्यामिततेजसः ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahasya bībhatsuḥ pṛthudhāreṇa kārmukam ,nyakṛntadgārdhrapatreṇa bhīṣmasyāmitatejasaḥ.
english: Then Bībhatsu (Arjuna), laughing aloud, cut down the bow (kārmukam) of the immensely energetic Bhishma with a broad-headed, vulture-feathered arrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 41. Then Bībhatsu (Arjuna), laughing aloud, cut down the bow (kārmukam) of the immensely energetic Bhishma with a broad-headed, vulture-feathered arrow.

sanskrit: अथैनं दशभिर्बाणैः प्रत्यविध्यत्स्तनान्तरे ।यतमानं पराक्रान्तं कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ॥४२॥
transliteration: athainaṁ daśabhirbāṇaiḥ pratyavidhyatstanāntare ,yatamānaṁ parākrāntaṁ kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ.
english: Then Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the son of Kunti, struck him (Bhishma)  -  who was valiantly striving  -  in the chest with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 42. Then Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the son of Kunti, struck him (Bhishma)  -  who was valiantly striving  -  in the chest with ten arrows.

sanskrit: स पीडितो महाबाहुर्गृहीत्वा रथकूबरम् ।गाङ्गेयो युधि दुर्धर्षस्तस्थौ दीर्घमिवातुरः ॥४३॥
transliteration: sa pīḍito mahābāhurgṛhītvā rathakūbaram ,gāṅgeyo yudhi durdharṣastasthau dīrghamivāturaḥ.
english: Though formidable in battle, the mighty-armed son of Ganga (Gāṅgeya), being distressed, stood for a long time as if ailing, clutching the chariot pole.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 43. Though formidable in battle, the mighty-armed son of Ganga (Gāṅgeya), being distressed, stood for a long time as if ailing, clutching the chariot pole.

sanskrit: तं विसंज्ञमपोवाह संयन्ता रथवाजिनाम् ।उपदेशमनुस्मृत्य रक्षमाणो महारथम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: taṁ visaṁjñamapovāha saṁyantā rathavājinām ,upadeśamanusmṛtya rakṣamāṇo mahāratham.
english: Recalling the instructions and protecting the great warrior (mahāratha), the charioteer, who controlled the chariot horses, drove him, now unconscious, away from the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 59 Verse 44. Recalling the instructions and protecting the great warrior (mahāratha), the charioteer, who controlled the chariot horses, drove him, now unconscious, away from the battlefield.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।भीष्मे तु संग्रामशिरो विहाय पलायमाने धृतराष्ट्रपुत्रः ।उच्छ्रित्य केतुं विनदन्महात्मा स्वयं विगृह्यार्जुनमाससाद ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,bhīṣme tu saṁgrāmaśiro vihāya; palāyamāne dhṛtarāṣṭraputraḥ ,ucchritya ketuṁ vinadanmahātmā; svayaṁ vigṛhyārjunamāsasāda.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When Bhishma retreated, abandoning the forefront of the battle, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), that great-souled (mahātmā) warrior, himself raised his banner, roaring loudly, and directly confronted Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When Bhishma retreated, abandoning the forefront of the battle, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), that great-souled (mahātmā) warrior, himself raised his banner, roaring loudly, and directly confronted Arjuna.

sanskrit: स भीमधन्वानमुदग्रवीर्यं धनंजयं शत्रुगणे चरन्तम् ।आकर्णपूर्णायतचोदितेन भल्लेन विव्याध ललाटमध्ये ॥२॥
transliteration: sa bhīmadhanvānamudagravīryaṁ; dhanaṁjayaṁ śatrugaṇe carantam ,ākarṇapūrṇāyatacoditena; bhallena vivyādha lalāṭamadhye.
english: He (Duryodhana) pierced Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), who wielded a formidable bow and displayed immense valor while moving amidst the enemy ranks. He struck him in the center of the forehead with a broad-headed arrow, shot with a full draw pulled up to his ear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 2. He (Duryodhana) pierced Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), who wielded a formidable bow and displayed immense valor while moving amidst the enemy ranks. He struck him in the center of the forehead with a broad-headed arrow, shot with a full draw pulled up to his ear.

sanskrit: स तेन बाणेन समर्पितेन जाम्बूनदाभेन सुसंशितेन ।रराज राजन्महनीयकर्मा यथैकपर्वा रुचिरैकशृङ्गः ॥३॥
transliteration: sa tena bāṇena samarpitena; jāmbūnadābhena susaṁśitena ,rarāja rājanmahanīyakarmā; yathaikaparvā ruciraikaśṛṅgaḥ.
english: O King, he, whose actions were praiseworthy, shone brightly with that well-sharpened, golden-hued arrow that had been discharged, just like a splendid mountain with a single peak.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 3. O King, he, whose actions were praiseworthy, shone brightly with that well-sharpened, golden-hued arrow that had been discharged, just like a splendid mountain with a single peak.

sanskrit: अथास्य बाणेन विदारितस्य प्रादुर्बभूवासृगजस्रमुष्णम् ।सा तस्य जाम्बूनदपुष्पचित्रा मालेव चित्राभिविराजते स्म ॥४॥
transliteration: athāsya bāṇena vidāritasya; prādurbabhūvāsṛgajasramuṣṇam ,sā tasya jāmbūnadapuṣpacitrā; māleva citrābhivirājate sma.
english: Then, from him, pierced by the arrow, hot, continuous blood gushed forth. That blood, variegated with golden flowers, shone splendidly on him like a garland.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 4. Then, from him, pierced by the arrow, hot, continuous blood gushed forth. That blood, variegated with golden flowers, shone splendidly on him like a garland.

sanskrit: स तेन बाणाभिहतस्तरस्वी दुर्योधनेनोद्धतमन्युवेगः ।शरानुपादाय विषाग्निकल्पान्विव्याध राजानमदीनसत्त्वः ॥५॥
transliteration: sa tena bāṇābhihatastarasvī; duryodhanenoddhatamanyuvegaḥ ,śarānupādāya viṣāgnikalpā;nvivyādha rājānamadīnasattvaḥ.
english: Struck by that arrow from Duryodhana, the powerful one, whose surge of anger was greatly aroused, then, with unyielding courage, took up arrows like poison and fire and pierced King Duryodhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 5. Struck by that arrow from Duryodhana, the powerful one, whose surge of anger was greatly aroused, then, with unyielding courage, took up arrows like poison and fire and pierced King Duryodhana.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनश्चापि तमुग्रतेजाः पार्थश्च दुर्योधनमेकवीरः ।अन्योन्यमाजौ पुरुषप्रवीरौ समं समाजघ्नतुराजमीढौ ॥६॥
transliteration: duryodhanaścāpi tamugratejāḥ; pārthaśca duryodhanamekavīraḥ ,anyonyamājau puruṣapravīrau; samaṁ samājaghnaturājamīḍhau.
english: And Duryodhana, endowed with fierce energy, also struck him (Bhīṣma), while Pārtha (Arjuna), the unique hero, struck Duryodhana. These two excellent heroes among men, both descendants of Ajamiḍha, thus equally struck each other in that battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 6. And Duryodhana, endowed with fierce energy, also struck him (Bhīṣma), while Pārtha (Arjuna), the unique hero, struck Duryodhana. These two excellent heroes among men, both descendants of Ajamiḍha, thus equally struck each other in that battle.

sanskrit: ततः प्रभिन्नेन महागजेन महीधराभेन पुनर्विकर्णः ।रथैश्चतुर्भिर्गजपादरक्षैः कुन्तीसुतं जिष्णुमथाभ्यधावत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prabhinnena mahāgajena; mahīdharābhena punarvikarṇaḥ ,rathaiścaturbhirgajapādarakṣaiḥ; kuntīsutaṁ jiṣṇumathābhyadhāvat.
english: Then, Vikarna, accompanied by a colossal, rutting elephant resembling a mountain, and supported by four chariots with elephant-foot guards, once again charged towards Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), the son of Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 7. Then, Vikarna, accompanied by a colossal, rutting elephant resembling a mountain, and supported by four chariots with elephant-foot guards, once again charged towards Arjuna (Jiṣṇu), the son of Kunti.

sanskrit: तमापतन्तं त्वरितं गजेन्द्रं धनंजयः कुम्भविभागमध्ये ।आकर्णपूर्णेन दृढायसेन बाणेन विव्याध महाजवेन ॥८॥
transliteration: tamāpatantaṁ tvaritaṁ gajendraṁ; dhanaṁjayaḥ kumbhavibhāgamadhye ,ākarṇapūrṇena dṛḍhāyasena; bāṇena vivyādha mahājavena.
english: Dhananjaya (Arjuna), with a mighty surge of speed, pierced that rapidly approaching lordly elephant (gajendra) in the middle of its temporal lobes, using a strong iron arrow drawn fully to his ear.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 8. Dhananjaya (Arjuna), with a mighty surge of speed, pierced that rapidly approaching lordly elephant (gajendra) in the middle of its temporal lobes, using a strong iron arrow drawn fully to his ear.

sanskrit: पार्थेन सृष्टः स तु गार्ध्रपत्र आ पुङ्खदेशात्प्रविवेश नागम् ।विदार्य शैलप्रवरप्रकाशं यथाशनिः पर्वतमिन्द्रसृष्टः ॥९॥
transliteration: pārthena sṛṣṭaḥ sa tu gārdhrapatra; ā puṅkhadeśātpraviveśa nāgam ,vidārya śailapravaraprakāśaṁ; yathāśaniḥ parvatamindrasṛṣṭaḥ.
english: That arrow, released by Arjuna (Pārtha) and adorned with vulture feathers, penetrated the elephant - which shone like a magnificent mountain - right up to its fletching, splitting it apart, just as Indra's thunderbolt cleaves a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 9. That arrow, released by Arjuna (Pārtha) and adorned with vulture feathers, penetrated the elephant - which shone like a magnificent mountain - right up to its fletching, splitting it apart, just as Indra's thunderbolt cleaves a mountain.

sanskrit: शरप्रतप्तः स तु नागराजः प्रवेपिताङ्गो व्यथितान्तरात्मा ।संसीदमानो निपपात मह्यां वज्राहतं शृङ्गमिवाचलस्य ॥१०॥
transliteration: śaraprataptaḥ sa tu nāgarājaḥ; pravepitāṅgo vyathitāntarātmā ,saṁsīdamāno nipapāta mahyāṁ; vajrāhataṁ śṛṅgamivācalasya.
english: That lordly elephant (nāgarāja), severely afflicted by the arrow, with its limbs trembling and its inner being (ātman) distressed, collapsed and fell to the ground, just like a mountain peak struck by a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 10. That lordly elephant (nāgarāja), severely afflicted by the arrow, with its limbs trembling and its inner being (ātman) distressed, collapsed and fell to the ground, just like a mountain peak struck by a thunderbolt.
