sanskrit: निपातिते दन्तिवरे पृथिव्यां त्रासाद्विकर्णः सहसावतीर्य ।तूर्णं पदान्यष्टशतानि गत्वा विविंशतेः स्यन्दनमारुरोह ॥११॥
transliteration: nipātite dantivare pṛthivyāṁ; trāsādvikarṇaḥ sahasāvatīrya ,tūrṇaṁ padānyaṣṭaśatāni gatvā; viviṁśateḥ syandanamāruroha.
english: When the chief elephant was struck down on the earth, Vikarna, out of fear, quickly dismounted. Having swiftly gone eight hundred paces, he mounted Vivimshati's chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 11. When the chief elephant was struck down on the earth, Vikarna, out of fear, quickly dismounted. Having swiftly gone eight hundred paces, he mounted Vivimshati's chariot.

sanskrit: निहत्य नागं तु शरेण तेन वज्रोपमेनाद्रिवराम्बुदाभम् ।तथाविधेनैव शरेण पार्थो दुर्योधनं वक्षसि निर्बिभेद ॥१२॥
transliteration: nihatya nāgaṁ tu śareṇa tena; vajropamenādrivarāmbudābham ,tathāvidhenaiva śareṇa pārtho; duryodhanaṁ vakṣasi nirbibheda.
english: And having killed the elephant (nāga) with that arrow, which was like a thunderbolt, and which resembled a great mountain cloud, Partha (Arjuna) then pierced Duryodhana in the chest with an arrow of the very same kind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 12. And having killed the elephant (nāga) with that arrow, which was like a thunderbolt, and which resembled a great mountain cloud, Partha (Arjuna) then pierced Duryodhana in the chest with an arrow of the very same kind.

sanskrit: ततो गजे राजनि चैव भिन्ने भग्ने विकर्णे च सपादरक्षे ।गाण्डीवमुक्तैर्विशिखैः प्रणुन्नास्ते योधमुख्याः सहसापजग्मुः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato gaje rājani caiva bhinne; bhagne vikarṇe ca sapādarakṣe ,gāṇḍīvamuktairviśikhaiḥ praṇunnā;ste yodhamukhyāḥ sahasāpajagmuḥ.
english: Then, with the king's elephant struck and Vikarna, along with his attendants, defeated, those chief warriors, pierced by arrows released from the Gāṇḍīva (bow), suddenly fled.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 13. Then, with the king's elephant struck and Vikarna, along with his attendants, defeated, those chief warriors, pierced by arrows released from the Gāṇḍīva (bow), suddenly fled.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वैव बाणेन हतं तु नागं योधांश्च सर्वान्द्रवतो निशम्य ।रथं समावृत्य कुरुप्रवीरो रणात्प्रदुद्राव यतो न पार्थः ॥१४॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvaiva bāṇena hataṁ tu nāgaṁ; yodhāṁśca sarvāndravato niśamya ,rathaṁ samāvṛtya kurupravīro; raṇātpradudrāva yato na pārthaḥ.
english: And indeed, having seen the elephant (nāga) killed by an arrow, and having perceived all the warriors fleeing, the best of the Kurus (Duryodhana), turning his chariot around, fled from the battle to a place where Partha (Arjuna) was not.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 14. And indeed, having seen the elephant (nāga) killed by an arrow, and having perceived all the warriors fleeing, the best of the Kurus (Duryodhana), turning his chariot around, fled from the battle to a place where Partha (Arjuna) was not.

sanskrit: तं भीमरूपं त्वरितं द्रवन्तं दुर्योधनं शत्रुसहो निषङ्गी ।प्राक्ष्वेडयद्योद्धुमनाः किरीटी बाणेन विद्धं रुधिरं वमन्तम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: taṁ bhīmarūpaṁ tvaritaṁ dravantaṁ; duryodhanaṁ śatrusaho niṣaṅgī ,prākṣveḍayadyoddhumanāḥ kirīṭī; bāṇena viddhaṁ rudhiraṁ vamantam.
english: Arjuna, the wearer of the diadem (kirīṭī), bearing a quiver and determined to fight, challenged Duryodhana, who had a terrifying appearance, was rapidly fleeing, pierced by an arrow, and vomiting blood.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 15. Arjuna, the wearer of the diadem (kirīṭī), bearing a quiver and determined to fight, challenged Duryodhana, who had a terrifying appearance, was rapidly fleeing, pierced by an arrow, and vomiting blood.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।विहाय कीर्तिं विपुलं यशश्च युद्धात्परावृत्य पलायसे किम् ।न तेऽद्य तूर्याणि समाहतानि यथावदुद्यान्ति गतस्य युद्धे ॥१६॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,vihāya kīrtiṁ vipulaṁ yaśaśca; yuddhātparāvṛtya palāyase kim ,na te'dya tūryāṇi samāhatāni; yathāvadudyānti gatasya yuddhe.
english: Arjuna said: Why do you flee, abandoning great fame and glory and turning back from battle? Today, your war-drums do not sound properly for one who has fled the fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 16. Arjuna said: Why do you flee, abandoning great fame and glory and turning back from battle? Today, your war-drums do not sound properly for one who has fled the fight.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्यास्मि निदेशकारी पार्थस्तृतीयो युधि च स्थिरोऽस्मि ।तदर्थमावृत्य मुखं प्रयच्छ नरेन्द्रवृत्तं स्मर धार्तराष्ट्र ॥१७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirasyāsmi nideśakārī; pārthastṛtīyo yudhi ca sthiro'smi ,tadarthamāvṛtya mukhaṁ prayaccha; narendravṛttaṁ smara dhārtarāṣṭra.
english: I am obedient to Yudhiṣṭhira's commands. I am the third Pārtha (Arjuna), and I am resolute in battle. Therefore, turn your face (to fight) for that very purpose. O son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, remember the conduct of kings!
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 17. I am obedient to Yudhiṣṭhira's commands. I am the third Pārtha (Arjuna), and I am resolute in battle. Therefore, turn your face (to fight) for that very purpose. O son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, remember the conduct of kings!

sanskrit: मोघं तवेदं भुवि नामधेयं दुर्योधनेतीह कृतं पुरस्तात् ।न हीह दुर्योधनता तवास्ति पलायमानस्य रणं विहाय ॥१८॥
transliteration: moghaṁ tavedaṁ bhuvi nāmadheyaṁ; duryodhanetīha kṛtaṁ purastāt ,na hīha duryodhanatā tavāsti; palāyamānasya raṇaṁ vihāya.
english: This name 'Duryodhana' (meaning "difficult to fight"), which was given to you on earth in the past, is now meaningless. For indeed, there is no 'Duryodhana-ness' in you, who are abandoning the battle and fleeing.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 18. This name 'Duryodhana' (meaning "difficult to fight"), which was given to you on earth in the past, is now meaningless. For indeed, there is no 'Duryodhana-ness' in you, who are abandoning the battle and fleeing.

sanskrit: न ते पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतो वा पश्यामि दुर्योधन रक्षितारम् ।परैहि युद्धेन कुरुप्रवीर प्राणान्प्रियान्पाण्डवतोऽद्य रक्ष ॥१९॥
transliteration: na te purastādatha pṛṣṭhato vā; paśyāmi duryodhana rakṣitāram ,paraihi yuddhena kurupravīra; prāṇānpriyānpāṇḍavato'dya rakṣa.
english: O Duryodhana, I see no protector for you, neither in front nor behind. O best of Kurus, withdraw from this battle. Today, protect your dear lives from the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 60 Verse 19. O Duryodhana, I see no protector for you, neither in front nor behind. O best of Kurus, withdraw from this battle. Today, protect your dear lives from the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।आहूयमानस्तु स तेन संख्ये महामना धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रः ।निवर्तितस्तस्य गिराङ्कुशेन गजो यथा मत्त इवाङ्कुशेन ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,āhūyamānastu sa tena saṁkhye; mahāmanā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraḥ ,nivartitastasya girāṅkuśena; gajo yathā matta ivāṅkuśena.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: But the proud son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having been addressed by him in battle, turned back due to his speech, just as a maddened elephant is turned by a goad.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: But the proud son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, having been addressed by him in battle, turned back due to his speech, just as a maddened elephant is turned by a goad.

sanskrit: सोऽमृष्यमाणो वचसाभिमृष्टो महारथेनातिरथस्तरस्वी ।पर्याववर्ताथ रथेन वीरो भोगी यथा पादतलाभिमृष्टः ॥२॥
transliteration: so'mṛṣyamāṇo vacasābhimṛṣṭo; mahārathenātirathastarasvī ,paryāvavartātha rathena vīro; bhogī yathā pādatalābhimṛṣṭaḥ.
english: That swift and mighty warrior (Atirathi), unable to tolerate being provoked by the words of the great warrior, then turned his chariot around, that hero, just as a serpent is provoked when touched by the sole of a foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 2. That swift and mighty warrior (Atirathi), unable to tolerate being provoked by the words of the great warrior, then turned his chariot around, that hero, just as a serpent is provoked when touched by the sole of a foot.

sanskrit: तं प्रेक्ष्य कर्णः परिवर्तमानं निवर्त्य संस्तभ्य च विद्धगात्रः ।दुर्योधनं दक्षिणतोऽभ्यगच्छत्पार्थं नृवीरो युधि हेममाली ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ prekṣya karṇaḥ parivartamānaṁ; nivartya saṁstabhya ca viddhagātraḥ ,duryodhanaṁ dakṣiṇato'bhyagaccha;tpārthaṁ nṛvīro yudhi hemamālī.
english: Upon seeing him (Duryodhana) turning around, Karṇa, a hero among men wearing a golden garland, and having a wounded body, halted and steadied himself. Then he approached Duryodhana from the right side, [ready to face] Pārtha in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 3. Upon seeing him (Duryodhana) turning around, Karṇa, a hero among men wearing a golden garland, and having a wounded body, halted and steadied himself. Then he approached Duryodhana from the right side, [ready to face] Pārtha in battle.

sanskrit: भीष्मस्ततः शांतनवो निवृत्य हिरण्यकक्ष्यांस्त्वरयंस्तुरंगान् ।दुर्योधनं पश्चिमतोऽभ्यरक्षत्पार्थान्महाबाहुरधिज्यधन्वा ॥४॥
transliteration: bhīṣmastataḥ śāṁtanavo nivṛtya; hiraṇyakakṣyāṁstvarayaṁsturaṁgān ,duryodhanaṁ paścimato'bhyarakṣa;tpārthānmahābāhuradhijyadhanvā.
english: Then, Bhishma, the son of Shantanu, having turned his gold-trapped horses, quickly protected Duryodhana from Arjuna (Pārtha) from the west. He was mighty-armed and had his bow strung.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 4. Then, Bhishma, the son of Shantanu, having turned his gold-trapped horses, quickly protected Duryodhana from Arjuna (Pārtha) from the west. He was mighty-armed and had his bow strung.

sanskrit: द्रोणः कृपश्चैव विविंशतिश्च दुःशासनश्चैव निवृत्य शीघ्रम् ।सर्वे पुरस्ताद्विततेषुचापा दुर्योधनार्थं त्वरिताभ्युपेयुः ॥५॥
transliteration: droṇaḥ kṛpaścaiva viviṁśatiśca; duḥśāsanaścaiva nivṛtya śīghram ,sarve purastādvitateṣucāpā; duryodhanārthaṁ tvaritābhyupeyuḥ.
english: Drona, Kripa, Vivimshati, and Duhshasana, all quickly turned back. With their bows strung, all of them swiftly advanced from the front for Duryodhana's protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 5. Drona, Kripa, Vivimshati, and Duhshasana, all quickly turned back. With their bows strung, all of them swiftly advanced from the front for Duryodhana's protection.

sanskrit: स तान्यनीकानि निवर्तमानान्यालोक्य पूर्णौघनिभानि पार्थः ।हंसो यथा मेघमिवापतन्तं धनंजयः प्रत्यपतत्तरस्वी ॥६॥
transliteration: sa tānyanīkāni nivartamānā;nyālokya pūrṇaughanibhāni pārthaḥ ,haṁso yathā meghamivāpatantaṁ; dhanaṁjayaḥ pratyapatattarasvī.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha), upon seeing those returning armies, which resembled a surging flood, then, powerful and swift, counter-attacked them, just as a swan would attack an approaching cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 6. Arjuna (Pārtha), upon seeing those returning armies, which resembled a surging flood, then, powerful and swift, counter-attacked them, just as a swan would attack an approaching cloud.

sanskrit: ते सर्वतः संपरिवार्य पार्थमस्त्राणि दिव्यानि समाददानाः ।ववर्षुरभ्येत्य शरैः समन्तान्मेघा यथा भूधरमम्बुवेगैः ॥७॥
transliteration: te sarvataḥ saṁparivārya pārtha;mastrāṇi divyāni samādadānāḥ ,vavarṣurabhyetya śaraiḥ samantā;nmeghā yathā bhūdharamambuvegaiḥ.
english: They (the warriors), having surrounded Arjuna (Pārtha) from all directions, and wielding divine weapons, then approached him and showered him with arrows from all sides, just as clouds drench a mountain with downpours of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 7. They (the warriors), having surrounded Arjuna (Pārtha) from all directions, and wielding divine weapons, then approached him and showered him with arrows from all sides, just as clouds drench a mountain with downpours of water.

sanskrit: ततोऽस्त्रमस्त्रेण निवार्य तेषां गाण्डीवधन्वा कुरुपुंगवानाम् ।संमोहनं शत्रुसहोऽन्यदस्त्रं प्रादुश्चकारैन्द्रिरपारणीयम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tato'stramastreṇa nivārya teṣāṁ; gāṇḍīvadhanvā kurupuṁgavānām ,saṁmohanaṁ śatrusaho'nyadastraṁ; prāduścakāraindrirapāraṇīyam.
english: Then, Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, who was among those foremost Kurus and capable of overcoming enemies, first repelled their weapon with his own. After that, he manifested another irresistible weapon, the saṃmohana (stupefying) weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 8. Then, Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow, who was among those foremost Kurus and capable of overcoming enemies, first repelled their weapon with his own. After that, he manifested another irresistible weapon, the saṃmohana (stupefying) weapon.

sanskrit: ततो दिशश्चानुदिशो विवृत्य शरैः सुधारैर्निशितैः सुपुङ्खैः ।गाण्डीवघोषेण मनांसि तेषां महाबलः प्रव्यथयां चकार ॥९॥
transliteration: tato diśaścānudiśo vivṛtya; śaraiḥ sudhārairniśitaiḥ supuṅkhaiḥ ,gāṇḍīvaghoṣeṇa manāṁsi teṣāṁ; mahābalaḥ pravyathayāṁ cakāra.
english: Then, the immensely powerful Arjuna, having enveloped all the cardinal and intermediate directions with his sharp, excellent, and well-feathered arrows, also distressed the minds of the enemies with the reverberating sound of his Gāṇḍīva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 9. Then, the immensely powerful Arjuna, having enveloped all the cardinal and intermediate directions with his sharp, excellent, and well-feathered arrows, also distressed the minds of the enemies with the reverberating sound of his Gāṇḍīva bow.

sanskrit: ततः पुनर्भीमरवं प्रगृह्य दोर्भ्यां महाशङ्खमुदारघोषम् ।व्यनादयत्स प्रदिशो दिशः खं भुवं च पार्थो द्विषतां निहन्ता ॥१०॥
transliteration: tataḥ punarbhīmaravaṁ pragṛhya; dorbhyāṁ mahāśaṅkhamudāraghoṣam ,vyanādayatsa pradiśo diśaḥ khaṁ; bhuvaṁ ca pārtho dviṣatāṁ nihantā.
english: Then, Partha (Arjuna), the destroyer of enemies, once again took up the great conch, which had a mighty roar, and with both his arms, he made it resound, filling the cardinal directions, intermediate directions, the sky, and the earth with its sound.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 10. Then, Partha (Arjuna), the destroyer of enemies, once again took up the great conch, which had a mighty roar, and with both his arms, he made it resound, filling the cardinal directions, intermediate directions, the sky, and the earth with its sound.

sanskrit: ते शङ्खनादेन कुरुप्रवीराः संमोहिताः पार्थसमीरितेन ।उत्सृज्य चापानि दुरासदानि सर्वे तदा शान्तिपरा बभूवुः ॥११॥
transliteration: te śaṅkhanādena kurupravīrāḥ; saṁmohitāḥ pārthasamīritena ,utsṛjya cāpāni durāsadāni; sarve tadā śāntiparā babhūvuḥ.
english: Then, those Kuru heroes, bewildered by the sound of the conch blown by Partha (Arjuna), abandoned their formidable bows. All of them became calm and sought peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 11. Then, those Kuru heroes, bewildered by the sound of the conch blown by Partha (Arjuna), abandoned their formidable bows. All of them became calm and sought peace.

sanskrit: तथा विसंज्ञेषु परेषु पार्थः स्मृत्वा तु वाक्यानि तथोत्तरायाः ।निर्याहि मध्यादिति मत्स्यपुत्रमुवाच यावत्कुरवो विसंज्ञाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: tathā visaṁjñeṣu pareṣu pārthaḥ; smṛtvā tu vākyāni tathottarāyāḥ ,niryāhi madhyāditi matsyaputra;muvāca yāvatkuravo visaṁjñāḥ.
english: While his enemies were unconscious, Partha (Arjuna), remembering Uttarā's words, told the son of Matsya (Uttara), "Go out from the midst as long as the Kurus remain unconscious."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 12. While his enemies were unconscious, Partha (Arjuna), remembering Uttarā's words, told the son of Matsya (Uttara), "Go out from the midst as long as the Kurus remain unconscious."

sanskrit: आचार्य शारद्वतयोः सुशुक्ले कर्णस्य पीतं रुचिरं च वस्त्रम् ।द्रौणेश्च राज्ञश्च तथैव नीले वस्त्रे समादत्स्व नरप्रवीर ॥१३॥
transliteration: ācārya śāradvatayoḥ suśukle; karṇasya pītaṁ ruciraṁ ca vastram ,drauṇeśca rājñaśca tathaiva nīle; vastre samādatsva narapravīra.
english: O best among men (Uttara), take the very white garments for the teacher (Droṇa) and Śāradvata (Kṛpa), the beautiful yellow garment for Karṇa, and similarly, the two blue garments for Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) and the king (Duryodhana).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 13. O best among men (Uttara), take the very white garments for the teacher (Droṇa) and Śāradvata (Kṛpa), the beautiful yellow garment for Karṇa, and similarly, the two blue garments for Droṇi (Aśvatthāman) and the king (Duryodhana).

sanskrit: भीष्मस्य संज्ञां तु तथैव मन्ये जानाति मेऽस्त्रप्रतिघातमेषः ।एतस्य वाहान्कुरु सव्यतस्त्वमेवं हि यातव्यममूढसंज्ञैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhīṣmasya saṁjñāṁ tu tathaiva manye; jānāti me'strapratighātameṣaḥ ,etasya vāhānkuru savyatastva;mevaṁ hi yātavyamamūḍhasaṁjñaiḥ.
english: "I believe Bhishma's distinguishing mark is the same, as he alone knows how to counter my weapons. Therefore, turn his horses to the left, for this is how one must proceed against those who are not unconscious."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 14. "I believe Bhishma's distinguishing mark is the same, as he alone knows how to counter my weapons. Therefore, turn his horses to the left, for this is how one must proceed against those who are not unconscious."

sanskrit: रश्मीन्समुत्सृज्य ततो महात्मा रथादवप्लुत्य विराटपुत्रः ।वस्त्राण्युपादाय महारथानां तूर्णं पुनः स्वं रथमारुरोह ॥१५॥
transliteration: raśmīnsamutsṛjya tato mahātmā; rathādavaplutya virāṭaputraḥ ,vastrāṇyupādāya mahārathānāṁ; tūrṇaṁ punaḥ svaṁ rathamāruroha.
english: Having released the reins, the great-souled son of Virāṭa (Uttara) then jumped down from the chariot. After quickly taking the garments of the great charioteers, he again climbed onto his own chariot.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 15. Having released the reins, the great-souled son of Virāṭa (Uttara) then jumped down from the chariot. After quickly taking the garments of the great charioteers, he again climbed onto his own chariot.

sanskrit: ततोऽन्वशासच्चतुरः सदश्वान्पुत्रो विराटस्य हिरण्यकक्ष्यान् ।ते तद्व्यतीयुर्ध्वजिनामनीकं श्वेता वहन्तोऽर्जुनमाजिमध्यात् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato'nvaśāsaccaturaḥ sadaśvā;nputro virāṭasya hiraṇyakakṣyān ,te tadvyatīyurdhvajināmanīkaṁ; śvetā vahanto'rjunamājimadhyāt.
english: Then, Virāṭa's son (Uttara) commanded his four excellent horses, which were adorned with golden harnesses. Those white horses, carrying Arjuna, proceeded through the enemy army from the midst of the battlefield.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 16. Then, Virāṭa's son (Uttara) commanded his four excellent horses, which were adorned with golden harnesses. Those white horses, carrying Arjuna, proceeded through the enemy army from the midst of the battlefield.

sanskrit: तथा तु यान्तं पुरुषप्रवीरं भीष्मः शरैरभ्यहनत्तरस्वी ।स चापि भीष्मस्य हयान्निहत्य विव्याध पार्श्वे दशभिः पृषत्कैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tathā tu yāntaṁ puruṣapravīraṁ; bhīṣmaḥ śarairabhyahanattarasvī ,sa cāpi bhīṣmasya hayānnihatya; vivyādha pārśve daśabhiḥ pṛṣatkaiḥ.
english: Then, the powerful Bhīṣma struck with arrows that foremost among men (puruṣapravīra) who was thus advancing. And Arjuna, in turn, having struck down Bhīṣma's horses, pierced him in the side with ten arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 17. Then, the powerful Bhīṣma struck with arrows that foremost among men (puruṣapravīra) who was thus advancing. And Arjuna, in turn, having struck down Bhīṣma's horses, pierced him in the side with ten arrows.

sanskrit: ततोऽर्जुनो भीष्ममपास्य युद्धे विद्ध्वास्य यन्तारमरिष्टधन्वा ।तस्थौ विमुक्तो रथवृन्दमध्याद्राहुं विदार्येव सहस्ररश्मिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tato'rjuno bhīṣmamapāsya yuddhe; viddhvāsya yantāramariṣṭadhanvā ,tasthau vimukto rathavṛndamadhyā;drāhuṁ vidāryeva sahasraraśmiḥ.
english: Then Arjuna, whose bow was intact (ariṣṭadhanvā), having set aside Bhīṣma in battle and having pierced his charioteer, stood freed from the midst of the multitude of chariots, just as the sun (sahasra-raśmi) emerges after having split Rāhu.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 18. Then Arjuna, whose bow was intact (ariṣṭadhanvā), having set aside Bhīṣma in battle and having pierced his charioteer, stood freed from the midst of the multitude of chariots, just as the sun (sahasra-raśmi) emerges after having split Rāhu.

sanskrit: लब्ध्वा तु संज्ञां च कुरुप्रवीरः पार्थं समीक्ष्याथ महेन्द्रकल्पम् ।रणाद्विमुक्तं स्थितमेकमाजौ स धार्तराष्ट्रस्त्वरितो बभाषे ॥१९॥
transliteration: labdhvā tu saṁjñāṁ ca kurupravīraḥ; pārthaṁ samīkṣyātha mahendrakalpam ,raṇādvimuktaṁ sthitamekamājau; sa dhārtarāṣṭrastvarito babhāṣe.
english: But then, the foremost hero of the Kurus (Bhīṣma), having regained his consciousness (saṃjñā), and observing Pārtha (Arjuna)  -  who was like Mahendra (Indra), freed from the battle, and standing alone in the fight  -  that son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) quickly spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 19. But then, the foremost hero of the Kurus (Bhīṣma), having regained his consciousness (saṃjñā), and observing Pārtha (Arjuna)  -  who was like Mahendra (Indra), freed from the battle, and standing alone in the fight  -  that son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana) quickly spoke.

sanskrit: अयं कथं स्विद्भवतां विमुक्तस्तं वै प्रबध्नीत यथा न मुच्येत् ।तमब्रवीच्छांतनवः प्रहस्य क्व ते गता बुद्धिरभूत्क्व वीर्यम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ayaṁ kathaṁ svidbhavatāṁ vimukta;staṁ vai prabadhnīta yathā na mucyet ,tamabravīcchāṁtanavaḥ prahasya; kva te gatā buddhirabhūtkva vīryam.
english: “How did he escape from you all? Indeed, bind him so that he cannot escape!” Śāntanava (Bhīṣma), laughing, said to him, “Where has your intellect gone? Where is your valor?”
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 20. “How did he escape from you all? Indeed, bind him so that he cannot escape!” Śāntanava (Bhīṣma), laughing, said to him, “Where has your intellect gone? Where is your valor?”

sanskrit: शान्तिं पराश्वस्य यथा स्थितोऽभूरुत्सृज्य बाणांश्च धनुश्च चित्रम् ।न त्वेव बीभत्सुरलं नृशंसं कर्तुं न पापेऽस्य मनो निविष्टम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: śāntiṁ parāśvasya yathā sthito'bhū;rutsṛjya bāṇāṁśca dhanuśca citram ,na tveva bībhatsuralaṁ nṛśaṁsaṁ; kartuṁ na pāpe'sya mano niviṣṭam.
english: You remained in peace (śānti), having cast aside your extraordinary arrows and bow. But Arjuna (Bībhatsu) is not capable of performing a cruel act, nor is his mind inclined towards sin.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 21. You remained in peace (śānti), having cast aside your extraordinary arrows and bow. But Arjuna (Bībhatsu) is not capable of performing a cruel act, nor is his mind inclined towards sin.

sanskrit: त्रैलोक्यहेतोर्न जहेत्स्वधर्मं तस्मान्न सर्वे निहता रणेऽस्मिन् ।क्षिप्रं कुरून्याहि कुरुप्रवीर विजित्य गाश्च प्रतियातु पार्थः ॥२२॥
transliteration: trailokyahetorna jahetsvadharmaṁ; tasmānna sarve nihatā raṇe'smin ,kṣipraṁ kurūnyāhi kurupravīra; vijitya gāśca pratiyātu pārthaḥ.
english: For the sake of the three worlds, he would not abandon his own inherent nature (svadharma). Therefore, not all were slain in this battle. O best of the Kurus, go quickly to the Kurus! And may Pārtha (Arjuna) return after conquering the cows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 22. For the sake of the three worlds, he would not abandon his own inherent nature (svadharma). Therefore, not all were slain in this battle. O best of the Kurus, go quickly to the Kurus! And may Pārtha (Arjuna) return after conquering the cows.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्तस्य तु तन्निशम्य पितामहस्यात्महितं वचोऽथ ।अतीतकामो युधि सोऽत्यमर्षी राजा विनिःश्वस्य बभूव तूष्णीम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: duryodhanastasya tu tanniśamya; pitāmahasyātmahitaṁ vaco'tha ,atītakāmo yudhi so'tyamarṣī; rājā viniḥśvasya babhūva tūṣṇīm.
english: Duryodhana, having heard that beneficial (ātman) counsel from his grandfather, and being one whose desires had passed away in battle, and exceedingly wrathful, that king (Duryodhana) sighed and became silent.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 23. Duryodhana, having heard that beneficial (ātman) counsel from his grandfather, and being one whose desires had passed away in battle, and exceedingly wrathful, that king (Duryodhana) sighed and became silent.

sanskrit: तद्भीष्मवाक्यं हितमीक्ष्य सर्वे धनंजयाग्निं च विवर्धमानम् ।निवर्तनायैव मनो निदध्युर्दुर्योधनं ते परिरक्षमाणाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tadbhīṣmavākyaṁ hitamīkṣya sarve; dhanaṁjayāgniṁ ca vivardhamānam ,nivartanāyaiva mano nidadhyu;rduryodhanaṁ te parirakṣamāṇāḥ.
english: Considering Bhishma's beneficial words and the ever-increasing martial prowess (literally, 'fire') of Arjuna, all of them, while protecting Duryodhana, firmly resolved to retreat.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 24. Considering Bhishma's beneficial words and the ever-increasing martial prowess (literally, 'fire') of Arjuna, all of them, while protecting Duryodhana, firmly resolved to retreat.

sanskrit: तान्प्रस्थितान्प्रीतमनाः स पार्थो धनंजयः प्रेक्ष्य कुरुप्रवीरान् ।आभाषमाणोऽनुययौ मुहूर्तं संपूजयंस्तत्र गुरून्महात्मा ॥२५॥
transliteration: tānprasthitānprītamanāḥ sa pārtho; dhanaṁjayaḥ prekṣya kurupravīrān ,ābhāṣamāṇo'nuyayau muhūrtaṁ; saṁpūjayaṁstatra gurūnmahātmā.
english: Observing those Kuru heroes who had set out, that glad-minded Partha (Arjuna), Dhananjaya, the great-souled one, followed them for a moment, conversing with and honoring his elders (guru) there.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 25. Observing those Kuru heroes who had set out, that glad-minded Partha (Arjuna), Dhananjaya, the great-souled one, followed them for a moment, conversing with and honoring his elders (guru) there.

sanskrit: पितामहं शांतनवं स वृद्धं द्रोणं गुरुं च प्रतिपूज्य मूर्ध्ना ।द्रौणिं कृपं चैव गुरूंश्च सर्वाञ्शरैर्विचित्रैरभिवाद्य चैव ॥२६॥
transliteration: pitāmahaṁ śāṁtanavaṁ sa vṛddhaṁ; droṇaṁ guruṁ ca pratipūjya mūrdhnā ,drauṇiṁ kṛpaṁ caiva gurūṁśca sarvā;ñśarairvicitrairabhivādya caiva.
english: Having honored his aged grandfather, the son of Shantanu (Bhishma), and Drona, his preceptor (guru), by bowing his head; and having also saluted Drona's son (Ashvatthama), Kripa, and all his other elders (guru) with wondrous arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 26. Having honored his aged grandfather, the son of Shantanu (Bhishma), and Drona, his preceptor (guru), by bowing his head; and having also saluted Drona's son (Ashvatthama), Kripa, and all his other elders (guru) with wondrous arrows.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्योत्तमरत्नचित्रं चिच्छेद पार्थो मुकुटं शरेण ।आमन्त्र्य वीरांश्च तथैव मान्यान्गाण्डीवघोषेण विनाद्य लोकान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: duryodhanasyottamaratnacitraṁ; ciccheda pārtho mukuṭaṁ śareṇa ,āmantrya vīrāṁśca tathaiva mānyā;ngāṇḍīvaghoṣeṇa vinādya lokān.
english: With an arrow, Partha (Arjuna) severed Duryodhana's crown, which was adorned with excellent gems. Having then addressed the heroes and the respected elders in the same way, he made the worlds resound with the roar of his Gandiva bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 27. With an arrow, Partha (Arjuna) severed Duryodhana's crown, which was adorned with excellent gems. Having then addressed the heroes and the respected elders in the same way, he made the worlds resound with the roar of his Gandiva bow.

sanskrit: स देवदत्तं सहसा विनाद्य विदार्य वीरो द्विषतां मनांसि ।ध्वजेन सर्वानभिभूय शत्रून्स हेमजालेन विराजमानः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa devadattaṁ sahasā vinādya; vidārya vīro dviṣatāṁ manāṁsi ,dhvajena sarvānabhibhūya śatrū;nsa hemajālena virājamānaḥ.
english: That hero, shining splendidly with a golden network, having suddenly sounded his conch Devadatta loudly, having shattered the minds of his adversaries, and having overpowered all enemies with his banner...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 28. That hero, shining splendidly with a golden network, having suddenly sounded his conch Devadatta loudly, having shattered the minds of his adversaries, and having overpowered all enemies with his banner...

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा प्रयातांस्तु कुरून्किरीटी हृष्टोऽब्रवीत्तत्र स मत्स्यपुत्रम् ।आवर्तयाश्वान्पशवो जितास्ते याताः परे याहि पुरं प्रहृष्टः ॥२९॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā prayātāṁstu kurūnkirīṭī; hṛṣṭo'bravīttatra sa matsyaputram ,āvartayāśvānpaśavo jitāste; yātāḥ pare yāhi puraṁ prahṛṣṭaḥ.
english: Having seen the Kurus indeed fleeing, Arjuna (the one wearing a diadem), delighted, spoke there to the son of Matsya (Uttara): 'Turn back the horses! The cattle have been won by you. The enemies have fled. Go to the city, greatly delighted!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 61 Verse 29. Having seen the Kurus indeed fleeing, Arjuna (the one wearing a diadem), delighted, spoke there to the son of Matsya (Uttara): 'Turn back the horses! The cattle have been won by you. The enemies have fled. Go to the city, greatly delighted!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो विजित्य संग्रामे कुरून्गोवृषभेक्षणः ।समानयामास तदा विराटस्य धनं महत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato vijitya saṁgrāme kurūngovṛṣabhekṣaṇaḥ ,samānayāmāsa tadā virāṭasya dhanaṁ mahat.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, having conquered the Kurus in battle, the hero with eyes like a bull (Arjuna) then brought back the immense wealth of Virāta.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, having conquered the Kurus in battle, the hero with eyes like a bull (Arjuna) then brought back the immense wealth of Virāta.

sanskrit: गतेषु च प्रभग्नेषु धार्तराष्ट्रेषु सर्वशः ।वनान्निष्क्रम्य गहनाद्बहवः कुरुसैनिकाः ॥२॥
transliteration: gateṣu ca prabhagneṣu dhārtarāṣṭreṣu sarvaśaḥ ,vanānniṣkramya gahanādbahavaḥ kurusainikāḥ.
english: And when the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas) had entirely fled and been defeated, many Kuru soldiers came out from the dense forest...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 2. And when the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Kauravas) had entirely fled and been defeated, many Kuru soldiers came out from the dense forest...

sanskrit: भयात्संत्रस्तमनसः समाजग्मुस्ततस्ततः ।मुक्तकेशा व्यदृश्यन्त स्थिताः प्राञ्जलयस्तदा ॥३॥
transliteration: bhayātsaṁtrastamanasaḥ samājagmustatastataḥ ,muktakeśā vyadṛśyanta sthitāḥ prāñjalayastadā.
english: Their minds terrified by fear, they gathered from all directions. Then, with dishevelled hair, they were seen standing with folded hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 3. Their minds terrified by fear, they gathered from all directions. Then, with dishevelled hair, they were seen standing with folded hands.

sanskrit: क्षुत्पिपासापरिश्रान्ता विदेशस्था विचेतसः ।ऊचुः प्रणम्य संभ्रान्ताः पार्थ किं करवाम ते ॥४॥
transliteration: kṣutpipāsāpariśrāntā videśasthā vicetasaḥ ,ūcuḥ praṇamya saṁbhrāntāḥ pārtha kiṁ karavāma te.
english: Exhausted by hunger and thirst, bewildered and disoriented in this foreign land, they bowed down and asked, "O Pārtha, what can we do for you?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 4. Exhausted by hunger and thirst, bewildered and disoriented in this foreign land, they bowed down and asked, "O Pārtha, what can we do for you?"

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।स्वस्ति व्रजत भद्रं वो न भेतव्यं कथंचन ।नाहमार्ताञ्जिघांसामि भृशमाश्वासयामि वः ॥५॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,svasti vrajata bhadraṁ vo na bhetavyaṁ kathaṁcana ,nāhamārtāñjighāṁsāmi bhṛśamāśvāsayāmi vaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: "Go in peace; may good fortune be with you. There is absolutely no need to be afraid. I do not wish to kill those who are suffering and distressed; rather, I greatly reassure you."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 5. Arjuna said: "Go in peace; may good fortune be with you. There is absolutely no need to be afraid. I do not wish to kill those who are suffering and distressed; rather, I greatly reassure you."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य तामभयां वाचं श्रुत्वा योधाः समागताः ।आयुःकीर्तियशोदाभिस्तमाशिर्भिरनन्दयन् ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya tāmabhayāṁ vācaṁ śrutvā yodhāḥ samāgatāḥ ,āyuḥkīrtiyaśodābhistamāśirbhiranandayan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing his fearless, reassuring words, the assembled warriors delighted him with blessings for long life, renown, and glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing his fearless, reassuring words, the assembled warriors delighted him with blessings for long life, renown, and glory.

sanskrit: ततो निवृत्ताः कुरवः प्रभग्ना वशमास्थिताः ।पन्थानमुपसंगम्य फल्गुनो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: tato nivṛttāḥ kuravaḥ prabhagnā vaśamāsthitāḥ ,panthānamupasaṁgamya phalguno vākyamabravīt.
english: Then the Kurus, defeated and brought under control, turned back. Approaching the road, Arjuna (phalguna) spoke.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 7. Then the Kurus, defeated and brought under control, turned back. Approaching the road, Arjuna (phalguna) spoke.

sanskrit: राजपुत्र प्रत्यवेक्ष समानीतानि सर्वशः ।गोकुलानि महाबाहो वीर गोपालकैः सह ॥८॥
transliteration: rājaputra pratyavekṣa samānītāni sarvaśaḥ ,gokulāni mahābāho vīra gopālakaiḥ saha.
english: O Prince (rājaputra), O mighty-armed (mahābāhu) hero (vīra), thoroughly inspect all the collected herds of cattle (gokulāni), accompanied by the cowherds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 8. O Prince (rājaputra), O mighty-armed (mahābāhu) hero (vīra), thoroughly inspect all the collected herds of cattle (gokulāni), accompanied by the cowherds.

sanskrit: ततोऽपराह्णे यास्यामो विराटनगरं प्रति ।आश्वास्य पाययित्वा च परिप्लाव्य च वाजिनः ॥९॥
transliteration: tato'parāhṇe yāsyāmo virāṭanagaraṁ prati ,āśvāsya pāyayitvā ca pariplāvya ca vājinaḥ.
english: Then, in the afternoon, we shall go towards the city of Virata, after comforting, watering, and bathing the horses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 9. Then, in the afternoon, we shall go towards the city of Virata, after comforting, watering, and bathing the horses.

sanskrit: गच्छन्तु त्वरिताश्चैव गोपालाः प्रेषितास्त्वया ।नगरे प्रियमाख्यातुं घोषयन्तु च ते जयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: gacchantu tvaritāścaiva gopālāḥ preṣitāstvayā ,nagare priyamākhyātuṁ ghoṣayantu ca te jayam.
english: Let the cowherds dispatched by you go quickly and announce the good news in the city, proclaiming your victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 10. Let the cowherds dispatched by you go quickly and announce the good news in the city, proclaiming your victory.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उत्तरस्त्वरमाणोऽथ दूतानाज्ञापयत्ततः ।वचनादर्जुनस्यैव आचक्षध्वं जयं मम ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,uttarastvaramāṇo'tha dūtānājñāpayattataḥ ,vacanādarjunasyaiva ācakṣadhvaṁ jayaṁ mama.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Uttara, in a hurry, commanded the messengers, "Announce my victory, exactly as per Arjuna's instructions."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 62 Verse 11. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Uttara, in a hurry, commanded the messengers, "Announce my victory, exactly as per Arjuna's instructions."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अवजित्य धनं चापि विराटो वाहिनीपतिः ।प्राविशन्नगरं हृष्टश्चतुर्भिः सह पाण्डवैः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,avajitya dhanaṁ cāpi virāṭo vāhinīpatiḥ ,prāviśannagaraṁ hṛṣṭaścaturbhiḥ saha pāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: King Virāṭa, the commander of the army, having recovered the cattle (dhana) and feeling delighted, entered the city along with the four Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: King Virāṭa, the commander of the army, having recovered the cattle (dhana) and feeling delighted, entered the city along with the four Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: जित्वा त्रिगर्तान्संग्रामे गाश्चैवादाय केवलाः ।अशोभत महाराजः सह पार्थैः श्रिया वृतः ॥२॥
transliteration: jitvā trigartānsaṁgrāme gāścaivādāya kevalāḥ ,aśobhata mahārājaḥ saha pārthaiḥ śriyā vṛtaḥ.
english: Having conquered the Trigartas in battle and recovered all the cattle, the great king (Virāṭa) shone splendidly, surrounded by glory, together with the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 2. Having conquered the Trigartas in battle and recovered all the cattle, the great king (Virāṭa) shone splendidly, surrounded by glory, together with the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तमासनगतं वीरं सुहृदां प्रीतिवर्धनम् ।उपतस्थुः प्रकृतयः समस्ता ब्राह्मणैः सह ॥३॥
transliteration: tamāsanagataṁ vīraṁ suhṛdāṁ prītivardhanam ,upatasthuḥ prakṛtayaḥ samastā brāhmaṇaiḥ saha.
english: All the ministers and subjects (prakṛtayaḥ), along with the brāhmaṇas, approached that seated hero (Virāṭa), who enhanced the happiness of his friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 3. All the ministers and subjects (prakṛtayaḥ), along with the brāhmaṇas, approached that seated hero (Virāṭa), who enhanced the happiness of his friends.

sanskrit: सभाजितः ससैन्यस्तु प्रतिनन्द्याथ मत्स्यराट् ।विसर्जयामास तदा द्विजांश्च प्रकृतीस्तथा ॥४॥
transliteration: sabhājitaḥ sasainyastu pratinandyātha matsyarāṭ ,visarjayāmāsa tadā dvijāṁśca prakṛtīstathā.
english: The King of Matsya (Matsyarāṭ), who had been honored, then, along with his army, returned their greetings and dismissed the Brahmins and his subjects (prakṛtīḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 4. The King of Matsya (Matsyarāṭ), who had been honored, then, along with his army, returned their greetings and dismissed the Brahmins and his subjects (prakṛtīḥ).

sanskrit: ततः स राजा मत्स्यानां विराटो वाहिनीपतिः ।उत्तरं परिपप्रच्छ क्व यात इति चाब्रवीत् ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā matsyānāṁ virāṭo vāhinīpatiḥ ,uttaraṁ paripapraccha kva yāta iti cābravīt.
english: Then, that King of the Matsyas, Virāṭa, the commander of the army, inquired about Uttara, asking, 'Where has he gone?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 5. Then, that King of the Matsyas, Virāṭa, the commander of the army, inquired about Uttara, asking, 'Where has he gone?'

sanskrit: आचख्युस्तस्य संहृष्टाः स्त्रियः कन्याश्च वेश्मनि ।अन्तःपुरचराश्चैव कुरुभिर्गोधनं हृतम् ॥६॥
transliteration: ācakhyustasya saṁhṛṣṭāḥ striyaḥ kanyāśca veśmani ,antaḥpuracarāścaiva kurubhirgodhanaṁ hṛtam.
english: To him, the overjoyed women, maidens, and indeed the residents of the inner palace (antaḥpuracarāḥ) related that the cattle had been seized by the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 6. To him, the overjoyed women, maidens, and indeed the residents of the inner palace (antaḥpuracarāḥ) related that the cattle had been seized by the Kurus.

sanskrit: विजेतुमभिसंरब्ध एक एवातिसाहसात् ।बृहन्नडासहायश्च निर्यातः पृथिवींजयः ॥७॥
transliteration: vijetumabhisaṁrabdha eka evātisāhasāt ,bṛhannaḍāsahāyaśca niryātaḥ pṛthivīṁjayaḥ.
english: Pṛthivīṃjaya (Uttara), zealous to conquer, has departed alone due to his great audacity, accompanied by Bṛhannāḍā.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 7. Pṛthivīṃjaya (Uttara), zealous to conquer, has departed alone due to his great audacity, accompanied by Bṛhannāḍā.

sanskrit: उपयातानतिरथान्द्रोणं शांतनवं कृपम् ।कर्णं दुर्योधनं चैव द्रोणपुत्रं च षड्रथान् ॥८॥
transliteration: upayātānatirathāndroṇaṁ śāṁtanavaṁ kṛpam ,karṇaṁ duryodhanaṁ caiva droṇaputraṁ ca ṣaḍrathān.
english: The arrived great charioteers: Drona, Bhishma (the son of Shantanu), Kripa, Karna, Duryodhana, and also Drona's son (Ashvatthama), who are warriors equal to six charioteers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 8. The arrived great charioteers: Drona, Bhishma (the son of Shantanu), Kripa, Karna, Duryodhana, and also Drona's son (Ashvatthama), who are warriors equal to six charioteers.

sanskrit: राजा विराटोऽथ भृशं प्रतप्तः श्रुत्वा सुतं ह्येकरथेन यातम् ।बृहन्नडासारथिमाजिवर्धनं प्रोवाच सर्वानथ मन्त्रिमुख्यान् ॥९॥
transliteration: rājā virāṭo'tha bhṛśaṁ prataptaḥ; śrutvā sutaṁ hyekarathena yātam ,bṛhannaḍāsārathimājivardhanaṁ; provāca sarvānatha mantrimukhyān.
english: King Virata, greatly distressed then, having heard that his son, who had Brihannala as his charioteer and who was escalating the battle, had indeed gone with a single chariot, then addressed all his chief ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 9. King Virata, greatly distressed then, having heard that his son, who had Brihannala as his charioteer and who was escalating the battle, had indeed gone with a single chariot, then addressed all his chief ministers.

sanskrit: सर्वथा कुरवस्ते हि ये चान्ये वसुधाधिपाः ।त्रिगर्तान्निर्जिताञ्श्रुत्वा न स्थास्यन्ति कदाचन ॥१०॥
transliteration: sarvathā kuravaste hi ye cānye vasudhādhipāḥ ,trigartānnirjitāñśrutvā na sthāsyanti kadācana.
english: Certainly, the Kurus and indeed the other rulers of the earth, having heard that the Trigartas were defeated, will never stand (still).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 10. Certainly, the Kurus and indeed the other rulers of the earth, having heard that the Trigartas were defeated, will never stand (still).

sanskrit: तस्माद्गच्छन्तु मे योधा बलेन महता वृताः ।उत्तरस्य परीप्सार्थं ये त्रिगर्तैरविक्षताः ॥११॥
transliteration: tasmādgacchantu me yodhā balena mahatā vṛtāḥ ,uttarasya parīpsārthaṁ ye trigartairavikṣatāḥ.
english: Therefore, let my warriors go, accompanied by a mighty force, for the purpose of recovering Uttara – those warriors who are uninjured by the Trigartas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 11. Therefore, let my warriors go, accompanied by a mighty force, for the purpose of recovering Uttara – those warriors who are uninjured by the Trigartas.

sanskrit: हयांश्च नागांश्च रथांश्च शीघ्रं पदातिसंघांश्च ततः प्रवीरान् ।प्रस्थापयामास सुतस्य हेतोर्विचित्रशस्त्राभरणोपपन्नान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: hayāṁśca nāgāṁśca rathāṁśca śīghraṁ; padātisaṁghāṁśca tataḥ pravīrān ,prasthāpayāmāsa sutasya heto;rvicitraśastrābharaṇopapannān.
english: Then, for his son's sake, he quickly dispatched mighty warriors, along with horses, elephants, chariots, and throngs of foot soldiers, all equipped with diverse weapons and ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 12. Then, for his son's sake, he quickly dispatched mighty warriors, along with horses, elephants, chariots, and throngs of foot soldiers, all equipped with diverse weapons and ornaments.

sanskrit: एवं स राजा मत्स्यानां विराटोऽक्षौहिणीपतिः ।व्यादिदेशाथ तां क्षिप्रं वाहिनीं चतुरङ्गिणीम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa rājā matsyānāṁ virāṭo'kṣauhiṇīpatiḥ ,vyādideśātha tāṁ kṣipraṁ vāhinīṁ caturaṅgiṇīm.
english: Thus, King Virata of the Matsyas, the commander of an akṣauhiṇī (a large army division), then quickly commanded that four-fold army.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 13. Thus, King Virata of the Matsyas, the commander of an akṣauhiṇī (a large army division), then quickly commanded that four-fold army.

sanskrit: कुमारमाशु जानीत यदि जीवति वा न वा ।यस्य यन्ता गतः षण्ढो मन्येऽहं न स जीवति ॥१४॥
transliteration: kumāramāśu jānīta yadi jīvati vā na vā ,yasya yantā gataḥ ṣaṇḍho manye'haṁ na sa jīvati.
english: Quickly ascertain whether the prince lives or not. I think that he whose charioteer was a eunuch cannot be alive.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 14. Quickly ascertain whether the prince lives or not. I think that he whose charioteer was a eunuch cannot be alive.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीद्धर्मराजः प्रहस्य विराटमार्तं कुरुभिः प्रतप्तम् ।बृहन्नडा सारथिश्चेन्नरेन्द्र परे न नेष्यन्ति तवाद्य गास्ताः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tamabravīddharmarājaḥ prahasya; virāṭamārtaṁ kurubhiḥ prataptam ,bṛhannaḍā sārathiścennarendra; pare na neṣyanti tavādya gāstāḥ.
english: Dharmaraja (Yudhiṣṭhira), having laughed, spoke to Virata, who was distressed and tormented by the Kurus: "O King (narendra), if Bṛhannalā is the charioteer, then today your enemies will not take away those cows."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 15. Dharmaraja (Yudhiṣṭhira), having laughed, spoke to Virata, who was distressed and tormented by the Kurus: "O King (narendra), if Bṛhannalā is the charioteer, then today your enemies will not take away those cows."

sanskrit: सर्वान्महीपान्सहितान्कुरूंश्च तथैव देवासुरयक्षनागान् ।अलं विजेतुं समरे सुतस्ते स्वनुष्ठितः सारथिना हि तेन ॥१६॥
transliteration: sarvānmahīpānsahitānkurūṁśca; tathaiva devāsurayakṣanāgān ,alaṁ vijetuṁ samare sutaste; svanuṣṭhitaḥ sārathinā hi tena.
english: Your son, indeed, being excellently guided by that charioteer, is fully capable of conquering in battle all kings, the united Kauravas, and likewise gods, asuras, yakshas, and nagas.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 16. Your son, indeed, being excellently guided by that charioteer, is fully capable of conquering in battle all kings, the united Kauravas, and likewise gods, asuras, yakshas, and nagas.

sanskrit: अथोत्तरेण प्रहिता दूतास्ते शीघ्रगामिनः ।विराटनगरं प्राप्य जयमावेदयंस्तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: athottareṇa prahitā dūtāste śīghragāminaḥ ,virāṭanagaraṁ prāpya jayamāvedayaṁstadā.
english: Then, the swift-moving messengers, dispatched by Uttara, arrived at the city of Virata and announced the victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 17. Then, the swift-moving messengers, dispatched by Uttara, arrived at the city of Virata and announced the victory.

sanskrit: राज्ञस्ततः समाचख्यौ मन्त्री विजयमुत्तमम् ।पराजयं कुरूणां चाप्युपायान्तं तथोत्तरम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: rājñastataḥ samācakhyau mantrī vijayamuttamam ,parājayaṁ kurūṇāṁ cāpyupāyāntaṁ tathottaram.
english: Then the minister reported to the king the excellent victory, the defeat of the Kauravas, and likewise the approaching Uttara.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 18. Then the minister reported to the king the excellent victory, the defeat of the Kauravas, and likewise the approaching Uttara.

sanskrit: सर्वा विनिर्जिता गावः कुरवश्च पराजिताः ।उत्तरः सह सूतेन कुशली च परंतप ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarvā vinirjitā gāvaḥ kuravaśca parājitāḥ ,uttaraḥ saha sūtena kuśalī ca paraṁtapa.
english: All the cows have been completely recovered, and the Kauravas have been defeated. Uttara, along with his charioteer, is safe, O scorcher of foes (parantapa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 19. All the cows have been completely recovered, and the Kauravas have been defeated. Uttara, along with his charioteer, is safe, O scorcher of foes (parantapa).

sanskrit: कङ्क उवाच ।दिष्ट्या ते निर्जिता गावः कुरवश्च पराजिताः ।दिष्ट्या ते जीवितः पुत्रः श्रूयते पार्थिवर्षभ ॥२०॥
transliteration: kaṅka uvāca ,diṣṭyā te nirjitā gāvaḥ kuravaśca parājitāḥ ,diṣṭyā te jīvitaḥ putraḥ śrūyate pārthivarṣabha.
english: Kaṅka said: "Fortunately, the cows have been recovered by you, and the Kurus have been defeated. Also, O best among kings, it is heard that your son is alive."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 20. Kaṅka said: "Fortunately, the cows have been recovered by you, and the Kurus have been defeated. Also, O best among kings, it is heard that your son is alive."

sanskrit: नाद्भुतं त्वेव मन्येऽहं यत्ते पुत्रोऽजयत्कुरून् ।ध्रुव एव जयस्तस्य यस्य यन्ता बृहन्नडा ॥२१॥
transliteration: nādbhutaṁ tveva manye'haṁ yatte putro'jayatkurūn ,dhruva eva jayastasya yasya yantā bṛhannaḍā.
english: Indeed, I do not consider it a wonder that your son defeated the Kurus, for victory is certain for him whose charioteer is Bṛhannadā.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 21. Indeed, I do not consider it a wonder that your son defeated the Kurus, for victory is certain for him whose charioteer is Bṛhannadā.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो विराटो नृपतिः संप्रहृष्टतनूरुहः ।श्रुत्वा तु विजयं तस्य कुमारस्यामितौजसः ।आच्छादयित्वा दूतांस्तान्मन्त्रिणः सोऽभ्यचोदयत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato virāṭo nṛpatiḥ saṁprahṛṣṭatanūruhaḥ ,śrutvā tu vijayaṁ tasya kumārasyāmitaujasaḥ ,ācchādayitvā dūtāṁstānmantriṇaḥ so'bhyacodayat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then King Virāṭa, his body hair bristling with great joy upon hearing of the victory of that exceedingly mighty prince, first honored those messengers, and then instructed his ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 22. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then King Virāṭa, his body hair bristling with great joy upon hearing of the victory of that exceedingly mighty prince, first honored those messengers, and then instructed his ministers.

sanskrit: राजमार्गाः क्रियन्तां मे पताकाभिरलंकृताः ।पुष्पोपहारैरर्च्यन्तां देवताश्चापि सर्वशः ॥२३॥
transliteration: rājamārgāḥ kriyantāṁ me patākābhiralaṁkṛtāḥ ,puṣpopahārairarcyantāṁ devatāścāpi sarvaśaḥ.
english: Let my royal roads be adorned with banners. And let the deities also be worshipped everywhere with offerings of flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 23. Let my royal roads be adorned with banners. And let the deities also be worshipped everywhere with offerings of flowers.

sanskrit: कुमारा योधमुख्याश्च गणिकाश्च स्वलंकृताः ।वादित्राणि च सर्वाणि प्रत्युद्यान्तु सुतं मम ॥२४॥
transliteration: kumārā yodhamukhyāśca gaṇikāśca svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,vāditrāṇi ca sarvāṇi pratyudyāntu sutaṁ mama.
english: Princes, chief warriors, and well-adorned courtesans, along with all types of musical instruments, should go forth to meet my son.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 24. Princes, chief warriors, and well-adorned courtesans, along with all types of musical instruments, should go forth to meet my son.

sanskrit: घण्टापणवकः शीघ्रं मत्तमारुह्य वारणम् ।शृङ्गाटकेषु सर्वेषु आख्यातु विजयं मम ॥२५॥
transliteration: ghaṇṭāpaṇavakaḥ śīghraṁ mattamāruhya vāraṇam ,śṛṅgāṭakeṣu sarveṣu ākhyātu vijayaṁ mama.
english: A crier, with a bell and drum, should swiftly mount an elephant in musth and announce my victory at all crossroads.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 25. A crier, with a bell and drum, should swiftly mount an elephant in musth and announce my victory at all crossroads.

sanskrit: उत्तरा च कुमारीभिर्बह्वीभिरभिसंवृता ।शृङ्गारवेषाभरणा प्रत्युद्यातु बृहन्नडाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: uttarā ca kumārībhirbahvībhirabhisaṁvṛtā ,śṛṅgāraveṣābharaṇā pratyudyātu bṛhannaḍām.
english: And Uttara, surrounded by many maidens and adorned in festive garments and ornaments, should go forth to meet Brihannala.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 26. And Uttara, surrounded by many maidens and adorned in festive garments and ornaments, should go forth to meet Brihannala.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु तद्वचनं पार्थिवस्य सर्वे पुनः स्वस्तिकपाणयश्च ।भेर्यश्च तूर्याणि च वारिजाश्च वेषैः परार्ध्यैः प्रमदाः शुभाश्च ॥२७॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu tadvacanaṁ pārthivasya; sarve punaḥ svastikapāṇayaśca ,bheryaśca tūryāṇi ca vārijāśca; veṣaiḥ parārdhyaiḥ pramadāḥ śubhāśca.
english: Having heard that command of the king, all (the people responded), again with auspicious folded hands. And drums, and trumpets, and conch shells, and beautiful women in splendid attire (were present).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 27. Having heard that command of the king, all (the people responded), again with auspicious folded hands. And drums, and trumpets, and conch shells, and beautiful women in splendid attire (were present).

sanskrit: तथैव सूताः सह मागधैश्च नन्दीवाद्याः पणवास्तूर्यवाद्याः ।पुराद्विराटस्य महाबलस्य प्रत्युद्ययुः पुत्रमनन्तवीर्यम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tathaiva sūtāḥ saha māgadhaiśca; nandīvādyāḥ paṇavāstūryavādyāḥ ,purādvirāṭasya mahābalasya; pratyudyayuḥ putramanantavīryam.
english: In the same manner, bards and panegyrists, accompanied by drummers and trumpeters, went forth from the city of the mighty Virāṭa to greet his son of boundless valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 28. In the same manner, bards and panegyrists, accompanied by drummers and trumpeters, went forth from the city of the mighty Virāṭa to greet his son of boundless valor.

sanskrit: प्रस्थाप्य सेनां कन्याश्च गणिकाश्च स्वलंकृताः ।मत्स्यराजो महाप्राज्ञः प्रहृष्ट इदमब्रवीत् ।अक्षानाहर सैरन्ध्रि कङ्क द्यूतं प्रवर्तताम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: prasthāpya senāṁ kanyāśca gaṇikāśca svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,matsyarājo mahāprājñaḥ prahṛṣṭa idamabravīt ,akṣānāhara sairandhri kaṅka dyūtaṁ pravartatām.
english: Having sent forth the army, and well-adorned maidens and courtesans, the greatly wise King of Matsya, delighted, spoke this: 'Sairandhrī, bring the dice, and Kaṅka, let the game of dice proceed!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 29. Having sent forth the army, and well-adorned maidens and courtesans, the greatly wise King of Matsya, delighted, spoke this: 'Sairandhrī, bring the dice, and Kaṅka, let the game of dice proceed!'

sanskrit: तं तथा वादिनं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवः प्रत्यभाषत ।न देवितव्यं हृष्टेन कितवेनेति नः श्रुतम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: taṁ tathā vādinaṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavaḥ pratyabhāṣata ,na devitavyaṁ hṛṣṭena kitaveneti naḥ śrutam.
english: Seeing him speak in that manner, the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) replied, 'We have heard that a joyful gambler should not gamble.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 30. Seeing him speak in that manner, the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) replied, 'We have heard that a joyful gambler should not gamble.'

sanskrit: न त्वामद्य मुदा युक्तमहं देवितुमुत्सहे ।प्रियं तु ते चिकीर्षामि वर्ततां यदि मन्यसे ॥३१॥
transliteration: na tvāmadya mudā yuktamahaṁ devitumutsahe ,priyaṁ tu te cikīrṣāmi vartatāṁ yadi manyase.
english: I am not inclined to gamble with you today while you are filled with joy. But I wish to do what is pleasing to you; let it proceed if you deem it proper.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 31. I am not inclined to gamble with you today while you are filled with joy. But I wish to do what is pleasing to you; let it proceed if you deem it proper.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।स्त्रियो गावो हिरण्यं च यच्चान्यद्वसु किंचन ।न मे किंचित्त्वया रक्ष्यमन्तरेणापि देवितुम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,striyo gāvo hiraṇyaṁ ca yaccānyadvasu kiṁcana ,na me kiṁcittvayā rakṣyamantareṇāpi devitum.
english: Virāṭa said: Women, cows, gold, and whatever other wealth (vasu) there may be of mine, nothing needs to be protected by you, even without gambling (devitum).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 32. Virāṭa said: Women, cows, gold, and whatever other wealth (vasu) there may be of mine, nothing needs to be protected by you, even without gambling (devitum).

sanskrit: कङ्क उवाच ।किं ते द्यूतेन राजेन्द्र बहुदोषेण मानद ।देवने बहवो दोषास्तस्मात्तत्परिवर्जयेत् ॥३३॥
transliteration: kaṅka uvāca ,kiṁ te dyūtena rājendra bahudoṣeṇa mānada ,devane bahavo doṣāstasmāttatparivarjayet.
english: Kaṅka said: O King (rājendra), O giver of honor (mānada), what is the purpose of gambling (dyūtena) for you, as it has many faults? There are many defects in gambling, therefore one should completely avoid it.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 33. Kaṅka said: O King (rājendra), O giver of honor (mānada), what is the purpose of gambling (dyūtena) for you, as it has many faults? There are many defects in gambling, therefore one should completely avoid it.

sanskrit: श्रुतस्ते यदि वा दृष्टः पाण्डवो वै युधिष्ठिरः ।स राज्यं सुमहत्स्फीतं भ्रातॄंश्च त्रिदशोपमान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: śrutaste yadi vā dṛṣṭaḥ pāṇḍavo vai yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sa rājyaṁ sumahatsphītaṁ bhrātṝṁśca tridaśopamān.
english: Perhaps you have heard of or even seen the Pāṇḍava, Yudhiṣṭhira. He possessed a very great and prosperous kingdom, and brothers who were comparable to gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 34. Perhaps you have heard of or even seen the Pāṇḍava, Yudhiṣṭhira. He possessed a very great and prosperous kingdom, and brothers who were comparable to gods.

sanskrit: द्यूते हारितवान्सर्वं तस्माद्द्यूतं न रोचये ।अथ वा मन्यसे राजन्दीव्याव यदि रोचते ॥३५॥
transliteration: dyūte hāritavānsarvaṁ tasmāddyūtaṁ na rocaye ,atha vā manyase rājandīvyāva yadi rocate.
english: He lost everything in gambling (dyūta). Therefore, I do not approve of gambling. However, if you, O king (rājan), think it appropriate, let us two play dice if it pleases you.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 35. He lost everything in gambling (dyūta). Therefore, I do not approve of gambling. However, if you, O king (rājan), think it appropriate, let us two play dice if it pleases you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्रवर्तमाने द्यूते तु मत्स्यः पाण्डवमब्रवीत् ।पश्य पुत्रेण मे युद्धे तादृशाः कुरवो जिताः ॥३६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pravartamāne dyūte tu matsyaḥ pāṇḍavamabravīt ,paśya putreṇa me yuddhe tādṛśāḥ kuravo jitāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: While the game of dice was underway, King Virāṭa (Matsya) said to the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), "Look! In battle, such formidable Kauravas have been defeated by my son!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 36. Vaiśampāyana said: While the game of dice was underway, King Virāṭa (Matsya) said to the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), "Look! In battle, such formidable Kauravas have been defeated by my son!"

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीन्मत्स्यराजं धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।बृहन्नडा यस्य यन्ता कथं स न विजेष्यति ॥३७॥
transliteration: tato'bravīnmatsyarājaṁ dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bṛhannaḍā yasya yantā kathaṁ sa na vijeṣyati.
english: Then Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), said to King Virāṭa (Matsya): "How could he not be victorious, when Bṛhannalā is his charioteer?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 37. Then Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharma), said to King Virāṭa (Matsya): "How could he not be victorious, when Bṛhannalā is his charioteer?"

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः कुपितो राजा मत्स्यः पाण्डवमब्रवीत् ।समं पुत्रेण मे षण्ढं ब्रह्मबन्धो प्रशंससि ॥३८॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ kupito rājā matsyaḥ pāṇḍavamabravīt ,samaṁ putreṇa me ṣaṇḍhaṁ brahmabandho praśaṁsasi.
english: Having been thus spoken to, the enraged King Virāṭa (Matsya) said to the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), "O unworthy Brahmin (brahmabandhu)! You praise a eunuch equally with my son!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 38. Having been thus spoken to, the enraged King Virāṭa (Matsya) said to the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), "O unworthy Brahmin (brahmabandhu)! You praise a eunuch equally with my son!"

sanskrit: वाच्यावाच्यं न जानीषे नूनं मामवमन्यसे ।भीष्मद्रोणमुखान्सर्वान्कस्मान्न स विजेष्यति ॥३९॥
transliteration: vācyāvācyaṁ na jānīṣe nūnaṁ māmavamanyase ,bhīṣmadroṇamukhānsarvānkasmānna sa vijeṣyati.
english: You do not distinguish between what should be spoken and what should not be spoken (vācyāvācya); surely you disrespect me. Why would he not conquer all of them, including those led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 39. You do not distinguish between what should be spoken and what should not be spoken (vācyāvācya); surely you disrespect me. Why would he not conquer all of them, including those led by Bhīṣma and Droṇa?

sanskrit: वयस्यत्वात्तु ते ब्रह्मन्नपराधमिमं क्षमे ।नेदृशं ते पुनर्वाच्यं यदि जीवितुमिच्छसि ॥४०॥
transliteration: vayasyatvāttu te brahmannaparādhamimaṁ kṣame ,nedṛśaṁ te punarvācyaṁ yadi jīvitumicchasi.
english: O Brahmin, because of our friendship, I forgive this offense of yours. However, if you wish to live, such a thing must not be said by you again.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 40. O Brahmin, because of our friendship, I forgive this offense of yours. However, if you wish to live, such a thing must not be said by you again.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।यत्र द्रोणस्तथा भीष्मो द्रौणिर्वैकर्तनः कृपः ।दुर्योधनश्च राजेन्द्र तथान्ये च महारथाः ॥४१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yatra droṇastathā bhīṣmo drauṇirvaikartanaḥ kṛpaḥ ,duryodhanaśca rājendra tathānye ca mahārathāḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: "O king of kings, where Drona, Bhishma, Drauni (Aśvatthāmā, son of Drona), Vaikartana (Karṇa, son of Sūrya), Kripa, and Duryodhana, as well as other great charioteers (mahārathas) are present..."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 41. Yudhishthira said: "O king of kings, where Drona, Bhishma, Drauni (Aśvatthāmā, son of Drona), Vaikartana (Karṇa, son of Sūrya), Kripa, and Duryodhana, as well as other great charioteers (mahārathas) are present..."

sanskrit: मरुद्गणैः परिवृतः साक्षादपि शतक्रतुः ।कोऽन्यो बृहन्नडायास्तान्प्रतियुध्येत संगतान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: marudgaṇaiḥ parivṛtaḥ sākṣādapi śatakratuḥ ,ko'nyo bṛhannaḍāyāstānpratiyudhyeta saṁgatān.
english: Even if it were Indra (Śatakratu) himself, directly surrounded by the Marut hosts, who else could possibly fight against them (the opponents), allied with Bṛhannadā?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 42. Even if it were Indra (Śatakratu) himself, directly surrounded by the Marut hosts, who else could possibly fight against them (the opponents), allied with Bṛhannadā?

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।बहुशः प्रतिषिद्धोऽसि न च वाचं नियच्छसि ।नियन्ता चेन्न विद्येत न कश्चिद्धर्ममाचरेत् ॥४३॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,bahuśaḥ pratiṣiddho'si na ca vācaṁ niyacchasi ,niyantā cenna vidyeta na kaściddharmamācaret.
english: Virata said: "You have been forbidden many times, yet you do not control your speech. If there were no controller, no one would practice righteousness (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 43. Virata said: "You have been forbidden many times, yet you do not control your speech. If there were no controller, no one would practice righteousness (dharma)."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः प्रकुपितो राजा तमक्षेणाहनद्भृशम् ।मुखे युधिष्ठिरं कोपान्नैवमित्येव भर्त्सयन् ॥४४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ prakupito rājā tamakṣeṇāhanadbhṛśam ,mukhe yudhiṣṭhiraṁ kopānnaivamityeva bhartsayan.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then the greatly enraged king, filled with anger, struck Yudhiṣṭhira severely on the face with the die, rebuking him with words like, "Not like this!"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 44. Vaiśampāyana said: Then the greatly enraged king, filled with anger, struck Yudhiṣṭhira severely on the face with the die, rebuking him with words like, "Not like this!"

sanskrit: बलवत्प्रतिविद्धस्य नस्तः शोणितमागमत् ।तदप्राप्तं महीं पार्थः पाणिभ्यां प्रत्यगृह्णत ॥४५॥
transliteration: balavatpratividdhasya nastaḥ śoṇitamāgamat ,tadaprāptaṁ mahīṁ pārthaḥ pāṇibhyāṁ pratyagṛhṇata.
english: From the nose of him who was violently struck, blood flowed. Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira) caught that blood with both hands before it could reach the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 45. From the nose of him who was violently struck, blood flowed. Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira) caught that blood with both hands before it could reach the ground.

sanskrit: अवैक्षत च धर्मात्मा द्रौपदीं पार्श्वतः स्थिताम् ।सा वेद तमभिप्रायं भर्तुश्चित्तवशानुगा ॥४६॥
transliteration: avaikṣata ca dharmātmā draupadīṁ pārśvataḥ sthitām ,sā veda tamabhiprāyaṁ bhartuścittavaśānugā.
english: And Yudhiṣṭhira, the virtuous one whose soul (ātman) embodied righteousness (dharma), looked at Draupadī, who was standing beside him. She, being devoted to her husband's will, understood his intention.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 46. And Yudhiṣṭhira, the virtuous one whose soul (ātman) embodied righteousness (dharma), looked at Draupadī, who was standing beside him. She, being devoted to her husband's will, understood his intention.

sanskrit: पूरयित्वा च सौवर्णं पात्रं कांस्यमनिन्दिता ।तच्छोणितं प्रत्यगृह्णाद्यत्प्रसुस्राव पाण्डवात् ॥४७॥
transliteration: pūrayitvā ca sauvarṇaṁ pātraṁ kāṁsyamaninditā ,tacchoṇitaṁ pratyagṛhṇādyatprasusrāva pāṇḍavāt.
english: And the blameless Draupadī, having filled a golden-bronze vessel, collected that blood which flowed forth from the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 47. And the blameless Draupadī, having filled a golden-bronze vessel, collected that blood which flowed forth from the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: अथोत्तरः शुभैर्गन्धैर्माल्यैश्च विविधैस्तथा ।अवकीर्यमाणः संहृष्टो नगरं स्वैरमागमत् ॥४८॥
transliteration: athottaraḥ śubhairgandhairmālyaiśca vividhaistathā ,avakīryamāṇaḥ saṁhṛṣṭo nagaraṁ svairamāgamat.
english: Then, Uttara, being showered with auspicious scents and various garlands, joyfully and leisurely entered the city.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 48. Then, Uttara, being showered with auspicious scents and various garlands, joyfully and leisurely entered the city.

sanskrit: सभाज्यमानः पौरैश्च स्त्रीभिर्जानपदैस्तथा ।आसाद्य भवनद्वारं पित्रे स प्रत्यहारयत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: sabhājyamānaḥ pauraiśca strībhirjānapadaistathā ,āsādya bhavanadvāraṁ pitre sa pratyahārayat.
english: Being honored by the citizens, women, and country folk, he, upon reaching the palace gate, had his arrival announced to his father.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 49. Being honored by the citizens, women, and country folk, he, upon reaching the palace gate, had his arrival announced to his father.

sanskrit: ततो द्वाःस्थः प्रविश्यैव विराटमिदमब्रवीत् ।बृहन्नडासहायस्ते पुत्रो द्वार्युत्तरः स्थितः ॥५०॥
transliteration: tato dvāḥsthaḥ praviśyaiva virāṭamidamabravīt ,bṛhannaḍāsahāyaste putro dvāryuttaraḥ sthitaḥ.
english: Then, the doorkeeper, having entered, spoke to Virāṭa thus: "Your son Uttara, accompanied by Bṛhannadā, is at the gate."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 50. Then, the doorkeeper, having entered, spoke to Virāṭa thus: "Your son Uttara, accompanied by Bṛhannadā, is at the gate."

sanskrit: ततो हृष्टो मत्स्यराजः क्षत्तारमिदमब्रवीत् ।प्रवेश्यतामुभौ तूर्णं दर्शनेप्सुरहं तयोः ॥५१॥
transliteration: tato hṛṣṭo matsyarājaḥ kṣattāramidamabravīt ,praveśyatāmubhau tūrṇaṁ darśanepsurahaṁ tayoḥ.
english: Then, the joyful King of Matsya spoke this to his chamberlain: "Let both of them be brought in quickly, for I am eager to see them."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 51. Then, the joyful King of Matsya spoke this to his chamberlain: "Let both of them be brought in quickly, for I am eager to see them."

sanskrit: क्षत्तारं कुरुराजस्तु शनैः कर्ण उपाजपत् ।उत्तरः प्रविशत्वेको न प्रवेश्या बृहन्नडा ॥५२॥
transliteration: kṣattāraṁ kururājastu śanaiḥ karṇa upājapat ,uttaraḥ praviśatveko na praveśyā bṛhannaḍā.
english: But the Kaurava king (Duryodhana) slowly whispered to the charioteer and Karna: "Uttara should enter alone; Brihannala must not be allowed to enter."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 52. But the Kaurava king (Duryodhana) slowly whispered to the charioteer and Karna: "Uttara should enter alone; Brihannala must not be allowed to enter."

sanskrit: एतस्य हि महाबाहो व्रतमेतत्समाहितम् ।यो ममाङ्गे व्रणं कुर्याच्छोणितं वापि दर्शयेत् ।अन्यत्र संग्रामगतान्न स जीवेदसंशयम् ॥५३॥
transliteration: etasya hi mahābāho vratametatsamāhitam ,yo mamāṅge vraṇaṁ kuryācchoṇitaṁ vāpi darśayet ,anyatra saṁgrāmagatānna sa jīvedasaṁśayam.
english: O mighty-armed one, this vow of his (Uttara's) is firmly established: 'Whoever makes a wound on my body or causes blood to be shed, unless it is in battle, that person will undoubtedly not live.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 53. O mighty-armed one, this vow of his (Uttara's) is firmly established: 'Whoever makes a wound on my body or causes blood to be shed, unless it is in battle, that person will undoubtedly not live.'

sanskrit: न मृष्याद्भृशसंक्रुद्धो मां दृष्ट्वैव सशोणितम् ।विराटमिह सामात्यं हन्यात्सबलवाहनम् ॥५४॥
transliteration: na mṛṣyādbhṛśasaṁkruddho māṁ dṛṣṭvaiva saśoṇitam ,virāṭamiha sāmātyaṁ hanyātsabalavāhanam.
english: Extremely enraged, just by seeing me covered in blood, he would not tolerate it. He would kill King Virata here, along with his ministers, army, and vehicles.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 63 Verse 54. Extremely enraged, just by seeing me covered in blood, he would not tolerate it. He would kill King Virata here, along with his ministers, army, and vehicles.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो राज्ञः सुतो ज्येष्ठः प्राविशत्पृथिवींजयः ।सोऽभिवाद्य पितुः पादौ धर्मराजमपश्यत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato rājñaḥ suto jyeṣṭhaḥ prāviśatpṛthivīṁjayaḥ ,so'bhivādya pituḥ pādau dharmarājamapaśyata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the king's eldest son, Pṛthivīṃjaya, entered. Having bowed to his father's feet, he saw Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the king's eldest son, Pṛthivīṃjaya, entered. Having bowed to his father's feet, he saw Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: स तं रुधिरसंसिक्तमनेकाग्रमनागसम् ।भूमावासीनमेकान्ते सैरन्ध्र्या समुपस्थितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: sa taṁ rudhirasaṁsiktamanekāgramanāgasam ,bhūmāvāsīnamekānte sairandhryā samupasthitam.
english: Uttara saw him (Kanka/Yudhisthira), drenched in blood, agitated, and innocent, seated on the ground in a secluded place, attended by Sairandhri.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 2. Uttara saw him (Kanka/Yudhisthira), drenched in blood, agitated, and innocent, seated on the ground in a secluded place, attended by Sairandhri.

sanskrit: ततः पप्रच्छ पितरं त्वरमाण इवोत्तरः ।केनायं ताडितो राजन्केन पापमिदं कृतम् ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ papraccha pitaraṁ tvaramāṇa ivottaraḥ ,kenāyaṁ tāḍito rājankena pāpamidaṁ kṛtam.
english: Then Uttara, appearing anxious, asked his father, "O King, who struck him? Who committed this offense?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 3. Then Uttara, appearing anxious, asked his father, "O King, who struck him? Who committed this offense?"

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।मयायं ताडितो जिह्मो न चाप्येतावदर्हति ।प्रशस्यमाने यः शूरे त्वयि षण्ढं प्रशंसति ॥४॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,mayāyaṁ tāḍito jihmo na cāpyetāvadarhati ,praśasyamāne yaḥ śūre tvayi ṣaṇḍhaṁ praśaṁsati.
english: Virata said, "I struck this contemptible man. He does not even deserve this much (lenience), for he praises an eunuch while you, the hero, are being lauded."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 4. Virata said, "I struck this contemptible man. He does not even deserve this much (lenience), for he praises an eunuch while you, the hero, are being lauded."

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।अकार्यं ते कृतं राजन्क्षिप्रमेव प्रसाद्यताम् ।मा त्वा ब्रह्मविषं घोरं समूलमपि निर्दहेत् ॥५॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,akāryaṁ te kṛtaṁ rājankṣiprameva prasādyatām ,mā tvā brahmaviṣaṁ ghoraṁ samūlamapi nirdahet.
english: Uttara said, "O King, you have done an improper thing. He must be appeased immediately, lest the terrible curse (brahmaviṣam) of a brahmin consume you completely, even from your very roots."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 5. Uttara said, "O King, you have done an improper thing. He must be appeased immediately, lest the terrible curse (brahmaviṣam) of a brahmin consume you completely, even from your very roots."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स पुत्रस्य वचः श्रुत्वा विराटो राष्ट्रवर्धनः ।क्षमयामास कौन्तेयं भस्मच्छन्नमिवानलम् ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa putrasya vacaḥ śrutvā virāṭo rāṣṭravardhanaḥ ,kṣamayāmāsa kaunteyaṁ bhasmacchannamivānalam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Upon hearing his son's words, Virāṭa, the augmentor of the kingdom, appeased Kuntī's son (Arjuna), who was like a fire hidden under ashes.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: Upon hearing his son's words, Virāṭa, the augmentor of the kingdom, appeased Kuntī's son (Arjuna), who was like a fire hidden under ashes.

sanskrit: क्षमयन्तं तु राजानं पाण्डवः प्रत्यभाषत ।चिरं क्षान्तमिदं राजन्न मन्युर्विद्यते मम ॥७॥
transliteration: kṣamayantaṁ tu rājānaṁ pāṇḍavaḥ pratyabhāṣata ,ciraṁ kṣāntamidaṁ rājanna manyurvidyate mama.
english: To the king who was thus appeasing him, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) replied, "O King, this has been endured for a long time; I have no anger."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 7. To the king who was thus appeasing him, the son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna) replied, "O King, this has been endured for a long time; I have no anger."

sanskrit: यदि ह्येतत्पतेद्भूमौ रुधिरं मम नस्ततः ।सराष्ट्रस्त्वं महाराज विनश्येथा न संशयः ॥८॥
transliteration: yadi hyetatpatedbhūmau rudhiraṁ mama nastataḥ ,sarāṣṭrastvaṁ mahārāja vinaśyethā na saṁśayaḥ.
english: "For if this blood of ours were to fall upon the ground, then you, O great king, would perish along with your kingdom; there is no doubt about it."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 8. "For if this blood of ours were to fall upon the ground, then you, O great king, would perish along with your kingdom; there is no doubt about it."

sanskrit: न दूषयामि ते राजन्यच्च हन्याददूषकम् ।बलवन्तं महाराज क्षिप्रं दारुणमाप्नुयात् ॥९॥
transliteration: na dūṣayāmi te rājanyacca hanyādadūṣakam ,balavantaṁ mahārāja kṣipraṁ dāruṇamāpnuyāt.
english: O King, I do not blame you for striking an innocent one. For, O great king, a terrible fate quickly befalls a powerful person.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 9. O King, I do not blame you for striking an innocent one. For, O great king, a terrible fate quickly befalls a powerful person.

sanskrit: शोणिते तु व्यतिक्रान्ते प्रविवेश बृहन्नडा ।अभिवाद्य विराटं च कङ्कं चाप्युपतिष्ठत ॥१०॥
transliteration: śoṇite tu vyatikrānte praviveśa bṛhannaḍā ,abhivādya virāṭaṁ ca kaṅkaṁ cāpyupatiṣṭhata.
english: After the battle (lit. bloodshed) had ceased, Brihannala entered. She greeted both Virata and Kanka, and then respectfully approached them.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 10. After the battle (lit. bloodshed) had ceased, Brihannala entered. She greeted both Virata and Kanka, and then respectfully approached them.

sanskrit: क्षमयित्वा तु कौरव्यं रणादुत्तरमागतम् ।प्रशशंस ततो मत्स्यः शृण्वतः सव्यसाचिनः ॥११॥
transliteration: kṣamayitvā tu kauravyaṁ raṇāduttaramāgatam ,praśaśaṁsa tato matsyaḥ śṛṇvataḥ savyasācinaḥ.
english: Having pardoned prince Uttara, who was of the Kuru lineage and had returned from the battle, the Matsya king then praised him while Savyasācin (Arjuna) listened.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 11. Having pardoned prince Uttara, who was of the Kuru lineage and had returned from the battle, the Matsya king then praised him while Savyasācin (Arjuna) listened.

sanskrit: त्वया दायादवानस्मि कैकेयीनन्दिवर्धन ।त्वया मे सदृशः पुत्रो न भूतो न भविष्यति ॥१२॥
transliteration: tvayā dāyādavānasmi kaikeyīnandivardhana ,tvayā me sadṛśaḥ putro na bhūto na bhaviṣyati.
english: Through you, I have progeny, O enhancer of Kaikeyi's joy. A son like you has never been mine, nor will there ever be one.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 12. Through you, I have progeny, O enhancer of Kaikeyi's joy. A son like you has never been mine, nor will there ever be one.

sanskrit: पदं पदसहस्रेण यश्चरन्नापराध्नुयात् ।तेन कर्णेन ते तात कथमासीत्समागमः ॥१३॥
transliteration: padaṁ padasahasreṇa yaścarannāparādhnuyāt ,tena karṇena te tāta kathamāsītsamāgamaḥ.
english: How could you, my dear one, have encountered Karna, who would not err even by a single step while walking a thousand steps?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 13. How could you, my dear one, have encountered Karna, who would not err even by a single step while walking a thousand steps?

sanskrit: मनुष्यलोके सकले यस्य तुल्यो न विद्यते ।यः समुद्र इवाक्षोभ्यः कालाग्निरिव दुःसहः ।तेन भीष्मेण ते तात कथमासीत्समागमः ॥१४॥
transliteration: manuṣyaloke sakale yasya tulyo na vidyate ,yaḥ samudra ivākṣobhyaḥ kālāgniriva duḥsahaḥ ,tena bhīṣmeṇa te tāta kathamāsītsamāgamaḥ.
english: In the entire human world, there is no one equal to him. He is unagitated like the ocean and irresistible like the fire of destruction. Dear son, how did you encounter Bhīṣma?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 14. In the entire human world, there is no one equal to him. He is unagitated like the ocean and irresistible like the fire of destruction. Dear son, how did you encounter Bhīṣma?

sanskrit: आचार्यो वृष्णिवीराणां पाण्डवानां च यो द्विजः ।सर्वक्षत्रस्य चाचार्यः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।तेन द्रोणेन ते तात कथमासीत्समागमः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ācāryo vṛṣṇivīrāṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ ca yo dvijaḥ ,sarvakṣatrasya cācāryaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,tena droṇena te tāta kathamāsītsamāgamaḥ.
english: He who is the teacher of the Vṛṣṇi heroes and the Pāṇḍavas, a brahmin (dvija), and indeed the preceptor of all kṣatriyas and the foremost among all who bear arms - dear son, how did you encounter Droṇa?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 15. He who is the teacher of the Vṛṣṇi heroes and the Pāṇḍavas, a brahmin (dvija), and indeed the preceptor of all kṣatriyas and the foremost among all who bear arms - dear son, how did you encounter Droṇa?

sanskrit: आचार्यपुत्रो यः शूरः सर्वशस्त्रभृतामपि ।अश्वत्थामेति विख्यातः कथं तेन समागमः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ācāryaputro yaḥ śūraḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāmapi ,aśvatthāmeti vikhyātaḥ kathaṁ tena samāgamaḥ.
english: How did you encounter Aśvatthāman, the brave son of the preceptor, who is renowned even among all who bear arms?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 16. How did you encounter Aśvatthāman, the brave son of the preceptor, who is renowned even among all who bear arms?

sanskrit: रणे यं प्रेक्ष्य सीदन्ति हृतस्वा वणिजो यथा ।कृपेण तेन ते तात कथमासीत्समागमः ॥१७॥
transliteration: raṇe yaṁ prekṣya sīdanti hṛtasvā vaṇijo yathā ,kṛpeṇa tena te tāta kathamāsītsamāgamaḥ.
english: Dear son, how did you encounter Kṛpa, seeing whom in battle, warriors become disheartened just like merchants whose goods have been seized?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 17. Dear son, how did you encounter Kṛpa, seeing whom in battle, warriors become disheartened just like merchants whose goods have been seized?

sanskrit: पर्वतं योऽभिविध्येत राजपुत्रो महेषुभिः ।दुर्योधनेन ते तात कथमासीत्समागमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: parvataṁ yo'bhividhyeta rājaputro maheṣubhiḥ ,duryodhanena te tāta kathamāsītsamāgamaḥ.
english: O dear one, how was your encounter with Duryodhana, [a prince] who might pierce a mountain with mighty arrows?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 18. O dear one, how was your encounter with Duryodhana, [a prince] who might pierce a mountain with mighty arrows?

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।न मया निर्जिता गावो न मया निर्जिताः परे ।कृतं तु कर्म तत्सर्वं देवपुत्रेण केनचित् ॥१९॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,na mayā nirjitā gāvo na mayā nirjitāḥ pare ,kṛtaṁ tu karma tatsarvaṁ devaputreṇa kenacit.
english: Uttara said: The cows were not conquered by me, nor were the enemies. Indeed, all that deed (karma) was accomplished by a certain son of a god.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 19. Uttara said: The cows were not conquered by me, nor were the enemies. Indeed, all that deed (karma) was accomplished by a certain son of a god.

sanskrit: स हि भीतं द्रवन्तं मां देवपुत्रो न्यवारयत् ।स चातिष्ठद्रथोपस्थे वज्रहस्तनिभो युवा ॥२०॥
transliteration: sa hi bhītaṁ dravantaṁ māṁ devaputro nyavārayat ,sa cātiṣṭhadrathopasthe vajrahastanibho yuvā.
english: Indeed, that son of a god stopped me when I was frightened and fleeing. And he, a young man resembling one with a thunderbolt in hand, stood on the chariot platform.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 20. Indeed, that son of a god stopped me when I was frightened and fleeing. And he, a young man resembling one with a thunderbolt in hand, stood on the chariot platform.

sanskrit: तेन ता निर्जिता गावस्तेन ते कुरवो जिताः ।तस्य तत्कर्म वीरस्य न मया तात तत्कृतम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tena tā nirjitā gāvastena te kuravo jitāḥ ,tasya tatkarma vīrasya na mayā tāta tatkṛtam.
english: By him those cows were indeed conquered, and by him those Kurus were defeated. That deed (karma) belongs to that hero, O dear one; it was not done by me.
