search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 21. By him those cows were indeed conquered, and by him those Kurus were defeated. That deed (karma) belongs to that hero, O dear one; it was not done by me.

sanskrit: स हि शारद्वतं द्रोणं द्रोणपुत्रं च वीर्यवान् ।सूतपुत्रं च भीष्मं च चकार विमुखाञ्शरैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa hi śāradvataṁ droṇaṁ droṇaputraṁ ca vīryavān ,sūtaputraṁ ca bhīṣmaṁ ca cakāra vimukhāñśaraiḥ.
english: Indeed, the mighty one (Arjuna) forced Droṇa, the son of Śaradvan, as well as Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), Karṇa (the son of Sūta), and Bhīṣma to retreat with his arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 22. Indeed, the mighty one (Arjuna) forced Droṇa, the son of Śaradvan, as well as Droṇa's son (Aśvatthāmā), Karṇa (the son of Sūta), and Bhīṣma to retreat with his arrows.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं च समरे सनागमिव यूथपम् ।प्रभग्नमब्रवीद्भीतं राजपुत्रं महाबलम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ ca samare sanāgamiva yūthapam ,prabhagnamabravīdbhītaṁ rājaputraṁ mahābalam.
english: And to Duryodhana in battle, who was like a commander of elephants (sanāgam iva yūthapam), he (Arjuna) spoke, addressing the mighty prince who was shattered and terrified.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 23. And to Duryodhana in battle, who was like a commander of elephants (sanāgam iva yūthapam), he (Arjuna) spoke, addressing the mighty prince who was shattered and terrified.

sanskrit: न हास्तिनपुरे त्राणं तव पश्यामि किंचन ।व्यायामेन परीप्सस्व जीवितं कौरवात्मज ॥२४॥
transliteration: na hāstinapure trāṇaṁ tava paśyāmi kiṁcana ,vyāyāmena parīpsasva jīvitaṁ kauravātmaja.
english: O son of Kuru, I see no refuge for you anywhere in Hastinapura. Strive with effort (vyāyāmena) to protect your life.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 24. O son of Kuru, I see no refuge for you anywhere in Hastinapura. Strive with effort (vyāyāmena) to protect your life.

sanskrit: न मोक्ष्यसे पलायंस्त्वं राजन्युद्धे मनः कुरु ।पृथिवीं भोक्ष्यसे जित्वा हतो वा स्वर्गमाप्स्यसि ॥२५॥
transliteration: na mokṣyase palāyaṁstvaṁ rājanyuddhe manaḥ kuru ,pṛthivīṁ bhokṣyase jitvā hato vā svargamāpsyasi.
english: O king, you will not escape by fleeing. Instead, focus your mind (manaḥ) on the battle. You will enjoy the earth if you conquer, or attain heaven if you are killed.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 25. O king, you will not escape by fleeing. Instead, focus your mind (manaḥ) on the battle. You will enjoy the earth if you conquer, or attain heaven if you are killed.

sanskrit: स निवृत्तो नरव्याघ्रो मुञ्चन्वज्रनिभाञ्शरान् ।सचिवैः संवृतो राजा रथे नाग इव श्वसन् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa nivṛtto naravyāghro muñcanvajranibhāñśarān ,sacivaiḥ saṁvṛto rājā rathe nāga iva śvasan.
english: The king, that tiger among men, returned, releasing arrows like thunderbolts. Surrounded by his counselors, he breathed heavily in his chariot like an elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 26. The king, that tiger among men, returned, releasing arrows like thunderbolts. Surrounded by his counselors, he breathed heavily in his chariot like an elephant.

sanskrit: तत्र मे रोमहर्षोऽभूदूरुस्तम्भश्च मारिष ।यदभ्रघनसंकाशमनीकं व्यधमच्छरैः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tatra me romaharṣo'bhūdūrustambhaśca māriṣa ,yadabhraghanasaṁkāśamanīkaṁ vyadhamaccharaiḥ.
english: O respected one, at that moment, I experienced a thrill (romaharṣa) and a stiffening of my thighs, because he scattered with arrows that army which resembled a dense mass of clouds.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 27. O respected one, at that moment, I experienced a thrill (romaharṣa) and a stiffening of my thighs, because he scattered with arrows that army which resembled a dense mass of clouds.

sanskrit: तत्प्रणुद्य रथानीकं सिंहसंहननो युवा ।कुरूंस्तान्प्रहसन्राजन्वासांस्यपहरद्बली ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatpraṇudya rathānīkaṁ siṁhasaṁhanano yuvā ,kurūṁstānprahasanrājanvāsāṁsyapaharadbalī.
english: O king, that young and powerful warrior, who had a lion's physique, having repelled that chariot army, laughed as he stripped those Kurus of their garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 28. O king, that young and powerful warrior, who had a lion's physique, having repelled that chariot army, laughed as he stripped those Kurus of their garments.

sanskrit: एकेन तेन वीरेण षड्रथाः परिवारिताः ।शार्दूलेनेव मत्तेन मृगास्तृणचरा वने ॥२९॥
transliteration: ekena tena vīreṇa ṣaḍrathāḥ parivāritāḥ ,śārdūleneva mattena mṛgāstṛṇacarā vane.
english: By that one hero, six charioteers were surrounded, just as grass-eating deer in a forest are surrounded by a frenzied tiger.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 29. By that one hero, six charioteers were surrounded, just as grass-eating deer in a forest are surrounded by a frenzied tiger.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।क्व स वीरो महाबाहुर्देवपुत्रो महायशाः ।यो मे धनमवाजैषीत्कुरुभिर्ग्रस्तमाहवे ॥३०॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,kva sa vīro mahābāhurdevaputro mahāyaśāḥ ,yo me dhanamavājaiṣītkurubhirgrastamāhave.
english: Virāṭa said, 'Where is that hero, the mighty-armed, the son of a god, the very famous one, who recovered my wealth that was seized by the Kurus in battle?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 30. Virāṭa said, 'Where is that hero, the mighty-armed, the son of a god, the very famous one, who recovered my wealth that was seized by the Kurus in battle?'

sanskrit: इच्छामि तमहं द्रष्टुमर्चितुं च महाबलम् ।येन मे त्वं च गावश्च रक्षिता देवसूनुना ॥३१॥
transliteration: icchāmi tamahaṁ draṣṭumarcituṁ ca mahābalam ,yena me tvaṁ ca gāvaśca rakṣitā devasūnunā.
english: I wish to see and honor that very powerful one, by whom you and my cows were protected, that son of a god.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 31. I wish to see and honor that very powerful one, by whom you and my cows were protected, that son of a god.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।अन्तर्धानं गतस्तात देवपुत्रः प्रतापवान् ।स तु श्वो वा परश्वो वा मन्ये प्रादुर्भविष्यति ॥३२॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,antardhānaṁ gatastāta devaputraḥ pratāpavān ,sa tu śvo vā paraśvo vā manye prādurbhaviṣyati.
english: Uttara replied, 'Dear father, that glorious son of a god has gone into hiding. But I think he will reappear either tomorrow or the day after tomorrow.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 32. Uttara replied, 'Dear father, that glorious son of a god has gone into hiding. But I think he will reappear either tomorrow or the day after tomorrow.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमाख्यायमानं तु छन्नं सत्रेण पाण्डवम् ।वसन्तं तत्र नाज्ञासीद्विराटः पार्थमर्जुनम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamākhyāyamānaṁ tu channaṁ satreṇa pāṇḍavam ,vasantaṁ tatra nājñāsīdvirāṭaḥ pārthamarjunam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said, 'Though the Pāṇḍava Arjuna, living there concealed by his disguise (satra), was thus being spoken of, Virāṭa did not recognize Partha (Arjuna).'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 33. Vaiśaṃpāyana said, 'Though the Pāṇḍava Arjuna, living there concealed by his disguise (satra), was thus being spoken of, Virāṭa did not recognize Partha (Arjuna).'

sanskrit: ततः पार्थोऽभ्यनुज्ञातो विराटेन महात्मना ।प्रददौ तानि वासांसि विराटदुहितुः स्वयम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tataḥ pārtho'bhyanujñāto virāṭena mahātmanā ,pradadau tāni vāsāṁsi virāṭaduhituḥ svayam.
english: Then, having been permitted by the great-souled Virāṭa, Pārtha (Arjuna) himself gave those clothes to Virāṭa's daughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 34. Then, having been permitted by the great-souled Virāṭa, Pārtha (Arjuna) himself gave those clothes to Virāṭa's daughter.

sanskrit: उत्तरा तु महार्हाणि विविधानि तनूनि च ।प्रतिगृह्याभवत्प्रीता तानि वासांसि भामिनी ॥३५॥
transliteration: uttarā tu mahārhāṇi vividhāni tanūni ca ,pratigṛhyābhavatprītā tāni vāsāṁsi bhāminī.
english: But Uttarā, the beautiful lady, became delighted after receiving those very valuable, various, and delicate garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 35. But Uttarā, the beautiful lady, became delighted after receiving those very valuable, various, and delicate garments.

sanskrit: मन्त्रयित्वा तु कौन्तेय उत्तरेण रहस्तदा ।इतिकर्तव्यतां सर्वां राजन्यथ युधिष्ठिरे ॥३६॥
transliteration: mantrayitvā tu kaunteya uttareṇa rahastadā ,itikartavyatāṁ sarvāṁ rājanyatha yudhiṣṭhire.
english: O King, then Kaunteya (Arjuna), after having secretly consulted with Uttara regarding the entire course of action, just as he would with Yudhiṣṭhira...
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 36. O King, then Kaunteya (Arjuna), after having secretly consulted with Uttara regarding the entire course of action, just as he would with Yudhiṣṭhira...

sanskrit: ततस्तथा तद्व्यदधाद्यथावत्पुरुषर्षभ ।सह पुत्रेण मत्स्यस्य प्रहृष्टो भरतर्षभः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tatastathā tadvyadadhādyathāvatpuruṣarṣabha ,saha putreṇa matsyasya prahṛṣṭo bharatarṣabhaḥ.
english: O best of men, O chief of the Bhāratas, then (Arjuna), greatly pleased, arranged that matter appropriately with the son of Matsya (King Virāṭa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 64 Verse 37. O best of men, O chief of the Bhāratas, then (Arjuna), greatly pleased, arranged that matter appropriately with the son of Matsya (King Virāṭa).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्तृतीये दिवसे भ्रातरः पञ्च पाण्डवाः ।स्नाताः शुक्लाम्बरधराः समये चरितव्रताः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatastṛtīye divase bhrātaraḥ pañca pāṇḍavāḥ ,snātāḥ śuklāmbaradharāḥ samaye caritavratāḥ.
english: Vaiśampayana said: Then, on the third day, the five Pāṇḍava brothers, having bathed and dressed in white garments, having fulfilled their vows, were present at the appointed time.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 1. Vaiśampayana said: Then, on the third day, the five Pāṇḍava brothers, having bathed and dressed in white garments, having fulfilled their vows, were present at the appointed time.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं पुरस्कृत्य सर्वाभरणभूषिताः ।अभिपद्मा यथा नागा भ्राजमाना महारथाः ॥२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ puraskṛtya sarvābharaṇabhūṣitāḥ ,abhipadmā yathā nāgā bhrājamānā mahārathāḥ.
english: Having made Yudhiṣṭhira their leader, adorned with all their ornaments, those great charioteers shone like magnificent elephants amidst lotuses.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 2. Having made Yudhiṣṭhira their leader, adorned with all their ornaments, those great charioteers shone like magnificent elephants amidst lotuses.

sanskrit: विराटस्य सभां गत्वा भूमिपालासनेष्वथ ।निषेदुः पावकप्रख्याः सर्वे धिष्ण्येष्विवाग्नयः ॥३॥
transliteration: virāṭasya sabhāṁ gatvā bhūmipālāsaneṣvatha ,niṣeduḥ pāvakaprakhyāḥ sarve dhiṣṇyeṣvivāgnayaḥ.
english: Having gone to Virāṭa's assembly hall, they then sat down on the royal seats, all of them appearing like blazing fires on their altars.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 3. Having gone to Virāṭa's assembly hall, they then sat down on the royal seats, all of them appearing like blazing fires on their altars.

sanskrit: तेषु तत्रोपविष्टेषु विराटः पृथिवीपतिः ।आजगाम सभां कर्तुं राजकार्याणि सर्वशः ॥४॥
transliteration: teṣu tatropaviṣṭeṣu virāṭaḥ pṛthivīpatiḥ ,ājagāma sabhāṁ kartuṁ rājakāryāṇi sarvaśaḥ.
english: When they had taken their seats there, Virāṭa, the lord of the earth, came to the assembly to attend to all royal duties.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 4. When they had taken their seats there, Virāṭa, the lord of the earth, came to the assembly to attend to all royal duties.

sanskrit: श्रीमतः पाण्डवान्दृष्ट्वा ज्वलतः पावकानिव ।अथ मत्स्योऽब्रवीत्कङ्कं देवरूपमवस्थितम् ।मरुद्गणैरुपासीनं त्रिदशानामिवेश्वरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: śrīmataḥ pāṇḍavāndṛṣṭvā jvalataḥ pāvakāniva ,atha matsyo'bravītkaṅkaṁ devarūpamavasthitam ,marudgaṇairupāsīnaṁ tridaśānāmiveśvaram.
english: After seeing the glorious Pāṇḍavas, who shone like blazing fires, King Virāṭa (Matsya) then spoke to Kaṅka (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was present in a divine form, attended by the hosts of Maruts like the lord of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 5. After seeing the glorious Pāṇḍavas, who shone like blazing fires, King Virāṭa (Matsya) then spoke to Kaṅka (Yudhiṣṭhira), who was present in a divine form, attended by the hosts of Maruts like the lord of the gods.

sanskrit: स किलाक्षातिवापस्त्वं सभास्तारो मया कृतः ।अथ राजासने कस्मादुपविष्टोऽस्यलंकृतः ॥६॥
transliteration: sa kilākṣātivāpastvaṁ sabhāstāro mayā kṛtaḥ ,atha rājāsane kasmādupaviṣṭo'syalaṁkṛtaḥ.
english: 'You are, it is said, a dice player appointed by me as a courtier. Why then are you seated on the royal throne, so richly adorned?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 6. 'You are, it is said, a dice player appointed by me as a courtier. Why then are you seated on the royal throne, so richly adorned?'

sanskrit: परिहासेप्सया वाक्यं विराटस्य निशम्य तत् ।स्मयमानोऽर्जुनो राजन्निदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥७॥
transliteration: parihāsepsayā vākyaṁ virāṭasya niśamya tat ,smayamāno'rjuno rājannidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: O King, having heard Virāṭa's words, spoken with the intention of jest, Arjuna, smiling, said this in reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 7. O King, having heard Virāṭa's words, spoken with the intention of jest, Arjuna, smiling, said this in reply.

sanskrit: इन्द्रस्याप्यासनं राजन्नयमारोढुमर्हति ।ब्रह्मण्यः श्रुतवांस्त्यागी यज्ञशीलो दृढव्रतः ॥८॥
transliteration: indrasyāpyāsanaṁ rājannayamāroḍhumarhati ,brahmaṇyaḥ śrutavāṁstyāgī yajñaśīlo dṛḍhavrataḥ.
english: O king, this person (Arjuna) is worthy of ascending even Indra's throne, for he is devoted to sacred knowledge (brāhmaṇya), highly learned, a generous renunciant (tyāgī), diligent in performing Vedic rituals (yajñaśīla), and steadfast in his vows (dṛḍhavrata).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 8. O king, this person (Arjuna) is worthy of ascending even Indra's throne, for he is devoted to sacred knowledge (brāhmaṇya), highly learned, a generous renunciant (tyāgī), diligent in performing Vedic rituals (yajñaśīla), and steadfast in his vows (dṛḍhavrata).

sanskrit: अयं कुरूणामृषभः कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।अस्य कीर्तिः स्थिता लोके सूर्यस्येवोद्यतः प्रभा ॥९॥
transliteration: ayaṁ kurūṇāmṛṣabhaḥ kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,asya kīrtiḥ sthitā loke sūryasyevodyataḥ prabhā.
english: This is Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, the foremost among the Kurus. His fame is established in the world like the splendor of the rising sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 9. This is Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, the foremost among the Kurus. His fame is established in the world like the splendor of the rising sun.

sanskrit: संसरन्ति दिशः सर्वा यशसोऽस्य गभस्तयः ।उदितस्येव सूर्यस्य तेजसोऽनु गभस्तयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: saṁsaranti diśaḥ sarvā yaśaso'sya gabhastayaḥ ,uditasyeva sūryasya tejaso'nu gabhastayaḥ.
english: The rays of his fame spread throughout all directions, just as the rays follow the brilliance of the risen sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 10. The rays of his fame spread throughout all directions, just as the rays follow the brilliance of the risen sun.

sanskrit: एनं दश सहस्राणि कुञ्जराणां तरस्विनाम् ।अन्वयुः पृष्ठतो राजन्यावदध्यावसत्कुरून् ॥११॥
transliteration: enaṁ daśa sahasrāṇi kuñjarāṇāṁ tarasvinām ,anvayuḥ pṛṣṭhato rājanyāvadadhyāvasatkurūn.
english: O king, ten thousand mighty elephants followed him from behind, for as long as he dwelled among the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 11. O king, ten thousand mighty elephants followed him from behind, for as long as he dwelled among the Kurus.

sanskrit: त्रिंशदेनं सहस्राणि रथाः काञ्चनमालिनः ।सदश्वैरुपसंपन्नाः पृष्ठतोऽनुययुः सदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: triṁśadenaṁ sahasrāṇi rathāḥ kāñcanamālinaḥ ,sadaśvairupasaṁpannāḥ pṛṣṭhato'nuyayuḥ sadā.
english: Thirty thousand gold-adorned chariots, equipped with excellent horses, always followed him from behind.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 12. Thirty thousand gold-adorned chariots, equipped with excellent horses, always followed him from behind.

sanskrit: एनमष्टशताः सूताः सुमृष्टमणिकुण्डलाः ।अस्तुवन्मागधैः सार्धं पुरा शक्रमिवर्षयः ॥१३॥
transliteration: enamaṣṭaśatāḥ sūtāḥ sumṛṣṭamaṇikuṇḍalāḥ ,astuvanmāgadhaiḥ sārdhaṁ purā śakramivarṣayaḥ.
english: Eight hundred bards, adorned with gleaming jeweled earrings, along with panegyrists from Magadha, formerly praised him, just as sages praised Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 13. Eight hundred bards, adorned with gleaming jeweled earrings, along with panegyrists from Magadha, formerly praised him, just as sages praised Indra.

sanskrit: एनं नित्यमुपासन्त कुरवः किंकरा यथा ।सर्वे च राजन्राजानो धनेश्वरमिवामराः ॥१४॥
transliteration: enaṁ nityamupāsanta kuravaḥ kiṁkarā yathā ,sarve ca rājanrājāno dhaneśvaramivāmarāḥ.
english: O king, the Kurus always served him like attendants, and indeed, all kings served him as the gods serve Kubera, the lord of wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 14. O king, the Kurus always served him like attendants, and indeed, all kings served him as the gods serve Kubera, the lord of wealth.

sanskrit: एष सर्वान्महीपालान्करमाहारयत्तदा ।वैश्यानिव महाराज विवशान्स्ववशानपि ॥१५॥
transliteration: eṣa sarvānmahīpālānkaramāhārayattadā ,vaiśyāniva mahārāja vivaśānsvavaśānapi.
english: O great king, he then collected taxes from all rulers, just as one collects from Vaishyas, whether those rulers were unwilling or even self-reliant.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 15. O great king, he then collected taxes from all rulers, just as one collects from Vaishyas, whether those rulers were unwilling or even self-reliant.

sanskrit: अष्टाशीतिसहस्राणि स्नातकानां महात्मनाम् ।उपजीवन्ति राजानमेनं सुचरितव्रतम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: aṣṭāśītisahasrāṇi snātakānāṁ mahātmanām ,upajīvanti rājānamenaṁ sucaritavratam.
english: Eighty-eight thousand noble graduates (snātaka) who have faithfully observed their sacred vows depend on this king.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 16. Eighty-eight thousand noble graduates (snātaka) who have faithfully observed their sacred vows depend on this king.

sanskrit: एष वृद्धाननाथांश्च व्यङ्गान्पङ्गूंश्च मानवान् ।पुत्रवत्पालयामास प्रजा धर्मेण चाभिभो ॥१७॥
transliteration: eṣa vṛddhānanāthāṁśca vyaṅgānpaṅgūṁśca mānavān ,putravatpālayāmāsa prajā dharmeṇa cābhibho.
english: O lord, he protected the elderly, the helpless, the disabled, and the lame people, as well as his subjects, like his own sons, in accordance with his intrinsic nature (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 17. O lord, he protected the elderly, the helpless, the disabled, and the lame people, as well as his subjects, like his own sons, in accordance with his intrinsic nature (dharma).

sanskrit: एष धर्मे दमे चैव क्रोधे चापि यतव्रतः ।महाप्रसादो ब्रह्मण्यः सत्यवादी च पार्थिवः ॥१८॥
transliteration: eṣa dharme dame caiva krodhe cāpi yatavrataḥ ,mahāprasādo brahmaṇyaḥ satyavādī ca pārthivaḥ.
english: This king (pārthiva) is disciplined in his observances concerning (dharma) natural law, self-control, and even anger. He is greatly benevolent, devoted to Brahmins, and truthful.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 18. This king (pārthiva) is disciplined in his observances concerning (dharma) natural law, self-control, and even anger. He is greatly benevolent, devoted to Brahmins, and truthful.

sanskrit: श्रीप्रतापेन चैतस्य तप्यते स सुयोधनः ।सगणः सह कर्णेन सौबलेनापि वा विभुः ॥१९॥
transliteration: śrīpratāpena caitasya tapyate sa suyodhanaḥ ,sagaṇaḥ saha karṇena saubalenāpi vā vibhuḥ.
english: And due to the glorious might of this one, that Suyodhana (Duryodhana), along with his retinue, Karṇa, and also Saubala (Śakuni), is tormented. Indeed, even the powerful ruler (Duryodhana) suffers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 19. And due to the glorious might of this one, that Suyodhana (Duryodhana), along with his retinue, Karṇa, and also Saubala (Śakuni), is tormented. Indeed, even the powerful ruler (Duryodhana) suffers.

sanskrit: न शक्यन्ते ह्यस्य गुणाः प्रसंख्यातुं नरेश्वर ।एष धर्मपरो नित्यमानृशंस्यश्च पाण्डवः ॥२०॥
transliteration: na śakyante hyasya guṇāḥ prasaṁkhyātuṁ nareśvara ,eṣa dharmaparo nityamānṛśaṁsyaśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Indeed, O lord of men, his virtues cannot be fully enumerated. This Pāṇḍava is always dedicated to (dharma) his intrinsic nature and is benevolent.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 20. Indeed, O lord of men, his virtues cannot be fully enumerated. This Pāṇḍava is always dedicated to (dharma) his intrinsic nature and is benevolent.

sanskrit: एवंयुक्तो महाराजः पाण्डवः पार्थिवर्षभः ।कथं नार्हति राजार्हमासनं पृथिवीपतिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṁyukto mahārājaḥ pāṇḍavaḥ pārthivarṣabhaḥ ,kathaṁ nārhati rājārhamāsanaṁ pṛthivīpatiḥ.
english: How is it that such a great king, the Pāṇḍava, the foremost among rulers and lord of the earth, being thus appropriately endowed, is not worthy of a royal seat?
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 65 Verse 21. How is it that such a great king, the Pāṇḍava, the foremost among rulers and lord of the earth, being thus appropriately endowed, is not worthy of a royal seat?

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।यद्येष राजा कौरव्यः कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।कतमोऽस्यार्जुनो भ्राता भीमश्च कतमो बली ॥१॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,yadyeṣa rājā kauravyaḥ kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,katamo'syārjuno bhrātā bhīmaśca katamo balī.
english: Virāṭa said: 'If this king is Yudhiṣṭhira, the scion of Kuru and son of Kuntī, then who among these is his brother Arjuna, and who is the mighty Bhīma?'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 1. Virāṭa said: 'If this king is Yudhiṣṭhira, the scion of Kuru and son of Kuntī, then who among these is his brother Arjuna, and who is the mighty Bhīma?'

sanskrit: नकुलः सहदेवो वा द्रौपदी वा यशस्विनी ।यदा द्यूते जिताः पार्था न प्राज्ञायन्त ते क्वचित् ॥२॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ sahadevo vā draupadī vā yaśasvinī ,yadā dyūte jitāḥ pārthā na prājñāyanta te kvacit.
english: And who are Nakula, Sahadeva, or the renowned Draupadī? For when the Pārthas were defeated in the gambling game, they were not recognized anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 2. And who are Nakula, Sahadeva, or the renowned Draupadī? For when the Pārthas were defeated in the gambling game, they were not recognized anywhere.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।य एष बल्लवो ब्रूते सूदस्तव नराधिप ।एष भीमो महाबाहुर्भीमवेगपराक्रमः ॥३॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,ya eṣa ballavo brūte sūdastava narādhipa ,eṣa bhīmo mahābāhurbhīmavegaparākramaḥ.
english: Arjuna said: 'O lord of men, this one who speaks, this Ballava, your cook - he is Bhīma, the mighty-armed, possessing formidable speed and prowess.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 3. Arjuna said: 'O lord of men, this one who speaks, this Ballava, your cook - he is Bhīma, the mighty-armed, possessing formidable speed and prowess.'

sanskrit: एष क्रोधवशान्हत्वा पर्वते गन्धमादने ।सौगन्धिकानि दिव्यानि कृष्णार्थे समुपाहरत् ॥४॥
transliteration: eṣa krodhavaśānhatvā parvate gandhamādane ,saugandhikāni divyāni kṛṣṇārthe samupāharat.
english: Having slain those influenced by anger on Mount Gandhamādana, he gathered and brought divine, fragrant lotuses for Kṛṣṇa's sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 4. Having slain those influenced by anger on Mount Gandhamādana, he gathered and brought divine, fragrant lotuses for Kṛṣṇa's sake.

sanskrit: गन्धर्व एष वै हन्ता कीचकानां दुरात्मनाम् ।व्याघ्रानृक्षान्वराहांश्च हतवान्स्त्रीपुरे तव ॥५॥
transliteration: gandharva eṣa vai hantā kīcakānāṁ durātmanām ,vyāghrānṛkṣānvarāhāṁśca hatavānstrīpure tava.
english: Indeed, this Gandharva is the slayer of the wicked Kīcakas. He also killed tigers, bears, and boars in your women's quarter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 5. Indeed, this Gandharva is the slayer of the wicked Kīcakas. He also killed tigers, bears, and boars in your women's quarter.

sanskrit: यश्चासीदश्वबन्धस्ते नकुलोऽयं परंतपः ।गोसंख्यः सहदेवश्च माद्रीपुत्रौ महारथौ ॥६॥
transliteration: yaścāsīdaśvabandhaste nakulo'yaṁ paraṁtapaḥ ,gosaṁkhyaḥ sahadevaśca mādrīputrau mahārathau.
english: And this Nakula, the tormentor of enemies, who was your horse-keeper, and Sahadeva, your cow-keeper – these two are the sons of Mādrī and great chariot-warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 6. And this Nakula, the tormentor of enemies, who was your horse-keeper, and Sahadeva, your cow-keeper – these two are the sons of Mādrī and great chariot-warriors.

sanskrit: शृङ्गारवेषाभरणौ रूपवन्तौ यशस्विनौ ।नानारथसहस्राणां समर्थौ पुरुषर्षभौ ॥७॥
transliteration: śṛṅgāraveṣābharaṇau rūpavantau yaśasvinau ,nānārathasahasrāṇāṁ samarthau puruṣarṣabhau.
english: They are adorned with fine garments and ornaments, handsome, and glorious. They are two chief among men, capable of fighting thousands of diverse chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 7. They are adorned with fine garments and ornaments, handsome, and glorious. They are two chief among men, capable of fighting thousands of diverse chariots.

sanskrit: एषा पद्मपलाशाक्षी सुमध्या चारुहासिनी ।सैरन्ध्री द्रौपदी राजन्यत्कृते कीचका हताः ॥८॥
transliteration: eṣā padmapalāśākṣī sumadhyā cāruhāsinī ,sairandhrī draupadī rājanyatkṛte kīcakā hatāḥ.
english: O King, this woman with eyes like lotus petals, a slender waist, and a beautiful smile is Draupadī, disguised as a female attendant (sairandhrī). It was for her sake that the Kīcakas were slain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 8. O King, this woman with eyes like lotus petals, a slender waist, and a beautiful smile is Draupadī, disguised as a female attendant (sairandhrī). It was for her sake that the Kīcakas were slain.

sanskrit: अर्जुनोऽहं महाराज व्यक्तं ते श्रोत्रमागतः ।भीमादवरजः पार्थो यमाभ्यां चापि पूर्वजः ॥९॥
transliteration: arjuno'haṁ mahārāja vyaktaṁ te śrotramāgataḥ ,bhīmādavarajaḥ pārtho yamābhyāṁ cāpi pūrvajaḥ.
english: O great king, I am Arjuna. Surely, this news has reached your ears: Pārtha (Arjuna) is younger than Bhīma, and also the elder brother of the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 9. O great king, I am Arjuna. Surely, this news has reached your ears: Pārtha (Arjuna) is younger than Bhīma, and also the elder brother of the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva).

sanskrit: उषिताः स्म महाराज सुखं तव निवेशने ।अज्ञातवासमुषिता गर्भवास इव प्रजाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: uṣitāḥ sma mahārāja sukhaṁ tava niveśane ,ajñātavāsamuṣitā garbhavāsa iva prajāḥ.
english: O great king, we have resided comfortably in your dwelling, spending our period of incognito exile like beings residing within a womb.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 10. O great king, we have resided comfortably in your dwelling, spending our period of incognito exile like beings residing within a womb.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।यदार्जुनेन ते वीराः कथिताः पञ्च पाण्डवाः ।तदार्जुनस्य वैराटिः कथयामास विक्रमम् ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,yadārjunena te vīrāḥ kathitāḥ pañca pāṇḍavāḥ ,tadārjunasya vairāṭiḥ kathayāmāsa vikramam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: When Arjuna had identified those five heroic Pāṇḍavas, then Uttarā, the son of Virāṭa, recounted Arjuna's valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 11. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: When Arjuna had identified those five heroic Pāṇḍavas, then Uttarā, the son of Virāṭa, recounted Arjuna's valor.

sanskrit: अयं स द्विषतां मध्ये मृगाणामिव केसरी ।अचरद्रथवृन्देषु निघ्नंस्तेषां वरान्वरान् ॥१२॥
transliteration: ayaṁ sa dviṣatāṁ madhye mṛgāṇāmiva kesarī ,acaradrathavṛndeṣu nighnaṁsteṣāṁ varānvarān.
english: He, like a lion among deer, moved amidst the throngs of chariots, striking down the foremost of his enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 12. He, like a lion among deer, moved amidst the throngs of chariots, striking down the foremost of his enemies.

sanskrit: अनेन विद्धो मातङ्गो महानेकेषुणा हतः ।हिरण्यकक्ष्यः संग्रामे दन्ताभ्यामगमन्महीम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: anena viddho mātaṅgo mahānekeṣuṇā hataḥ ,hiraṇyakakṣyaḥ saṁgrāme dantābhyāmagamanmahīm.
english: By him, a mighty elephant adorned with a golden harness was struck and killed by a single arrow in battle, falling to the earth on its tusks.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 13. By him, a mighty elephant adorned with a golden harness was struck and killed by a single arrow in battle, falling to the earth on its tusks.

sanskrit: अनेन विजिता गावो जिताश्च कुरवो युधि ।अस्य शङ्खप्रणादेन कर्णौ मे बधिरीकृतौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: anena vijitā gāvo jitāśca kuravo yudhi ,asya śaṅkhapraṇādena karṇau me badhirīkṛtau.
english: He conquered the cows and defeated the Kurus in battle. My ears have been deafened by the sound of his conch.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 14. He conquered the cows and defeated the Kurus in battle. My ears have been deafened by the sound of his conch.

sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा मत्स्यराजः प्रतापवान् ।उत्तरं प्रत्युवाचेदमभिपन्नो युधिष्ठिरे ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā matsyarājaḥ pratāpavān ,uttaraṁ pratyuvācedamabhipanno yudhiṣṭhire.
english: Having heard those words, the mighty Matsya king offered this reply to Yudhiṣṭhira, who had addressed him.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 15. Having heard those words, the mighty Matsya king offered this reply to Yudhiṣṭhira, who had addressed him.

sanskrit: प्रसादनं पाण्डवस्य प्राप्तकालं हि रोचये ।उत्तरां च प्रयच्छामि पार्थाय यदि ते मतम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: prasādanaṁ pāṇḍavasya prāptakālaṁ hi rocaye ,uttarāṁ ca prayacchāmi pārthāya yadi te matam.
english: I certainly approve of conciliating the Pāṇḍava, as it is appropriate now. And I will give Uttarā to Arjuna (Pārtha) if it is your wish.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 16. I certainly approve of conciliating the Pāṇḍava, as it is appropriate now. And I will give Uttarā to Arjuna (Pārtha) if it is your wish.

sanskrit: उत्तर उवाच ।अर्च्याः पूज्याश्च मान्याश्च प्राप्तकालं च मे मतम् ।पूज्यन्तां पूजनार्हाश्च महाभागाश्च पाण्डवाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: uttara uvāca ,arcyāḥ pūjyāśca mānyāśca prāptakālaṁ ca me matam ,pūjyantāṁ pūjanārhāśca mahābhāgāśca pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Uttara said: 'My opinion (mata) is that they are worthy of honor, reverence, and respect, and that this is indeed appropriate now. Let the greatly fortunate Pāṇḍavas, who are truly deserving of worship, be honored.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 17. Uttara said: 'My opinion (mata) is that they are worthy of honor, reverence, and respect, and that this is indeed appropriate now. Let the greatly fortunate Pāṇḍavas, who are truly deserving of worship, be honored.'

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।अहं खल्वपि संग्रामे शत्रूणां वशमागतः ।मोक्षितो भीमसेनेन गावश्च विजितास्तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,ahaṁ khalvapi saṁgrāme śatrūṇāṁ vaśamāgataḥ ,mokṣito bhīmasenena gāvaśca vijitāstathā.
english: King Virāṭa said: 'Indeed, I myself had fallen under the sway of enemies in battle. I was freed by Bhīmasena, and thus the cows were also recovered.'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 18. King Virāṭa said: 'Indeed, I myself had fallen under the sway of enemies in battle. I was freed by Bhīmasena, and thus the cows were also recovered.'

sanskrit: एतेषां बाहुवीर्येण यदस्माकं जयो मृधे ।वयं सर्वे सहामात्याः कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ।प्रसादयामो भद्रं ते सानुजं पाण्डवर्षभम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: eteṣāṁ bāhuvīryeṇa yadasmākaṁ jayo mṛdhe ,vayaṁ sarve sahāmātyāḥ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,prasādayāmo bhadraṁ te sānujaṁ pāṇḍavarṣabham.
english: Because our victory in that battle (mṛdhe) was due to the might of their arms, we, all the ministers included, shall conciliate Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, that best of the Pāṇḍavas, along with his younger brothers. May you be well (bhadra).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 19. Because our victory in that battle (mṛdhe) was due to the might of their arms, we, all the ministers included, shall conciliate Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, that best of the Pāṇḍavas, along with his younger brothers. May you be well (bhadra).

sanskrit: यदस्माभिरजानद्भिः किंचिदुक्तो नराधिपः ।क्षन्तुमर्हति तत्सर्वं धर्मात्मा ह्येष पाण्डवः ॥२०॥
transliteration: yadasmābhirajānadbhiḥ kiṁcidukto narādhipaḥ ,kṣantumarhati tatsarvaṁ dharmātmā hyeṣa pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Whatever was said by us unknowingly to the king, he should forgive all of it, for this Pandava is indeed a person of righteous (dharma) character.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 20. Whatever was said by us unknowingly to the king, he should forgive all of it, for this Pandava is indeed a person of righteous (dharma) character.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो विराटः परमाभितुष्टः समेत्य राज्ञा समयं चकार ।राज्यं च सर्वं विससर्ज तस्मै सदण्डकोशं सपुरं महात्मा ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato virāṭaḥ paramābhituṣṭaḥ; sametya rājñā samayaṁ cakāra ,rājyaṁ ca sarvaṁ visasarja tasmai; sadaṇḍakośaṁ sapuraṁ mahātmā.
english: Vaiśampaāyana said: Then, King Virata, exceedingly pleased, made an agreement with King Yudhishthira after meeting him. That great-souled (mahātmā) one relinquished to him the entire kingdom, along with its army, treasury, and cities.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 21. Vaiśampaāyana said: Then, King Virata, exceedingly pleased, made an agreement with King Yudhishthira after meeting him. That great-souled (mahātmā) one relinquished to him the entire kingdom, along with its army, treasury, and cities.

sanskrit: पाण्डवांश्च ततः सर्वान्मत्स्यराजः प्रतापवान् ।धनंजयं पुरस्कृत्य दिष्ट्या दिष्ट्येति चाब्रवीत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavāṁśca tataḥ sarvānmatsyarājaḥ pratāpavān ,dhanaṁjayaṁ puraskṛtya diṣṭyā diṣṭyeti cābravīt.
english: Then, the powerful king of Matsya, having placed Dhananjaya (Arjuna) at the forefront, joyfully exclaimed to all the Pandavas, 'How fortunate! How fortunate!'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 22. Then, the powerful king of Matsya, having placed Dhananjaya (Arjuna) at the forefront, joyfully exclaimed to all the Pandavas, 'How fortunate! How fortunate!'

sanskrit: समुपाघ्राय मूर्धानं संश्लिष्य च पुनः पुनः ।युधिष्ठिरं च भीमं च माद्रीपुत्रौ च पाण्डवौ ॥२३॥
transliteration: samupāghrāya mūrdhānaṁ saṁśliṣya ca punaḥ punaḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca bhīmaṁ ca mādrīputrau ca pāṇḍavau.
english: (King Virata) repeatedly embraced them, smelling the heads of Yudhishthira, Bhima, and the two Pandavas, the sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 23. (King Virata) repeatedly embraced them, smelling the heads of Yudhishthira, Bhima, and the two Pandavas, the sons of Madri (Nakula and Sahadeva).

sanskrit: नातृप्यद्दर्शने तेषां विराटो वाहिनीपतिः ।संप्रीयमाणो राजानं युधिष्ठिरमथाब्रवीत् ॥२४॥
transliteration: nātṛpyaddarśane teṣāṁ virāṭo vāhinīpatiḥ ,saṁprīyamāṇo rājānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiramathābravīt.
english: Virāṭa, the commander of the army, could not get enough of seeing them. Extremely pleased, he then addressed King Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 24. Virāṭa, the commander of the army, could not get enough of seeing them. Extremely pleased, he then addressed King Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या भवन्तः संप्राप्ताः सर्वे कुशलिनो वनात् ।दिष्ट्या च पारितं कृच्छ्रमज्ञातं वै दुरात्मभिः ॥२५॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā bhavantaḥ saṁprāptāḥ sarve kuśalino vanāt ,diṣṭyā ca pāritaṁ kṛcchramajñātaṁ vai durātmabhiḥ.
english: Fortunately, all of you have arrived safely from the forest. And fortunately, the hardship of your incognito period has been successfully overcome, remaining unknown to the wicked (durātmans).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 25. Fortunately, all of you have arrived safely from the forest. And fortunately, the hardship of your incognito period has been successfully overcome, remaining unknown to the wicked (durātmans).

sanskrit: इदं च राज्यं नः पार्था यच्चान्यद्वसु किंचन ।प्रतिगृह्णन्तु तत्सर्वं कौन्तेया अविशङ्कया ॥२६॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca rājyaṁ naḥ pārthā yaccānyadvasu kiṁcana ,pratigṛhṇantu tatsarvaṁ kaunteyā aviśaṅkayā.
english: O Pārthas, this kingdom of ours and whatever other wealth there may be – let the Kaunteyas accept all of it without hesitation.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 26. O Pārthas, this kingdom of ours and whatever other wealth there may be – let the Kaunteyas accept all of it without hesitation.

sanskrit: उत्तरां प्रतिगृह्णातु सव्यसाची धनंजयः ।अयं ह्यौपयिको भर्ता तस्याः पुरुषसत्तमः ॥२७॥
transliteration: uttarāṁ pratigṛhṇātu savyasācī dhanaṁjayaḥ ,ayaṁ hyaupayiko bhartā tasyāḥ puruṣasattamaḥ.
english: Let Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the ambidextrous one, accept Uttarā. For this best among men is indeed a suitable husband for her.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 27. Let Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the ambidextrous one, accept Uttarā. For this best among men is indeed a suitable husband for her.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तो धर्मराजः पार्थमैक्षद्धनंजयम् ।ईक्षितश्चार्जुनो भ्रात्रा मत्स्यं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: evamukto dharmarājaḥ pārthamaikṣaddhanaṁjayam ,īkṣitaścārjuno bhrātrā matsyaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Having been thus addressed, Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) looked at Arjuna (Dhananjaya). And Arjuna, having been looked at by his brother, then spoke these words to King Virata (Matsya).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 28. Having been thus addressed, Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) looked at Arjuna (Dhananjaya). And Arjuna, having been looked at by his brother, then spoke these words to King Virata (Matsya).

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्णाम्यहं राजन्स्नुषां दुहितरं तव ।युक्तश्चावां हि संबन्धो मत्स्यभारतसत्तमौ ॥२९॥
transliteration: pratigṛhṇāmyahaṁ rājansnuṣāṁ duhitaraṁ tava ,yuktaścāvāṁ hi saṁbandho matsyabhāratasattamau.
english: O King, I accept your daughter as my daughter-in-law. Indeed, this relationship is fitting for us both, O best among the Matsyas (King Virata) and the Bharatas (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 66 Verse 29. O King, I accept your daughter as my daughter-in-law. Indeed, this relationship is fitting for us both, O best among the Matsyas (King Virata) and the Bharatas (Arjuna).

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।किमर्थं पाण्डवश्रेष्ठ भार्यां दुहितरं मम ।प्रतिग्रहीतुं नेमां त्वं मया दत्तामिहेच्छसि ॥१॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,kimarthaṁ pāṇḍavaśreṣṭha bhāryāṁ duhitaraṁ mama ,pratigrahītuṁ nemāṁ tvaṁ mayā dattāmihecchasi.
english: Virata said: "O best of the Pandavas, why do you not wish to accept my daughter, whom I am offering here, as a wife?"
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 1. Virata said: "O best of the Pandavas, why do you not wish to accept my daughter, whom I am offering here, as a wife?"

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।अन्तःपुरेऽहमुषितः सदा पश्यन्सुतां तव ।रहस्यं च प्रकाशं च विश्वस्ता पितृवन्मयि ॥२॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,antaḥpure'hamuṣitaḥ sadā paśyansutāṁ tava ,rahasyaṁ ca prakāśaṁ ca viśvastā pitṛvanmayi.
english: Arjuna said: "I have resided in the inner chambers, always seeing your daughter, both privately and publicly. She placed her trust in me as if I were her father."
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 2. Arjuna said: "I have resided in the inner chambers, always seeing your daughter, both privately and publicly. She placed her trust in me as if I were her father."

sanskrit: प्रियो बहुमतश्चाहं नर्तको गीतकोविदः ।आचार्यवच्च मां नित्यं मन्यते दुहिता तव ॥३॥
transliteration: priyo bahumataścāhaṁ nartako gītakovidaḥ ,ācāryavacca māṁ nityaṁ manyate duhitā tava.
english: I am dear and highly esteemed, a dancer and an expert in singing. Your daughter always regards me like a teacher.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 3. I am dear and highly esteemed, a dancer and an expert in singing. Your daughter always regards me like a teacher.

sanskrit: वयःस्थया तया राजन्सह संवत्सरोषितः ।अतिशङ्का भवेत्स्थाने तव लोकस्य चाभिभो ॥४॥
transliteration: vayaḥsthayā tayā rājansaha saṁvatsaroṣitaḥ ,atiśaṅkā bhavetsthāne tava lokasya cābhibho.
english: O King, O Lord, having resided with her, a grown-up young woman, for a year, great suspicion would justifiably arise for you and for the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 4. O King, O Lord, having resided with her, a grown-up young woman, for a year, great suspicion would justifiably arise for you and for the people.

sanskrit: तस्मान्निमन्त्रये त्वाहं दुहितुः पृथिवीपते ।शुद्धो जितेन्द्रियो दान्तस्तस्याः शुद्धिः कृता मया ॥५॥
transliteration: tasmānnimantraye tvāhaṁ duhituḥ pṛthivīpate ,śuddho jitendriyo dāntastasyāḥ śuddhiḥ kṛtā mayā.
english: Therefore, O King, O lord of the earth, I entreat you for your daughter. I am pure, self-controlled (jitendriya), and disciplined; her purity has been established by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 5. Therefore, O King, O lord of the earth, I entreat you for your daughter. I am pure, self-controlled (jitendriya), and disciplined; her purity has been established by me.

sanskrit: स्नुषाया दुहितुर्वापि पुत्रे चात्मनि वा पुनः ।अत्र शङ्कां न पश्यामि तेन शुद्धिर्भविष्यति ॥६॥
transliteration: snuṣāyā duhiturvāpi putre cātmani vā punaḥ ,atra śaṅkāṁ na paśyāmi tena śuddhirbhaviṣyati.
english: Whether regarding a daughter-in-law, a daughter, a son, or even one's own self (ātman), I see no reason for doubt here. Therefore, her purity will be established.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 6. Whether regarding a daughter-in-law, a daughter, a son, or even one's own self (ātman), I see no reason for doubt here. Therefore, her purity will be established.

sanskrit: अभिषङ्गादहं भीतो मिथ्याचारात्परंतप ।स्नुषार्थमुत्तरां राजन्प्रतिगृह्णामि ते सुताम् ॥७॥
transliteration: abhiṣaṅgādahaṁ bhīto mithyācārātparaṁtapa ,snuṣārthamuttarāṁ rājanpratigṛhṇāmi te sutām.
english: O scorcher of foes, I am afraid of disgrace (abhiṣaṅga) arising from improper conduct (mithyācāra). O King, I accept your daughter Uttara for the purpose of a daughter-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 7. O scorcher of foes, I am afraid of disgrace (abhiṣaṅga) arising from improper conduct (mithyācāra). O King, I accept your daughter Uttara for the purpose of a daughter-in-law.

sanskrit: स्वस्रीयो वासुदेवस्य साक्षाद्देवशिशुर्यथा ।दयितश्चक्रहस्तस्य बाल एवास्त्रकोविदः ॥८॥
transliteration: svasrīyo vāsudevasya sākṣāddevaśiśuryathā ,dayitaścakrahastasya bāla evāstrakovidaḥ.
english: He is the nephew of Vāsudeva (Krishna), truly like a divine child. He is dear to the wielder of the discus (Krishna), and though just a boy, he is indeed an expert in weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 8. He is the nephew of Vāsudeva (Krishna), truly like a divine child. He is dear to the wielder of the discus (Krishna), and though just a boy, he is indeed an expert in weapons.

sanskrit: अभिमन्युर्महाबाहुः पुत्रो मम विशां पते ।जामाता तव युक्तो वै भर्ता च दुहितुस्तव ॥९॥
transliteration: abhimanyurmahābāhuḥ putro mama viśāṁ pate ,jāmātā tava yukto vai bhartā ca duhitustava.
english: O lord of people (viśām pate), my mighty-armed son Abhimanyu is indeed a suitable son-in-law for you and the husband of your daughter.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 9. O lord of people (viśām pate), my mighty-armed son Abhimanyu is indeed a suitable son-in-law for you and the husband of your daughter.

sanskrit: विराट उवाच ।उपपन्नं कुरुश्रेष्ठे कुन्तीपुत्रे धनंजये ।य एवं धर्मनित्यश्च जातज्ञानश्च पाण्डवः ॥१०॥
transliteration: virāṭa uvāca ,upapannaṁ kuruśreṣṭhe kuntīputre dhanaṁjaye ,ya evaṁ dharmanityaśca jātajñānaśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Virata said: 'O best among the Kurus, O son of Kunti, O Dhananjaya, it is perfectly appropriate that a Pāṇḍava like you, who is thus constantly devoted to his intrinsic nature (dharma) and endowed with knowledge, [should make such a proposal].'
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 10. Virata said: 'O best among the Kurus, O son of Kunti, O Dhananjaya, it is perfectly appropriate that a Pāṇḍava like you, who is thus constantly devoted to his intrinsic nature (dharma) and endowed with knowledge, [should make such a proposal].'

sanskrit: यत्कृत्यं मन्यसे पार्थ क्रियतां तदनन्तरम् ।सर्वे कामाः समृद्धा मे संबन्धी यस्य मेऽर्जुनः ॥११॥
transliteration: yatkṛtyaṁ manyase pārtha kriyatāṁ tadanantaram ,sarve kāmāḥ samṛddhā me saṁbandhī yasya me'rjunaḥ.
english: Arjuna (Pārtha), whatever action you deem appropriate, let it be performed immediately thereafter. All my desires are fulfilled because Arjuna is my kinsman.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 11. Arjuna (Pārtha), whatever action you deem appropriate, let it be performed immediately thereafter. All my desires are fulfilled because Arjuna is my kinsman.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं ब्रुवति राजेन्द्रे कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।अन्वजानात्स संयोगं समये मत्स्यपार्थयोः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ bruvati rājendre kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,anvajānātsa saṁyogaṁ samaye matsyapārthayoḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: As the king of kings (rājendra) spoke thus, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, consented to that alliance (saṃyoga) regarding Matsya (King Virāṭa) and Arjuna (Pārtha).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 12. Vaiśampāyana said: As the king of kings (rājendra) spoke thus, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, consented to that alliance (saṃyoga) regarding Matsya (King Virāṭa) and Arjuna (Pārtha).

sanskrit: ततो मित्रेषु सर्वेषु वासुदेवे च भारत ।प्रेषयामास कौन्तेयो विराटश्च महीपतिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tato mitreṣu sarveṣu vāsudeve ca bhārata ,preṣayāmāsa kaunteyo virāṭaśca mahīpatiḥ.
english: Then, O Bhārata, Kuntī's son (Yudhiṣṭhira) and King Virāṭa sent messages to all their allies and to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 13. Then, O Bhārata, Kuntī's son (Yudhiṣṭhira) and King Virāṭa sent messages to all their allies and to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: ततस्त्रयोदशे वर्षे निवृत्ते पञ्च पाण्डवाः ।उपप्लव्ये विराटस्य समपद्यन्त सर्वशः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastrayodaśe varṣe nivṛtte pañca pāṇḍavāḥ ,upaplavye virāṭasya samapadyanta sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, after the thirteenth year had ended, all five Pāṇḍavas gathered together at Upaplavya, which was part of King Virāṭa's domain.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 14. Then, after the thirteenth year had ended, all five Pāṇḍavas gathered together at Upaplavya, which was part of King Virāṭa's domain.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्वसंश्च बीभत्सुरानिनाय जनार्दनम् ।आनर्तेभ्योऽपि दाशार्हानभिमन्युं च पाण्डवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasminvasaṁśca bībhatsurānināya janārdanam ,ānartebhyo'pi dāśārhānabhimanyuṁ ca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), dwelling there as Bibhatsu, brought Janardana (Krishna), along with Abhimanyu and the Daśārhas from the Anarta region.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 15. The son of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna), dwelling there as Bibhatsu, brought Janardana (Krishna), along with Abhimanyu and the Daśārhas from the Anarta region.

sanskrit: काशिराजश्च शैब्यश्च प्रीयमाणौ युधिष्ठिरे ।अक्षौहिणीभ्यां सहितावागतौ पृथिवीपते ॥१६॥
transliteration: kāśirājaśca śaibyaśca prīyamāṇau yudhiṣṭhire ,akṣauhiṇībhyāṁ sahitāvāgatau pṛthivīpate.
english: O lord of the earth, the King of Kāśi and Śaibya, pleased with Yudhiṣṭhira, arrived accompanied by two akṣauhiṇīs.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 16. O lord of the earth, the King of Kāśi and Śaibya, pleased with Yudhiṣṭhira, arrived accompanied by two akṣauhiṇīs.

sanskrit: अक्षौहिण्या च तेजस्वी यज्ञसेनो महाबलः ।द्रौपद्याश्च सुता वीराः शिखण्डी चापराजितः ॥१७॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇyā ca tejasvī yajñaseno mahābalaḥ ,draupadyāśca sutā vīrāḥ śikhaṇḍī cāparājitaḥ.
english: And Yajnasena (Drupada), brilliant and immensely powerful with an akṣauhiṇī army, along with Draupadi's heroic sons and the unconquered Śikhaṇḍī.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 17. And Yajnasena (Drupada), brilliant and immensely powerful with an akṣauhiṇī army, along with Draupadi's heroic sons and the unconquered Śikhaṇḍī.

sanskrit: धृष्टद्युम्नश्च दुर्धर्षः सर्वशस्त्रभृतां वरः ।समस्ताक्षौहिणीपाला यज्वानो भूरिदक्षिणाः ।सर्वे शस्त्रास्त्रसंपन्नाः सर्वे शूरास्तनुत्यजः ॥१८॥
transliteration: dhṛṣṭadyumnaśca durdharṣaḥ sarvaśastrabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,samastākṣauhiṇīpālā yajvāno bhūridakṣiṇāḥ ,sarve śastrāstrasaṁpannāḥ sarve śūrāstanutyajaḥ.
english: And Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who is formidable and the foremost among all weapon-bearers. Also, all the commanders of entire akshauhinis, who are performers of Vedic rituals (yajña) and bestow ample gifts. All of them are fully equipped with weapons and missiles, and all are valiant warriors ready to lay down their lives.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 18. And Dhṛṣṭadyumna, who is formidable and the foremost among all weapon-bearers. Also, all the commanders of entire akshauhinis, who are performers of Vedic rituals (yajña) and bestow ample gifts. All of them are fully equipped with weapons and missiles, and all are valiant warriors ready to lay down their lives.

sanskrit: तानागतानभिप्रेक्ष्य मत्स्यो धर्मभृतां वरः ।प्रीतोऽभवद्दुहितरं दत्त्वा तामभिमन्यवे ॥१९॥
transliteration: tānāgatānabhiprekṣya matsyo dharmabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,prīto'bhavadduhitaraṁ dattvā tāmabhimanyave.
english: Observing those who had arrived, Matsya, the best among those who uphold righteousness (dharma), became pleased after giving his daughter to Abhimanyu.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 19. Observing those who had arrived, Matsya, the best among those who uphold righteousness (dharma), became pleased after giving his daughter to Abhimanyu.

sanskrit: ततः प्रत्युपयातेषु पार्थिवेषु ततस्ततः ।तत्रागमद्वासुदेवो वनमाली हलायुधः ।कृतवर्मा च हार्दिक्यो युयुधानश्च सात्यकिः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pratyupayāteṣu pārthiveṣu tatastataḥ ,tatrāgamadvāsudevo vanamālī halāyudhaḥ ,kṛtavarmā ca hārdikyo yuyudhānaśca sātyakiḥ.
english: Then, after the kings had returned from their various places, Vāsudeva, adorned with a forest garland and bearing a plow for a weapon, arrived there. Kṛtavarmā, the Hārdikya, and Yuyudhāna, known as Sātyaki, also came.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 20. Then, after the kings had returned from their various places, Vāsudeva, adorned with a forest garland and bearing a plow for a weapon, arrived there. Kṛtavarmā, the Hārdikya, and Yuyudhāna, known as Sātyaki, also came.

sanskrit: अनाधृष्टिस्तथाक्रूरः साम्बो निशठ एव च ।अभिमन्युमुपादाय सह मात्रा परंतपाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: anādhṛṣṭistathākrūraḥ sāmbo niśaṭha eva ca ,abhimanyumupādāya saha mātrā paraṁtapāḥ.
english: Anādhṛṣṭi, Akrūra, Sāmba, and Niśatha also arrived, these tormentors of foes, bringing Abhimanyu with his mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 21. Anādhṛṣṭi, Akrūra, Sāmba, and Niśatha also arrived, these tormentors of foes, bringing Abhimanyu with his mother.

sanskrit: इन्द्रसेनादयश्चैव रथैस्तैः सुसमाहितैः ।आययुः सहिताः सर्वे परिसंवत्सरोषिताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: indrasenādayaścaiva rathaistaiḥ susamāhitaiḥ ,āyayuḥ sahitāḥ sarve parisaṁvatsaroṣitāḥ.
english: Indrasena and the others also came, all together, with their well-equipped chariots. They had been staying for a full year.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 22. Indrasena and the others also came, all together, with their well-equipped chariots. They had been staying for a full year.

sanskrit: दश नागसहस्राणि हयानां च शतायुतम् ।रथानामर्बुदं पूर्णं निखर्वं च पदातिनाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: daśa nāgasahasrāṇi hayānāṁ ca śatāyutam ,rathānāmarbudaṁ pūrṇaṁ nikharvaṁ ca padātinām.
english: Ten thousand elephants, a million horses, a full ten million chariots, and ten billion foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 23. Ten thousand elephants, a million horses, a full ten million chariots, and ten billion foot soldiers.

sanskrit: वृष्ण्यन्धकाश्च बहवो भोजाश्च परमौजसः ।अन्वयुर्वृष्णिशार्दूलं वासुदेवं महाद्युतिम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vṛṣṇyandhakāśca bahavo bhojāśca paramaujasaḥ ,anvayurvṛṣṇiśārdūlaṁ vāsudevaṁ mahādyutim.
english: Many Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, along with Bhājas of supreme might, followed Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the best of the Vṛṣṇis, who possessed great splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 24. Many Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas, along with Bhājas of supreme might, followed Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the best of the Vṛṣṇis, who possessed great splendor.

sanskrit: पारिबर्हं ददौ कृष्णः पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ।स्त्रियो रत्नानि वासांसि पृथक्पृथगनेकशः ।ततो विवाहो विधिवद्ववृते मत्स्यपार्थयोः ॥२५॥
transliteration: pāribarhaṁ dadau kṛṣṇaḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām ,striyo ratnāni vāsāṁsi pṛthakpṛthaganekaśaḥ ,tato vivāho vidhivadvavṛte matsyapārthayoḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa gave many separate gifts to the high-souled Pāṇḍavas, including women, jewels, and garments. Then, the marriage of the Matsya princess and Pārtha (Arjuna) proceeded according to ritual.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 25. Kṛṣṇa gave many separate gifts to the high-souled Pāṇḍavas, including women, jewels, and garments. Then, the marriage of the Matsya princess and Pārtha (Arjuna) proceeded according to ritual.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च गोमुखाडम्बरास्तथा ।पार्थैः संयुज्यमानस्य नेदुर्मत्स्यस्य वेश्मनि ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ śaṅkhāśca bheryaśca gomukhāḍambarāstathā ,pārthaiḥ saṁyujyamānasya nedurmatsyasya veśmani.
english: Then, conch shells, drums, and cow-mouth trumpets (gomukhas) resounded in the palace of the Matsya king, who was celebrating the alliance with the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas).
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 26. Then, conch shells, drums, and cow-mouth trumpets (gomukhas) resounded in the palace of the Matsya king, who was celebrating the alliance with the Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas).

sanskrit: उच्चावचान्मृगाञ्जघ्नुर्मेध्यांश्च शतशः पशून् ।सुरामैरेयपानानि प्रभूतान्यभ्यहारयन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: uccāvacānmṛgāñjaghnurmedhyāṁśca śataśaḥ paśūn ,surāmaireyapānāni prabhūtānyabhyahārayan.
english: They slaughtered various kinds of deer and hundreds of animals suitable for sacrifice. They also arranged for abundant quantities of sura (wine) and maireya (liquor) to be brought.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 27. They slaughtered various kinds of deer and hundreds of animals suitable for sacrifice. They also arranged for abundant quantities of sura (wine) and maireya (liquor) to be brought.

sanskrit: गायनाख्यानशीलाश्च नटा वैतालिकास्तथा ।स्तुवन्तस्तानुपातिष्ठन्सूताश्च सह मागधैः ॥२८॥
transliteration: gāyanākhyānaśīlāśca naṭā vaitālikāstathā ,stuvantastānupātiṣṭhansūtāśca saha māgadhaiḥ.
english: Those skilled in singing and storytelling, along with actors and bards, attended upon them, offering praise. Charioteers and Magadha bards were also present.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 28. Those skilled in singing and storytelling, along with actors and bards, attended upon them, offering praise. Charioteers and Magadha bards were also present.

sanskrit: सुदेष्णां च पुरस्कृत्य मत्स्यानां च वरस्त्रियः ।आजग्मुश्चारुसर्वाङ्ग्यः सुमृष्टमणिकुण्डलाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: sudeṣṇāṁ ca puraskṛtya matsyānāṁ ca varastriyaḥ ,ājagmuścārusarvāṅgyaḥ sumṛṣṭamaṇikuṇḍalāḥ.
english: Placing Sudeṣṇā in front, and also accompanied by the chief ladies of the Matsyas, women arrived, beautiful in every limb and adorned with brightly polished jeweled earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 29. Placing Sudeṣṇā in front, and also accompanied by the chief ladies of the Matsyas, women arrived, beautiful in every limb and adorned with brightly polished jeweled earrings.

sanskrit: वर्णोपपन्नास्ता नार्यो रूपवत्यः स्वलंकृताः ।सर्वाश्चाभ्यभवत्कृष्णा रूपेण यशसा श्रिया ॥३०॥
transliteration: varṇopapannāstā nāryo rūpavatyaḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,sarvāścābhyabhavatkṛṣṇā rūpeṇa yaśasā śriyā.
english: Those women, endowed with good qualities (or caste), beautiful, and well-adorned, were all surpassed by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) in terms of beauty, fame, and splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 30. Those women, endowed with good qualities (or caste), beautiful, and well-adorned, were all surpassed by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) in terms of beauty, fame, and splendor.

sanskrit: परिवार्योत्तरां तास्तु राजपुत्रीमलंकृताम् ।सुतामिव महेन्द्रस्य पुरस्कृत्योपतस्थिरे ॥३१॥
transliteration: parivāryottarāṁ tāstu rājaputrīmalaṁkṛtām ,sutāmiva mahendrasya puraskṛtyopatasthire.
english: The women of the palace surrounded Uttara, the adorned princess, and, honoring her as if she were the daughter of Mahendra (Indra), they approached.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 31. The women of the palace surrounded Uttara, the adorned princess, and, honoring her as if she were the daughter of Mahendra (Indra), they approached.

sanskrit: तां प्रत्यगृह्णात्कौन्तेयः सुतस्यार्थे धनंजयः ।सौभद्रस्यानवद्याङ्गीं विराटतनयां तदा ॥३२॥
transliteration: tāṁ pratyagṛhṇātkaunteyaḥ sutasyārthe dhanaṁjayaḥ ,saubhadrasyānavadyāṅgīṁ virāṭatanayāṁ tadā.
english: Then, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the son of Kunti, accepted her, the flawless-limbed daughter of Virata, for the sake of his son, Abhimanyu.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 32. Then, Dhananjaya (Arjuna), the son of Kunti, accepted her, the flawless-limbed daughter of Virata, for the sake of his son, Abhimanyu.

sanskrit: तत्रातिष्ठन्महाराजो रूपमिन्द्रस्य धारयन् ।स्नुषां तां प्रतिजग्राह कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatrātiṣṭhanmahārājo rūpamindrasya dhārayan ,snuṣāṁ tāṁ pratijagrāha kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: There, the great king Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, assuming the appearance of Indra, accepted her as his daughter-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 33. There, the great king Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, assuming the appearance of Indra, accepted her as his daughter-in-law.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य च तां पार्थः पुरस्कृत्य जनार्दनम् ।विवाहं कारयामास सौभद्रस्य महात्मनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya ca tāṁ pārthaḥ puraskṛtya janārdanam ,vivāhaṁ kārayāmāsa saubhadrasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: And having accepted her, Partha (Arjuna), honoring Janardana (Krishna), arranged the marriage of the great-souled Abhimanyu.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 34. And having accepted her, Partha (Arjuna), honoring Janardana (Krishna), arranged the marriage of the great-souled Abhimanyu.

sanskrit: तस्मै सप्त सहस्राणि हयानां वातरंहसाम् ।द्वे च नागशते मुख्ये प्रादाद्बहु धनं तदा ॥३५॥
transliteration: tasmai sapta sahasrāṇi hayānāṁ vātaraṁhasām ,dve ca nāgaśate mukhye prādādbahu dhanaṁ tadā.
english: Then, to him, he gave seven thousand horses as swift as the wind, two hundred excellent elephants, and much wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 35. Then, to him, he gave seven thousand horses as swift as the wind, two hundred excellent elephants, and much wealth.

sanskrit: कृते विवाहे तु तदा धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।ब्राह्मणेभ्यो ददौ वित्तं यदुपाहरदच्युतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: kṛte vivāhe tu tadā dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,brāhmaṇebhyo dadau vittaṁ yadupāharadacyutaḥ.
english: Then, after the marriage was performed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (natural law), distributed to the Brahmins the wealth that Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) had brought.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 36. Then, after the marriage was performed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (natural law), distributed to the Brahmins the wealth that Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) had brought.

sanskrit: गोसहस्राणि रत्नानि वस्त्राणि विविधानि च ।भूषणानि च मुख्यानि यानानि शयनानि च ॥३७॥
transliteration: gosahasrāṇi ratnāni vastrāṇi vividhāni ca ,bhūṣaṇāni ca mukhyāni yānāni śayanāni ca.
english: He distributed thousands of cows, jewels, various garments, excellent ornaments, vehicles, and couches.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 37. He distributed thousands of cows, jewels, various garments, excellent ornaments, vehicles, and couches.

sanskrit: तन्महोत्सवसंकाशं हृष्टपुष्टजनावृतम् ।नगरं मत्स्यराजस्य शुशुभे भरतर्षभ ॥३८॥
transliteration: tanmahotsavasaṁkāśaṁ hṛṣṭapuṣṭajanāvṛtam ,nagaraṁ matsyarājasya śuśubhe bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, that city of the Matsya king, bustling with joyful and well-nourished people and resembling a great festival, shone brilliantly.
search_text: Mahabharata Virāṭa Parva Chapter 67 Verse 38. O best of the Bharatas, that city of the Matsya king, bustling with joyful and well-nourished people and resembling a great festival, shone brilliantly.
